chapters.title: Chapter 1997: Sparring With Kelan(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1997: Sparring With Kelan(3)

Chapter 1997: Sparring With Kelan(3)

“Fuck, I have never met or heard of anyone that’s even close to being as monstrous as you,” Kelan muttered after learning that Yuan was barely showing all of his potential but was still able to make a True Immortal feel helpless while only being a mortal.

“You know, you should join… No, I’ll tell you about it later.”

Kelan proceeded to take a deep breath before increasing his cultivation even further, stepping into the first level of God Ascension.

“Well? Do you think you’ll be able to handle the God Ascension realm?” he asked Yuan with a provocative smile.

“We won’t know until we try,” said Yuan.

“Then here I come!”

Kelan made the first move, and the two of them clashed shortly later. However, despite being in the God Ascension realm, Kelan didn’t use Celestial Qi to directly attack Yuan, only using it to strengthen his own physique.

Yet, even with the power of Celestial Qi, Kelan only found himself on par with Yuan, who was still only using his physique.

“Why don’t we fight seriously now? You can use your full cultivation, and I’ll do the same,” Yuan suggested after thousands of exchanges.

“Don’t blame me if anything goes wrong!” Kelan responded as he unleashed his full cultivation at the fifth level of God Ascension realm.

Yuan also no longer held back, and his body surged with Immortal Qi.

<God of War’s Astral Arts!>

Yuan activated God of War’s Astral Arts, perfectly controlling the avatar’s size to be just slightly bigger than his own frame.

Since Kelan fought with his fists, Yuan decided not to use a weapon and continued fighting with his bare hands.

When they clashed again, the sheer force of their blows fractured the very space around them, sending cracks rippling through the air, though the rifts swiftly mended themselves.

“HAHAHAHA!” Kelan roared with exhilaration as he traded blows with Yuan, his eyes flickering with delight.

“This is it! This is the kind of manly fight I’ve been craving!”

Kelan was primarily a body refiner, having honed his physique to the extreme. Few cultivators could rival him in sheer physical prowess, making battles like the one he now faced with Yuan exceedingly rare. And even when such encounters did occur, they never left him satisfied, as his opponents would fall too quickly to offer any true challenge.

“I never imagined I’d experience a battle like this with a mortal! You’re truly something else, Yuan! At this rate, I might really lose myself and try to kill you!” Sёarch* The ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan smiled at his words and said, “Kill me? I’d like to see you try!”

Yuan and Kelan’s battle raged on for nearly an entire month without pause. In the final days, Kelan unleashed everything he had, fighting with the intent to kill.

Yet no matter how hard he pushed, the most he could achieve was tearing off a few limbs—wounds that Yuan quickly recovered from.

In the end, Kelan lay sprawled on the ground, his chest rising and falling with exhaustion as he stared blankly at the endless white, his expression dazed, caught between disbelief and awe.

Yuan sat on the ground, his entire body soaked in sweat. However, his breathing was calm, and his body was still surging with strength.

While Kelan looked as if he had sprinted through a marathon, Yuan, by contrast, appeared as though he had merely taken a leisurely jog around the park.

“Does the Golden Immortal Physique give you unlimited stamina, as well?” Kelan suddenly asked.

“No, that’s something else.”

“Something else…? Just how many secrets are contained within that small body of yours?”

“Who knows?” Yuan replied with a casual shrug.

It might have come off as evasive, but in truth, he genuinely didn’t have the answer.

“Are you two finally done?” Wang Ming called out as he and the others approached.

None of them had left during the month-long battle—they had remained there from start to finish, eyes fixed on every clash and exchange.

And just from observing, each of them had gained insight, advancing their understanding and even strengthening their own cultivation in the process.

“Sorry, I didn’t intend on taking up so much of your time,” Kelan said to Yuan.

He shook his head and said, “No, it’s fine. I needed time to adjust to my new condition, too.”

“You’ll be staying at the sect, right? I’ll prepare a place for you.”

“No need. I can just live with Meixiu or Lulu.” He turned to them and continued, “Are you okay with that?”

“Of course! In fact, I was going to make that suggestion!” Chu Liuxiang said.

Meixiu nodded in agreement.

“Okay…”

Just as Kelan stood up and prepared to leave, Yuan suddenly stopped him.

“Wait a minute.”

“What is it?”

“Don’t reject this.”

Yuan tapped his forehead, drawing forth a glowing orb of light. With a casual flick of his finger—almost as if launching a flicked booger—he sent the orb sailing through the air, striking Kelan squarely between the brows.

Kelan trusted Yuan and didn’t resist the surge of information flooding into his mind.

“T-THIS IS?!?!?!” he cried out, his voice trembling with shock, as if he’d just seen a ghost. His expression twisted with disbelief, eyes wide and filled with utter astonishment.

Yuan smiled and said, “Don’t worry, it’s real. Consider it a gift for taking care of my friends for the last few years.”

After taking a moment to process the information Yuan had shared with him, Kelan suddenly dropped to his knees and kowtowed without hesitation.

“Please,” he said with deep reverence, “allow me to call you Father.”

“What the heck?” Shi Lang and the others stared with wide eyes, wondering what Yuan had given Kelan for him to act like this.

“Rejected.” Yuan calmly refused.

“Then, how about Young Master?!”

“No. I don’t want to hear that from you. Just continue treating me normally.”

“As you command!” Kelan shouted before jumping to his feet.

He turned to look at Meixiu and the others and said, “I’ll be entering seclusion for a bit. You can train yourselves in the meantime, or take a break. You all deserve it.”

Then, like an overjoyed child with a new toy, he skipped away, grinning from ear to ear.

chapters.title: Chapter 1998: A Week With Meixiu
chapters.body:
Chapter 1998: A Week With Meixiu

Chapter 1998: A Week With Meixiu

Once Kelan left the sparring grounds, Yuan said, “We will have plenty of time to catch up, so I’m going to take some time to rest for now.”

“Who are you going to stay with?” Chu Liuxiang asked. “Me or Meixiu?”

He quickly replied, “I was going to take turns, so I would stay with one of you for a week, then switch, and repeat.”

“That sounds good.” Chu Liuxiang nodded.

She turned to look at Meixiu and continued, “To make things fair, let’s rock-paper-scissors with our eyes closed. No divine sense, either.”

Meixiu nodded and closed her eyes.

“Rock… paper… scissors!”

They opened their eyes to check the results.

“Ugh. Looks like you win this one…” Chu Liuxiang sighed.

Feng Yuxiang, Lan Yingying, and Yingzi decided to leave Yuan’s Dantian for some fresh air and to allow Yuan some privacy. As for Yu Ning, she simply sealed her senses and went to slumber like usual.

“I’m going to take a look around the sect with the other two,” said Feng Yuxiang.

“Try not to cause any trouble…” Yuan said.

Feng Yuxiang chuckled.

“We’re not you, Young Master.”

Some time later, Yuan followed Meixiu back to her living quarters.

“I’ll go take a shower first,” Yuan said as he approached the bathroom.

“Wait…” Meixiu suddenly stopped him.

“Since it’s been a while… let’s go together.”

In response, Yuan silently nodded with a smile on his face, and the two of them entered the bathroom together.

Inside, Meixiu helped Yuan wash his body like in the old times, but instead of just using her hands, she used her entire body.

Over the past few years, she had devoted herself not only to cultivation but also to tempering her physique, refining it until it neared perfection. Her body, though strong enough to deflect ordinary weapons with ease, retained a softness that was almost unreal. Her skin was also as smooth as silk, gliding effortlessly across Yuan’s body with a delicate, irresistible grace.

A few minutes later, Meixiu washed the soap off their body.

“Yuan…”

Unable to restrain herself any longer, Meixiu closed the distance between them and pressed her lips to his in a passionate, heartfelt kiss.

Though she had planned to wait until they returned to the bedroom, the longing that had simmered in her heart for years could no longer be contained, overflowing with unrestrained desire.

Yuan also didn’t realize how much he missed her presence and touch until they were alone.

Soon, their bodies intertwined in an intense cultivation session, and Meixiu’s blissful voice filled the bathroom.

Several hours later, they emerged from the bathroom, still unclothed, and made their way to the bedroom, where they continued to make up for the time they had long been apart, their bond deepening with every passing moment.

After nearly a week of uninterrupted intimacy, the two of them lay side by side in silence, their eyes fixed on the ceiling.

“How’s your mother, Meifeng, doing nowadays?” Yuan eventually asked.

“She’s spending more time in the Nine Heavens since there’s not much for her to do on Earth nowadays. She even started a business as a merchant.”

“A merchant?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at this unexpected news.

“Yes,” Meixiu said. “She started in the Lower Heavens, gradually building her influence as she ascended through each realm. Now, she’s in the Fourth Heaven, and from what I’ve heard, she’s become quite renowned.”

“I see… hopefully she’s not mad at me for not being around lately.”

“Of course not. She misses you a lot and would always ask me about you whenever we encounter each other. Oh, we visit the Myriad of Techniques once every two months to catch up with each other. Yu Rou and the others are also there.”

“Yu Rou… how is she?” Yuan asked, an air of melancholy surrounding him.

“She probably misses you the most out of all of us… and she…”

Meixiu suddenly paused, almost as if contemplating whether to continue.

“Nothing’s happened to her, right?” Yuan sat up on the bed with a worried frown on his face.

Meixiu let out a quiet sigh before continuing, “Honestly, I haven’t seen a genuine smile from her in a long time. Whenever she does smile… it always feels forced.” Searᴄh the ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“At first, we thought someone might be causing her trouble. But according to her close friends, Xia Jingyi and Bai Lianhua, there’s no such person. The real reason she’s been acting that way… is because she misses you.”

Upon hearing those words, Yuan suddenly realized just how much he had been neglecting Yu Rou—something that had quietly begun the moment he first stepped into Cultivation Online and had only deepened with time.

Yuan let out a heavy sigh as he covered his face with his arm.

“To have caused Yu Rou so much pain… I’ve failed her as a brother.”

“But this is mostly because of the Celestial Emperor’s—”

“No,” Yuan interrupted firmly. “I can’t blame the Celestial Emperor. While he may be the reason I haven’t been able to return to Earth these past few years… I started neglecting Yu Rou long before that. This is on me—entirely.”

“…”

Meixiu became silent, and an awkward silence filled the room.

“When’s the next meeting?” Yuan then asked.

“Our last meeting was a month and two weeks ago, so we’ll be meeting again in two weeks.”

“Alright. In the meantime, I need to think of a way to make it up to her.”

Early on the next day, Yuan washed up before leaving Meixiu’s living quarters to head to Chu Liuxiang’s place, which was only a few steps away.

When he arrived at her doorstep, Yuan gave a gentle knock.

Almost instantly, the door swung open.

“Welcome, darling!” Chu Liuxiang greeted him with a playful smile, dressed in a strikingly provocative outfit that left little to the imagination.

Before Yuan could even utter a word, Chu Liuxiang seized his arm and pulled him inside, pressing her lips to his in a deep, eager kiss the moment the door closed behind them.

chapters.title: Chapter 1999: A Week With Chu Liuxiang
chapters.body:
Chapter 1999: A Week With Chu Liuxiang

Chapter 1999: A Week With Chu Liuxiang

“Relax, I won’t be going anywhere for the next week,” Yuan said with a smile after their kiss.

Chu Liuxiang then dragged him to the bedroom.

“I’ve been preparing for this moment,” she said.

“Prepare?” Yuan asked with raised eyebrows.

Chu Liuxiang nodded and retrieved several manuals from her spatial ring and proudly displayed them to him.

“Those are…” Yuan’s eyes widened with surprise when he read the titles on these techniques manuals.

Chu Liuxiang chuckled when she saw Yuan’s expression.

“That’s right! Dual cultivation techniques!” she proudly declared.

During her spare time, she’d study these dual cultivation techniques to prepare for Yuan’s return.

“I haven’t had the opportunity to test it out yet, so it might not be as good. However, I’ll use this time to perfect them!”

Without waiting any time, Chu Liuxiang began testing out her newly acquired techniques on Yuan, letting him experience a new world that even his incarnations had never experienced.

A week later, Yuan and Chu Liuxiang lay beside each other on the bed, their bodies soaked in sweat and other wet substances.

“So… what do you think?” she asked.

Yuan smiled and said, “It’s hard to explain, but if I had to describe it with one word, it’d be otherworldly.”

Chu Liuxiang chuckled and said, “It was a little awkward and embarrassing at first, but I’m glad I didn’t stop and continued. I was worried that I might’ve wasted my time learning these techniques, too.”

Yuan then said, “Dual cultivation techniques, huh? I should learn a few. Did you get them from the Myriad of Techniques?”

“Yes, there’s a whole section just for them!”

Their conversation eventually drifted to family.

“Have you visited your family since what happened?” Yuan asked.

“Yes, I visit them once a year now to have dinner.” Chu Liuxiang said.

After a brief pause, she continued, “Have you heard about Yu Rou from Meixiu yet?”

“She told me. I am still trying to think of a way to make amends for being a bad brother,” he sighed.

“You’re not a bad brother…”

“…”

Some time later, Chu Liuxiang asked, “What are you going to do now?”

“I’ll spend the next week with the others, then meet up with Yu Rou at the Myriad of Techniques.”

“Alright.”

The following day, Yuan went to seek out the others, starting with Wang Ming.

“Hey, want to have a spar? I want your opinion on my current standing,” Wang Ming said after catching up on some things.

Yuan agreed, and the two of them sparred for the next three hours.

Naturally, Wang Ming lost.

“So, how much have I improved?”

“Compared to when we first fought? It’s incomparable,” said Yuan. “You’ve worked hard.”

“Thanks…” Wang Ming said with a somewhat bashful reaction.

“Take this.”

Yuan repeated what he did to Kelan, transferring some information directly inside Wang Ming’s mind. seaʀᴄh thё Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“These are…” Wang Ming’s eyes widened after realizing what Yuan had just given him.

“Nine Supreme Swords and the Limitless Sword Domain. They’re ancient sword techniques from the Primordial Era.” Yuan explained after giving him the sword techniques he’d acquired at Han Zexian’s Tomb.

“I-I don’t know what to say…” Wang Ming muttered in a dazed voice.

“You don’t need to say anything.”

“Thank you!” Wang Ming bowed, feeling overwhelming with joy.

Yuan left Wang Ming to meet with Shi Lang next.

Just like Wang Ming, Shi Lang asked Yuan for a spar after catching up, and Yuan obliged.

After their spar, Yuan shared some of the Evil God’s spear techniques with Shi Lang.

Though they were not as powerful as the sword techniques he’d given Wang Ming, they were powerful in their own right.

After Shi Lang, Yuan continued to visit the members of the Demon Sealing Faction, and he would give them all martial techniques that suited their fighting style.

“How’s Doctor Wang these days?” Yuan asked Wang Xiuying.

“He’s getting old, but because he started cultivating, his health and energy have never been better,” she chuckled.

“You know, he asks me about you often.”

Yuan smiled and said, “It’s a pity I haven’t been able to see him for a long time now. The next time I visit Earth, I’ll definitely give him a visit.”

Some time later, Yuan retrieved a glass vial that contained a pill inside and offered it to Wang Xiuying.

“I don’t really have anything alchemy-related besides this Tier 8 pill I acquired from the Azure Dragon Clan. Take it.”

“Wow… Are you sure?” Wang Xiuying stared at the blue and green pill with excitement. This was her first time seeing a Tier 8 pill, much less having one.

“I gave the others martial techniques, but I won’t be able to give you any techniques.”

Wang Xiuying shook her head and said, “I don’t need any techniques. As an Alchemist, I only need to refine my flames and improve my knowledge, and I have acquired enough information from the Myriad of Techniques to last me many years.”

By the time Yuan visited almost everyone in the Demon Sealing Faction, a week had passed, and it was time to visit Yu Rou and the others at the Myriad of Techniques.

However, most members of the Demon Sealing Faction chose to stay behind, claiming they wished to study the techniques Yuan had shared with them. Of course, that was merely a convenient excuse—in truth, they didn’t want to intrude on his time with Yu Rou, and quietly decided to give them space.

In the end, only Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang followed Yuan to the Myriad of Techniques.

“There’s actually a Myriad of Techniques located within the sect,” Chu Liuxiang revealed as they left together.

Chu Liuxiang and Meixiu led Yuan to the Outer Court, where disciples moved about, tending to their own affairs. Nearly all of them, regardless of gender, possessed impressively muscular builds.

“Who’s that with the Untouchable Faries?”

The disciples quickly took notice of Yuan’s group, though it was Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang who drew the most attention. Their reputation within the sect had soared to incredible heights thanks to their unique status and the fact that they were personally trained by none other than Kelan, the Sect Leader himself.

chapters.title: Chapter 2000: Grown Up
chapters.body:
Chapter 2000: Grown Up

Chapter 2000: Grown Up

“Look at Fairy Chu! She has her arms linked around that man!”

The disciples quickly took notice of the intimate atmosphere around the two, which greatly shocked them.

Chu Liuxiang and the other ladies within the Demon Sealing Faction had earned a reputation for themselves throughout the year for being unobtainable because even Core Disciples were shot down without any hesitation.

“Is that why she’s rejected everyone who approached her? Cause she’s already taken?”

“That’s the only logical explanation.”

“It’s a pity, but I can see why she chose that man. He has this hard-to-describe natural charm about him.”

Yuan found himself constantly being stared at by the disciples until they reached the Myriad of Techniques.

“Wow, so this is what it looks like in the Seventh Heaven,” Yuan muttered in surprise as they stepped inside.

Unlike the compact, space-saving libraries of the lower heavens, the Myriad of Techniques here resembled a grand, traditional archive with vast halls stretching in every direction, towering shelves, and an abundance of open space.

The place was so vast that Yuan couldn’t even see the other walls—only the one behind them near the entrance. It was as if the space extended endlessly in all directions.

“Impressive, right? We were also quite shocked when we first came here,” Chu Liuxiang smiled.

Yuan nodded and glanced around. The first thing he noticed was that most of the people present weren’t disciples of the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect. Some wore the robes of other sects, while others had no uniform at all, suggesting they were independent cultivators or unaffiliated visitors.

This suggested that regardless of which Myriad of Techniques one entered in the Seventh Heaven, they all led to the same vast, interconnected space.

Despite being filled with thousands of cultivators, the place was incredibly silent, with the only noise being the sound of pages being flipped. Since most of the cultivators there flew around when they traveled, the place lacked even the sound of footsteps.

Some time later, they arrived before a guarded teleportation device.

“Welcome back, Lady Meixiu and Lady Chu.” The guard greeted them with respect before turning to look at Yuan and asking, “Will he be accompanying you today?”

Meixiu nodded and said, “He doesn’t have a medallion, but he’s very close friends with Senior Bai.”

“Oh, I have Senior Bai’s medallion if that’s what you mean.” Yuan showed the guard the medallion.

The guard subconsciously straightened his back before bowing to Yuan, “Welcome, Esteemed Guest!”

The guard then activated the teleportation device.

Once Yuan and the others entered the device, they were teleported directly to a familiar place—Senior Bai’s world.

“When does Yu Rou and the others usually show up?” Yuan asked.

“They should show up in an hour. We came here early because we wanted to surprise Yu Rou,” said Meixiu.

“With that said, you should hide until they arrive.” Chu Liuxiang added.

Yuan nodded and completely erased his presence, dumbfounding them.

“Wow… even though I am staring right at you, I cannot sense your presence. Is this what it feels like to hallucinate?” Chu Liuxiang remarked.

Yuan smiled and said, “Want to learn it? I have given the others martial techniques, but I haven’t given the two of you anything yet. It’s a technique that hides your presence.”

Chu Liuxiang and Meixiu nodded.

Thus, Yuan transferred the Shadow Veil to them.

“This is a Celestial-rank technique?!” They exclaimed after learning of its rarity.

“Are you sure we can actually learn this?” Meixiu asked, swallowing nervously. This would be their very first Celestial-rank technique.

Yuan smiled and responded, “That’s a silly thing to ask. Of course, you can learn it. No matter how precious this technique is, it’d be useless if it could not be learned. Also, this will help you guys in case you encounter some trouble.”

“Once we return to the sect, I plan to share even more techniques with all of you,” Yuan said, his tone solemn. “What happened at Dragon Spiral Mountain reminded me—just being connected to me can put you in danger. I want to make sure you’re prepared.”

Yuan went into hiding shortly after to wait for Yu Rou and the others’ arrival.

Three hours later, several new presences appeared in Senior Bai’s world.

“Sorry, we’re late. We ran into some trouble on our way here.”

A beautiful woman with white hair spoke as she entered the place. It was Bai Lianhua, and following behind her were two women who were just as beautiful.

“Trouble? Are you guys okay?”

“Yes… Just the usual womanizers trying to hit on us,” Bai Lianhua said with a sigh, her tone a mix of irritation and resignation.

“Hm? Is it just the two of you today? What happened to the others?” Xia Jingyi asked when she could only see Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang.

Chu Liuxiang smiled and said, “Yes, it’s just us today. The others are busy with some new techniques.”

“How have you been, Yu Rou?” Meixiu asked softly, her gaze resting on the tall, graceful woman quietly standing beside Xia Jingyi.

“The same as usual, but we’re preparing to head to the Fourth Heaven,” said Yu Rou, who had matured into a graceful, poised woman over the past few years, her presence now marked by a calm, naturally aloof aura that set her apart. seaʀᴄh thё NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

However, there was a faint weariness in her face—subtle, but telling—like someone who hadn’t been sleeping well for some time.

Hanging by her hips was a beautiful fan. However, if one looked closely, it was only a Spirit-grade treasure.

“Um… have you found out anything about my brother?” Yu Rou proceeded to ask, her voice laced with worry.

It was always the first question she asked whenever they met, ever since she’d learned that Yuan had returned to Earth and reclaimed his body.

“…”

Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang remained silent.

Seeing this, Yu Rou lowered her head in a dejected manner and sighed, “The same as usual, huh?”

However, as she lowered her head, she suddenly caught sight of a pair of legs standing just behind her, startling her so much that she jumped in surprise.

“Who the hell—”

Yu Rou spun around sharply, but the rest of her words froze in her throat the moment her eyes met the familiar, handsome face standing behind her.

Though Yuan had also matured and grown even more handsome over the years, Yu Rou would have recognized his face anywhere.

“Brother…?” she whispered, her voice quivering as her eyes grew moist.

Yuan greeted with an apologetic smile, “It’s been a while, Yu Rou.”

Happy 2,000th chapter!

chapters.title: Chapter 2001: Yu Rou’S True Feelings
chapters.body:
Chapter 2001: Yu Rou’S True Feelings

Chapter 2001: Yu Rou’s True Feelings

“Brother!”

Unable to contain herself, Yu Rou subconsciously threw herself into his arms, wrapping him in a tight embrace, years of longing and emotion pouring into that single, desperate gesture.

“Y-Yuan?!” Bai Lianhua exclaimed in a shocked tone, her eyes wide with surprise.

Xia Jingyi swallowed nervously, her face slightly red after seeing Yuan’s handsome face.

“I’m sorry it took this long to see you guys again,” Yuan said as he caressed Yu Rou’s back, who was only a head shorter than him now.

“…”

Yu Rou didn’t respond—she simply held him tighter, burying her face into his robes as if afraid he might vanish if she let go.

“You were unable to log off Cultivation Online for several years, right? How is your body right now?” Bai Lianhua asked since it seemed like Yu Rou wasn’t going to speak.

“My body is fine. If I weren’t a cultivator, it would’ve been dangerous.”

“So that is your real body, correct?”

“Correct,” he confirmed.

“Does this mean we won’t be able to see you on Earth again?” Bai Lianhua continued to ask.

“For now, but it’s not like I cannot return to Earth ever again. Unfortunately, I don’t know when that will happen. It could take years if not decades before I get to return safely.”

Yu Rou’s body trembled at such words.

Yuan noticed this and looked down, but he wasn’t sure what to say.

Yu Rou suddenly lifted her face from his chest and looked at him with tears flowing down her face.

“Brother… What if I want to come to the Nine Heavens?” she asked.

“That’s…” Yuan became speechless.

While he was certain the Celestial Emperor would do everything in his power to prevent him from returning to the Nine Heavens if he visited Earth, he wasn’t sure how the Celestial Emperor would respond to others attempting to enter the Nine Heavens.

After a moment of silence, Yuan finally spoke. “That’s not a decision you make on a whim. Choosing this path means giving up the life you’ve known—it’s not something to take lightly.”

“I know that!” she shouted.

“…” Sёarch* The NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

After a brief pause, she continued softly,

“When you first left home with Meixiu, I realized just how much I hated being apart from you. Every day felt like a small ache. But I endured it, because, back then, I could still see you often enough.”

“But after you moved to Dragon Spiral Mountain and I joined the Eternal Lotuses… we’ve barely seen each other at all. Then you couldn’t log out of Cultivation Online… and now it’s been over half a decade since we last saw each other.”

Her voice trembled as she continued, “I can’t do this anymore, Brother. It’s become too much for me to bear. You’re the only family member I have left…”

“Yu Rou…” Yuan didn’t know what to say.

He shifted his gaze to the others, who shook their heads in silence.

After a moment of silence, Yuan sighed and said, “If that’s what you really want, I won’t stop you. However, it’s going to take some time to open the gateway between our worlds again, so you’ll have to wait until then. In the meantime, I’ll try to visit you as much as possible.”

Yu Rou nodded.

“Okay…” she mumbled.

Once Yu Rou had calmed down, they gathered around a table, and Yuan began recounting his experiences over the past few years, sharing the long and winding journey that had kept them apart.

However, aside from Yu Rou, the others struggled to focus, still caught up in the weight of her earlier outburst. None of them had noticed her pain before since she had always carried herself with quiet strength, acting composed and independent.

Even Meixiu, who had always been the closest to Yu Rou, had failed to see the loneliness hiding behind her calm demeanor. It wasn’t until recently, when Yu Rou had finally stopped pretending, that Meixiu began to see her pain.

Once Yuan finished recounting his journey, Yu Rou and the others began recalling theirs.

Bai Lianhua would spend most of her time and effort growing her faction both on Earth and in the Nine Heavens, while Xia Jingyi focused on improving her zither techniques, even joining a music sect for three years.

As for Yu Rou, cultivation had become her only escape from the loneliness she carried. She immersed herself in it for most of her days, using it as a shield against the emptiness, hence why her cultivation had surpassed even Bai Lianhua, being at the 7th level of Spirit Lord. On occasion, she took on monster extermination quests—not for merit or recognition, but simply to vent the emotions she kept buried inside.

“Brother, it’s been a while since you last saw Snowy, right? Do you even remember her?”

“Oh, that tiger cub from back then? I remember.” He nodded.

“She’s grown a lot since then, and she even saved my life many times.”

“Come out, Snowy.”

Yu Rou suddenly called.

In the next moment, a towering figure emerged from Yu Rou’s dantian and appeared at her side. No longer the small, adorable cub from years past, Snowy had grown into a majestic white tiger—massive in size, and with a fierce and commanding presence.

“Wow, you’ve really grown.”

Surprisingly, Snowy’s cultivation was at the peak of Spirit King, an entire realm stronger than her own master.

“…”

However, the moment Snowy laid eyes on Yuan, she instinctively shrank back, her massive form trembling ever so slightly—an involuntary reaction born of instinctual reverence.

“Snowy? There’s no need to be afraid. This is my brother. Don’t you remember?” Yu Rou was quite surprised by Snowy’s reaction. In fact, she’d never seen Snowy acting nervous before, much less afraid.

Seeing this, Yuan smiled and said, “She must be reacting to my bloodline.”

He suddenly retrieved a glass vial that contained his blood and offered it to Yu Rou.

“Feed her this. It’ll help her grow.”

“This… is blood?” Yu Rou looked at the vial with interest and confusion, wondering why Yuan wanted her to feed Snowy blood.

chapters.title: Chapter 2002: Snowy’S Transformation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2002: Snowy’S Transformation

Chapter 2002: Snowy’s Transformation

“Is this blood from a magical beast or something?” Yu Rou asked as she uncorked the vial.

A distinct scent immediately filled the air, one perceptible only to beasts. Snowy’s ears perked up, and her eyes widened with excitement, her instincts awakened by the unmistakable aroma.

Like a beast in heat, Snowy pounced onto Yu Rou, who quickly shouted, “Ah! You’re going to make me drop it!”

However, Snowy didn’t move away. Instead, she leaned in even closer, her tongue poking out eagerly as she pressed her head toward the vial—like someone desperately trying to catch a single drop of water in the desert.

“Okay! Okay! I get it! Here!” Yu Rou said in a flustered tone as she quickly poured the blood onto Snowy’s outstretched tongue.

The moment the blood touched her taste buds, Snowy’s entire body quivered with delight, her pupils dilating as a surge of satisfaction rushed through her.

In the very next moment, Snowy let out a thunderous roar as her aura exploded outward, wild and untamed, shaking the air around her.

Meanwhile, notifications began appearing before Yu Rou.

<Snowy has consumed ??? blood>

<Snowy’s bloodline has started to transform!>

<Progress: 5%>

“Snowy’s bloodline is transforming!” Yu Rou exclaimed after seeing these notifications.

“What?! Really?!” Bai Lianhua and Xia Jingyi were just as surprised to hear this.

“What was that blood you fed her?” Yu Rou turned to look at Yuan for answers.

Yuan smiled and said, “It’s my blood.”

“Huh?” Yu Rou and the others stared at him with wide eyes, seemingly unable to comprehend it.

“My bloodline is quite unique and has the ability to evolve a beast’s bloodline. It’s not guaranteed, but it has a higher chance of working on those with a weaker bloodline.”

“What an incredible ability… as expected of my brother,” Yu Rou murmured with quiet admiration.

She then glanced at Snowy, who had suddenly crouched down, her massive form curling slightly as if she had suddenly drifted off to sleep.

“She’ll be fine. Just give her some time to evolve her bloodline,” Yuan explained.

Yu Rou nodded.

Some time later, Yu Rou spoke in a faint, dispirited voice, “Brother… are you going to leave again soon?”

“No, I’ll be staying at the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect for some time to let my body finish merging some more.”

Yu Rou’s expression immediately brightened, and she asked, “Then, can I spend more time with you?”

“Of course,” he nodded.

They continued chatting for almost an entire day until Bai Lianhua said, “It was very nice seeing you again, Yuan. I’d like to stay here for longer, but I have a lot of work to do at the faction. Let’s meet again soon.”

“S-See you later…” Xia Jingyi spoke in a timid voice.

“Before you go, let me give you something…”

Yuan transferred several techniques to them, bestowing cultivation and martial arts methods to Bai Lianhua, while entrusting Xia Jingyi with a collection of profound zither techniques.

“T-These are Ancient and Mythic-rank techniques!” Bai Lianhua exclaimed in shock after realizing what Yuan had given her.

Xia Jingyi was too shocked to even say anything.

“No need to thank me. Just consider it a gift for taking care of my sister when I couldn’t,” Yuan said.

“You can leave without me. I’ll stay here for a little longer,” Yu Rou said.

“Okay…”

Once Bai Lianhua and Xia Jingyi left, Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang also left a little bit later, leaving Yuan and Yu Rou alone.

“How long are you going to stay?” Yu Rou asked him.

“I’ll stay here until you want to leave,” he said.

“Are you sure? Don’t you have a lot of things to do?”

“Yes, but they can wait for now.” Sёarch* The nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“In that case, I’ll stay here as long as possible!”

Over the next several days, Yu Rou and Yuan spent their time reminiscing about their childhood. Yuan would often play the zither for her, guide her through her cultivation, and pass down several techniques to help her grow stronger.

Time passed quickly, and before they realized, they’d spent almost an entire month inside Senior Bai’s world.

“I’ll leave tomorrow,” Yu Rou said.

Although she wanted to spend more time with Yuan, she knew that she couldn’t keep him there forever.

“Okay.”

Later that night.

“Brother, can I sleep with you tonight?” Yu Rou knocked on his door and asked.

“You’re not a child anymore, you know.”

“So what? I still want to snuggle with you like we used to. I’m coming in!”

Inside his room, Yuan was seated on the floor.

“You’re cultivating?”

“Now that I’ve reached Immortal Ascension, I don’t need to sleep anymore,” Yuan explained. “In fact, I feel far more rested through cultivation than I would from sleeping.”

“Since this is going to be my last day with you, sleep with me!”

She pulled him off the floor and dragged him to the bed.

“You may have grown up, but you’re still a bit spoiled, aren’t you?” Yuan said with a warm smile.

Yu Rou didn’t reply, but a quiet smile spread across her face.

A few quiet minutes passed as they lay side by side on the bed.

Then, in a soft voice, Yu Rou whispered, “Brother…”

“What is it?”

“When do you think I’ll be able to travel to the Nine Heavens?”

“Hard to say. After all, we need someone who can activate the teleportation device on Earth, but the Lord has already sacrificed his cultivation to open it.”

He continued, “Even if you make it to the Nine Heavens, what will you do then?”

“I want to go where you go,” she immediately said.

“That’s too dangerous. There’s no way I can bring you to the places I’ll be going.”

“I don’t need to be exactly by your side. I just want to be near enough so you can visit more conveniently.”

“But you can do that already through the Myriad of Techniques.”

“It doesn’t feel the same…” she sighed. “I want to be with you physically… not in this avatar…”

Eventually, Yu Rou drifted into a deep, peaceful sleep—the kind she hadn’t experienced in years.

chapters.title: Chapter 2003: Meeting Li Jinxi
chapters.body:
Chapter 2003: Meeting Li Jinxi

Chapter 2003: Meeting Li Jinxi

The following morning, Yu Rou slowly opened her eyes.

“Brother…” she whispered, a warm smile blossoming on her face as she gazed at Yuan, peacefully asleep beside her.

A sudden impulse stirred within her, and after a brief moment of hesitation, she leaned in and pressed a soft, gentle kiss on his lips.

Yuan opened his eyes a few moments later.

“Good morning, brother.” Yu Rou acted normally, but her face was flushed with redness.

“Morning.”

Yuan sat up, pretending like he didn’t notice the kiss.

“I’ll make breakfast.” Yu Rou said as she got off the bed and left the room.

After she left, Yuan released a deep sigh. With his experience, there was no way he hadn’t noticed it—Yu Rou’s feelings for him went beyond simple sibling affection. While it might appear as familial love on the surface, he could clearly sense the deeper emotions she tried to hide.

Even if they weren’t blood-related, they had grown up together as siblings, and that bond carried weight. Yuan wasn’t sure how he would respond if Yu Rou ever chose to confess her feelings.

Would he be able to accept her… or would that line between them remain one he couldn’t cross?

Some time later, after eating breakfast, they made their way to the exit.

“Brother… do you think I’ll be able to see you again soon?” she asked.

He nodded and said, “I’ll try to meet you again before I return to my journey.”

“Okay. It’s a promise.”

Yu Rou suddenly embraced him tightly.

“Take this before you go.” Yuan retrieved a spatial ring and offered it to her.

“What’s this?” she asked.

“There are a few treasures inside that will help you if you ever get yourself into trouble that you cannot handle.”

“Thank you…”

Once Yu Rou left, Yuan also left the Myriad of Techniques and returned to the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect.

“Wow, I didn’t think you’d spend an entire month with Yu Rou.” Chu Liuxiang remarked when he returned.

“A month is nothing if you consider how long we’ve been apart,” he said.

“That’s true.”

“What are you going to do now?” Meixiu asked.

“I’ll stay at this sect for a little longer. Also, there’s still someone I need to talk to…” Searᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Oh, you mean Jinxi?” Chu Liuxiang said.

He nodded and said, “She’s definitely waiting for me.”

“Good luck,” Meixiu said.

Yuan left to find Li Jinxi shortly afterward.

After asking around, he made his way to the training grounds, but not the one reserved exclusively for the Sect Leader. Instead, he headed to the grounds designated for sect elders, where Li Jinxi was known to train. She often sparred with the elders, as none of the regular disciples were strong enough to challenge her.

When he arrived, Li Jinxi was in the middle of a sparring match with a sect elder who was at the Bronze Immortal stage while only being at the peak of Divine Ancestor herself. Moreover, though faint, Li Jinxi was unmistakably using Immortal Qi.

“No matter how many times I witness it, it still amazes me how she can wield Immortal Qi at her current cultivation level. She’s a true prodigy.”

“I heard she had just stepped into the Divine Realm when she joined the sect around five years ago. If she’d arrived a few years earlier, she might already be an Immortal by now.”

“Her combat experience is frighteningly high for someone her age. I heard she’s not even a hundred years old, yet she fights like someone who’s lived through millennia of battle.”

Yuan could hear the murmurs of the sect elders watching from the sidelines, their voices laced with awe and deep respect for Li Jinxi’s talent and rapid rise.

Eventually, the sparring ended in a draw.

“As amazing as always, Lady Ji.” The sect elder clasped his hands together and gave her a polite bow.

“Thank you for your time.” Ji Linxi returned the bow.

“No, the pleasure is mine. It’s an honor to exchange swords with a genius like you. By the way, my grandson, who’s a Core Disciple here, asked me to—”

The sect elder abruptly stopped mid-sentence, his gaze shifting as he noticed Li Jinxi had turned her head, her attention clearly fixed elsewhere.

Everyone quickly noticed her sudden distraction and turned to see what had caught her attention, only to find an unfamiliar, strikingly handsome man standing there, someone none of them recognized.

“I didn’t think you’d actually come see me,” Li Jinxi suddenly said.

“Why wouldn’t I?”

“You visited everyone but me, and that was a month ago.”

“That’s… I was going to visit you sooner, but I ended up staying with Yu Rou for longer than anticipated…”

“Relax, I’m aware. I was just joking with you.”

Yuan was speechless. Li Jinxi wasn’t the type to joke around.

‘How much has she changed since we last met?’ he wondered inwardly.

“Do you remember our arrangements?” Li Jinxi suddenly asked.

“Of course. I’ll take you on anytime.”

“Then meet me at the place you sparred with Senior Kelan a month ago tomorrow,” she said before flying away.

“…”

Yuan could only stand there with a dazed look on his face.

“Excuse me, but who are you?” One of the sect elders suddenly asked, snapping him out of his daze.

“I’m just a guest.”

After saying that, Yuan activated Shadow Veil and disappeared before them like a ghost.

The following day, Yuan made his way to the white space training ground. Not seeing Li Jinxi outside, he decided to head inside to check if she was already there.

Sure enough, once inside, he could see Li Jinxi standing not far away with the Golden Empress already in her grasp.

“Before we begin, I want to make one thing clear—fight me with everything you have from the very start,” Li Jinxi said calmly, her gaze locked with Yuan’s. “If you hold back or look down on me… I won’t forgive you.”

Yuan gave a silent nod in response, his expression unreadable.

chapters.title: Chapter 2004: Li Jinxi’S True Feelings
chapters.body:
Chapter 2004: Li Jinxi’S True Feelings

Chapter 2004: Li Jinxi’s True Feelings

“Come at me whenever you’re ready.” Li Jinxi said as she unleashed her full cultivation and as much Immortal Qi as she could muster without going out of control.

However, despite her seemingly confident expression, Li Jinxi already knew that she wouldn’t be able to defeat Yuan, who could fight on par with God Ascension cultivators.

In response, Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and activated God of War’s Astral Arts from the get-go.

“Here I come,” Yuan warned her before speeding toward her.

Li Jinxi raised her sword high and swung with all her might, unleashing a powerful technique that made the very air around them tremble with its force.

Yet with a single, effortless motion, Yuan swung his sword, shattering her technique completely, as though it were no more than a breeze before a storm.

Li Jinxi gritted her teeth and unleashed one technique after another in rapid succession, each strike fueled by relentless determination.

But Yuan didn’t even bother to defend. He continued to approach her, allowing every attack to land directly on his body, unflinching, as if her efforts couldn’t even reach him.

“Damn it!”

Li Jinxi roared as she unleashed her strongest technique.

[Golden Empress’ Absolute Decree!]

Suddenly, a golden energy surged around the Golden Empress in Li Jinxi’s grasp, radiating a powerful pressure that rippled through the air.

Had she unleashed this technique during her spar with the sect elder the day before, there was no doubt she would have claimed victory with ease.

However, Li Jinxi didn’t stop there and infused the energy with Immortal Qi and Enhanced Sword Aura.

Seeing this, Yuan tightened his grip on his sword, his eyes narrowing as he prepared to meet her strike head-on.

In the next moment, Yuan swung his sword, channeling Immortal Qi through the blade and layering it with Enhanced Sword Aura.

A split second later, their swords collided, unleashing a shockwave so powerful it sent ripples through the air and carved fractures into the very fabric of space around them.

Li Jinxi had expected to hold her ground for at least a few seconds, but the instant their swords collided, the overwhelming force behind Yuan’s strike shattered her balance. Before she could react, her body was hurled through the air, utterly powerless to resist.

Li Jinxi coughed up a mouthful of blood, her body trembling as she struggled to rise to her feet.

’So I couldn’t even withstand a single strike from him, huh?’

Li Jinxi let out a quiet breath and stopped trying to stand. Instead, she remained on the ground, her gaze drifting upward as she stared into the endless white space, her expression dazed.

Seeing this, Yuan withdrew his sword and approached her.

“Are you okay?” he asked gently.

“No, I’m not,” she replied without hesitation. “No matter how hard I try, no matter how much effort I put in… I still can’t seem to catch up to you.”

“Why are you so hung up on catching up? It’s not like I will leave you behind or anything,” Yuan said. “In fact, you’re soaring when compared to the others.”

She glared at him and said, “How else am I supposed to get you to accept me?”

“What?” Yuan raised his eyebrows, puzzled by her words.

“If it isn’t obvious by now, I have regained my memories as Jin Xi,” she said. “And with them came everything she carried, such as her experiences… and her feelings for you.”

“I’m well aware of that… but those memories belong to your previous life.”

“So what?” she snapped, her voice trembling with frustration. “Are you saying I should just cast aside everything I felt and experienced back then? Even if that life ended and we’ve been reborn, we’re still the same soul—still the same people.”

She took a breath, her eyes locked onto his.

“What about Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang? You accepted them in this life. So why can’t you accept me?”

Yuan let out a quiet sigh and said, “While it’s true that my feelings for them have strengthened after regaining my memories, I’ve known and loved them long before my memories returned. Their feelings for me… they were already there, even before they began recalling anything from their past lives.”

He paused for a moment, his gaze steady as he continued.

“But you…”

He stopped there—not because he didn’t want to continue, but because he couldn’t. Yuan didn’t know Li Jinxi’s feelings well enough to say more.

When they had first met, she had seemed like nothing more than a battle fanatic. Their relationship had never felt like anything deeper than companionship, similar to how he viewed the other members of the Demon Sealing Faction, though perhaps… just slightly more.

If Li Jinxi’s feelings for him had only awakened after regaining Jin Xi’s memories, then to Yuan, it felt less like love born naturally, and more like something inherited, even forced, and he wasn’t sure how to respond to it.

Li Jinxi quickly understood what he was trying to say.

“So you think I only feel this way because of my past life?” she asked, her voice tight with emotion.

She clenched her fists before shouting, “That’s not true! I’ve had these feelings for you long before my memories as Jin Xi returned! I just… I’ve never been good with words—or expressing my feelings.”

Her voice softened, and her face flushed with redness as she continued.

“Yes, Jin Xi’s memories helped me understand my emotions better, and they strengthened what I already felt—but don’t think for a second that I didn’t already love you before then…”

Li Jinxi’s mind drifted back to their first encounter on the stage at the Jaded Garden. Searᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

At the time, she hadn’t recognized it for what it was, but she eventually realized that she had started falling for him in he middle of that very battle, and that feeling would only continue to grow as they continued to spar and spend more time with each other.

Unfortunately, fighting was the only thing she excelled at. To others, it may have seemed like she viewed Yuan as nothing more than a rival, someone to challenge and chase. But in truth, it had always been more than that, and she just never knew how to show it.

chapters.title: Chapter 2005: A New Addition To The Family
chapters.body:
Chapter 2005: A New Addition To The Family

Chapter 2005: A New Addition to the Family

After listening to Li Jinxi’s confession, Yuan smiled and said, “I’m flattered you feel that way about me, but I wasn’t doubting your feelings.”

He continued, “After all, even though I have reincarnated many times, the feelings my incarnations experienced and their emotions are still mine, but not everyone will think like me. I just wanted to make sure that was what you really wanted.”

“I am certain that’s what I want,” she responded with a serious face.

“I see…”

“So…” Li Jinxi sat up and stared at him intently, clearly waiting for his response.

Yuan extended his hands toward her and smiled, “I was unable to answer Jin Xi’s feelings because I lacked the courage before, and I lost her before I could. This time, however, I will not make the same mistake.”

Li Jinxi gently took his hand, her voice barely above a whisper.

“Does that mean—”

Before she could finish, Yuan pulled her to her feet—and straight into his arms. Without a word, he leaned in and pressed a soft, tender kiss to her lips.

Li Jinxi’s eyes widened with surprise, but she quickly closed her eyes to enjoy the moment.

’Ah… so this is what it feels like to be finally accepted…’

Jin Xi had spent thousands of years chasing after Tian Xin, only to meet a lonely, unfulfilled end, so one could only imagine the fulfillment Li Jinxi, who had her experience and emotions, was feeling at this moment.

“Let’s go back to my place…” Li Jinxi said afterward.

She wanted to embrace him on the spot, but she was afraid that someone else might enter the training ground and witness it.

Yuan nodded, and the two of them left the training ground the next moment.

Once they arrived at her living quarters, Li Jinxi brought Yuan directly to the bedroom, where she immediately removed her robes, revealing her defined and flawlessly toned body.

“W-What do you think…?” she asked, her face flushed with redness.

“It’s beautiful.” Yuan smiled before kissing her all over her body.

Once Yuan was finished, Li Jinxi swapped roles and began licking all of his body.

Many moments later, Yuan inserted his sword into her scabbard, connecting their bodies together.

The room soon echoed with Li Jinxi’s blissful cries, a sound so pure and unrestrained, unlike anything she had ever made before.

Time slipped by unnoticed as they lost themselves in waves of pleasure, the outside world forgotten. Before they realized it, days had passed.

Though it was her first experience, Li Jinxi’s monstrous endurance allowed her to cultivate with him for days without rest.

“It’s a pity that my first experience is through my avatar…” Li Jinxi sighed.

“Does it really matter? It feels no different from your real body,” Yuan commented.

“No, there is a difference.”

“…”

Yuan couldn’t refute her words. Once a cultivator reached a certain level, the subtle flaws in an avatar became apparent. Though it might feel identical to a real body at first, there were nuanced differences.

After all, no matter how powerful a formation might be, it was impossible to perfectly replicate one’s physical body using only the soul.

“What are you going to do now?” Li Jinxi asked him.

“I’ll continue to stay here until I have a solid plan.”

“I see… By the way…” Li Jinxi paused, a hint of nervousness creeping into her voice. “Which of us is going to tell Meixiu and Liuxiang about this…?”

Caught up in the moment, she had completely forgotten that Yuan already had two official partners. And now, she had crossed that line with him… without ever speaking to them first. Sёarch* The nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan smiled and said, “We can do it together.”

“Do you think they’ll be angry…?” she asked.

“It’s not impossible, but it’s unlikely, since they’re just like you.”

Li Jinxi felt slightly better and silently nodded, but she wasn’t completely relieved.

Some time later, Yuan and Li Jinxi met up with Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang.

“Um… this might be sudden, but I’ve decided to accept Li Jinxi into the family,” Yuan said, getting straight to the point without hesitation.

“…”

Chu Liuxiang and Meixiu didn’t respond immediately. Instead, they turned to face each other and exchanged silent glances, their expressions unreadable.

“Sorry, I know I should’ve come to you two first for permission, but I—”

Just as Li Jinxi opened her mouth to speak, Chu Liuxiang smiled and said, “Why do you need our permission? It’s not like we own him. If he accepted you, then that’s that.”

Meixiu nodded and added, “We have long accepted the fact that he’ll add more people into the family. Honestly, I’m a little surprised it took this long.”

Chu Liuxiang chuckled and continued, “Right? I was expecting you to join the family long ago.”

Li Jinxi was speechless. She never thought they’d be so calm and accepting. However, now that she thought about it, the two of them wouldn’t have been able to co-exist with each other if they couldn’t accept Yuan having multiple partners.

“Anyway, welcome to the family, Jinxi,” Meixiu said.

“Thank you…”Li Jinxi didn’t know what else to say.

“So, who else do you plan on adding to the family?” Chu Liuxiang turned to look at Yuan.

He responded with a weird smile, “Don’t make it sound like I have a list of potential partners. I don’t have anything planned.”

“Considering how many lives you’ve lived, I wouldn’t be surprised if we have a few more additions to the family soon,” she chuckled.

After chatting for a while, Li Jinxi and Chu Liuxiang eventually returned to their own living quarters, leaving Yuan to spend the week with Meixiu.

The following week, he devoted his time to Chu Liuxiang, and then to Li Jinxi the week after.

In the blink of an eye, two months had passed, and it was once again time to visit Yu Rou and the others at the Myriad of Techniques.

However, upon their arrival, an unexpected figure appeared.

“Yuan? You’re back?”

It was none other than Senior Bai.

chapters.title: Chapter 2006: Faceless Sword Emperor
chapters.body:
Chapter 2006: Faceless Sword Emperor

Chapter 2006: Faceless Sword Emperor

“It’s been a while, Senior Bai.” Yuan greeted him with a warm smile. Sёarch* The ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Indeed, it has been a while. Last I heard from you, you were still in the Sixth Heaven. Where are you right now?” Senior Bai asked.

“I’m currently in the Seventh Heaven, and I’ve been staying at Kelan’s sect for the last several months,” he said.

“What?” Senior Bai frowned after hearing this. “You’ve been at Kelan’s place for several months already? How come I didn’t hear anything about this from him?”

“Oh, he went into seclusion shortly after I showed up.”

Senior Bai rubbed his eyes and sighed, “That meathead… he should’ve at least notified me about your return before he went into seclusion.”

Yuan chuckled before asking, “How have you been?”

“Same as usual, but I’ve been spending more time at my sect,” he said.

“Your sect… that would be the Gates of Heaven, correct?” Yuan recalled participating in a trial that required him to fight a disciple from this sect.

“Yes, that’s correct. Whenever you have the time, you should visit. There’s one within the Seventh Heaven, as well.”

“As well? There’s more than one Gates of Heaven?” Chu Liuxiang asked.

Senior Bai nodded and explained, “I have three sects, one in the Fifth Heaven, the Seventh Heaven, and the Ninth Heaven. Of course, they all share the same name and are essentially the same sect.”

“What is the purpose of having three sects?” Bai Lianhua asked out of curiosity.

“It’s quite pointless to have multiple sects if you place them all in the same realm. However, if you spread them apart, you’ll be able to gain many more disciples. Also, it gives those in the lower heavens another method of ascending.”

“I see…” Bai Lianhua muttered, as she’d been contemplating branching out her faction recently.

Senior Bai turned to look at Yuan and continued, “The next Sword Emperor Qualifications will be happening soon, and it’ll take place within the Gates of Heaven. You use the sword, right? You might learn a thing or two if you go.”

“Sword Emperor Qualifications? What is that?” Li Jinxi asked, her curiosity piqued.

Senior Bai then explained, “In the world of swordmasters, the highest attainable title is the Sword Emperor, much like how blacksmiths have the title God of Creation, and alchemists have the God of Alchemy. The Sword Emperor is, in essence, the God of Swords. However, there can be multiple Sword Emperors.”

“The Sword Emperor Qualification is exactly what it sounds like—an event where swordmasters compete for the chance to earn the title of Sword Emperor.”

“Sword Emperor…” Yuan muttered.

Li Jinxi glanced over at Yuan. In Jin Xi’s memories, Tian Xin also acquired the title of Sword Emperor. In fact, he was the strongest Sword Emperor in history.

“How does one join this event?” Yuan suddenly asked.

Senior Bai smiled and said, “Are you looking to join? Unfortunately, even with your talents, it’d be impossible for you to join in your current state. Only those who have achieved Supreme Sword Aura may join, after all.”

“Supreme Sword Aura, huh? Indeed, I can’t use that right now. However, if I have enough time…” Yuan showed a confident smile. “When is the event happening?”

“It’s going to happen in seven years.”

“Seven years?” Yuan raised his eyebrows. “I thought you said it’d be soon. That’s quite far away.”

“Seven years is nothing for Immortals like me.”

“I guess that’s true. Anyway, seven years should be more than enough for me to master Supreme Sword Aura,” Yuan said casually.

Senior Bai stared at him, utterly dumbfounded. He couldn’t believe what he was hearing. Most cultivators spent their entire lives unable to even grasp Enhanced Sword Aura, let alone Supreme Sword Aura. And the participants in the Sword Emperor Qualification? Nearly all of them would be God Ascension realm experts who had lived for tens of thousands of years.

“Well, if you’re truly serious about mastering Supreme Sword Aura,” Senior Bai said thoughtfully, “then you should visit the Holy Sword Temple. It’s a unique place that exists beyond the bounds of the Nine Heavens, and nearly every Sword Emperor throughout history has gone there to learn their Supreme Sword Aura. In fact, the strongest Sword Emperor in history, the Faceless Sword Emperor, also trained there.”

“Faceless Sword Emperor…” Yuan raised his eyebrows at this title.

Senior Bai mistook Yuan’s reaction as intrigue and explained, “The Faceless Sword Emperor earned his title because he always wore a mask and almost nobody knew his true identity. He had other titles such as the Undefeated Sword Emperor and the Mystical Sword Emperor. He’s one of the most revered figures in the world of swordmasters.”

Li Jinxi couldn’t help but smile after hearing this. The Faceless Sword Emperor was, without doubt, Tian Xin.

Senior Bai continued, “No one knows where the Faceless Sword Emperor came from—only that from the very beginning of his recorded history, he lived as an undefeated prodigy. However, just as mysterious as his rise was his disappearance. After attaining the title of Sword Emperor, he vanished without a trace, almost as if he had never existed at all.”

He lifted his head to look at the clear blue sky and sighed, “To tell you the truth, I’ve encountered him once. However, he was just passing by, and I was only a snooty little brat back then. Though I was a mortal, I could tell how formidable he was at a glance. He was a true monster—one that might rival even you, Yuan.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Alright, I’ve decided. I’ll visit the Holy Sword Temple next.”

Of course, it wasn’t solely because he wanted to participate in the Sword Emperor Qualifications. If he wanted to fight and kill God Ascension realm cultivators, he’d need to learn Supreme Sword Aura eventually.

“I should’ve mentioned this first, but not just anyone can enter the Holy Sword Temple. You’ll need a referral and then pass a test before you can train there,” Senior Bai suddenly said.

chapters.title: Chapter 2007: The Great One’S Appearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2007: The Great One’S Appearance

Chapter 2007: The Great One’s Appearance

“Referral? From whom?” Yuan asked.

“Only Sword Emperors and reputable swordmasters are allowed to give out referrals. Fortunately, we have someone in the faction who can give you a referral. I’ll contact them after this.”

“Okay. What about the test?”

“Oh, it’s quite simple. Your Enhanced Sword Aura just needs to be powerful enough to damage the Sword Wall at the Holy Sword Temple.”

“I see. Should be doable.”

“By the way, what is your cultivation at right—” Senior Bai stopped his sentence midway when Yuan exuded some of his cultivation, as he’d been suppressing it.

“Peak of Immortal Ascension?!” Senior Bai almost couldn’t believe his eyes. The last time they met, Yuan was still in the Spirit Realm.

’His cultivation increased by an entire Major Realm in less than a decade?! At this rate, he’ll become an Immortal by our next meeting!’

“Is my cultivation not enough?” Yuan asked.

“No, there shouldn’t be any problems as long as you can use Enhanced Sword Aura.” S~eaʀᴄh the NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan nodded.

“Then there’s no problem.”

He showed off his Enhanced Sword Aura, which had reached its maximum mastery long ago.

“Even in the Ninth Heaven, only a handful of prodigies have ever managed to attain Enhanced Sword Aura before reaching the Immortal Realm,” Senior Bai said, swallowing nervously. “But none of them could hold a candle to your level.”

“That’s only natural,” Chu Liuxiang remarked in a prideful tone, acting like she was the one being praised.

“By the way… about what happened at the Dragon Spiral Mountain…” Senior Bai suddenly brought up the topic, his tone more serious now. It was an incident witnessed by the Celestial Overlords, after all.

“Are you okay? Do you know why the Heaven’s Mandate and the Celestial Emperor showed up, even causing such a major scene?”

Though Yuan trusted Senior Bai deeply enough to entrust his life to him, he chose not to reveal everything. He didn’t want to drag him into such a dangerous matter, especially with the Celestial Overlord’s situation.

“I don’t know why, but I think the Celestial Emperor doesn’t want me in the Nine Heavens,” he said while shaking his head.

Senior Bai became silent. He was aware of the Celestial Emperor’s Curse, and while he never figured out why the Celestial Emperor would target Yuan, he knew there was something between them.

“If you ever find yourself in a situation that you cannot handle alone, don’t hesitate to rely on us—on the Celestial Overlords. Regardless of your rank, you’re still one of us, so as long as you didn’t commit any crimes, we’ll back you with our full power.”

Senior Bai could tell that Yuan wasn’t revealing everything, but he didn’t press. He trusted Yuan—and believed that, when the time was right, he would share the truth on his own terms.

Yuan offered a grateful smile and said, “I’ll keep that in mind. Thank you.”

“Anyway, I won’t intrude on your time any longer. I’ll leave now to speak with that individual who will give you a referral.”

With that, Senior Bai left shortly after.

“Looks like you’ll be busy again soon, brother.” Yu Rou sighed.

“Senior Bai didn’t mention how long it’ll take for him to get that referral, so I might still have some time left to spend with you,” he said.

Not wanting to waste any time, they began chatting with each other, talking about their recent experiences and future plans.

Eventually, everyone but Yu Rou and Yuan left. The siblings spent the next week with each other like their last meeting.

“If I don’t show up for the next meeting, that means I have already left.”

“Don’t worry, I’ll be okay… even if I’ll be a little lonely,” Yu Rou said softly as she embraced him. “Now that you’ve regained your body, the thing I worried about most is finally behind us.”

Meanwhile, after he left, Senior Bai reported to Xu Jiaqi about Yuan’s return and Kelan’s blunder.

“I see… so he’s regained his true body and traveled to the Nine Heavens with it…” Xu Jiaqi muttered to herself after hearing the report, a sense of relief washing over her.

“By the way, did you ask him about what happened at the Dragon Spiral Mountain?” she suddenly asked.

“Yes, but he didn’t say much. However, he’s definitely hiding something, though.”

Xu Jiaqi sighed, “It’s fine. He probably doesn’t want to cause us any trouble. My father was also like that. He’d always try to solve everything on his own.”

“Now then, about the Holy Sword Temple… I’ll contact that person myself,” she continued.

Senior Bai smiled and said, “That’s relieving to hear. I don’t want to deal with that eccentric person.”

“I know,” she calmly remarked.

Some time later, after finishing her current work, Xu Jiaqi left to find this eccentric person to give Yuan the referral.

Meanwhile, at the Azure Dragon Clan, just as peace had become the norm, an unfathomable presence suddenly descended out of nowhere, blanketing their entire territory in a suffocating pressure.

Upon sensing the overwhelming presence, everyone in the city froze in terror. Not a single soul dared to move, let alone glance around in search of the source, as immense fear gripped their hearts.

Jiao Zhenhai and the others immediately stopped their cultivation and rushed out of their residences, moving toward the source of the presence, which was rapidly closing in on them.

However, despite the audacity with which this individual had unleashed their presence across the Azure Dragon Clan’s territory, not a single member felt offended, nor did they approach with anger. Instead, they felt a deep sense of humility, as if standing in the presence of something far beyond themselves.

Moments later, Jiao Zhenhai and the other clan members came face to face with the individual. He stood half-naked, his upper torso exposed, revealing a physique so flawless it was as though heaven itself had craved it. His long, golden hair flowed behind him with unnatural grace, swaying gently as if caught in a soft breeze, even though they were all submerged in water.

“The Azure Dragon Clan humbly greets the Great One!” Jiao Zhenhai and the others called out in unison, their heads lowered in deep submission and reverence, their voices echoing with solemn respect.

chapters.title: Chapter 2008: The Great One’S Appearance(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2008: The Great One’S Appearance(2)

Chapter 2008: The Great One’s Appearance(2)

Despite the Great One’s seemingly sudden appearance, the Azure Dragon Clan was not surprised at all. In fact, they’d anticipated his appearance when they learned that it was Dragon Goddess Yeyou who had stopped the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan’s invasion from Yuan.

The Great One narrowed his golden eyes, his naturally arrogant gaze sweeping over the Azure Dragon Clan like he had absolute authority over them.

“So, you’ve anticipated my arrival, have you?” he said, his voice calm yet imposing. “Then you must also know why I’m here. Let’s not waste time and tell me what I want to know. Is it true that Dragon Goddess Yeyou appeared here recently?”

“Yes, that is correct.” Jiao Zhenhai didn’t dare to lie before the Great One since he could see through everything.

“Tell me everything, and do it from the very beginning. If you dare try to mislead me or leave even the slightest details out, I’ll completely wipe your bloodline from existence. Do I make myself clear?”

The Great One’s aura surged in a threatening manner.

Jiao Zhenhai and the others trembled beneath the weight of the Great One’s overwhelming presence. Knowing full well that this being did not bluff or tolerate deceit, Zhenhai quickly recalled everything—from the moment their family had been poisoned by the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan, to their fateful encounter with Yuan, and ultimately, the appearance of Dragon Goddess Yeyou herself, which ended up driving the Mystic Sea Dragon Clan away.

“Yuan…” The Great One narrowed his eyes after hearing this familiar name.

However, no matter how vast his experience or knowledge, there was simply no way he could have linked Yuan’s existence to the Immortal Monarch—even with all the information he had been given.

Even with the information that Yuan possessed the Immortal Monarch’s blood, he assumed it had been bestowed upon him by Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

’I need more information.’

The Great One set his sights on the Holy Dragon Clan next.

Before leaving the Azure Dragon Clan, the Great One said, “Since you’ve experienced Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s prowess first-hand, I want you to do some quick comparison.”

Without warning, he suddenly unleashed a surge of his aura, so immense that it didn’t just engulf the Azure Dragon Clan’s territory, but swept across the entire Sea of Dragons like a tidal wave.

The sudden eruption of aura startled every living entity within the sea, sending beasts and cultivators alike fleeing from his direction as if their very lives depended on it.

Within the Azure Dragon Clan, the pressure was unbearable and so overwhelming that even the strongest among them coughed up mouthfuls of blood. Not even a God Ascension realm cultivator like Jiao Zhenhai was spared.

Fortunately, the Great One quickly retracted his aura a second later, or everyone around him would’ve died from the pressure alone.

“If you were to compare my aura to that of Dragon Goddess Yeyou,” the Great One asked, his voice devoid of guilt or apology, “how did I measure up? Would you say I’m stronger?”

After taking a moment to catch his breath, Jiao Zhenahi responded, “Your aura was definitely stronger than what I felt from Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

A satisfied smile tugged at the corners of the Great One’s handsome face. Without another word, he turned and soared away—his figure cutting through the water like it didn’t exist as he headed straight toward the Holy Dragon Clan.

“…”

A heavy silence lingered over the Azure Dragon Clan and the Underwater City, persisting long after the Great One had departed.

Eventually, Jiao Zhenhai let out a deep, weary sigh. “I have the feeling that even if we had succeeded in claiming the Dragon Spiral Mountain, the Great One still wouldn’t have offered us his help.”

The others silently nodded, agreeing with him.

A few days later, the Great One arrogantly announced his arrival by engulfing the entire Dragon Spiral Mountain in his overwhelming presence, just as he did to the Azure Dragon Clan.

Immediately, the Holy Dragon Emperor approached him and greeted, “The Holy Dragon Clan greets the Great One.”

The Great One narrowed his eyes and remarked, “Just like the Azure Dragon Clan, you anticipated my appearance. Did Dragon Goddess Yeyou warn you that I’d show up?”

“No, but knowing your history with her, anyone would make this assumption,” he said.

“Then you must also know why I am here.”

“Yes. It’s true that Dragon Goddess Yeyou has returned.” The Holy Dragon Emperor admitted.

Based on the Great One’s earlier comment, the Holy Dragon Emperor knew that he had visited the Azure Dragon Clan prior to showing up at the Holy Dragon Clan, so there was no reason to hide it, and it’d only enrage him.

“Tell me everything you know,” said the Great One, his overbearing tone leaving no room for discussion.

However, the Holy Dragon Emperor shook his head and said, “Unfortunately, that is not possible.”

The Great One narrowed his eyes ever so slightly, fixing the Holy Dragon Emperor with a gaze that radiated silent menace.

“Since I am in a good mood, I’ll pretend I didn’t hear your last sentence. You better choose your next words wisely, or I might sink the Dragon Spiral Mountain by accident,” said the Great One as his aura began to surge violently.

“Who knows? It might even summon Dragon Goddess Yeyou once she learns of it.”

“Why are you still chasing after Dragon Goddess Yeyou? I heard she wants nothing to do with you. Aren’t you afraid of incurring her wrath?” Sёarch* The Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I might have feared her in the past, but things are different now. I don’t know where she has been this entire time, but I am quite certain that I have surpassed her during her absence. Once I find her, I am going to battle and defeat her.”

In the past, when the Great One pursued Dragon Goddess Yeyou, she would always say that she’d only accept someone stronger than herself, hence why he’d started collecting treasures and using them to strengthen himself.

chapters.title: Chapter 2009: The Great One’S Appearance(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2009: The Great One’S Appearance(3)

Chapter 2009: The Great One’s Appearance(3)

The Holy Dragon Emperor could only sigh inwardly after hearing the Great One’s declaration—that he’d defeat Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

While it was true that Dragon Goddess Yeyou may not have been improving her strength since her disappearance because she’d lost her body, and her soul had split into several parts, nobody could say for certain that the Great One had surpassed her, but it was definitely a possibility.

“Once I defeat her, I’ll finally obtain the one thing I’ve yearned but failed to claim in this life,” the Great One declared, his eyes burning with fervent determination.

The Holy Dragon Emperor narrowed his eyes at the Great One’s words, which carried an unsettling undertone, as if he saw Dragon Goddess Yeyou not as a person, but as something to be claimed.

“In any case, I cannot help you. They didn’t mention where they’d go after leaving the Dragon Spiral Mountain.” seaʀᴄh thё NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The smile on the Great One’s face slowly disappeared, and he responded, “Maybe, but I won’t know for sure until I force it out of you. How many do I have to kill to make you talk? Half?”

The Holy Dragon Emperor sneered in disdain, “You may have gotten stronger over the years, but your character has fallen quite lowly, Great One. Even if you kill everyone, my answer will remain the same. I don’t know where Dragon Goddess Yeyou went.”

The Great One said nothing in return, but his aura surged violently, speaking louder than any words ever could.

The Holy Dragon Emperor wasted no time, instantly transforming into his dragon form. Though he knew full well that the Great One stood as an even greater threat than the Celestial Emperor had been, he refused to retreat. Defeat was likely, but cowering was never an option.

“It’s been a while since a dragon has dared to defy me, so I’ll enjoy this for a while.”

Moments later, the Great One and the Holy Dragon Emperor collided in a thunderous clash, their fierce exchanges unleashing shockwaves in every direction, each impact chipping away at the Dragon Spiral Mountain, piece by piece.

“Heavens! The Great One is attacking us!”

“W-What should we do?!”

The Holy Dragon Clan was thrown into momentary panic, but their hesitation didn’t last. Just like when the Celestial Emperor attacked, they surged forward to assist him, even if their efforts barely made a dent in the overwhelming power of the Great One.

Though the Great One’s individual power surpassed even that of the Celestial Emperor, the Holy Dragon Clan managed to hold out against him far longer than expected. Of course, this endurance was only possible because the Great One was clearly holding back.

If the Great One had gone all out from the beginning, the Holy Dragon Clan would’ve perished almost instantly.

Eventually, the Great One hovered alone above the Dragon Spiral Mountain, his presence dominating the skies. Below him, the Holy Dragon Emperor and the rest of the clan lay scattered across the battered landscape, defeated and motionless, unable to lift even a finger after their overwhelming defeat.

Their fight had attracted many spectators again, though not as many as when the Celestial Emperor attacked.

“What the hell? Isn’t that the Great One? Why is he attacking the Holy Dragon Clan, too?”

“I don’t know what the Holy Dragon Clan did, but to offend both the Celestial Emperor and the Great One… they’re fated to be erased from existence soon.”

“And since it’s the Great One, the other Royal Dragon Clans wouldn’t be able to do anything about it.”

Some time later, the Great One’s voice echoed across the battered peaks, loud and clear, rippling through the mountain like thunder, “Though I do not wish to do this because of my respect for Dragon Goddess Yeyou, your defiance cannot go unpunished. As retribution, I shall destroy the final Dragon Spiral Mountain in the Nine Heavens.”

The Great One raised a single finger, and in an instant, an overwhelming aura surged from his body. At the tip of his finger, a small orb of condensed energy began to form, glowing intensely. It rapidly expanded, growing larger and brighter until it blazed in the sky like a miniature sun, radiating power that shook the heavens.

Moments later, the Great One gave a casual flick of his finger, and the blazing miniature sun shot through the air toward the mountain. As it descended, the heat radiating from it was so intense that it caused the surrounding sea to boil violently, sending waves of steam rising into the heavens.

The Holy Dragon Clan could do nothing but watch in helpless silence as the blazing sun descended upon them. One by one, they began to close their eyes—not out of fear, but in solemn acceptance of the death they could no longer escape.
