However, amid the chaos and impending doom, a lone figure silently appeared between the descending sun and the Dragon Spiral Mountain like a ghost.

He was small in stature, appearing no older than a young teenager, yet his eyes gleamed with ancient knowledge and unfathomable experience. Strangely, he emitted no presence whatsoever, and his body was semi-transparent, as if he didn’t exist within the fabric of reality itself.

Though the Holy Dragon Clan failed to notice him because of his lack of presence, the Great One did not.

The moment his eyes fell upon the small figure, his body tensed, and a flicker of something rare flashed in his eyes: hesitation. Without a word, he clenched his fists, and in an instant, the blazing sun above dissipated, abruptly canceling his attack.

“What is the meaning of this?” The Great One spoke with a frown on his face.

“It’d be problematic if you were to destroy the Dragon Spiral Mountain,” said the young teenager.

“I wasn’t talking about the Dragon Spiral Mountain. Why are you here?”

“Isn’t it obvious? I’m here to stop you.”

“With an avatar that doesn’t even have an ounce of power?” The Great One sneered. “Are you looking down on me?”

“You’re right. I don’t have any power in this state, so I cannot physically stop your actions. However, I can offer you a word of advice. If you were to destroy the Dragon Spiral Mountain, Senior Sister Yeyou will not forgive you,” said the young teenager.

“You speak as if it’s a certainty. Both you and Yeyou vanished without a trace after that bastard’s era came to an end—only to reappear now, at the exact same time?” The Great One’s eyes narrowed, voice edged with suspicion and frustration.

“What is the meaning of this… Dragon Ancestor?”

The Great One’s last words shocked all of the spectators. Nobody there could’ve anticipated the reappearance of the Dragon Ancestor.

“That teenager is the Dragon Ancestor?! The third Dragon of Origin?!”

“I thought he died long ago!”

“No, he simply disappeared, just like Dragon Goddess Yeyou. For both of them to show up now… something big will happen in the Nine Heavens very soon…”

As murmurs of concern spread among the spectators, the Dragon Ancestor finally spoke, his tone calm but laced with biting sarcasm, “Are you upset we didn’t include you? Feeling left out?”

He paused, his gaze unflinching.

“Well, maybe that wouldn’t have happened if you hadn’t spent those days hounding Senior Sister Yeyou like some lovesick, perverted maniac.”

The Great One trembled, his eyes flaring with rage.

“Do not mock my feelings for her!” he roared.

“I am not mocking you. I am merely reminding you why Senior Sister Yeyou despised you and why she ultimately chose to keep you out of our group. And if you continue to chase after her like you did in the past… I doubt she’ll be able to resist killing you.”

“Hahaha! Kill me?! Maybe she had that power in the past, but things are different now! I am stronger than her!” The Great One laughed.

The Dragon Ancestor merely shook his head with a pitiful smile, “Oh, you ignorant one. Senior Sister Yeyou’s power is far more than you can fathom, and what you witnessed in the past was merely a fraction of her true power since neither you nor I was strong enough to make her fight seriously.”

“…”

The Great One was left speechless, his eyes widening in disbelief.

’What she showed me… was only a fraction of her true strength?’

Even for someone like him, such a thought was difficult to accept—nearly unfathomable.

The Dragon Ancestor suddenly glanced down at his body, watching as it began to fade, his form slowly unraveling into fragments of light.

“Looks like my time is running out,” he muttered, then lifted his gaze to meet the Great One’s.

“If you truly intend to destroy the Dragon Spiral Mountain, there’s nothing I can do to stop you. However, bear this in mind—that Senior Sister Yeyou would skin you alive if you did. And instead of chasing after her, why don’t you come find me? I’ll share with you one of Senior Sister Yeyou’s greatest secrets.”

Before the Great One could respond, the Dragon Ancestor’s body vanished as if he had never existed in the first place.

“…”

The Great One continued to hover above the Dragon Spiral Mountain long after the Dragon Ancestor had faded, his expression unreadable as he lingered in silence, lost in deep thought.

Eventually, the Great One clicked his tongue in annoyance, and without saying a word, he vanished from the skies above the Dragon Spiral Mountain, leaving behind only the weight of his presence and the silence that followed.

The Holy Dragon Emperor could only stare at the sky in disbelief.

“Looks like we’re fated to continue serving Dragon Goddess Yeyou…” he mumbled.

chapters.title: Chapter 2010: The Dragon Ancestor’S Unexpected Appearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2010: The Dragon Ancestor’S Unexpected Appearance

Chapter 2010: The Dragon Ancestor’s Unexpected Appearance

Shortly after the second incident, news of the events at the Dragon Spiral Mountain spread through the Nine Heavens like wildfire.

“You will not believe what just happened at the Dragon Spiral Mountain!”

“Aren’t you a little late to the party? I heard about that literally a day after it happened several months ago.”

“No! This is a new incident that just happened recently! This time, it was the Great One who attacked the Holy Dragon Clan at the Dragon Spiral Mountain!”

“Heavens! The Great One? You’re lying! For dragons like them, that’s even worse than offending the Celestial Emperor! After all, not even the Royal Dragon Clans could do anything about it!”

“I’m serious! Not only that, but the Dragon Ancestor, who was thought to have died long ago, also made an appearance! Though, he was in the form of an avatar, so something might’ve happened to his physical body.”

“The Dragon Ancestor is back?! We’d just received news of Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s return! What in heaven’s name is happening in the Nine Heavens?!”

News of the Dragon Ancestor’s appearance sent unprecedented shockwaves throughout the Nine Heavens—far greater than those caused by the mention of Dragon Goddess Yeyou—primarily because his name carried even greater fame and influence.

Unlike the Great One, who lived a selfish life, and Dragon Goddess Yeyou, who lived a secluded one, the Dragon Ancestor was the only one who communicated with and offered assistance to the other dragon clans.

To many dragon clans, he was regarded as their forefather—the true Dragon of Origin. Beyond that, the Dragon Ancestor had established his own lineage, one that continues to reign as the foremost dragon clan in all of the Nine Heavens. His influence was nothing short of unfathomable.

Upon hearing of the Dragon Ancestor’s return and his words to ’find him’—even though they were meant for the Great One—the Ten Royal Dragon Clans wasted no time, dispatching their armies across the Nine Heavens in a relentless search for his whereabouts.

Naturally, the Celestial Emperor became even more cautious when he received this news.

Meanwhile, while the entire world was in an uproar, Yuan continued to spend his time with his own people, especially his partners, spending most of his time in the bedroom with them.

A month passed since his meeting with Senior Bai, a familiar figure appeared at the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect.

It was none other than Xi Meili.

“Sister Meili!” Feng Yuxiang was very happy to see her.

“Hello, everyone. I’m sorry it took this long for me to come here. I had to travel the Seventh Heaven without using any teleportation formations, which took a while,” she apologized.

“Are you okay? I hear you were in the fight with Heaven’s Mandate,” Yuan asked her.

She nodded, “Thankfully. If it weren’t for Yin and Yang, I would’ve definitely died that day,” she sighed.

“What about Dong Ye? Do you know where he’s at?” he then asked.

“We went our separate ways once I was close enough to you guys. Unfortunately, he didn’t tell me anything before leaving.”

“That’s fine. He’s a crafty and cautious person. He’ll return to us soon.”

With Xi Meili’s return, the others began catching up with her.

“By the way, did you hear? The Great One attacked the Dragon Spiral Mountain after the Celestial Emperor, and the Dragon Ancestor showed up to stop him,” Xi Meili revealed what she heard during her journey.

“What? The Dragon Ancestor?” Yuan’s eyes widened at this information.

“Yes, but from what I heard, it was just his avatar, so he didn’t appear in the flesh.”

Yuan narrowed his eyes and said, “Either he was too far away, so he sent his avatar, or something happened to his physical body.”

Xi Meili nodded and added, “He also told the Great One to find him. However, it could just be a stalling tactic.”

“Maybe Yeyou knows something. I will ask her later,” said Yuan.

“Okay.”

And shortly after Xi Meili’s appearance, Kelan emerged from his seclusion. Though his cultivation remained the same, his aura had changed drastically since his seclusion. Sёarch* The nôvelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Yuan! Let’s fight again!”

The first thing he did after coming out of seclusion was ask for a fight.

“Sure.” Yuan immediately accepted since he was also itching for a fight after spending so much time in the bedroom.

Yuan and Kelan met up at the white training ground the following day. The others also showed up to spectate.

“Although I lost to you last time, I have a good feeling the results will be different this time,” Kelan proudly declared as they stood a dozen meters away from each other.

Yuan smiled and said, “You’d better have improved drastically after learning such a precious technique.”

Kelan fell silent for a moment.

Then, with a solemn expression, he said, “I know I shouldn’t pry, but my curiosity won’t let me stay quiet. Where in heaven’s name did you obtain this technique, which was believed to be lost to time itself?”

He quickly added, “Of course, you don’t have to answer it. I just wanted to get the question off my chest.”

Yuan then said, “I acquired it from the Nameless Emperor’s Tomb.”

“The Nameless Emperor’s Tomb! To think such a precious treasure has been hiding there for this long. It really makes one wonder what else is hidden in that death trap.”

Kelan had visited the Nameless Emperor’s Tomb more than once, but each venture yielded nothing of true value. After barely escaping with his life during his last expedition, he swore never to return, as the risks simply outweighed the rewards.

However, upon learning that Yuan had acquired such a priceless technique from that very place, doubt crept into his conviction, and he began to reconsider.

“Alright, enough yapping from me. Let me show you how far I’ve come with the Great Mammoth Body Refinement Technique!” Kelan’s aura erupted as his body began to swell, muscles bulging and bones creaking, growing taller and bigger until he resembled that of a true giant.

chapters.title: Chapter 2011: Referral
chapters.body:
Chapter 2011: Referral

Chapter 2011: Referral

Though his size paled in comparison to Kulas during his reign as the Giant Emperor, Kelan had reached a level of mastery that allowed him to amplify both his size and strength in just a few short months, something that required geniuses decades to achieve during the Primordial Era.

“Come.”

When Yuan beckoned him, Kelan charged like a sumo wrestler, each thunderous step shaking the ground beneath him. Just like before, Yuan met him head-on, unarmed and relying solely on his body.

Wanting to test the limit of his new technique, Kelan only used his physique and nothing else. He figured he would be able to rival Yuan with his new technique somehow.

Unfortunately for him, it didn’t take long for Kelan to return to reality.

’Fuck! It’s only been a few months, but his physique has gotten even stronger!’

Kelan quickly realized that he wasn’t the only one who had grown stronger during the last few months. Of course, Yuan didn’t do any real training, and the only exercise he did was in the bedroom with his partners and a few light spars with the others. However, his soul and body were still in the process of merging, so he was constantly getting stronger without needing to do anything, much like Feng Yuxiang’s situation.

Yuan smiled and said, “You may have learned a new technique, but you still haven’t learned your lesson.”

“What? What lesson?” Kelan frowned.

“To never underestimate me!” Yuan laughed out loud as he began pushing Kelan back despite the difference in their size.

“Also, have you already forgotten how you acquired the technique?”

A profound smile appeared on Yuan’s face as his body suddenly began growing, quickly reaching Kelan’s size.

“Y-You!”

Though he knew he shouldn’t be surprised—after all, he had learned the technique from Yuan—Kelan still couldn’t help but be flabbergasted after seeing Yuan use the Great Mammoth Body Refinement Technique.

However, Yuan didn’t remain in his giant form for long and said, “Though I can use the technique, I don’t like increasing my size to make myself an easier target, and it hinders my mobility, too.”

Of course, the biggest reason why Yuan stopped using the Great Mammoth Body Refinement Technique was simply because he didn’t need it.

“What’s wrong? Are you done already?” Yuan asked when Kelan stopped moving and stood still like an iron statue.

“A-As you wish!” Kelan shouted before he resumed his assault.

Since he couldn’t defeat Yuan with the Great Mammoth Body Refinement Technique alone, he began using his martial techniques. Sёarch* The Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Eventually, he started using his cultivation and Immortal Qi. Yet, the results remained the same, and Yuan continued to push him back.

However, despite how badly he was still losing to Yuan, Kelan refused to use Celestial Qi. It was the one thing he swore he wouldn’t use unless Yuan could use Celestial Qi, as it would be too unfair.

Their sparring lasted for nearly an entire week until Kelan was forced to stop because he received a certain message.

“Okay, let’s stop here,” Kelan suddenly said. “Senior Bai has contacted me, saying that your referral is ready, whatever that means. I was also summoned by Celestial Overlord Xu.”

“Oh, I’m going to train at the Holy Sword Temple,” Yuan explained.

“D-Did you just say the Holy Sword Temple?! But you’re still a mortal!”

“So what? One’s sword understanding has nothing to do with their cultivation. Theoretically, a mortal can use Supreme Sword Aura.”

“Yeah, there’s no way…” Kelan shook his head. “You may be a monster, but even you cannot learn Supreme Sword Aura at your age.”

“Wanna bet?” Yuan said with a smile.

“Uhh…” Kelan instantly hesitated despite his earlier confidence.

“No… I’m fine.”

“What a pity. I was going to bet another lost technique compatible with your giant bloodline.” Yuan shrugged.

“WHAT?!” Kelan exclaimed. “IS THAT TRUE?!”

“It doesn’t matter anymore since you don’t want to bet.”

“I’ll bet!” Kelan hastily changed his mind.

He continued, “What do you want? If that technique is as valuable as the Great Mammoth Body Refinement Technique, I don’t think I have anything I can use to bet.”

“If I win, you’ll let me take a look at your treasury and pick any one treasure I want. How about it?”

“Are you sure? I may own a sect, but my treasury is quite lackluster. You will definitely be disappointed,” he said.

“That’s up for me to decide.”

“Alright. If that’s what you want, don’t complain afterward—not that you’ll win. Also, I’ll give you a hundred years to train at the Holy Sword Temple.”

Yuan raised his eyebrows.

“A hundred years? You’re being too generous. You might as well give me the treasure now.”

Kelan frowned and said, “Then ten years. You have ten years to learn Supreme Sword Aura.”

“Sure.” Yuan nodded.

Kelan extended his hand and said, “Let’s shake on it.”

After shaking on it, they left the training grounds and made their way to the Myriad of Techniques.

The others stayed back so they wouldn’t end up crowding the place.

When they arrived at the Myriad of Techniques, it wasn’t just Senior Bai present, but Xu Jiaqi as well.

“It’s been a while, Sister Xu.”

“…”

However, Xu Jiaqi didn’t respond. She simply stared at him with wide eyes, frozen in place. Although she had long believed Yuan to be the reincarnation of the Celestial Overlord’s founder, seeing him fully grown left her breathless, and her memories of the founder were forcefully resurfaced.

However, she quickly snapped out of it and said, “Sorry, but you look just like the Celestial Overlord’s founder. If I had seen you outside, I would have definitely mistaken you for him.”

She swiftly reached into her spatial ring and retrieved a crimson, sword-shaped badge, its surface etched with ancient symbols. Holding it with both hands, she offered it to him.

“This is your referral,” she said. “Present it to the masters of the Holy Sword Temple, and they will allow you to enter.”

“Thank you.”

Yuan accepted the badge and inspected it with interest.

chapters.title: Chapter 2012: Grand Sword Crater
chapters.body:
Chapter 2012: Grand Sword Crater

Chapter 2012: Grand Sword Crater

“When will you head to the Holy Sword Temple?” Xu Jiaqi asked Yuan shortly after giving him the sword-shaped badge.

“I’ll have to figure out how to get there first,” he said.

Though he’d been to the Holy Sword Temple before as Tian Xin, he couldn’t recall how to get there.

“Only the Holy Sword Clan, who owns the place, can bring you to the Holy Sword Temple,” Xu Jiaqi explained.

“So I have to contact the Holy Sword Clan, huh? Where do I find them?”

“While their main headquarters is in the Ninth Heaven, they also have a branch in the Seventh and Eighth Heaven. You can find them at the Grand Sword Crater.”

“The Grand Sword Crater, right? I’ll go find them as soon as possible.”

“Good luck. The Holy Sword Clan is known for being eccentric, so try not to cause any trouble. They’re also a top 20 faction with the most swordmasters in the world,” said Senior Bai.

“No promises,” Yuan said with a playful smile. S~eaʀᴄh the novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan left shortly after, leaving Kelan behind.

Once Yuan was gone, Xu Jiaqi turned her attention to Kelan.

“Now then… Commander Kelan.”

“Y-Yes!”

Kelan subconsciously straightened his back after being called.

“I’m pretty sure I instructed you to notify me immediately when Yuan returns. Yet, you ignored my order and went straight to seclusion after his return. What do you have to say about that?” she narrowed her gaze at him.

Kelan lowered his head and apologized, “I’m sorry. I got too excited when he gave me a technique.”

“A technique?” Senior Bai and Xu Jiaqi raised their eyebrows.

Kelan was extremely picky when it came to learning new techniques, so it was pretty surprising to hear that he’d gotten so excited about a technique that he forgot about his mission.

Though they were intrigued by the technique, they didn’t force Kelan to reveal it since that was his private business with Yuan.

“I’ll let you go with a warning this time,” Xu Jiaqi said.

“Thank you, Celestial Overlord!”

After returning to the sect, Yuan explained the situation to the others.

“So you’ll be leaving again soon, huh?” Chu Liuxiang sighed. Though she knew he would eventually leave again, it didn’t make her feel better when that time finally arrived.

“I won’t be leaving immediately. I promised Yu Rou that I’d let her know before I leave, so I’ll stay here until our next meeting,” said Yuan.

Thus, Yuan spent his remaining time in the sect with them until it was time for his meeting with Yu Rou.

At the Myriad of Techniques, Yuan announced his next destination to Yu Rou and the others at the end of their meeting.

“How long do you think you’ll be gone this time?” Yu Rou asked.

“It’s hard to say, but it’ll definitely be at least a few years.”

“I see…” Yu Rou lowered her head for a moment, but she quickly raised her head again and spoke with a calm smile, “Be safe, brother.”

“The same goes for you.”

Yu Rou went to embrace him the next moment.

“See you later.”

After leaving the Myriad of Techniques, Yuan immediately began preparing to head to the Holy Sword Temple.

“Since I cannot enter your dantian like the others, I’ll stay here and train with the others,” Xi Meili said to Yuan before he left.

Yuan nodded.

After saying his goodbyes with the others, Yuan left the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect and made his way toward the Grand Sword Crater. Unfortunately, there was no teleportation device near his destination, so he could only teleport to the nearest place and travel from there.

After emerging from the teleportation device, Yuan wasted no time and used Feng Yuxiang’s flying treasure to travel.

Three months later, Yuan arrived at his destination.

“This is the Grand Sword Crater…?” Feng Yuxiang murmured, her voice filled with awe as her gaze swept across the horizon.

True to its name, the Grand Sword Crater was a monumental sight—a single, flawless gash carved into the land, stretching thousands of miles in length and plunging deep into the earth like a wound left by a heavenly blade.

Though the landscape resembled the aftermath of a cataclysmic battle, the vast fissure was far from abandoned, with countless swordmasters moving busily around and within its depths.

Legend has it that this colossal fissure was carved by a Sword Emperor with a single strike. Now, swordmasters from across the world gather here, hoping to glean insight from the remnants of his might and deepen their own understanding of the sword.

Of course, not every swordmaster came here in pursuit of insight. In truth, the majority sought to attract the attention of the Holy Sword Clan, who frequented the crater in search of talented swordmasters to recruit for their faction.

To draw the attention of the Holy Sword Clan, many swordmasters would periodically release their Sword Aura, feigning moments of insight, while others openly practiced their sword techniques in hopes of being noticed.

“What a bunch of weirdos,” Feng Yuxiang remarked when she saw the odd scene before her.

Some time later, Yuan began wandering the Grand Sword Crater in search of the Holy Sword Clan.

“White and golden robes…” Yuan occasionally muttered to himself as he used divine sense to quickly scout the area without stopping.

Several days later, Yuan came to a stop when he finally found someone who looked to be from the Holy Sword Clan.

He focused his divine sense on a bald old man clad in elegant white and gold robes adorned with intricate sword engravings.

The old man was surrounded by swordmasters, yet his gaze was fixed on a lone youth quietly cultivating before him, exuding a decent amount of Enhanced Sword Aura.

“Not bad… not bad at all…” The old man nodded in acknowledgement.

Seeing this, envy flickered in the eyes of the surrounding swordmasters, each secretly wishing it was their Sword Aura that had captured the old man’s attention.

chapters.title: Chapter 2013: Holy Sword Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2013: Holy Sword Clan

Chapter 2013: Holy Sword Clan

When Yuan saw the situation, he decided not to interrupt but to spectate instead.

A few minutes later, the young man who’d caught the attention of the old man from the Holy Sword Clan opened his eyes and stood up, seemingly unaware of the elder’s presence.

The old man cleared his throat loudly to announce his presence before speaking, “Young man, what’s your name?”

The young man was startled momentarily, but he quickly recovered when he saw the old man’s white and golden robes.

Then, he hastily lowered his body and bowed, “This junior is called Chu Ling. I apologize for not noticing you earlier, Senior.”

The old man smiled and said, “It’s my fault for sneaking up on you. Anyway, if you don’t mind me asking, how old are you?”

“This junior is currently 3,291 years old,” the young man responded.

The surrounding swordmasters expressed surprise after hearing the young man’s response.

“So you’ve achieved Enhanced Sword Aura in under 5,000 years. Very impressive indeed. Do you currently have a teacher?” the old man continued to ask, almost like he was giving a job interview.

“No, I do not, but I did spend a thousand years at the Gleaming Sword Sect.”

The old man nodded before retrieving what looked like a slab of metal and said, “If you can leave even the slightest scratch on this plate using your Enhanced Sword Aura, I’ll recruit you into the Holy Sword Clan.”

Chu Ling swallowed nervously at the opportunity before him. The Holy Sword Clan was home to three Sword Emperors. Joining them was a dream pursued by countless swordmasters.

“Are you willing to accept this challenge?” the old man asked.

“I am willing!” Chu Ling immediately responded.

The old man nodded and placed the metal on the ground.

“Whenever you are ready,” he said.

Chu Ling retrieved his sword and took a moment to prepare himself.

Then, everyone watched as Chu Ling’s body surged with Enhanced Sword Aura, and he struck the metal plate with a resounding clang that echoed through the crater.

The spectators anticipated Chu Ling to leave a mark on the metal plate, but to their shock, he was suddenly hurled backward like a ragdoll.

Before anyone could make sense of what had just happened, a cold, mocking laughter echoed through the air. All eyes turned toward the old man, who was now laughing hysterically, as though he’d just witnessed the most absurd joke in the world.

“W-What is going on…?” Chu Ling muttered in a daze as he slowly got back to his feet.

The old man responded, “That was the best you could do with that level of Enhanced Sword Aura? What a waste of talents!”

“What’s happening, Young Master?” Feng Yuxiang asked, as she was just as confused as the others.

Yuan calmly explained, “While his Enhanced Sword Aura is indeed commendable, he has no idea how to wield it. It’s like handing a divine blade to a child—powerful, yet utterly wasted.”

“I didn’t know you needed to know how to use Sword Aura,” Feng Yuxiang remarked. Searᴄh the NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“While Weapon Aura is inherently powerful and can be used as is, you still need to learn to control it to utilize its full potential. It’s like comparing a sharp knife with a dull knife.”

The old man approached Chu Ling and shook his head, “Sorry, but it looks like you didn’t pass the test.”

“P-Please give me one more chance! I will do it properly this time!” Chu Ling fell to his knees and begged.

The old man narrowed his eyes and sneered in disdain, “Another chance? Do you know how many people here would kill for a first chance? You were given a chance, but you squandered it.”

Without giving Chu Ling another look, the old man turned around and walked away, leaving him devastated.

However, before the old man could get too far, Yuan descended right before him, blocking his path.

“…”

The old man halted his movements and stared at Yuan with a somewhat surprised face. He’d been wandering the Grand Sword Crater for many years, but nobody had so boldly stood in his path before.

“Do you think I can give it a go?” Yuan asked.

“What?” The old man frowned.

“That metal slab. I want to try striking it with my Sword Aura,” he clarified.

“I don’t know if you’re acting on boldness or pure stupidity, but I will give you a chance to walk away with your life.”

Yuan didn’t say anything, but he exuded some of his Sword Aura. It wasn’t his Enhanced Sword Aura, but just his ordinary Sword Aura.

The old man’s eyes widened after sensing Yuan’s ordinary Sword Aura, even doubting his own eyes for a second.

’His ordinary Sword Aura feels as powerful as the last guy’s Enhanced Sword Aura! But how is that possible?!’ he cried inwardly, not daring to believe what he was sensing.

In order to confirm the phenomenon before him, the old man retrieved the metal slab and tossed it on the ground.

“Go ahead.”

Without saying a word, Yuan retrieved a random sword from his spatial ring and swung it at the metal slab.

The people around them expected to hear a loud clanging sound like usual, but to their puzzlement, Yuan’s strike didn’t make even the slightest sound.

“Did he miss?” They wondered out loud as their eyes shifted to the metal slab on the ground.

“No way…”

Despite staring at the object with their own eyes, nobody there dared to believe what they were witnessing.

“Th-This is…” The old man’s voice trembled as he stared at the metal slab lying on the ground, now cleanly split in two.

“Hm? It’s not as durable as I thought it would be…” Yuan muttered.

The old man’s body shook. Then, without warning, he lunged forward and grabbed both of Yuan’s arms.

“You must join our Holy Sword Clan!” he declared.

However, Yuan remained calm and said, “I think there’s been a misunderstanding. I’m not trying to join the Holy Sword Clan.”

“What?”

It wasn’t just the old man—everyone present wore the same stunned expression after hearing Yuan’s words, their minds struggling to grasp such nonsense.

chapters.title: Chapter 2014: Holy Sword Clan(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2014: Holy Sword Clan(2)

Chapter 2014: Holy Sword Clan(2)

After a moment of silence, the old man cleared his throat and spoke in a forceful tone, “I must have misheard you just now. Can you repeat what you said?”

“I said that I am not interested in joining the Holy Sword Clan,” Yuan calmly repeated, shocking everyone there for a second time.

“Blasphemy! If you’re not interested in joining the Holy Sword Clan, why did you even bother with the metal plate?! Are you messing with me?!”

The old man exclaimed, his face flushed in redness. However, he didn’t give up and continued trying to recruit Yuan.

“The Holy Sword Clan is the number one faction for swordmasters in the Nine Heavens! If you wish to become a Sword Emperor, your best chance lies with us. We not only house three Sword Emperors, but the majority of Sword Emperors in history have hailed from our clan!” the old man proclaimed, his tone filled with pride as he tried to sway Yuan’s decision.

“I’ll think about it after I finish training at the Holy Sword Temple,” Yuan calmly responded.

“What? The Holy Sword Temple?”

The old man wore a dumbfounded expression on his face, but before he could speak further, Yuan retrieved the sword-shaped badge from his spatial ring and showed it off.

“This is my actual reason for approaching you,” he said.

“That’s the Holy Sword Emblem! Where did you get that from?!” The old man exclaimed.

“Does it matter where?”

“Of course, it does! How do I know that you didn’t steal it or find it by chance?”

Yuan didn’t argue further and showed the old man his faction symbol.

“I got it from someone in my faction.”

“Celestial Overlords!” The old man and those around him sucked in a breath of cold breath after seeing his faction symbol.

“Alright, come with me. I’ll take you to our headquarters to speak with the elders.”

Yuan nodded and silently followed the old man into the depths of the Grand Sword Crater. After descending to the bottom of the massive fissure, they proceeded northward without pause.

Though the number of swordmasters within the Grand Sword Crater wasn’t as overwhelming as outside, their presence was still significant, and Yuan couldn’t go more than a few minutes without witnessing one practicing their sword techniques.

After several days of travel and passing by numerous members of the Holy Sword Clan, they finally arrived at the clan’s headquarters, located in the widest expanse of the Grand Sword Crater.

Sandwiched between two colossal walls of earth stood several sword-shaped buildings, each varying in height but radiating the same grand presence.

The old man brought Yuan to the tallest building and then had him wait inside one of the guest rooms.

“Wait here while I bring one of our elders here,” said the old man before walking out and closing the door.

However, Yuan could tell that the old man didn’t leave and remained outside for some reason.

A few moments later, the door opened, and the old man walked back inside.

“My name is Li Bowen, the 4th elder of the Holy Sword Clan,” he introduced himself. S~eaʀᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The old man’s unusual actions left Yuan speechless.

Li Bowen smiled and said, “There’s no need for the suspicious looks. I really am an elder.”

He then took a seat right in front of Yuan and continued, “Alright, let’s get straight to the topic. The Holy Sword Temple.”

Yuan quietly nodded.

“Although you have the Holy Sword Emblem, that is only one of the requirements to enter the Holy Sword Temple. Is that your true cultivation?” Li Bowen asked.

“Yes, it is.”

“In all my years at the Holy Sword Clan, I have never heard of a mortal asking to enter the Holy Sword Temple, much less stand before one myself. Do you even know what you’re getting yourself into?”

“As long as I can learn Supreme Sword Aura, I don’t care.”

Li Bowen narrowed his eyes and said, “The Holy Sword Temple may serve as a training ground, but it is a perilous place where even Immortals can easily lose their life, much less a mortal like you. I’m sure you already know that, so I won’t waste my breath trying to change your mind. However, I still have to test your qualifications.”

“How will you do that?” Yuan asked.

“It’s very simple and straightforward. I just need to test your Enhanced Sword Aura.”

“Sounds good.”

“Then follow me.”

The next moment, they left the room, and Li Bowen led Yuan to another building. Within it lay a vast training ground, where several swordmasters were striking a towering wall with their Enhanced Sword Aura. Naturally, the wall was forged from a special material, similar to the metal slab Yuan had effortlessly split in two, but also vastly different at the same time.

The swordmasters in the room struck the wall with meticulously controlled Enhanced Sword Aura and had been doing so for several hours now, yet not a single one of them managed to leave even the faintest scratch.

“This wall is forged from a unique material with unparalleled resistance to slashing attacks. Don’t even think about comparing it to the metal slab you cut through a few days ago—this is at least a thousand times more durable,” Li Bowen explained with a somewhat proud look on his face.

“So, how deep does the cut need to be for me to qualify?” Yuan asked.

Li Bowen smiled and replied, “One inch. If you can somehow manage to leave a cut that deep on this wall, I’ll personally take you to the Holy Sword Temple. But there’s a condition—you can only use a Spirit-grade sword and rely solely on your Enhanced Sword Aura. No martial techniques, no powerful treasures.”

“Fair enough.”

Li Bowen retrieved an ordinary Spirit-grade sword that one could purchase from anywhere and handed it to Yuan.

“Whenever you’re—”

Li Bowen abruptly paused mid-sentence, his expression tightening.

Sensing several unfamiliar auras entering the building, he turned toward the entrance, and his eyes widened in disbelief after seeing their faces.

“W-What are they doing here?!” he muttered under his breath, his voice tinged with shock and unease.

Meanwhile, those training at the wall halted their training and quietly bowed to the group.

Yuan followed Li Bowen’s gaze toward the group that had just stepped into the building.

There were six individuals in total, but all eyes were naturally drawn to the two at the front—a strikingly beautiful young woman and a handsome young man, both adorned in the distinguished robes of the Holy Sword Clan.

“I’ll be back. Don’t do anything until then.” Li Bowen said to Yuan before approaching the group.

“Greetings, Holy Sword Maiden, Holy Sword King, and everyone else.”

“Elder Li, it’s been a while,” said the handsome man, who was known as the Holy Sword King within the Holy Sword Clan.

“Yes, it’s been around 150 years. What brought you guys to the Seventh Heaven?”

“The Sword Emperor Qualifications, of course. I know there are still a few years before it begins, but we decided to come down earlier,” he explained.

“I see…”

“Do you mind if we use the training grounds for a bit?” The Holy Sword Maiden suddenly said.

“Of course not… but at your level…”

“We won’t be the ones to use it,” she elaborated, looking at the other four behind her.

“Oh, I see. In any case, feel free to use it. If you need anything, don’t hesitate to call me.”

“Thank you.”

Li Bowen returned to Yuan shortly after and said to him, “Sorry, but we’ll have to do your qualifications another time.”

“But it won’t even take a minute… I just need to hit the wall once,” Yuan said.

Li Bowen responded with voice transmission, “I know, but this isn’t about that. Do you see that beautiful woman with silver hair and that young man with the short red hair? They’re the Holy Sword Maiden and the Holy Sword King. Their status within the Holy Sword Clan is only below the Sword Emperors. While the Holy Sword Maiden might not mind your presence, the Holy Sword King is known for being easily irritable. I am doing this for your own good. If you offend them, you can kiss your chance to enter the Holy Sword Temple, even with a referral. Don’t worry, I doubt they’ll stay for too long.”

Yuan found it a pain in the ass, but he’d rather wait a few days, even weeks, if it meant not causing any unnecessary trouble.

“I don’t mind waiting a little,” he said.

“Thank you for your understanding. You can stay in one of our guest rooms for now,” Li Bowen said.

The next moment, Yuan followed Li Bowen to the exit.

“Who’s that?” The Holy Sword Maiden asked one of the swordmasters who had been training there before their arrival.

“I’m not sure. He arrived only a few moments earlier than you guys, but I think he was trying to qualify for the Holy Sword Temple.”

The Holy Sword King laughed and said, “There’s no way. That guy was a mortal.”

“Oh… then I don’t know… Sorry.”

“It’s fine.” The Holy Sword Maiden shook her head.

After leaving the training ground, Li Bowen brought Yuan to his guest room.

“You can stay here for now. I’ll contact you when they are finished with the place, which shouldn’t take too long.”

Once Li Bowen left and he was alone, Yuan sat on the ground and closed his eyes.

’I might as well use this time to perfect my Enhanced Sword Aura,’ he thought inwardly before immersing himself in Tian Xin’s memories.

chapters.title: Chapter 2015: Holy Sword Clan(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2015: Holy Sword Clan(3)

Chapter 2015: Holy Sword Clan(3)

Unlike his predecessors, Tian Xin was unique in the sense that he was born with all of his previous life’s memories intact. This gave him an unfathomable advantage, allowing him to train with all of his previous knowledge since the day he was born.

Because of this, Tian Xin was always considered a prodigy no matter where he went. Moreover, not only was Tian Xin born with all of his memories, but he also came from a renowned background, one that was known for creating many powerful swordmasters.

One could consider Tian Xin being born with a diamond spoon in his mouth. Yet, despite this, he did not rely on his family even in the slightest and left home at a young age to travel the Nine Heavens.

During his journey, Tian Xin quickly gained fame as an undefeated and mysterious warrior, his identity shrouded in mystery beneath the mask he always wore.

He encountered Jin Xi early in his journey. After being defeated by him, she chose to follow him to get stronger, whether he liked it or not.

Although he ignored her in the beginning, they eventually became something akin to master and disciple. Of course, Tian Xin never admitted that he was teaching her, only giving her cryptic advice.

It didn’t take long for Jin Xi to develop feelings for Tian Xin, and in time, he began to feel the same. Yet, neither of them ever voiced their hearts. Though Jin Xi’s affection often showed in her actions, quite obvious at times, she never found the courage to confess.

As for Tian Xin, while he was aware of Jin Xi’s feelings, he chose to feign ignorance.

Together, they honed their swordsmanship. While Jin Xi struggled to achieve Enhanced Sword Aura, Tian Xin had long mastered Enhanced Sword Aura and was already looking to reach the next realm—the level of Supreme Sword Aura.

As Yuan recalled his experiences as Tian Xin, Enhanced Sword Aura naturally emanated from his body. Since the strength of one’s Weapon Aura stems primarily from comprehension, his Enhanced Sword Aura became sharper and toward perfection at an unfathomable rate.

In the blink of an eye, a whole month has passed since Yuan arrived at the Holy Sword Clan.

*Knock* *Knock*

Yuan opened his eyes when someone suddenly knocked on the door.

“About time,” he sighed out loud, assuming Li Bowen had finally arrived to inform him that the training ground was ready.

He went to open the door a moment later, only to be surprised that it wasn’t Li Bowen who’d knocked on the door.

“Uh… how can I help you?” Yuan asked the beautiful young woman standing outside his door.

This woman had long silver hair and red eyes that glowed faintly. Unexpectedly, it was the Holy Sword Maiden.

The Holy Sword Maiden didn’t respond right away. Instead, she glanced into his room to confirm he was alone. Then, with a slightly puzzled expression, she said, “I happened to be passing by when I sensed Enhanced Sword Aura coming from this room. I may be mistaken, but… was that you?”

The Holy Sword Maiden spoke with a tone of disbelief. The Enhanced Sword Aura she had sensed was so powerful, it could only be compared to that of a Sword Emperor—yet the man before her was clearly just a mortal.

“Yes, it was mine. I was training. Sorry, I didn’t realize it was leaking outside. I hope that didn’t bother you.”

“How…?” she muttered in a low voice before asking in a loud and clear voice, “How did you achieve that level of Enhanced Sword Aura?”

“I have already reached the level of Supreme Sword Aura, but my Enhanced Sword Aura couldn’t be compared to it. Can you also use Supreme Sword Aura?”

“No, I can’t.”

“That doesn’t make any sense. Can you explain how you did it?”

“I don’t know what to tell you… The strength of one’s weapon aura is based on their understanding, so…”

The Holy Sword Maiden frowned slightly and said, “Are you saying that you, who hasn’t even learned Supreme Sword Aura, somehow has a better understanding than I?”

“Well, each level of Sword Aura carries its own depth of understanding,” Yuan said. “So it’s possible for someone to grasp one level more profoundly than another.”

“I know that! But it still doesn’t make sense! How can a mortal understand more than I, who is at the God Ascension Realm?!”

“…”

Yuan didn’t know what else to say, but he had a feeling that he’d get into trouble soon.

“Come with me,” she suddenly said.

“I really shouldn’t… I was told to stay here by Elder Li,” Yuan said.

“Do you know who I am?” she then asked.

He nodded and answered, “You’re the Holy Sword Maiden, right?”

“That’s right, and my authority within the Holy Sword Clan is only second to the Sword Emperors. Nothing will happen to you if you come with me. I promise,” she said.

“Okay…” Yuan responded with a stiff smile. It wasn’t Li Bowen he was concerned about. However, he couldn’t tell her that he was worried about getting involved with her, as that would definitely offend her.

Without saying another word, the Holy Sword Maiden turned around and began walking away. Seeing this, Yuan could only sigh inwardly and follow her.

They arrived at their destination a few minutes later. Unexpectedly, the Holy Sword Maiden brought him to the building with the metal wall. However, the place was empty.

Upon their arrival, the Holy Sword Maiden silently approached the wall, retrieving one of the Spirit-grade swords hanging nearby. She then stepped forward, positioning herself before the metal wall. After drawing a steady breath, she activated her Enhanced Sword Aura. In one graceful, fluid motion, she swung the sword, unleashing a streak of shimmering aura that sliced through the air toward the wall.

When her Enhanced Sword Aura struck the wall, it carved a six-inch-deep gash into the metal, leaving a clear mark that stood out amid the countless faint scratches left by others.

Before Yuan could even say anything, the Holy Sword Maiden tossed her sword at him and said, “Your turn.”

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 2016: Holy Sword Clan(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2016: Holy Sword Clan(4)

Chapter 2016: Holy Sword Clan(4)

“Um… before I strike at the wall, do you mind calling Elder Li here?” Yuan asked after catching the Spirit-grade sword.

“Why? Are you worried that I might do something to you?” The Holy Sword Maiden spoke with a frown.

“No, that’s not it. I just don’t want to do this again later.”

“Why would you—”

The Holy Sword Maiden abruptly halted her sentence when she recalled something that was mentioned before.

“So it was true. You’re here to qualify for the Holy Sword Temple,” she said.

Yuan nodded, “That’s right.”

“In that case, there’s no need to trouble Elder Li. I can verify it myself. Go ahead—whenever you’re ready,” she said, urging him to proceed.

“Alright.”

Yuan approached the metal wall, took a deep breath, and fell silent. The next moment, Enhanced Sword Aura surged from his body before enveloping the sword.

The Holy Sword Maiden remained silent, but her fists were tightly clenched. From a single glance, she could tell that his Enhanced Sword Aura far surpassed hers. Yet, she couldn’t fathom just how vast that difference was.

Once he was ready, Yuan flicked his arm with lightning speed. In that instant, the sword in his grasp flickered like a phantom.

In the moments that followed, Yuan’s Enhanced Sword Aura struck the six-inch gash left by the Holy Sword Maiden. Not only did it erase her mark entirely, but it even pierced straight through the wall itself, leaving a clean hole in its wake.

“No way…”

The Holy Sword Maiden’s legs buckled beneath her, and she collapsed onto her buttocks with a soft thud, her eyes wide with disbelief.

Yuan’s Enhanced Sword Aura hadn’t merely exceeded her expectations—it likely surpassed even that of the Sword Emperors within the Holy Sword Clan.

“Uh… sorry, but I didn’t mean to destroy the wall,” Yuan muttered awkwardly, scratching his head. “Elder Li made it sound indestructible, after all…”

However, the Holy Sword Maiden was too shocked to even respond, and she merely continued staring at the hole with a dazed face.

Seeing her in a daze, Yuan could only wait patiently for her to return to reality.

However, his expression subtly shifted when he sensed several powerful presences drawing near. It was likely the Holy Sword King and the others.

Even without being told, Yuan understood how terrible the situation appeared. He could already picture himself trying to explain the situation to them, only for things to worsen. Hell, he could even forsee the Holy Sword King picking a fight with him over this.

With no other choice, Yuan hastily placed the Spirit-grade sword on the ground beside the Holy Sword Maiden, then turned around and exited through the hole he’d created.

Seeing this, the Holy Sword Maiden finally snapped out of her daze. However, Yuan had already left, and before she could give chase, the Holy Sword King and the others entered the building.

“What the hell happened here?” the Holy Sword King exclaimed as his gaze fell upon the gaping hole in the wall.

His first instinct was to assume the Holy Sword Maiden was responsible, yet he couldn’t fathom why she’d do such a thing.

However, before he could ask her anything, the Holy Sword Maiden stood up and ran toward the hole in the wall.

“Wait! Where are you going?!” The Holy Sword King immediately chased after her.

With the Holy Sword King close behind, the Holy Sword Maiden rushed after Yuan. However, even with her divine sense enveloping the entire area, she couldn’t detect his presence.

’Why can’t I find him? Don’t tell me he already left this place! But there’s no way he could’ve gone that far in such a short time!’

Of course, Yuan was still within the Holy Sword Clan’s territory. The reason the Holy Sword Maiden couldn’t find him was because he was concealing his presence with Shadow Veil.

Some time later, after giving up on finding Yuan, the Holy Sword Maiden went to find Li Bowen instead.

“That young man who came to qualify for the Holy Sword Temple—who is he?” she questioned him.

“Excuse me?” Li Bowen wore a dumbfounded look on his face. He couldn’t understand why the Holy Sword Maiden was suddenly looking for Yuan, who hadn’t left the guest room for over a month.

“Just answer my question!” she raised her voice, startling him and the Holy Sword King, who had never seen her so agitated before.

“Are you looking for that mortal from a month ago?” The Holy Sword King finally recalled.

“Why? Did he do something to you? I’ll find and skin that bastard—”

“Why are you even here? This doesn’t concern you!” she interrupted him.

“But…”

“Get out!” she pointed toward the exit.

Seeing how annoyed she was, the Holy Sword King reluctantly left the room.

Once they were alone, the Holy Sword Maiden continued questioning Li Bowen.

“Tell me everything you know about that mortal with you a month ago,” she demanded.

Li Bowen swallowed nervously before responding, “The only thing I know about him is that he’s with the Celestial Overlords.”

“What about his name?”

“That’s… uhh…” It was at this moment that Li Bowen realized he’d never asked Yuan for his name.

“I’m sorry, but I never got the chance to ask for his name…” he admitted a moment later.

“Can you tell me what this is all about?” he then asked.

The Holy Sword Maiden rubbed her eyes and sighed before explaining, “He cut through the wall.”

“What?” Li Bowen raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner, unable to comprehend her words.

“The Impenetrable Sword Wall! He cut through the entire thing with his Sword Aura!” she repeated, this time with more detail.

“HE WHAT?!” Li Bowen exclaimed, his eyes as wide as saucers.

“L-Let me go see!”

Before the Holy Sword Maiden could respond, Li Bowen rushed to the training ground.

The moment Li Bowen laid eyes on the gaping hole in the wall, his jaw nearly hit the ground in disbelief. After all, the only time he’d witnessed such devastation was from Supreme Sword Aura—yet Yuan couldn’t possibly know it if he was seeking training at the Holy Sword Temple.

When the Holy Sword Maiden caught up, he asked her, “How did he achieve this?”

“With Enhanced Sword Aura,” she responded, confirming his suspicions.

“…”

Unsure of how to respond, Li Bowen quietly stood there, staring at the hole with a dazed face.

chapters.title: Chapter 2017: Holy Sword Clan(5)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2017: Holy Sword Clan(5)

Chapter 2017: Holy Sword Clan(5)

“Hey.”

The Holy Sword Maiden suddenly called out, snapping Li Bowen out of his daze.

When he turned to look at her, she held a sword-shaped badge in front of her, seemingly offering it to him.

This badge looked similar to the one in Yuan’s possession, but it was golden in color and had more refined details.

Li Bowen’s eyes widened at the sight of this badge, but before he could say anything, the Holy Sword Maiden spoke, “The mortal left before I could give this to him. If he returns, give it to him for me. I promised to verify his qualifications, after all.”

The sword badge was akin to a referral, but it came from none other than the Holy Sword Maiden, which guaranteed Yuan’s admission to the Holy Sword Temple.

“Yes…” Li Bowen accepted the badge with stiff movements. Even someone of his stature had never touched this object before.

“Wait. Did you say he’d left?” Li Bowen suddenly asked.

“Yes, he left after destroying the wall. He probably thought he’d get in trouble or something. I don’t know why, but I have a feeling that he’s afraid of me.”

“I see…” Li Bowen remarked in a flat voice. He couldn’t tell her that it was most likely due to what he told Yuan a month ago.

“Anyway, I’ll be leaving now. If you ever find more about that mortal, let me know. We need him in the Holy Sword Clan,” said the Holy Sword Maiden.

“But he’s already with the Celestial Overlords,” Li Bowen reminded her.

“So what? As far as I am aware, the Celestial Overlords don’t restrict their members to a single faction. And since there are no grievances between our faction, there’s no reason he cannot join us as well.”

Most factions out there allow their members to join multiple factions to keep their numbers up, so it wasn’t uncommon for someone to belong to multiple factions.

Li Bowen could only silently nod his head. He recalled Yuan being at the lowest rank for the Celestial Overlords, so there was a good chance they could recruit him into the Holy Sword Clan.

Some time later, the Holy Sword Maiden left the Holy Sword Clan. The Holy Sword King and the others were puzzled by her abrupt departure, but they still left with her.

“Fuck, what am I supposed to do with this badge? If he doesn’t return…” Li Bowen muttered as he stared at the golden badge resting on his palm.

However, he suddenly sensed a presence appear behind him like a ghost.

When he turned around and saw Yuan standing behind him, Li Bowen nearly had a heart attack.

“Son of a bi—!”

“Sorry for startling you.”

“You! I thought you had left!”

“No, I only hid my presence since I didn’t want to deal with the Holy Sword Maiden after what happened. I’m glad she left so quickly,” said Yuan.

Li Bowen rubbed his eyes and released a stressful sigh.

“I told you to stay away from them, but you went ahead and caused such a scene.”

Yuan shrugged, “Don’t make it sound like it was my fault that she dragged me out of my room and forced me to do her bidding. If I had refused, it would have definitely offended her.”

“I am not blaming you…” Li Bowen said, admitting that it wasn’t his fault.

“In fact, I am quite thankful for what you did. Like you said, things could’ve turned out much worse, but it didn’t. Hell, the Holy Sword Maiden even approved of you.”

Li Bowen extended his hand toward Yuan, showing the golden badge, and continued, “She has granted you permission to enter the Holy Sword Temple.”

“Thank you.” Yuan accepted the badge.

Then, he asked, “About the wall… how much will the repairs cost? I’ll pay for it.”

“It’ll cost a few hundred million spirit stones, but you don’t need to worry about it. It’s pretty worn out, and we’ve been wanting to replace it, but we got lazy.”

“If you say so…”

“Anyway, when do you want to head to the Holy Sword Temple?”

“Can we go there now?”

“Unfortunately, it’ll take some time to prepare the transport, so the earliest you can go is three months.”

“Then I’ll leave as soon as possible.”

“I understand. Oh, one more thing. How would you like to join the Holy Sword Temple? This isn’t just my request, but also the Holy Sword Maiden’s.”

“But I am already part of the Celestial Overlords.”

“The Celestial Overlords doesn’t prevent its members from joining other factions. In fact, several of our members are also members of the Celestial Overlords. There’s no downside to having more backers, either.”

Li Bowen’s words made sense to Yuan. It was common for people to join multiple factions to expand their influence. After all, having the support of several powerful backers was always advantageous.

However, Yuan didn’t want to just join any faction.

“Can you give me some time to think about it? I would like to learn more about the Holy Sword Clan before I decide,” he said.

Li Bowen’s face immediately lit up with excitement.

“Of course! Though there’s not much to see here since we’re just a small branch location, I’ll try my best to answer any questions that you may have.”

Some time later, Li Bowen left to get the transportation ready. In the meantime, Yuan wandered their territory. However, just as Li Bowen had warned, there wasn’t much to see, much less learn there.

However, he did find a library that contained the history of the Holy Sword Clan, and with nothing else better to do, he started reading it.

“Hm?”

After reading several scrolls, Yuan learned some peculiar information about the Holy Sword Temple.

According to the Holy Sword Clan, they weren’t the first owners of the Holy Sword Temple, and they’d merely acquired it from its creator, the Exalted Blacksmith. However, Yuan couldn’t recall creating it. Of course, he still lacked some of Tian Qiyuan’s memories, mostly of his time after becoming the God of Creation.

chapters.title: Chapter 2018: Entering The Starry Sky
chapters.body:
Chapter 2018: Entering The Starry Sky

Chapter 2018: Entering the Starry Sky

After spending a few days in the library, Yuan went back to his room, where he tried to learn Supreme Sword Aura.

Despite having the understanding, memories, and experience of using Supreme Sword Aura as Tian Xin, Yuan couldn’t use it for some reason, almost as if his attempt was being blocked by an unseen force.

After failing several times, Yuan stopped trying and began focusing on his soul strength instead. Since there was no limit to how strong his soul strength could get, it was his current priority.

Time passed, and in the blink of an eye, three months had passed since Yuan received permission to visit the Holy Sword Temple.

Once the preparations were complete, Li Bowen notified Yuan and brought him outside, where a golden carriage awaited. Naturally, this was no ordinary carriage. It was a flying treasure crafted by the Holy Sword Clan, reserved for journeys to the Holy Sword Temple.

“The carriage will take you to the Holy Sword Temple, which will take two months. Also, just in case you didn’t know, you still have to pass a trial to enter the Holy Sword Temple. Right now, you only have permission to visit the place and partake in the trial,” Li Bowen said.

Yuan nodded.

“I am aware.”

“Alright. Whenever you’re ready.” Li Bowen opened the door and gestured for him to enter.

The moment Yuan stepped into the carriage, he found himself in a surprisingly spacious room, as though he’d entered another world. High-grade flying treasures like the golden carriage were crafted with spatial techniques, allowing their interiors to be far larger than their outward appearance suggested.

“Good luck at the Holy Sword Temple,” Li Bowen said before closing the door.

The golden carriage departed soon after, ascending swiftly into the skies before piercing through the clouds and vanishing into the boundless void in the blink of an eye.

Inside the room, Yuan approached the window and gazed outside. Beyond the glass stretched a vast expanse of darkness, illuminated by countless flickering stars of varying sizes.

Lan Yingying emerged from Yuan’s dantian to look at the starry sky since this was her first time traveling beyond the Nine Heavens.

“So this is what the starry sky looks like up close…” Lan Yingying muttered with a look of awe on her face.

Feng Yuxiang and the others also appeared.

“I find it rather dull,” Feng Yuxiang remarked. “The place is mostly empty, and when you do find something, it’s usually just useless rocks. That said, the starry sky holds some of the rarest and most precious treasures in the Nine Heavens—so there’s still reason to explore it.”

“How does one explore the starry sky?” Lan Yingying asked.

“Well, there are treasures out there that help you find other treasures. However, they are extremely rare and expensive. Other than that… I guess you’ll just have to rely on your luck and wander the place until you find something.”

“What if you get lost?”

“It’s hard to explain, but once you reach a certain cultivation, it’s almost impossible to get lost even in the starry sky. You will instinctively know where you are and where to go. Of course, people still take precautions and have teleporting treasures just in case. Nobody is foolish enough to enter the starry sky without life-saving treasures, either.”

“Life-saving treasures? Is the starry sky that dangerous?” Lan Yingying asked.

“Of course. With no rules in the starry sky and distances too vast to call for help, you can only rely on yourself,” Feng Yuxiang said.

“So what happens if we encounter someone?”

“Well, since we’re in a flying treasure and won’t be venturing too far away from the Nine Heavens, even if we do encounter someone, it is very unlikely that anything will happen. We’re also in the Holy Sword Clan’s signature carriage. I doubt anyone would be foolish enough to attack us.”

Yuan smiled and said, “You’d be surprised at how many bold individuals are out there.”

“Even if someone attacks us, we’ll be fine since you’re here, Young Master.” Feng Yuxiang chuckled.

However, Yuan shook his head and said, “As long as they’re not in the God Ascension realm, I’ll be able to handle them.”

“But you’ve already defeated several God Ascension realm cultivators,” she said.

“No, I only destroyed their physical bodies,” Yuan replied. “Besides, God Ascension realm cultivators have restricted power within the Nine Heavens, just as the lower heavens impose their own cultivation limits. However, that restriction will be lifted once they leave the Nine Heavens.”

“Really? I didn’t know that God Ascension realm cultivators have their strength restricted in the Nine Heavens…” Lan Yingying said with a surprised look on her face.

God Ascension realm cultivators possessed the strength to shatter stars with a mere clench of their fists. If they were free to wield such power within the Nine Heavens, that world would have long since ceased to exist.

“I can only fight God Ascension realm cultivators in the Nine Heavens. Once we leave… even I am powerless against them until I learn Celestial Qi,” said Yuan.

“Is that so…”

Lan Yingying continued asking questions, her curiosity about the starry sky growing with each passing moment as she gazed out the window. It was as if she were instinctively drawn to it, like a moth to a flame.

Time passed swiftly, and before they knew it, nearly two months had gone by.

“Hey… I think I see something…” Lan Yingying, who had spent most of her time gazing at the void, suddenly said.

“What do you see?” Feng Yuxiang asked as she sipped on her tea at the couch.

Lan Yingying narrowed her eyes and murmured uncertainly, “I think… it’s some kind of serpent—a white serpent.”

“What?” Feng Yuxiang raised her eyebrows.

“And I think it’s consuming stars…”

“What?!”

The moment Yuan heard this detail, he stopped cultivating at once and sprang to his feet, rushing to the window.

And sure enough, when he looked outside, he saw a tiny, serpent-like entity drifting through the void. Wherever it passed, the flickering stars vanished—almost as if they were being devoured.

“It can’t be…” Yuan murmured in disbelief.

chapters.title: Chapter 2019: White Serpent In The Starry Sky
chapters.body:
Chapter 2019: White Serpent In The Starry Sky

Chapter 2019: White Serpent in the Starry Sky

Yuan could scarcely believe his eyes as he watched the white serpent drift through the starry sky, devouring stars in its wake.

“Xingrui…” Yuan muttered this name.

It was likely that this entity was the Eater of Stars—one of the Nine Divine Supreme—who had vanished since the Immortal Monarch’s Era. The evidence wasn’t conclusive, but in Yuan’s eyes, there was no doubt.

After all, how many white serpents that could devour stars were out there?

“Xingrui… Isn’t that one of the Nine Divine Supreme?” Feng Yuxiang remarked when she heard this name.

Yuan nodded, “Yes. According to Yeyou, she returned to the starry sky to consume stars after my—the Immortal Monarch’s death.”

Feng Yuxiang set down her tea and walked to the window for a look herself, but by the time she arrived, the white serpent had already drifted so far into the void that it looked no more than a speck of white dust.

“She’s… so far away,” Feng Yuxiang murmured.

Hearing this, Yuan clenched his fists in frustration. Though they were fortunate to have glimpsed the Eater of Stars, that fortune was bittersweet, as there was no realistic way for them to pursue her.

The flying treasure was on autopilot, and even if Yuan could redirect its course to pursue Xingrui, their chances of catching up were nonexistent. After all, Xingrui was a colossal beast capable of devouring multiple stars in a single bite. For her to appear as nothing more than a speck of dust, she had to be light-years away.

Without the proper flying treasure and sufficient time, it was impossible to catch Xingrui.

’It’d be impossible to pursue Xingrui even if I were a God Ascension realm cultivator right now… If I really want to catch up to her, I’ll need to master the power of the void—at least to the point where I can teleport vast distances.’

’Just wait a little longer, Xingrui. I’ll find you again… soon.’ Yuan swore inwardly as he stood by the window, silently watching the tiny white speck fade into the depths of the void.

For the rest of their journey to the Holy Sword Temple, Yuan found himself reminiscing about his time with Xingrui. In the beginning, she had reminded him of Yingzi—aloof, curious, and utterly lacking in common sense. But as time passed, she grew more composed, and her loyalty to him deepened to unfathomable depths.

Eventually…

“Yuan, we’ve arrived at the Holy Sword Temple,” Lan Yingying said, pointing ahead at a massive floating island. At its center stood a towering, majestic structure that resembled a grand temple.

The Holy Sword Temple radiated a brilliant golden light, piercing through the surrounding darkness like a divine treasure suspended in the void.

The golden carriage descended onto the island, landing right in front of the temple gates.

Lan Yingying and the others returned to Yuan’s Dantian before he left the carriage.

“Greetings.”

A middle-aged woman wearing golden robes greeted Yuan when he exited the carriage.

“Hello.”

“I am the Sixth Holy Sword of the Holy Sword Temple, but you may address me as the Sixth Elder. Can I see your referral?”

Yuan nodded and retrieved not just the crimson badge he received from Xu Jiaqi but also the golden badge from the Holy Sword Maiden, handing it to the Sixth Elder.

“This is…”

The Sixth Elder’s eyes widened with surprise when she saw the golden badge.

“So you’ve received the Holy Sword Maiden’s approval… I believe this is her first time handing out her badge to someone,” revealed the Sixth Elder.

“Is that so? I feel honored.”

The Sixth Elder nodded and said, “Just so you know, even with the Holy Sword Maiden’s approval, you must pass a trial before you can enter the Holy Sword Temple.”

“I am aware. What kind of trial is it?”

The Sixth Elder turned to face a specific direction, where a colossal sword hovered silently above a circular platform, resembling that of an execution site.

“To enter the Holy Sword Temple, you must survive the Blade of Tribulation, a Celestial-grade treasure imbued with Enhanced Sword Qi and a hint of Supreme Sword Aura, with nothing but your own Sword Qi. Even if you somehow manage to survive, it still counts as a fail if you allow the Blade of Tribulation to injure your body,” explained the Sixth Elder.

“In other words, using nothing but my Sword Aura, I have to emerge unscathed,” said Yuan.

“That is correct.”

This meant that Yuan couldn’t exploit the trial with his regeneration abilities and had to rely solely on his Enhanced Sword Aura.

“I’m curious… how many people die in this trial?” Yuan asked.

A cold smile tugged at the corners of the Sixth Elder’s lips as she said, “The Holy Sword Temple receives thousands of challengers each year, yet only five percent manage to pass its trial. In other words, for ninety-five percent, it becomes a graveyard. That said, only those who have yet to enter the God Ascension realm perish since they cannot live without a physical body.”

“If I survive but fail the trial, can I try it again?” Yuan then asked.

“Of course, but you’ll have to wait ten years before trying again,” she said.

“Alright. Let’s get this over with.”

Yuan said as he approached the platform.

“Are you sure? Everyone is given a month to prepare,” she stopped him.

He nodded.

“I am sure.”

“If you say so.”

The moment Yuan stepped onto the platform, an overwhelming pressure crashed down upon him, and that was merely the natural aura of the Blade of Tribulation.

Yuan raised his head to gaze at the blade, a mysterious smile forming on his lips. The Holy Sword Clan likely wouldn’t believe him even if he told them, but this wasn’t his first time seeing the Blade of Tribulation. After all, he was the one who forged it when he was the Exalted Blacksmith. In fact, the Blade of Tribulation was crafted during the God of Creation Ceremony.

chapters.title: Chapter 2020: Holy Sword Temple
chapters.body:
Chapter 2020: Holy Sword Temple

Chapter 2020: Holy Sword Temple

“Whenever you’re ready to begin the trial, activate your Sword Aura and use it to protect your body,” said the Sixth Elder after Yuan stepped onto the platform. “The Blade of Tribulation won’t attack unless it senses your Sword Aura.”

Yuan silently nodded in acknowledgement and took a moment to prepare himself. Then, without hesitation, he unleashed as much Enhanced Sword Aura as he could muster.

The Sixth Elder’s eyes widened with awe when she sensed Yuan’s Enhanced Sword Aura.

’No wonder she gave him her sword badge!’ The Sixth Elder finally understood what caused the Holy Sword Maiden to approve of him.

In response to Yuan’s Sword Aura, the Blade of Tribulation began to tremble—then, within moments, it unleashed its own Enhanced Sword Aura.

And then, as if heralding an execution, the blade descended from the sky, hurling itself toward Yuan with terrifying speed.

Since the trial required him to endure the attack using only his Enhanced Sword Aura, Yuan concentrated solely on that. He remained motionless, calmly awaiting the blade’s descent.

Half a second later, the blade reached Yuan, colliding with his Enhanced Sword Aura. Despite its powerful descent, the Blade of Tribulation came to an abrupt halt the moment its tip touched his aura, frozen in midair.

But it didn’t end there, and the Blade of Tribulation suddenly surged with an even greater wave of Enhanced Sword Aura, this time laced with a faint trace of Supreme Sword Aura.

Yet, Yuan remained firm, his Enhanced Sword Aura unwavering.

“How long do I have to resist this sword?” Yuan turned to look at the Sixth Elder.

“When the Blade of Tribulation stops its attack,” she responded.

Even without waiting for the end results, the Sixth Elder could tell that Yuan was going to pass the trial with ease the moment she sensed his Sword Aura. His Enhanced Sword Aura was the most powerful she’d ever seen, and she’d met a lot of swordmasters, including Sword Emperors.

Sure enough, exactly thirty seconds after its descent, the Blade of Tribulation withdrew its Enhanced Sword Aura and silently returned to its place in the sky.

“Congratulations on passing the trial,” said the Sixth Elder. “You are the first mortal in history to have ever succeeded—not that there were any mortals before you who’d attempted it.”

Yuan calmly walked off the platform and said, “So, when can I enter the Holy Sword Temple?”

“Only one person is allowed inside the Holy Sword Temple at a time, and someone is currently training inside,” she explained. “There’s also a waitlist, and…”

She stopped there and retrieved a jade slip to look at the contents inside before continuing, “You are currently 149th on the list.”

Yuan’s jaw dropped after hearing this. He was never told about a waitlist, which could take years if not longer.

Noticing his expression, the Sixth Elder explained, “There’s no strict time limit, but once you step outside, your training is considered complete. Also, there’s no opportunity to rest once you’re inside, so most cultivators only remain in the temple for a few years at a time before coming out to recuperate.”

“That’s too long. I don’t have the time to wait for my turn. Is there nothing that can be done?”

“Unfortunately, that’s just how the Holy Sword Temple was set up. There’s nothing we, the Holy Sword Clan, can do about it, either. However, there is one way to move up the waitlist…”

“How?” Yuan instantly inquired.

“You can try swapping places with the next person on the waitlist. It has happened before, but it’s extremely rare and often involves some sort of payment.”

Yuan rubbed his eyes and muttered, “What a pain in the ass.”

Since most cultivators who trained at the Holy Sword Temple were Immortals, waiting a few hundred years to enter one of the Nine Heavens’ most effective training grounds was a small price to pay. But Yuan didn’t have that kind of time.

The Sixth Elder then continued, “It just so happens that the person who is supposed to go next is already at the Holy Sword Temple. We can talk to them about a potential position swap, and if you want to increase your chances of even having a negotiation, you should make an offer.”

“Who is this individual? Knowing a bit more about them would help me prepare a suitable offer,” Yuan said.

“This person is the Sect Leader of the Sword Descending Pavilion. Unfortunately, I can’t tell you what he wants since we don’t like prying.”

“In that case, can you see if they’re willing to negotiate?”

“We can do that. Give me a few minutes.”

The Sixth Elder flew away the next moment.

While she was away, Yuan pondered what he should do if the negotiations failed—if this Sect Leader even wanted to negotiate at all.

“I really don’t want to leave empty-handed after spending so much time and effort getting here, but there’s really nothing I can do if I have to wait for my turn. It’s not like I can force my way inside, either,” he sighed out loud.

The Sixth Elder returned about fifteen minutes later, and before Yuan could even ask her about the negotiation, she silently shook her head.

“I see…” Yuan sighed.

Was there truly nothing he could do but wait for his turn?

After another sigh, Yuan spoke, “Do you think I can take a look at the Holy Sword Temple before I leave?”

The Sixth Elder nodded, “If it’s just looking around, there’s no harm in that. Follow me.”

Yuan followed the Sixth Elder to the Holy Sword Temple, which was only a few minutes away.

Once he was standing before the majestic Holy Sword Temple, a sudden wave of nostalgia surged within Yuan.

“It’s a pity, but it seems like I am not fated to train here today.”

A few moments later, Yuan turned around and began walking away.

*Rumble*

However, before he could take even two steps, the ground began to tremble, prompting Yuan to turn around—just in time to see the doors of the Holy Sword Temple slowly begin to open.

chapters.title: Chapter 2021: Holy Sword Temple(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2021: Holy Sword Temple(2)

Chapter 2021: Holy Sword Temple(2)

“The door is opening…?” The Sixth Elder mumbled.

However, she didn’t think it had anything to do with Yuan and figured it was just a coincidence—that the person inside was finished with their training.

In the very next moment, the Sixth Elder watched as someone emerged from the Holy Sword Temple—no, they were launched out, as if forcefully ejected.

“What the…?”

The Sixth Elder followed the figure flying through the air with wide eyes.

“Fuck!” the individual shouted as they steadied themselves after being thrown from the Holy Sword Temple.

Once Yuan got a clear look at her, he saw an extraordinarily beautiful woman with shimmering silver hair and radiant jade-green eyes.

Then, she exclaimed in a loud voice, “What the fuck was that about?!”

“Senior…” The Sixth Elder didn’t understand where the woman’s anger came from. It was not uncommon for people to exit the Holy Sword Temple in ridiculous fashions, and she’d seen it many times.

“Th-that doesn’t count!” the woman suddenly said.

“Excuse me…?”

“I said that doesn’t count! I was forcefully ejected from the Holy Sword Temple, so I didn’t come out on my own free will! I’m going back inside!”

“W-Wait! Even if you’re the Sword Empress, you cannot—”

“Shut up! I just told you that I was forcefully removed!” said the woman, who turned out to be a Sword Emperor—a Sword Empress since she was female.

Even after one learns Supreme Sword Aura and becomes a Sword Emperor, the Holy Sword Temple remains an excellent training ground to further improve their Supreme Sword Aura, hence why Sword Emperors continue to return to the place even after they achieve their title.

“But that’s not possible! The Holy Sword Temple doesn’t even have such a function! We’ve been managing the Holy Sword Temple for several eras now, but the only people it has ejected are those who have become corpses! You should know that since you’re also part of our Holy Sword Clan!” The Sixth Elder exclaimed.

Surprisingly, the silver-haired also belonged to the Holy Sword Temple.

’She’s one of the three Sword Emperors in the Holy Sword Clan?’ Yuan thought to himself as he watched the situation unfold.

“I know, but I am telling you that’s what happened! Why would I lie about this? It’s not like this is my first time entering the temple!” said the Sword Empress.

The Sixth Elder was left speechless, but she couldn’t imagine the Sword Empress lying about such a thing, either.

“Unfortunately, regardless of the circumstances, once you leave the Holy Sword Temple, you are not allowed to enter again until your next turn,” she said a moment later.

“Son of a bitch!” The Sword Empress cursed again. “I was so close to reaching the next level of Supreme Sword Aura, too!”

The Sword Empress suddenly snapped her head at Yuan and shouted, “What the fuck are you looking at, huh?!”

“Senior, let’s not take your anger out on a junior… Your reputation is already in the gutters because of your foul mouth,” the Sixth Elder said as she shook her head.

“That’s bullshit! He was staring at me so intensely, so I told him off!”

Yuan couldn’t deny that he had been staring at her, though it was because of her potty mouth that was so out of place for someone of her stature and graceful appearance.

“I apologize. I didn’t mean to stare.” Yuan apologized.

The Sword Empress suddenly narrowed her eyes and asked, “You… have we met before?”

“No, I don’t think so.”

“What’s your name?”

“Yuan.”

The Sword Empress fell silent, seemingly in deep thought.

“Whatever,” she said a moment later.

“I’ll go get the next person on the waitlist. Please don’t do anything rash while I am away,” said the Sixth Elder.

“What? You think I am going to sneak back inside while you leave? You’re mocking me.”

“I didn’t say that.” The Sixth Elder said before leaving.

Instead of leaving, the Sword Empress decided to stay around. Of course, she didn’t have any intention of sneaking inside the Holy Sword Temple.

“Hey.”

The Sword Empress called out Yuan again.

“What is it?”

“You’re quite calm despite being in the presence of a Sword Empress, even though you’re just a mortal. What are you even doing here?”

“I was going to enter the temple to train, but I don’t have the time to wait for my turn.”

“You? Enter the Holy Sword Temple? There’s no way. Don’t lie to me.” The Sword Empress immediately doubted him since she couldn’t fathom a mortal passing the Blade of Tribulation trial.
