“Why would I lie?” Yuan showed her the golden badge given to him by the Holy Sword Maiden.

Her eyes widened with surprise after seeing the badge, and she muttered, “No way…”

She fell silent again, but she was staring at him with a piercing gaze.

“Are you sure we haven’t met before? I can’t shake this feeling that we have,” she said.

Yuan looked at her face and quickly dug through his memories.

“Sorry, but I really don’t think we’ve met before,” he responded a moment later.

The Sword Empress remained silent, but she continued inspecting his appearance from head to toe.

The Sixth Elder returned a few minutes later with a handsome middle-aged man following behind.

“Sword Empress Huang, it’s an honor to meet you today.” The Sect Leader of the Sword Descending Pavilion greeted her the moment he saw her.

The Sword Empress merely nodded in acknowledgment.

The Sect Leader glanced at Yuan but quickly ignored him after seeing that he was a mortal.

“You can enter the Holy Sword Temple whenever you’re ready,” the Sixth Elder said to the Sect Leader a moment later.

The Sect Leader nodded before approaching the massive entrance, quickly disappearing into the temple.

But not even two seconds later, the Sect Leader could be see flying out of the temple, just as the Sword Empress had been ejected minutes earlier.

The Sixth Elder and the Sword Empress’ eyes widened with disbelief after seeing this.

“What?” they both muttered in a daze, unable to comprehend the situation.

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 2022: Holy Sword Temple(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2022: Holy Sword Temple(3)

Chapter 2022: Holy Sword Temple(3)

“What the hell was that?!” The Sect Leader exclaimed with a confused face after stabilizing himself.

“W-What just happened?” The Sixth Elder asked even though she already had an idea of what he was going to say.

“I don’t know. After I entered the temple, I felt a powerful force pulling me back before I even made it to the other side. The next thing I knew, I was outside again,” said the Sect Leader, disbelief etched across his face.

“So exactly what happened to me, but in my case, I was in the middle of training…” said the Sword Empress.

“Hey, that doesn’t count, right? I clearly didn’t even begin my training!” The Sect Leader turned to look at the Sixth Elder, who wore a perplexed expression on her face.

Since this was the first time anything like this had happened at the Holy Sword Temple, she wasn’t sure how to deal with it. However, considering the situation, it wouldn’t be fair for the Sect Leader, who clearly didn’t get any training done, if they didn’t allow him to try again.

“Since this is an unprecedented event, and it’s clear that you didn’t enter the temple properly, I’ll allow you to enter again.”

The Sixth Elder eventually decided to do the unprecedented and allow someone to enter the temple again after they left.

The Sect Leader thanked her before walking toward the entrance again.

However, the Sword Empress stopped him and said, “Wait a moment.”

When the Sect Leader stopped and looked at her with a questioning gaze, she continued, “You have already been rejected by the Holy Sword Temple once. If you try to enter again, there is a chance that it might hurt you instead of just ejecting you.”

The Sect Leader’s body trembled slightly at this possibility.

“Now that I think about this… she’s right.” The Sixth Elder agreed. “This is a highly unusual and unprecedented issue. If you attempt to enter again, we cannot guarantee your safety.”

While countless people have died while training inside the Holy Sword Temple, this was a different situation.

The Sect Leader swallowed nervously. He wouldn’t have any regrets if he died while training inside the Holy Sword Temple, but he’d never be able to die peacefully if he died trying to enter the Holy Sword Temple.

“Then what should I do…?” The Sect Leader asked for their opinion even though he already knew the answer.

“You can either try to enter again or just leave it as it is,” said the Sword Empress.

The Sect Leader gritted his teeth in frustration. He’d waited hundreds of years for this moment, even leaving his sect for a prolonged period of time just to prepare for the Holy Sword Temple. Yet, after all that preparation, does he have to leave empty-handed? He couldn’t accept this result.

He started at the open entrance, which was now emitting an ominous aura, with a serious pondering face.

Eventually, he walked forward and said, “I’ll take the risk.”

The Sixth Elder and the Sword Empress didn’t say anything since they had no authority in his decision, and they’d already warned him of the potential danger.

They all watched as the Sect Leader disappeared into the Holy Sword Temple.

“…”

Time seemed to slow as everyone waited, tense and uncertain, to see if the Sect Leader would be ejected from the Holy Sword Temple once more.

Seconds passed, but nothing happened.

“Did he manage to get inside?” The Sixth Elder questioned out loud.

“Looks like—”

Before the Sword Empress could even finish her sentence, something came out of the Holy Sword Temple.

However, it wasn’t the Sect Leader, but a glowing orb of light.

“Holy fuck…” The Sword Empress subconsciously cursed when she saw this.

The Sect Leader had been ejected once more—but this time, it was only his soul. His physical body was nowhere to be seen. This could mean only one of two things: either his body had been destroyed, or he had been forcefully stripped from it as punishment for defying the Holy Sword Temple’s warning.

“M-My physical body!” The Sect Leader exclaimed in shock after realizing he’d emerged from the Holy Sword Temple as just his soul.

“Sixth Elder! This is unacceptable! How are you going to compensate me for this?!” The Sect Leader’s angry voice rippled throughout the place.

“That’s…”

Although they had warned him of the risks of entering again—and the Holy Sword Temple’s malfunction wasn’t directly their fault—others might not see it that way. After all, the Holy Sword Clan owned and managed the temple, and if anything went wrong, they would be held accountable.

The Sword Empress stepped forward and said, “Listen here, you little bastard. I warned you loud and fucking clear that something like this would happen, but you went ahead and risked it. You’re lucky to have escaped with your life.”

“You—!”

The Sect Leader wanted to say something but was immediately interrupted by the Sword Empress, “However, it’s true that the Holy Sword Temple is malfunctioning, and while we currently have no idea why, we’ll compensate you for the trouble. We’ll pay for the resources for your new physical body and some more, and we’ll also allow you to enter the Holy Sword Temple without needing to wait once we figure out and fix the issue. Of course, that is if you want to come back here in the future.”

“If you’re not satisfied with the compensation, we can continue this at a later date in a more formal setting.”

The Sect Leader remained silent for a moment before responding, “This isn’t over. I’ll contact the Holy Sword Clan again after I deal with my physical body.”

After saying that, the Sect Leader flew away, disappearing into the Starry Sky.

“Fuck… what a pain in the ass…” The Sword Empress cursed out loud after he left, rubbing her eyes in a stressed manner.

“This is indeed quite troublesome… How are we supposed to fix a problem we don’t even understand?” the Sixth Elder sighed.

chapters.title: Chapter 2023: Holy Sword Temple(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2023: Holy Sword Temple(4)

Chapter 2023: Holy Sword Temple(4)

“So, what should we do about the Holy Sword Temple? We don’t know what’s causing it to reject people.” The Sixth Elder looked at the Sword Empress for her thoughts.

The Sword Empress shrugged, “How the hell am I supposed to know? Unless the creator of the temple is here, there’s no way we will know for sure.”

“The God of Creation…? He died several eras ago, so that’s impossible.”

Unbeknownst to them, the individual they were talking about was standing right behind them.

“Looks like we’ll have to shut down the Holy Sword Temple until we figure it out,” said the Sword Empress.

“We’re going to have a lot of complaints.”

“Fuck them.” The Sword Empress calmly cursed. “If they can’t understand why we have to shut the place down, then let them go inside and experience the reason for themselves. Of course, we won’t be compensating for anything.”

“And harm our reputation even more…?” The Sixth Elder shook her head.

The two of them fell silent.

“Umm…” Yuan suddenly spoke out.

“Huh? You’re still here? Get lost already,” the Sword Empress said, waving him off with a dismissive gesture.

He continued, “This might sound crazy, but I’d like to enter the Holy Sword Temple.”

The two ladies turned to look at him with raised eyebrows.

“Are you serious? Even after seeing everything that had happened?” The Sixth Elder remarked with a dumbfounded look on her face.

Yuan nodded, “Yes.”

“Listen here, you dumb brat. The Descending Sword Pavilion’s Sect Leader only survived because he was a God Ascension Realm cultivator and could live without a physical body. If you go inside, you’ll definitely die.”

“Even so, I’d like to try,” Yuan said with a stubborn expression.

The Sword Empress rubbed her eyes and looked at the Sixth Elder, saying, “Can you believe this fucking idiot?”

The Sixth Elder could only shake her head in silence.

“You know what? I don’t know why you’re so eager to die, but it’s none of our business. Go ahead and enter the temple if you want.” The Sword Empress said a moment later.

“Thank you.” Yuan bowed to them and turned to face the temple’s entrance.

“You’re not serious, are you? Please think this through. You are one of the most talented individuals I’ve come across. It’d be a waste to die here.”

While the Sword Empress couldn’t care less if Yuan died, the Sixth Elder, who’d witnessed his talents, couldn’t bear to see him die such a pointless death and decided to block his path.

“Since when were you such a busybody, Sixth Elder? Just let him die.”

“You’re only saying that because you didn’t see his trial. This young man… his Enhanced Sword Aura is likely even stronger than yours,” she responded.

The Sword Empress’ eyes widened with bafflement after hearing such outrageous words.

“Now that is a claim I cannot ignore. You think this stinking mortal brat’s Enhanced Sword Aura is stronger than mine? Have you gone senile from being in this place for too long?”

Who was she? She was the Sword Empress—a swordmaster who had reached the pinnacle of swordsmanship. And yet, the Sixth Elder had the audacity to claim that a mere mortal surpassed her? The notion was utterly absurd.

Before the Sixth Elder could respond, the Sword Empress looked at Yuan and asked him, “What do you think? Do you think your Enhanced Sword Aura is stronger than mine?”

“No,” Yuan answered almost instantly without any hesitation.

The Sword Empress narrowed her eyes at him.

“Your words are telling me one thing, but your eyes are telling me something else,” she eventually said.

Then, without another word, she walked away from the temple and stopped in an open area.

“Come, let me see your Enhanced Sword Aura for myself. If you can defeat me, I’ll let you enter the Holy Sword Temple,” she said.

“…”

Yuan sighed inwardly at how the situation turned out. He only wanted to enter the Holy Sword Temple without any issue, but the Sixth Elder had to open her mouth.

When the Sixth Elder noticed Yuan glaring at her with blame in his eyes, she could only respond with an apologetic smile.

“Are you really going to fight with a mortal? If this spreads, your reputation will truly hit rock bottom,” said the Sixth Elder, trying to stop the farce.

“Who the hell said we’re going to fight? We’re just clashing Enhanced Sword Auras with each other, nothing more, nothing less. Don’t worry, I won’t hurt him.”

Seeing the Sword Empress’ stubborn face, Yuan knew he had no choice but to play along. Since he couldn’t sense any ill intent coming from her, he wasn’t worried that she might try to kill him.

Some time later, the two stood facing each other, separated by a distance of roughly a dozen meters.

“Let’s keep this simple. When I say go, we’ll both release our Enhanced Sword Aura, and the one with the sharpest and strongest sword aura wins.”

Yuan nodded in acknowledgement.

“I’ll give you a few minutes to prepare.”

“No need.” Yuan calmly rejected.

“Well, would you look at that… Feeling confident, are you? Even after saying ’no’ to my question?” the Sword Empress said with an irritated smile, feeling like she was being looked down upon by Yuan, a mere mortal.

She retrieved a spirit stone from her spatial ring and said, “When this touches the ground, release your sword aura.”

Then, she flicked the spirit stone into the air.

Once it dropped to the ground a moment later, both Yuan and the Sword Empress unleashed their Enhanced Sword Aura, instantly flooding the Holy Sword Temple with powerful ripples of energy. Had the place not been constructed from materials resistant to sword aura, their clash would have torn everything apart.

“Son of a fucking bitch…” The Sword Empress cursed under her breath as she compared their Enhanced Sword Auras, only to realize she was slightly inferior to Yuan, causing her face to twist with disbelief.

chapters.title: Chapter 2024: True Enhanced Sword Aura
chapters.body:
Chapter 2024: True Enhanced Sword Aura

Chapter 2024: True Enhanced Sword Aura

“Are you satisfied now?” Yuan asked the Sword Empress once it was clear whose Enhanced Sword Aura was stronger.

The Sword Empress withdrew her sword aura, but she remained silent, seemingly trying to comprehend the situation.

How can a mere mortal have a deeper understanding of sword aura than a Sword Emperor, someone who has hundreds of thousands of years of experience? It just didn’t make sense no matter how she thought about it.

’No… what if he’s a reincarnator who was a Sword Emperor in his past life?’

The Sword Empress thought of this possibility, which made the most sense.

After clicking her tongue, the Sword Empress spoke in a defeated tone, “Fine, you win.”

“Then I’ll be entering the Holy Sword Temple now.” Yuan turned toward the entrance and immediately began walking to it.

“Wait!” The Sword Empress suddenly stopped him.

“What is it now?” Yuan sighed.

Now that she had witnessed Yuan’s talent firsthand, she agreed with the Sixth Elder that letting someone like him throw his life away would be a waste.

“Why are you so desperate? To the point of throwing your life away?” she asked.

“…”

Of course, Yuan had no intention of throwing his life away. He was certain that the Holy Sword Temple’s unusual behavior was connected to him. But it wasn’t something he could reveal—and even if he tried, they wouldn’t believe him.

“I won’t die,” he said.

“What makes you sound so sure?”

“Just a feeling,” he shrugged.

And before they could say anything else, Yuan suddenly sped into the temple, disappearing before their eyes.

“That fool…” The Sword Empress shook her head.

Moments passed, but Yuan wasn’t ejected from the Holy Sword Temple. Seeing this, they believed that Yuan had been killed inside.

“That idiot just wouldn’t listen…” The Sword Empress sighed.

“We can’t be sure he’s dead since there’s no corpse,” said the Sixth Elder.

“The Sword Descending Pavilion’s Sect Leader emerged with just his soul, so the Holy Sword Temple had likely destroyed his physical body. Since Yuan is just a mortal, he wouldn’t be able to survive without his body. It’s a pity that we lost such a talented individual, but there’s nothing we can do. Countless geniuses die every day.”

However, just as the Sword Empress turned around and began walking away, the Sixth Elder suddenly shouted, “Look! The temple’s door has closed!”

“What?”

The Sword Empress halted in her tracks and turned around. And sure enough, the entrance to the Holy Sword Temple had been sealed—an occurrence that only took place when the trial had officially begun.

“No way… he actually managed to make it inside? Then why the hell did it force the Sect Leader and me out?” she muttered in a confused daze.

The Sixth Elder had no response and could only stare at the closed door with a dumbfounded face.

Meanwhile, inside the Holy Sword Temple, Yuan found himself standing in a vast room that had the aesthetic of an ancient temple. However, he wasn’t alone.

At the far end of the room stood another figure—tall and imposing, clad in white and silver robes, with a faceless mask concealing his identity.

“Tian Xin…” Yuan instantly recognized himself.

He had expected some sort of exchange, but without a word, Tian Xin suddenly raised his arm, summoning thousands of blades into the air, each forged from pure Enhanced Sword Aura.

“What the—”

With a swift motion, Tian Xin swung his arm downward, sending the countless blades hurtling toward him like a deadly rain of arrows.

Yuan instantly enveloped himself in Enhanced Sword Aura. Since his sword aura matched Tian Xin’s in strength, the incoming blades wouldn’t slice through him outright, but they would gradually wear down his defense, chipping away at the sword aura protecting his body.

“I see… so the training has already begun.”

Quickly realizing this, Yuan stopped thinking about anything else and immediately began fighting back.

Mirroring Tian Xin’s move, Yuan conjured thousands of blades formed from Enhanced Sword Aura, intercepting the incoming assault. With the barrage deflected, he surged forward, gripping Number One Under Heaven tightly in his hand.

To Yuan’s surprise, Tian Xin drew a sword of his own, and it was also Number One Under Heaven.

With every clash, their Enhanced Sword Auras erupted violently, scattering in all directions like sparks.

Time flew by, and in the blink of an eye, nearly two weeks had passed since Yuan’s battle with Tian Xin began. And yet, neither had managed to gain the upper hand—it was as if they were locked in a perfect stalemate.

This didn’t surprise Yuan, since they were essentially the same person.

However, after another two weeks, at the beginning of their second month fighting, Tian Xin suddenly spoke in a somewhat mocking tone, “You are supposed to grow stronger with every reincarnation, yet you don’t seem any stronger than me. If you want to defeat me, you’ll have to become stronger than all of your incarnations!”

Hearing this, Yuan felt something click inside his head.

’He’s right… I have stopped seriously trying to improve myself after I began regaining my memories…”

He realized that he’d only been recovering his original strength, but he hadn’t been trying to become stronger than that.

Upon realizing this, Yuan stopped fighting with the intention to defeat Tian Xin, but to improve himself.

With tens of millions of years of experience under his belt, Yuan’s level of comprehension was unfathomable.

Soon, Yuan slipped into a trance mid-battle. Though his consciousness went elsewhere, his body moved instinctively, as if it were on autopilot.

As time continued to flow, Yuan’s Enhanced Sword Aura started growing stronger despite having reached the realm of perfection.

Tian Xin smiled behind his mask after seeing this.

After spending an entire year inside the Holy Sword Temple and fighting with Tian Xin nonstop, Yuan’s sword aura had reached a new level. While it wasn’t Supreme Sword Aura, it surpassed all Enhanced Sword Aura and came extremely close to Supreme Sword Aura.

“Let’s just call this… True Enhanced Sword Aura.” Yuan muttered with a satisfied smile on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 2025: True Sword Body
chapters.body:
Chapter 2025: True Sword Body

Chapter 2025: True Sword Body

After attaining True Enhanced Sword Aura, Yuan’s combat prowess surged to a new level. Tian Xin’s perfected Enhanced Sword Aura was no longer a threat, and each clash ended the same, with Yuan’s aura shattering Tian Xin’s like a rock smashing through glass.

Just a few hours after acquiring True Enhanced Sword Aura, Yuan defeated Tian Xin, putting an end to their year-long battle.

Tian Xin sat on the ground with half of his body severed, but since he was just an illusion, there was no blood, and he could still talk.

“Not bad,” muttered Tian Xin.

“However, this is just the beginning. Not only do you still have to learn Supreme Sword Aura, but you must eventually surpass it.”

Suddenly, Tian Xin’s severed body reassembled itself, merging seamlessly back into one.

“And I am here to help you achieve Supreme Sword Aura.”

Once he was back on his feet, Tian Xin’s body surged with Supreme Sword Aura.

“I’m sure you already have the understanding and experience to use Supreme Sword Aura, but you still can’t learn it for some reason, right?”

Yuan nodded.

“Then you just need to let it naturally reveal itself, and the best way to do that is through battle. It’s going to hurt, so brace yourself.”

Tian Xin warned before attacking with Supreme Sword Aura, sending it toward Yuan like a relentless storm.

The moment Tian Xin’s Supreme Sword Aura collided with Yuan’s True Enhanced Sword Aura, a brief struggle ensued—but soon, Yuan’s aura began to erode under the overwhelming force. Had he relied on mere Enhanced Sword Aura, the Supreme Sword Aura would have torn through it instantly, destroying his physical body without resistance.

“How are you going to deal with this?!”

Tian Xin laughed maniacally as he continued his onslaught, his Supreme Sword Aura growing stronger with every passing moment until it eventually was strong enough to destroy Yuan’s True Enhanced Sword Aura instantly.

Unfortunately, there was little Yuan could do but endure the onslaught. Without his regeneration abilities, he would have perished in that very moment. Yet, he persevered, enduring relentless pain for months on end.

After another year of fighting Tian Xin, Yuan improved himself once more. His Sword Body, which had been attained after fusing his Sword Aura into his body, evolved.

<After perfecting fusing your Enhanced Sword Aura with your body, your Sword Body has evolved into Enhanced Sword Body>

After acquiring Enhanced Sword Body, Yuan’s body became untouchable by Enhanced Auras. Of course, since he was up against Supreme Sword Aura, Tian Xin could still damage him.

Half a month later, Yuan improved his Enhanced Sword Body again, this time fusing his True Enhanced Sword Aura with it.

<After perfecting fusing your True Enhanced Sword Aura with your body, your Sword Body has evolved into True Sword Body>

Throughout the history of the Nine Heavens, fewer than a hundred swordmasters have ever attained a Sword Body, most of them being Sword Emperors. Yet, the number of those who have achieved an Enhanced Sword Body is even rarer, numbering fewer than ten. To reach that level, one must not only master but also perfect Enhanced Sword Aura—a feat even more difficult than attaining Supreme Sword Aura

As for the True Sword Body, Yuan had just become the first in history to attain it. No one had ever reached the level of True Enhanced Sword Aura before, as most abandoned their pursuit of Enhanced Sword Aura after achieving the more prestigious Supreme Sword Aura.

Now with the True Sword Body, Tian Xin’s Supreme Sword Aura could only damage his skin and leave scratches.

By now, three years had passed since Yuan parted ways with the others to train in the Holy Sword Temple. Though he had yet to attain Supreme Sword Aura, his overall combat prowess had grown tremendously, and he could feel its manifestation drawing near.

Most cultivators who attain Supreme Sword Aura spend millions of years honing their craft to manifest even a trace of it, and the majority who pursue it never succeed at all, but Yuan was confident that he would acquire it within the next three years.

Empowered by his True Sword Body, Yuan fought Tian Xin with even greater ferocity. Each clash between them shook the entire temple to its core, but because they battled within a separate dimension, the world outside remained undisturbed.

Meanwhile, news of the Holy Sword Temple’s malfunction spread across the Nine Heavens, revealed by the Sect Leader of the Sword Descending Pavilion after his devastating experience. The news left many disheartened and anxious for the future of the sword path, as the vast majority of those who had attained Supreme Sword Aura had done so within the temple’s training grounds.

The Holy Sword Clan, responsible for overseeing the temple, scrambled to find someone capable of analyzing and repairing the issue. Although Yuan had managed to enter without any issue, they didn’t want to risk it, and until he came out, there was no concrete evidence that the temple was safe.

However, the Holy Sword Temple was no ordinary structure but a divine treasure forged by the Exalted Blacksmith, who has long perished. As such, their only option was to seek out the most experienced blacksmiths in the Nine Heavens, that being the current God of Creation.

Time passed, and in the blink of an eye, another year had passed, making it nearly half a decade since Yuan made his way to the Holy Sword Temple.

Inside, he was still fighting Tian Xin. However, Yuan’s sword aura had begun showing signs of Supreme Sword Aura.

After another month of relentless combat, Yuan’s body suddenly became shrouded in an aura so sharp and intense that it seemed ready to slice through space itself.

<You have learned Supreme Sword Aura>

In just four years, three years since he entered the Holy Sword Temple, Yuan had achieved Supreme Sword Aura, something that took the others millions of years to achieve.

If this were made public, it would cause an unprecedented uproar.

chapters.title: Chapter 2026: Supreme Sword Aura
chapters.body:
Chapter 2026: Supreme Sword Aura

Chapter 2026: Supreme Sword Aura

“Supreme Sword Aura… I’ve missed this feeling,” Yuan muttered to himself, savoring the overwhelming power coursing through him.

But he wasn’t content. If he intended to compete in the Sword Emperor Qualifications, his current mastery wouldn’t be enough. At the very least, he needed to gain true control over it.

“Hey, I know you’re far more capable than what you’ve shown me so far.” Yuan suddenly said to him. “Fight me with all you’ve got.”

“With everything I’ve got? At your current level, I could instantly kill you.” Tian Xin mocked.

“I’m not asking you to kill me—just push me to my limits. Make me feel like I’m on the edge of death,” Yuan said, a cold smile curling on his lips.

Tian Xin also smiled behind his mask.

“Very well…” he responded a moment later.

In the very next moment, Tian Xin’s aura erupted, instantly transforming the atmosphere of the training grounds into something grim and suffocating, like death itself had descended. He was no longer holding back as a True Immortal; his cultivation surged into the God Ascension Realm. At the same time, his Supreme Sword Aura intensified, rising to a state of perfection.

Under Tian Xin’s overwhelming pressure, Yuan could barely remain standing. Each breath felt like a struggle, as though an invisible hand was tightening around his throat.

“Now, are you ready for the real training to begin?” Tian Xin spoke in a somewhat sadistic tone, sending chills down Yuan’s spine.

“Ready or not, here I come!”

Without giving Yuan a chance to respond, Tian Xin activated Shadow Veil, vanishing in an instant and reappearing behind him, Number One Under Heaven already piercing through Yuan’s back.

Yuan coughed up a mouthful of blood but quickly steadied himself and swung behind him, only to strike nothing but air. In the blink of an eye, Tian Xin was in front of him, his blade already mid-swing.

Before Yuan could even register what was happening, all of his limbs had been severed.

Tian Xin’s attacks weren’t powered by Supreme Sword Qi alone—they were also reinforced with Celestial Qi. That was why he could so effortlessly tear through Yuan’s body.

For the next several months, Yuan was pushed to the brink of death again and again, enduring countless near-death experiences. However, he was also improving at a rapid rate, and his Supreme Sword Aura became more refined and powerful.

Eventually, after spending nearly six years in the Holy Sword Temple, Yuan perfected his Supreme Sword Aura and even began to merge it with his True Sword Body.

Once he was satisfied with the results of his training, Yuan exited the Holy Sword Temple.

Before departing, Tian Xin handed him a sword. At first glance, it looked like a simple steel blade, but intricate symbols were etched along its surface. Despite its craftsmanship, it didn’t radiate the aura of a treasure. Instead, it seemed more like a unique ornament with hidden meaning.

“Do you know what this is?” Tian Xin asked him.

Yuan pondered for a moment before recalling something, “Yes, I do.”

“Then, do you know where to go?”

“Of course.”

After passing the sword to him, Tian Xin turned around and calmly walked away, disappearing like a ghost after a few steps.

Yuan left the Holy Sword Temple shortly afterward.

Meanwhile, outside the Holy Sword Temple, the Holy Sword Clan had spent the past several years tirelessly inspecting the structure, desperately trying to uncover the source of the problem.

However, since the Holy Sword Temple had shut its door, they were unable to go inside and could only work from the outside.

“Damn it… If only we could go inside the temple… It’s been closed for half a decade by now. When do you think it’ll open—if it’ll even open again?”

“The longest someone has spent inside the temple is about 100 years, but most people tend to leave after a decade or two, earlier if they come out as corpses.”

“But the one who went inside was a mortal, right? I’m surprised he hasn’t come out already.”

“He could already be long dead for all we know.”

“That’s true.”

However, just moments later, the doors to the Holy Sword Temple slowly creaked open, drawing the attention of everyone present. All eyes widened as a handsome man stepped out. Though he had already withdrawn his Supreme Sword Aura, its remnants clung to him—residue from years of continuous use.

Everyone there was a Swordmaster, and the moment they sensed the lingering aura surrounding Yuan, they instinctively swallowed hard and lowered their heads, humbled by the overwhelming presence.

“You’re alive!”

A familiar face suddenly approached him.

“It’s been a while, Sixth Elder,” Yuan said with a gentle smile.

She swallowed hard, her voice trembling as she stammered, “Y-Y-You… you learned Supreme Sword Aura?! In just five years?!”

Even though it was right before her eyes, the Sixth Elder could scarcely believe it. Not only had Yuan achieved Supreme Sword Aura in record time, but he was also likely the first mortal in history to ever do so.

“Looks like it.” Yuan calmly responded.

“Anyway, I’ll be leaving now.”

“W-Wait! Please wait!” The Sixth Elder stopped him.

There was no way she could let him leave just like that.

“Why don’t you join our Holy Sword Clan?” the Sixth Elder said eagerly. “Our Faction Leader isn’t here right now, but if he were, he’d be begging you himself! With your talent, you’re destined to become the greatest Swordmaster in history—perhaps even surpassing the Faceless Sword Emperor!”

“I’m flattered, but I don’t have time to talk about that now. I need to hurry to the Gates of Heaven for the Sword Emperor Qualification,” he calmly responded.

“Sword Emperor Qualification? You’re also going to participate?”

“Of course.”

“That’s perfect since our Faction Leader and the others are currently there to spectate it,” she said. “Come with me.”

Yuan nodded and followed her, leaving the others behind, still staring in a daze and struggling to comprehend what they had just witnessed.

chapters.title: Chapter 2027: Leaving The Holy Sword Temple
chapters.body:
Chapter 2027: Leaving The Holy Sword Temple

Chapter 2027: Leaving the Holy Sword Temple

Yuan followed the Sixth Elder to the same golden carriage that had brought him to the Holy Sword Temple years ago. Once inside, the flying treasure immediately began to move, but it moved at a much quicker pace than before.

“We will arrive directly at the Gates of Heaven in one month, so we have plenty of time to talk.”

Yuan had hoped to visit the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect first, but time was short. Besides, he was fairly certain they would already be at the Gates of Heaven since he had mentioned his intention to enter the Sword Emperor Qualifications, after all.

“You want me to join the Holy Sword Clan, right? While it wasn’t direct, I have already been recruited by the Holy Sword Maiden.”

“In that case… what do you think?”

Most people would leap at the opportunity to join the Holy Sword Clan, so this was the first time the Sixth Elder had encountered someone who didn’t. Yet she wasn’t offended. In fact, with talent like his, she felt he had every right to be picky.

“I said that I’d need some time to see whether the Holy Sword Clan was a place I wanted to be associated with. After all, I don’t want to join a place I know nothing about. What if I accidentally join a faction with a bad reputation? I don’t want that to happen.”

“Then you have nothing to worry about! Our Holy Sword Clan is not only reputable, but we’re also very respected. Besides the recent incident with the Holy Sword Temple, we don’t have any issues.”

Hearing this, Yuan felt a little guilty. The Holy Sword Temple only acted it did because of him, but the Holy Sword Temple took the blame.

“About the Holy Sword Temple… I think everything is functional again. I was able to train inside without any issues, after all,” said Yuan.

The Sixth Elder nodded and said, “I’m glad nothing happened to you, but we can’t assume it’s safe to enter again. We’ll need to conduct a thorough analysis.”

Yuan wanted to tell her it wasn’t necessary, but chose to remain silent, as explaining everything would be too troublesome, and she likely wouldn’t believe him anyway.

Since there was nothing actually wrong with the Holy Sword Temple, they’d find nothing unusual. Most likely, they’d just waste time investigating, only to resume using it in the near future.

“Anyway, I’ll consider joining the Holy Sword Clan, so you don’t need to try to convince me,” he then said.

“If you say so…” Not wanting to annoy him, the Sixth Elder stopped mentioning it and closed her eyes to cultivate.

Yuan spent the following month by the window, gazing into the distance each day in hopes of seeing the Eater of Stars again, but alas, she never appeared.

A month later, they arrived at the Gates of Heaven in the Seventh Heaven. Since most cultivators from the Eighth and Ninth Heavens possessed treasures that allowed them to spectate from afar and had the resources to travel downward, hosting the Sword Emperor Qualifications in the Seventh Heaven ensured the greatest possible audience.

The Gates of Heaven’s territory spanned thousands of miles, making it one of the most prominent sects in the Seventh Heaven, with a disciple count exceeding one hundred thousand. Much of its popularity stemmed from its vast and unparalleled collection of cultivation techniques, and unlike many others, the sect was generous in sharing them with its disciples.

The sect’s leader, Bai Ling, was among the most respected cultivators in the Nine Heavens—hailed as a hero by many and regarded as a role model throughout the cultivation world.

His contributions to the Nine Heavens were nearly unparalleled. Beyond making techniques affordable through the Myriad of Techniques, Bai Ling was renowned for aiding anyone in need, regardless of their status or background.

Despite holding only the rank of Overlord within the Celestial Overlords, Bai Ling’s contributions were unmatched, even surpassing those of the Celestial Overlords themselves.

Of course, if he wished, he could easily claim the title, having been offered the position multiple times. Yet, for reasons unknown, he had declined each offer and remained as Xu Jiaqi’s closest aide.

Thanks to Bai Ling’s esteemed reputation, news that the next Sword Emperor Qualifications would be held at the Gates of Heaven quickly drew the attention of countless prominent figures across the Nine Heavens—many of whom chose to attend in person. Naturally, as a once-in-100,000-year event, the Sword Emperor Qualifications already commanded immense attention, drawing an endless sea of spectators from across the world.

Even before reaching the Gates of Heaven, Yuan could see thousands of cultivators converging from all directions, almost as if there was a massive banquet.

“We’re here.” The Sixth Elder said as she walked to the door.

“Give me a moment.”

Yuan retrieved his mask and wore it.

The Sixth Elder raised her eyebrows after seeing this, and she couldn’t help but ask, “What’s with the mask?”

“There are too many people here, and I don’t like having too much attention,” he calmly responded.

“…”

The Sixth Elder remained silent, her gaze fixed on Yuan, his appearance strikingly reminiscent of the Faceless Sword Emperor.

Unbeknownst to Yuan, all who trained in the Holy Sword Temple had to face Tian Xin in battle, so they were very familiar with his appearance.

“Did you sign up for the event yet?” she suddenly asked.

“No, I haven’t. Is it too late?”

“No, you can still sign up now. Most people sign up a few days before the qualifications begin, anyway. Come with me. I’ll take you there.”

Yuan nodded.

After stepping out of the golden carriage, Yuan followed the Sixth Elder to the sect’s entrance, where hundreds of people were streaming in through the gates.

While the crowd made their way to the spectators’ area, the Sixth Elder led Yuan to the inner court of the sect, a far quieter place, as guests were only permitted to roam the outer court.

chapters.title: Chapter 2028: You Better Remember My Name!
chapters.body:
Chapter 2028: You Better Remember My Name!

Chapter 2028: You Better Remember My Name!

When they arrived at their destination, Yuan noticed a few familiar faces, all standing before a fist-sized red crystal that floated on a circular platform.

The first familiar face was Li Bowen from the Holy Sword Clan, and then there was the potty-mouthed Sword Empress. The other familiar faces were obviously the Holy Sword Maiden and the Holy Sword King.

Right as their arrival, Yuan could see a handsome man standing before the red crystal with a sword in his grasp and his body gushing with Supreme Sword Aura.

“You may begin,” said a middle-aged man clad in the Gates of Heaven’s Sect Elder uniform.

Then, the handsome man swung his sword at the floating red crystal, striking it with his Supreme Sword Aura.

However, the red crystal remained undamaged, emitting a faint red glow the next moment.

“I’m sorry, but your Supreme Sword Aura is not strong enough to participate,” said the Sect Elder.

“Damn it!” The handsome man clenched his teeth in anger and frustration.

“Hahaha!” The Holy Sword King suddenly laughed as he stepped forward.

“Let me show you how it’s done!”

He retrieved his sword and immediately surged with Supreme Sword Aura that was noticeably much stronger than the man’s.

Once the Sect Elder nodded, the Holy Sword King swung at the red crystal, causing it to emit a bright crimson light.

“Holy Sword King of the Holy Sword Clan, qualified,” said the Sect Elder.

The man sucked his teeth in annoyance at the Holy Sword King but didn’t say anything since the Holy Sword King was clearly stronger than him.

After the Holy Sword King, the Holy Sword Maiden stepped forward and repeated the same thing, striking the red crystal with her Supreme Sword Aura.

The red crystal radiated a brilliant crimson light, comparable to that of the Holy Sword King, and perhaps even subtly stronger.

“Holy Sword Maiden of the Holy Sword Clan, qualified.”

“Senior Huang, what do you think? Do you think we can become Sword Emperors?” The Holy Sword King asked the Sword Empress.

She snorted and said, “Just because you qualify to participate doesn’t mean you’ll qualify as a Sword Emperor. However, judging by your results, I think you’ll have a good chance of—”

The Sword Empress abruptly cut herself off when two figures caught her eye from the corner of her vision. Normally, such a sight wouldn’t warrant her attention, but one was the Sixth Elder, and the other wore a mask.

Ignoring the Sixth Elder, the Sword Empress focused her attention on Yuan, her eyes wide with disbelief.

“Faceless Sword Emperor?” she muttered in a daze.

“What?” The others followed her gaze.

Since everyone who trained inside the Holy Sword Temple had fought the Faceless Sword Emperor, they were all familiar with his appearance.

“No way…”

The Holy Sword Maiden and the others were just as stunned as the Sword Empress.

After all, Yuan didn’t just resemble the Faceless Sword Emperor in stature—he was, in almost every way, his mirror image.

Yuan stepped up and asked the Sect Elder, “I just need to hit this crystal with my Supreme Sword Aura, right?”

“Yes, but you’re…” The Sect Elder looked at Yuan with raised eyebrows because of his low cultivation.

However, Yuan ignored him.

Without even drawing a sword, he simply extended a finger toward the crystal before channeling a vast amount of Supreme Sword Aura, all concentrated at his fingertip.

The Holy Sword Maiden and the Holy Sword King immediately frowned as they sensed his Supreme Sword Aura, as it was far superior to their own.

A moment later, Yuan unleashed his Supreme Sword Aura, directing it straight at the red crystal.

The crystal flared with a brilliant crimson light for a brief second, then abruptly dimmed as cracks began to spread across its surface.

“Ah?!” The Sect Elder exclaimed in a shocked and nervous voice when he saw the cracks.

The red crystal was renowned as the strongest and most durable material in the Nine Heavens when it came to withstanding sword auras, and only Sword Emperors would be able to damage it. Yet, Yuan had cracked it with such ease.

“Sorry, I didn’t think it would be so fragile,” Yuan quickly apologized to the Sect Elder, who was speechless.

“Wait a second… that voice and cultivation!” The Sword Empress suddenly realized something and approached Yuan.

However, as she stood before him, she began doubting herself.

“No… it can’t be you…” she muttered.

She thought he was Yuan, but he’d entered the Holy Sword Temple a few years ago. There was simply no way he could’ve learned Supreme Sword Aura and honed it to such a terrifying degree in such a short amount of time.

“It is him.” The Sixth Elder suddenly spoke.

The Sword Empress turned to look at her with wide eyes.

“He came out of the Holy Sword Temple a month ago, and we came straight here because he wanted to participate in the Sword Emperor Qualifications,” she continued.

After hearing this, the Sword Empress turned to look at Yuan again.

Then, she spoke in a somewhat trembling voice, “I had expected you to be a True Reincarnator when I saw your talents at the Holy Sword Temple, and you’ve just confirmed it. If I had to gamble, I’d bet my entire fortune that you were the Faceless Sword Emperor in your past life.”

“Him?! A True Reincarnator?!” The others exclaimed, their eyes wide with shock and disbelief.

Yuan smiled behind his mask and said, “You’re giving me too much credit. I’m flattered.”

“Hmph!” A cold snort suddenly resounded, and the Holy Sword King stepped forward.

“Even if you’re the Faceless Sword Emperor’s reincarnation, I won’t lose to you! The Sword Emperor Qualifications are much more than just the strength of our Supreme Sword Aura!”

“…”

After staring at him for a second, Yuan pretended not to know him and said, “Sorry, but who are you?”

The Holy Sword King trembled in anger before responding in a loud voice, “I am the Holy Sword King, Zhang Wu Bing, the man who will defeat you! You better remember my name!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2029: A Month Delay
chapters.body:
Chapter 2029: A Month Delay

Chapter 2029: A Month Delay

“You’ll defeat me? I’ll look forward to it,” Yuan calmly responded to the Holy Sword King’s aggression.

The Sword Empress merely shook her head in silence. Although she wanted to smack some sense into the Holy Sword King’s head, there was also no guarantee that Yuan would win the Sword Emperor Qualifications just because his Supreme Sword Aura was stronger.

The qualifications tested more than just the strength of one’s Supreme Sword Aura. It also evaluated the swordmaster’s skill, versatility, and overall mastery of their swordsmanship. After all, becoming a Sword Emperor demanded more than raw power, and only the most refined and knowledgeable swordmasters could earn such a prestigious title.

The Holy Sword Maiden silently watched the events unfold before her, her eyes staring at Yuan, feeling like she’d met him before, but she couldn’t remember when. Of course, she had no idea that the man before her was Yuan, the person she’d given her sword badge to.

“Take this.” The Sect Elder approached Yuan and handed him a medallion. “This proves you’re qualified, and you’ll need it during the Sword Emperor Qualifications.”

“Thank you. And about the crystal…”

The Sect Elder turned to look at the cracked red crystal and sighed, “We’ll have to delay the Sword Emperor Qualifications until we receive another one.”

“Delay?! How long will it be delayed?!” The Sword Empress asked.

“A month at most.”

“Sorry…” Yuan apologized.

“Anyway, I have someplace to be, so I’ll talk to you guys later.”

And without waiting for their response, he turned around and flew away.

“Senior Huang… who was that man?” The Holy Sword Maiden turned to ask the Sword Empress shortly after.

“Hm? You don’t know?” The Sword Empress looked at her with raised eyebrows.

“Should I know him…?”

“Of course. Since you gave him your sword badge.”

The Holy Sword Maiden’s eyes widened with shock after hearing this, and she finally realized who the masked figure was.

“That was him?!” she exclaimed.

The Holy Sword King didn’t say anything, but he was looking at the Holy Sword Maiden with a slight frown on his face, as he couldn’t remember the last time she seemed so engaged.

Though the Holy Sword King never openly admitted it, those close to him had long suspected his affection for the Holy Sword Maiden. Since their parents were both Sword Emperors in the Holy Sword Clan, the two had grown up together.

Unfortunately, the Holy Sword Maiden did not seem to have the same affection for him, for from the moment of her birth, the only thing she had ever pursued was the way of the sword.

Because of this, the Holy Sword King grew even more agitated and resentful, especially now that she was showing interest in some unknown figure she’d barely even met.

Meanwhile, Yuan wandered through the Gates of Heaven until he came across a disciple he could approach for directions.

“Excuse me, do you know where I can find Senior Bai, your Sect Leader?”

“Sect Leader Bai? He’s not someone you can meet just because you want to,” the disciple said, eyeing Yuan from head to toe. To him, Yuan looked like just another mortal wasting his time.

And before Yuan could say anything else, the disciple flew away.

Yuan wasn’t bothered by the encounter and immediately went to find someone else.

Eventually, he found someone who was willing to help.

“The Sect Leader is currently greeting guests, so he could be anywhere. But if you really want to meet him, you’ll have a higher chance of meeting him at the venue.”

“Which direction is the venue?”

The disciple pointed in a specific direction, and Yuan followed it after thanking the disciple.

Some time later, Yuan arrived at the venue where the Sword Emperor Qualifications would take place.

The venue was nothing like Yuan had imagined. Hundreds of smaller islands floated around a massive central island. The smaller islands were packed with spectator seating, while the central island featured a vast platform surrounded by several imposing doors. Above it all, enormous projections hovered in the air, serving as viewing screens for the crowd.

Though the event was still months away, tens of millions had already gathered, a testament to the event’s significance and popularity.

Upon his arrival, Yuan immediately used his divine sense to sweep the place to see if he could find Senior Bai or anyone that he recognized.

Very quickly, he found Meixiu and the others since they were all grouped together and sat in a private area reserved for Senior Bai’s close friends.

After finding them, Yuan immediately approached them.

“Hey, everyone.”

Yuan greeted them as he landed before them.

“Yuan!”

They immediately recognized him despite his mask.

“You… you’re back already?” Kelan, who was also there, muttered in a dumbfounded voice.

He silently nodded.

“Does that mean you’ve learned Supreme Sword Aura?” Li Jinxi asked.

“There’s no way you learned Supreme Sword Aura in under a decade!” Kelan exclaimed, his voice laced with disbelief.

In response, Yuan calmly raised his hand and extended his middle finger, sheathed in a brilliant layer of Supreme Sword Aura.

Li Jinxi and the others chuckled at his response.

Kelan fell to his knees with his mouth wide open.

Yuan patted his shoulder and said, “I’m looking forward to checking out your treasury.”

Kelan sighed deeply. “Why did I ever agree to bet with you? I should’ve stood my ground and said no.”

He stood up and continued, “Are you really going to participate in the Sword Emperor Qualifications, though? Even though you’ve learned Supreme Sword Aura, you just learned it.”

“I’ve already qualified to enter the event,” said Yuan.

“Unbelievable…”

“Also, the Sword Emperor Qualification will be delayed for a month,” he added.

“Delay? Why?”

“Because I broke the thing needed to verify during my verification,” he explained.

“…”

“Why am I not surprised?” Kelan shook his head after a moment of silence.

“At least this will give me some time to spend with you guys before the event begins,” Yuan chuckled.

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 2030: Sword Emperor Qualifications
chapters.body:
Chapter 2030: Sword Emperor Qualifications

Chapter 2030: Sword Emperor Qualifications

“I’ll let Overlord Bai know that you’re here,” Kelan said as he sent a message to Senior Bai through his communication jade slip.

Meanwhile, Yuan began recalling his experience at the Holy Sword Clan and the Holy Sword Temple. The others didn’t have much to talk about since they spent all of their time training.

“Although we’re spending all of our time training now, we’ll be leaving the sect to train outside and gain real experience soon,” said Wang Ming.

Kelan nodded and said, “Don’t worry, I’ll make sure to have some experts accompany them.”

“I’m not worried about them,” Yuan said with a smile, and he continued, “But I am worried about the trouble they might cause.”

“You’re one to talk!” Shi Lang immediately said.

“Right? You’re always getting into trouble, Yuan. At least that’s what it seems like from the stories you tell us,” said Wang Bingbing.

“As much as I love you, I have to agree with the others.” Chu Liuxiang shook her head with a smile on her face.

Meixiu quietly nodded her head.

Seeing this, Yuan burst out laughing.

“You’re right, but it’s not like I am purposefully looking for trouble. They just somehow come to me.”

Some time later, a familiar figure approached from a distance, descending gracefully and landing right in front of them.

“This makes it our first time seeing each other outside of the Myriad of Techniques,” Senior Bai said to Yuan with a warm smile on his face.

Yuan returned the smile and said, “Yes, it does. Thank you for always taking care of me.”

“Since you’re here… does that mean you’ve learned Supreme Sword Aura?” Senior Bai then asked.

Kelan had only mentioned Yuan’s arrival and nothing else, so Senior Bai could experience the same surprise he’d felt.

Yuan nodded.

“Yes, I have already qualified for the Sword Emperor Qualifications.”

He revealed the medallion he received from the Sect Elder.

Senior Bai chuckled and said, “I heard. I also know that you broke the crystal, causing us to delay the event for a whole month.”

“Sorry…”

“It’s fine.”

Yuan then asked, “Is Sister Xu here?”

Senior Bai shook his head.

“No, she’s too busy to come down to the Seventh Heaven, but she’ll be watching it.”

“I see…”

“Anyway, I have to return to work now. Let’s talk again later. If you need anything, just ask Kelan,” said Senior Bai before flying away.

Shortly after he left, Senior Bai made a quick announcement to the people there.

“Due to some unexpected circumstances, we have to delay the Sword Emperor Qualification for a month. We apologize for the delay and any inconvenience this may have caused and hope for your understanding.”

Surprisingly, there were no complaints from the audience, or there were so few that it was unnoticeable.

“The crowd is more calm than I had expected,” Wang Ming commented, as he’d anticipated some complaints.”The Sword Emperor Qualifications can easily last several years, so everyone here is already prepared for a long stay. An extra month is nothing,” Kelan said.

“Several years?! What about our training?” Xi Murong exclaimed in shock, as he’d only anticipated spending a few weeks or months at most at this event.

Kelan shrugged and said, “You’re free to leave at any time you want. The whole thing will be recorded anyway, so you won’t miss anything but the experience.”

“…”

A few years may seem like nothing to Immortals or those who have already lived thousands of years. However, for Xi Murong and the others who have only lived for a few decades, a few years was a lot of time, and they were still at a point where their progress could be seen quickly.

“You don’t have to force yourself to stay here,” Yuan suddenly said. “Just stay as long as you want.”

“I’m staying for the entire event,” Chu Liuxiang said firmly. “A few years mean nothing in the grand scheme of things—but missing such a pivotal moment in my partner’s life? That’s unthinkable.”

“Me too,” Meixiu added. “But you don’t have to force yourself to stay because we choose to stay. We all have different priorities, after all.”

“I guess we’ll see how things go,” said Wu Zao. “If it’s entertaining enough, I’ll stay. Otherwise, I’ll just return to my training.”

“Me too,” said Hong Xiuquan.

“You can’t even take a small break? And you call me a training addict?” Li Jinxi shook her head.

Wang Bing chuckled and said, “We’re all training addicts.”

In the end, everyone but Li Jinxi, Meixiu, and Chu Liuxiang decided to watch until they couldn’t anymore.

Time passed, and two weeks later, while Yuan was chatting with everyone, a figure descended onto their area.

Kelan raised an eyebrow after seeing this individual, and he asked, “What are you doing here, Sword Empress Huang?”

“I don’t have business with you,” she said before pointing to Yuan.

“Since we have some time, let’s spar.”

“No.” Yuan immediately refused, which took her by surprise.

“W-Why not?!”

“Why should I? The Sword Emperor Qualification is about to begin, and you want me to waste my energy sparring with you? If you’re trying to help those two, don’t bother.” Yuan shook his head.

“What? Are you talking about the Holy Sword Maiden and the Holy Sword King? I’m not asking for a spar because I want to help those two. I just want to experience your Supreme Sword Aura myself,” she defended herself.

However, Yuan refused again, “If you want to spar, we can do it after I become a Sword Emperor.”

Sword Empress Huang’s eyes widened.

“You speak as if you’ve already won. Do you really think the Sword Emperor Qualification is that easy?”

“I hope not, or it wouldn’t be fun.”

“Fun…? You think this is a game?” Sword Empress Huang frowned, clearly offended by his words.

For the rare few swordmasters granted the chance to participate, the Sword Emperor Qualifications represented the pinnacle of their lives—an opportunity to carve their names into history. Yet Yuan spoke of it as if it were nothing more than a casual pastime.

Any swordmaster would have taken offense, as this was a sacred trial, not something to be taken lightly or treated as entertainment.

chapters.title: Chapter 2031: Nine Heavens’ Ranking
chapters.body:
Chapter 2031: Nine Heavens’ Ranking

Chapter 2031: Nine Heavens’ Ranking

“I apologize if my words offended you, but I can assure you that I don’t think of the Sword Emperor Qualification as a game,” said Yuan.

“Hmph.” The Sword Empress snorted. “Whatever, I’ve lost my desire to spar now.”

Without saying another word, she turned around and flew away.

“That was a Sword Emperor?” Wang Ming asked, his voice tinged with nervousness, sounding like he’d just witnessed something beyond comprehension.

Being a God Ascension realm cultivator and one of the strongest Swordmasters in the Nine Heavens, the Sword Empress naturally exuded an overwhelming presence that would make any Immortal timid, much less a mortal like Wang Ming.

“I’m surprised you can speak with her so casually… I wouldn’t even be able to respond properly…” Wu Zao remarked.

Kelan smiled and said, “Although she’s known for having a foul mouth, her abilities are undeniable and within the top 20 in the entire Nine Heavens.”

“Top 20? Then where do you rank?” Shi Lang asked, and everyone there stared at Kelan.

“Hahaha! There’s no need to look at me. I don’t even rank in the top 1,000, much less the top 20! However, if we’re talking about physiques alone, I am definitely top 10 in the Nine Heavens!” he proudly boasted.

“What?! You’re that strong and can’t even reach the top 1,000? That’s hard to believe,” Hong Xiuquan exclaimed.

“You have no idea how vast and scary the Nine Heavens can be. There are countless geniuses, and many monsters are out there. Even someone like Senior Bai doesn’t rank in the top 20,” said Kelan.

“I wonder where someone like Yuan ranks,” Wang Bingbing wondered out loud.

“Yuan?”

Kelan turned to look at him with a pondering face.

After a moment of serious thinking, he spoke, “Although he may have the power to fight God Ascension cultivators, that is limited only to the Nine Heavens, where our powers are limited by the Heavens. He wouldn’t last more than a minute against them outside, where most of the newer powerhouses make their territory.”

Due to the restrictions within the Nine Heavens, most powerful families and factions relocated their headquarters to the Starry Sky, where such limitations did not exist, allowing God Ascension cultivators to wield their full strength when necessary. Only those with deep, ancient roots—such as the Royal Dragon Clans—chose to remain in the Nine Heavens, enduring the drawbacks out of legacy and pride.

“That said, he’s definitely in the top 1,000 as long as he’s within the Nine Heavens, and this is overall, where most are monsters with millions of years of experience. If we consider his age, then he’s definitely in the top 10 within the young generation ranking.”

“What do you consider the ’younger generation’?” Li Jinxi asked.

“Anyone under a hundred thousand years old,” said Kelan.

“A hundred thousand years is young…?” Wang Ming muttered in a dumbfounded voice.

“There’s no way Yuan is only top 10. He’s definitely top 3 if not number one,” Chu Liuxiang remarked with certainty.

“I know Yuan is immensely talented, but you shouldn’t underestimate others. For example, the Holy Sword Maiden, who will also be participating in the Sword Emperor Qualifications, is currently ranked 9th in the Nine Heavens. As for the current top three, they’re all monsters in the Ninth Heaven.”

“Who are the top three?” Wang Xiuying asked.

“Currently ranked third is the Bloodthirsty Monk of the Hell Temple, though the rankings shift every few years as the rest of the top ten close the gap. The top two are no different—first and second place frequently trade positions. As of now, second belongs to the Holy Maiden of the Divine Essence Monastery, while first is held by the Heavenly Son of the Tian Family,” Kelan explained.

“The Hell Temple is an elite sect within the Ninth Heaven, and the Divine Essence Monastery is currently the second most powerful sect in the Nine Heavens. As for the Tian Family, they’re a powerhouse in the Ninth Heaven, known for creating countless geniuses. In fact, they’re only right behind the Celestial Emperor’s family in terms of influence.”

“Divine Essence Monastery…” Yuan muttered as he found this name familiar for some reason.

Then he remembered that his Soul Cultivation Technique had been given by Lady Xiang, who was from there.

“Do you know Sister Biyu from the Divine Essence Monastery?” Yuan suddenly asked Kelan.

“Sister Biyu?” Kelan raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner for a second before he realized who Yuan was talking about.

“There’s no way you’re talking about the Holy Empress…” he said.

“Last I spoke to her, she called herself the Holy Maiden.”

“So you are talking about the Holy Empress! Holy Empress Xiang, also known as Xiang Biyu, is the current leader of the Divine Essence Monastery. She acquired that title about a decade ago,” explained Kelan.

He then asked, “How did you meet someone like her…? She rarely leaves the Divine Essence Monastery. Even I have only ever seen her once. And for you to address her as Sister Biyu… you must be quite close with her.”

“I met her through Sister Xu, but that’s all I can say,” said Yuan.

“So, who’s the current Holy Maiden?” he then asked.

“That would be Azure.”

“What? Azure?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at this name.

“Oh, her real name is Wei Lan, but she prefers to be called Azure,” he explained. “Why? Do you know her, too?”

“No… but her name reminds me of someone I once knew,” he responded, a hint of regret and sorrow in his tone.

Though Kelan couldn’t understand it, the others knew who Yuan was referring to.

“How does the ranking work, anyway?” Xi Meili suddenly asked. “Do they fight for it or something?”

Kelan explained, “Yes and no. With one exception, everyone in the top 10 has fought with each other, and the Heavenly Son came out on top after defeating the others. The only exception is Azure, who has never fought with anyone. However, she did reveal her prowess once, and that was enough to convince everyone that she could rival the Heavenly Son, hence her rank.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2032: Heavenly Son
chapters.body:
Chapter 2032: Heavenly Son

Chapter 2032: Heavenly Son

“Heavens… she managed to reach the top 2 just by showcasing her prowess? I cannot imagine how powerful she must’ve been,” Wang Ming commented.

“What about Cultivation Gods and entities like the Great One?” Yuan then asked.

Kelan chuckled and said, “Cultivation Gods? You can count the number of them with one hand. They’re too powerful to be in the ranking. The same goes for the Great One. The ranking is mostly for those within the God Ascension realm, after all.”

“What about this Heavenly Son guy? How powerful is he?” Shi Lang suddenly asked.

“Well, he’s not ranked first in the younger generation for no reason. If you want to see his prowess, you’ll be able to do so soon, since he’s also participating in the Sword Emperor Qualifications.”

“Really? Now this is interesting…” Shi Lang remarked as he turned to look at Yuan.

The others knew what Shi Lang was thinking about and smiled, as they all thought the same.

“What are you looking at? It’s not like we’ll fight each other in the Sword Emperor Qualifications,” Yuan said.

“No, but you’ll be fighting each other indirectly for the ranking. Since more than one person can qualify for the title of Sword Emperor, there’ll be a ranking for your achievements. If you rank first, you’ll be rewarded with something at the end.”

“What? More than one person can qualify as Sword Emperor?” Wu Zao said.

The others were just as surprised since this was their first time hearing about it.

“Anyone who passes the trials will qualify. Why do you think there are many Sword Emperors running around the Nine Heavens?”

“So being a Sword Emperor isn’t anything too special…” Wang Ming shook his head. “What about a Sword God? Surely, there must be only one of them out there.”

“Sword God?” Kelan laughed out loud before responding, “There’s no such thing.”

“What? There isn’t? That doesn’t make any sense. Why can’t the strongest Sword Emperor just become the Sword God or something?” Wang Bingbing asked, clearly confused.

“Well, that’s because it would get far too bloody,” Kelan replied. “There was once a Sword Emperor who declared himself the Sword God, and the moment he did, the other Sword Emperors challenged him. He was killed, and the one who defeated him then tried to claim the title for himself, only to be killed as well. It kept happening, one after another, until several Sword Emperors had fallen. Since then, no one’s dared to call themselves the Sword God.”

“So they’re scared of being attacked by other Sword Emperors? A true Sword God wouldn’t care about being challenged because they know they’re the strongest.”

“You are right, but nobody has ever reached that point…” Kelan paused for a moment before continuing, “Actually, there is one person who might’ve qualified for that title.”

“The Faceless Sword Emperor,” Li Jinxi said.

Kelan nodded, “That’s right. The Faceless Sword Emperor was known to be undefeated in history, so he would’ve probably been able to claim that title and get away with it, but he disappeared somewhere and hasn’t been seen since then.”

“Who knows? Maybe someone like the Faceless Sword Emperor will appear again,” Li Jinxi said with a mysterious smile.

“If you’re talking about Yuan, while I don’t doubt his talents, he’s far from the Faceless Sword Emperor’s level.” Kelan shook his head.

Li Jinxi shrugged.

They continued chatting about the rankings and about some of the most powerful individuals in the Nine Heavens.

A few hours later, Kelan stopped talking when he noticed a powerful presence approaching in their direction.

“Speak of the devil…” he muttered.

Moments later, a handsome young man clad in golden robes landed before them, his sharp gaze scanning everyone there until it landed on Yuan.

“You must be the one who broke the crystal,” said the handsome young man.

“Yes, and who are you?”

“You don’t know who I am? That’s impossible,” the young man said, his tone dripping with disbelief, as if he were certain the entire world knew his name.

“That’s the Heavenly Son, Tian Juexing,” Kelan revealed the handsome young man’s identity.

“So you’re ranked first in the younger generation, huh? We were just talking about you,” Yuan said. “What’s a bigshot like you want from me?”

“So you do know me,” said Tian Juexing. “I came here after hearing about the reason for the delay. Even I couldn’t crack the crystal, so I had to see for myself the kind of person who did.”

“Is that so? Well, what do you think?” Yuan asked as he spread his arms, almost as if telling him to take a good look.

“Honestly? I’m disappointed. You’re nothing more than a wannabe Faceless Sword Emperor with that appearance. That crystal probably cracked because I already weakened it.”

Yuan smiled behind his mask and said, “I could say the same about you. I had some high expectations after Kelan boasted about you, but you’re just some egotistic brat.”

Despite Yuan’s rude remark, Tian Juexing did not seem offended.

“I get that often, but so what? This is the norm for the truly strong. You wouldn’t understand it.”

“If you’re so strong, you will definitely rank higher than me in the Sword Emperor Qualifications, right?”

“Without a doubt.” Tian Juexing answered without even the slightest hesitation.

“Wanna bet on it?”

Tian Juexing narrowed his eyes and pondered for a moment before retrieving a medallion.

“If you somehow rank higher than me, I, Tian Juexing, will accept any one request from you as long as it’s not unreasonable.”

“A request?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“Are you looking down on my bet? My family is literally only second to the Celestial Emperor’s family in the Nine Heavens. There’s almost nothing I cannot do for you,” he said.

“In that case, I’ll bet—”

Tian Juexing suddenly raised his hand and interrupted, “You don’t need to bet anything since there’s nothing I want, nor is there anything that you can possibly do for me.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2033: Sword Emperor Qualifications Begins!
chapters.body:
Chapter 2033: Sword Emperor Qualifications Begins!

Chapter 2033: Sword Emperor Qualifications Begins!

“If you say so,” Yuan calmly responded to Tian Yuexing’s words.

“Then I’ll see you during the Sword Emperor Qualifications,” said Tian Yuexing before flying away and disappearing in the blink of an eye.

“My god, I can believe you actually picked a fight with the Heavenly Son…” Kelan said with a dumbfounded look on his face.

Despite his status within the Celestial Overlords, even he didn’t have the guts to offend someone like Tian Yuexing.

“You probably don’t know how horrifying the Tian Family truly is… they’re so bold that they’re even willing to pick a fight with the Celestial Emperor,” he added.

“Oh? Why did they fight with the Celestial Emperor?” Yuan asked, clearly intrigued by this information.

“I don’t know all the details, but it happened just a few decades ago,” Kelan said. “The two families have always been at odds since ancient times, but something recently pushed the Tian Family over the edge. Whatever it was, it enraged them so much that both their Patriarch and Matriarch stormed the Celestial Emperor’s family estate.”

“If you’re that interested, ask Celestial Overlord Xu or Overlord Bai. They probably know.”

Yuan nodded, “When I get the chance.”

He reasoned that since he was already at odds with the Celestial Emperor, it might be wise to form alliances, and if the Tian Family also had grievances with the Celestial Emperor, they could prove to be valuable allies.

Meanwhile, Tian Yuexing returned to where his family was staying after his brief meeting with Yuan.

“Where did you go?” A beautiful young lady with black hair and brown eyes asked him.

“I met someone interesting,” answered Tian Yuexing.

The young lady raised an eyebrow and asked, “Interesting? How?”

“I don’t quite know how to describe it,” Tian Yuexing said, recalling his encounter with Yuan. “At first, he seemed ordinary, but the more I spoke with him, the more I felt something deeper. It was as if he was wrapped in an indescribable aura… something beyond what the eye could see.”

“That’s quite the high praise from you, brother. Now I want to meet this guy,” said the young lady, who turned out to be his sister.

“That guy called me arrogant, but he is obviously even more arrogant than I.” Tian Yuexing shook his head with an entertained smile.

“Even more arrogant than you? That’s hard to believe.”

“Right? That’s mostly why I found him interesting. Anyway, I made a bet with him—that if he outranks me in the Sword Emperor Qualifications, I’d do any one thing for him.”

“What…? What if you lose?”

Tian Yuexing frowned and said, “You think I’ll lose to some nobody? I’ll crush him quickly and show him why I am ranked first in the Nine Heavens.”

“Anyway, will father and mother be coming to watch or what?” he then asked.

“No, but they’ll be watching from home,” she said.

Tian Yuexing sighed out loud, “I wonder how long this will continue. It’s already been several decades, and they still haven’t gotten over it.”

His sister frowned and said, “Don’t talk nonsense. You have no idea what they’re going through now.”

Tian Yuexing looked at her with a curious gaze and asked, “What about you? How are you feeling?”

She shrugged, “I’m a bit disappointed, but that’s about it.”

“You’re quite heartless.”

“I don’t want more competition. You’re already too much for me to handle,” she sighed.

“Whatever. I’m going to cultivate.”

Without another word, Tian Yuexing sat down and closed his eyes.

The other participants also took this time to mentally prepare themselves. Whether it be the Holy Sword Maiden or the Holy Sword King, everyone took the Sword Emperor Qualifications seriously.

Only Yuan was spending his time casually chatting with his friends.

Time flew by, and in the blink of an eye, the moment everyone had been waiting for had arrived—the Sword Emperor Qualifications were about to begin.

“Ladies and gentlemen, may I have a moment of your time before we begin the Sword Emperor Qualifications?”

Senior Bai’s voice echoed through the skies just moments before his figure descended gracefully, coming to a halt above the platform on the central island.

Despite the presence of tens of millions of spectators, an eerie silence fell over the venue, as if the entire world had paused to listen.

“Thank you.”

Senior Bai then retrieved a long scroll with several names written on it and began calling them one by one.

“These will be this year’s Sword Emperor Qualifications. Please come to me when you’re called.”

“Holy Sword Clan’s Holy Sword Maiden.”

Seconds later, the Holy Sword Maiden’s beautiful figure appeared before Senior Bai, greeting him at her arrival.

“Holy Sword Clan’s Holy Sword King.”

The Holy Sword King appeared to greet him a moment later.

“Ethereal Sword Clan’s Vanishing Sword.”

A middle-aged man appeared and greeted him.

Several names later, “Tian Family’s Heavenly Son.”

Tian Yuexing made a grand entrance, radiating a powerful aura as he appeared before the crowd. His silky black hair shimmered under the sunlight, and a confident, handsome smile played across his face.

The moment he appeared, countless girls in the crowd erupted into cheers, swooning like fangirls at a concert.

Unsurprisingly, being the eldest son of the second most influential and powerful family in the Nine Heavens attracted a lot of attention and attraction.

Senior Bai cleared his throat loudly, instantly halting the loud noises.

Once the place calmed down, he continued, reading the last name on the list, “Yuan.”

“???”

The crowd raised their eyebrows at Yuan’s brief introduction.

The next moment, Yuan leaped from his island and flew toward Senior Bai with incredible speed, stopping right before him.

Both the Heavenly Son and the Holy Sword King narrowed their eyes at Yuan, but he ignored them and greeted Senior Bai.

“Good luck.”

Senior Bai responded to his greeting, which caught everyone off guard, as it was the first time he had acknowledged any participant. Both the spectators and the contestants were stunned, and whispers quickly spread as curiosity about Yuan’s identity began to grow.

chapters.title: Chapter 2034: Mystic Sword Tablet
chapters.body:
Chapter 2034: Mystic Sword Tablet

Chapter 2034: Mystic Sword Tablet

When the spectators looked closely at Yuan’s appearance, many raised their eyebrows.

“Hey, isn’t that the Faceless Sword Emperor’s appearance?”

“Who is this guy, and why is he dressing up as one of the greatest Sword Emperors to ever exist?”

“Is he mocking the others or something? What a rude and disrespectful bastard!”

“People like him tend to come in last.”

The spectators began cursing Yuan for his appearance.

“W-Wait a second! Look at his cultivation! He’s only at the peak of Immortal Ascension! He’s still a mortal!”

“What?! That’s impossible! He must be hiding his cultivation. There’s no way a mortal could comprehend Supreme Sword Aura, much less qualify for the Sword Emperor Qualifications.”

Almost everybody there figured Yuan was hiding his cultivation with some kind of trick.

Senior Bai ignored the slight commotion and continued to speak.

“Now that all of the participants are here, allow me to explain the rules for the Sword Emperor Qualifications.”

He gestured toward the standalone doors on the platform beneath them and continued, “I’ll keep things simple. Each door represents a trial. In order to be acknowledged as a Sword Emperor, you must pass all trials. Of course, this means more than one of you can become a Sword Emperor.”

“However, there’ll also be a ranking depending on your results. Whoever comes in first place will be awarded a priceless and one-of-a-kind treasure that was recently discovered—the Mystic Sword Tablet, which was left behind by an unknown swordmaster who seemed to have glimpsed into a realm above Supreme Sword Aura!”

“What? Above Supreme Sword Aura?”

The spectators expressed disbelief after hearing this.

To satisfy the doubters, Senior Bai retrieved a fist-sized jade tablet bearing a small sword cut, seemingly unremarkable and unassuming at first glance. To the ordinary eye, it was nothing special. But to swordmasters who had reached a certain level of mastery, that tiny cut held an unfathomable secret—an imprint of sword intent so profound it defied comprehension.

“What the fuck is that?!” Sword Empress Huang exclaimed in a shocked voice after seeing the tablet, her eyes wide with disbelief.

Not only Sword Empress Huang, but every swordmaster present trembled with a mix of shock and excitement upon seeing the Mystic Sword Tablet, as none of them had ever witnessed anything like it.

To them, Supreme Sword Aura was the pinnacle of swordsmanship. The mere suggestion that a higher realm of mastery existed shattered their understanding and left them in awe.

After giving everyone a glimpse of the Mystic Sword Tablet, Senior Bai quickly stored it back inside his spatial ring.

“First place… I have to claim it,” Tian Yuexing muttered, his eyes burning with determination and desire.

Around him, the other participants wore similar expressions—faces etched with ambition, each one ready to stake everything for the title.

Meanwhile, Yuan was looking at the notifications that had popped up before him when he glanced at the Mystic Sword Tablet.

<You have learned the existence of Flawless Sword Aura>

<You have met the criteria to learn Flawless Sword Aura>

<Your understanding of Flawless Sword Aura has increased slightly>

’Flawless Sword Aura, huh?’ Yuan smiled inwardly.

“Where did you find something like that?” Tian Yuexing asked Senior Bai a question that was on everyone’s mind.

“I’m sorry, but that is not something I can disclose right now,” said Senior Bai as he shook his head.

Although his answer left everyone disappointed, they understood why he’s keeping it a secret.

“Anyway, let’s return to the topic. I’m sure you already know this, but your life is not guaranteed during the Sword Emperor Qualifications, and participants have died while attempting the trials before.”

“That said, we will provide you with a talisman that will take you out of the trial if you think you are unable to continue. Just know that using the talisman will automatically disqualify you.”

Senior Bai retrieved a stack of black talismans and handed one to each participant.

“You can activate it by destroying it. None of the mechanisms in the trial will be able to touch the talisman, so you don’t have to worry about it being destroyed by accident.”

“When the trial begins, you will all be transported to the same realm, but in different locations,” Senior Bai explained. “There is a chance you may encounter other participants, but be warned—combat or intentional interference with others is strictly forbidden. Anyone caught doing so will be immediately disqualified. There will be tens of millions of pairs of eyes watching each of you, so don’t think you’ll be able to get away with it, either.”

“Any questions so far?”
