When no one spoke, Senior Bai continued, “One final note before we officially begin the Sword Emperor Qualifications: you are permitted to bring your own treasures into the trial. However, be aware—within the trial, all treasures will be automatically sealed, rendering them no more powerful than ordinary weapons.”

“Now then… the Sword Emperor Qualifications will officially begin!”

The very next moment, one of the sealed doors opened, revealing a white portal within.

“You may enter.”

Hearing this, the dozen participants flew into the portal one by one, disappearing into the trial.

Meanwhile, the projections above the island began displaying the situation inside the trial.

“Once again, good luck.” Senior Bai said to Yuan before he left and entered the portal.

After stepping through the portal, Yuan found himself beneath a clear blue sky, standing on a wide dirt path flanked by dense tree lines on either side.

“Since they didn’t mention any details about the trial, I guess it’s for us to figure it out ourselves…” he mumbled as he began walking down the straight path.

After walking for a few minutes, he spotted a lone figure resting against a distant tree, seemingly lost in sleep.

Yuan focused on this individual, but he couldn’t see the man’s cultivation, almost as if he were an ordinary mortal. However, he also exuded a mysterious aura.

Moments later, Yuan continued walking again, only to stop again a few steps later when the man suddenly opened his eyes and slowly stood up.

chapters.title: Chapter 2035: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ First Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2035: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ First Trial

Chapter 2035: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ First Trial

As the man rose to his feet, Yuan came to a halt, his focus sharpening as he watched the stranger’s every move.

Without warning, the man drew the sword at his waist and unleashed a swift slash from afar, sending a powerful wave of ordinary Sword Aura hurtling toward Yuan.

Yet, Yuan didn’t move a muscle and allowed the Sword Aura to hit him, and when it did, the Sword Aura shattered into countless fragments like glass.

“So it was just ordinary Sword Aura…” Yuan muttered as he retrieved a random sword he had in his spatial ring.

Since all treasures were essentially the same in the trial, he didn’t need to use Number One Under Heaven, nor did he want to use it before so many spectators.

Then, without hesitation, he swung at the man, sending a wave of Supreme Sword Aura at the man. However, as if copying Yuan’s actions, the man did not attempt to block it and allowed it to strike his body.

Once the Supreme Sword Aura struck his body, the mysterious aura around him flared up, deflecting Yuan’s Supreme Sword Aura.

Seeing this, Yuan raised an eyebrow. The trial had just begun, yet he was already fighting an enemy immune to Supreme Sword Aura? Something felt off.

Just to make sure, he struck the man again with Supreme Sword Aura, and sure enough, the results were the same.

After blocking his second attack, the man suddenly moved, charging toward Yuan with his sword raised and gushing with ordinary Sword Aura.

“If Supreme Sword Aura doesn’t work, then…”

Yuan swung his sword at the man without channeling any Sword Aura, yet once again, his blade was effortlessly deflected by the aura.

Yuan was certain now that the trial required him to somehow get past the man’s mysterious aura, and for the next several minutes, he analyzed the man’s aura to the best of his abilities.

Unfortunately, he was unable to figure out its secrets.

’There’s gotta be a clue…’

Yuan glanced around, searching for anything that might offer a clue to counter the man’s mysterious aura, but aside from the silent figure before him, there was nothing in sight to guide him.

Suddenly, realization flickered across Yuan’s face.

“Let’s see if this works…” he murmured.

Lifting his sword, he gathered only ordinary Sword Aura, just as the man had been using all along, and unleashed a focused slash.

The very next moment, his sword tore right through the aura and sliced the man in half.

“An aura that blocks Supreme Sword Aura but cannot resist ordinary Sword Aura, huh?” Yuan muttered out loud, his voice filled with intrigue.

After defeating the man, Yuan searched his body to see if there was anything to loot.

“Nothing.”

With nothing left to do, Yuan continued walking the path.

Before long, he came to another halt. In the distance stood not one, but two masked men, each wielding a sword, and both radiating the same mysterious aura as the one he had faced before.

Without any words, the two masked men charged at Yuan, moving at a speed that was noticeably faster than that of his last opponent. Not just that, but their attacks were also much quicker and precise.

However, now that Yuan knew the trick, he quickly defeated them with ordinary Sword Aura and continued moving forward.

A few minutes later, Yuan came across four masked men, twice as many as in his previous encounter. Each one stood silently, swords in hand, their presence radiating the same eerie aura as before.

Seeing this, Yuan smiled faintly and muttered, “Let’s see how many times you’ll multiply.”

This time, he took the initiative, moving before his opponents could react. Though their strength had clearly increased compared to the previous group, it still wasn’t nearly enough to pose any real threat to him.

In less than a minute, Yuan dispatched all four masked men with ease and continued down the path, fully expecting to face eight opponents next.

However, to his surprise, only a single man stood in his path. Though the aura he emitted resembled that of the previous masked opponents, Yuan could sense a subtle difference.

As Yuan focused, trying to discern the subtle difference in the man’s aura, the masked figure suddenly made his move.

Unlike the others, he didn’t strike with ordinary Sword Aura but Enhanced Sword Aura instead.

Seeing this, Yuan attacked with Enhanced Sword Aura as well, and sure enough, it was able to cut through the masked man’s aura.

Having grasped the pattern, Yuan pressed on through the trial with a quicker, more confident pace.

After defeating two masked opponents simultaneously—and then another wave of four—he eventually came to a halt again, and standing before him was a lone masked man wielding Supreme Sword Aura.

When the masked man struck, his swordsmanship was precise, fluid, and refined to perfection. It was incomparably superior to that of Yuan’s previous opponents, leaving no doubt that this foe was on an entirely different level.

Not only did the man move like an expert with tens of thousands of years of experience, but he even used martial techniques.

Yet, despite the drastic improvements, Yuan defeated the man in just a few exchanges.

“This can’t be the real trial. It’s too easy…” Yuan mumbled as he continued forward.

After overcoming two more waves of opponents wielding Supreme Sword Aura, Yuan finally arrived at the end of the path. It opened into a vast, circular field, one that radiated an unmistakable pressure, as if silently declaring the beginning of the real trial.

Without hesitation, Yuan walked to the center of the field and waited for the trial to begin.

Moments later, Yuan could sense several—many presences appearing all around him.

In the blink of an eye, hundreds… no, thousands of masked men surrounded him, each radiating a mysterious aura. Yet not all were the same. The three distinct types of aura Yuan had faced earlier—ordinary, enhanced, and Supreme Sword Aura—were now interwoven throughout the crowd.

“Now this is more like it.” Yuan muttered with an excited smile on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 2036: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ First Trial(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2036: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ First Trial(2)

Chapter 2036: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ First Trial(2)

While Yuan stood surrounded by thousands of masked men, the other participants were still making their way toward the end of the path. Although many had figured out the trial’s pattern just as quickly as Yuan, they struggled to overcome their opponents. Their Sword Auras lacked the overwhelming force Yuan possessed, requiring multiple strikes to break through the masked men’s defenses.

Of course, Tian Yuexing was an exception. As expected of the number one young genius in the Nine Heavens, he had reached the end of the path nearly at the same time as Yuan.

The spectators watching this were simply left dumbfounded by Yuan’s unexpected prowess and talents.

“Heavens… his pace is slightly faster than the Heavenly Son! Maybe I’ve underestimated him a little too much!”

“His fighting experience is also terrifyingly high—something I’d only expect from the older generation…”

“Where did a genius like him come from? And how come I’ve never heard of him?”

“He’s probably already well-known but is hiding his identity for whatever reason.”

The spectators began trying to speculate Yuan’s identity as he fought the thousands of masked men, constantly using all three types of Sword Aura.

Though everyone participating in the Sword Emperor Qualifications was at least in the God Ascension realm, their cultivation didn’t really matter since only Sword Aura could get past the masked men’s aura. That said, it didn’t mean their cultivation wasn’t important, since it allowed them to fight without getting tired easily.

Time continued to pass. Although the Sword Emperor Qualifications had just begun, the spectators were already on the edge of their seats from watching Yuan’s amazing performance.

Not only was he defeating his opponents with a single strike and progressing much quicker than the Heavenly Son, but he would likely finish the first trial in record time.

“You’re telling me that wannabe Faceless Sword Emperor is a mortal? Would a mortal be able to do that?!”

“If he’s able to do that while suppressing his cultivation, can you imagine how powerful he truly is?”

While it was difficult to imagine anyone willingly restraining their full strength during the Sword Emperor Qualifications, the spectators found it even harder to believe that Yuan was truly a mortal.

It only took a few hours, but Yuan finally defeated all of the masked men, and he’d done so without even getting hurt, as only Supreme Sword Aura could damage his body.

Of course, the spectators had noticed this.

“He completely ignored every opponent using ordinary and Enhanced Sword Aura… This bastard didn’t just attain a Sword Body—he’s achieved the legendary Enhanced Sword Body, which renders all Enhanced Sword Aura useless…” muttered one of the watching Sword Emperors, disbelief and awe lacing his voice.

While most of the Sword Emperors present possessed a Sword Body, not a single one among them had attained the Enhanced Sword Body. In terms of sword comprehension alone, Yuan had already surpassed them.

“Just what the fuck is that bastard…?” Sword Empress Huang clenched her fists in frustration after realizing Yuan had attained the Enhanced Sword Body before her, something she’d been trying to achieve for the past thirty thousand years.

If she’d known that it only took Yuan a few years to achieve it, she’d probably go crazy from envy.

After defeating all of the masked men, Yuan looked around and muttered, “Am I done?”

He waited for a while, but when nothing happened, he calmly sat down and began cultivating.

Hours passed in silence—until suddenly, Yuan’s eyes snapped open. He sensed something from above.

Lifting his gaze, he saw tens of thousands of semi-transparent swords materializing in the sky, each one forged from pure Supreme Sword Aura.

Without warning, they began to fall like a meteor shower, raining down upon him with the speed and force of a storm of arrows.

In response, Yuan activated his Limitless Sword Domain, unleashing a surge of Supreme Sword Aura to intercept the falling blades.

Yet to his surprise, despite his aura being clearly stronger, his sword aura was shattered upon contact, as though the incoming swords carried something beyond raw power, something deeper and more refined.

Seeing this, Yuan swiftly activated his movement technique, weaving through the rain of blades with precise, fluid motion. As he dodged, his mind raced, analyzing the clash, trying to understand why his Supreme Sword Aura had been so easily overwhelmed despite its superior strength.

However, the trial offered no mercy, and the blades pursued him relentlessly, tracking his every move like homing missiles.

It didn’t take long for Yuan to notice something unusual—that each sword was enveloped in a thin layer of mysterious aura, and each blade’s Supreme Sword Aura was slightly different, with some being more powerful than others.

Yuan narrowed his eyes, a glint of realization flashing within them.

To test his theory, he struck the nearest descending blade with a precise amount of Supreme Sword Aura, matching its level perfectly.

Sure enough, the sword shattered on impact. His guess had been correct—that he needed to strike these swords with perfectly matching Supreme Sword Aura, which tested his control and precision.

Now that he knew the trick, Yuan activated Limitless Sword Domain again. This time, he perfectly controlled the Supreme Sword Aura to match that of the incoming blades.

When the spectators witnessed Yuan’s unfathomable control over his Supreme Sword Aura, they were left in stunned silence.

The swordmasters among them, in particular, were overwhelmed with disbelief, for they understood how incredibly difficult it was to manipulate Supreme Sword Aura with such precision. And yet, Yuan made it look effortless.

By the time Yuan had finished destroying every last sword, Tian Yuexing was still locked in battle with the thousands of masked men. Even for a genius of his caliber, fending off so many powerful opponents at once was no simple feat, not to mention how he had to constantly swap his Sword Aura, something he wasn’t used to.

As for the others, they were still on the straight path, fighting their way to the end.

chapters.title: Chapter 2037: Immediately Starting The Second Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2037: Immediately Starting The Second Trial

Chapter 2037: Immediately Starting the Second Trial

Shortly after destroying all of the swords, a portal appeared before Yuan, prompting him to enter it.

“I guess this is it for the first trial…” Yuan mumbled as he walked straight into the portal, leaving the trial and returning to the platform.

“Heavens… he’s already finished the first trial? And it hasn’t even been a full day!”

The spectators were left in awe, their faces a mix of shock and disbelief. Yuan’s results weren’t just impressive, they were unprecedented. Even the most seasoned swordmasters found themselves speechless.

Yuan glanced around after returning, and upon seeing none of the other participants, he quickly realized he was the first to finish.

High above him, a massive display showed the names of all participants, and his name surged to the top, claiming first place with a perfect score of 100 points.

Senior Bai approached him with a smile and said, “Welcome back. Words cannot describe my surprise when you finished the trial so quickly.”

“Thank you.” Yuan said before asking, “Do I have to wait for the others to come out, or can I continue to the next trial?”

“You can immediately continue onto the next trial if you want, but you are allowed a week of rest after each trial,” said Senior Bai, and he continued, “And while you’re allowed to cultivate during your rest, you are not allowed to consume any treasures or pills.”

“I’d like to begin the next trial,” Yuan said calmly, choosing to forgo any rest.

The first trial had been little more than a warm-up for him, barely a walk in the park, and the brief conversation with Senior Bai had been enough for him to recover physically and mentally.

Senior Bai nodded and immediately opened the second door for him.

Without hesitation, Yuan walked into the second door, disappearing from the platform.

The spectators were stunned into silence. Not only had Yuan obliterated the first trial in record time, but now he was diving straight into the second without a moment’s rest.

The Sword Emperors present exchanged glances, recalling their own Sword Emperor Qualifications—how they had clung to every second of rest between trials.

“Hahaha! At this rate, we’ll probably be here for a few months before he’s completely done!” Wang Ming laughed out loud after seeing how fast Yuan was progressing through the Sword Emperor Qualifications.

Kelan wanted to respond, but he was left utterly speechless by Yuan’s performance. He had never imagined Yuan’s level of swordsmanship would be this high.

’How is his understanding of Supreme Sword Aura so profound when he’d only spent a few years inside the Holy Sword Temple?! This doesn’t make any sense! His existence doesn’t make any damn sense!’ Kelan cried inwardly.

It wasn’t just Kelan. The Sixth Elder and Supreme Empress Huang were just as puzzled as he, as they were also aware of how little time Yuan had spent inside the Holy Sword Temple.

Meanwhile, spectators from beyond the Seventh Heaven, such as those from the Eighth and Ninth Heavens, were stirred into action.

As Yuan’s performance was simply too outstanding, countless influential factions began investigating his origins, eager to uncover his true identity. Some even went so far as to dispatch recruiters, hoping to secure him for their ranks the moment the Sword Emperor Qualifications concluded.

Xu Jiaqi, who was watching from her own office, wore a warm smile on her face as she stared at Yuan’s figure through a treasure that broadcast the Sword Emperor Qualifications.

“As expected of father’s reincarnation…” she muttered.

Meanwhile, at the Divine Essence Monastery, a young lady wearing a white face veil approached Xiang Biyu and asked, “Master, what are you watching?”

“It’s the Sword Emperor Qualifications. You should watch it with me, Azure,” said Xiang Biyu.

“Why? It’s a waste of time.”

“Even though your rival is currently participating in it?”

“The Heavenly Son? I don’t even consider him a rival,” she said.

“No, I wasn’t talking about the Heavenly Son.” Xiang Biyu pointed at the masked man on the display and said, “I’m talking about him.”

Despite her disinterest, Azure asked, “Who’s that?”

She couldn’t fathom how someone she’s never met or heard of before could be her rival.

“You have never met him before, but his name is Yuan, and he’s definitely worthy to be your rival. After all, he was able to learn my soul cultivation technique.”

“What did you just say…?”

Behind her face veil, Azure’s eyes widened.

Xiang Biyu assumed she was talking about the soul cultivation technique, so she explained, “Remember when I told you that a mortal had managed to learn my soul cultivation technique, and you didn’t believe me? This is him.”

However, little did Xiang Biyu know—Azure couldn’t have cared less about the soul cultivation technique, and her attention was focused elsewhere.

“His name. Can you repeat it?”

“Yuan? Why? You recognize it?”

“…”

Azure didn’t respond, but she silently took a seat beside Xiang Biyu and began watching the Sword Emperor Qualifications, which surprised her master.

“I thought you said it’s a waste of time,” Xiang Biyu teased her.

“Perhaps, but it’s not like I have anything else to do,” Azure calmly responded.

Somewhere in the Ninth Heaven, the Heavenly Son’s family—the Tian Family—was also watching.

“I can’t believe someone is actually ahead of our eldest…” one of the elders muttered, his gaze shifting toward a nearby couple to see their reaction.

The two sat silently, their eyes sunken with exhaustion, dark circles beneath them as if they hadn’t slept in decades. Yet despite their weary appearance, their aura was unmistakably vast, commanding, and unfathomably powerful.

They were none other than Tian Yuexing’s parents.

“The trial’s just begun. He’ll have plenty of time to catch up,” said his father, his voice calm and full of confidence. He wore flowing black robes that matched the color of his long, neatly tied hair.

The woman beside him remained silent, her attention focused on Tian Yuexing, who was close to defeating all of his opponents.

While the entire world stirred in commotion over his existence, Yuan quietly stepped into his second trial.

chapters.title: Chapter 2038: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ Second Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2038: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ Second Trial

Chapter 2038: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ Second Trial

After stepping through the second door, Yuan found himself in a familiar setup—a solitary path stretching ahead, offering only one direction: forward.

But unlike before, it wasn’t a dirt path flanked by trees. This time, he stood on a narrow platform, sandwiched between two vast, seemingly endless seas on either side, their shimmering surfaces stretching out to the horizon.

Though the surface of the sea was calm and without any ripples, like that of a glass of water resting on a table, Yuan could sense countless sea beasts stirring beneath the tranquil waters, their presence concealed.

Once he took in his surroundings and grasped the nature of his situation, Yuan began walking forward at a calm, steady pace. With the trial’s rules still unknown to him, caution was essential.

After taking about a dozen careful steps, Yuan suddenly came to a halt when a faint ripple disturbed the otherwise motionless surface of the sea.

The very next second, a massive sea creature burst from the water, launching itself toward him with its jaws wide open, as if intending to swallow him whole in a single bite.

Yuan instinctively drew his sword, Supreme Sword Aura already surging to the surface. But just as he was about to strike, he paused—his eyes narrowing as he recognized the familiar aura enveloping the sea creature.

A knowing smile tugged at his lips. In the next instant, he withdrew his Supreme Sword Aura and channeled ordinary Sword Aura instead, slashing toward the creature with precision and confidence.

His sword cut right through the sea creature, killing it in a single strike. The sea creature not only had the same aura as his opponents from the last trial, but it also required Yuan to strike it with matching strength.

After dispatching the sea creature, Yuan resumed his path, his pace quickening slightly with confidence.

But just as he advanced a few more steps, his eyes narrowed sharply. Without hesitation, he raised his sword and swung it at the empty space in front of him, as if trying to hit an invisible enemy.

In the very next moment, a sharp shattering sound rang out as Yuan’s blade collided with something unseen. The invisible force became visible upon impact, revealing a sword forged from Supreme Sword Aura.

It functioned similarly to the swords from the previous trial, but with one critical difference: this one had been completely invisible to the naked eye.

“He actually blocked that?!” Sword Empress Huang exclaimed, eyes wide with disbelief.

Every Sword Emperor Qualification was unique, its trials ever-changing and unpredictable. Because of this, those who had passed them in the past couldn’t pass down any concrete knowledge to the next generation.

And when the current Sword Emperors witnessed the sudden invisible strike, many of them felt a rare twinge of uncertainty. Had they been in Yuan’s place, facing that same hidden assault, few were confident they could have blocked it as cleanly—or even at all.

“Is it just me, or does this year’s Sword Emperor Qualifications seem much harder than the last?” one of the Sword Emperors murmured aloud, his brows furrowed in concern.

“Not just the last one…” another replied solemnly. “This year’s qualifications might be the hardest in history.”

While it was normal, even expected, for the trials to gradually grow more difficult over time, reflecting the world’s constant evolution, such a sudden and immense spike in difficulty was completely unprecedented.

“I was fairly confident those two would succeed,” the Sixth Elder remarked, referring to the Holy Sword Maiden and the Holy Sword King. “But after seeing the trials this year… I can’t help but feel a little worried.”

“Though this year’s trials are especially difficult, I have confidence in my son and his ability,” said a fierce-looking red-haired man seated beside Sword Empress Huang.

He was one of the three Sword Emperors of the Holy Sword Clan, and his calm, unwavering tone reflected the confidence he had in his son, the Holy Sword King.

“The same goes for my baby girl,” said a handsome silver-haired man seated beside the red-haired Sword Emperor, a faint smile tugging at his lips.

He was the father of the Holy Sword Maiden, one of the most respected figures in the Holy Sword Clan as its faction leader, and his voice carried both pride and confidence.

Sword Empress Huang shook her head and said, “It’s fine to have confidence in your children, but you’re fooling yourselves if you think they have a good chance. Even we would’ve struggled if we had faced trials this difficult during our own qualifications.”

“And you shouldn’t look down on other people’s children, especially when you don’t know what it’s like to have any,” the red-haired Sword Emperor replied, a mocking smile tugging at his lips.

“Why the fuck would I want any children? They’re too much of a pain in the ass,” she quickly responded.

“With that potty mouth of yours, I’m honestly glad you don’t have any children,” said the silver-haired Sword Emperor, shaking his head with a sigh. “I don’t think I could handle multiple potty mouths around me.”

As word spread about the unprecedented difficulty of this year’s Sword Emperor Qualifications, more and more cultivators from across the heavens began tuning in, drawn by curiosity and the desire to witness history in the making.

Meanwhile, after handling the invisible strike, Yuan pressed onward.

The farther he traveled along the path, the more ripples appeared on the water’s surface—each one signaling an impending attack from the sea creatures below. And without fail, every time Yuan defeated a beast, an invisible sword would lash out from the air, attempting to catch him off guard.

This meant that the farther Yuan advanced, the more sea creatures he had to face at once, each wave more aggressive than the last. And as soon as he brought them down, invisible swords would strike without warning, giving him barely a moment to breathe.

The trial was no longer just a test of control—it had also become a relentless gauntlet of endurance, awareness, and speed.

chapters.title: Chapter 2039: Tian Yuexing’S First Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2039: Tian Yuexing’S First Trial

Chapter 2039: Tian Yuexing’s First Trial

It took almost an entire day, but Tian Yuexing finally defeated all of the masked men.

“This is just the first trial, but I am already sweating. Why’d they make this year’s qualification so difficult? Is it because of my reputation?” Tian Yuexing wondered out loud as he waited for the portal to appear so he could leave, unaware that the trial had yet to end.

However, he would soon learn the truth when nothing happened for several hours.

“The trial’s still ongoing? In that case, where are the enemies?” Tian Yuexing considered flying around, but just as he took to the sky, he noticed thousands of semi-transparent swords appearing in the sky above him.

His eyes widened with surprise, and he instinctively used a martial technique to attack all of the swords at once. However, his eyes widened even more after witnessing his martial technique unexpectedly shatter from clashing with the swords.

“What?!” he exclaimed, his voice filled with disbelief.

Soon, the sky above Tian Yuexing darkened as the swords descended upon him.

Refusing to even consider retreat, he chose to face them head-on, unleashing his full strength in a barrage of powerful strikes. Yet no matter how much force he poured into his attacks, his blows couldn’t even graze the falling swords, much less destroy them. It was as if the blades were untouchable, immune to brute strength alone.

After narrowly evading the descending swords, Tian Yuexing’s eyes narrowed. He noticed that the aura surrounding these swords was different from that of the masked men he had faced earlier.

Realization began to stir, but with the swords relentlessly pursuing him, he had little time to think. Forced to dodge while piecing together the puzzle, he racked his brain, desperately trying to decipher the key to passing the trial.

Eventually, Tian Yuexing uncovered the trick. Piece by piece, the puzzle came together, and he began destroying the swords one at a time.

Unlike Yuan, who possessed the skills and control to dismantle them all in a single sweeping motion, Tian Yuexing had to take a more methodical approach. He focused first on the swords imbued with Supreme Sword Aura, the type he was most familiar with.

After hours of relentless dodging and careful strikes, he cleared those away and shifted his attention to the blades carrying Enhanced Sword Aura. These proved more troublesome, often requiring multiple attempts to break through, each failure costing him precious energy and time.

After two more days of nonstop movement and relentless effort, Tian Yuexing finally destroyed the last sword. Despite his cultivation, exhaustion weighed heavily on him, and every fiber of his being longed to collapse onto the ground and sleep.

But then he remembered that his every movement was being watched by countless spectators across the Nine Heavens.

’I am the Heavenly Son—the number one genius in the Nine Heavens! I must not disgrace myself!’

With sheer willpower, he straightened his posture, composed himself, and stepped through the portal that appeared before him with graceful movements.

When Tian Yuexing returned to the platform outside, he glanced around and saw that no other participants had finished the first trial yet—a result he fully expected.

However, there was something off about the atmosphere, and the audience was oddly quiet.

But Tian Yuexing didn’t question it and turned to look at Senior Bai, who was standing not far away.

“I get a week to rest, correct?” he asked.

“That’s right.” Senior Bai nodded his head, confirming it.

Without wasting another second, Tian Yuexing sat down and began to cultivate, not to recover his spiritual energy, but to calm and steady his mind.

As a cultivator in the God Ascension Realm, it would take years of constant battle to deplete his spiritual energy. However, the trial had been mentally grueling, pushing his focus, perception, and discipline to their limits. Moreover, the constant swapping of sword aura depleted his soul strength much quicker than anticipated.

Shortly after, Tian Yuexing’s name jumped to the second rank with a score of 95. Of course, Tian Yuexing didn’t even bother to look at the ranking since he was convinced that he was first.

As Tian Yuexing focused on his cultivation, the spectators mumbled to each other.

“Seeing the Heavenly Son in second place always invokes a weird feeling.”

“Right? He’s usually first at whatever he does.”

Although Tian Yuexing was immersed in his cultivation, he had a strange feeling in his gut that prompted him to open his eyes, and when he saw the weird looks the spectators were giving him, he subconsciously tilted his head to look at the ranking, and when he did, he was left in disbelief.

Tian Yuexing’s eyes widened after seeing the name.

“Yuan!” he uttered, his voice laced with disbelief.

He then turned to ask Senior Bai, “There must be some sort of mistake. How is he above me when I am the only one here?!”

Senior Bai cleared his throat and calmly responded, “He’s not here because he already started his second trial.”

“When?! When did he finish?!” Tian Yuexing demanded answers.

“A little over two days ago.”

“Over two days ago?! He finished the first trial in less than a day?!” Tian Yuexing couldn’t believe what he was hearing, nor could he fathom how Yuan had accomplished such a feat.

’Damn it! I don’t have the time to rest if he’s already two days ahead of me!’

He immediately stood up and ran toward the second door, quickly disappearing from the platform after resting only for a few minutes.

Meanwhile, the rest of the participants had reached the end of the path and started fighting the thousands of masked men. Unfortunately for them, barely anyone was paying attention to their trial, as everyone was focused on Yuan, who was in the lead, and the Heavenly Son, who was playing catch-up—a rare sight for many.

As for Yuan, who was now two days deep into the second trial, he had killed tens of thousands of sea beasts and traveled dozens of miles on the path, but the end was still nowhere in sight.

chapters.title: Chapter 2040: Reaching The End Of The Second Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2040: Reaching The End Of The Second Trial

Chapter 2040: Reaching the End of the Second Trial

After spending two days in the second trial, Yuan progressed to the point where he had to face hundreds of sea creatures at once with each encounter. Fortunately, their attacks were still predictable and straightforward as long as he paid attention to the ripples appearing in the water.

Yuan had also realized that the sea creatures only appeared when he advanced, each encounter seemingly triggered by crossing an invisible threshold. This meant he could have paused between battles, taken breaks, and prepared for each wave.

But he chose not to, and for the past two days, he had moved without rest, facing each encounter head-on with unwavering resolve, as if testing the limits of both the trial and himself.

Yuan’s performance was nothing short of astonishing, leaving the spectators speechless, many with their mouths agape, unable to believe what they were witnessing.

When they watched the Heavenly Son and the other participants undergo their trials, the sheer difficulty was palpable. Every clash and every movement carried the weight of struggle.

But when they turned their gaze to Yuan, it was a different story entirely. He moved with such ease and speed that the trial itself seemed beneath him, almost as if he was mocking the efforts of every other participant.

“You know, watching him… it reminds me of the Faceless Sword Emperor’s qualification,” remarked one of the Sword Emperors, eyes fixed on the display.

Thanks to recorded archives of past trials, anyone with access could study them, and none were more watched or revered than the Faceless Sword Emperor’s, a trial he had dominated with unmatched grace and power.

“Are we witnessing the birth of a second Faceless Sword Emperor?” someone else murmured, the question lingering in the air like a prophecy waiting to be fulfilled.

Meanwhile, the moment Tian Yuexing stepped into the area for the second trial, he began walking forward without hesitation, wasting no time to observe his surroundings.

There was no room for delay in his mind. He knew he was already behind, and every second counted if he hoped to catch up to Yuan.

While his actions could be seen as reckless, Tian Yuexing was confident in his own abilities.

When the first sea creature attacked a few steps later, Tian Yuexing defeated it with a single strike.

’I don’t know how you managed to get ahead of me, but it won’t last!’ Tian Yuexing thought to himself, determination flaring in his eyes as he pressed forward.

But just a few steps later, his thoughts were abruptly cut off. His instincts flared like lightning, screaming danger. Without thinking, he raised his sword in front of him, just in time to somewhat block the invisible sword.

However, his reaction was just a moment too slow. Though he managed to intercept part of the strike, the invisible sword still slipped through his guard, slicing into his left arm and leaving a deep, bleeding gash.

Tian Yuexing gritted his teeth in frustration, his eyes narrowing at the wound.

The wound on his arm was minor, something he could easily ignore, but the fact that he’d been struck at all, and by such a simple trick, gnawed at his pride. He couldn’t accept making such a blunder—not with so many people watching.

Seconds later, Tian Yuexing sensed the invisible sword circling back for a second strike.

This time, fully alert and focused, he met it head-on. With a precise swing infused with his Supreme Sword Aura, he shattered the blade cleanly, redeeming his earlier mistake in a single, decisive motion.

After overcoming the first encounter, Tian Yuexing didn’t immediately charge forward to chase after Yuan.

Instead, he stood still, closed his eyes, and took a deep breath. The weight of his earlier misstep lingered, and he knew rushing in recklessly would only lead to more mistakes.

“What the heck am I doing? There’s no need to rush or panic. I’ll definitely catch up and reclaim my rank. It’s only natural and a matter of time,” he muttered to himself.

When he opened his eyes, they were filled with confidence and renewed focus, and he continued forward with immense concentration, no longer paying attention to Yuan being ahead.

Time passed swiftly, and Yuan had now entered his fourth day within the second trial.

Having advanced without pause, the difficulty had escalated dramatically; each encounter now unleashed thousands of sea creatures upon him at once, their numbers swelling like tidal waves with every step he took.

However, despite the overwhelming numbers, Yuan dispatched them all within moments, effortlessly cutting them down with his Limitless Sword Domain.

After days of honing his control to an even higher level, his manipulation of sword auras had become as natural as breathing. With each step forward, he moved calmly and gracefully, and what was meant to be a grueling trial now appeared more like a leisurely stroll through the park.

To the spectators, it was no longer just impressive—it was surreal.

“How long do you think he’ll be able to keep up this pace?” the Holy Sword Maiden’s father asked aloud, his gaze fixed on the image of Yuan effortlessly advancing the trial.

“Well,” the Holy Sword King’s father replied, arms crossed in thought, “the first and second trials follow the same general structure, but the second is far more brutal in scale. He might breeze through this… but the real challenge begins in the later trials when everything changes. That’s when we’ll see if he can keep his pace.”

By the end of the fourth day, Yuan finally caught sight of the path’s end in the distance, and just as he expected, it opened into another vast arena, its wide, circular expanse radiated an ominous stillness.

As Yuan stepped into the center of the arena, the stillness shattered. The calm water began to tremble, and a deep, resonating ripple spread outward across the surface.

Moments later, several glowing orbs appeared just beneath the water, pulsing with an eerie light. Then, with a thunderous roar and a violent surge, a colossal sea creature with many tentacles erupted from the depths like a volcanic explosion, water cascading from its body as it loomed high above Yuan.

“This is…”

Behind his mask, Yuan’s eyes widened in surprise the moment he laid eyes on the squid-like creature. He had encountered this beast before, and the sight of it stirred some unpleasant memories.

chapters.title: Chapter 2041: True Emperor Of The Seas
chapters.body:
Chapter 2041: True Emperor Of The Seas

Chapter 2041: True Emperor of the Seas

With a black body, pearlescent texture, and colorful circles all around its body, the squid-like sea creature that appeared before Yuan resembled the giant sea creature that killed Huang Xiao Li’s parents during their trip to the Desolate Continent.

Although it wasn’t as massive, and its aura was incomparably weaker than what he remembered, Yuan would never mistake its appearance.

‘Just what is this sea creature…?’ he wondered inwardly.

After that one encounter during the Primordial Era, Yuan never encountered it again despite his efforts to find it.

Seeing this monster on the display, Chu Liuxiang’s body trembled, and she asked Kelan with a slight frown on her face, “What is that sea creature?”

In a calm voice, Kelan responded, “If I am not mistaken, that is the True Emperor of the Seas, a mythical sea creature that has existed since the Primordial Era. Of course, the one in the trial isn’t the real thing but just an imitation.”

“True Emperor of the Seas?” Chu Liuxiang mumbled its name.

Kelan nodded and explained further, “This monster defies common sense, and it is neither a magical beast nor a divine beast. Nobody knows what it really is or where it came from, but it has existed since the Primordial Era, living deep beneath the sea. Also, it appears to have the ability to teleport from realm to realm, since it has been spotted in almost every single realm across the Nine Heavens.”

“How powerful is it?” Chu Liuxiang asked.

“Unfathomably powerful. A Cultivation God tried to hunt it before but was unsuccessful. Not only does it have regenerative abilities that rival even demons, but it has a unique power that completely renders spiritual energy useless,” Kelan said.

“It’s even stronger than a Cultivation God? That’s hard to believe…” Xi Meili muttered.

Kelan smiled and said, “In this world, there are a few entities that surpass our knowledge and defy common sense. They are known as the Four Mystic Wonders, and the True Emperor of the Seas is one of them.”

“Who are the other three?” Shi Lang asked.

Kelan pondered for a moment before responding, “I believe they are the Vanishing Emperor of the Skies, Immovable Emperor of the Earth, and the Calamity of the Void. They’re all heaven-defying entities that you do not want to mess with.”

Meanwhile, shortly after the monster’s emergence, it wasted no time and launched a fierce assault. Its colossal tentacles, each pulsing with an overwhelming concentration of Supreme Sword Aura, lashed out with terrifying momentum.

Rising high above, the tentacles blocked out the sky, casting a looming shadow over the arena like an omen. Then, in a single moment that tore through the silence, they came crashing down toward Yuan with speed and power that could pulverize mountains.

Since the arena was too small, Yuan abandoned it and flew into the sky to face the monster. Without hesitation, he fought back with Limitless Sword Domain, striking the monster with thousands of swords.

The sea creature didn’t even flinch as the attacks tore into its flesh, allowing thousands of gaping holes to be carved into its massive body. Yet, within moments, those wounds sealed themselves completely, the flesh knitting back together.

Seeing this, Yuan’s eyes turned sharp, and his entire body erupted with Supreme Sword Aura, the sheer intensity distorting the space around him. If minor wounds couldn’t slow the creature down, then he would carve a wound so deep it would take more than regeneration to recover.

He raised his sword, Supreme Sword Aura coiling around it like a dragon preparing to strike, and with a single thought, unleashed a devastating slash aimed to split the sea creature in half.

[Immortal Rendering Sword Strike!]

The very next moment, the massive sea creature’s colossal body was cleaved cleanly in half, but the Supreme Sword Aura continued its rampage, splitting the sea itself in a straight, endless line that stretched all the way to the distant horizon.

However, just like a demon, the sea creature immediately combined its split body back together and resumed its attack like nothing had happened.

“I see…” Yuan suddenly mumbled.

Just before the sea creature’s body fully regenerated, Yuan’s sharp eyes noticed several crystal balls embedded deep within its flesh, each glowing faintly with a subtle aura. Their presence had been completely concealed until his strike had momentarily laid the creature bare.

Now that he knew its weakness, Yuan wasted no time. His sword flickered constantly, quickly unleashing powerful sword techniques that tore through the monster’s flesh.

The moment a crystal ball was exposed, he struck them with perfectly controlled sword aura, tuned to match the aura surrounding the crystal.

One by one, the crystal balls shattered, each explosion causing the creature’s regeneration to falter more severely than the last.

The monster had thousands of crystal balls hidden within its massive body, so it took Yuan a few days to reveal and destroy them. Eventually, Yuan destroyed the last crystal ball.

Then, as if all strength had been drained from its body, the behemoth began to sink into the depths of the shimmering sea below.

“One day I will find the real you and kill you for real…” Yuan silently swore to himself as he watched the water swallow its massive form without resistance, leaving only ripples and the fading echo of its defeat.

Shortly after defeating the sea creature, the exit appeared, and Yuan left the second trial.

The first trial had taken him less than a day, but the second trial took ten days to clear.

When Yuan returned to the platform, he could see the other participants cultivating in silence, clearly resting after clearing their first trial.

Besides the Heavenly Son, every single participant was there.

After giving the other participants only a brief moment of his attention, Yuan turned to look at Senior Bai and said, “I’d like to start the next trial.”

When the other participants heard his words, they opened their eyes to look at him with disbelief all over their faces.

chapters.title: Chapter 2042: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Third Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2042: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Third Trial

Chapter 2042: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ Third Trial

Hearing that Yuan wanted to proceed to the next trial immediately after completing his second left the other participants feeling weak and inadequate, as they themselves still needed time to recover.

The Holy Sword King could only grit his teeth in frustration as he glared at Yuan, still reeling from the shock of learning that Yuan was ranked first, above even the Heavenly Son, a figure he could only dream of surpassing.

‘There’s no way he’s completed two trials while I’d just finished my first! He must be cheating somehow!’ The Holy Sword King scowled inwardly, racking his brain for an explanation, but no matter how hard he tried, he couldn’t think of a single way Yuan might have cheated.

In the end, he decided to let the others investigate instead, confident that he wasn’t the only one harboring such suspicions.

Naturally, some of the spectators did question Yuan’s astonishing pace and progress. However, having witnessed his performance firsthand, they found nothing that hinted at foul play, so they could only continue to look for anything suspicious.

“Honestly, I didn’t think you’d finish the second trial so quickly.” Senior Bai spoke as he approached the sealed door that led to the third trial.

After removing the seal on the door and opening it for Yuan, he continued, “Good luck.”

Yuan nodded and entered the third trial—but not before saying to Senior Bai, “See you soon.”

After Yuan left, the Holy Sword King asked out loud, “Senior, pardon me for asking, but are we all really taking the same trial?”

The other participants turned to look at Senior Bai. Though they all knew the answer in their hearts, they still hoped for a different answer.

“The trials are the same for everyone. There is no exception,” said Senior Bai. “If you’re worried that might not be the case, you may watch the recordings afterward.”

The Holy Sword King became silent and, without saying another word, returned to cultivating.

Meanwhile, within the second trial, Tian Yuexing steadily made his way forward. Though his pace couldn’t compare to Yuan’s, and he occasionally paused to recover between encounters, his progress was still remarkably swift. Had it not been for Yuan’s overwhelming presence, he would have undoubtedly claimed the spotlight in the Sword Emperor Qualifications.

‘Fuck… which bastard created this year’s Sword Emperor Qualifications? I’d like to have a word with them after this!’ Tian Yuexing cursed the creator inwardly as he continued to advance.

At the same time, Yuan began his third trial. Shortly after stepping through the door, Yuan found himself standing in the middle of a vast, barren field, surrounded by nothing but dirt and scattered pebbles.

He looked around but sensed nothing until the ground suddenly began to tremble, as if an earthquake had erupted beneath his feet.

Soon, the source of the tremor revealed itself—a horde of magical beasts surged over the horizon, thundering toward Yuan like a relentless stampede.

Yuan immediately shifted into a battle stance. But just as he was about to strike, a sharp and powerful ripple swept across the field. Before he could even react, the entire horde of magical beasts was slain in the blink of an eye, their bodies cleanly severed in half.

The next moment, Yuan watched as a figure descended from the sky, landing just a few meters away.

He was a handsome man clad in green robes, with short silver hair and a black sword gripped firmly in his hand. Though Yuan didn’t recognize him, many among the spectators did, especially the swordmasters, whose expressions filled with awe the moment they saw him.

“The Jade Sword Emperor!” Kelan remarked after seeing this handsome man.

“Who?” Wang Ming asked.

Li Jinxi answered instead, “The first swordmaster to claim the title of Sword Emperor. He’s basically the first officially recognized Sword Emperor in history.”

Moments after his arrival, the Jade Sword Emperor raised his black sword and, without the slightest warning, unleashed a relentless barrage of sword techniques at Yuan.

Thousands of green swords materialized around Yuan, swirling rapidly to form a powerful, sword-laced tornado that howled with destructive force.

However, despite its impressive display, the technique lacked the strength to truly threaten Yuan, as the Jade Sword Emperor’s cultivation was only at the Bronze Immortal level.

Realizing that his opponent possessed only the strength of a Bronze Immortal, Yuan paid no heed to the raging tornado around him and flew straight through it. In a flash, he closed the distance to the Jade Sword Emperor and, with a single decisive strike, cut down the handsome swordsman where he stood.

“…”

The spectators were left stunned by the outcome, not because of Yuan’s overwhelming prowess but because his opponent appeared too weak.

“What just happened? Why was his opponent so weak?” someone in the crowd exclaimed, their voice echoing through the area.

“Is there something wrong with the trial? He didn’t even do anything special to defeat the Jade Sword Emperor.”

“That’s…”

Senior Bai pondered a proper response.

The third trial was meant to test a participant’s talents and limits by pitting them against an opponent with superior cultivation, hence why Yuan, who was only at the peak of Immortal Ascension, had to fight against a Bronze Immortal.

However, Yuan was powerful enough to slay even True Immortals at his current level, making a mere Bronze Immortal hardly worth his attention.

Naturally, the spectators were unaware of the truth. In fact, most were convinced that Yuan was suppressing his cultivation to the Immortal Ascension realm, firmly believing that his true strength lay within the God Ascension Realm, hence why they were confused when the trial gave Yuan such an easy opponent.

But before Senior Bai could clear the misunderstanding, Yuan’s next opponent appeared—another Sword Emperor from the past, this one possessing the cultivation of a Silver Immortal.

In Yuan’s eyes, the third trial felt a little too easy. Still, he assumed it was merely the warm-up phase and that the real challenge had yet to begin. So, without giving it much thought, he effortlessly defeated the Silver Immortal.

chapters.title: Chapter 2043: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Third Trial(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2043: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Third Trial(2)

Chapter 2043: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ Third Trial(2)

Shortly after Yuan defeated his second opponent, a third figure emerged—once known as the Mad Sword Emperor, one of the most infamous Sword Emperors in history for the atrocities he committed. However, with his cultivation only at the Silver Immortal level, he posed little challenge to Yuan and was swiftly defeated as well.

The fourth and fifth Sword Emperors arrived soon after, and, as expected, Yuan defeated them both with ease.

The spectators were left utterly speechless—and some, especially those who had suspected Yuan of cheating, were visibly enraged.

“What the hell is this?! Why are all of his opponents so damn weak?! Compared to the first two trials, this one is laughably easy! Hell, many of us here can clear it without any issues!”

“Right?! Are we still watching the Sword Emperor Qualifications or something else?!”

“I’ll be right back,” Senior Bai said to the resting participants before stepping off the platform to address the situation.

The platform was protected by a soundproof formation, preventing any of the spectators’ voices from reaching the participants. Still, the participants could sense that something was wrong, judging by the crowd’s restless movements and increasingly agitated expressions.

It wasn’t just the spectators’ voices that were blocked—the participants were also unable to see the display screens showing the ongoing trials. This measure was in place to prevent them from gaining any foresight, ensuring the trials remained fair for everyone.

After leaving the platform, Senior Bai used a voice amplification technique, his words rippling through the entire space, reaching every corner, no matter how far the listeners were.

“I understand your confusion, but I assure you there is nothing wrong with the trials,” Senior Bai began, his voice steady as it echoed through the space. “Everything is progressing exactly as intended.”

He then went on to explain the nature of the third trial.

“In the third trial, participants must face a series of opponents, each possessing superior cultivation. These opponents are none other than Sword Emperors from the past, and the participants must rely solely on their sword techniques to prevail.”

Lastly, he addressed the reason behind the apparent disparity in Yuan’s opponents.

“As for why Yuan’s opponents are only at the Immortal Realm,” Senior Bai continued, “it is because his true cultivation is only at the peak of the Immortal Ascension Realm.”

“What?!”

“Impossible!”

Upon hearing that Yuan was only at the Immortal Ascension Realm, countless figures sprang to their feet in utter disbelief, their faces frozen in shock. Across the Nine Heavens, stunned gasps echoed from those watching from afar, unable to comprehend what they had just heard.

“Th-that’s his true cultivation…? He’s participating in the Sword Emperor Qualification as a mortal…?”

From the spectators gathered at the Gates of Heaven to the Tian Family watching from their estate in the Supreme Heaven, it was, without question, the most shocking revelation of the entire event.

None of them had ever heard of a mortal comprehending the Supreme Sword Aura, let alone participating in the Sword Emperor Qualifications.

“If you still doubt my words, I will verify his cultivation before everyone once he comes out of the third trial,” Senior Bai added.

Hearing this, the crowd could no longer complain without looking foolish and disrespectful. And so, everyone waited in quiet anticipation for Yuan to complete his third trial, which, unsurprisingly, didn’t take long at all.

Just a few hours after it began, Yuan had defeated a total of ten opponents and cleared the trial, completing it even faster than his first.

When Yuan returned to the platform, the others were still resting—at least until they saw him emerge so soon.

“For you to be back already, you must’ve given up on the trial! Hahaha!” the Holy Sword King laughed loudly, assuming Yuan had used the talisman to forfeit and disqualify himself.

Yuan slowly turned to the Holy Sword King, his voice calm and composed.

“If you have the energy to laugh that loudly, perhaps you should start your second trial. You won’t beat me by sitting there.”

The Holy Sword King scoffed, his tone defiant.

“So what if I’m a little behind? In the end, only the final result matters—and you’ve already forfeited! Once I pass the third trial, that’s my victory!”

“Forfeited? Says who?” Yuan replied.

“What…?” The Holy Sword King’s expression stiffened. If Yuan hadn’t forfeited, had he actually completed the third trial, which was supposed to be even more difficult than the first two, in even less time?

“There’s no way…” he muttered under his breath, his voice barely audible, like a whisper carried by a mosquito.

“Speaking of the third trial…” Senior Bai’s voice suddenly resounded, and moments later, he approached Yuan.

“I’m sorry, but due to some complications, I have to verify your cultivation.”

“Complications…? I knew it! You were cheating this whole time!” The Holy Sword King exclaimed.

However, Senior Bai quickly corrected him, “No, he’s not being accused of cheating. We just need to verify his cultivation to clear up some misunderstanding and confusion.”

“That doesn’t make any sense!”

Senior Bai didn’t bother trying to explain further and merely said, “The trial is still ongoing. If you continue to disturb the other participants, I’ll have to disqualify you.”

The Holy Sword King clicked his tongue in annoyance but said nothing further. Rising to his feet, he turned and silently made his way toward the door leading to the second trial.

Meanwhile, the other participants stayed behind to see what the deal with Yuan was.

“Go ahead and test my cultivation,” Yuan said to Senior Bai, his voice calm and without the slightest trace of displeasure. He trusted Senior Bai and knew he wasn’t acting out of malice.

Senior Bai gave a silent nod and signaled to someone. Moments later, a middle-aged man stepped onto the platform, carrying a cultivation-measuring device—one of the most advanced and accurate tools in all the Nine Heavens.

The middle-aged man activated the treasure, and without requiring any action from Yuan, a series of intricate formations unfolded around him, encircling him in a shimmering array of light.

chapters.title: Chapter 2044: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Fourth Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2044: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Fourth Trial

Chapter 2044: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ Fourth Trial

Every spectator watched with immense attention as the cultivation-measuring device assessed Yuan’s true cultivation.

Moments later, the device completed its assessment, and the words “Ninth Level Immortal Ascension” appeared in glowing script for all to see.

“He’s really a mortal!”

Several voices rang out across the crowd, filled with disbelief, awe, and a hint of reverence.

Though nobody there could comprehend how a mortal managed to learn Supreme Sword Aura or qualify for the Sword Emperor Qualifications, they were unable to deny the evidence before their very eyes.

“To think our son is being surpassed by a mere mortal…” Tian Yuexing’s father muttered, his face marked with bewilderment.

Even his wife, typically nonchalant and expressionless, couldn’t hide the surprise in her eyes.

“My god… where did such a genius come from? If we recruit him into our family and pair him with Tian Jinhui, the Tian Family might finally surpass the Celestial Family,” one of the elders remarked, eyes gleaming with ambition.

“…”

Though no one responded aloud, everyone was thinking the same thing.

Of course, the Tian Family wasn’t the only one thinking about recruiting Yuan into their family or sect, as nearly every influential background harbored the same intention and desire.

“H-He’s a mortal…?”

Every participant, except the Holy Sword Maiden, stared at Yuan in shock, their faces frozen with disbelief. It was a simple fact, yet none of them could fully grasp the weight of such an outrageous revelation.

They had been competing and losing against a mortal this whole time? None of them wanted to believe such a thing. They were all some of the most talented swordmasters across the Nine Heavens, but they were losing to a mere mortal? It wouldn’t make sense even in their dreams.

“Haha… someone slap me and tell me I’m dreaming…” one of the participants muttered with a dazed chuckle, his expression blank—like someone who had just suffered a mental blow.

“Was that satisfactory?” Yuan asked Senior Bai, who nodded with a warm smile on his face.

“Yes, it was. Once again, I apologize for the inconvenience.”

“Then I would like to start my next trial,” Yuan continued.

“Of course…”

Without delay, Senior Bai opened the fourth door for him.

And without a word, Yuan stepped through and vanished, beginning his fourth trial and widening the gap between himself and the other participants even further.

Shortly after Yuan’s departure, a heavy atmosphere settled over the remaining participants. One by one, without saying a word, they rose and stepped through the second door, each of them feeling an immense pressure not to be outshone by a mortal.

“This guy is quite the monster,” remarked Azure after she watched the events unfold.

Xiang Biyu smiled faintly and said, “I always knew he would shake the world with his existence, but even I couldn’t have imagined how far he’d come in just a few decades. You probably won’t believe this, but he was still a Spirit King when I met him about twenty years ago.”

Meanwhile, within the fourth trial, Yuan found himself standing before a majestic mountain, its towering peak pierced by a colossal sword embedded deep into the summit. A single path spiraled upward along the mountainside, reminiscent of the famed Dragon Spiral Mountain.

Yuan quickly noticed that his cultivation had been sealed, which meant it was likely that there would be no combat for this trial. After taking a moment to survey his surroundings, he stepped onto the path and began his ascent up the mountain.

Shortly after beginning his ascent, Yuan came across rows of swords embedded in the ground on either side of the path, positioned in perfect parallel, resembling a line of guardrails guiding the way.

At first, the swords appeared to be nothing more than ordinary weapons. But as Yuan climbed higher, they began to emit Sword Aura—faint at first, then gradually intensifying with every step, growing stronger the farther he ascended.

When the swords first began to emit Sword Aura, Yuan quickly realized he couldn’t proceed any farther unless he cloaked himself in Sword Aura stronger than that radiating from the swords themselves. If he tried to advance without doing so, he’d be blocked by an invisible barrier.

Meanwhile, outside the trial grounds, Senior Bai addressed the spectators, offering a detailed explanation of the fourth trial.

“To pass the fourth trial, participants must ascend the mountain,” he said. “But to do so, they must possess a sufficient level of comprehension in Sword Aura. The higher they climb, the deeper their understanding must be, or they will be blocked.”

He continued, “That said, reaching the peak is not required to pass the fourth trial. Once a participant reaches a certain height, an exit will appear, allowing them to leave. However, if they choose to continue climbing, additional rewards will be granted based on how far they go.”

“What level of comprehension is required to reach the peak?” someone suddenly asked.

“A good question,” Senior Bai responded. “To reach the summit, one must possess a near-perfect mastery of the Supreme Sword Aura, a level reached only by experienced Sword Emperors.”

The spectators were visibly surprised by the steep requirement. After all, most participants in the Sword Emperor Qualifications had yet to master the Supreme Sword Aura—an achievement so profound it was usually attained only after one became a Sword Emperor. For many, pursuing such mastery beforehand was seen as impractical, as it demanded an immense amount of time.

A few hours later, Yuan reached the point where the swords began to emit Enhanced Sword Aura. Though he could have arrived much sooner, he had chosen to walk at a leisurely pace, treating the climb as a peaceful stroll rather than a race. In fact, he would occasionally stop just to admire the view.

A day later, the swords around him started to emit Supreme Sword Aura.

Meanwhile, Tian Yuexing and the other participants were still struggling through the second trial. For those who had only recently entered, the harsh reality of the trial’s difficulty was just beginning to sink in, and with it, their hopes for the trials ahead quickly began to wither.

chapters.title: Chapter 2045: A Freak
chapters.body:
Chapter 2045: A Freak

Chapter 2045: A Freak

“That mortal managed to pass this trial in just ten days?! That’s ridiculous!”

When the other participants experienced the brutality of the second trial for themselves, none of them could believe that a mortal had managed to pass it in such a short time.

Though everyone progressed swiftly at first, they eventually slowed down after the number of enemies they had to deal with increased with each counter.

Moreover, one of the participants had already fallen, losing their physical body to the first invisible sword and being forced to forfeit the remainder of the Sword Emperor Qualifications.

Several others sustained injuries from the same unseen force, but as God Ascension cultivators, such wounds were easily endured and largely ignored.

Meanwhile, Yuan continued his steady ascent up the mountain, his body enveloped in Supreme Sword Aura.

Since maintaining Sword Aura required Soul Strength, Yuan had no difficulty keeping it active without rest. However, the audience was astonished by how effortlessly he sustained it, especially now that they knew he was only a mortal.

At first, they hadn’t thought much of it, assuming he was a God Ascension Realm cultivator. But after the truth was revealed, their shock deepened. For a mere mortal to possess Soul Strength on par with gods was nothing short of inconceivable.

Five days later, Yuan was still ascending the mountain. With each step, he had to gradually intensify the strength of his Supreme Sword Aura and keep it constantly active.

After a week within the fourth trial, Yuan finally reached the point required to pass. A shimmering exit portal quietly appeared behind him, signaling the trial’s completion.

Yuan raised an eyebrow at the sight of the exit. He could clearly see that the path continued upward.

After a brief moment of thought, he turned away from the portal and resumed his climb, completely ignoring it. In his eyes, the exit was nothing more than a distraction—placed there to tempt him into leaving before the true trial had ended.

Another week passed, and after spending a total of two weeks within the fourth trial, Yuan finally reached a point where he could no longer advance, as his current understanding of the Supreme Sword Aura had reached its limit.

But he didn’t turn back.

Instead, he sat down calmly, closed his eyes, and began to meditate, determined to push his comprehension even further.

“There’s no way…”

The audience almost couldn’t believe what they were seeing.

“Is he… trying to advance his Supreme Sword Aura? In the middle of the trial?”

Although Yuan was now close to the mountain’s peak, the remaining distance—though seemingly short—was as vast as the gap between heaven and earth. After all, even Sword Emperors required hundreds of thousands of years to approach perfection in their Supreme Sword Aura. For Yuan to attempt the same within the trial meant he was willingly giving away his first-place position.

“There’s no denying his talent, but he’s too arrogant,” one of the spectators scoffed.

“Does he really think he can perfect his Supreme Sword Aura in just a few years? It’s like he’s mocking every swordmaster who’s ever lived.”

The Sword Emperors present remained silent, but subtle displeasure flickered in their expressions. Though their faces betrayed little, a quiet tension hung around them.

If Yuan managed to deepen his mastery of the Supreme Sword Aura during the trial—even by a small margin—it would cast a shadow over their own accomplishments, making a mockery of the centuries, even millennia, they had spent refining their Supreme Sword Aura.

As time went on, the other participants steadily advanced through the second trial.

After nearly three months into his trial, the Heavenly Son, who was far ahead of the rest, finally reached the end of the path, where he was met by the final challenge: a replica of the legendary True Emperor of the Seas.

As for the other participants, a few were forced to forfeit midway, unable to endure the relentless pressure of the trial.

Just as the Heavenly Son began his battle with the True Emperor of the Seas, Yuan suddenly opened his eyes and rose to his feet.

The audience, who had shifted their attention away from him to focus on the others, immediately snapped their heads back toward him.

“There’s no chance in hell he improved his Supreme Sword Aura in just a few months…” someone muttered in disbelief, echoing the thoughts of many.

However, Yuan wasted no time in proving them wrong.

In the very next moment, his Supreme Sword Aura—previously stagnant—began to surge, its intensity rising steadily, shocking all who watched.

“He actually improved his Supreme Sword Aura in months! This guy is a monster—a freak!”

“A sword freak!”

At that moment, many had already made up their minds—if Yuan truly became a Sword Emperor, they would call him the Freak Sword Emperor.

However, despite the clear advancement in his Supreme Sword Aura, Yuan still couldn’t reach the peak. After only a few more steps, he was once again forced to stop.

Without hesitation, he sat down and resumed his cultivation, determined to break through the next threshold.

“H-He’s trying to improve his Supreme Sword Aura again!”

“This guy is ridiculous!”

“Call me crazy, but I think this guy might be even more monstrous than the Faceless Sword Emperor.”

“I don’t doubt it.”

“My god… does he not intend to leave us even a shred of face?” muttered the Holy Sword King’s father, his expression frozen in utter disbelief.

“If ‘that’ guy is watching now, I wonder what he’s thinking,” said the Holy Sword Maiden’s father.

“You mean the Peerless Sword Emperor?” Sword Empress Huang said.

The Peerless Sword Emperor was the current strongest Sword Emperor in the Nine Heavens, widely believed by many to have even surpassed the legendary Faceless Sword Emperor.

“That guy’s probably itching to fight with him, but with the disparity between their cultivation… he’ll have no choice but to wait.”

Soon, another month passed, and the Heavenly Son had finally defeated the fake True Emperor of the Seas, passing the second trial.

chapters.title: Chapter 2046: A True Freak
chapters.body:
Chapter 2046: A True Freak

Chapter 2046: A True Freak

After defeating the final opponent and clearing the second trial, the Heavenly Son, Tian Yuexing, returned to the platform outside with a weary expression.

Though he had experienced battles that stretched for years without pause, none of them compared to the sheer strain and intensity of the past few months within the trial.

When Tian Yuexing returned to the platform, the first thing he did was glance around, searching for Yuan. After confirming he was alone, he lifted his gaze to the rankings above, hoping—perhaps even expecting—that he had finally surpassed him.

But the moment his eyes landed on the display, they widened in disbelief.

Yuan’s name still stood firmly above his own, now marked with 300 points, while his score remained at 190.

“Three hundred points?!” Tian Yuexing exclaimed aloud, his voice filled with disbelief.

“Does that mean he’s already completed the second and the third trial?!”

Realizing that the gap between him and Yuan had widened, Tian Yuexing immediately turned to look at the door to the third trial, and after a brief moment of hesitation, entered it without resting.

When his parents saw this, they could only shake their heads inwardly at his rash decision.

“Though he’s undoubtedly one of the most talented individuals in the Nine Heavens, he still lacks the patience his sister possesses,” remarked one of the Tian Family elders.

“What can we do? He’s too prideful for his own good sometimes,” another elder said with a sigh.

Meanwhile, Tian Jinhui, who had been quietly observing from one of the spectators’ islands, shook her head and let out a soft sigh.

“Will it kill you to stop comparing yourself to others?” she muttered. “You don’t have to be the best at everything.”

Ever since his birth, Tian Yuexing had been first in pretty much everything he did, as if he were destined to be number one. In his mind, not being number one meant something was wrong, and he would stop at nothing to correct it.

Of course, it wasn’t as though Tian Yuexing had never experienced defeat. It was just never at the hands of someone from his own generation.

“I’ve never seen the Heavenly Son acting like this before.”

“Probably because he’s never had to chase after someone this badly. It’s honestly quite refreshing.”

Those who had once been defeated by Tian Yuexing—or had long harbored envy toward his overwhelming talent—couldn’t help but rejoice at the sight of him finally being the inferior one.

Shortly after entering the third trial, Tian Yuexing encountered the Jade Sword Emperor.

However, unlike during Yuan’s trial, the Jade Sword Emperor now possessed the cultivation of a fourth-level God Ascension expert, one full level above Tian Yuexing, who was only at the third level.

Although being at the third level of God Ascension might not seem particularly impressive at first glance—especially for the number one genius in the Nine Heavens—one had to consider Tian Yuexing’s age. He was still under a hundred thousand years old, making his cultivation level nothing short of extraordinary.

At birth, Tian Yuexing’s cultivation was already at the Immortal Ascension Realm. It took him less than a thousand years to step into immortality, and before reaching ten thousand years of age, he had already ascended to the True Immortal Realm.

As for the other participants, they were all only at the first level of God Ascension realm while being older than Tian Yuexing.

“That appearance… You must be the Jade Sword Emperor!” Tian Yuexing exclaimed, a glint of excitement in his eyes. “If I weren’t in a hurry, I’d relish the chance for a proper duel!”

Without wasting another moment, he unleashed his full power right from the start.

Though he lagged behind Yuan in the Sword Emperor Qualifications, Tian Yuexing was a true prodigy, hailed as the number one genius of the Nine Heavens for good reason.

During his battle with the Jade Sword Emperor, he reminded everyone of exactly why he held that title, defeating his opponent decisively in just a few days.

Yet, when the audience compared his performance to Yuan’s—who had defeated the Jade Sword Emperor in just a few strikes—Tian Yuexing’s victory, while impressive, no longer seemed extraordinary.

While Tian Yuexing continued to chase after him with everything he had, Yuan had already advanced his Supreme Sword Aura again—this time, in even less time than before.

“What?! He’s improved his Supreme Sword Aura already?!” The spectators had to do a double-take when they saw Yuan ascending the mountain again.

“How did it take him even less time to improve this time?! Shouldn’t it take even longer than before?! It doesn’t make any sense!”

“A freak! He’s a true freak!”

“Good heavens… I’d do anything for his genes…” one of the female spectators suddenly muttered, drawing startled stares from those around her.

Yet, no one could truly blame her. After all, who in their right mind would refuse the chance to bear the child of a once-in-a-lifetime genius?

Forget about the females. Even the males there wouldn’t mind if they could give birth.

After taking just a few more steps, Yuan came to a halt once again.

Now, he stood mere steps away from the mountain’s peak. The summit was within sight, yet still just barely out of reach.

While Yuan continued refining his Supreme Sword Aura with steady progress, the Heavenly Son found himself struggling.

Having expended too much energy early on in an attempt to rush through the trial, he was now forced to slow down, and he had only defeated six opponents so far.

As for the other participants, aside from the Holy Sword King and the Holy Sword Maiden, they were hardly worth mentioning. In fact, the spectators had long since lost interest in them, their attention wholly consumed by Yuan and the Heavenly Son.

At the Tian Family, Tian Yuexing’s father, who had been sitting down since the beginning of the Sword Emperor Qualifications, suddenly stood up and left his seat.

“My lord, where are you going?” one of the elders asked.

Without turning around, he spoke as he walked toward the exit, “The Seventh Heaven.”

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 2047: I Caught Up!
chapters.body:
Chapter 2047: I Caught Up!

Chapter 2047: I Caught Up!

“The Seventh Heaven? Is it for that mortal?” another elder asked, raising an eyebrow.

“If you’re thinking of recruiting him, we’ve already sent someone,” another replied. “The Young Lady is there as well—there’s no need for you to go personally.”

Tian Yuexing’s father shook his head and sighed.

“Do any of you even understand what sincerity is? We’re talking about a mortal who not only qualifies as a Sword Emperor, but whose talent points to a future without limits.”

“But to send the head of the family so soon… we might come across as desperate,” someone else remarked.

Once a background or individual reached a certain level of influence and power, there was little left to pursue but reputation. At that point, face became everything—an asset so valuable that countless experts would willingly risk their lives just to preserve it.

“So what if we lose a little face? If it helps us surpass that arrogant Celestial Family, then it’s a small price to pay!”

“Even if you say that…” one of the elders began, hesitating. “Wouldn’t it be better to let the Young Lady recruit him instead? That way, not only would a connection be established, but it would deepen far more quickly.”

“I agree,” another elder chimed in. “The Young Lady isn’t just a top beauty, but she’s also exceptionally persuasive. It would be best to let her handle the recruitment.”

Tian Yuexing’s father fell silent, deep in thought. After a brief pause, he returned to his seat.

“Very well,” he said at last. “We’ll let Jinhui handle this—for now.”

He then added, “Also, you’re all moving too fast. Forming a connection with him is one thing—having him join our family is another matter entirely. No matter how talented he is, I won’t simply hand Jinhui over without first testing him thoroughly. He must prove that he’s truly worthy.”

“That’s only natural.” The others nodded their heads in agreement.

After another month, Tian Yuexing finally defeated his tenth opponent, successfully passing the third trial.

Upon returning to the platform, Tian Yuexing immediately looked up at the rankings. He let out a quiet sigh of relief when he saw that Yuan’s score hadn’t changed. He had managed to narrow the gap.
