“I can do it… I will catch up to him,” he muttered to himself, determination flashing in his eyes as he turned to face the door to the fourth trial.

He considered resting for a moment since the third trial had taken a serious toll on him, both physically and mentally.

But in the end, he couldn’t bring himself to stop. After all the effort it had taken to close the gap, he couldn’t risk falling behind again.

After taking a deep breath, Tian Yuexing entered the fourth door, beginning his fourth trial.

“The Heavenly Son has caught up! They’re both in the fourth trial now!” The spectators exclaimed with excitement.

“No, the Heavenly Son is still behind the mortal.”

“For now, but he’ll definitely catch up. Look at how fast he’s sprinting up the mountain,” said another spectator.

Once Tian Yuexing understood the objective of the fourth trial, he didn’t hesitate, immediately beginning his ascent with unwavering determination.

While Yuan had taken his time, even pausing to enjoy the scenery along the way, Tian Yuexing advanced with immense focus. His eyes never wavered from the path ahead, and he had no interest in anything but reaching the peak as fast as possible.

Within just a few hours, Tian Yuexing reached the section of the mountain that required Supreme Sword Aura.

By the next day, he had climbed far enough to meet the requirement for passing the trial, and a glowing exit portal appeared before him.

Tian Yuexing paused at the threshold, taking a moment to consider his next move. It was the first time he had truly rested since completing the third trial.

Lifting his gaze toward the mountain peak, he could sense the overwhelming Supreme Sword Aura radiating from above—it was far beyond what he could currently withstand. Reaching the summit was, for now, out of the question.

Accepting this, Tian Yuexing turned toward the portal and prepared to exit the trial.

Moments later, Tian Yuexing stepped back onto the platform. Just like before, the first thing he did was lift his head to check the rankings.

“I—I did it! I caught up!” Tian Yuexing exclaimed, eyes wide with satisfaction and triumph.

Clenching his fists, he stood tall with excitement surging through him, as his name now shone at the top of the rankings, just above Yuan’s.

It was the first time Tian Yuexing had ever felt such genuine excitement from being in first place.

Normally, it was something he accepted with calm indifference—an expectation rather than a thrill. But this time was different. This victory felt earned.

Yet, despite Tian Yuexing finally claiming first place, nobody seemed to be paying attention to him. Their eyes were fixed elsewhere, their attention completely consumed by another presence.

A few seconds before Tian Yuexiang completed the fourth trial, Yuan, who had been cultivating in silence this whole time, opened his eyes and slowly stood up.

The instant the spectators saw his aura, a wave of shock swept through the crowd. Their eyes widened, expressions frozen in disbelief.

“H-He didn’t just reach near-perfection…” someone gasped. “This freak actually perfected his Supreme Sword Aura!”

Yuan only needed to reach near perfection to climb to the mountain’s peak, but he went above and beyond by perfecting his Supreme Sword Aura.

Shrouded in perfected Supreme Sword Aura, a level attained only by true Sword Emperors, Yuan took the final few steps that brought him to the summit of the mountain.

As Yuan returned to the platform, his gaze immediately landed on Tian Yuexing, who stood not far away.

Tian Yuexing’s eyes were fixed on the rankings, and a look of satisfaction lingered on his face, completely unaware that Yuan had returned.

A second later, Yuan watched as Tian Yuexing’s blissful expression twisted with shock and horror.

The ranking had updated, and the position had changed again. Now, Tian Yuexing was once again behind Yuan.

“What?!” Tian Yuexing cried out loud, sounding as if a ghost spooked him.

chapters.title: Chapter 2048: Esteemed Guests
chapters.body:
Chapter 2048: Esteemed Guests

Chapter 2048: Esteemed Guests

After staring at the rankings for a moment, Tian Yuexing suddenly snapped out of his daze.

His head whipped around—

“You!” he exclaimed, eyes locking onto Yuan.

“What about me?” Yuan calmly responded.

“How did you acquire 200 points when I clearly finished the fourth trial first?! You cheated!” Tian Yuexing questioned, even throwing cheating accusations.

Yuan lifted his head to look at the points, and sure enough, he had 500 points while Tian Yuexing only had 385 points.

He shrugged and replied, “How am I supposed to know? I didn’t make the rules, nor do I know how it works.”

Hearing their exchange, Senior Bai stepped forward and said, “Allow me to explain the scoring system.”

“The first person to complete a trial is awarded a full one hundred points, and each person who finishes after that receives five points fewer than the one before.”

“In this case, you received a hundred points for finishing first,” Senior Bai said, glancing at Tian Yuexing.

“However, the fourth trial is unique in the way that it has two finish lines. While you completed the first, Yuan went above and beyond and managed to reach the peak of the mountain, which is substantially harder, hence the two hundred points.”

“He climbed to the peak of the mountain?! That’s impossible!” Tian Yuexing exclaimed, his voice tinged with disbelief.

He vividly remembers the Supreme Sword Aura radiating from the summit being near perfection. Even with his level of talent, he was still far from reaching that level.

The thought that Yuan’s Supreme Sword Aura was so vastly superior to his own was almost too much for Tian Yuexing to accept.

However, what truly irritated him was the possibility that he might never get to surpass Yuan again and reclaim first place.

There was only one trial left, and he was still 115 points behind. Even if he finished first and earned the full 100 points, he would still fall short—unless, by some miracle, Yuan were to be disqualified during the trial.

As if Senior Bai could read Tian Yuexing’s mind, he suddenly spoke, “There is still a chance to catch up in the final trial. Though it won’t be easy, it’s possible.”

“Is that true?!” Tian Yuexing trembled with excitement, and his eyes reignited with ambition.

Senior Bai nodded, “Yes. I can’t give you any details, but it’s definitely possible.”

Hearing this, Tian Yuexing turned to look at Yuan and said, “Why don’t we both take a rest and enter the final trial at the same time, a week from now? Unless you’re too afraid of me passing you, that is.”

Yuan smiled behind his mask and said, “You don’t need to provoke me. If you want me to slow down for you, just ask.”

“T-That’s not it!” Tian Yuexing blurted out, clearly flustered by Yuan’s words.

Yuan casually took a seat on the platform and said, “Clearly, the last few trials have worn you out. I’ll give you a week to catch your breath.”

Tian Yuexing trembled, a burning sensation creeping across his face, as if Yuan’s words had slapped him harder than any hand ever could.

But he held his tongue, afraid that saying anything might make Yuan change his mind and proceed to the final trial immediately.

Though he had managed to get some rest during the fourth trial, the mental strain still weighed heavily on him. He needed the time, whether he admitted it or not.

And so, the two began their rest in preparation for the final trial.

However, while Tian Yuexing focused on restoring every ounce of his strength, Yuan appeared far more relaxed. Though his expression was hidden behind a mask, the audience couldn’t shake the feeling that he wasn’t cultivating at all but just sitting there casually, as if simply waiting for Tian Yuexing to finish.

As the two rested, the audience buzzed with speculation over who would ultimately claim first place.

While many were undeniably impressed by Yuan, the majority still placed their bets on Tian Yuexing. Despite everything that had happened, they believed that the number one genius of the Nine Heavens would not allow himself to be overtaken in the end.

As the days went by, more and more people gathered at the Gates of Heaven, drawn by the growing anticipation surrounding the final trial and the rivalry between Yuan and Tian Yuexing.

On the sixth day of their rest—just one day before the final trial was set to begin—a new commotion stirred within the Gates of Heaven.

But this time, it wasn’t caused by any of the participants.

The source of the commotion was a grand silver carriage, steadily approaching from a distance. Draped in a majestic silver-and-blue flag bearing an unmistakable emblem, it radiated an aura of unquestionable prestige and authority.

Surrounding the carriage was a formidable escort—an entire contingent of God Ascension realm cultivators—moving in perfect formation as it advanced, further emphasizing the significance of its arrival.

The moment the audience caught sight of the emblem, a hush fell over the crowd. Regardless of status or cultivation, everyone rose to their feet and bowed their heads in silent respect, paying wordless tribute to the ones within the carriage.

Noticing the sudden shift in the crowd’s behavior, Yuan turned his gaze toward the approaching carriage. His eyes narrowed at the emblem, a flicker of recognition flashing behind his mask.

When Senior Bai noticed them, he flew off the platform without a word, making his way toward the newcomers to personally welcome them.

The carriage came to a halt moments later, and one of the God Ascension realm cultivators opened the door. From within, two figures emerged, each exuding a presence that immediately commanded the crowd’s attention and awe.

“Welcome to the Gates of Heaven, esteemed guests from the Celestial Family,” Senior Bai greeted them respectfully.

The newcomers were none other than members of the Celestial Family—the number one family in the Nine Heavens and the direct bloodline of the Celestial Emperor himself.

chapters.title: Chapter 2049: Celestial Family
chapters.body:
Chapter 2049: Celestial Family

Chapter 2049: Celestial Family

“It’s been a while, Sect Leader Bai,” said one of the two figures—a stunning woman clad in pure white robes, her silver accessories shimmering with grace. Her beauty was ethereal, almost otherworldly, resembling that of a celestial fairy descended from the heavens.

“It has indeed been a while, Madam Jun,” Senior Bai responded with a respectful nod. “Had I known of your arrival in advance, I would have prepared a more fitting welcome.”

“Don’t sweat about it,” Madam Jun replied with a gentle smile. “My daughter and I were simply passing through when I remembered the Sword Emperor Qualifications were underway, so we thought we’d stop by for a quick look. Don’t mind us and act like we’re not even here.”

Senior Bai nodded and returned to the platform.

“Who are they?” Yuan asked Senior Bai, pretending not to know about the Celestial Family.

“You don’t know—oh, right.” Senior Bai almost forgot that Yuan was originally from Earth.

“That is the Celestial Family. They’re the Celestial Emperor’s family,” he gave a brief explanation.

“Are they here to watch the Sword Emperor Qualifications?” Yuan continued to ask.

Senior Bai nodded, “Yes, they showed up on a whim since they were nearby.”

“I see…” Yuan breathed an inward sigh of relief upon realizing that the Celestial Family hadn’t come because of him. For now, their presence was merely a coincidence and not a confrontation, and he hoped it stayed that way.

While Yuan waited for Tian Yuexing to finish resting, the Celestial Family analyzed the situation.

“The Heavenly Son, huh? I’d almost forgotten he was participating,” Madam Jun muttered as her gaze settled on Tian Yuexing.

She spared a brief glance at Yuan, but quickly lost interest—until her eyes shifted to the ranking board.

“Huh?” A soft, dumbfounded sound escaped her lips as she noticed something unexpected: Tian Yuexing’s name was not at the top.

“Yuan?” the young lady beside her echoed softly, a flicker of curiosity lighting up her eyes as she spoke this name, her voice tinged with intrigue.

No one was more familiar with Tian Yuexing and the Tian Family than the Celestial Family, whose rivalry with them stretched back long before they were ever granted the title of Celestial.

Though they were known as the Celestial Family now—thanks to their connection to the current Celestial Emperor—that hadn’t always been their name.

In truth, the Celestial Family was more of a title than a permanent lineage. Whenever a new Celestial Emperor was chosen, their family would inherit the title, becoming the next Celestial Family for the duration of their reign.

“Now this is a real surprise,” Madam Jun chuckled, amusement glinting in her eyes. “Who would’ve thought that arrogant little prick would end up beneath someone? Just witnessing this made the entire trip worthwhile.”

However, what puzzled her was the unfamiliar name. If someone more talented than Tian Yuexing existed, there was no way she wouldn’t have heard of him before now.

Madam Jun turned her gaze to the screen displaying the participants’ trials, intent on finding Yuan, unaware that she had been looking directly at him just moments earlier.

When she saw that all of the participants being displayed were doing the same trial, Madam Jun turned to look at the masked figure again, this time with more attention.

“He’s… a mortal?” Madam Jun almost couldn’t believe her eyes.

What was a mortal doing in the Sword Emperor Qualifications? Was he Yuan? How did he manage to overtake Tian Yuexing?

Madam Jun had many questions in her mind.

Instead of asking Senior Bai—the quickest and most direct way to learn about Yuan—Madam Jun chose another approach. She turned to one of her guards and gave a quiet command to gather information on him.

After receiving her command, the God Ascension realm cultivator swiftly made his way to the spectator area and began questioning those nearby. The spectators, eager to speak, wasted no time in revealing everything they knew about Yuan, from his feats to the remarkable achievements he had displayed throughout the trials.

As the God Ascension realm cultivator listened to the spectators, he couldn’t help but feel dumbfounded.

The details they provided sounded so exaggerated and unbelievable that it felt more like they were reciting a fantasy story.

However, he knew there was no way the spectators would dare lie so blatantly in front of the Celestial Family.

With that in mind, the God Ascension cultivator returned to Madam Jun shortly after and dutifully recited everything he had learned.

Madam Jun and the young lady beside her listened with wide eyes, their faces full of disbelief and shock.

“He’s an actual mortal?” Madam Jun repeated, disbelief flickering in her eyes.

The idea of a mere mortal not only entering the Sword Emperor Qualifications but also outperforming the top genius in the Nine Heavens was something she struggled to comprehend.

Suddenly, Madam Jun retrieved an elegant chair from her spatial ring and placed it behind her, where it hovered effortlessly in midair, as if supported by an invisible platform beneath her.

“You might want to get comfortable,” she said to the young lady at her side, a hint of amusement in her voice. “Looks like we’ll be staying a bit longer than planned.”

The young lady nodded and also retrieved a chair.

A day later, Senior Bai said, “Your time for resting is over. Please head to the next trial immediately.”

Tian Yuexing opened his eyes for the first time in a week, a resolute flame burning within them.

The week of rest had worked wonders. His mind had cleared, and his energy was fully restored. In that moment, he felt unstoppable, as if there was nothing he couldn’t overcome.

Tian Yuexing rose to his feet, his gaze locked onto Yuan.

“About our bet… I’m changing the terms,” he said firmly. “Once I win, I want you to remove that mask.”

Yuan stood as well, saying nothing, only offering a silent nod of acknowledgment.

Without another word, the two of them turned and approached the final door, ready to begin the last trial of the Sword Emperor Qualifications.

chapters.title: Chapter 2050: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Final Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2050: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Final Trial

Chapter 2050: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ Final Trial

Upon stepping through the final door, Yuan found himself standing just a few meters away from a towering pagoda, its vast structure rising endlessly into the heavens, disappearing beyond the clouds as if it pierced the very fabric of the sky.

However, Yuan wasn’t alone. Standing beside him was Tian Yuexing, who had entered just moments earlier.

Tian Yuexing cast a brief glance in Yuan’s direction, then turned away without a word and began making his way toward the pagoda.

Yuan also made his way toward the pagoda shortly after.

Moments later, the two came to a halt before the entrance of the pagoda, where a sign stood prominently beside the doorway.

The sign read:

Welcome to the Endless Pagoda—the final trial of the Sword Emperor Qualifications.

The rules are simple. Reach the 40th floor to pass the trial. The difficulty will increase with each floor. You will earn two points for every floor successfully cleared. Once you start the trial, you will not be able to rest.

After reading the sign, Tian Yuexing silently entered the pagoda, disappearing inside.

“Two points per floor, huh?” Yuan muttered as he also entered the pagoda.

Once inside, Yuan found himself standing on a vast, flat surface that stretched endlessly toward the horizon, reminiscent of the training ground at Kelan’s sect.

Shortly after, a black circle manifested on the ground a few dozen meters away from Yuan. From its swirling depths emerged a shadowy figure wielding a sword, its cultivation at the peak of the Immortal Ascension Realm.

Yuan remained motionless, his gaze fixed on the shadowy figure. He chose not to attack, opting instead to let his opponent make the first move to gauge its combat capabilities before taking action.

At the same time, Tian Yuexing immediately began his fight with the shadowy figure that mirrored his cultivation at the 3rd level of God Ascension.

A few exchanges later, Tian Yuexing defeated his opponent and was teleported to the next floor.

Meanwhile, Yuan spent a few minutes playing with his opponent.

Once he was satisfied and understood the opponent’s combat prowess, which was no different from someone without any talents, Yuan defeated the shadowy figure in one strike and teleported to the next floor.

“How far do you think Yuan will be able to climb?” Wang Bingbing questioned out loud, and the others began pondering.

“We’ll have to see how much difficulty each floor adds before we can make any educated guess,” said Kelan.

“That makes sense…”

Thus, they continued watching.

Yuan, who wanted to know the same thing, began testing the second floor’s opponent. Very quickly, he realized that his opponent on the second floor wasn’t much stronger than the first.

And for the next several floors, there was little change in his opponents’ strength. However, upon reaching the 10th floor, everything changed. His opponent’s power surged dramatically, leaping into the Bronze Immortal Realm.

Moreover, his opponent started using Enhanced Sword Aura when they’d only been using ordinary Sword Aura before.

Of course, despite the spike in difficulty, Yuan dispatched his opponent with ease, barely breaking a sweat.

Both he and Tian Yuexing continued ascending the Endless Pagoda at a similar pace, though Tian Yuexing maintained a slight lead. Unlike Yuan, he didn’t waste time gauging his opponents and simply overwhelmed them the moment they showed up.

“At this pace, he should be able to clear 40 floors without any issues,” Kelan commented after seeing the change in difficulty on the 10th floor.

“If his opponent on the 20th floor is a Silver Immortal, then on the 40th floor, he should be fighting True Immortals, which shouldn’t be any issue for him,” said Wang Ming.

“But he can defeat even Kelan, who is a God Ascension cultivator. Surely, he’ll be able to climb much higher,” said Hong Xiuquan.

“Hah!” Kelan sneered and said, “Do you really think I went all out on him? God Ascension cultivators have their powers restricted within the Nine Heavens. I also didn’t use my Celestial Qi during our spar. As talented as he may be, he won’t be able to defeat a God Ascension cultivator at their full strength.”

Meanwhile, on the tenth floor, Tian Yuexing’s opponent remained at the third level of the God Ascension Realm. However, despite the identical cultivation, the opponent’s strength had grown exponentially. This was due to the immense power gap between levels within the God Ascension Realm.

By the end of the next day, Yuan had already ascended to the 30th floor. At this stage, all of his opponents were Silver Immortals. Meanwhile, Tian Yuexing had only reached the 20th floor, his pace beginning to fall behind as the gap between them gradually widened.

“Doesn’t Yuan have an immense advantage because of his lower cultivation?” someone in the crowd suddenly remarked, drawing curious looks from those around them.

“While their cultivations may differ, that doesn’t mean the one with the lower cultivation has it easier,” another spectator replied, shaking their head. “Tell me—how many Immortal Ascension cultivators do you know who can defeat a Bronze Immortal, much less a Silver Immortal?”

“Then why does it seem like Yuan is progressing with much more ease?”

“Isn’t that simple? He’s more talented than Tian Yuexing, hence why he’s able to advance quicker and with less effort.”

While there had been uncertainty before the final trial about whether Yuan was truly more talented than Tian Yuexing, that doubt had now vanished.

It was clear as day—Yuan’s potential far surpassed that of Tian Yuexing.

By the time Tian Yuexing reached the 30th floor, Yuan had already soared to the 50th.

Tian Yuexing’s pace had noticeably slowed compared to when he first began, each battle demanding more from him.

Yuan, on the other hand, was still cutting through his opponents with the same effortless ease he’d displayed on the very first floor.

“Heavens… he’s defeating Golden Immortals like nothing—treating them as if they’re Bronze Immortals…”

The spectators were left utterly dumbfounded by Yuan’s heaven-defying prowess, and they all wondered how far he’d be able to climb.

chapters.title: Chapter 2051: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Final Trial(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2051: Sword Emperor Qualifications' Final Trial(2)

Chapter 2051: Sword Emperor Qualifications’ Final Trial(2)

A week had passed since Yuan entered the Endless Pagoda, and he had already ascended to the 90th floor.

From the 60th floor onward, his opponents were Golden Immortals, and it wasn’t until the 90th floor that he finally faced a True Immortal.

The spectators expected him to struggle against a True Immortal. Yet, to their utter shock and disbelief, Yuan defeated his opponent with ease.

“How is this possible?! Where is his power coming from?!”

For many, this was their first time witnessing a mortal defeat an immortal, let alone a True Immortal.

“Daughter, do you think you could’ve defeated a True Immortal when you were still in the Immortal Ascension Realm?” Madam Jun asked, her gaze fixed on the display.

The young woman beside her remained silent for a moment before replying, “I never had the chance to face an Immortal back then, so it’s hard to say. But even if I could’ve won, it wouldn’t have been an easy fight. That’s for sure.”

“And yet, this ‘nobody’ can defeat True Immortals with ease? I’m truly curious about his background now. I wonder which hidden family he belongs to…”

Madam Jun narrowed her eyes slightly. In her experience, when prodigies appeared out of nowhere like ghosts, they almost always hail from one of the many hidden families in the Ninth Heaven.

A few days later, Yuan arrived at the 100th floor. However, his opponent was still at True Immortal, albeit much stronger than the one on the 90th floor.

“Heavens… he’s already on the 100th floor. That’s already twice as much as my earlier prediction…” Kelan muttered with a dumbfounded expression on his face.

“The Heavenly Son is only on the 40th floor. That’s quite the difference,” remarked Wang Ming.

“So, what were you saying about Yuan only being top 10 amongst the younger generation?” Chu Liuxiang said, glaring at Kelan.

Kelan awkwardly cleared his throat before responding, “Fine, I underestimated him a little, but can you really blame me? I’ve known the others for far longer than I’ve known Yuan, and while he’s definitely impressive, he doesn’t have any achievements.”

At the Holy Sword Clan’s area, the Sixth Elder spoke, “We have to recruit him into our faction no matter the cost. He’s too talented for us to ignore.”

The silver-haired Sword Emperor sighed, “Do you have any idea how many others are thinking the same thing? It’s not going to be an easy fight. In fact, it might become a bloodbath.”

“We have the upper hand because we already have a connection with him,” said Sword Empress Huang.

As the days passed, more and more people gathered at the Gates of Heaven to witness Yuan’s relentless ascent through the Endless Pagoda—many of them hoping to recruit him once it was over.

By the end of the second week, Yuan had already reached the 199th floor, while Tian Yuexing lagged far behind on the 50th—only a quarter of Yuan’s progress.

“Is the Endless Pagoda truly endless? Surely, there must be an end, like the mountain from the last trial,” someone wondered out loud.

“The Endless Pagoda is clearly inspired by the Heaven’s Thousand Trials. If it’s like the real thing, there should be 1,000 floors in total.”

“A thousand floors…? I doubt it. There would be no point since nobody would be able to finish it.”

“Right? The Endless Pagoda is clearly more difficult than Heaven’s Thousand Trials, which has existed for millions of years and has been challenged by countless cultivators. Yet the record is only 600 floors.”

“Hm? Look! He’s reached the 200th floor—and something’s different!” one of the spectators suddenly exclaimed.

At once, all eyes turned to Yuan’s screen.

Sure enough, the 200th floor stood apart from the rest.

Yuan’s opponent was no longer a vague, shadowy figure but a masked man. At first glance, he looked oddly familiar, and then the realization struck the crowd like thunder.

“It’s the Faceless Sword Emperor!”

When Yuan saw the Faceless Sword Emperor, he smiled faintly and muttered, “So this place isn’t endless after all.”

Not only was his final opponent the legendary Faceless Sword Emperor himself, but his cultivation had also reached the first level of God Ascension.

“Asking a mortal to fight a god… this is too much.” Yuan shook his head.

Moments after his appearance, the Faceless Sword Emperor lunged at Yuan, a silver sword flashing in his grasp.

Yuan didn’t dare to underestimate his opponent and fought with caution.

When their swords clashed, Yuan was pushed back for the first time since he began the trial. Though both wielded perfected Supreme Sword Aura, the Faceless Sword Emperor’s higher cultivation gave him the upper hand, just enough to overpower Yuan slightly.

Despite losing the first exchange, Yuan remained calm and struck back without hesitation.

Yet with each clash, he was steadily pushed back, narrowly losing to the Faceless Sword Emperor time and again.

Seeing this, the audience began to believe that Yuan stood no chance against the Faceless Sword Emperor—that it was only a matter of time before he was defeated.

Of course, Yuan didn’t think he’d lose. If anything, he was pretty confident that he’d be able to win.

“So you can’t use Celestial Qi, huh? In that case, you stand no chance.”

Realizing that the Faceless Sword Emperor couldn’t use Celestial Qi, Yuan felt no fear. He continued the battle calmly, gradually increasing his strength over time.

Before long, Yuan was no longer the one being pushed back, and he was the one driving the Faceless Sword Emperor backward.

“What?! How is he still getting stronger?!”

Shock rippled through the crowd as Yuan reversed the momentum in an instant, leaving everyone speechless.

Of course, it didn’t take long for them to realize that Yuan had never been fighting at full strength. No… even now, they couldn’t be certain he was using everything he had.

Two days and tens of thousands of exchanges later, Yuan defeated the Faceless Sword Emperor, clearing the 200th floor.

chapters.title: Chapter 2052: Acquiring The Title Of Sword Emperor
chapters.body:
Chapter 2052: Acquiring The Title Of Sword Emperor

Chapter 2052: Acquiring the Title of Sword Emperor

“That freak has actually done it… He’s passed the Sword Emperor Qualifications with a perfect score, and as a mortal, no less!”

The audience wasn’t just left in awe of Yuan’s feat. They felt a tinge of fear as well.

After all, there was nothing more terrifying than the unknown, and Yuan was the embodiment of that right now. From his identity to his true potential, nobody there knew anything about him.

Once it became clear that Yuan had conquered the final trial, the atmosphere at the Gates of Heaven shifted dramatically. Every recruiter present was poised to approach him the instant he emerged.

However, Senior Bai suddenly spoke, “Please refrain from approaching the participants until after the ceremony.”

At his words, those who had already begun inching toward the platform froze in place.

Defying Senior Bai’s warning wouldn’t just be disrespectful—it would make them look bad in front of the countless spectators, and worse, in the eyes of the very person they hoped to recruit.

Thus, these recruiters had no choice but to hold back their desire for now.

Moments after clearing the final trial, Yuan stepped through the portal and returned to the platform.

Under normal circumstances, such a feat would be met with thunderous applause, but this time, not a single voice was raised. The crowd was silent.

“Congratulations on completing the Sword Emperor Qualifications. You are now officially recognized as a Sword Emperor,” Senior Bai announced as he approached Yuan.

He then retrieved a beautifully crafted, sword-shaped badge, forged from a crystal that shimmered like a diamond, yet gleamed with a deep emerald hue.

“This is proof that you’re a Sword Emperor.”

He handed the badge to Yuan, who calmly accepted it.

Senior Bai continued, “Traditionally, those who earn the title of Sword Emperor are given the right to choose their own epithet. Have you decided on one yet?”

Yuan shook his head.

“That’s fine,” Senior Bai said with a slight nod. “You can reveal it whenever you’re ready.”

Of course, regardless of what name Yuan would eventually choose, the people had already given him one of their own—the Freak Sword Emperor.

“Then, do you have any words for everyone watching? A lot of them will try to recruit you after this.”

Yuan paused in thought for a moment before speaking, “There is one thing I’d like to say.”

At those words, every eye locked onto him, like hawks fixing their gaze on prey.

“I’ve heard that many of you wish to recruit me. I’m honored, but I simply don’t have the time to meet with everyone individually. So, if you’re serious about recruiting me, please direct your intentions to Celestial Overlord Qiang of the Celestial Overlords.”

A stunned silence followed. Not just the crowd, but even Senior Bai wore a dumbfounded expression.

‘He’s going to use Celestial Overlord Qiang as a scapegoat?!’ Senior Bai cried inwardly.

A few members of the Celestial Overlords were watching, and none of them had expected Yuan to already be part of their faction. While they were pleasantly surprised, they were also curious about how Celestial Overlord Qiang managed to find him.

The recruiters exchanged perplexed glances. Now that Yuan had revealed his affiliation with the Celestial Overlords and even invoked Celestial Overlord Qiang by name, they couldn’t approach him directly without speaking to her first, even if it’s just to let her know their intentions, as doing so could be seen as disrespect.

However, one individual clearly couldn’t care less about propriety and began approaching Yuan without hesitation. The moment the others saw who it was, a wave of tension swept through them, causing them to swallow nervously.

And of all people, it was the one Yuan least wanted to deal with—Madam Jun.

The moment he saw her approaching, a subtle frown formed on his face. Of all the recruiters present, she was the one he had hoped would stay far away.

Madam Jun landed gracefully on the platform, stopping just a few meters from Yuan.

Before he could say a word, she offered a warm smile and said, “Don’t worry, I’m not here to recruit you.”

“Then you must be here to invite me for tea,” he calmly replied.

A deafening silence fell over the crowd as they were left in disbelief by Yuan’s casual response to someone of her stature.

However, instead of being offended, the smile on Madam Jun’s face widened, and she spoke, “If that’s what you want.”

“Maybe another time. I have a lot of things to do right now.”

“I don’t doubt it.” Madam Jun suddenly retrieved a silver medallion and casually tossed it at Yuan, who instinctively caught it.

“Whenever you do have time for tea, come find me with that medallion.”

After saying that, she turned to look at Senior Bai and said, “This year’s Sword Emperor Qualifications was quite entertaining.”

Without saying another word, Madam Jun turned around and returned to her carriage. Moments later, she entered it with her daughter and left the Gates of Heaven.

‘That’s it…?’ Yuan wore a stunned look behind his mask, as he was expecting the worst.

Once the Celestial Family had departed, Senior Bai sent a voice transmission to Yuan, “I understand you may not have the best relationship with the Celestial Emperor, but Madam Jun is a righteous individual. Try not to antagonize her so hastily.”

“In truth, the Celestial Family has had little to do with the Celestial Emperor ever since he attained that title. They’re forbidden from direct involvement to prevent one family from becoming too powerful.”

Yuan was quite surprised to hear this, “Is this how it works now?”

Senior Bai nodded and elaborated, “While it’s inevitable that the Celestial Family holds some influence due to their connection to the Celestial Emperor, they’re strictly prohibited from using his name for personal gain—be it in politics, business, or otherwise. As for the Celestial Emperor, he is also not allowed to use his family while bearing that title.”

“That’s good to know. Thank you,” Yuan said.

chapters.title: Chapter 2053: Sword Emperor Yuan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2053: Sword Emperor Yuan

Chapter 2053: Sword Emperor Yuan

Sometime later, Senior Bai said to Yuan out loud, “Normally, we’d have a ceremony for the new Sword Emperors, but as you can see, the other participants are still nowhere near done, so we’ll have to postpone it until every participant is finished.”

“Do I have to participate in this ceremony?” Yuan asked with raised eyebrows, as it sounded like a hassle.

“While we can’t force you, it should be noted that every Sword Emperor before you has participated in the ceremony,” said Senior Bai.

“I’ll think about it,” Yuan said.

Senior Bai then retrieved the Mystic Sword Tablet and handed it to him.

“This is your reward for ranking first place. Once again, congratulations, Sword Emperor Yuan.”

Since Yuan had completed the final trial with a perfect score, Tian Yuexing was guaranteed to take second place, regardless of whether he managed to clear all 200 floors or not.

“Thank you.” Yuan tossed the Mystic Sword Tablet into his spatial ring without even looking at it.

But before he could do anything else, another figure stepped forward.

“I’m allowed to approach you as long as I’m not trying to recruit you, right?” the man asked with a faint smile.

Yuan turned to face him and said calmly, “If you’re here to ask about the Mystic Sword Tablet, I don’t have time for that right now. That said, I don’t intend to keep it for myself forever. Once I’ve finished studying it, I’ll make sure everyone has a chance to examine it as well.”

Hearing this, the man’s smile widened, and a wave of relief spread through the crowd.

Many of the swordmasters had been silently wondering the same thing ever since the tablet appeared.

“Then I won’t bother you any longer.”

Satisfied with Yuan’s response, the man turned around and flew away.

“Where are you going to go now?” Senior Bai asked Yuan shortly after.

He responded through voice transmission, “I’ll stay in the Myriad of Techniques for a while first. Can you let the others know for me?”

“Sure.”

“Thank you.”

Yuan activated Shadow Veil the next moment, and before anyone could react, he vanished like a ghost beneath their very eyes.

“Where did he go?!” someone in the audience exclaimed, eyes darting around in confusion.

Several individuals had planned to follow Yuan the moment he left, but he didn’t even give them the chance.

After leaving the platform, Yuan entered the Myriad of Techniques and waited for the others to arrive.

Meanwhile, Senior Bai relayed Yuan’s location to Kelan.

“Do you guys want to continue watching?” Kelan asked the others.

“What’s the point?”

They all shook their heads.

“Then let’s go see him,” Kelan said as he stood up.

Thus, they quietly left to meet Yuan.

Some time later, they arrived at the Myriad of Techniques.

“Congratulations on your new title, Sword Emperor Yuan.”

They congratulated him the moment they met.

“What did you think of the Sword Emperor Qualifications? Was it as easy as you made it look?” Wang Ming asked.

Yuan chuckled and said, “While it wasn’t easy, it definitely wasn’t as difficult as I was expecting. I had more trouble training at the Holy Sword Temple, if I have to be honest.”

“As expected from the Freak Sword Emperor,” Shi Lang spoke in a teasing voice.

“Freak Sword Emperor?” Yuan raised his eyebrows at this unfamiliar nickname.

“That’s what the others are calling you,” explained Li Jinxi.

“Because you’re a freak,” Wang Bingbing elaborated.

After chatting for a bit, Yuan said, “Can one of you let Yu Rou know that I have returned so that we can spend some time together before I leave again?”

“I can do that, but where will you be going next?” Meixiu asked.

With a serious look on his face, he spoke, “The Primordial Realm.”

“The Primordial Realm? Isn’t that where Xiao Hua is from?”

He nodded and continued, “She hasn’t woken up once in several years. I’m worried about her.”

“W-Wait a second. The Primordial Realm? What are you talking about? That’s not a place you can enter whenever you want,” Kelan said after hearing Yuan’s plans.

“I am aware,” said Yuan, and he proceeded to explain Xiao Hua’s situation to him.

“I understand your situation, but not only does the Primordial Realm house some of the most dangerous criminals within the Nine Heavens, you’ll definitely become a wanted criminal if you try to enter without the Celestial Emperor’s permission,” Kelan said afterward.

“Permission from the Celestial Emperor? That’s definitely not possible. Regardless, I am already targeted by him, so it won’t make any difference.”

“It makes a huge difference! If you do something that reckless, not even Celestial Overlord Xu can protect you!” Kelan exclaimed with a deep frown on his face.

“In any case, don’t even think about approaching the Primordial Realm because I’ll stop you with everything I’ve got!” Kelan declared firmly.

Yuan didn’t blame him. He understood that Kelan was only trying to protect him. Unfortunately, he was fated to enter the Primordial Realm.

“Fine, I’ll give up on the Primordial Realm—for now,” Yuan said, raising his hands in a gesture of surrender.

“Good. As for your Exile friend, I’ll find an expert to treat her,” Kelan replied, his tone softening.

Yuan nodded calmly.

But in truth, he had no intention of giving up.

No matter what he said on the surface, his resolve remained unchanged.

A few days later, Yu Rou visited the Myriad of Techniques, and the two of them started catching up with each other.

Meanwhile, the Demon Sealing Faction members returned to the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect with Kelan to continue their training.

Time flew by, and in the blink of an eye, two months had passed since Yuan earned the title of Sword Emperor.

The Sword Emperor Qualifications were still underway, with every participant—except for the Heavenly Son—still struggling to clear the second trial.

As for Tian Yuexing, he had managed to push his way up to the 84th floor of the Endless Pagoda. Though it was taking him longer and longer to clear each floor, it was clear to the spectators that he wasn’t close to his limit, and they wondered how long it would take him to finish.

chapters.title: Chapter 2054: God Of War, Tian Xian(End Of Leaving Earth Arc)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2054: God Of War, Tian Xian(End Of Leaving Earth Arc)

Chapter 2054: God of War, Tian Xian(End of Leaving Earth Arc)

“Brother, is it strange that even though it’s been years since we last saw each other, it didn’t feel nearly as long as the last time?” Yu Rou asked aloud.

Yuan smiled gently and responded, “That’s because as your cultivation deepens, your sense of time changes. The more accustomed you become to it, the less you notice time passing.”

“I see… I don’t know if that’s a good or bad thing.”

“It can be both.”

“You’ll be leaving again soon, right?” Yu Rou asked, the expression on her face turning sour.

He nodded, “Yes, I’m going to the Primordial Realm to see if Xiao Hua’s family can cure her.”

“Xiao Hua… I miss her,” Yu Rou sighed.

“Me too.”

“Since this is for Xiao Hua, I won’t say much. Just be safe, okay?”

“I won’t go unprepared. I promise.”

After spending two months together, Yu Rou left the Myriad of Techniques to resume her journey while Yuan prepared for the Primordial Realm.

However, he was still missing two of the four Ancient Seals required to open the Primordial Realm. While he could force his way inside the Primordial Realm, that would cause too much of a commotion, and he’d attract the Celestial Emperor’s attention, something he wished to avoid at all costs.

Fortunately, the Evil God’s Shadow Army had been investigating the last two Ancient Seal holders.

After leaving the Myriad of Techniques, Yuan went to find Xi Meili, who was given a method to contact the Evil God’s Shadow Army by Dong Ye.

“Dong Ye told you how to contact the Shadow Army, right?” Yuan asked for confirmation after interrupting her cultivation.

“Yes. Are you going to contact them now?” Xi Meili asked.

He nodded.

Xi Meili then retrieved a jade slip and a purple token from her spatial ring and handed it to Yuan, saying, “Dong Ye said the instructions are inside.”

“Like usual, I’ll leave Liya in your hands,” Yuan then said.

Xi Meili responded with a smile, “Don’t worry about us. We’re practically like sisters now.”

After bringing Liya to the Nine Heavens, he asked her what she wanted to do. Unsurprisingly, she said that she wanted to become stronger, as she was bottlenecked on Earth by its lack of spiritual energy. Thus, she started training with Xi Meili, who was a fellow dragon.

Of course, Yuan hadn’t forgotten about the Lord’s request, and he still planned on bringing her to the Red Heaven Dragon Clan.

Some time later, Yuan went to say goodbye to Meixiu and the others. He also left them some spirit stones for their future journey outside the sect.

“Until next time, everyone.”

Before leaving, Yuan created a new identity and disguised himself.

Then, he left Kelan’s sect through the teleportation device.

After a few days and several teleportations, Yuan arrived at a renowned commercial hub known as Red Dusk City. However, most of the businesses in this city revolved around information brokerage. It was a place people went to seek answers.

Upon entering the city, the first thing Yuan noticed was the abundance of tent-like structures lining the streets, each exuding a shady atmosphere.

Despite their shady appearance, the tents saw a steady stream of people entering and leaving. Most of them wore masks, clearly intent on concealing their identities.

Yuan wasn’t wearing a mask at the moment, but that was only because he was already in disguise. Following the instructions inscribed in the jade slip Xi Meili had given him, he soon arrived before a large building known as Dusk Intelligence.

Dusk Intelligence was a well-established information market that had existed for tens of thousands of years. Not only do they have a stellar reputation with many branches throughout the Nine Heavens, but they were also ranked among the top businesses in Red Dusk City.

Of course, in truth, it was operated by the Evil God’s Shadow Army, serving as a front to gather intelligence from all corners of the world.

When Yuan entered the building, he was greeted by a young man.

“Hello, are you here to buy or sell information?”

“I’m here to buy information.”

“I understand. Please head to the waiting room over there and wait for your number to be called.” The young man handed Yuan a small wooden token before pointing toward a large waiting area where hundreds of people could be seen sitting around.

Yuan then said, “Actually, I already have a reservation.”

“In that case, may I have your name?”

Yuan responded through Voice Transmission, “God of War, Tian Xian.”

The young man’s face immediately froze. Then, after taking a moment to comprehend what he’d just heard, his eyes widened with shock and disbelief.

“P-P-Please follow me, Esteemed Guest!” The young man stuttered, his entire body trembling uncontrollably.

Minutes later, after Yuan followed the young man into a private room and the door closed softly behind them, the young man hesitantly asked, “E-Esteemed Guest… may I be so bold as to request proof of your reservation?”

Yuan silently retrieved the purple token and showed it to the young man.

The moment his eyes fell upon the purple token, the young man dropped to his knees, his voice filled with reverence and awe as he said, “This lowly servant greets the God of War.”

“Were you expecting my presence?” Yuan asked.

“Yes.”

“Then do you know why I am here?”

“I do not, but our leader should. May I notify him of your presence?”

“Go ahead.”

“Please allow me to prepare you some tea before I—”

“No need. Just get your leader.”

“As you command.”

The young man didn’t dare to waste another second and immediately left to find the leader of the Dusk Intelligence.

Not even a minute later, Yuan heard a gentle knock on the door.

“Come inside.”

The door opened at Yuan’s command, and a middle-aged man hurriedly entered the room.

“This lowly servant is honored to stand in the presence of the God of War,” the man said as he kowtowed deeply before Yuan.

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 2055: Location Of The Remaining Two Ancient Seals
chapters.body:
Chapter 2055: Location Of The Remaining Two Ancient Seals

Chapter 2055: Location of the Remaining Two Ancient Seals

“Stand up. There’s no need to be so formal. Just tell me what I need to know, and I’ll quickly be on my way,” Yuan said to the man kowtowing on the floor.

The man nodded and stood up before asking, “What do you wish to know, God of War? All of our intel is at your disposal.”

“I’d like to know where the Ancient Seals to unlock the Primordial Realm are right now,” Yuan said.

“The Ancient Seals, huh… We’ll have to look at our records, but I’m sure we already have this information since the Commander asked us to look into it several years ago.”

“How long will it take?”

“It shouldn’t take long. A few hours at most.”

“Alright. I’ll wait here then.”

“Would you like some tea while you wait?”

Yuan shook his head.

“As you wish. Please excuse me while I retrieve the information now.”

The man bowed to Yuan again before leaving the room to look for the information he requested.

While he waited, Yuan retrieved the Mystic Sword Tablet to study it. Now that he’d perfected Supreme Sword Aura, he only had two things he needed to do—fuse it with his body and attain the Supreme Sword Body and achieve a realm even higher than Supreme Sword Aura.

A few hours later, the leader of the Dusk Intelligence returned to the room with two scrolls in his grasp.

“This is the information you requested, God of War.” The leader said as he offered it to Yuan as if he were giving an offering to a god.

“Thank you.”

Yuan accepted the scrolls and immediately opened them to read.

Moments later, he rubbed his eyes and sighed.

“A-Are the information not satisfactory…?” The leader swallowed nervously after seeing Yuan’s reaction.

“Hm? No, that’s not it. I was just thinking that this will become a bit pain in the ass,” said Yuan as he waved the scrolls in the air.

“Is that so…”

“Anyway, I’d like to request more information, specifically about the two families listed here,” he continued.

“As you command. I will be right back.” The leader bowed before leaving again.

Once he was alone again, Yuan looked at the scrolls and mumbled, “The Rising Phoenix Clan and the Heavenly White Tiger Clan, huh?”

He was already familiar with one of these names, the Heavenly White Tiger Clan. Not only was their Ancestor one of the Nine Divine Supremes, but he had some run-ins with their current young generation.

“Young Master, I know the Rising Phoenix Clan,” Feng Yuxiang’s voice suddenly resounded, snapping him out of his thoughts.

“Oh?”

Feng Yuxiang emerged from his Dantian to continue, “In fact, I was staying with the Rising Phoenix Clan before I was cursed and was chased to the Lower Heaven.”

“So you have quite the relationship with them, huh?”

Feng Yuxiang nodded and said, “Most phoenixes today are born from other phoenixes, but I am one of the rare few naturally born directly from the flames themselves. I spent most of my earlier days wandering the Nine Heavens alone, but when I met the Rising Phoenix Clan, they offered me a place to stay, and wanting to experience what it was like living with other phoenixes, I accepted it.”

She continued, “Honestly, it was a pain. Everything seemed normal at first, but they eventually started trying to rope me into their clan by pushing their sons on me. That’s when I left and returned to wandering the Nine Heavens.”

“Oh, right. They’re also among the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans, ranked fifth.”

Yuan continued asking questions about the Rising Phoenix Clan until the leader returned with a tray holding several spatial rings.

“Here is all the information we have on the Rising Phoenix Clan and the Heavenly White Tiger Clan.”

Yuan stared at the spatial rings and asked in a somewhat nervous voice, “How many scrolls are inside?”

“One thousand three hundred and fifty-two scrolls in total.” The leader responded without any pause.

Yuan was speechless. He only anticipated a few scrolls—a few dozen at most.

“T-Thank you… I will read them later.” Yuan said, even though he had no intention of reading all of them, as there was simply too much.

“Will you be leaving now?” The leader then asked.

“I’ll leave in a bit.”

The leader then retrieved a black medallion and offered it to him, “Please accept this. As long as you have this token, we, the Shadow Army, recognize your identity no matter where you are.”

After accepting the medallion, Yuan spent a few more hours in the room to read a few scrolls of each clan and to ponder which of the two places he should visit first.

However, when he realized that both the Rising Phoenix Clan and the Heavenly White Tiger Clan existed in the Eighth Heaven, Yuan decided to visit the Stairway to Heaven first. Now that he had gotten rid of the Celestial Emperor’s curse and his presence was known, he no longer had any reason not to use it.

After leaving Dusk Intelligence, Yuan made his way to the teleportation device and teleported to the city nearest to the Stairway to Heaven.

A few days later, he arrived at his destination.

When he arrived, he was surprised to see many people at the Stairway to Heaven, counting hundreds at a glance.

Yuan wondered if there was a special occasion, but in reality, this is just a typical day at the Stairway to Heaven in the Seventh Heaven.

Unlike the lower heavens, there were simply many more incentives for cultivators in the Seventh Heaven to want to ascend. Moreover, it was much easier to ascend to the Eighth and Ninth Heaven, which the Celestial Emperor himself purposefully set.

After acquiring a token with a number, Yuan patiently waited for his turn.

A few days later, Yuan approached the Stairway to Heaven and entered it.

“Welcome back, Master.” Tian’er greeted him inside.

“It’s been a while.”

“Are you here to take the trial?”

He nodded and said, “Yes, and I’ll take the trial for the Seventh Heaven while I am at it.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2056: Returning To The Stairway To Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 2056: Returning To The Stairway To Heaven

Chapter 2056: Returning to the Stairway to Heaven

“The trial for the Seventh Heaven and the Eighth Heaven, correct? We can do that.” Tian’er nodded.

“Would you like to start now?”

“Let’s.”

Shortly later, Yuan began his trial, which was supposed to be meant for the Seventh Heaven.

When the trial began, Yuan found himself in an unusual location. He was not in the wilderness or a city. Instead, he was surrounded by the void with countless stars flickering in the distance.

He was outside the Nine Heavens in the Starry Sky.

“I have to survive for a whole year?” Yuan raised his eyebrows after seeing the trial.

The very next moment, before he could even start to ponder, Yuan sensed hundreds of powerful presences appearing behind him.

When he turned around, he could see an army of people chasing after him. However, most of their cultivation was between Bronze Immortal and Silver Immortal, with a few Golden Immortals.

Seeing this, the thought of escaping didn’t even cross Yuan’s mind. Wielding Number One Under Heaven in his grasp, he charged at the incoming army of cultivators with a calm smile on his face, looking like he was going on a stroll in the park.

However, Yuan didn’t just fight normally. He fought while training at the same time, fusing his Supreme Sword Aura with his body and slowly inching toward attaining the Supreme Sword Body.

After slaying all of the cultivators, Yuan was given two hours of rest before another wave of enemies appeared, their numbers increased compared to the first wave.

This reminded Yuan of the Sword Emperor Qualifications, which tested his endurance and ability to overcome a large number of enemies at once.

Meanwhile, as Yuan remained inside the Stairway to Heaven, Meixiu and the others prepared to leave Kelan’s sect to gain experience outside.

Although Kelan wanted to follow them, he had other responsibilities as the Sect Leader, and he’d already pushed back some just training them. Of course, he wasn’t going to let them travel alone, and for their safety, had several experts watch them from a distance.

These experts were not allowed to intervene with their training unless their lives were in danger.

Also, since their group was a little big, they decided to split up into smaller groups.

For the first group, Meixiu, Chu Liuxiang, and Li Jinxi were together.

For the second group, it was Wang Ming, Wang Bingbing, and Xi Murong.

The third group had Shi Lang, Wu Zao, and Hong Xiuquan.

The fourth and final group consisted of Xi Meili, Liya, and Wang Xiuying.

After splitting into four smaller groups, they wandered the Seventh Heaven separately, experiencing their own journeys.

Four months later, at the Gates of Heaven, the Holy Sword Maiden finally finished her second trial and returned to the platform.

When she looked at the ranking, her eyes widened with shock.

“900 points?! What did he do to acquire so many points?!” The Holy Sword Maiden muttered in a dumbfounded voice, completely unaware that Yuan had long passed the Sword Emperor Qualifications and earned his title as Sword Emperor.

After realizing that she had no chance of catching up, the Holy Sword Maiden didn’t immediately rush to the third trial. Instead, she sat down and used what little time she had to rest.

Seven days later, just as the Holy Sword Maiden entered the third trial, the Holy Sword King finished his trial and returned to the platform.

He was just as bewildered as the Holy Sword Maiden when he saw the ranking, but he also felt much more irritated.

‘Is the difference between us this vast…? Just who the hell did I challenge?’ The Holy Sword King felt embarrassed just recalling how he’d challenged Yuan.

Instead of resting like the Holy Sword Maiden, the Holy Sword King immediately went into the third trial.

As for the Heavenly Son, he’d managed to pass the 110th floor of the Endless Pagoda. While he was still climbing, the time it took to pass each floor increased with every floor.

After another four months, Yuan completed his trial after defeating over 100,000 Immortals.

A glowing orb of light appeared before him.

After consuming the orb, Yuan recalled more memories of his past lives.

“Congratulations on passing the trial for the Seventh Heaven,” said Tian’er.

“Hm? That was it?”

Tian’er nodded and said, “Master, you’ve already reached the point where the trials won’t be much of a challenge. It’d only be a waste of time to give you more.”

“Is that so? Then let’s start the trial for the Eighth Heaven,” said Yuan.

Moments later, the scenery shifted as Yuan began his trial for the Eighth Heaven.

“This place…”

The landscape, the structure—it all felt strikingly familiar. Yuan’s gaze settled on the towering mountain ahead, its spiraling path coiling upward like a serpent. It was none other than Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“Ten years to defeat my opponent?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at this seemingly generous amount of time.

However, in the very next moment, Yuan sensed an unfathomable pressure descend upon Dragon Spiral Mountain, causing the air around him to tremble.

When he looked up, he saw a half-naked, strikingly handsome man hovering above Dragon Spiral Mountain, his long golden hair flowing behind him like a river of light.

“The Great One…” Yuan mumbled, recognizing this man almost instantly.

“Immortal Monarch!” the Great One suddenly declared, his voice thundering like a lightning strike. “I have come to defeat you and to claim Dragon Goddess Yeyou! A human like you is not worthy of her!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2057: Fighting The Great One
chapters.body:
Chapter 2057: Fighting The Great One

Chapter 2057: Fighting the Great One

Shortly after his appearance, the Great One unleashed his cultivation, which was at the first level of God Ascension realm.

“I’m at a point where the trials won’t be much of a challenge, huh?” Yuan repeated what Tian’er had said in a low voice and a stiff smile.

Although the Great One was only at the first level of God Ascension, he might be the toughest opponent Yuan has encountered up until now.

The Great One was not just a Divine Beast—he was a Dragon of Origin, one of the most ancient dragons to ever exist. His existence traced back to the Primordial Era, and in terms of experience, he rivaled Yuan, perhaps even surpassed him.

Yuan didn’t bother responding to the Great One and immediately prepared for combat.

Seeing this, the Great One charged at Yuan, his body enveloped in a golden aura.

Yuan didn’t dare to underestimate the Great One and fought seriously from the get-go.

Seconds later, the two clashed, unleashing a powerful shockwave that swept across the area, sending violent ripples surging across the waters surrounding Dragon Spiral Mountain.

For the first several days, the two fought using only their physical bodies. Despite possessing the Golden Immortal Physique, Yuan found himself merely on par with the Great One.

While the Golden Immortal Physique was considered the best physique in the Nine Heavens, that was only in human standards.

That said, matching the Great One in physical combat—one of the mightiest beings in the Nine Heavens—was an achievement worthy of pride.

“You’re an eyesore!” the Great One suddenly roared. “If not for you, Yeyou would already be mine!”

His aura surged violently, growing stronger with each passing moment, shaking the very air around him.

Hearing this, Yuan responded with a calm smile, “Is that really the case? From what I’ve heard, you’re nothing more than an obsessive stalker in her eyes. Even if I never existed, you never had a chance.”

“Shut up! What do you know about our relationship?! You’re just an outsider! A mere human!”

“If I am just a mere human, then why are you so upset? So scared of my presence? Aren’t we like ants in your eyes?”

“Scared? You think I’m scared of you?!” the Great One bellowed with laughter, as though he’d just heard the most absurd joke in the world.

“Allow me to show the insurmountable difference between us! Golden Scale Armor!”

A semi-transparent armor shimmering with a golden glow materialized around the Great One, exuding a majestic and overwhelming pressure.

In an instant, the Great One’s power surged to new heights. When they clashed again moments later, he effortlessly overwhelmed Yuan.

Like an unstoppable force, the Great One drove Yuan back hundreds of meters with every clash.

In response, Yuan activated the God of War’s Astral Arts, cloaking himself in its celestial avatar as though donning a suit of divine armor. Though it helped him close the gap between their strength, it was still not enough.

“Do you understand it now? The difference between me and your inferior existence?!” The Great One laughed out loud as he continued his relentless assault.

Yuan smiled at the Great One’s provocation and said, “You put on this act of strength, but deep down, it’s just a mask to hide how fragile you really are. You hoard treasures not out of desire, but to fill the void left by the one treasure you’ll never obtain.”

The moment the Great One heard those words, his movements faltered. Then, his aura erupted with renewed fury, surging to even greater heights as his expression twisted with rage.

“How dare you mock me?!” he roared.

From that moment on, the Great One ceased speaking, focusing entirely on the battle, and Yuan did the same. With each passing exchange, their strength and intensity steadily escalated, shaking the heavens with their clash.

Time passed in the blink of an eye, and it’s been a whole year since Yuan started fighting the Great One in the Stairway to Heaven.

The Sword Emperor Qualifications were still ongoing, and all of the participants—at least the ones that still remained—had passed the second trial and had begun their third trial. The Holy Sword Maiden and the Holy Sword King also finished their third trial and had begun their fourth trial.

As for the Heavenly Son, he was still climbing the Endless Pagoda, now on the 145th floor.

After another year, the Heavenly Son reached the 170th floor.

“Do you think the Heavenly Son will be able to clear the Endless Pagoda?” The audience asked each other.

“Most likely. While he wasn’t able to clear it as fast as the Freak Sword Emperor, he has the capabilities to clear it.”

The audience has already begun calling Yuan the Freak Sword Emperor.

The Holy Sword Maiden and the Holy Sword King also started climbing the Endless Pagoda a few months ago, as the fourth trial was the quickest to clear.

It wasn’t until they entered the final trial that these two realized Yuan had actually already completed the Sword Emperor Qualifications and had become a Sword Emperor, which left him utterly dumbfounded.

“He became a Sword Emperor even before I completed the second trial…?” The Holy Sword Maiden muttered in a daze. “I know he’s talented, but this is just too much…”

The Holy Sword King could only stare at the Endless Pagoda with a blank expression on his face, his thoughts unknown. However, the audience could tell that he was devastated, looking like someone experiencing an existential crisis.

Seeing this, the audience wondered if the Holy Sword King would give up there. Many believed that even if he continued, he was not in the right state of mind and might fail because of it.

Eventually, the Holy Sword King entered the Endless Pagoda and began his climb.

Then, after another two years, the Heavenly Son finally arrived at the 200th floor and began his final challenge with the Faceless Sword Emperor.

As for Yuan, his battle with the Great One raged on with seemingly no end in sight.

Join Privilege for 50 Adv Chapters! Only Available in the App!

chapters.title: Chapter 2058: Entering The Immortal Realm
chapters.body:
Chapter 2058: Entering The Immortal Realm

Chapter 2058: Entering the Immortal Realm

When the Heavenly Son realized he was fighting the Faceless Sword Emperor, an excited smile appeared on his face, as this meant that he had finally reached the end of the trial.

However, he didn’t dare to underestimate his opponent, who was several times stronger than his previous opponent on the 199th floor.

That said, he was very confident that he would be able to defeat the Faceless Sword Emperor.

Moments later, they clashed, their battle sending violent tremors rippling through the surrounding space without pause. Had they been fighting within the Nine Heavens, their clash would surely have brought unimaginable devastation.

Meanwhile, Yuan and the Great One’s battle raged on with undiminished ferocity. Their clash had endured for so long that Dragon Spiral Mountain had been worn down to an unrecognizable shape. It no longer resembled a towering mountain, but a mere hill.

<Your understanding of Celestial Qi has slightly improved>

Over these past several years, Yuan had been constantly exposed to Celestial Qi. Though it wasn’t potent enough to kill him, it inflicted just enough pain to deepen his understanding of its nature.

However, he was still nowhere near learning it. That was just how difficult it was to learn Celestial Qi.

“Dragon’s Gaze!”

The Great One suddenly used Dragon’s Gaze for the first time.

Yet, it didn’t even make Yuan flinch, as his Soul Strength was simply too overwhelming.

In response, the Great One used Dragon’s Gaze again, but it was somewhat different this time.

“Dragon’s Authority!”

This Dragon’s Gaze was several times stronger, and it was something only a few dragons in the world could use.

The Great One’s Dragon Authority was strong enough to shake Yuan’s soul slightly, but that was all it could do.

“I guess it’s about time we put an end to this farce,” said Yuan.

For the first year, he had been slightly behind the Great One in terms of prowess, but as his soul continued to merge with his body, Yuan’s strength climbed steadily. Now, he was the one pushing the Great One back.

Ding!

<The Sacred Chalice of Purification has successfully purified ???>

<The Sacred Chalice of Purification has begun purifying the next curse>

The moment Yuan received the notification, he felt as if an invisible chain had been lifted from his body, leaving him feeling incomparably lighter.

“What?!”

The Great One exclaimed in shock when Yuan’s aura suddenly exploded.

Then, like a volcano, Yuan’s cultivation erupted.

<You have met the requirements for a breakthrough>

<You have reached Early-stage Bronze Immortal>

<You have met the requirements for a breakthrough>

<You have reached Mid-stage Bronze Immortal>

<You have met the requirements for a breakthrough>

<You have reached Late-stage Bronze Immortal>

<You have met the requirements for a breakthrough>

<You have reached Peak-stage Bronze Immortal>

In the blink of an eye, Yuan’s cultivation soared to the peak of Bronze Immortal, on the verge of Silver Immortal.

After finally stepping into the Immortal Realm, Yuan’s Immortal Qi underwent a profound surge in both depth and potency, and it was at least several times stronger than before.

<True Dragon Awakening>

<God of War’s Astral Arts>

<God of War’s Final Execution>

Yuan unleashed a relentless onslaught against the Great One, wielding all of his most formidable techniques.

Already struggling to hold his ground, the Great One suddenly found himself utterly unable to retaliate, crushed beneath the sheer force of Yuan’s overwhelming power.

“Just stand there and accept your defeat!” Yuan shouted, using the Immortal Monarch’s authority.

The Great One’s movement froze at his command, even if it was just for a split second. However, a split second was more than enough for Yuan to land his attack.

As the God of War’s Final Execution grew more devastating with each strike, relentlessly building in power until its target was utterly annihilated, the Great One managed to withstand a total of seventy-two blows before he could no longer defend himself.

After several years of relentless battle, Yuan finally defeated the Great One, marking this as the longest time he had ever spent within a single trial of the Stairway to Heaven.

“You’ve won this time… but I will never give up on Yeyou…” The Great One’s voice echoed faintly as his form faded into the thin air.

<You have successfully defeated your enemy before the time limit>

A glowing orb of light appeared before him, and as Yuan absorbed it, fragments of his lost memories began to return.

However, that wasn’t all he received from clearing the trial.

Tian’er appeared before him with a wooden box, offering it to him.

“This is…”

When Yuan opened the box and saw the object resting inside, he raised his eyebrows, wondering why he had been given it. However, he didn’t question it and figured that it would come in handy one day.

At the same time, the Heavenly Son’s battle with the Faceless Sword Emperor reached its conclusion.

“Damn it! I was so close to defeating him!” Tian Yuexing cursed aloud, moments after being teleported out of the Endless Pagoda upon his loss to the Faceless Sword Emperor.

Had he faced the Faceless Sword Emperor from the very start, he would have surely won. But after climbing 199 floors, exhaustion took its toll, leading him to make a fatal mistake at the very end.

Fortunately, since he had surpassed the threshold needed to complete the Sword Emperor Qualifications, he still earned the title of Sword Emperor.

When Tian Yuexing exited the trial and returned to the platform, the first thing he did was look at the ranking.

“N-Nine hundred points?! That means he cleared all 200 floors!” Tian Yuexing was in disbelief.

He turned to Senior Bai and demanded, “Where is he?!”

“If you’re asking about Yuan, he finished quite some time ago, and he’s already gone,” Senior Bai replied calmly.

“Some time ago…?” Tian Yuexing frowned.

Senior Bai nodded and continued, “He finished the trial in a little over two weeks.”

Upon hearing this shocking revelation, Tian Yuexing’s expression went blank, as if he couldn’t quite comprehend what he had just heard.

chapters.title: Chapter 2059: Arriving At The Eighth Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 2059: Arriving At The Eighth Heaven

Chapter 2059: Arriving at the Eighth Heaven

“He finished the trial in just two weeks? There’s no way that’s possible…” Tian Yuexing mumbled with a bewildered expression on his face.

“Y-You’re lying! There’s no way anyone could’ve finished that trial in just two weeks! Unless he took a different trial than I!” he exclaimed a moment later, refusing to believe such an outrageous result.

Senior Bai merely shook his head and responded calmly, “What would I gain from lying to you? If you don’t want to believe me, you can watch the recordings later.”

Tian Yuexing fell silent.

“I really lost…? To a mere mortal? But I am the Heavenly Son…” he muttered, his voice distant, as if trapped in a waking nightmare.

Though this wasn’t his first defeat, he’d never experienced such a crushing defeat before from anyone, much less to a nobody like Yuan, who had seemingly crawled out from nowhere.

He was the Heavenly Son—born to stand at the pinnacle, number one in nearly everything he did. He had always been the one ahead, the one others chased endlessly. But now, for the first time, he found himself in pursuit of another, and he finally tasted the frustration and helplessness that others had felt trying to catch up to him. It was a surreal feeling.

Eventually, Tian Yuexing accepted his defeat and asked Senior Bai, “Where did he go?”

“I don’t know.” Senior Bai shook his head.

“Do you know how I can contact him? I want to pay my debt as soon as possible.”

Senior Bai wanted to lie and say he didn’t, but with Yuan having already revealed to everyone that he was part of the Celestial Overlords, it would be far too easy to catch him in a lie.

“I’ll let him know that you want to speak with him,” Senior Bai said.

“Tell him to find me at the True Heaven Sect or the Tian Family. If he cannot reach me for some reason, I’ll go to him,” said Tian Yuexing.

“I understand. Now, do you have a word for the audience?”

Hearing this, Tian Yuexing turned to face the crowd. Yet his eyes didn’t truly see them. Instead, his gaze seemed fixed on some distant place far beyond their reach.

After a moment of silence, he spoke in a loud and clear voice, “I may have lost this time, but it will not happen again! Mark my words, I will defeat you the next time we cross paths!”

It was clear to everyone there that Tian Yuexing was addressing Yuan, who may or may not be watching from afar.

“Do you have a name in mind for your new title?” Senior Bai then asked.

“I don’t care what I am called, but I will only respond to the name Heavenly Sword Emperor,” he spoke in an adamant tone.

“Congratulations, Heavenly Sword Emperor. Regarding the Sword Emperor Ceremony, it will take place at a later date once all of the participants are done.”

Tian Yuexing narrowed his eyes slightly at the mention of the ceremony. He had little interest in it himself, but realizing that Yuan might attend, he said, “I’ll be there.”

Without saying another word, he left the platform and flew directly to where his sister was.

“I’m heading home.”

He knew that his sister would mock him for losing, so before she could even open her mouth, Tian Yuexing turned around and sped away.

“What a loser,” Tian Jinhui muttered before leaving as well.

Meanwhile, after passing his trial, Yuan arrived at the Eighth Heaven.

“Eighth Heaven, huh? Now I am only one away from reaching the Supreme Heaven…” Yuan muttered.

“So, which clan will you be visiting first?” Feng Yuxiang asked him a few moments later.

“Probably the Rising Phoenix Clan,” he said.

“Are you going to disguise yourself as a phoenix like you disguised yourself as a Dragon Emperor for the previous Ancient Seals?”

Yuan smiled and said, “That’s only because I have the resources to disguise myself as a Dragon Emperor. But as for pretending to be a phoenix… I don’t think I have what I need for that. Other than my Heaven Freezing Flame and your movement technique, I don’t really have anything that could help me pull off a phoenix disguise.”

“What about your Fire Harmony? That’s something even phoenixes have a hard time achieving. Also, I have plenty of things that you can use to pull off the perfect phoenix disguise.”

Feng Yuxiang emerged from his Dantian and began looking through her spatial ring.

A few moments later, she pulled out several items, and Yuan recognized them, as he’d peeked inside her spatial ring during his time at the Primal Expanse.

“The Ancient Phoenix Robes? But only those with a phoenix bloodline can wear it,” Yuan said.

“Hm? You know this treasure?” Feng Yuxiang looked at him with a surprised face.

Yuan scratched his head and explained in an apologetic tone, “I forgot to tell you, but I looked through your spatial ring in the Primal Expanse to find anything that could help me approach the phoenixes there. I wore the Ancient Phoenix Robe and even used it to train my fire resistance.”

He revealed everything to her, including what treasures he’d used.

Feng Yuxiang listened to every word with a calm expression. When he finished, she said softly, “There’s no need to apologize, Young Master. You did it for my sake, after all. And even if that weren’t true, I still wouldn’t mind—because everything I have is already yours, including my body and soul.”

She continued, “Also, I am aware of the Ancient Phoenix Robe’s effect. However, that can easily be fixed with this.”

Feng Yuxiang showed him the small bottle that contained a single drop of blood within.

“Isn’t this…” Yuan instantly recognized this treasure.

“Yup. This is the Primordial Phoenix Blood Essence. If you consume it, you should acquire the power and bloodline of a phoenix.”

Yuan swallowed nervously, but he didn’t dare to accept it so easily.

“Are you sure?” he asked.

Join Privilege for 50 Adv Chapters! Only Available in the App!

chapters.title: Chapter 2060: Primordial Phoenix’S Blood Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2060: Primordial Phoenix’S Blood Essence

Chapter 2060: Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence

“Of course. Like I said, what’s mine is yours,” Feng Yuxiang said with a calm nod.

She continued, “To be honest with you, Young Master, I’ve always been a little envious that you possess so much dragon power but almost no phoenix power. It makes my bloodline feel a little inferior. Hopefully, this will help balance things out.”

“It’s not like I am purposefully picking dragon powers over phoenix powers. It’s just that I’ve had more encounters with dragons than phoenixes,” Yuan said. “In any case, I’ll accept it. Thank you.”

Feng Yuxiang handed the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence to him the next moment. This then reminded Yuan that he still had the Dragon Ancestor’s Blood Essence.

“I will need a quiet and secure place to absorb this blood essence, especially since I don’t know how long it’d take…”

At first, he considered absorbing the blood essence in Senior Bai’s world within the Myriad of Techniques, since he was familiar with the place and it was accessible to only a select few. However, there was a chance that his cultivation might become violent, and he didn’t want to risk causing damage to the place.

Eventually, Yuan decided to find a secluded place somewhere in the Eighth Heaven to consume the blood essence. However, he didn’t know where to go.

This was when Feng Yuxiang spoke up again, “Young Master, if you’re looking for a place to enter seclusion, why don’t you visit my Immortal Cave? It’s located in the Eighth Heaven.”

“Oh? That sounds like a good idea. Where is it?”

“I’ll take you there.”

She retrieved her flying treasure and boarded it a moment later. Once Yuan was aboard, she guided it into motion.

“It might take some time to get there since it’s quite far away,” she said.

Yuan nodded, and in the meantime, studied the Mystic Sword Tablet.

Several months later, they arrived at a place of searing heat, where volcanoes and rivers of flowing magma stretched in every direction, as if an apocalypse had recently swept through the land.

This sort of extreme environment would’ve been great for Yuan to train his fire resistance before, but he was already at the point where the heat didn’t affect him in the slightest.

Feng Yuxiang finally brought the flying treasure to a halt and said, “Young Master, we’ve arrived at the location, but there’s a problem…”
