Yuan stowed away the Mystic Sword Tablet and stepped to the edge of the flying treasure to see for himself.

In the distance, he spotted several groups of cultivators encircling a particular area. Judging by their distinct robes, each group appeared to hail from a different faction.

“Is that where your Immortal Cave is located?” Yuan asked Feng Yuxiang.

However, Feng Yuxiang shook her head and said, “No, my Immortal Cave is a little further ahead. However, with so many people here, I don’t think it’ll be a good idea to use it anymore.”

Yuan then said, “There’s no need to come to a conclusion so quickly. Let’s check out the situation first before we decide what to do next.”

Yuan leapt from the flying treasure and flew toward these cultivators, who were all within the Immortal Realm.

There were about seven hundred cultivators gathered there, most of them ranging from Bronze Immortal to Silver Immortal. Within each group, a single Golden Immortal stood at the forefront, acting as their leader.

When these cultivators noticed Yuan, they immediately became alert.

“Stop right there.” One of the Golden Immortals demanded in a tone of authority.

Yuan slowly came to a halt, but before he could even open his mouth, the Golden Immortal continued, “Turn around and leave immediately. We’re busy.”

Because Yuan’s cultivation was only at the Bronze Immortal stage, the Golden Immortal didn’t even bother to show him any courtesy and brazenly ordered him to leave.

“That would be difficult, as I have some business ahead,” Yuan said.

“That business will have to wait. If you know what’s good for you, leave while I’m still patient enough to let you!” the Golden Immortal snapped, his voice rising sharply.

“This bastard… Who the hell do you think you are to speak to my Young Master in that tone? Have you lost your damn mind?!” Feng Yuxiang snapped as she caught up with Yuan.

Instantly, all eyes shifted to her—a peerless beauty with flaming red hair and golden eyes, unmistakably marking her as a beast in human form. Yet what truly seized their attention was the terrifying aura of her True Immortal cultivation.

Over the past several years, her cultivation had recovered enough to surpass Yuan once more, now just a hair’s breadth away from stepping into the God Ascension Realm.

The Golden Immortal who commanded Yuan tried to respond but was quickly interrupted, “First of all, you’re all in my territory. If anyone should leave, it’s you lot.”

“Your territory…? I’m sorry, but we’ve traveled through this region for the past hundred years, and this is the first time we’ve ever seen you. Who exactly are you people?” another Golden Immortal asked, his tone far more respectful now that they were standing before a True Immortal—someone who could easily kill them all.

“A mere hundred years?” Feng Yuxiang scoffed with a cold sneer. “To an Immortal, a hundred years could easily pass in the blink of an eye. You might as well admit you’ve only just arrived with such a trivial span of time.”

What Feng Yuxiang said made sense, but it was not like they could just leave, especially after pouring so many resources into their business.

“Ah, it seems we’ve forgotten to introduce ourselves. I am the Second Elder of the Blazing Orchid Sect, and—” The Golden Immortal tried to reveal his background, but Feng Yuxiang couldn’t care less and said.

“I don’t give a hot damn about your identity or where you came from. If you don’t apologize to my Young Master and get lost within ten seconds, I’ll turn you all into ashes.”

Feng Yuxiang threatened as she revealed some of her flames and unique aura.

“A phoenix!” These Immortals quickly realized her identity and swallowed nervously.

chapters.title: Chapter 2061: Hell Molten Eel
chapters.body:
Chapter 2061: Hell Molten Eel

Chapter 2061: Hell Molten Eel

The cultivators trembled at Feng Yuxiang’s threatening pressure, but before any of them could respond, Yuan spoke in a calm voice, “Relax, Feng Feng. There’s no need to spill blood over something like this.”

Feng Yuxiang immediately retrieved her aura and fell silent, but she continued staring at them, especially the Golden Immortal who spoke first.

Yuan continued, “I’m sure you all want to resolve this without unnecessary deaths, right?”

They silently nodded their heads.

“In that case, why don’t we start by explaining your situation?” Yuan said.

The Golden Immortals exchanged uneasy glances, clearly reluctant to reveal their true purpose.

Seeing this, Feng Yuxiang let out a cold sneer and said, “They’re probably here to seize some natural treasure—this place is perfect for birthing treasures rich in yang energy, after all.”

The Golden Immortals flinched slightly at her words, their reactions speaking louder than any words could.

“A treasure, huh? If you’re worried that I’ll fight you over it, rest assured—I have no interest in it,” Yuan said calmly a moment later. “However, I’d like to know when you’ll be done with your business and on your way.”

After a moment of silence, one of the Golden Immortals answered without revealing any details, “We should be out of your territory within a month.”

Yuan nodded and said, “Then we’ll wait for you to finish your business.”

He turned and returned to the flying treasure with Feng Yuxiang shortly after.

Though he had left, his presence still lingered like an invisible weight, forcing the cultivators to remain on high alert.

“What sort of treasure do you think they’re trying to acquire?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

“Well, the most common treasures found in the Infernal Gorge are minerals for forging artifacts and rare yang element herbs that can only grow in extreme environments like this,” Feng Yuxiang explained.

“But for so many people to gather here—and given that we’re right above the Flowing Molten Serpent—the treasure is likely the Hell Molten Eel, a rare creature that only appears once every few thousand years,” she continued.

Directly beneath them lay a massive river of flowing magma that resembled a serpent from afar, hence why it was known as the Flowing Molten Serpent. Though it might seem far too extreme to sustain life, there were actually magical beasts born and thriving within its fiery depths. The Hell Molten Eel was one such creature.

“What’s so special about this creature?” Yuan asked.

“Well, consuming its meat can drastically enhance one’s fire resistance and improve one’s yang qi. Beyond that, its skin and heart are prized ingredients for various pills and are highly sought after by alchemists.”

“I see… but why are they all just waiting there?”

“They probably lack the fire resistance to enter the Flowing Molten Serpent to capture the Hell Molten Eel. The creature surfaces once a month, so they might be waiting for that chance.”

A few days later, Yuan sensed new presences approaching in the distance, prompting him to pause his cultivation.

The moment the newcomers arrived, they immediately demanded that those already present leave at once. Naturally, their demand was refused, and not long after, a battle erupted.

As for Yuan, he simply observed the chaos from a distance, intending to stay uninvolved. However, the newcomers, unaware of who he was, foolishly targeted him as well in the heat of battle.

Seeing this, Feng Yuxiang said, “Young Master, please leave this to me. There’s no need for you to lift a finger for these weaklings.”

Yuan nodded.

In the next instant, Feng Yuxiang leapt from the flying treasure, intercepting the seven Silver Immortals who had charged toward them.

“How dare you weaklings raise your weapons against the Young Master?! Do you even know who you’re dealing with?!” Feng Yuxiang shouted as she unleashed her flames, instantly trapping them in a massive sphere of fire.

“Phoenix flames?!” The Silver Immortals recoiled in shock, stunned by the true identity of their opponent.

“For your crime, you shall be punished with death!” Feng Yuxiang declared, making a squeezing motion with her hand. Instantly, the sphere of flames began to contract, shrinking rapidly around her helpless prey.

“W-Wait! We were wrong!” the Immortals cried out, begging for their lives as their skin began to blister and burn away under the relentless flames.

“Of course you were in the wrong. Why else would you be dying?” Feng Yuxiang said coldly, shaking her head as she ignored their desperate pleas and mercilessly reduced them to ashes.

“I never knew she could be this scary…” Yu Ning commented after seeing Feng Yuxiang’s brutal side for the first time.

Meanwhile, the cultivators fighting nearby witnessed this scene and trembled in fear. Those who had chosen to ignore Yuan and Feng Yuxiang let out a sigh of relief, grateful they hadn’t provoked them earlier. As for the newcomers, they stood frozen in shock at the sight of a phoenix—a rare and mighty mythical being that many of them were encountering for the first time.

Feng Yuxiang then proclaimed in a clear, commanding voice, “I don’t care if you fight among yourselves, but if you dare disturb us, I’ll kill you without a second thought!”

Soon, the newcomers, unaware that Yuan had no interest in the Hell Molten Eel, quickly chose to retreat once they realized they were facing a phoenix at the True Immortal Realm.

However, that was far from the end of the commotion, as more and more cultivators continued to arrive, drawn by rumors of the Hell Molten Eel’s appearance, each ready to fight for a share of the prize.

Those who had arrived first refrained from fighting among themselves, as they had already agreed to share the treasure peacefully.

As for the newcomers, they would all attack Yuan’s flying treasure, only to be met with Feng Yuxiang’s wrath.

Despite the chaos, Yuan chose to remain at the Infernal Gorge, knowing that things would quiet down once the treasure was claimed, giving him the chance to absorb the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence in peace.

chapters.title: Chapter 2062: Hell Molten Eel(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2062: Hell Molten Eel(2)

Chapter 2062: Hell Molten Eel(2)

“This Hell Molten Serpent must be really valuable for so many people to show up for it,” Yuan remarked after being attacked for the fifth time in just two weeks.

“It’s not just the Hell Molten Serpent,” Feng Yuxiang replied. “That’s simply how things are in the higher Heavens. It’s common for cultivators to clash over every resource they can get their hands on.”

“Why? It’s not like there’s a shortage of resources.”

“I suppose that’s just the nature of the higher heavens—competition is simply a way of life.”

Yuan’s gaze suddenly shifted to a certain direction, and he said, “Looks like we have more newcomers.”

“Time to take out more trash,” Feng Yuxiang said as she excitedly prepared for battle.

“Why don’t you just put up a sign or something if you don’t want them attacking us?” Yuan asked.

Feng Yuxiang chuckled and said, “That would ruin all the fun. Do you have any idea how bored I was when I lost my powers? Now that they’re back, I intend to enjoy them to the fullest!”

Yuan merely shook his head at her response, having no intention of stopping her, even if it would spare some lives. After all, those who attacked them had only themselves to blame.

Soon enough, the newcomers launched their attack, and as before, a few broke away from the main battle to target the lone flying treasure that had been quietly minding its own business in the distance.

Before long, Feng Yuxiang’s beautiful flames swept across the sky like a crimson tsunami, and the Immortals who dared attack them were reduced to ashes in an instant.

“A phoenix?!” the newcomers exclaimed in shock, their reaction mirroring the disbelief of the cultivators who had come before them.

However, instead of fleeing like the others, one of them shouted, “C-Contact the Elders! Tell them there’s a True Immortal Phoenix here and that we need their assistance!”

At his command, one of the cultivators broke from the group and vanished into the distance.

A few minutes later, a massive flying ship appeared on the horizon, its arrival heralded by the unmistakable aura of three approaching True Immortals.

Once the ship arrived, three figures leapt from its deck and swiftly closed in on Yuan’s flying treasure, surrounding it in a tight triangular formation.

“Need my help?” Yuan asked Feng Yuxiang, who responded with a smile on her face, “No need, Young Master. I can handle them alone.”

She left the flying treasure to face the three True Immortals shortly later.

Once they were face to face, one of the True Immortals questioned, “You’re a phoenix—why would you need the Hell Molten Serpent?”

“Who said I need it?” Feng Yuxiang replied calmly.

“Then why are you here, fighting over it with us?!”

“Your people attacked us first, so it’s self-defense.”

“Are you telling me you’re just here to watch the spectacle?!” another True Immortal snapped, his voice trembling with anger.

“Something like that, I guess.”

The three True Immortals exchanged glances with one another. After a brief moment of silence, they all gave a slight nod, as if having come to an unspoken agreement.

Then, without warning, they began attacking Feng Yuxiang.

Though they would’ve preferred not to clash with a Divine Beast—a Phoenix, no less—they couldn’t let the Hell Molten Eel be taken away.

However, knowing there was a chance that Feng Yuxiang belonged to one of the Royal Phoenix Clans, they didn’t dare risk killing her outright. Instead, they attacked with the sole intention of subduing and suppressing her.

“Are you bastards looking down on me?” Feng Yuxiang snarled, realizing exactly what they were doing as flames surged violently around her.

The battle’s intensity escalated, and despite being outnumbered, Feng Yuxiang still managed to drive the three True Immortals back.

However, a few hours later, as the battle neared its conclusion and the True Immortals began to falter, a sudden tremor shook the land, and the surrounding volcanoes erupted in a roaring cascade of fire and molten rock.

Everyone on the battlefield froze mid-motion, their eyes instinctively drawn to the Flowing Molten Serpent below.

The river of lava suddenly began to churn and bubble ominously, as if something was emerging.

Seeing this, the Golden Immortal who had once tried to drive Yuan away shouted, “The Hell Molten Serpent is surfacing! Prepare to activate the formation! We’ll hold them off!”

Although their numbers had dwindled drastically over the past two weeks of constant fighting, they decided to remain there because of Feng Yuxiang’s presence.

Moments later, the surface of the lava river began to swell and rise, until a massive black eel emerged, its body etched with glowing cracks that pulsed like molten veins.

The cultivators activated the formation, instantly binding the Hell Molten Serpent with powerful chains that prevented it from escaping. However, that was all they could manage for now, as they were still under relentless attack from rival factions.

Unfortunately, they were hopelessly outnumbered, and the opposing forces kept whittling away at their ranks with relentless assaults.

With no other choice, they turned to Yuan for help.

“Please! Young Master! Help us!”

But Yuan had no reason to intervene. They were there to hunt for treasures, and he was merely a passerby.

While Feng Yuxiang had indirectly aided them by eliminating those who attacked their flying treasure, it didn’t make them allies in any sense of the word.

While it might seem right to save them, doing so would be unfair to everyone else who had come to compete for the treasure. After all, they hadn’t been attacked by bandits. They were simply participants in the same ruthless struggle for the Hell Molten Eel.

“If you want to survive, just give up on the treasure and leave,” Yuan told them calmly.

“Impossible!” one of them shouted back. “We’ve already lost so many trying to claim it! If we abandon it now, all those deaths would be meaningless!”

Yuan shook his head and sighed. “Use your brain a little. Since it’s clear you don’t have what it takes to claim it, you’ll also die a meaningless death if you continue to struggle for it. You might as well save the lives you still can.”

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 2063: Hell Molten Eel(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2063: Hell Molten Eel(3)

Chapter 2063: Hell Molten Eel(3)

Upon hearing Yuan’s words, it was as if a switch had been flipped inside their minds, snapping them back to their senses.

“He’s right… We’d just be throwing away our lives at this point…”

Moreover, it was clear that Yuan had no intention of helping them.

Very quickly, the cultivators who had been there from the start began to retreat, abandoning the Hell Molten Eel, which remained bound by the formation.

Seeing this, the opposing faction shouted, “Don’t let them escape! Hunt them down!”

But just as they surged forward to give chase, a figure silently appeared before them, like a ghost materializing from thin air.

After blocking them from the retreating cultivators, Yuan spoke in a cold voice, “I can’t tell if you’re bold or just stupid.”

Upon hearing Yuan’s voice, they realized that he was the one who had advised the others to run. However, when they saw that he was just a Bronze Immortal, they lowered their guard slightly.

“I thought you weren’t going to intervene,” said one of the Immortals there.

“I wasn’t, but that was because I had no reason to.”

“What’s your reason for intervening now, then?”

“Are you really asking me that?” Yuan replied calmly. “Those people behind me have already given up on the Hell Molten Eel, so why go out of your way to slaughter them? If you were still fighting for the treasure, I could understand, but this… this makes you no different from bandits.”

The Immortals scoffed and retorted, “What do you think this is—a game you can just walk away from whenever you feel like it? We’re all risking our lives here, you know?”

Yuan shrugged. “Where I come from, even in the midst of war, if your enemy surrenders, you have no right to kill them.”

“Where are you from? The boonies?” The Immortals laughed out loud.

Realizing his words were falling on deaf ears, Yuan let out a quiet sigh and said, “If you’re determined to kill them, then you’ll have to get through me first.”

“Who the fuck do you think you are? You’re just a Bronze Immortal, and we outnumber you three hundred to one!”

“If you think your phoenix friend can help you, she’s busy dealing with the Elders right now!”

Yuan glanced at Feng Yuxiang and smiled faintly.

“Judging by how things look, she’ll be done with them soon enough. But I should be able to deal with you lot long before that.”

“Arrogant bastard! Let’s see you try!”

The cultivators immediately surrounded Yuan and launched their assault.

Yuan could have easily wiped them all out with his Supreme Sword Aura, but instead, he chose to practice his phoenix disguise and unleashed his Heaven Freezing Flames instead.

In the next moment, beautiful light blue flames—clear and pure as an unpolluted sea—suddenly erupted from his body, surrounding him in a radiant blaze.

“This is—! He’s a phoenix, too?!”

The cultivators recoiled in shock at the sight of Yuan’s flames.

While they stood frozen in shock, Yuan conjured a massive ring of azure flames, forming a blazing arena that trapped them within its searing walls.

The Heaven Freezing Flames weren’t merely scorching hot—they were freezing cold as well. Those caught near them felt as if they were being burned and frozen simultaneously.

“I will give you all one last chance to lower your weapons and focus on that thing instead,” said Yuan as he pointed at the Hell Molten Eel beneath them. “Otherwise, I’ll turn everyone here into popsicles.”

“Hmph! Empty threats! Get him! Once we kill him, we’ll help the Elders with the other one!”

Without another word, these cultivators began bombarding Yuan with their martial techniques, some even using treasures to suppress his movements.

Yuan merely shook his head and mumbled, “Ask and you shall receive…”

In the moments that followed, Yuan unleashed his Heaven Freezing Flames upon the cultivators. Even the slightest touch of the flames caused their bodies to freeze over in an instant, like demons being sealed away, except instead of turning to stone, they became lifeless statues of ice.

The flames also inflicted an agony beyond description—burning their internal organs while encasing their outer bodies in bitter, unyielding ice.

In the blink of an eye, more than half of the three hundred cultivators were frozen into lifeless ice statues. Their frozen bodies then fell into the Flowing Molten Serpent below, vanishing beneath the roiling lava.

Realizing they stood no chance against Yuan, the remaining cultivators fell to their knees, begging for their lives.

“Please, spare us! Didn’t you say you’d even show mercy to your enemies in war if they surrendered where you’re from? This isn’t even close to that level of conflict!”

Yuan’s icy gaze stayed steady as he replied, “You’re right. This isn’t war—nowhere near it. I’m simply taking out the trash.”

Before the cultivators could utter another word, Yuan flicked his sleeve, unleashing his Heaven Freezing Flames once again and engulfing them like a tidal wave. In an instant, the remaining cultivators were frozen solid, their lifeless statues dropping into the lava river below like scattered pebbles, sending ripples across the molten surface.

Shortly later, Feng Yuxiang also dealt with the three True Immortals.

“What now?” Feng Yuxiang asked him after things calmed down and they were alone again.

Yuan lowered his gaze to the Hell Molten Eel, only to find that it had broken free from its bindings during the chaos and slipped back into the depths of the Flowing Molten Serpent.

“I’ll be right back.”

Without another word, he abruptly dove toward the lava river, vanishing beneath its molten surface.

Thanks to his Fire Harmony, the lava felt comforting—like being wrapped in a warm blanket on a cold winter’s night—and it even replenished his energy as he moved through it.

Eventually, he caught up to the Hell Molten Serpent, seized it effortlessly, and returned to Feng Yuxiang.

“What are you going to do with that? It won’t benefit your fire resistance at that level,” Feng Yuxiang asked him.

chapters.title: Chapter 2064: Consuming The Primordial Phoenix’S Blood Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2064: Consuming The Primordial Phoenix’S Blood Essence

Chapter 2064: Consuming the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence

Yuan looked at the Hell Molten Eel and shrugged, “No clue, but I’ll think of something.”

He then tossed the entire thing inside his spatial ring.

“Alright, now that things are peaceful and quiet, let’s head to my Immortal Cave,” Feng Yuxiang said a moment later.

Yuan nodded and followed her to a massive volcano that rivaled the Dragon Spiral Mountain in size, located just a few hundred miles from where they had found the Hell Molten Eel.

The temperature in this area was nearly unbearable for anyone lacking at least Supreme Fire Resistance. The volcano would also constantly erupt with lava, sending showers of molten rock and scorching debris that could easily kill any cultivator who wasn’t an Immortal.

Some time later, Feng Yuxiang came to a stop above the volcano and said, “My Immortal Cave is just inside.”

When Yuan looked down into the massive crater, he saw a seething world of flames within, greatly resembling that of the Nine Levels of Hell from the Primal Expanse.

Of course, it wasn’t nearly as intensive and dangerous as the training ground from the Primal Expanse, and he followed Feng Yuxiang inside without any hesitation.

After descending to about halfway down the mountain, stopping just above the roiling sea of lava, they finally reached their destination.

“So, where’s the Immortal Cave?” Yuan asked aloud, glancing around but seeing nothing that even remotely resembled a cave.

“Give me a moment to bring it out,” said Feng Yuxiang.

Then, she retrieved an object from her spatial ring and pointed it toward the lava below.

The volcano began to tremble almost immediately, and Yuan watched as something slowly rose from the molten depths. When it fully emerged, it revealed a massive cave entrance with a path that appeared to descend beneath the lava’s surface.

“Let’s go, Young Master. We’re almost there,” Feng Yuxiang said, speaking calmly as she stepped into the cave entrance without hesitation.

Yuan followed her into the darkness. However, after just a few steps, the scenery suddenly brightened, and he found himself in an entirely different place. The volcano and its blistering heat were gone, replaced by a cool forest filled with the fresh scent of greenery. Dense trees surrounded them, and not far away stood a massive tree, towering a hundred times larger than the rest, like some ancient, grand sentinel at the heart of the forest.

“This is your Immortal Cave?” Yuan muttered, raising an eyebrow.

Her ’cave’ was, in truth, an entire world of its own, much like Senior Bai’s realm within the Myriad of Techniques.

Feng Yuxiang smiled and asked teasingly, “Did you think it would be an actual cave? Maybe something like that would satisfy human cultivators, but for me, it has to be something like this.”

Yuan glanced at the massive tree and remarked, “Honestly, this scenery doesn’t really match a phoenix’s image. After all, fire and nature don’t exactly mix well.”

“Maybe not an ordinary forest, where the trees would burn to ash at the slightest spark. But this place is different,” she said with a smile. “Even if you tried to set the entire forest ablaze, nothing would happen.”

“Anyway, you can consume the blood essence here without worrying about being disturbed. I’ll also keep guard from the outside just in case,” she continued.

“Alright. Thank you.” Yuan nodded.

Feng Yuxiang left the Immortal Cave shortly after to stand guard, even though it was highly unlikely anyone would come to cause trouble. After all, she had spent thousands of years here without a single disturbance.

Once outside, Feng Yuxiang sat outside the entrance like a guardian and closed her eyes to cultivate.

Meanwhile, Yuan flew to the top of the grand tree and settled at its peak, where a large nest was nestled among the branches, clearly Feng Yuxiang’s resting place.

He sat in the nest and gazed out at the breathtaking scenery before him—an unobstructed view of the entire forest stretching out in every direction.

A few minutes later, he retrieved the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence and stared at it in silence. Once he felt ready, he removed the seal on the vial.

The moment he broke the seal, a searing wave of heat surged out. The heat was so intense that, even with his Fire Harmony, Yuan felt a burning sting on his face, and this overwhelming heat radiated from just a single drop of blood.

Yuan swallowed nervously after this experience.

“It’s been a while since I last felt this nervous…” he muttered with a stiff smile on his face.

He closed his eyes and took several deep breaths to steady himself. Once ready, Yuan raised the vial to his lips and let the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence pour into his mouth.

The moment the blood essence touched his tongue, it was instantly absorbed into his body.

<You have consumed the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence>

However, before Yuan could even process what was happening, his entire body was suddenly engulfed in flames—invisible flames that couldn’t be seen with the naked eye.

This invisible flame was so fierce and potent that it tore straight through his fire resistance, flooding his body with an indescribable pain.

The pain was nearly unbearable for Yuan, so intense that he found himself howling in agony during those first few moments.

<Your fire resistance has improved drastically>

<Your fire resistance has improved drastically>

<Your fire resistance has improved drastically>

“H-Hang in there, Yuan!” Lan Yingying cheered him from within his Dantian, but Yuan was in too much pain to even hear her voice.

Yet, the strength and heat of the invisible flames only grew with each passing moment. The space around Yuan warped under the searing force, the distortion spreading wider as the heat intensified.

His body was constantly being burned and torn apart, yet his regeneration kept pace so perfectly that it seemed as if no damage was being done at all.

Soon, the flames began to burn not only his body, but his very soul as well, and the only thing keeping his existence from being burned away was Heaven’s Undying Soul.

chapters.title: Chapter 2065: Refining The Primordial Phoenix’S Blood Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2065: Refining The Primordial Phoenix’S Blood Essence

Chapter 2065: Refining the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence

After absorbing the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence, time seemed to slow to a standstill for Yuan as he was consumed by endless, indescribable pain.

Minutes turned into hours, and hours into days, yet the invisible flames continued to burn Yuan relentlessly, showing no sign of fading anytime soon.

Even worse, the pain from the invisible flames only grew more intense with each passing moment.

Two weeks later, the invisible flames had spread across the entire world, engulfing it completely and causing the very fabric of space to warp and ripple without end. Yet, the trees appeared completely fine, as if the flames didn’t even exist.

As for Yuan, he had stopped screaming, but that didn’t mean the pain had vanished. Even as his tolerance grew, the strength of the invisible flames always outpaced it by just enough to keep him in constant, unrelenting agony.

Suddenly—

Ding!

<Your Fire Immunity has evolved into True Fire Immunity>

Though it may seem like Yuan had achieved Fire Harmony, that was not the case, and he was only able to absorb flames and turn them into his power because of his physique. This meant that his fire resistance still had considerable room to grow.

After attaining True Fire Immunity, Yuan managed to endure the invisible flames with slightly more ease, though only for a moment. Almost as if it were sentient and realized it was no longer as effective as before, the flames suddenly surged in power.

Though it was painful, Yuan actually preferred it this way since it meant his fire immunity wouldn’t slow down its progress.

Then, after another month, Yuan achieved Fire Harmony.

<Your True Fire Immunity has evolved into Fire Harmony>

<Completed Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Completed Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun to evolve>

<Progress: 55%>

Then, after another three months, his fire immunity improved again.

<Your Fire Harmony has evolved into Perfect Fire Harmony>

Upon attaining Perfect Fire Harmony, the invisible flames lost their power over Yuan. They could no longer inflict pain on him, finally bringing an end to his relentless torment.

However, this didn’t mean he was finished absorbing the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence. In truth, he was only just beginning—the ordeal so far had merely been a test to determine if he was worthy.

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline is reacting to the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence>

Unlike when he consumed the Dragon God’s Blood Essence, Yuan was strong enough to refine the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence without any assistance. However, it would take some time.

After an entire year of relentless refinement, Yuan finally succeeded in fully absorbing the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence.

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline evolution has progressed slightly>

<Progress: 22%>

<You have acquired the power of a Primordial Phoenix through its Blood Essence>

<You have learned ’True Phoenix Awakening’>

<You have learned ’True Primordial Fire’>

[True Primordial Fire]

[Rank: ???]

[Mastery Level: 1]

[A unique flame possessed only by the Primordial Phoenix—completely invisible to the eye, yet so destructive it can burn through reality itself.]

<Your Completed Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has evolved into True Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique>

The instant his physique evolved, Yuan’s cultivation—which had been bottlenecked at the peak of Bronze Immortal—immediately surged to the next level, elevating him to Silver Immortal. But it didn’t stop there, and his cultivation continued to soar until it reached the peak of Silver Immortal in one go.

Shortly after finishing the refinement of the blood essence, Yuan activated True Phoenix Awakening to test its power.

Instantly, his long black hair turned a brilliant crimson and ignited with flickering flames, while his eyes gleamed a radiant gold. His appearance now bore an uncanny resemblance to Feng Yuxiang’s when she uses her phoenix powers.

It wasn’t just his appearance—his aura, and even his scent, became indistinguishable from that of a true phoenix, as if he had truly transformed into one.

Yuan raised his arm and activated the True Primordial Fire. Instantly, invisible flames surged forth like a tidal wave, engulfing everything in front of him.

“Not bad,” he murmured, clearly satisfied with the results. Now, he could easily pass himself off as a true phoenix.

He retrieved the Ancient Phoenix Robe a moment later and wore it without any issues. Even if it didn’t recognize him as a phoenix, its curse wouldn’t have any effect on his Perfect Fire Harmony.

Sometime later, Yuan emerged from the Immortal Cave to reunite with Feng Yuxiang, who had been standing watch outside the entire time.

“Y-Young Master…?” Feng Yuxiang’s eyes widened, her mouth slightly agape as she stared at Yuan, still clad in his phoenix form.

Unbeknownst to her, a soft flush of red had already spread across her cheeks.

“What do you think?” Yuan asked, a calm smile on his face.

“…”

But Feng Yuxiang couldn’t respond, as she was utterly speechless. She had always believed Yuan was perfect, yet seeing him in his phoenix form and wearing the Ancient Phoenix Robe felt like witnessing an entirely new, breathtaking side of him.

A sudden tingling sensation ran through her body, sending an involuntary shiver down her spine. The last time she had felt something like this was when she had used her hands to assist Yuan, but now, just his scent alone was enough to arouse her.

However, Feng Yuxiang quickly snapped herself out of her daze by forcefully slapping her own cheeks, just as one might do to jolt oneself awake.

“C-Congratulations, Young Master. If I were seeing you for the first time, I wouldn’t have been able to tell you’re actually human and not a true phoenix,” she said.

“If even you can’t tell the difference, then fooling everyone else should be easy,” Yuan said with a faint smile. “Anyway, thank you for the blood essence, Feng Feng. If there’s anything you need, I’ll do my best to make it happen.”

Still feeling a bit dazed, Feng Yuxiang silently nodded.

Sometime later, Feng Yuxiang and Yuan departed from the volcano and set out on their journey toward the Rising Phoenix Clan.

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 2066: Arriving At The Rising Phoenix Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2066: Arriving At The Rising Phoenix Clan

Chapter 2066: Arriving at the Rising Phoenix Clan

“So, how should we approach the Rising Phoenix Clan?” Yuan asked Feng Yuxiang as they traveled on the flying treasure.

“…”

When Feng Yuxiang didn’t respond, Yuan opened his eyes and turned to her, only to find her staring at him with a dazed expression, completely mesmerized by his new appearance.

“Feng Feng?” he called out to her again.

This time, she snapped out of it and stammered in a panic, “H-Huh? What did you just say? Sorry, I was thinking about something…”

“I asked if you have any suggestions on how we should approach the Rising Phoenix Clan,” he repeated calmly.

“I’ve thought about it for the past year, but I still can’t come up with anything. After all, I severed our ties when I left—so it would be strange to just return as if nothing ever happened,” she sighed.

“Why don’t you just do what you did with the dragon clans?” she suddenly said.

“You mean pretend to be a Phoenix Emperor? Does such a title even exist among the phoenix clans?” Yuan asked, raising an eyebrow.

“Of course. It would be weird if we didn’t.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before speaking, “While that might have worked in the lower heavens, I doubt the Rising Phoenix Clan, one of the Royal Phoenix Clans, would fall for such a trick.”

“That’s true…”

She added after a brief pause, “Additionally, even if you show up as a Phoenix Emperor, I cannot imagine how you’ll convince them to hand over something as precious as the Ancient Seal.”

Yuan closed his eyes to ponder again.

A few days later, he asked, “Dragons deeply respect—sometimes even revere—those with a stronger bloodline. Is it the same with phoenixes?”

Feng Yuxiang nodded, “Not just phoenixes. It’s basically like that for every single beast race out there.”

“In that case…” He paused for a moment before continuing, “This might be a long shot, but if pretending to be a Phoenix Emperor won’t work, why not appear as a Primordial Phoenix instead? Your bloodline is essentially the ancestor of phoenixes, right?”

“That’s correct. The Primordial Phoenix is the most ancient and powerful phoenix bloodline.”

Suddenly, Feng Yuxiang’s expression shifted, as if a thought had struck her.

“If you’re going to do that, why not try to recruit the Rising Phoenix Clan?” she suggested. “As far as I know, they’ve been trying to improve their bloodline for a long time. With your Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline, you could easily win them over. Of course, you won’t be going there as the Immortal Monarch but as a Primordial Phoenix.”

“Now that I think about it, the position of Phoenix Emperor is too lowly for someone like you, Young Master,” she added.

Yuan nodded, “That does sound like a good idea. Not only would it benefit me to have one of the Royal Phoenix Clans on my side, but they might be more willing to hand over the Ancient Seal if I present myself as a Primordial Phoenix.”

“Then let’s go over our plans.”

Time passed, and in the blink of an eye, three months had already gone by since Yuan consumed the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence.

The Rising Phoenix Clan was a small yet powerful clan situated in the Nirvana Crest—a mountain range where, according to legend, those who perish within its bounds could be reborn as a phoenix.

Just like the dragon clans, due to the rarity of pure and authentic phoenix bloodlines, the phoenix clans were primarily composed of beasts related to phoenixes but not true phoenixes themselves. Only the core members of the clan were true phoenixes.

To expand their power and influence, the Royal Phoenix Clan continuously recruits new beasts into their ranks. Because of this, the Nirvana Crest is always bustling with a steady flow of immortal beasts coming and going.

Much like a sect, the Rising Phoenix Clan puts every recruit through rigorous trials and tests the quality of their bloodline. In the world of beasts, the strength of one’s bloodline determined one’s potential, making it the most vital measure of worth.

As thousands of powerful beasts lined up, forming a long queue at the Nirvana Crest, they suddenly sensed a formidable presence steadily approaching their way.

“Which idiot is showing off before the Rising Phoenix Clan? Who are they trying to impress?”

Though the presence was formidable, it was nothing they hadn’t felt before. After all, there was always someone trying to show off now and then, hoping to attract the attention of the Rising Phoenix Clan.

However, when the phoenixes within the Rising Phoenix Clan sensed this presence, they were shocked to find their own bloodlines reacting to it—something that only happened in the presence of a bloodline vastly superior to their own.

Very quickly, members of the Rising Phoenix Clan began to gather outside their residence, standing in quiet anticipation as the powerful presence drew steadily closer.

Soon, they realized it wasn’t just a single presence approaching but two, and one of them felt oddly familiar, yet strangely unfamiliar at the same time.

Eventually, the figures came into view, and the phoenixes’ eyes widened in shock when they recognized one of them.

“That woman… isn’t that Feng Yuxiang?”

“What?”

“…You’re right! That’s Feng Yuxiang!”

“What’s she doing here? It’s been thousands of years since I last heard from her.”

“Do you think she’s trying to return to the clan?”

“So soon? Didn’t she just leave?”

The phoenixes immediately began to speculate upon seeing Feng Yuxiang. Although it had been thousands of years since she had left them, such a span of time was nothing to them, and in their eyes, it felt as if she had only just departed.

“Is it just me, or is there something very different about her? Though her appearance is the same, her aura is very different, not to mention her presence.”

“Who’s that next to her?”

“Wait a second… Isn’t that the Ancient Phoenix Robe he’s wearing?”

Soon, Yuan and Feng Yuxiang arrived at the Rising Phoenix Clan, coming to a halt directly in front of their grand residence.

chapters.title: Chapter 2067: Rising Phoenix Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2067: Rising Phoenix Clan

Chapter 2067: Rising Phoenix Clan

Shortly after their arrival, one of the phoenixes from the Rising Phoenix Clan approached them in the air.

“It’s been a while, Junior Sister Yuxiang.” The phoenix, a middle-aged woman, greeted her.

“It’s only been a few thousand years, Senior Sister Zhenyan,” said Feng Yuixang.

It was pretty common for phoenixes from different clans to share the surname ’Feng’, much like how many dragon clans have the surname ’Long’ despite coming from different backgrounds.

“Where have you been all these years? We haven’t heard a word from you since you left us.”

“I went on a little adventure in the lower heavens…” Feng Yuxiang replied, purposefully avoiding any mention of the curse.

“The lower heavens…? There’s nothing to see down there.” Feng Zhenyan shook her head.

“That’s not true. There are a lot of things to do there, and my life has changed a lot because of it.”

“If you say so. Anyway, why have you come here? Have you decided to return to us? And who is that handsome phoenix beside you?” Feng Zhenyan’s gaze suddenly shifted to Yuan, and her face flushed slightly at Yuan’s handsome appearance and distinct scent.

“This is my Young Master, whom I met in the lower heavens,” Feng Yuxiang said, a warm smile on her face.

“Young Master…?” Feng Zhenyan’s eyes widened at how she addressed him.

Everyone in the Rising Phoenix Clan knew of Feng Yuxiang’s proud and haughty nature, so to hear her call someone ’Young Master’ was simply unimaginable.

’No, maybe I misheard or am misunderstanding it. His actual name must be Young Master…’ Feng Zhenyan thought to herself.

Almost as if she could read Feng Zhenyan’s thoughts, Feng Yuxiang added, “That’s right. I’m serving him now, so he is my Young Master.”

Feng Zhenyan was left speechless. She had already been curious about Yuan’s identity, but now, realizing that he had managed to subdue someone as difficult as Feng Yuxiang, she felt a flicker of nervousness. What kind of heaven-defying background did he possess?

“Anyway, we came here because the Young Master has some business with the Rising Phoenix Clan. Is the Matriarch available right now?” Feng Yuxiang asked a moment later.

Feng Zhenyan shook her head and replied, “No… the Matriarch had some business and left a few months ago. That said, we received news that she would be back soon.”

Feng Yuxiang looked at Yuan and asked, “What do you think, Young Master? Should we leave for now and return later?”

“What a hassle.” Yuan sighed.

Hearing this, Feng Zhenyan quickly said, “If you’re willing, you’re welcome to stay with us until the Matriarch returns.”

“I’m a bit pressed for time,” Yuan replied calmly. “Why don’t I just speak with whoever holds the highest authority available right now?”

“Right now…? That would be the Grand Elder, Feng Lingtian,” Feng Zhenyan replied after pondering for a brief moment.

“I’ll speak to him, but he’ll want to know what he’s dealing with…”

Yuan then said calmly, “My name isn’t important. Just tell him that a Primordial Phoenix is here.”

“P-P-Primordial Phoenix?!” Feng Zhenyan gasped, her voice trembling with shock and disbelief.

“Primordial Phoenix? What are they talking about?”

Those who had been watching from a distance were startled by Feng Zhenyan’s sudden outburst, their curiosity instantly ignited.

Shortly after her outburst, Feng Zhenyan turned to look at Feng Yuxiang for confirmation.

In response, Feng Yuxiang nodded her head and calmly confirmed, “My Young Master carries with him the most ancient and noble phoenix bloodline in existence—the Primordial Phoenix Bloodline.”

Hearing this, Feng Zhenyan instantly lowered her head and bowed toward Yuan, “P-Please excuse my lack of respect, Senior Primordial Phoenix!”

“I don’t really care about such things, but if you want to repent, just notify your Grand Elder of my presence.”

“As you wish! In fact, please come with me. I’ll bring you to him.”

Yuan nodded and followed Feng Zhenyan into the depths of the Rising Phoenix Clan’s territory.

They stopped before a specific mountain some time later, and Feng Zhenyan said, “Please wait here for a moment. Only members of the Rising Phoenix Clan or those permitted are allowed past this point.”

Feng Zhenyan flew away and disappeared behind the mountain shortly later.

Upon reaching her destination, Feng Zhenyan called out, “Grand Elder, we have an emergency.”

Moments later, a short old man with white and red hair appeared before her like a ghost.

“What happened?” he asked her in a stern tone.

“A guest claiming to be a Primordial Phoenix has appeared at our Rising Phoenix Clan, and he wishes to speak with you.”

The Grand Elder’s half-closed eyes snapped open in surprise at the mention of ’Primordial Phoenix.’

“Are you certain you heard that correctly? A Primordial Phoenix hasn’t appeared in this world for millions of years,” he said, his voice tinged with disbelief.

“I am certain. In fact, Feng Yuxiang is here with him, and she even addresses him as ’Young Master’.”

“Feng Yuxiang? That young phoenix who recently left?”

“That’s correct.”

“Do you know what they want?” The Grand Elder then asked.

“No, but they initially wanted to speak with the Matriarch. Unfortunately, she’s not here right now, so they want to speak with you instead.”

“Very well. Bring them here. I shall see for myself whether we’re dealing with a true Primordial Phoenix or just some fraud.”

Without another word, Feng Zhenyan left to return to Yuan and Feng Yuxiang.

“The Grand Elder has agreed to meet with you. Please, follow me.” Feng Zhenyan said to them the moment she returned.

Sometime later, Yuan and Feng Yuxiang followed her to the Grand Elder, who was waiting at a sprawling flower garden nestled behind a secluded mountain.

When the Grand Elder laid eyes on Yuan and sensed his presence fully, he suddenly felt a warmth stir within him, as if his very blood was responding to Yuan’s existence.

’This feeling… Could it be? Could he truly be a Primordial Phoenix?’ The Grand Elder swallowed hard, a flicker of nervousness passing through him.

chapters.title: Chapter 2068: True Primordial Fire
chapters.body:
Chapter 2068: True Primordial Fire

Chapter 2068: True Primordial Fire

“Welcome to the Rising Phoenix Clan, esteemed guest. My name is Feng Lingtian, the Grand Elder of this clan.”

The short old man introduced himself to Yuan shortly after their arrival.

But before Yuan could do the same, he continued, “I heard that you have the Primordial Phoenix’s Bloodline, but before we can even begin our conversation, I must verify something first. We haven’t seen this bloodline for millions of years—since the Immortal Monarch’s Era, so I apologize for being skeptical, nor am I trying to offend you.”

Yuan shook his head calmly and spoke, “No, I fully understand your skepticism. I just need to prove my identity, correct?”

Naturally, Yuan had predicted this would happen and was prepared for it.

“Then, allow me to apologize in advance.” Yuan suddenly said.

“Huh?” The Grand Elder couldn’t understand his words at first, but quickly realized why he was apologizing.

“Kneel.”

Yuan activated Immortal Monarch’s Dominion, commanding the Grand Elder to kneel on the ground.

The Grand Elder was at the 5th level of the God Ascension Realm, but Yuan had not only improved his cultivation but also his Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline after consuming the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence. This allowed him to command God Ascension Realm beasts with much more ease and for far longer than before.

“G-Grand Elder!” Feng Zhenyan gasped in shock when she saw him kneel to the ground—something he wouldn’t do even for the Matriarch of the Rising Phoenix Clan.

Although Yuan had withdrawn the Immortal Monarch’s Dominion almost immediately, the Grand Elder’s knees remained firmly rooted on the ground. His head was bowed, but if one could see his face, they would find his eyes wide with shock and his mouth agape, as if he had just witnessed a miracle.

Just as dragons could wield Dragon Authority to command those of weaker bloodlines, phoenixes could invoke Phoenix Authority to command phoenixes with lesser bloodlines.

Though the Grand Elder’s bloodline wasn’t the absolute strongest, it was among the very best, strong enough to elevate him to the position of Grand Elder within the Rising Phoenix Clan, one of the Royal Phoenix Clans.

However, while impressive, it wasn’t enough to prove Yuan’s identity as a Primordial Phoenix.

Aware of this, Yuan said calmly, “That was just an introduction. Now, here’s the real proof.”

He lifted his hand and, with a smooth motion, flipped his palm over—summoning the Primordial Flames into existence.

Feng Zhenyan’s eyes widened as she silently stared at the invisible flames dancing above Yuan’s palm. The Grand Elder, who had begun to lift his head at Yuan’s words, suddenly sprang to his feet and instinctively backed away—every fiber of his being warning him of the flames’ danger, despite his nature as a phoenix.

“This is…” The Grand Elder swallowed hard, his voice trembling.

Though he had never seen them with his own eyes, he had read of them in ancient records—flames invisible to the eye, capable of burning even a phoenix with fire immunity. A power wielded only by the most ancient and exalted bloodline, the True Primordial Fire.

“Would you like to experience the flames yourself, to be certain it’s no trick?” Yuan asked suddenly.

The Grand Elder trembled at the mere thought of subjecting himself to the strongest fire in the universe.

As phoenixes, it was in their very nature to immerse themselves in the strongest fires they could find, both to temper their bodies and as a mark of pride worth boasting about, almost like moths drawn to flames.

Therefore, when the Grand Elder was offered a chance to experience the strongest fire known to exist—something long thought extinct—there was no way he could pass up such an opportunity, even if it might cost him his life.

The Grand Elder nodded his head with a stern expression on his face, agreeing to Yuan’s offer.

“Very well.”

Yuan looked at Feng Zhenyan and said, “You should stand back a little.”

However, Feng Zhenyan shook her head and responded in a resolute but somewhat nervous voice, “No, please allow me to experience the True Primordial Fire as well.”

After all, she was also a phoenix.

“If you say so.”

Without hesitation, Yuan released the True Primordial Fire, instantly engulfing their bodies in its invisible, all-consuming blaze.

Feng Zhenyan immediately let out a scream of pain, while the Grand Elder clenched his fists and gritted his jaw in silence, veins bulging across his body as he endured the searing agony.

Yet, despite their obvious agony, both Feng Zhenyan and the Grand Elder wore almost sadistic smiles on their faces, clearly relishing the pain.

“Yes! This is it! This is the pinnacle of fire! I’ve survived all these years just for this moment!” the Grand Elder shouted, spreading his arms wide and crying out to the heavens.

Moments later, Feng Zhenyan suddenly collapsed to the ground. Seeing this, Yuan withdrew the flames from her, leaving her body steaming with wisps of smoke, almost like a roasted bird.

At a glance, the Grand Elder seemed fine, but Yuan could sense that his body was sustaining damage. Since he had no intention of harming them, there was no reason to continue, so he withdrew the flames from the Grand Elder as well.

However, the very next moment, the Grand Elder suddenly let out a scream, but unlike Feng Zhenyan’s, his cry wasn’t just from physical pain. It carried the raw edge of emotional anguish, as if he’d just lost something precious or endured a deep, mental wound.

“No! The light is gone! Ahhhh!” he cried out in despair, even dropping to the ground and rolling around like he was on fire.

Yuan could only watch in stunned silence, his eyes wide, speechless at such an unexpected reaction. He couldn’t help but wonder if the True Primordial Fire had some side effects that caused hallucinations.

“Don’t worry about him. This is quite normal,” Feng Yuxiang said to Yuan.

“If you say so…”

A moment later, the Grand Elder crawled toward Yuan and pleaded in a desperate voice, “Please… use the True Primordial Fire on me again… I’m so cold…”

chapters.title: Chapter 2069: Immortal Monarch’S Blessing
chapters.body:
Chapter 2069: Immortal Monarch’S Blessing

Chapter 2069: Immortal Monarch’s Blessing

“Please! I beg of you! Burn me with the True Primordial Fire!” The Grand Elder continued to beg when Yuan didn’t respond the first time.

“…”

“If I do that, you could die—your physical body, that is,” Yuan shook his head.

“I don’t care! I am willing to take that risk!” The Grand Elder immediately responded without any pause.

Yuan turned to look at Feng Zhenyan, who was still lying on the ground, her body twitching slightly. If not for the euphoric expression on her face, Yuan might have been genuinely concerned for her well-being.

“Please! The fire! I am freezing to death here!”The Grand Elder continued to beg.

After a brief moment of silence, Yuan decided to grant the Grand Elder’s wish. After all, it would only work in his favor, helping him bring the Rising Phoenix Clan to his side.

Without uttering a word, Yuan used True Primordial Fire on the Grand Elder for a second time.

“YES! THE LIGHT! IT’S BACK!” the Grand Elder exclaimed joyfully, his voice filled with delight as he reveled in the embrace of the invisible flames.

Moments later, the Grand Elder suddenly sat down cross-legged on the ground and closed his eyes, slipping into cultivation.

Feng Zhenyan snapped out of her daze a few hours after the Grand Elder entered cultivation and said, “It seems the Grand Elder has gained enlightenment. I apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. We’ll definitely compensate you for it.”

Yuan calmly shook his head and said, “It’s fine.”

“Would you like to wait at our residence? We’ll prepare our finest guest house for you,” Feng Zhenyan offered quickly.

Yuan glanced at the Grand Elder and shook his head. “We’ll return at a later date.”

“That’s…” Feng Zhenyan began, not wanting to let them leave so easily, but she couldn’t think of any way to keep them there.

“There’s no need.”

The Grand Elder suddenly opened his eyes.

“You’re done already?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

He stood up and bowed to Yuan, “Thank you, Esteemed Guest, for granting me a moment of enlightenment. As brief as it may be, I have gained a lot of insight. Now, let’s talk about your reason for coming to the Rising Phoenix Clan, shall we?”

Yuan nodded and said, “Then I will get straight to the point. I am here to recruit the Rising Phoenix Clan.”

“Recruit… us?” The Grand Elder lifted his head, surprise written all over his face.

Given their status as one of the Royal Phoenix Clans, Yuan’s offer could have easily been seen as brazen and arrogant—an outright claim that he stood above the Rising Phoenix Clan itself. But as a Primordial Phoenix, Yuan had every right to make such an offer.

Yuan continued calmly, “If you agree to serve me, I will elevate your bloodline and your standing within the Royal Phoenix Clan to a far greater height.”

The Grand Elder trembled at the thought of their bloodline being strengthened. Knowing Yuan was a Primordial Phoenix, he had no doubt about his capability. After all, the Primordial Phoenix wasn’t just the most ancient bloodline—it also possessed the power to elevate the bloodlines of other phoenixes.

“If you doubt my capabilities or sincerity, I’m willing to give you a demonstration,” Yuan said, shocking them all. “Bring me one of your phoenixes, and I will strengthen their bloodline.”

“Are you really willing to do that for us?!” The Grand Elder asked.

Yuan silently nodded.

The Grand Elder immediately turned to look at Feng Zhenyan and commanded, “Bring Feng Haoyu here immediately!”

“Yes!”

Some time later, Feng Zhenyan returned with another figure at her side—a handsome young man with long red hair and striking crimson eyes. He carried himself with a certain quiet charm and a scholar-like demeanor.

Feng Yuxiang immediately recognized the handsome young man—he was the eldest and most talented genius of the Rising Phoenix Clan, the very one they had once tried to pair her with.

Once they arrived, the Grand Elder introduced, “This is Feng Haoyu, the pride of our Rising Phoenix Clan.”

“This junior greets Senior Primordial Phoenix,” Feng Haoyu said, bowing deeply. Having been briefed by Feng Zhenyan, he knew what was about to take place and showed Yuan the utmost respect.

He then bowed to Feng Yuxiang and said, “It’s good to see you again, Junior Sister.”

Naturally, he didn’t forget Feng Yuxiang.

“Do you know why you were summoned?” Yuan asked him.

“I had been briefed.”

“Then I will proceed to strengthen your bloodline now.”

“W-Wait. What about the preparations?” the Grand Elder suddenly interjected.

He had expected some elaborate ritual—after all, enhancing a bloodline was no simple feat and was usually an extremely complex and time-consuming process.

“Don’t treat him like an ordinary phoenix,” Feng Yuxiang said with a faint smile. “My Young Master isn’t bound by common sense.”

“Well, are you ready?” Yuan asked Feng Haoyu.

Feng Haoyu knelt on one knee and said firmly, “I am prepared.”

Yuan gave a silent nod, then raised a finger and, a moment later, lightly touched it to Haoyu’s forehead.

<Immortal Monarch’s Blessing has activated>

<Target ’Feng Haoyu’ has received your blessing for one year>

After consuming the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence, Yuan had gained a new technique through his Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline—one that allowed him to bestow power and strengthen the bloodlines of beasts.

Feng Haoyu suddenly felt a surge of power flow from Yuan’s fingertips into him, flooding his body with strength unlike anything he had ever felt before.

The change was so profound that even the Grand Elder could immediately sense that something was different about Feng Haoyu.

“How do you feel?” Yuan asked, withdrawing his finger.

Feng Haoyu rose to his feet and clenched his fists, a look of awe on his face. “I’ve never felt better. This overwhelming energy coursing through my body… I was only expecting a slight improvement, so this has far surpassed my expectations.”

Yuan turned to look at the Grand Elder and continued, “You’re more than welcome to test his bloodline.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2070: Heavenly White Valley
chapters.body:
Chapter 2070: Heavenly White Valley

Chapter 2070: Heavenly White Valley

Shortly after Yuan used the Immortal Monarch’s Blessing on Feng Haoyu, the Grand Elder retrieved a crimson needle shaped like a feather and used it to test his blood.

With a quick prick, he collected a single drop of Feng Haoyu’s blood.

Moments later, the Grand Elder excitedly exclaimed, “It worked! Your bloodline has improved by nearly five percent—not just in strength, but in purity as well!”

Although five percent might not sound like much, it was actually an extraordinary improvement, given how quickly it had been achieved. In fact, even among the other Royal Phoenix Clans, a one or two-percent increase in bloodline purity could take decades to attain.

“F-Five percent?!” Feng Haoyu almost couldn’t believe what he was hearing and nearly collapsed from shock.

However, Yuan quickly added, “I should remind you that this improvement isn’t permanent. After a year, your bloodline will return to its original state. But if the Rising Phoenix Clan agrees to serve me, I won’t just make it permanent—I’ll raise it far beyond five percent.”

The Grand Elder trembled at the thought of the entire clan’s bloodline being elevated by more than five percent. It was an offer no phoenix could resist. However, there was one issue.

“As much as I’d like to decide on the spot, I don’t have the authority to do so. In fact, not even the Matriarch could make such a decision alone. Once she returns, we’ll convene a meeting with all the elders to reach a conclusion. That said, given the circumstances, I believe the outcome will be favorable.”

“In that case, I will take my leave now and return exactly one year from today,” Yuan said, already planning to visit the Heavenly White Tiger Clan in the meantime.

Hearing this, the Grand Elder felt a flicker of worry, fearful that Yuan might leave and never return. Unfortunately, there was truly nothing he could do to prevent it.

Suddenly, Feng Haoyu stepped forward, dropped to his knees, and said firmly, “Even if the Rising Phoenix Clan does not agree, I am personally willing to serve you.”

“What?!”

Both the Grand Elder and Feng Zhenyan exclaimed in shock.

“Are you doing this just to impress me?” Feng Yuxiang asked, narrowing her eyes at him suspiciously. “Unfortunately for you, my heart, body, and soul already belong to the Young Master.”

Feng Haoyu chuckled at her assumption and calmly shook his head. “I don’t mean to sound rude, but I have no interest in you. That idea was my mother’s doing from the start.”

“Then what’s your reason for wanting to serve me?” Yuan asked.

With a serious expression, Feng Haoyu responded without hesitation, “My instincts.”

“You have good instincts.” Yuan smiled. “Very well. If you wish to serve me, I won’t disagree.”

“Thank you, Young Master.”

“Then I will return in a year. Until then.” Yuan left with Feng Yuxiang shortly later.

Once Yuan had left, Feng Haoyu turned to the Grand Elder and said, “I’m sorry if my decision makes things more difficult for the clan, but I truly believe in this. The moment I first saw him, I could tell there was something extraordinary about him, and his abilities only confirmed it.”

The Grand Elder shook his head and said, “There’s no need to apologize. If I have to be honest, I also wanted to do the same, but I didn’t have the courage.”

After leaving the Rising Phoenix Clan’s territory, Yuan said, “They weren’t bad. I was expecting much worse.”

“You just haven’t been with them long enough to see their flaws. While they’re not bad people, they’re a hassle to deal with,” said Feng Yuxiang.

“Who isn’t a hassle nowadays? I bet you were also a handful before going to the lower heavens,” Yuan chuckled.

Feng Yuxiang could only lower her head in silence.

“Anyway, let’s head to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan now,” he said. “Hopefully, it won’t take longer than a year.”

“Fortunately, there is a teleportation device near their territory, so it won’t take long to arrive at their territory,” said Feng Yuxiang.

Once they arrived at the nearest city with a teleportation device, Yuan said to the operator, “Heavenly White Valley.”

Some time later, Yuan and Feng Yuxiang arrived at the Heavenly White Valley—a vast expanse blanketed in a thin veil of white mist, almost like they were standing on clouds.

The spiritual energy in this area was incredibly dense, much more than even the Rising Phoenix Clan’s territory.

“So, how are you going to approach them?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“Since their ancestor was one of the Nine Divine Supreme, there’s only one way to approach them,” he said.

“You’re really going as the Immortal Monarch? Won’t that cause a huge commotion once it’s revealed?” Feng Yuxiang asked, raising an eyebrow.

“Who said anything about revealing it to everyone?” Yuan replied calmly. “I’ll only show it to a select few. And even if word does get out, it was bound to happen sooner or later anyway.”

“I only hope the Heavenly White Tiger Clan still respects the Immortal Monarch. It has been countless years, after all.”

“Should I hide in your Dantian?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

Yuan pondered for a moment before shaking his head.

“No need.”

He smiled and continued, “We still have some business with Bai Xutao, after all.”

Although the Heavenly White Tiger Clan ruled over the entire Heavenly White Valley, their main residence lay deep within a massive valley nestled between two towering, snow-covered mountains.

The Heavenly White Valley teemed with magical beasts, many of which soared through the skies. So even while flying, they still had to contend with these creatures.

Most of the magical beasts were around the Immortal Ascension level, posing no real threat to Yuan, only a mild inconvenience. However, as they drew closer to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s residence, the number of magical beasts diminished significantly.

Suddenly, Yuan spotted a shadowy figure moving swiftly in the distance—its movements sharp and fluid, as if locked in combat. Because the white mist had grown so thick, he could only make out the vague outline of the figure. From what he could see, it appeared quite small—almost the size of a child.

After pondering for a moment, he decided to approach this figure.

chapters.title: Chapter 2071: Bai Sulan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2071: Bai Sulan

Chapter 2071: Bai Sulan

As Yuan drew closer, he realized there were actually two figures—one a small, human-like silhouette and the other resembling a massive bull.

Once he was close enough to see clearly, Yuan spotted a little girl with short black-and-white hair wielding a metal rod, locked in a fierce battle with a red bull radiating the aura of a peak Immortal Ascension beast. Yet, on closer inspection, it was clear the girl was merely toying with the bull, despite her cultivation being only at the fifth level of Immortal Ascension.

Her metal rod was also cloaked in weapon aura, and each swing sent visible ripples coursing through the air.

The little girl, who had been gleefully toying with the bull, suddenly paused when she sensed Yuan’s presence. Then, as if she’d discovered a new plaything, she turned her attention back to the bull and ended the fight with a single, decisive strike to its head, killing it instantly.

“Are you lost, handsome brother?” The little girl faced Yuan and asked, her face wearing a playful smile.

“Something like that. I’m looking for the Heavenly White Tiger Clan. Do you know the way?” Yuan asked.

“Of course. That’s my home, after all,” nodded the little girl. “But what business do you have there? Ah! Are you looking for a mate? Unless you’re really strong, you won’t be able to attract my Senior Sisters even if you’re handsome.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow at her unusual words, but then he recalled the Heavenly White Tiger’s lustful nature.

“Actually, I have some business with Bai Ning. Do you know her?”

The little girl’s eyes widened at the unexpected name, and she repeated in a surprised voice, “You’re looking for Senior Sister Ning?”

He nodded.

The little girl shook her head and sighed, “Don’t even bother, handsome brother. Senior Sister Ning is way out of your league. And even if you’re the most talented individual in the Nine Heavens, she only has eyes for someone you can never replace.”

“Oh? And who might that be?” Yuan asked, deciding to play along since he found it amusing.

“That would be the Immortal Monarch, of course!” the little girl declared. “Everyone knows Senior Sister Ning is obsessed with him. Even though he’s long gone, she still only has him in her eyes.”

“And why is she so fascinated with the Immortal Monarch? It’s not like they’ve ever met,” Yuan asked.

The little girl shrugged, “How would I know? I am not her.”

She continued, “Anyway, if you’re looking for Senior Sister Ning, you’re only wasting your time.”

“In that case, is Bai Xutao home?” Yuan then asked.

The little girl looked at him with a horrified expression and slowly backed away. “Handsome brother… You actually swing that way?”

Yuan sighed, “I’m not here to mate. I just have some unfinished business with them.”

“Oh… why didn’t you say so then? Senior Brother Xutao is currently at home, but you probably won’t be able to see him.”

“Why not?”

“Because he’s been in closed cultivation for the last several years. Something happened when he went to the lower heavens, and he’s been training like crazy since then.”

“That sounds terrible. What did he experience?”

“No clue. He refuses to tell us, so it’s most likely something embarrassing. According to Senior Sister Ning, he would’ve died if she hadn’t shown up in time.”

“Poor guy. He must’ve had a hard time,” said Yuan.

“Well, he’s a known troublemaker, so don’t feel too sorry for him. He probably deserved whatever happened to him.”

Yuan smiled and said, “I’m Tian Yang. What should I call you?”

The little girl smiled back and introduced herself, “I’m Bai Sulan.”

“Then, can you bring me to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan? I’ll be in your debt.”

Though they knew the Heavenly White Tiger Clan was located in the Heavenly White Valley, they didn’t know the exact location, and Yuan didn’t want to waste time wandering around.

Bai Sulan didn’t immediately respond and pondered in silence.

“I don’t mind, but you’ll have to wait until I am finished with my trial,” she said. “I cannot return home until I am finished, after all.”

It was common practice for powerful families to send their inexperienced members out on trials to help them gain real-world experience, and Bai Sulan was currently in the middle of this trial.

“How long will that take? I’m short on time,” Yuan said.

“It won’t be too long. I am almost finished. I just need to slay one more magical beast to finish the trial,” she said.

“Alright, I’ll follow along. Let me speak with my companion real quick.”

“Okay.”

Yuan returned to Feng Yuxiang, who was waiting at a distance, and explained the situation to her.

“I guess a small detour is fine. It’ll be much easier for us to approach the Heavenly White Tiger Clan if we have one of them with us, too,” she said.

Yuan returned to Bai Sulan shortly later with Feng Yuxiang.

“Your companion… is a phoenix?” Bai Sulan’s eyes widened in surprise as she recognized Feng Yuxiang’s identity from her unique scent alone.

Though she tried to maintain a brave front, her body betrayed her true feelings, trembling slightly with fear.

“Why are you looking at me like I’m about to eat you?” Feng Yuxiang asked, raising an eyebrow.

“I-It’s just… this is my first time meeting a phoenix…” Bai Sulan replied timidly.

“Don’t worry, she won’t do anything to you.”

Bai Sulan nodded with stiff movements.

“So, where are we going to go for your trial?” Yuan then asked.

“The Phantasm Serpent’s Nest,” she answered.

“Ah, I almost forgot…”

Bai Sulan suddenly turned to the corpse of the bull she had defeated moments ago and casually tossed its head into her spatial ring.

“I need evidence, or it won’t count,” she subconsciously explained her actions to Yuan.

A few moments later, Bai Sulan transformed into a massive white tiger and said, “Try not to fall behind.”

Then, she shot forward, her speed so great that it left a trail of afterimages in her wake.

Join Privilege for 50 Adv Chapters! Only Available in the App!

chapters.title: Chapter 2072: Bai Sulan(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2072: Bai Sulan(2)

Chapter 2072: Bai Sulan(2)

When Bai Sulan sped off, Yuan and Feng Yuxiang immediately chased after her.

“Hey, how old are you?” Yuan asked her as they traveled the misty mountain valley.

“I’m almost eight hundred this year,” she calmly responded.

Yuan was caught off guard by her answer and said, “Eight hundred years? You’re even older than me.”

“Really?” Bai Sulan looked at him with intrigue, almost as if she was seeing someone younger than her for the first time.

He nodded.

“But why do you have the appearance of a little kid?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“Because I am still a child. In our clan, we don’t become adults until we reach 2,000 years old,” Bai Sulan explained.

“Two thousand years, huh? That’s quite low compared to some of the other bloodlines out there,” Feng Yuxiang remarked.

Some bloodlines required tens of thousands—if not hundreds of thousands—of years of experience before one could even be considered an adult.

“Can you tell me more about this trial of yours?” Yuan then asked.

Bai Sulan nodded and explained, “In the Heavenly White Tiger Clan, we’re all required to go out and hunt specific magical beasts as part of our trials. We have to complete one trial every hundred years until we reach adulthood.”

“Each trial increases in difficulty, and I am currently on my eighth trial,” she added.

“What happens if you fail a trial?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“Then we’re deemed defects and executed for staining the bloodline,” Bai Sulan replied calmly, as if it were the most ordinary thing in the world.

“Executed for failing a trial…? That’s not right,” Yuan said while shaking his head.

Yet, Feng Yuxiang didn’t seem surprised in the slightest.

“The Heavenly White Tiger Clan has no use for the weak,” said Bai Sulan. “If we don’t want to be disposed of, we’ll need to succeed.”

Due to how fast the Heavenly White Tiger Clan increases its population, they believed it would be for the best to cull those unworthy of carrying their prestigious bloodline. In fact, many were disposed of even before their first trial for having an insufficient bloodline.

However, this harsh reality wasn’t unique to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan, and similar ruthless practices existed among many other powerful bloodlines as well. After all, this would save them a lot of time and resources, allowing them to invest in those truly worthy.

“Young Master, you grew up among humans, so you wouldn’t know, but this kind of practice is very common in the world of beasts,” Feng Yuxiang explained. “Especially among the stronger clans, as this is how they preserve resources and ensure they produce only the best of the best.”

“To many, our bloodline is everything, and having an inferior bloodline is akin to having no talents for humans.”

“I understand the reasoning behind it, but I don’t have to accept it or like it.”

Feng Yuxiang sighed, “Unfortunately, there’s not much you can do about it… Unless you repeat what the Immortal Monarch did during his era.”

“You mean bringing most of the beast clans under my control?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

She silently nodded.

“I’ll think about it,” he said, seriously contemplating it.

Feng Yuxiang smiled, but she didn’t say anything else.

A few days later, Bai Sulan came to a halt as they finally arrived at the Phantasm Serpent’s Nest—a large cave hidden beneath one of the countless mountains scattered throughout the Heavenly White Valley.

Bai Sulan returned to her human form and retrieved her metal rod.

“I’ll be right back. You can wait here for me. It shouldn’t take too long,” she said to them.

“Are you sure you want to go inside alone?” Yuan asked.

“Yes. Don’t worry about me.”

Bai Sulan calmly strolled into the cave a moment later, her small figure soon vanishing into the thick mist that drifted out from within.

“I have a bad feeling about this…” Yuan muttered.

“I’m sure she’ll be fine.” Feng Yuxiang said.

However, Yuan wanted to be certain, so he swept the entire cave with his divine sense. He immediately detected several large snakes with pearlescent scales scattered throughout the cavern, most of them between the seventh and ninth levels of Immortal Ascension.

’Since she could toy with a beast at the peak of Immortal Ascension, she should be fine…’ Yuan thought to himself and stopped thinking about it.

He sat down and closed his eyes, waiting patiently for Bai Sulan’s return.

Two days later, his eyes snapped open as the ground began to shake beneath him. Turning around, he saw the entire mountain quivering, and a moment later, a powerful pressure filled the air.

“This pressure belongs to a being at the True Immortal realm!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed.

BOOM!

Before they could even react, the entire mountain suddenly erupted, shattering outward as if a massive bomb had detonated within it.

Then, a massive serpent figure emerged within the swirling mist, its size nearly rivaling that of the mountain itself.

“Where the hell did that thing come from?” Yuan muttered in surprise. He had already scanned the entire mountain with his divine sense, and there was simply no way he could have overlooked such a massive and powerful entity.

However, he didn’t have time to dwell on it, as there was something far more important demanding his attention.

“Bai Sulan! Are you okay?!” He shouted, his voice echoing in every direction.

“…”

Silence. There was no response.

“Young Master! Over there!” Feng Yuxiang suddenly shouted, pointing toward the serpent’s head. The mist had parted just enough for them to see a bloodied white tiger trapped between its massive jaws.

Yuan clicked his tongue in annoyance, then immediately summoned Number One Under Heaven. Without a moment’s hesitation, he launched an attack on the serpent.

With his Silver Immortal cultivation, a mere True Immortal was no match for him. It took only a few seconds for Yuan to completely defeat the serpent.

After slicing the serpent’s body into countless pieces, Yuan retrieved Bai Sulan, who was unconscious but still alive.

chapters.title: Chapter 2073: Familiar Inscriptions
chapters.body:
Chapter 2073: Familiar Inscriptions

Chapter 2073: Familiar Inscriptions

After saving Bai Sulan, Yuan placed her on the ground and quickly fed her some medicine.

Then he realized that he didn’t have any on him since he mainly relied on his regenerative abilities and didn’t need them. Fortunately, Feng Yuxiang was there, and she had plenty on her.

“What happened to me…? Am I dead?” Bai Sulan mumbled as she slowly opened her eyes.

“You’re still alive,” Yuan responded.

Her eyes snapped open, and she quickly sat up.

“T-The Phantasm Serpent! What happened to it?!” she asked him.

“I took care of it. Are you going to complain and say that you could’ve defeated it yourself?”

Bai Sulan shook her head and said, “No, why would I say that? That thing was beyond my league.”

After a moment of silence, she looked at him and said, “Thank you, handsome little brother. You saved my life.”

“Little brother, huh?” Yuan smiled.

“Well, you are younger than I.”

A moment later, Yuan turned to look at where the mountain had once been, and he noticed a massive hole in the ground.

“So that’s where it came from…” he muttered.
