Bai Sulan nodded and explained, “I was hunting the Phantasm Serpents when the ground suddenly started shaking. Before I knew it, that thing came out of the ground with its mouth wide open. That’s the last thing I remember before waking up and seeing you.”

Yuan looked at the tunnel again and used divine sense to scan it.

After a brief moment of silence, he smiled and said, “I’ll be right back.”

Then he turned and walked toward the gaping hole in the ground. Without hesitation, he leapt in, leaving Bai Sulan staring after him in stunned disbelief.

“What’s he doing?” Bai Sulan asked Feng Yuxiang.

“I don’t know, but he’ll be fine.”

After jumping into the tunnel, Yuan followed the winding path, retracing the serpent’s movements step by step.

Eventually, he reached the end of the tunnel, which opened into a vast underground cavern that appeared to be the serpent’s lair.

The air was thick with a pungent scent, and piles of bones from all kinds of magical beasts were scattered throughout the cavern.

However, what truly caught Yuan’s attention was the intricate carving on the wall at the far end of the cavern. Upon closer inspection, he realized it wasn’t just a wall—it was actually a massive sealed gate, and there was a powerful formation protecting it.

Yuan approached the sealed gate and spoke out loud, “Yu Ning, can you take a look at the formation?”

She responded a few moments later, “There are several defensive formations protecting this gate. All of them are between level eight and nine. Whatever is behind it… it must be extremely valuable, perhaps even priceless.”

“What are the chances of you cracking the formations?” Yuan then asked.

“None. This is way beyond me, even during my peak days,” she sighed.

“So I will have to force it open, huh?”

Without hesitation, Yuan drew Number One Under Heaven and struck the gate with all his strength, making the entire cavern quake from the force. Hell, even Feng Yuxiang and Bai Sulan felt the tremor even though they were thousands of miles away.

“T-That was extremely reckless, Young Master!” Yu Ning exclaimed afterward. “What if the defensive formations had retaliated?!”

Fortunately, there was no response from the formations, but the gates remained sealed.

Yuan smiled and said, “I struck it because I couldn’t feel anything from it—not even a hint of danger.”

“Still, you should warn me before you do something like that next time. It’s bad for the heart—soul,” she sighed.

“Alright.”

Yuan turned his focus back to the gate, studying its intricate inscriptions.

“These markings… they look familiar,” he muttered to himself.

Then, his eyes widened in realization, and he exclaimed, “That’s it! Han Zexian’s Cavern!”

Though it wasn’t the exact same, the inscriptions around this gate resembled the ones found in Han Zexian’s Cavern—the method to contact an Outer God.

’Whatever is behind this gate is likely connected to the Outer Gods… I need to get inside—but if brute force won’t work…’

Yuan narrowed his eyes. For now, the only option was to hone his skills as an Array Master and unlock it properly.

Suddenly, the entire cavern began to shake violently as an ominous presence flooded the space, thick and oppressive.

Yuan immediately put distance between himself and the sealed gate, which was now leaking an ominous aura.

BANG! BANG! BANG!

The gate shuddered under three thunderous strikes from the other side, each impact shaking the entire cavern even more violently.

BANG! BANG! BANG!

Whatever was pounding on the gate didn’t stop, as if it were desperately trying to break free—or to announce its presence to the world outside.

“How creepy…” Yuan muttered, shaking his head as he lowered his guard when it was clear whatever was inside wouldn’t be breaking out anytime soon.

He stopped paying attention to the gate and began looking around the cavern with more scrutiny.

Once he was unable to find anything of interest, he decided to leave.

However, just as he turned to leave, Yuan found himself unable to move, almost as if something unseen was holding him in place.

Then, as if sensing Yuan’s attempt to leave, the entity behind the gate began pounding even harder, each impact echoing with greater force. It was to the point that Yuan worried the entire cavern might collapse.

Yuan sighed before shouting at it, “What the hell do you want me to do?! Unless you have a method to help me open the seal, stop causing such a ruckus!”

Surprisingly, the banging stopped.

’It understood my words?’ Yuan swallowed nervously.

“Knock once if you understand me,” Yuan spoke to it again.

Bang!

“Knock twice if you’re a human.”

“…”

“Knock once if you’re an Outer God.”

BANG!

Yuan’s eyes widened with surprise at this response.

“There’s an Outer God behind this door…? What in heaven’s name is this place?” he mumbled in a dazed voice.

chapters.title: Chapter 2074: Sealed Outer God
chapters.body:
Chapter 2074: Sealed Outer God

Chapter 2074: Sealed Outer God

After a brief silence, Yuan spoke again, his voice calm yet tinged with amusement. “Well, Outer God or not, there’s nothing I can do about this situation. And if you really are an Outer God, you wouldn’t be trapped behind this door by a few formations. Aren’t you lot supposed to be all-powerful?”

BANG!

In response, the Outer God slammed against the gate, and in the very next instant, every formation protecting it shattered like fragile glass.

Yuan was jumpscared and immediately distanced himself from the gate.

However, nothing happened even after many moments, almost as if the formations had no effect on the gates in the first place.

“It appears that the formations weren’t holding it back in the first place, but something else,” Yu Ning muttered.

“The hell? Then what was the point of that?” Yuan remarked.

“It was probably made as an assurance, but who really knows…”

“If it’s not the formations keeping it inside, then what is…?” Yuan mumbled as he stared at the sealed gate.

Suddenly, Yuan noticed a trail of red smoke seeping through the narrow gap between the gates. His body instinctively shifted into a battle-ready stance, preparing for whatever was about to emerge.

The smoke began to twist and swirl, slowly forming words until an entire wall of text hovered in the air before Yuan. However, the words weren’t written in any human language and they closely resembled the strange inscriptions carved into the gate itself.

Since Tian Yang had deciphered the inscriptions in Han Zexian’s Cavern, he could somewhat understand the words before him.

“This is… a technique,” Yuan muttered with raised eyebrows.

“A technique?” Yu Ning repeated.

“I’m not certain, but I think so.”

Yuan sat down and closed his eyes after memorizing the entire.

A few hours later, he opened his eyes and spoke, “It’s definitely a technique, but I don’t understand their language enough to decipher it right now. It will take some time.”

Yuan stood back up and spoke out loud, “Why can’t you just use my language? You clearly understand me, so why can’t you use it?”

“…”

After a brief moment of silence, the red smoke began to shift once more.

Then, two massive words formed before him.

“OPEN SEAL”

This time, Yuan could read the words.

“Are you telling me that the technique just now would open the seal to this gate?”

Bang!

The Outer God acknowledged with a loud bang.

“So the gate can only be opened from the outside, huh? Anyway, why should I help you escape? You’re an Outer God, and as far as I am aware, you’re nothing but bad news. It would benefit me and the entire world if you remained in there for an eternity.”

The red smoke changed again, this time forming into a single word.

“FATE”

“…”

Yuan silently frowned at this word. Despite its vagueness, Yuan knew what the Outer God was trying to say—that they were connected in some way and that fate would lead him to opening the gate eventually.

Although Yuan did not like the idea of unsealing this unknown Outer God, he knew that not all Outer Gods were evil, as he’d recieved help from Shura.

’If it’s true that we’re connected by fate, I will definitely remove the seal and release this Outer God eventually…’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

’Who knows? Maybe I have another Outer God as an ally and just don’t remember. Whatever. I’ll worry about it once I actually decypher the technique.’

“Can you not tell me the technique using my language?”

“…”

No response.

’If he cannot translate the technique, then it’s likely because the technique would not make sense in our language. A technique used by Outer Gods, huh? How intriguing…’

Then, he spoke a moment later, “In that case, I can only return here later once I decipher the technique and learn it, so wait a little longer.”

Bang!

The Outer God acknowledged.

“Then I’ll leave now.”

Yuan turned around and left. This time, the Outer God did not try to stop him.

Once Yuan left the cavern, Yu Ning asked him, “Are you really going to release whatever is inside? I mean, it was probably sealed for a reason, right?”

“I doubt it was humans who sealed it. If it’s truly an Outer God, then it was likely sealed by another Outer God. I’m also not sure what I should do. I guess I’ll figure it out as I try to decypher the technique.”

“By the way, what’s an Outer God?” Yu Ning suddenly asked.

Though she’d listened to his conversation with it just now, she didn’t understand its existence.

Yuan shook his head and said, “I would tell you, but it might destroy your soul.”

“So they’re an existence beyond my understanding, huh?” Yu Ning remarked.

“You’re quite perceptive.” Yuan smiled.

“Probably because I’ve already experience this before.”

“You what?” Yuan suddenly stopped moving.

Yu Ning explained, “During the Primordial Era, shortly after cultivators gained the ability to traverse the Starry Sky, some claimed to have seen things out there—entities so incomprehensible that those without sufficient soul strength would perish just from hearing about it. Some even began to revere them as gods. I believe they were also called True Rulers of the Universe.”

“True Rulers of the Universe…” Yuan mumbled.

“What else do you know about them?”

“Not much. Since no one could prove their existence—and those who claimed to witness them couldn’t truly share what they’d seen—they eventually faded into myth. Some even consider it nothing more than a hoax,” she said.

“Then, once the war with the Celestial Emperor begun, it became irrelevant to most people.”

“I see… Well, in that case, there’s a good chance these True Rulers of the Universe are Outer Gods,” said Yuan.

“They’re basically beings that exist exclusively within the Starry Sky, so I can’t fathom why one would be sealed here in the Nine Heavens—or what it must have done to be sealed away. They’re also unimaginably powerful. Even a Cultivation God would be like an ant in their eyes.”

“A Cultivation God… akin to an ant…?” Yu Ning whispered, swallowing nervously at his words.

chapters.title: Chapter 2075: Heavenly White Tiger Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2075: Heavenly White Tiger Clan

Chapter 2075: Heavenly White Tiger Clan

After nearly an entire day, Yuan returned to Bai Sulan and Feng Yuxiang’s side.

“Where did you go?” Bai Sulan asked, her eyes filled with curiosity.

“To the serpent’s lair.”

Bai Sulan continued asking, “Then did you find any treasures?”

In her eyes, there was only one reason why Yuan went out of his way to go there, and that reason was to find treasures.

“No, there was no treasure.”

“Then why are you covering up the tunnel?” Bai Sulan raised her eyebrows in a doubtful manner, as Yuan was currently closing up the hole to the tunnel, clearly trying to hide something.

“What’s wrong with fixing a massive hole in the ground? I don’t want anyone accidentally falling into it.”

Bai Sulan’s jaw dropped at his terrible excuse and said a moment later, “You do realize that we’re deep into the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s territory, right? Only magical beasts roam this area, and even they’re not stupid enough to fall into such a massive hole.”

Yuan smiled and said, “If you don’t believe me, you’re free to check it out yourself. There’s really no treasure there.”

“…”

Bai Sulan looked at the hole with intrigue, but she ultimately shook her head and said, “No, it’s fine. I don’t really care.”

Yuan then spoke, “Anyway, what about your trial?”

Bai Sulan retrieved a Phantasm Serpent corpse from her spatial ring and said, “Fortunately, I defeated one before that massive serpent ambushed me, so I didn’t fail my trial.”

“So you’re ready to return to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan?” Yuan asked.

She nodded, “Yes, I can return now.”

Once Yuan completely filled the hole, he followed Bai Sulan to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan, which took nearly a week of journey.

“We will arrive at my home soon,” said Bai Sulan, who was traveling in her beast form.

A few hours later, Yuan and Feng Yuxiang spotted a cluster of white buildings appearing in the distance. As they drew closer, Yuan could sense numerous powerful presences emanating from the area.

“We’re here!” Bai Sulan declared.

When they reached the massive gates, the guard there approached Bai Sulan and asked, “Who are these two with you?”

“They’re my friends and savior,” she said. “They are also here for Senior Brother Xutao and Senior Sister Ning.”

“Your savior…?” The guard looked at her with raised eyebrows.

“Yes, they saved me from an accident,” she said, purposefully not giving too many details as she didn’t want to appear weak.

“Alright.”

The guard gave her a quick nod before opening the gates for them.

“Let’s go, handsome little brother.” Bai Sulan walked over to Yuan and suddenly held his hand, pulling him toward the entrance.

When she noticed Feng Yuxiang staring at her with a sharp gaze, she chuckled and explained, “I am doing this so the others won’t bother him. This may sound weird, but handsome little brother here has an attractive scent to him. I fear he might become overwhelmed by the females in our clan if they see him.”

“An attractive scent?” Feng Yuxiang tilted her head closer to Yuan and leaned in, giving him a deep, deliberate sniff.

“He does smell very good, but I don’t get it—not to say my Young Master isn’t attractive. Or maybe I am just used to it by now,” she remarked.

“It’s very subtle, but we Heavenly White Tigers have a strong sense of smell,” said Bai Sulan.

Once they were inside, Bai Sulan said, “I’ll bring you to our guest room for now. As for meeting with Senior Sister Ning or Senior Brother Xutao, you’ll have to speak with their servants.”

“Sure.”

As Bai Sulan led them to the guest room, Yuan and Feng Yuxiang could sense numerous presences gathering around them, though none revealed themselves directly.

“I knew you’d attract the others, but I didn’t expect this many to show up this quickly,” Bai Sulan remarked when she also noticed their presence.

“Should I be worried?” Yuan asked calmly.

“No… at least not yet,” Bai Sulan replied. “We’re still in the outer area, where most of the lower-ranking members live. As long as I’m with you, they won’t dare come too close.”

“Lower-ranking?” Yuan asked.

“It’s similar to how sects work,” Bai Sulan explained. “We’re divided by the quality of our bloodline.”

“I’m from the main family, so my status is automatically at the top. Also, while everyone is required to complete trials, those of us in the main family face far harsher requirements, and for us, failure simply isn’t an option, as we are killed for it.”

“Meanwhile, the others train as soldiers or servants. If their bloodline meets the standards, they might earn a chance to move up in rank. Oh—and it’s strictly forbidden for those with high-quality bloodlines to breed with lower-quality ones, so only those of the same rank are allowed to mate.”

To preserve and strengthen their bloodline, it was common for beasts to engage in selective breeding practices like this. Of course, humans weren’t much better, and they simply used talent as their measure instead. That’s why geniuses often look down on those with inferior talent and arranged marriages between two powerful families are incredibly common.

“What’s the population like in your clan?” Feng Yuxiang suddenly asked.

Bai Sulan pondered for a moment before responding, “Around 3,000,000, I guess.”

“That’s quite the population…” Yuan mumbled with a surprised look on his face.

“That’s because we Heavenly White Tigers often give birth to more than one child at a time. In fact, anything beyond four children is considered abnormal,” said Bai Sulan.

“So not only are you guys perverted, but you also have ridiculous reproductive capabilities?” Feng Yuxiang remarked.

“P-Perverted…?” Bai Sulan could only shake her head in silence at Feng Yuxiang’s remark since she wasn’t entirely wrong.

Eventually, they reached the next area, where the middle-ranking members lived.

“I only have enough authority to bring you this far into our territory. Wait here while I contact Senior Sister Ning and Senior Brother Xutao’s servants for you,” Bai Sulan said once they arrived at the guest house.

chapters.title: Chapter 2076: Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2076: Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)

Chapter 2076: Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)

Once Yuan and Feng Yuxiang entered the guest house, Bai Sulan retrieved a silver medallion and placed it on the doorstep before leaving to find the servants.

Shortly after Bai Sulan left, the presences that had been shadowing them from a distance finally revealed themselves and began approaching the guest house. However, when they saw Bai Sulan’s medallion resting on the doorstep like a ward for ghosts, these Heavenly White Tigers hesitated to approach the door.

Just as they were preparing to leave, the door swung open and Yuan stepped out to meet the tigers head-on.

At a glance, there were around two hundred of them, all sharing similar traits—white and black hair that marked their lineage. As for the gender ratio, about 90 percent of them were females.

“How can I help you guys?” Yuan asked.

The female tigers exchanged glances with each other before one of them spoke, “Um…Sorry, we didn’t know you were Lady Sulan’s partner.”

Yuan raised his eyebrows at this remark and said, “What are you talking about?”

The tiger pointed at the medallion by his feet and explained, “That is Lady Sulan’s medallion. It’s a declaration that you belong to her…”

Yuan looked at the medallion and picked it up.

“What were you going to do if I weren’t her partner?” Yuan then asked.

The female tigers exchanged glances again.

“We wanted to see if you wanted to have some fun…” one of them eventually responded.

Yuan cleared his throat and said, “Ah, then I’ll respectfully decline.”

The female tigers lowered their heads in a dejected manner. As for the men, one of them said, “We’re here for the lady with you.”

“Get lost!” Feng Yuxiang’s irritated voice rang out from inside the house.

The tigers immediately scattered, scrambling away in a panic.

Yuan then placed Bai Sulan’s medallion back down on the doorstep and returned inside the house.

A few hours later, three figures approached the house.

“Handsome little brother, I am back.” Bai Sulan spoke as she knocked on the door.

Yuan opened the door.

“Welcome back.”

Bai Sulan and the two servants entered the house shortly after.

“They are Senior Brother Xutao and Senior Sister Ning’s servants,” Bai Sulan gestured toward the two female tigers.

The one on the left, who had short hair, bowed and said, “I am Young Master Xutao’s servant.”

The one on the right, whose long hair reached all the way to her lower back, followed suit. “I am Lady Ning’s servant.”

“I’d like to meet with your masters,” said Yuan, going straight to the point.

The short-haired servant responded first, “I apologize, but Young Master Xutao is currently in closed cultivation and won’t be able to meet you.”

The long-haired servant then spoke, “Lady Ning is also in closed cultivation right now.”

“Any idea when they’ll come out?” Yuan asked.

“No.” They both shook their heads.

Yuan pondered for a moment before retrieving a jade slip from his spatial ring. He offered it to the long-haired servant and said, “I’d like to return this to your master.”

“This…?” The servant raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

“This is—!”

When she glimpsed inside the jade slip and saw what was inside, her eyes widened with shock and disbelief. She was so shocked that she almost dropped the jade slip.

“What’s that?” Bai Sulan asked, her eyes flickering with curiosity.

“T-This is Lady Ning’s personal logbook…”

“A logbook? On what?”

“The Immortal Monarch…”

“WHAT?!” Bai Sulan exclaimed, her face filled with bewilderment.

Everyone in the Heavenly White Tiger Clan knew of Bai Ning’s obsession with the Immortal Monarch. It had reached the point where even the Clan Leader had given up trying to convince her to find a suitable partner.

“Senior Sister Ning actually handed you something so precious? She wouldn’t even let father touch it, let alone borrow it… Unless… you actually stole it from her?” Bai Sulan looked at Yuan with worry.

Yuan chuckled, “Of course not. She allowed me to borrow it. If you don’t believe me, you’ll know once you return it to her.”

The long-haired servant nodded and said, “I’ll return it to her immediately. Is there anything else you need me for?”

“No, you can go.”

“I understand.”

The long-haired servant left shortly after.

“What about me?” asked the short-haired servant.

Yuan paused for a moment before asking, “I’d like to learn more about your master, Bai Xutao. Is that possible?”

The servant hesitated—sharing such information could easily be seen as betrayal, a crime punishable by death. Yet Yuan was clearly someone important to Bai Ning, and refusing him outright didn’t seem wise.

Noticing this, Bai Sulan spoke, “She might get in trouble with Bai Xutao if she reveals information about him. I don’t know as much as his personal servant, but I’ll tell you everything I know about him.”

Yuan nodded, “Sure.”

While Bai Sulan revealed everything she knew about Bai Xutao to Yuan, the long-haired servant headed straight to Bai Ning.

Somewhere deep within the Heavenly White Valley, Bai Ning was honing her techniques—each strike demolishing entire mountains with ease. Ever since her humiliating defeat to Yuan, she had been training in this place without rest.

“Lady Ning, I apologize for interrupting your training, but this is an emergency.”

The servant dropped to her knees the moment she appeared before her.

“I don’t care,” Bai Ning immediately dismissed her.

“…”

After a moment of silence, the servant retrieved the jade slip and placed it on the ground.

“Then I’ll leave this here for when you have time.”

Bai Ning subconsciously glanced at the jade slip, her eyes narrowing slightly as she caught a familiar scent lingering on it.

The moment she recognized the object, her eyes widened with shock.

Just as the servant stood and turned to leave, Bai Ning suddenly roared, “STOP RIGHT THERE!”

The servant was so startled by her voice that she tripped over her own feet and fell face-first to the ground.

As the servant scrambled back to her feet and turned around, she saw Bai Ning charging toward her with a terrifying expression, scaring the living soul out of her.

chapters.title: Chapter 2077: Heavenly White Tiger Clan(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2077: Heavenly White Tiger Clan(3)

Chapter 2077: Heavenly White Tiger Clan(3)

“L-Lady Ning! I am sorry! I swear I will never disturb you during your training again! Please, spare my humble life!” The servant immediately went on her knees and began begging for her life, thinking that Bai Ning was going to kill her, judging by her angry expression.

However, Bai Ning stopped in front of the jade slip and picked it up. Once she confirmed the contents inside, she looked at the servant and spoke, “Why would I kill you? Stand up.”

“Yes!”

The servant immediately stood back up, but her head remained lowered.

“Tell me, where did you acquire this?” Bai Ning asked while holding up the jade slip.

“F-From a guest brought by Young Lady Sulan…” she quickly replied.

Bai Ning immediately recalled her interaction with Yuan at the Pavilion of Knowledge. Hell, she still had the Immortal Monarch’s blood that was given to her by him.

“Is he still there?!” Bai Ning asked.

“Umm… I think so?”

“Bring me to him right now!” Bai Ning commanded urgently, as if it were a life-or-death matter.

“Right away!”

The servant said as she began leading Bai Ning to where she had met Yuan.

Meanwhile, back at the guest house, Bai Sulan had finished telling Yuan everything she knew about Bai Xutao, though most of what she shared painted him as a perpetual troublemaker.

Yuan had hoped to hear something redeeming about Bai Xutao, something that might give him a reason to show leniency, but instead, it only made him dislike Bai Xutao even more.

According to Bai Sulan, Bai Xutao was an infamous predator—and not the kind found in nature. He was a vile scumbag known for harassing females, often relying on force and his privileged background to bend them into submission.

He was so vile that he even terrorized his own people within the Heavenly White Tiger Clan. The only reason he hadn’t faced serious punishment was because he carried a trace amount of their ancestor’s bloodline and was considered the hope of the clan.

While that trace of bloodline was far too weak to have any real effect, the Heavenly White Tiger Clan still hoped to strengthen it through future generations.

“Why am I not shocked that he’s a true scumbag?” Feng Yuxiang shook her head with a look of disdain on her face.

Yuan looked at his servant and spoke, “You must have it hard being his servant.”

The servant trembled at Yuan’s words, and tears began flowing from her eyes uncontrollably.

“Huh?” Yuan did not expect her to cry and didn’t know how to respond.

“Senior Brother Xutao often changes his servants because he often breaks them,” Bai Sulan explained. “Being his servant is basically a death sentence. That said, she holds the current record for staying alive the longest.”

“Young Master, you should do everyone a favor and just kill that son of a bitch,” Feng Yuxiang suddenly cursed out loud.

“Eh?” Bai Sulan stared at them with wide eyes, having assumed they were on friendly terms with Bai Xutao.

But before she could ask any questions, someone suddenly pounded on the door—so hard that cracks splintered across its surface.

Bai Sulan immediately went to open the door, and to her surprise, Bai Ning was standing at the doorstep with a serious expression on her face.

“S-Senior Sister Ning? What are you—”

“Where is he?!” Bai Ning shouted as she stepped inside the house.

“I am right here,” Yuan calmly responded as he approached her.

Bai Ning’s eyes widened at Yuan’s handsome appearance.

“It’s been a while. We last spoke at the Pavilion of Knowledge. Do you remember?” Yuan asked.

“Of course, I remember! You think I could forget an event like that after what you gave me?!” she exclaimed.

“Since you’re here to find me, does this mean you’re finally going to let me meet the Immortal Monarch?” she asked, her body trembling uncontrollably with excitement at just the thought of it.

“I don’t mind letting you meet him, but you’ll have to do something for me first,” he said.

“Anything!” she replied without any hesitation.

“Bai Xutao. I mentioned that I wanted to speak with him, right? Bring him to me and I’ll let you meet the Immortal Monarch.”

“Bai Xuatao…?” Bai Ning quickly recalled having this conversation with him before.

“Alright. I’ll bring him to you,” she immediately agreed.

Before leaving, she turned to glance at Feng Yuxiang, who stood boldly at Yuan’s side.

’Why does she look so familiar? Where have I seen her before?’

It wasn’t just Feng Yuxiang’s appearance that stirred her memory—her scent, too, was strikingly familiar. Yet Bai Ning couldn’t quite put her finger on it. Their past encounter had been brief, and right now, her mind was consumed by something far more important.

Thus, after just a quick glance, Bai Ning stopped paying attention to Feng Yuxiang and left the house.

“Stay here until I return,” Bai Ning commanded her servant before leaving.

Once Bai Ning left to fetch Bai Xutao, Feng Yuxiang mumbled, “She didn’t recognize me…”

Yuan looked at her and said, “Well, you’ve changed a lot since then.”

Yuan returned to his seat shortly after to wait for Bai Ning’s return.

Meanwhile, after leaving, Bai Ning traveled straight to Bai Xutao’s training area, which existed underground beneath a mountain.

Upon arriving before a specific mountain, Bai Ning approached the sealed entrance at the bottom and said to the guard standing before it, “Tell Bai Xutao to get his ass out here.”

The guard was baffled by Bai Ning’s sudden appearance and demand.

“Lady Ning… The Young Lord has ordered that no one is to disturb him unless it’s the Clan Leader. If I disobey, I’ll definitely face a gruesome death,” the guard said nervously.

Bai Ning fell silent for a moment.

“I understand,” she said softly.

But just as the guard let out a sigh of relief, Bai Ning suddenly shot forward, slammed her foot into the sealed entrance, and kicked the door down with a deafening bang.

chapters.title: Chapter 2078: Meeting Bai Xutao Again
chapters.body:
Chapter 2078: Meeting Bai Xutao Again

Chapter 2078: Meeting Bai Xutao Again

“L-Lady Ning! What are you doing?!” The guard exclaimed with a shocked expression on his face after she kicked down the door to Bai Xutao’s training area.

“You didn’t disobey Xutao’s order since I will be forcing my way to him,” she said to him.

“That’s not the issue here…” the guard wore a look of disbelief.

Bai Ning narrowed her eyes at him and spoke in a threatening voice, “What? Should I tear one of your limbs to make it more convincing?”

The guard trembled in fear, and he immediately took several steps back.

“That won’t be necessary, Lady Ning!”

Then, without hesitation, the guard punched himself in the face with so much force that he knocked himself out cold.

“…”

Bai Ning stared at the guard’s unconscious body for a brief moment before turning around and walking into the cave.

Inside was a long descending staircase that continued for several minutes.

Eventually, Bai Ning arrived before another closed door.

Bai Ning glanced at the metal door for a moment before lifting her slender leg and also kicking it down with a loud bang.

Bai Ning stepped through the shattered entrance into a massive training hall. The space was filled with a wide array of amenities—racks of weapons lined the walls, several cauldrons brimmed with medicinal pills, and further in, a luxurious bathtub sat quietly.

In the middle of this training hall was none other than Bai Xutao, who appeared to be in a vicious battle against a ghost as he constantly swung at the empty air before him.

Bai Ning approached him, but before she could even call out to him, Bai Xutao suddenly lunged in her direction with immense killing intent.

Judging by Bai Xutao’s blank expression, he was likely too absorbed in his training and instinctively attacked Bai Ning.

“You dare bare your claws at me? You must have forgotten how to behave in just a few short years!” Bai Ning sneered without an ounce of concern within her.

Then, she raised her hand and gave a powerful slap to Bai Xutao in the face once he was close enough.

Pap!

Bai Xutao was sent flying instantly, his body slamming into the metal wall moments later.

“Which fucking bitch dares to disturb my training?!” Bai Xutao cursed out loud the moment he snapped out of his daze.

However, when his eyes snapped to Bai Ning, they immediately widened with horror.

“S-Senior Sister Ning? What are you doing here…?” He asked her in a timid voice, even appearing as harmless as possible before her presence.

“Come with me,” she said, not even bothering to offer an explanation.

“What is this about? I don’t have that much time to spare. Once I finish my training, I am immediately going to return to the lower heaven to find that bastard who humiliated me,” he said.

“Are you questioning me?” Bai Ning narrowed her eyes at him.

“I-I am just asking for clarification… I have received Father’s approval, too.”

“You can get your revenge later. This matter is much more important, and it probably won’t take long. If you dare dillydally any longer, I’ll forcefully drag you out of here.”

“Ok, ok! I will come with you! Just give me a minute to clean up…”

Sometime later, Bai Xutao followed Bai Ning outside. The first thing he noticed was the guard who was still lying unconscious by the entrance.

“What a useless bastard…” he mumbled under his breath.

As they traveled to Yuan, Bai Ning said, “Listen here, you troublemaker. We’re about to meet someone incredibly important. I don’t know why he wants to meet you, but you will treat him like you treat our father. If I dare sense any disrespect from you, I’ll make you beg me to kill you.”

Bai Xutao wore a look of disbelief on his face as he realized the reason for Bai Ning’s appearance.

“I have never seen you treat someone with such importance. Who the hell is this guy, and what does he want with me?” Bai Xutao frowned.

Bai Ning suddenly stopped moving and turned around to slap Bai Xutao in the face.

“That will be my first and last warning to you. Don’t you dare use that disrespectful tone before him. Also, I already said I don’t know why he wants to meet you, but from the sound of it, you seem to know each other.”

Bai Xutao rubbed his face with a defeated face, looking like he had been wronged.

’Who the hell is this bastard? I don’t recall knowing anyone so important to her… It’s not like we’re meeting the Immortal Monarch, so why is she acting like this?’ Bai Xutao sighed inwardly, no longer daring to speak out loud.

Eventually, they arrived at their destination.

“I have returned with Bai Xutao.” Bai Ning knocked on the door this time to announce their presence.

Bai Xutao swallowed nervously as they waited for the door to open.

The door opened a few moments later, and it was Bai Xutao’s servant who greeted them.

“The esteemed guest is waiting inside…”

Bai Ning entered the house without a hint of hesitation, her steps firm and confident.

Meanwhile, Bai Xutao stood motionless at the threshold, a strange expression twisting his face, as though he’d forgotten how to move.

’What is this feeling…?’ he wondered, staring at the open doorway.

His instincts screamed at him—warning him not to step inside.

“Bai Xutao! What are you doing? Hurry up and come inside!” Bai Ning’s voice snapped him out of his daze, and before he realized, Bai Xutao had entered the house.

The sense of dread became undeniable and was almost suffocating, but Bai Xutao ignored it and subconsciously followed Bai Ning’s presence.

Once he entered the living room with Bai Ning, Bai Xutao could see three other individuals there. He instantly recognized Bai Sulan, but he didn’t instantly recognize Feng Yuxiang or Yuan.

’Who the hell is this guy? I definitely don’t know him!’ Bai Xutao exclaimed inwardly after seeing Yuan’s handsome face.

chapters.title: Chapter 2079: Meeting Bai Xutao Again(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2079: Meeting Bai Xutao Again(2)

Chapter 2079: Meeting Bai Xutao Again(2)

When Bai Xutao saw Yuan, his eyebrows immediately raised in a puzzled manner. Bai Ning made it sound like they knew each other, but he was certain that he’d never seen Yuan before in his life.

His gaze slowly shifted to Feng Yuxiang, whose beauty caught his attention. However, he didn’t immediately recognize her, as Feng Yuxiang’s aura and scent had changed drastically since she awakened her bloodline and got rid of the curse.

Noticing Bai Xutao’s lustful gaze on her body, Feng Yuxiang had the urge to strangle him to death, but controlled herself.

“What are you doing? Greet the esteemed guest,” Bai Ning suddenly spoke.

“Uh… I greet… um… who are you?” Bai Xutao couldn’t help himself and asked.

Bai Ning snapped her head to glare at him with a threatening gaze, even clenching her fists in anger. After all, she’d given Yuan’s name to him before they arrived.

However, Yuan spoke before she could act, “It’s only been a few years and you’ve already forgotten about me, old friend?”

“A few years?” Bai Xutao became even more confused, as he’d spent the last few years training inside the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s territory and hadn’t met any new individuals.

Yuan calmly nodded before leaving his seat to approach Bai Xutao.

Then, using voice transmission, Yuan spoke to Bai Xutao, “Have you already forgotten about our time together in the Third Heaven? How you were utterly humiliated by me?”

Bai Xutao’s face twisted in horror, his eyes wide with shock and disbelief. Without a moment’s hesitation, he instinctively unleashed his cultivation and launched an attack at Yuan.

“Y-You?!” Bai Ning gasped, her voice laced with fear. She was so stunned by his action that her body refused to move.

Yuan calmly raised his hand, intercepting Bai Xutao’s strike with a single motion. His fingers closed around the fist, halting the attack effortlessly.

“W-What?!” Bai Xutao’s eyes widened in disbelief.

He tried to pull away, but no matter how much strength he used, his hand wouldn’t budge from Yuan’s grasp. It was like being caught in an unbreakable vice.

“What is the meaning of this, Bai Xutao, you little bastard?!”

Bai Ning finally snapped out of her daze. In a flash, she appeared beside Bai Xutao, seizing him by the neck and yanking him away. Yuan released his grip without resistance, allowing her to drag him away.

“If you don’t have a good explanation, I’ll snap your neck where I stand!” she roared.

“W-Wait! Why are you helping him?! He’s the one who attacked me in the lower heavens!” Bai Xutao exclaimed.

“Bullshit!” Bai Ning didn’t believe him even for a second. “That masked individual had the scent of a dragon! Tian Yang here is clearly a human! Also, he was only a Spirit King a few years ago!”

Bai Xutao’s gaze suddenly landed on Feng Yuxiang, and his eyes widened with realization. He then pointed at her and shouted, “T-That’s the phoenix who offended me in the lower heavens! I’m sure you met her, too!”

Bai Ning turned to look at Feng Yuxiang and frowned. However, she couldn’t believe it.

“Nonsense! That phoenix was nowhere near the realm of True Immortal! Her scent is also different!”

“Young Master, may I?” Feng Yuxiang suddenly stepped forward and asked.

Yuan calmly nodded, “Go ahead. He’s all yours.”

“What?” Bai Ning raised her eyebrows in confusion. However, before she could even ask, Feng Yuxiang shot forward like a bolt of lightning, seizing Bai Xutao by the face. With a single, fluid motion, she tore him from Bai Ning’s grasp and slammed his body into the wall with bone-rattling force.

Bai Xutao coughed up a mouthful of blood, though his injuries weren’t anything serious.

However, Feng Yuxiang didn’t stop there. She yanked him away from the wall, only to slam his face into it again—relentlessly, over and over—until the entire wall crumbled beneath the repeated blows.

“So you enjoy bullying the weak and preying on women, huh?!” Feng Yuxiang snarled. “Today, I’ll return the favor—for me and for Xi Meili—and turn you into a roasted tiger!”

With a burst of fiery laughter, she hurled Bai Xutao’s body into the next wall, shaking the entire house.

“Senior Tian Yang…” Bai Ning turned to look at Yuan for answers.

“What’s wrong?” Yuan asked with a nonchalant expression on his face.

“I understand that you’re upset he attacked you, but can you tell her to calm down? I’ll make sure he recieves the proper punishment,” she said. “It won’t be a good look on our clan if we let outsiders harm our members… whether they deserve it or not.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan sighed, “That is what’s wrong with the current Heavenly White Tiger Clan. You guys have fallen too much.”

“What are you…?”

“Bai Xutao has harmed so many people, causing irreparable damage to your clan’s image and reputation. Yet, you continue to protect him like he’s some priceless treasure. Why? Because he has a miniscule amount of your ancestor’s blood in him? How pathetic.”

Yuan stared at Bai Ning’s dumbfounded face with a calm expression as he continued, “I’m sure you know what happened to your ancestor because of his unruly behavior. If your ancestor can be punished, then what about Bai Xutao? Are you trying to say that he’s even more important than your ancestor?”

“I…” Bai Ning gritted her teeth and clenched her fists in frustration.

“Even if I want to punish him, I do not have that authority. Only our Clan Head can make such a decision.”

One of the reasons Bai Ning treated Bai Xutao so harshly was because it was all she could do. Any further, and their father would undoubtedly punish her. Although she was the clan’s top genius and held more status than Bai Xutao at the moment, her bloodline was still inferior to his, and that was what truly mattered.

“That’s just an excuse. A poor one, at that.” Yuan shook his head.

Meanwhile, Feng Yuxiang’s relentless beating of Bai Xutao had grown so intense that the entire guest house collapsed under the pressure.

chapters.title: Chapter 2080: Chaos Within The Heavenly White Tiger Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2080: Chaos Within The Heavenly White Tiger Clan

Chapter 2080: Chaos Within the Heavenly White Tiger Clan

After beating Bai Xutao until he was almost unconscious, Feng Yuxiang released him and said, “Go ahead and heal yourself. I don’t want to end this too quickly.”

Hearing this, Bai Xutao immediately distanced himself from her and consumed a recovery pill. However, he was only a Golden Immortal while Feng Yuxiang was a True Immortal. The difference between their cultivation was simply too great for him to do anything to her.

Meanwhile, their commotion attracted the attention of those around them, but nobody dared to approach them after realizing Bai Xutao and Bai Ning were there and only watched from afar.

After healing himself, Bai Xutao immediately transformed into his beast form, and despite the difference in their cultivation, he attempted to fight back.

“Good! If you’d given up too quickly, my revenge wouldn’t feel as satisfying!” Feng Yuxiang laughed out loud as she resumed his beating.

Her phoenix flames scattered everywhere like fireworks, engulfing the entire area with flames. However, she wasn’t using True Primordial Fire, so these flames didn’t do too much damage.

Bai Xutao was relentlessly scorched by her flames. With each blow, his body charred black, only for him to hastily consume a recovery pill and repeat the cycle, just as he’d done during his battle with Yuan.

Eventually, Feng Yuxiang summoned the True Primordial Fire. The moment it touched Bai Xutao, his screams pierced the heavens. These flames were relentless, clinging to him like a curse, burning not just his body but reaching deep into the core of his soul.

“Senior Sister! Please! Help me!” Bai Xutao pleaded for her help.

Bai Ning gritted her teeth and urged Yuan, “That is enough! You’re really going to kill him at this rate!”

Yuan’s face remained nonchalant as he responded in a calm tone, “What if we want to kill him? What are you going to do about it?”

Bai Ning roared, “Why are you doing this?! Are you really related to the Immortal Monarch?!”

Yuan narrowed his eyes and replied, “My precious companion nearly lost her life because of him. Not only that, but he’d harmed many innocent lives when he attacked them, not to mention the countless other lives he’d ruined.”

“SISTER! PLEASE!!!” Bai Xutao continued to scream for help.

Bai Ning could no longer afford to remain still. Regardless of her hatred toward Bai Xutao, allowing his death would seal her own fate as well.

However, Yuan blocked her path and said, “If you want to save him, you’ll have to get past me.”

“Are you trying to start a war with the entire Heavenly White Tiger Clan?!” she roared.

However, Yuan didn’t say anything and retrieved Number One Under Heaven.

Bai Ning’s eyes widened with shock after seeing his weapon.

“Y-You are—!” She almost couldn’t believe her eyes.

“That’s right. This will be our third time fighting each other, and it will also be your third time losing.”

“YOU BASTARD!!!” Bai Ning roared, her True Immortal cultivation erupting like a dam bursting under pressure.

“How dare you trick me using the Immortal Monarch’s name?! I’ll skin you alive!”

In the next moment, Bai Ning and Yuan clashed, their exchanges unleashing violent ripples that tore through the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s territory. Buildings crumbled under the pressure, and the clan’s members fled in panic like frightened cats.

’This bastard’s cultivation went from Spirit Emperor to Silver Immortal in just a few years?! What the hell has he been doing since our last encounter?!’ Bai Ning was shocked by Yuan’s cultivation progress, not to mention his overwhelming prowess.

Despite many years of relentless training, Bai Ning found herself utterly overwhelmed by Yuan. Forced entirely on the defensive, she couldn’t even catch a glimpse of an opening to counterattack.

Yet, Yuan wasn’t even fighting with all of his strength and was merely toying with her like he’d done before. Of course, Bai Ning realized this, but there was nothing she could do.

“What’s happening…? How did things end up like this…?” Bai Sulan muttered, her voice dazed as she stood amidst the rubble of the guest house.

Everything happened so quickly that she failed to comprehend the situation.

“Young Lady, we must leave this place immediately!” Bai Ning’s servant cried as she grabbed her and swiftly carried her away.

Meanwhile, Bai Xutao’s servant rushed toward the battered figure of her master. She knew all too well that if she didn’t at least try to save him, she would face severe punishment… likely a death sentence.

“Why are you even helping him after everything he’s done to you?! Get lost if you don’t want to die a pointless death!” Feng Yuxiang warned her.

“I’ll still die if I don’t try!”

Despite only being in the Immortal Ascension realm, the servant rushed to help Bai Xutao.

However, Feng Yuxiang knocked her out before she could even reach him. Then, she sent the servant’s unconscious body far away with a flick of her sleeves.

“There. You tried.” Feng Yuxiang mumbled before returning her focus to Bai Xutao, who was attempting to run away while his body was still engulfed in flames.

“And where do you think you’re going?!” Feng Yuxiang immediately chased after him.

The commotion from their battle shook the entire Heavenly White Tiger Clan, its intensity so great that it reached even the inner sanctum. Alarmed by the chaos, the elders immediately assumed they were under attack and moved into action. However, they were quite far away and would need several minutes to reach the scene.

Meanwhile, Feng Yuxiang continued to torture Bai Xutao, who had returned to his human form in an attempt to get rid of the fire.

“Even if I can’t kill you today, I’ll make sure you’ll never harm another woman again!” Feng Yuxiang snarled, her finger suddenly pointing toward the area between Bai Xutao’s legs.

“Burn that vile thing away!” she commanded, and her flames surged forth with murderous intent.

In the next moment, a surge of True Primordial Fire exploded at Bai Xutao’s crotch, incinerating his manhood in an instant and erasing any trace of his identity as a man.

“!@*#)!@&#(*!@&$” Bai Xutao let out a bloodcurdling scream, shrill and pitiful like a butchered pig, before collapsing into unconsciousness from the agony.

chapters.title: Chapter 2081: Chaos Within The Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2081: Chaos Within The Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)

Chapter 2081: Chaos Within the Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)

“Bai Xutao!” Bai Ning exclaimed in a terrified voice after seeing his body fall from the sky.

“Do you even have time to worry about others?” Yuan blocked her from attempting to approach Bai Xutao again.

“You! Do you have any idea what you have done?!” She shouted at him. “To attack us in the middle of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s territory, you’re extremely foolish! Once our elders arrive, you’ll regret attacking us!”

“I’ll deal with that when the time comes.” Yuan smiled before continuing his attack.

Meanwhile, Feng Yuxiang went to catch Bai Xutao’s falling body. She caught him by the leg and dangled him like a butchered chicken.

“Wake up.”

“…”

Feng Yuxiang used her True Primordial Fire to wake him up when shaking him didn’t work.

“Ahhh!” Bai Xutao immediately woke up screaming.

“Welcome back. Are you ready to continue?” Feng Yuxiang asked with a sadistic smile on her face.

“N-No! Please! I’m sorry for what happened before! I swear I won’t ever approach you again!” Bai Xutao begged.

“You think I’m doing this just for myself? I’m also acting for those who never had the strength to seek justice!” Feng Yuxiang laughed coldly. “We’re just getting started, so don’t you dare die on me too soon!”

Bai Xutao’s agonized screams returned, echoing like a curse in the ears of all who heard them.

Eventually, the elders and true powerhouses of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan arrived at the scene, only to be struck with shock and terror at the sight that awaited them.

Bai Ning, their number one genius of the younger generation, was being tossed around like a rag doll by a mere human Silver Immortal.

As for Bai Xutao, they failed to recognize him at first. Feng Yuxiang’s flames had reduced him to a scorched husk: naked, hairless, and with every inch of his skin charred black.

“What in heaven’s name is going on here?!” one of the elders snapped, his 2nd-level God Ascension cultivation erupting forth. The surge of power flooded the area with immense pressure, instantly bringing Yuan and Feng Yuxiang to a halt.

“E-Eleventh Elder! We’re being attacked! These are the bastards that nearly killed Bai Xutao when he went to the lower heavens!” Bai Ning revealed to them.

“What!? Surround them!”

Thousands of Immortals surrounded Yuan and Feng Yuxiang at the elder’s command. Yet, neither of them appeared worried in the slightest, almost as if they had the situation under control.

Yuan then spoke, speaking directly to Bai Ning, “I guess you don’t have what it takes to defeat me, after all.”

“Shut up!” The Eleventh Elder roared.

He moved toward Yuan and demanded, “Who the hell are you? Why are you attacking the Heavenly White Tiger Clan? Do you even realize who you’re dealing with?”

Yuan replied calmly, “Of course I do. Your ancestor was one of the Nine Divine Supremes who once served the Immortal Monarch. But so what? That was long ago. You hold no ties to him or his legacy now, so stop waving around a title that no longer means anything.”

“Blasphemy!” Bai Ning roared.

“Then let me ask you this… Do you know who I am?”

“You’re a nobody who tried to claim to have ties with the Immortal Monarch! I was foolish to have believed you!”

Yuan looked at her and smiled, “Do you still have what I gave you at the Pavilion of Knowledge?”

Bai Ning’s body trembled the moment she was reminded of the vial of blood. Retrieving it with shaky hands, she exclaimed, “Whose blood is this?! It’s definitely not the Immortal Monarch’s!”

She had revered this vial of blood ever since she acquired it, but now, realizing it was a fake all along, she was shaken to the core.

“If you think it’s fake, why don’t you hand it back to me?” Yuan extended his hand toward her.

Bai Ning instinctively drew her hand back as if to throw the vial away, but her motion froze halfway, hesitation clearly written in her eyes.

“Immortal Monarch? What nonsense is this?” the Eleventh Elder sneered.

He motioned toward Bai Ning and barked, “Hand me that vial!”

Bai Ning hesitated once more, a flicker of doubt in her eyes, but eventually released her grip and passed the vial to him.

Once the vial of blood was in his grasp, the Eleventh Elder didn’t even bother to check it and immediately crushed it, destroying the vial and the blood within it.

Bai Ning felt a gripping pain in her heart after seeing this, even though she knew it was fake.

“What a waste…” Feng Yuxiang shook her head with an exaggerated sigh. “Do you have any idea how many beasts would kill for that blood?”

“Silence!” the Eleventh Elder roared, his voice thundering across the valley.

After a heavy pause, he continued coldly, “For the crimes of attacking the Heavenly White Tiger Clan and attempting to deceive us with the Immortal Monarch’s name—I hereby sentence both of you to death!”

“How scary.” Feng Yuxiang chuckled, still treating their situation like it was a joke.

“Young Master, what should we do now?” she then asked him.

Yuan turned to the Eleventh Elder and asked calmly, “Where is your Clan Leader?”

The Eleventh Elder sneered with disdain. “You really think the Clan Leader would appear for two insignificant nobodies?”

Yuan swept his gaze across the tens of thousands of Immortals encircling him, then said coolly, “Then, until your Clan Leader shows up, let’s keep playing.”

After saying these words, Yuan activated True Dragon Awakening, causing his aura to skyrocket.

The moment they sensed Yuan’s overwhelming aura, the surrounding Immortals instinctively backed away, their faces pale with fear and bodies drenched in cold sweat.

Bai Ning’s eyes widened, and in that moment, she finally realized just how vastly superior Yuan had been all along.

“So it’s finally time to get serious, huh?” Feng Yuxiang muttered as a sphere of flames appeared around her, rapidly expanding and engulfing her figure in a blazing aura.

When the flames subsided, Feng Yuxiang was no longer in human form. In her place stood a majestic phoenix wreathed in golden fire, radiating immense heat and awe-inspiring presence.

chapters.title: Chapter 2082: Chaos Within The Heavenly White Tiger Clan(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2082: Chaos Within The Heavenly White Tiger Clan(3)

Chapter 2082: Chaos Within the Heavenly White Tiger Clan(3)

Following her transformation, Feng Yuxiang unleashed a vast sea of flames that surged outward, engulfing those nearby. Though the fire scorched their skin and singed their hair, it carried no killing intent, as she had no desire to take their lives.

As for Yuan, he stowed his sword away and began beating the Heavenly White Tigers with his bare fists.

“A human dares challenge us in physique? Laughable!” a Golden Immortal scoffed as he stepped forward to confront Yuan.

But the moment their fists collided, his laughter died. His eyes widened in shock as his entire arm shattered like brittle glass under Yuan’s strike.

“Can’t you sense his aura? He’s no human! He’s a dragon!” Someone exclaimed.

Despite being surrounded by Immortals, neither Yuan nor Feng Yuxiang could be stopped by the Heavenly White Tiger Clan.

The Eleventh Elder and the other elders quickly realized that they needed to personally act if they wanted to subdue them. However, they didn’t immediately act, so the others could wear them down first.

Eventually, after several hours of trying to subdue Yuan and Feng Yuxiang, the Eleventh Elder shouted, “All of you, get back! We’ll handle this ourselves!”

There were twenty elders in total, each one at the first or second level of the God Ascension Realm. Naturally, they didn’t all attack at once, as such a move would’ve been excessive. Instead, two elders moved to confront Yuan, while only one turned their focus to Feng Yuxiang. Despite his lower cultivation, they saw Yuan as the greater threat.

Since they were still within the Nine Heavens, Yuan wasn’t too worried about facing a God Ascension cultivator. As for Feng Yuxiang, her True Primordial Fire still affected them.

“Since you decided to struggle, we won’t delay your death sentence and will directly kill you right here and now,” said one of Yuan’s opponents.

Yuan smiled and calmly responded, “Do you even have that ability?”

The elders couldn’t understand where Yuan’s confidence came from, but they dismissed it without concern and launched their attack.

As the battle erupted, the surrounding Immortals quickly retreated to clear the field, while the remaining elders lingered at the edges, ready to intervene if needed.

Despite only being a True Immortal, Feng Yuxiang held her ground against her opponent, a first-level God Ascension expert, and was even forcing him back. The elder had no way to counter her True Primordial Fire, which burned with relentless ferocity.

“Damn it! What kind of flames are these? Even Celestial Qi can’t snuff them out!” the elder cursed, struggling to contain the blaze. “I’ve never seen or even heard of anything like this!”

The other elders were equally stunned. None of them possessed the slightest knowledge of Primordial Phoenixes.

However, what shocked them the most was Yuan, who was overwhelming two God Ascension cultivators at once with nothing but his raw prowess.

“Such overwhelming innate strength… Does he belong to the Ten Royal Dragon Clans?” one of the elders suddenly thought of this possibility.

“What about the phoenix? I have seen several phoenixes from the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans, but none of them could even compare to her fire. Where the hell did these two monsters come from?”

The elders were worried that they might have to answer to both Royal Clans if they killed Yuan and Feng Yuxiang.

However, the Eleventh Elder remained calm and reassured them, “They’re not with the Royal Clans. There’s no way the two clans would work together, much less attack our Heavenly White Tiger Clan in such a manner.”

What the Eleventh Elder said made a lot of sense to the others, and they quickly stopped worrying about it.

“W-We cannot defeat him with just the two of us! We need more help!” One of the elders fighting Yuan suddenly said to the others.

“I’ll go help.”

Soon, another God Ascension cultivator entered the fray, turning the fight into a three-on-one assault.

“I also need some help!” said the elder fighting Feng Yuxiang.

Thus, her battle became one-on-two the very next moment.

Yet, the new addition did not seem to help, and Yuan still dominated the battlefield. However, Feng Yuxiang was slowly being pushed back because she was still not used to using her True Primordial Fire.

Despite that, she still didn’t seem to be worried in the slightest.

“Young Master, I’ll start using ’that’ power, okay?” Feng Yuxiang suddenly asked.

“Go ahead, but try not to kill them.” Yuan nodded.

“Understood!”

In the very next moment, Feng Yuxiang’s aura suddenly skyrocketed toward the heavens, shocking the elders.

“What?! Where is this power coming from?!”

Unbeknownst to the elders, Feng Yuxiang had just activated one of the Immortal Monarch’s powers through her Supreme Seal.

<Immortal Monarch’s True Blessing has activated>

[Immortal Monarch’s True Blessing]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Mastery Level: 1]

[Description: Grants those bearing the Immortal Monarch’s Supreme Seal overwhelming prowess, increasing their overall strength by 10,000%. This skill will remain active until deactivated by the Seal Bearer or the Creator and will constantly drain the Creator’s soul strength.]

The Immortal Monarch’s True Blessing was a direct upgrade of Immortal Monarch’s Blessing, which granted temporary strength to those who did not have the Immortal Monarch’s Supreme Seal.

Meanwhile, Yuan could feel his soul strength being drained at a rapid rate. However, since only Feng Yuxiang was using it, he could easily replenish it with his soul cultivation technique, essentially allowing him to keep it active forever if he wanted.

With the aid of the Immortal Monarch’s True Blessing, Feng Yuxiang instantly seized the upper hand once more, becoming the dominant force on the battlefield.

“Hahaha! I haven’t felt this alive in ages!” Feng Yuxiang laughed as she soared higher into the heavens. With a mighty beat of her majestic wings, she ignited a massive fireball in the sky—a blazing explosion that bloomed like fireworks.

“S-Stop her!”

Two more elders suddenly joined the battle, turning it into a four-against-one siege against Feng Yuxiang.

Meanwhile, another pair moved to support the three elders fighting Yuan, escalating the clash into a five-on-one confrontation.

chapters.title: Chapter 2083: The Clan Leader’S Appearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2083: The Clan Leader’S Appearance

Chapter 2083: The Clan Leader’s Appearance

“Good heavens, he’s fighting five of our elders all by himself! What a freaking monster!” The Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s members were bewildered by what they were witnessing. A few even believed they were currently dreaming.

“What should we do?” One of the elders on standby asked the others.

“Maybe we should just attack them at once and get this over with,” one of them suggested.

“You want twenty God Ascension cultivators to gang up on two Immortals? That would look terrible for our clan if news of it spreads!” another rejected the idea.

“And how do you think it currently looks now?! How will it look if he defeats even one of our elders?!” another elder snapped back.

The Eleventh Elder closed his eyes and sighed.

“Let’s just get this over with. As long as word doesn’t spread, it’ll be fine,” he said a moment later.

He turned to look at one of the elders there with a suggestive gaze.

The elder gave a firm nod before swiftly weaving array symbols through the air. Once completed, a colossal formation rose around the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s territory, shrouding it from the outside world, like a massive black curtain descending to conceal the chaos within.

“It’s ready,” he said a moment later.

“Then let’s go.” The Eleventh Elder took charge and joined the battle against Yuan.

Seeing this, the others followed.

In the blink of an eye, all twenty elders had joined the fray. Now, both Yuan and Feng Yuxiang had to deal with ten God Ascension cultivators at the same time.

“Young Master, I cannot deal with so many of them at once!” Feng Yuxiang said to him through voice transmission.

“You’ve done enough. Come back to me. I’ll deal with them,” he said.

Feng Yuxiang nodded and immediately backed away from her fight.

“She’s running away!”

“Stop her!”

Although the elders tried to stop her, Feng Yuxiang’s speed was simply too fast for them, not to mention her True Primordial Fire that constantly blocked their path.

Upon reaching Yuan, she immediately dove into his body, entering his Dantian.

“What the—?”

The elders watching were stunned. A dragon allowing a phoenix into his Dantian? It defied everything they knew, as such trust between two ancient, opposing bloodlines was beyond comprehension.

“Yu Ning, I’ll have to rely on you again,” Yuan suddenly said to her.

“Huh? Don’t tell me you want to do ’that’ again?”

“Don’t worry, I am no longer a mortal, and my understanding of Celestial Qi has improved significantly since we last did it.”

“If you say so…”

Yu Ning began gathering Celestial Qi for Yuan, shocking the elders.

“H-He’s using Celestial Qi?! As a Silver Immortal?!”

None of them dared to believe their eyes.

“How do you feel?” Yu Ning asked Yuan a moment later.

“It’s still a little painful, but it’s manageable.”

He looked at the twenty elders and said, “The real fun begins now.”

The elders exchanged uneasy glances. Then, after a brief, heavy silence, they all turned toward Yuan—striking in unison without a word.

A battle involving twenty God Ascension cultivators was nothing short of cataclysmic, unleashing devastation beyond imagination. Within mere minutes, the entire residential area of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan had been reduced to nothingness.

“Is your Clan Leader still not going to show himself?” Yuan asked. “Or is he planning to wait until the entire Heavenly White Valley is reduced to rubble?”

Despite being outnumbered twenty to one, Yuan showed no signs of faltering. The elders couldn’t so much as scratch his unrivaled physique, and not a single one could contend with the overwhelming force of his soul strength.

It felt as though they were battling someone far beyond their cultivation, yet in reality, they were up against a mere Silver Immortal. Thankfully, the formation had shielded their struggle from the outside world. Had it not, they would have likely perished from the weight of their own shame.

“You are not worthy to meet the Clan Leader!” One of the elders roared as he transformed into his beast form, becoming a massive white tiger that was almost as large as a mountain.

The other elders did the same, and very soon, twenty massive white tigers surrounded Yuan.

“How scary.” Yuan chuckled.

“Since your Clan Leader won’t show up, it looks like I will have no choice but to kill some of you.”

Yuan suddenly closed his eyes and took a deep breath, allowing his dragon transformation to fade.

But moments later, a new aura surged forth, and when his eyes reopened, they glowed with a majestic, silver brilliance.

Yuan’s presence shifted dramatically, now radiating an unfathomable aura that sent a primal fear surging through the surrounding beasts, forcing them to bow to his very existence.

“What?!”

The elders were stunned to find themselves subconsciously kneeling before Yuan, their bodies betraying a reverence their minds had yet to accept.

“You wish to know my identity? Then allow me to tell you…”

Yuan’s voice rumbled like thunder, its resonance sending a chill through their entire being.

Just as the tension reached its peak, another formidable presence suddenly emerged. Though it was initially distant, it closed the gap in the blink of an eye, arriving with astonishing speed.

Yuan turned to face the newcomer—an imposing middle-aged man with long, flowing white and black hair, his expression naturally stern and commanding. He wore a robe split in half, black on one side and white on the other, with a white tiger emblem emblazoned on the black side.

This individual’s cultivation far surpassed that of the elders, standing at the 7th level of God Ascension.

“Clan Leader!” the elders exclaimed in disbelief, astonished that he had appeared so suddenly.

“There’s no need for you to act, Clan Leader! We can handle this ourselves!” The Eleventh Elder said.

However, the Clan Leader paid no heed to the elders, his gaze fixed solely on Yuan’s glowing silver eyes.

“So you have finally decided to show yourself, huh?” Yuan said, staring back at him.

chapters.title: Chapter 2084: Immortal Monarch’S Supremacy
chapters.body:
Chapter 2084: Immortal Monarch’S Supremacy

Chapter 2084: Immortal Monarch’s Supremacy

The Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s Leader swept the scene with a cold, calculating gaze, his eyes briefly lingering on Bai Ning before moving to Bai Xutao, who lay buried beneath a mountain of rubble and dust. Although Bai Xutao appeared to be a burned corpse, he was still alive.

Eventually, the Clan Leader returned to Yuan and spoke in an emotionless tone, “What are you trying to achieve here?”

Yuan braced for the Clan Leader to erupt in fury, certain he would attack in a blind rage over the devastation to his clan. But to his surprise, the Clan Leader remained unnervingly calm.

“I have two objectives here,” Yuan said calmly. “First, to deal with Bai Xutao, who harmed my companion. And second, to acquire your Ancient Seal.”

At the mention of the Ancient Seal, the Clan Leader’s eyes narrowed, his expression betraying nothing. However, the elders couldn’t hold back, their voices erupting in shock. “How does he know about the Ancient Seal?!”

The Clan Leader whipped his gaze toward the elder who had spoken, his eyes brimming with deadly intent.

“Shit!” The elder cursed under his breath, immediately clamping his hand over his mouth as he realized his grave mistake.

“There’s no use trying to hide it. I know you have it,” Yuan said.

“Let’s assume we do have it, for the sake of this conversation,” the Clan Leader said, his tone laced with mockery. “Do you really expect us to simply hand it over to you, after everything you’ve done here?”

Yuan paused for a moment, then casually gestured toward Bai Xutao. In an instant, Bai Xutao’s body was yanked from the rubble, soaring toward Yuan as though an invisible force were pulling him.

Seeing this, the Clan Leader raised his hand, instantly halting Yuan’s attempt to seize Bai Xutao with an unseen force.

“You’re quite foolish if you think you can act with impunity in my presence,” the Clan Leader said coldly.

Yuan smiled, his tone unruffled. “It was worth a try. That said, I could kill him at any moment, and there’s nothing you can do to stop it unless you hand over the Ancient Seal.”

“An empty threat.” The Clan Leader did not believe Yuan for a moment.

“I don’t usually resort to threats,” Yuan said with a sigh, “but your Heavenly White Tiger Clan has fallen so low that I simply don’t care anymore. If the Immortal Monarch were here, he’d be furious at how you’ve been running things.”

“Killing your own children simply because their bloodline doesn’t meet your standards or they fail a trial? Even barbarians wouldn’t stoop so low.”

The Clan Leader sneered. “Who are you to judge how our family operates? Unless you’re our ancestor, you have no right to speak on it.”

“I may not be your ancestor, but I do have a connection with him.”

“Hahaha!” The Clan Leader suddenly laughed out loud, his tone filled with mockery.

“Alright, enough of this farce. I don’t know who you are or why you’re truly here, but don’t think you’ll get out of here alive today.”

The Clan Leader glanced at the elders, his voice cold and commanding. “Get lost. I’ll clean up your mess, but be prepared for punishment later.”

The elders trembled under his gaze, too fearful to protest. They silently bowed and quickly withdrew, leaving Yuan and the Clan Leader alone.

The Eleventh Elder didn’t forget to carry Bai Xutao away as he departed.

Yuan watched them leave, his voice cold as he mumbled, “I’ll let you live a little longer.”

Once they were alone, the Clan Leader unleashed his cultivation and, without a word, began his attack on Yuan.

Though the Clan Leader was at the seventh level of God Ascension, the Nine Heavens’ limitation kept him from being vastly stronger than the elders. Of course, he was also holding back, unwilling to completely destroy his own territory, and not seeing Yuan as a threat that warranted full effort.

As expected, Yuan was being pushed back by the Clan Leader’s overwhelming power. Yet, Yuan remained unfazed, his confidence unwavering as he continued to exude an aura of calm certainty.

Suddenly—

<Immortal Monarch’s Supremacy activated>

Yuan’s silver eyes suddenly blazed with intensity, glowing as if they had transformed into a silver sun.

[Immortal Monarch’s Supremacy]

[Rank: ???]

[Mastery Level: 1]

[Description: A mysterious power bestowed by the Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline. When activated during a battle against a beast, your overall prowess will increase by 50,000%]

The Immortal Monarch’s Supremacy functioned similarly to Heaven’s Supremacy, but it only activated when battling beasts. This unique skill had been awakened within Yuan after he consumed the Primal Monarch’s Blood Essence and unlocked his bloodline. However, until now, he had never been able to fully utilize it, as his cultivation wasn’t strong enough to activate its true power, and his bloodline before consuming the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence was also lacking.

Yet, despite its overwhelming power, Yuan could sense that he was only scratching the surface of its true potential.

The instant Yuan activated Immortal Monarch’s Supremacy, his previous disadvantage vanished, and he stood on equal footing with the Clan Leader, their battle now burning with renewed intensity.

Though the Clan Leader’s expression remained barely altered, he was inwardly shocked by Yuan’s sudden increase in prowess.

Realizing that he was beginning to fall behind, the Clan Leader’s expression darkened, and he began to unleash more of his power, determined to reclaim control of the fight.

Meanwhile, the Eleventh Elder hurriedly transported Bai Xutao to the innermost, most restricted area of the clan’s territory—accessible only to those of the highest authority. There, they immediately began working to heal the grievous injuries he had sustained from Feng Yuxiang’s flames.

However, they soon realized that even with their most precious and valuable treasures, healing Bai Xutao would not be an easy task. Despite their personal disdain for his character, they knew they had no choice but to spare no expense in saving him, as the future of their clan relied on his bloodline.

chapters.title: Chapter 2085: Heavenly White Tiger’S Ancestral Altar
chapters.body:
Chapter 2085: Heavenly White Tiger’S Ancestral Altar

Chapter 2085: Heavenly White Tiger’s Ancestral Altar

It took several hours and an unfathomable amount of treasure—enough to send even the wealthiest merchant into a panic—but the elders managed to restore Bai Xutao’s burned appearance and heal all but one of his injuries.

When Bai Xutao woke up, the first thing he did was reach for the area between his legs.

“I-It’s not there! Where’s my thing?!” he exclaimed in a terrified voice.

“I’m sorry, Young Master, but we could not heal it…” one of the elders said in a defeated voice.

“N-NO! YOU MUST FIND A WAY! IT MUST BE HEALED!” he roared, even grabbing the elder by his collar.

The Eleventh Elder slapped his hand and shouted back, “So what if it’s gone?! You don’t need it to conceive!”

“If I don’t have it, how am I going to satisfy myself?! What am I going to do about my natural lust?!”

“We’ll find a way, but for the time being, you’ll have to live without it,” said the Eleventh Elder as he shook his head.

Bai Xutao lowered his head and fell silent.

“Unforgivable…” he muttered in a cold voice.

“UNFORGIVABLE!”

Bai Xutao suddenly leapt from the bed, roaring with fury as his entire cultivation base erupted in a violent surge of power.

Then, without another word, he rushed out of the building.

“Young Master, where are you going?!” the elders shouted, hurrying after him, a growing sense of dread twisting in their guts as Bai Xutao stormed off.

“I will get my revenge! I don’t care how!” he roared.

“How will you do that when even we cannot defeat them?!”

Bai Xutao didn’t respond and continued speeding ahead.

It didn’t take long for the elders to realize something was off—Bai Xutao wasn’t heading toward the battlefield, but in the opposite direction.

“Where is he going?”

“Maybe he’s still in a daze…”

Eventually, they realized where they were heading.

“Wait a second… Aren’t we heading toward the Heavenly White Tiger’s Ancestral Altar?”

“But why would he—”

A sudden realization struck the elders again, and when it did, their hearts lurched with terror.

“He can’t be!”

“Is he trying to consume the Ancestor’s Blood Essence?!”

Before its death, the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s ancestor left behind a single drop of its Blood Essence—a sacred remnant the clan had safeguarded ever since.

The Blood Essence was kept at the Heavenly White Tiger’s Ancestral Altar, a sacred area where the Clan Leader spends most of his time.

“Calm down. Even if he’s trying to acquire it, he won’t be able to get past the barrier without the Clan Leader’s approval,” said the Eleventh Elder.

Hearing this, the elders quickly calmed down.

However, when they arrived at the Heavenly White Tiger’s Ancestral Altar, they were met with another shock. The barrier that was meant to protect the sacred site had vanished without a trace.

“What?! Where’s the barrier?! There’s no way the Clan Leader would’ve left this place without ensuring it’s protected!”

The Eleventh Elder exclaimed in disbelief.

The Ancestral Altar was arguably the most important area in their territory. It was simply unthinkable for the Clan Leader to leave it unprotected.

“We don’t have the time to think about that! The Young Master has already entered the site! We need to stop him before he does something terrible!”

The elders wasted no time and rushed into the Ancestral Altar with growing panic in their hearts.

When they arrived at the end, they could see Bai Xutao standing right before the altar with a glass vial containing a drop of blood in his grasp.

“Young Master! Put that back immediately!” The Eleventh Elder screamed after seeing the object in Bai Xutao’s grasp. “The Clan Leader will kill you if you do any more!”

However, Bai Xutao merely laughed, “Kill me? Do you know who I am?! I was born with our ancestor’s bloodline in me! If anybody should touch his Blood Essence, it would be me!”

“Who knows?! Maybe my bloodline will fully awaken after I consume his Blood Essence!”

“Your body will not be able to handle the Ancestor’s Blood Essence! You’ll die a violent death if you consume it without preparation! Are you trying to waste the Ancestor’s Blood Essence and suicide right afterward?! That’s double the waste!”

“Die? I won’t die until I kill those two bastards who humiliated me, especially that damn phoenix who burned my manhood! I’ll make sure that bitch suffers the most!”

The very next moment, Bai Xutao opened the seal on the glass vial.

“STOP HIM!!!” the Eleventh Elder bellowed, and in an instant, everyone charged at Bai Xutao, desperate to halt his reckless action.

Unfortunately, they were a split second too late. By the time they reached him, Bai Xutao had already consumed the Blood Essence.

“No way…”

The elders fell to their knees, despair etched on their faces. None of them could have ever imagined such a thing was possible. They had always known Bai Xutao to be reckless, but it seemed they had never truly grasped the depth of his recklessness until today.

“S-Spit it out! Immediately!” the Eleventh Elder pleaded, his voice trembling.

But Bai Xutao remained silent, his body trembling before unleashing a deafening howl so powerful it nearly shattered the elders’ eardrums. In the next instant, his form began to swell, his body morphing into his beastly shape.

“It hurts! It hurts! IT HURRRTS!” Bai Xutao screamed out in pain, but there was nothing that could be done.

In mere moments, Bai Xutao’s body swelled to an unimaginable size, far surpassing his usual limits. He grew so colossal that he tore through the walls of the Ancestral Altar, the entire structure collapsing in the wake of his transformation.

When the transformation finally ceased, Bai Xutao let out another earth-shattering howl, his aura rippling across the entire Heavenly White Valley. In an instant, his cultivation, once at Golden Immortal, surged explosively to the first level of God Ascension, and it continued to rise toward the next level with every passing moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 2086: Gone Berserk
chapters.body:
Chapter 2086: Gone Berserk

Chapter 2086: Gone Berserk

“Heavens! He’s actually managed to consume the ancestor’s Blood Essence without dying!” The elders were shocked by the results.

However, the Eleventh Elder’s expression shifted with concern. “No, something feels wrong.”

Though Bai Xutao was still alive, his energy was a turbulent storm, chaotic and wild, as if he had lost all control over his own body.

A moment later, one of the elders stepped forward to confront Bai Xutao.

“Young Master! How are you feeling?” he asked, his voice filled with concern.

But instead of answering, Bai Xutao turned to face him and, without warning, launched a brutal attack.

The elder was instantly sent flying, crashing into a nearby mountain.
