“Be careful! The Young Master has gone berserk!” The Eleventh Elder warned everyone before distancing himself from Bai Xutao.

The others quickly followed.

Meanwhile, after hours of fierce combat, both Yuan and the Clan Leader abruptly paused as they sensed a powerful presence appearing out of thin air.

Yuan recognized the presence as strangely familiar, but couldn’t place its origin. The Clan Leader felt the same unease, his brow furrowing in confusion.

Though the presence wasn’t stronger than the Clan Leader’s, it carried a far more menacing and threatening aura, making it feel far more dangerous.

It was at this moment that one of the elders at the scene notified the Clan Leader of the situation through his communication jade slip.

“That bastard did what?!” The Clan Leader didn’t believe the news at first.

“The Young Master entered the Ancestral Altar and consumed the Ancestor’s Blood Essence!” The Eleventh Elder repeated.

“That’s impossible! How did he enter the Ancestral Altar?! The barrier should’ve kept him out!” the Clan Leader exclaimed in disbelief.

“The barrier was gone when we arrived!” The Eleventh Elder hurriedly responded. “I don’t know if the Young Master tampered with it or if something else caused its disappearance, but it wasn’t there when we arrived!”

Noticing the Clan Leader’s distraught expression, Yuan couldn’t help but ask, “Ancestral Altar? What are you talking about?”

Since the Eleventh Elder was conversing through the communication jade slip, he had no idea about the situation.

Yet, the Clan Leader ignored him and focused on Bai Xutao’s situation.

“What happened to Bai Xutao?!”

The Eleventh Elder quickly explained Bai Xutao’s situation.

“He’s alive, but he’s gone berserk and is destroying everything in sight! He doesn’t even recognize his own people!”

“That useless bastard! Keep him restrained until I get there! There must be a way to extract the Blood Essence he consumed!”

After putting away the communication jade slip, the Clan Leader looked at Yuan with a serious expression on his face.

“I don’t have time to waste on you any longer,” the Clan Leader snarled. “I’ll grant you a painless death—be grateful.”

Before Yuan could respond, the Clan Leader unleashed the full force of his cultivation, striking with his most powerful martial technique.

“Heavenly Tiger’s White Flash!”

The Clan Leader swung his claw-like hand toward Yuan, unleashing a blinding flash of light, as bright as a flashbang. In an instant, Yuan’s world turned pure white, and before he could react, a forceful, unstoppable blow struck his body, tearing him apart in the blink of an eye.

After delivering the crushing blow and being certain it had struck Yuan, the Clan Leader wasted no time in flying away, not even bothering to check if Yuan was still alive. He was absolutely certain that no Immortal could survive such an attack.

Moments after the Clan Leader flew off, Yuan emerged from the mountain of rubble, completely unharmed, his expression as calm as ever.

“He must be in quite the hurry to leave so quickly, not even bothering to check if I’m still alive,” Yuan murmured, his gaze following the direction the Clan Leader had disappeared to.

“Are you going to chase after him, or are we going to leave now?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“Leave?” Yuan shook his head and continued, “Why would I leave when we still have plenty of time before we return to the Rising Phoenix Clan? Not to mention the Ancient Seal isn’t in our possession yet.”

“Then…”

“Let’s see what’s got him so worried,” Yuan muttered, before soaring off in the direction the Clan Leader had vanished.

Sometime later, the Clan Leader arrived at the scene, his eyes widening as he saw the elders still struggling to subdue Bai Xutao, whose cultivation had now surged to the 3rd level of God Ascension.

“You’re here, Clan Leader!” The Eleventh Elder hurried toward him upon noticing his arrival.

“What is this? Why is it taking you so long to subdue him?” The Clan Leader demanded, his frown deepening with frustration.

“That’s… He’s growing stronger with each passing moment,” the Eleventh Elder said hesitantly, “but his cultivation isn’t the real problem we’re facing.”

Before the Eleventh Elder could continue, the Clan Leader’s attention snapped to Bai Xutao, who unleashed a powerful roar that resonated with profound energy. The sound reverberated through the air, causing the Clan Leader’s bloodline to tremble in response.

“This feeling is—!” The Clan Leader’s eyes widened with disbelief after realizing something.

Seeing the Clan Leader’s reaction, the Eleventh Elder let out a deep sigh. “We believe the Young Master’s bloodline has awakened after consuming the Ancestor’s Blood Essence, and his ’Bloodline Authority’ is now above ours, making him nearly impossible to handle. We can’t even land a proper attack on him because our instincts force us not to harm him.”

“What a fucking pain in the ass,” the Clan Leader snapped, his face reddening and veins bulging with rage. “How many times do I have to clean up your mess today?!”

“I’m sorry for being useless…” The Eleventh Elder murmured, lowering his head in shame.

“I’ll deal with you all later. Right now, I need to subdue him before his bloodline becomes too powerful for me to control,” the Clan Leader growled, his tone full of annoyance.

With that, he went to confront Bai Xutao, intending to subdue him without taking his life.

Meanwhile, Yuan watched from a distance while hiding his presence with Shadow Veil.

chapters.title: Chapter 2087: I Am The Immortal Monarch
chapters.body:
Chapter 2087: I Am The Immortal Monarch

Chapter 2087: I am the Immortal Monarch

“Good lord, what happened to that pervert?” Feng Yuxiang commented after seeing Bai Xutao’s new appearance.

Not only was Bai Xutao much bigger than before, but the patterns on his body had also changed.

“The Clan Leader mentioned Blood Essence, so that was most likely what he consumed,” said Yuan.

“Blood Essence… What are the chances that he consumed the Heavenly White Tiger—one of the Nine Divine Supreme’s Blood Essence?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“Judging by the Clan Leader’s reaction, it’s likely.”

“Isn’t that really bad? I mean, it was likely prepared for you.”

Yuan fell silent for a moment before responding, “Don’t worry, I have a way to extract the Blood Essence even if he consumed it. However, it’s going to be very painful for him, and he’s certainly going to die afterward.”

“How are you going to do that?” Feng Yuxiang asked, unaware of Yuan’s abilities as the Immortal Monarch.

“You’ll see in a bit.” Yuan said with a mysterious smile on his face.

Meanwhile, the Clan Leader struggled to subdue Bai Xutao, facing increasing difficulty. In the world of beasts, superior cultivation alone wasn’t the key to dominance—it was the bloodline. Those with a more powerful bloodline could command those of lesser heritage, regardless of cultivation.

Though Bai Xutao’s bloodline was only slightly stronger than the Clan Leader’s, it had been amplified by their ancestor’s blood essence. Moreover, the Clan Leader had to act in a way that wouldn’t kill Bai Xutao, making the process incredibly tricky.

The other members of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan watched from afar, their eyes wide with shock and disbelief.

None of them realized that Bai Xutao had consumed their ancestor’s blood essence and figured his rampage was caused by another factor.

Several hours had passed since Bai Xutao consumed the blood essence and lost control, yet the Clan Leader still hadn’t been able to subdue him. Meanwhile, Bai Xutao’s cultivation had soared to the fourth level of God Ascension, his power growing exponentially.

The Clan Leader eventually had to accept a harsh truth—that he alone was incapable of subduing Bai Xutao without killing him. However, doing so would not only squander Bai Xutao’s bloodline potential but also waste the precious blood essence he had consumed.

’Fuck! What should I do with him?!’

Killing Bai Xutao was out of the question for the Clan Leader. If he couldn’t handle the situation on his own, he would rather allow Bai Xutao to continue rampaging until a solution presented itself—regardless of whether it meant the destruction of the entire Heavenly White Valley.

“Looks like you’re having trouble. Need some help?”

A familiar voice suddenly echoed, pulling the Clan Leader out of his stunned daze.

“W-What’s he doing here?!”

The elders gasped in shock as Yuan materialized out of thin air. They had believed that the Clan Leader had already taken care of him.

When the Clan Leader saw Yuan, his face went pale, his expression filled with disbelief as if he had just seen a ghost. Startled, he jumped back, instinctively distancing himself from the unexpected appearance.

“Y-You! How the hell are you still alive?!” he exclaimed.

“Why wouldn’t I be alive? You never killed me.” Yuan calmly shrugged.

“Bullshit! I know my attack landed on you!”

“It did. But that doesn’t mean I’ll die from it.”

The Clan Leader was speechless.

“So, do you need help?” Yuan asked again, his voice snapping the Clan Leader out of his daze.

“Do you really think you can pretend you didn’t attack our clan?” the Clan Leader shot back, his voice hardening. “Even if we accept your help, it won’t save you from your death sentence.”

“Sounds like you want my help,” said Yuan.

“Get lost!” The Clan Leader roared, launching the same devastating technique he had used before.

“Heavenly Tiger’s White Flash!”

The attack tore through Yuan’s body, but this time, the Clan Leader watched in astonishment as Yuan’s injuries healed almost instantly, his form regenerating with a fluid, liquid-like motion.

“What the fuck are you…?” The Clan Leader asked, his voice laced with nervousness.

“I’m a human,” Yuan replied calmly, his tone unwavering.

He then turned toward Bai Xutao, still rampaging, and added, “Even if you don’t want my help, I’m going to do something about him, as he consumed what belongs to me.”

“What? Do you even know what he consumed?”

“One of the Nine Divine Supreme’s blood essence, correct? The Heavenly White Tiger—your ancestor’s blood essence.”

“If you already know, then why would you claim it to be yours—” The Clan Leader suddenly halted his words when Yuan turned around to face Bai Xutao.

Before the Clan Leader could continue, Yuan flew at Bai Xutao.

“What are you planning to do to him?!” The Clan Leader hastily chased after him.

“What I was trying to do from the very beginning—kill him.”

“Bastard! If I wanted to kill him, do you really think I would need your help?! I will not allow you to kill him!”

“And how are you going to stop me when you can’t even kill me?”

“You bastard!!!”

The Clan Leader erupted with fury, unleashing his most powerful martial techniques upon Yuan in rapid succession. The sheer force of his attacks obliterated the surrounding area, as if a nuclear blast had detonated at the heart of the valley.

The attack didn’t manage to kill Yuan, but it did attract Bai Xutao’s attention, who immediately pounced at them with surging killing intent.

“Stay away!” The Clan Leader roared before attempting to throw Bai Xutao as far as possible with his spiritual energy.

Though he couldn’t pinpoint it, there was something about Yuan that made him fearful for Bai Xutao’s life.

However, Bai Xutao forcefully stopped himself from being blown away and immediately pounced at them again.

“This stubborn fool!” The Clan Leader roared.

A moment later, Bai Xutao reached Yuan and opened his mouth in an attempt to consume him.

Yuan’s eyes suddenly flickered with immense killing intent, and an indescribable aura exuded from his entire being, halting Bai Xutao’s movements.

Although he had no control over his body, his primal instincts screamed at him to run away before it was too late.

Then, he turned around and started running away, dumbfounding the Clan Leader and the others watching.

Yuan narrowed his eyes and coldly sneered, “Do you really think I will let you escape a third time?”

He raised his hand and made a grasping motion toward Bai Xutao’s escaping figure, instantly halting him.

“I don’t know what you’re planning on doing, but it will not happen on my watch!”

The Clan Leader quickly moved to stand between them before extending his claw-like hands at Yuan’s neck.

Yuan’s silver eyes flickered, and he spoke in a commanding tone, “Stand down.”

In an instant, the Clan Leader’s movements halted.

“W-What is this?!” The Clan Leader exclaimed in shock and confusion.

Yuan didn’t answer him and made another command, “Move aside.”

The Clan Leader tried to resist his command this time, but alas, it was a futile attempt.

“Who the hell are you?!” The Clan Leader roared as his body forcefully listened to Yuan’s command.

Yuan stared at him dead in the eyes and calmly revealed, “Who am I? I am the Immortal Monarch.”

“What…?” The Clan Leader’s eyes widened with shock and disbelief.

From a distance, Bai Ning watched in bewilderment, her jaw dropping to the ground.

chapters.title: Chapter 2088: Forbidden Blood Extraction
chapters.body:
Chapter 2088: Forbidden Blood Extraction

Chapter 2088: Forbidden Blood Extraction

“You… are the Immortal Monarch?” The Clan Leader muttered after snapping out of his daze.

“Do you really expect me to believe such nonsense?”

Yuan smiled and said, “You don’t have to believe me immediately, as I’ll show you evidence in just a moment.”

“A-Are you really going to kill Bai Xutao?” The Clan Leader asked with a deep frown on his face.

“If you had raised him properly, he wouldn’t be in this situation.”

In the next moment, Yuan activated Forbidden Blood Extraction on Bai Xutao.

Instantly, the entire sky darkened, as though the heavens themselves were displeased, casting an ominous shadow over the valley.

Nothing had happened when Yuan used the Forbidden Blood Extraction in the Primal Expanse, for there was no ’heaven’ there to respond.

But now, back in the Nine Heavens, the technique invoked Heaven’s wrath, as it was a heaven-defying power that the heavens did not want to exist.

The dark clouds rumbled ominously, as if the heavens themselves were warning Yuan to stop.

But Yuan paid no heed, his focus unwavering as he continued to activate the Forbidden Blood Extraction, unshaken by the growing threat above him.

“He’s invoked Heaven’s Judgment! And it doesn’t seem to be a small one!” the elders exclaimed in shock, their eyes drawn to the dark clouds swirling above their territory.

“What the hell is he trying to do?!”

As everyone watched in stunned silence, Bai Xutao, still frozen in midair, suddenly screamed in agony. Blood began pouring from his eyes, nose, and ears, staining the air around him.

Seeing that Yuan ignored the warning, the heavens unleashed its judgment upon him, striking with a massive bolt of lightning far more powerful than what Yuan had faced at the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect.

BANG!

The bolt was so thick and colossal that it engulfed both Yuan and the Clan Leader entirely, swallowing them in a blinding flash of raw power.

“Clan Leader!!!” The elders cried out in terror, their eyes wide with fear as they saw him struck by Heaven’s Judgment.

But their panic quickly shifted to relief when the dust cleared, revealing the Clan Leader, who was only slightly injured.

However, their eyes widened in disbelief when they saw Yuan emerge from the chaos, completely unharmed by the devastating attack.

Yuan gazed up at the dark clouds with a mocking smile. “Didn’t you learn anything from the last time? You won’t get rid of me with that pathetic power!”

The dark clouds above him responded instantly, expanding massively, its ominous presence now covering the entire Heavenly White Valley, stretching across hundreds of thousands of miles.

“Heavens!”

Seeing the overwhelming power above, the elders and everyone else scrambled for their lives, including the Clan Leader, who had finally regained control of his body after the lightning strike.

Yet Yuan remained rooted in place, unfazed, as he continued extracting the blood essence from Bai Xutao.

When the process was complete, the heavens responded with its second Heaven’s Judgment—a strike so powerful and devastating that it sent a tremor through the entire Eighth Heaven for a brief moment, startling experts throughout the realm.

As the attack subsided and the dust settled, a massive crater was revealed.

Despite the catastrophic force of the attack, Yuan remained completely unharmed, his Flawless Heavenly Physique rendering Heaven’s Judgment utterly useless against him.

“If this is all you’ve got, I guess you don’t really mind me using this technique.”

Yuan continued the Forbidden Blood Extraction on Bai Xutao, but due to the complexity of Bai Xutao’s cultivation and the volatile nature of his situation, the process was taking far longer than Yuan had anticipated.

Realizing its efforts were futile against Yuan, the Heavens withdrew, and the dark clouds slowly dissipated, leaving behind a clear sky once more.

Seeing this, everyone returned to spectating Yuan’s actions.

“What is he doing to the Young Master?”

“That bastard is forcefully extracting his blood essence!”

“What?! Such a technique exists?!”

“What a fearsome technique!”

The members of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan were stunned and terrified by Yuan’s mythical technique, their minds slowly grasping the reason behind his invocation of Heaven’s Judgment.

“That technique… It’s definitely Forbidden Blood Extraction…” The Eleventh Elder suddenly muttered, his body trembling in fear and awe.

“You know about it?” The others turned to look at him for answers.

“You would know as well if you’d spend some time in our family’s archive,” said the Eleventh Elder. “According to our Ancestor, the Forbidden Blood Extraction is one of the Immortal Monarch’s techniques, used to extract a beast’s blood essence forcefully.”

“Are you saying that he’s the real deal? That the Immortal Monarch has returned?”

The Eleventh Elder nodded nervously, his mind racing as he suddenly recalled the vial of blood he had destroyed.

’Fuck… Did I actually destroy the Immortal Monarch’s blood…?’ The thought struck him like a bolt of lightning, and he desperately hoped it wasn’t true. The guilt would be unbearable if it were.

“Forbidden Blood Extraction…” The Clan Leader stood at a distance, his gaze vacant. Like the Eleventh Elder, he recognized the technique. Yet, even with the undeniable evidence before him, he struggled to accept the truth—that Yuan was the Immortal Monarch. No, he refused to believe it.

If Yuan was truly the Immortal Monarch, then there was no saving his son, Bai Xutao.

Meanwhile, Bai Ning collapsed to her knees, her expression twisted with an emotion that seemed to mirror the anguish of the worst breakup imaginable. As the Immortal Monarch’s most ardent admirer, there was no mistaking his technique.

Yet, much like the Clan Leader, she refused to believe it. For if she did, it would mean that she had fought the Immortal Monarch and sought to end his life on more than one occasion. It was a reality that she didn’t want to accept.

Unfortunately for her, there was simply too much evidence that supported Yuan being the Immortal Monarch.

“What have I done…?” Bai Ning muttered in a low voice as she came to accept reality.

chapters.title: Chapter 2089: Leaving The Heavenly White Valley
chapters.body:
Chapter 2089: Leaving The Heavenly White Valley

Chapter 2089: Leaving the Heavenly White Valley

After many minutes of using Forbidden Blood Extraction on Bai Xutao, Yuan was finally able to extract his blood essence—or more specifically, the blood essence he’d consumed and hadn’t fully refined yet.

Once his blood essence was fully extracted, Bai Xutao, wracked with indescribable pain throughout the ordeal, reverted to his human form and plummeted from the sky, disappearing into the gaping crater below.

Yuan stowed the blood essence away before turning to face the Clan Leader, who was still staring at him with a perplexed face.

“Was that not enough evidence to prove my identity? Should I give you some more evidence?” Yuan asked, making a grasping gesture at him.

The Clan Leader trembled, and after a brief, heavy silence, he suddenly dropped to his knees and bowed deeply before Yuan, kowtowing in the air.

“This humble one greets the Immortal Monarch!” he proclaimed.

As the Clan Leader demonstrated his reverence, the others quickly followed suit, soaring into the air before bowing in unison to greet Yuan.

“The Heavenly White Tiger Clan greets the Immortal Monarch!”

Yuan calmly faced them and spoke, “You’re all probably wondering why I have returned or where I have been since my disappearance. To tell you the truth, I died back then and have been reincarnated.”

“Although I have returned, I do not have any intention of getting involved with your Heavenly White Tiger Clan, especially after learning about how you’ve been using my name and how you’re protecting a filth like Bai Xutao because of his bloodline.”

“However, since your Ancestor was one of my closest companions, I will give you all a chance to change your ways and stop smearing your Ancestor’s legacy. If you can prove to me that you’re not irredeemable, I will personally elevate your bloodline beyond your imagination.”

Everyone there trembled uncontrollably after hearing this, especially the Clan Leader, who was beginning to lose hope.

“Are you willing?” Yuan asked.

“WE ARE WILLING!”

The entire clan responded in unison, their voice loud and clear.

“Good. From this moment onward, I forbid your clan from using my name for your own benefit. Cease the senseless slaughter of your own kin for possessing an inferior bloodline. Moreover, you will take full responsibility and correct every wrong Bai Xutao has brought upon the Nine Heavens. Do I have any objections?”

“WE HAVE NO OBJECTIONS!”

“Clan Leader,” Yuan’s voice rang out, sharp and commanding.

“I am at your command!” The Clan Leader replied without hesitation.

Yuan extended his arm, making a subtle beckoning gesture.

“Do you remember the two reasons I came here?” he asked, his tone carrying an air of authority. “Now that I have dealt with one…”

The Clan Leader immediately realized what Yuan wanted and shouted, “Eleventh Elder! Present the Ancient Seal to the Immortal Monarch!”

The Eleventh Elder reacted swiftly, kneeling before Yuan and retrieving the Ancient Seal with reverence.

“Here is the Ancient Seal, Your Excellence.”

Yuan accepted the Ancient Seal and tossed it inside his spatial ring.

The Eleventh Elder hesitated before speaking, his voice trembling, “Your Excellency, if I may…”

“What is it?” Yuan asked, his tone unwavering.

“Please, forgive my earlier actions. In my ignorance, I destroyed your precious blood… If you wish to take my life as punishment, I accept it without hesitation.”

Yuan then spoke, “I already gave that blood to Bai Ning, so if you’re looking for forgiveness, you’re asking the wrong person.”

“I understand. Forgive me for troubling you, Your Excellency.” The Eleventh Elder quickly withdrew, lowering his head in shame, too embarrassed to lift his gaze.

“Bai Ning.” Yuan suddenly called out to her.

“Y-Yes!”

She instinctively stood up.

“If I recall correctly, you wished to serve me. Has that changed since you last spoke of it?” Yuan asked.

“No, it has not!” she immediately responded.

“I see. Unfortunately, I cannot accept you in your current state. However, I am willing to give you a chance to become someone worthy of serving me.”

“I am willing to do anything! What must I do to prove myself?!”

“That is for you to figure out.”

“For myself to figure out…?” Bai Ning was dumbfounded.

“In the meantime, I will give you a small blessing.”

Yuan bestowed the Immortal Monarch’s Blessing upon her, and in an instant, her bloodline and prowess surged to new heights.

Witnessing this miraculous transformation, those who had still harbored doubts about Yuan’s identity cast aside all suspicion, finally accepting him as the true Immortal Monarch.

“Bai Sulan!” Yuan called out suddenly.

“H-Huh? Me?” Bai Sulan, positioned at the back of the crowd, looked up in surprise, her face filled with astonishment.

Yuan retrieved a glass vial containing his blood and tossed it toward her.

“Catch!”

Bai Sulan fumbled as she hastily caught the vial, almost dropping it in the process.

“That’s my blood,” Yuan explained. “It’ll improve your bloodline and make you stronger. Consider it a token of gratitude for guiding me here.”

All eyes turned to Bai Sulan, their gazes filled with envy, yet none dared to harbor any malicious intent in the presence of Yuan.

“T-Thank you!” she stammered, bowing deeply in gratitude.

“Now that I have accomplished my goals here, I will be taking my leave,” Yuan declared, his voice firm. “If anyone asks, I was never here.”

“W-When will you return to us?!” Bai Ning hurriedly asked, her voice tinged with worry.

“Who knows?” Yuan replied with a faint smile. “But the Heavenly White Tiger Clan better have changed their ways by the next time I visit, or… You don’t want to know what I’ll do.”

“We won’t disappoint you, Immortal Monarch!” the Clan Leader vowed, his voice filled with determination.

Yuan gave a brief nod, then, in the blink of an eye, he departed from the Heavenly White Valley, swiftly disappearing into the horizon.

Shortly after Yuan’s departure, the Clan Leader called for an emergency meeting with the entire clan. With everyone already present, it was the perfect time to discuss the situation at hand and plan for the clan’s future.

chapters.title: Chapter 2090: Refining The Heavenly White Tiger’S Blood Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2090: Refining The Heavenly White Tiger’S Blood Essence

Chapter 2090: Refining the Heavenly White Tiger’s Blood Essence

“Is it really okay for you to leave them just like that?” Feng Yuxiang questioned after they left. “You should’ve punished the Clan Leader and the elders, even if it’s just a little, since they enabled Bai Xutao’s behavior.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Who said I’m leaving?”

“What?” Feng Yuxiang muttered, confusion evident in her voice.

Yuan paused in his tracks and said, “I still have plenty of time before I must return to the Rising Phoenix Clan. I intend to revisit the Heavenly White Tiger Clan before then.”

“What are you going to do in the meantime?”

Yuan retrieved the Heavenly White Tiger’s Blood Essence and said, “I’m going to refine this blood essence.”

“So we’re going to return to my Immortal Cave right now?”

Yuan shook his head and said, “I have another place in mind, and it’s much closer.”

Some time later, they returned to where the Phantasm Serpent’s Nest once existed.

“Isn’t this…”

“I’m going to refine the blood essence in that serpent’s lair,” he said.

“In that case, I’ll guard the entrance,” said Feng Yuxiang as she emerged from his Dantian to stand guard.

“Thanks.”

Yuan dug a small hole and disappeared into the ground.

Once he returned to the underground area with the sealed gate, Yuan spoke to the Outer God on the other side, “I’m going to cultivate here for a bit, so don’t disturb me, or I’ll leave you in there forever.”

“…”

No response.

Yuan sat down in the center of the cave and retrieved the Heavenly White Tiger’s Blood Essence a moment later. Once he prepared himself, he removed the seal and poured the blood essence into his mouth.

<You have consumed the Heavenly White Tiger’s Blood Essence>

<Completed Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Completed Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun to refine the Heavenly White Tiger’s Blood Essence>

Upon consuming the blood essence, Yuan unleashed a deafening roar, one that reverberated through the air like the mighty roar of a tiger.

An immense torrent of spiritual energy suddenly surged forth, so powerful that even Yuan struggled to absorb it. The overwhelming flow threatened to tear his body apart, pushing his Dantian to the brink of destruction.

To prevent his body from being overwhelmed, Yuan had no choice but to expend the spiritual energy within him constantly. Fortunately, there was an indestructible gate near him, allowing him to unleash as much spiritual energy as he wanted.

Though the process was agonizing, it was far more manageable than when he had to refine the Primordial Phoenix’s Bloodline.

Time seemed to fly, and before Yuan realized it, half a year had passed since he began refining the Heavenly White Tiger’s Blood Essence.

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline evolution has progressed slightly>

<Progress: 33%>

<Your Immortal Monarch’s techniques have improved>

<You have acquired the power of a Heavenly White Tiger through its Blood Essence>

<You have learned ’Heavenly White Tiger Transformation’>

<Your Dantian has been strengthened, evolving into a Heavenly Dantian>

<Due to your Heavenly Dantian, your cultivation speed has increased by 500,000%>

<You have met the requirements for a breakthrough>

<Your cultivation has reached Golden Immortal>

Yuan opened his eyes, and a faint golden glow radiated from within.

<Heavenly White Tiger Transformation activated>

Yuan’s aura instantly shifted, becoming primal and overwhelmingly domineering, like a predator reigning unchallenged at the pinnacle of the food chain.

Half of his jet-black hair turned stark white, manifesting the signature trait of the Heavenly White Tiger.

Beyond the changes in his appearance, his Heavenly Dantian also strengthened, allowing him to absorb spiritual energy twice as fast.

At his current state, it was essentially impossible for Yuan to run out of spiritual energy if he didn’t exhaust all of it in a single attack. Even if he did, it would replenish in the blink of an eye.

Yuan left the underground cave shortly after refining the blood essence and returned to Feng Yuxiang.

“It only took you half a year this time, huh?” she remarked after seeing him in his Heavenly White Tiger transformation.

“Yes. That said, the Heavenly White Tiger’s blood essence wasn’t as strong as the Primordial Phoenix’s.”

“Congratulations on your breakthrough. Now, you’re only one level away from reaching True Immortal and catching up to me,” said Feng Yuxiang.

“Have you fully recovered your cultivation?” Yuan asked her since her cultivation had remained at the True Immortal level for some time now.

She nodded, “Yes, this was my peak cultivation before I got cursed, but I can feel myself inching toward God Ascension.”

“I see…”

“So, are we going to return to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan now?” she then asked.

Yuan nodded.

“Yes, let’s see how much they’ve changed in half a year.”

Once Feng Yuxiang entered his Dantian, Yuan made his way back to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan.

“Wow, what happened to your Dantian? It feels so different now,” Feng Yuxiang remarked.

“It evolved after I refined the blood essence. It’s a Heavenly Dantian now, whatever that is.”

“I’ve also never heard of such a thing,” she said.

However, Yu Ning spoke up, “A Heavenly Dantian was a legend during the Primordial Era. It’s said to be a superior Dantian, a concept that has only ever existed in theory. You know how Body Refiners temper their physiques? Some believed they could temper their Dantian, but it was considered incredibly dangerous, as even the slightest mistake could cripple one’s cultivation.”

“A legend, huh? Well, it’s no longer just a theory since we’re living in one right now,” Feng Yuxiang chuckled.

Some time later, Yuan returned to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan. The place was nearly unrecognizable, having undergone drastic restoration since the devastation he left behind half a year ago.

Yuan activated Shadow Veil, his form fading into the shadows as he silently infiltrated their territory. Once inside, Yuan deactivated Shadow Veil and began wandering the territory in the guise of a Heavenly White Tiger.

Though he had disguised his appearance, he couldn’t change his overwhelming, domineering aura, which immediately drew the attention of everyone nearby, especially the women, who gazed at him with passionate looks on their faces.

chapters.title: Chapter 2091: Returning To The Heavenly White Tiger Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2091: Returning To The Heavenly White Tiger Clan

Chapter 2091: Returning to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan

“Hey, handsome. Where did you come from? I haven’t seen you around here before,” one of the female tigers said, her movements teasing and suggestive as she approached Yuan.

Yuan calmly faced her and replied, “That’s probably because I haven’t returned to this place in a few hundred years. What happened here? It’s completely different from before.”

“That’s… We decided to renovate the place,” answered the female.

The female decided not to mention the Immortal Monarch even though Yuan was a Heavenly White Tiger at this moment, as it was essentially forbidden to mention the Immortal Monarch to keep his return a secret.

“Are you sure? I feel like you’re not telling me everything.” Yuan said, sounding somewhat aggressive, to increase the pressure.

The woman then said, “If you give me a good time, I’ll consider telling you about it.”

Yuan let out a quiet sigh. Without another word, he activated Shadow Veil and vanished like a ghost, disappearing before the stunned onlookers could react.

After leaving, he went deeper into the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s territory. Although everything appeared normal at a glance, Yuan could tell that something was off about the atmosphere, as it felt unnaturally heavy.

’Everyone seems nervous. They’re probably still having a difficult time accepting the fact that the Immortal Monarch has returned,’ Feng Yuxiang remarked after seeing the situation.

’Or, there could be another reason why they’re so nervous,’ said Yuan.

As Yuan wandered the streets, he continued to draw attention despite his efforts to suppress his aura.

As for those who were bold enough to approach him, he would question them about the state of their clan and if anything weird had happened recently. Of course, none of them would reveal anything about the Immortal Monarch, only mentioning that the clan had decided to change.

“What? The clan decided to change? Where did this come from? It’s hard to believe.”

“It’s true. If you want to know more, you should speak with the elders,” said the woman Yuan was speaking to.

Since he wasn’t having any luck with the ordinary members of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan, Yuan decided to approach those of higher status. He made his way to the center of their residential area, where the most talented individuals and those with the purest bloodlines resided.

Once a strictly forbidden zone for anyone unqualified, Yuan was pleasantly surprised to find that no one even attempted to stop him. In fact, the area now resembled a tourist attraction, with people freely coming and going, many of whom had never been allowed to set foot there before.

The heavy, oppressive atmosphere that lingered in the outer areas was absent here. Instead, the people seemed more at ease, relaxed and carefree, as if untouched by the concerns weighing down the rest of the clan.

Of course, just like the outer areas, Yuan immediately attracted the people there with his presence.

When one of them approached him, Yuan made some small talk before asking about the situation.

“I haven’t been to the clan for hundreds of years, and I couldn’t help but notice how different this place has become. What happened while I was away?”

“The Clan Leader and the Elders received enlightenment and realized they had been managing the clan improperly, so they decided to change things for the better.”

“What enlightened them? I doubt they woke up one day and decided to change the clan so drastically,” Yuan continued to ask.

“Since you’re part of the clan, you’ll eventually learn the truth, but it’s forbidden for us ordinary members to reveal it.”

“Then, do you know why the atmosphere in the outer areas is so heavy?”

“They’re probably struggling to trust the Clan Leader, especially after so many generations of being treated poorly. I don’t blame them. I was like that too for the first two months. The change was simply too sudden.”

“Still struggling to adapt, huh? What about the Clan Leader? What’s he doing these days?”

“Who knows? Ever since that incident half a year ago, he hasn’t shown himself. But that’s nothing new—he was rarely seen even before everything changed.”

The residents in this area were far more willing to speak, yet not a single one mentioned the return of the Immortal Monarch.

’They’re really keeping a tight lip about your appearance, huh?’ Feng Yuxiang chuckled.

Once he was satisfied, Yuan left to find Bai Sulan, but since he didn’t know where she lived, he had to ask for directions.

“Young Lady Sulan? She’s currently living within the main household with the Clan Leader and the elders, so you won’t be able to meet her without their permission.”

“Why did she move there, you ask? Well… let’s just say her bloodline has awakened, and now she’s the clan’s top priority.”

Yuan casually dropped a name to gauge their response. “Really? That’s amazing. Bai Xutao must be pretty upset about that.”

As expected, the moment the name left his lips, the people around him exchanged uneasy glances, their expressions turning awkward and tense.

“What’s wrong?” Yuan asked after seeing their reaction.

“The Young Master—No, that troublemaker is no longer with us. Apparently, he died in an accident,” one of them said.

“He’s dead?” Yuan’s eyes widened, pretending to be shocked by this news. “How did he die?”

They shook their heads and shrugged their shoulders.

“I see. What about Bai Ning?”

“She’s also at the main household. I heard she went into closed cultivation.”

“Thank you.”

Since there were no other places he could go to investigate, Yuan decided to head to the main household to find the Clan Leader and the Elders.

However, unlike entering the center of their residential area, it was still prohibited for ordinary members to approach the main household without permission.

“Stop right there. Who are you, and what are you doing here?” The guard, a Bronze Immortal, questioned Yuan.

Yuan looked at the guard and spoke a single word in a commanding tone, “Sit.”

Instantly, the guard sat down on the ground with a dazed look on his face.

“You… you can’t be…” he swallowed hard, his eyes as wide as saucers.

chapters.title: Chapter 2092: Returning To The Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2092: Returning To The Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)

Chapter 2092: Returning to the Heavenly White Tiger Clan(2)

Still in his Heavenly White Tiger form, Yuan offered the guard a calm smile. “I am who you think I am. Do me a favor and keep it quiet, will you? I don’t want to cause a panic.”

The guard gave a silent nod, his face pale with disbelief.

“Thanks,” Yuan said before turning to approach the massive residence before him.

Even after Yuan had disappeared from his sight, the guard remained seated on the ground, motionless, as if he had forgotten how to stand.

Before entering the residence, Yuan activated Shadow Veil and completely erased his presence.

Then, once inside, he began wandering the place.

After wandering for several minutes, Yuan noticed several powerful presences gathered in one room, so he approached the room and eavesdropped from outside.

“It’s been half a year since the Immortal Monarch left. Do you think he’ll return any time soon?”

Yuan knew the people gathered inside the room were the elders even before they spoke based on their aura.

“Who knows? He could be coming here right now for all we know,” one of them casually remarked, unaware that he’d hit the nail on the head.

“I just hope that he’s satisfied with the changes. It wasn’t easy to suddenly change rules that had been in place for many generations.”

“If not, we’re probably screwed. The Immortal Monarch could kill all of us with just his words alone.”

“There’s no way he’d do something like that, right? According to historical records, the Immortal Monarch worked to protect the beast race from humans and from each other. He’s essentially the reason beasts and humans can co-exist in this day and age,” said another.

“He’s saved some and completely eradicated others, especially those who defied him. Some even considered him a tyrant,” the Eleventh Elder revealed, his voice carrying a clear tremor of fear.

“In that case, aren’t we defying him right now? After all, we—”

“Enough.” The Clan Leader’s voice suddenly interrupted. “Nobody is to mention this subject again, especially not before the Immortal Monarch.”

“But if he finds out…”

“I said enough! I will figure things out myself!” the Clan Leader snapped.

Yuan narrowed his eyes slightly after hearing this.

“What do you think they’re trying to hide from you?” Feng Yuxiang asked, her voice full of curiosity.

“Who knows? But I will figure it out.”

Yuan stopped listening to them shortly after when their conversation drifted off topic, and he continued to wander the residence.

Sometime later, Yuan stopped before another room when he sensed a familiar presence within, and it was none other than Bai Sulan.

Yuan knocked on the door after realizing it was her.

“W-Who’s there?!” Bai Sulan’s terrified voice rang out a moment later, as if she’d just seen a ghost.

With Yuan’s Shadow Veil still active, she couldn’t sense anyone outside her door, despite the unmistakable sound of knocking. In her panic, she assumed it could only be a ghost.

“It’s me,” Yuan calmly replied.

It took a moment, but Bai Sulan recognized his voice. Although she couldn’t believe it, she still had to confirm it.

Once she opened the door and saw Yuan standing outside, her eyes widened with shock and confusion.

“Who are you?” she asked, not recognizing his new appearance.

Yuan deactivated his Heavenly White Tiger and returned to his original appearance.

“Handsome lit—” Bai Sulan quickly covered her mouth after recalling his true identity.

Yuan smiled and said, “Just call me whatever you feel comfortable.”

“B-But you’re the Immortal Monarch… You’re our Ancestor’s Master…” she spoke nervously.

“So what?”

“I might get punished for not respecting you.”

“I gave you permission, so who would dare to punish you?”

“If you say so, handsome big brother…” Bai Sulan said.

“Can I come inside? I came here secretly, and I don’t want the others to find out yet.”

She nodded and gestured for him to enter her room.

“How come I cannot sense your presence even though I am staring right at you?” she asked once he closed the door.

“I am using a technique to hide my presence.”

“I see… Then why are you hiding from the others?”

“I want to see if the Heavenly White Tiger Clan—the Clan Leader and the elders actually acted on their promise and changed the way they managed the clan.”

“In that case, you don’t have to worry,” Bai Sulan said reassuringly. “Things have definitely changed for the better. While a hierarchy still exists within the clan, it’s no longer as rigid, and we’re no longer executed for failing our trials.”

“That’s good to hear. What about you? Did anyone try to steal the blood I gave you?” Yuan asked.

Bai Sulan let out a soft chuckle. “No one’s dared to take it by force, but plenty have tried to convince me to share it, offering treasures and all sorts of things. Naturally, I refused.”

“By the way, is this really your blood?” Bai Sulan asked, retrieving an empty glass vial. “Why does it taste so good? I meant to try just a drop, but before I knew it, I’d finished the whole thing.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Want to confirm it?”

She nodded eagerly.

Yuan retrieved a drop of his own blood before her eyes and flicked it into her mouth.

“Wow! This tastes even better than before!” she exclaimed.

Since Yuan’s bloodline had advanced after refining the Heavenly White Tiger’s Blood Essence, it was only natural that the quality of his blood had improved as well. Of course, Bai Sulan wasn’t aware of this fact.

“How does this work? Why does it taste so good?” Bai Sulan then asked.

“I don’t know, but it likely has something to do with my bloodline.”

“Do you do something special to give it this taste? Can you give out as much blood as you want? Is there a limit?” She then bombarded him with several questions.

Yuan raised an eyebrow and pondered silently for a moment before responding, “That’s a good question, but I’ve never considered it, nor have I ever reached a limit.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2093: The Clan Leader’S Secret
chapters.body:
Chapter 2093: The Clan Leader’S Secret

Chapter 2093: The Clan Leader’s Secret

“You’ve never reached a limit? So you basically have an unlimited supply of blood?” Bai Sulan muttered, a strange flicker in her eyes. Unbeknownst to her, she was licking her lips, looking like a child staring longingly at a jar of candy.

Yuan smiled at her reaction and said calmly, “No, it just means I rarely give out my blood. Even if it were unlimited, giving away too much wouldn’t be wise, considering the powerful effects it carries.”

The Immortal Monarch’s blood was precious for a reason. If Yuan were to give it freely to anyone who asked, it could lead to unimaginable consequences, disrupting the balance of power across the Nine Heavens.

“My bloodline awakened and became the most powerful within the clan after consuming that little amount you gave me. I cannot imagine what could happen if it were accessible to everyone…” Bai Sulan mumbled.

She looked at him thoughtfully and asked, “Since your blood is so powerful and precious, I imagine you’ve had more than a few troublemakers trying to get their hands on it, haven’t you?”

To beasts, Yuan’s blood was the most coveted treasure, and their greed was no different from that of humans. Some would go to any lengths to claim it for themselves.

Yuan let out a calm chuckle and said, “That’s right. Back in the Immortal Monarch’s Era, I was constantly dealing with beasts trying to steal my blood. They’d ambush me without warning, hoping to claim even a single drop.”

“Because of that, I had to learn how to control my blood, ensuring it would only be effective when I allowed it to. It took some time, but I eventually managed to do it. Of course, that didn’t stop the troublemakers from trying anyway.”

This meant that even if Yuan were injured and bled in a battle, the blood he shed wouldn’t carry the Immortal Monarch’s power.

After chatting for a little longer, Yuan asked, “Do you know where I can find Bai Ning?”

“Senior Sister Ning? She left the clan a month ago,” she replied.

Before Yuan could ask, Bai Sulan continued, “She had a falling out with the Eleventh Elder, who destroyed your blood. Of course, that wasn’t her only reason for leaving. She said something about exploring the Nine Heavens to seek enlightenment.”

“I see…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan asked, “On my way here, I overheard your elders and the Clan Leader’s meeting. Apparently, they’re hiding something from me. Do you know what they’re hiding?”

Bai Sulan raised her eyebrows and looked genuinely puzzled.

“No,” she eventually shook her head.

“Is that so…”

“Do you want me to find out what they’re hiding?” Bai Sulan suddenly asked.

Yuan quickly responded, “No, there’s no need for you to do that. I’ll figure it out myself.”

“Okay.”

“Anyway, I guess it’s about time I check up with them.”

Yuan suddenly deactivated Shadow Veil, allowing his presence to spread throughout the residence.

Not far away, the Clan Leader and the elders instantly froze when they sensed his appearance out of thin air like a ghost.

“T-This presence! It can’t be!”

“The Immortal Monarch has returned!”

They hastily rushed out of the room, even breaking down the door in the process, and followed the presence to Bai Sulan’s room.

When they arrived, Yuan was calmly standing inside.

“We greet the Immortal Monarch!”

They all lowered their bodies and bowed to him.

“It’s been half a year.” Yuan faced them with a calm expression on his face.

“Did you just arrive?” the Clan Leader asked.

“No,” Yuan replied calmly. “I’ve been here for some time. I chose not to announce my return since I wanted to observe the current state of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan first.”

The elders trembled at these words.

“We’ve implemented many changes already, but some require more time to take full effect. If there’s anything you find lacking, please let us know and we’ll address it immediately,” said the Clan Leader.

Yuan looked at him and spoke calmly, “I’m not asking you to change the entire clan or force yourselves to live a completely different life. But understand this—no matter how much you refine the surface, if the core remains rotten, it’s all meaningless.”

At that moment, Yuan activated Immortal Monarch’s Supremacy. His eyes turned silver, and his presence flooded the entire space with an overwhelming pressure. Then, in a firm and commanding voice, he said, “If you think you can hide anything from me, just know that I need only speak a few words, and you’ll reveal everything on your own. I’ll give you a chance to speak willingly now. Is there anything you want to tell me?”

The elders trembled with fear, instantly turning their eyes to the Clan Leader, awaiting his response. It was clear that Yuan had somehow learned of their secret.

Realizing that silence would be futile—and that one of the elders would surely speak up if he didn’t—he let out a deep sigh and said, “There is one thing I need to tell you. But… it’ll be easier if I show you instead.”

“Please, follow me,” the Clan Leader said before turning and walking away.

Yuan and the elders followed in silence, their expressions solemn. Driven by curiosity, Bai Sulan quietly trailed behind them.

A few minutes later, they stopped before another room. However, this room had several formations on the sealed door, preventing anyone from looking inside.

’Whatever they’re trying to hide from you, it must be quite serious.’ Yu Ning remarked after seeing the formations.

After taking a moment to undo the formations, the Clan Leader spoke cautiously, “Immortal Monarch, please understand that this wasn’t done to defy you…”

“Spare the excuses for later,” Yuan interrupted, striding forward and pushing the door open without hesitation.

As Yuan stepped into the room and took in the sight before him, his eyes widened in surprise, a look of genuine amazement spreading across his face.

Lying peacefully on the bed was none other than Bai Xutao. Though unconscious, he was still breathing. His appearance had also changed drastically, now looking like a shriveled old man on his deathbed, a far cry from the healthy young figure he once was.

chapters.title: Chapter 2094: Bai Xutao’S Persistence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2094: Bai Xutao’S Persistence

Chapter 2094: Bai Xutao’s Persistence

“That’s… Bai Xutao?”

At first glance, Yuan didn’t recognize Bai Xutao due to the drastic change in his appearance, but even in its weakened state, his aura was unmistakable. It was undeniably Bai Xutao.

“No way… he actually survived? Maybe he’s not a Heavenly White Tiger after all, and his true bloodline might just be that of a cockroach,” Feng Yuxiang remarked, sounding utterly dumbfounded by his persistence that rivaled cockroaches.

’I only extracted his ancestor’s blood essence, so that must be what kept him alive. Still, even then, it’s nothing short of a miracle,’ Yuan explained to her.

Yuan turned to look at the Clan Leader and the elders, who were still standing outside the room with guilty expressions on their faces.

“Explain yourselves,” Yuan said.

The Clan Leader spoke immediately, his tone heavy with reluctance. “We all believed he had perished. But during the restoration of our territory, we found Bai Xutao barely clinging to life. We thought about letting him die, but… my fatherly instincts compelled me to save him. Though he’s still breathing, he hasn’t woken up since we found him.”

“Your fatherly instincts?” Yuan sneered coldly, his gaze turning to the Clan Leader with disdain. “That’s rich, coming from someone who’s killed countless of their own children simply because their bloodlines didn’t meet your standards.”

“T-That’s…” The Clan Leader fell silent, unable to refute Yuan’s sharp rebuke.

“What’s so special about Bai Xutao that you decided to save him, despite everything that’s happened?” Yuan pressed, his voice cold. “Looking at him now, his injuries are likely permanent. He’ll be crippled for the rest of his life. Is he really worth the risk?”

Before the Clan Leader could respond, Yuan approached him and continued, “What if I were to say that I will eradicate the entire Heavenly White Tiger Clan over this? Have you thought of this possibility? I doubt you didn’t. Yet, you still decided to take the risk.”

“I have no excuses…” The Clan Leader sighed, his voice heavy with defeat.

Yuan turned his gaze to the elders, his tone sharp. “You’re no better for allowing him to act so selfishly. You’re supposed to be the elders of this clan. Where is your authority? Are you only capable of flaunting it when trying to suppress others?”

“We have no excuses.”

The elders lowered their heads in shame.

“I’m deeply disappointed in you all,” Yuan said, his voice cold. “And don’t misunderstand. This isn’t about the fact that you allowed Bai Xutao to live. I’m actually impressed by how quickly you’ve changed, at least on the surface. But inside, you’re still selfish, and when the time comes, you wouldn’t hesitate to sacrifice your own people.”

Yuan began walking toward the door, his voice steady and firm. “Until your Heavenly White Tiger Clan shows real change, I won’t be able to accept you. And don’t think you can win my favor by killing Bai Xutao now.”

“In fact, it’s mostly the leadership that’s the problem. I could kill all of you and replace you with more competent individuals, but I won’t do that.”

The Clan Leader and the elders trembled at Yuan’s words, but what truly caused their hearts to sink wasn’t the threat itself—it was the crushing realization that they had lost the chance to receive the Immortal Monarch’s aid.

“As for Bai Xutao…” Yuan continued. “Since he’s managed to survive time and time again, I’m sure fate hasn’t finished with him yet. So, I won’t kill him—provided he remains in this territory for the rest of his life.”

As Yuan walked out of the room, Bai Sulan suddenly grabbed his robes and asked with a saddened face, “Will I ever see you again?”

“If fate allows it,” Yuan replied as he rubbed her head.

“Okay…” Bai Sulan nodded her head as she released his robes.

Yuan turned to look at the others and warned them in a cold voice, “I hope you won’t be foolish enough to reveal my identity for revenge.”

“W-We wouldn’t dare!” They quickly shook their heads.

Then, Yuan activated Shadow Veil and disappeared from their sight like a ghost.

Although the Clan Leader and the elders were desperate to stop him, they hesitated, unwilling to risk angering him further, fearing that it might prompt him to change his mind and wipe them all out.

“Are we really leaving this time?” Feng Yuxiang asked Yuan after they left the Heavenly White Tiger Clan for a second time.

He nodded, “Yes, we’re going to return to the Rising Phoenix Clan now.”

“Alright. Although you didn’t kill the Clan Leader or the elders, I feel like this is a more fitting punishment for them since they will have to live the rest of their lives with regret. Hopefully nothing will happen to Bai Sulan, though.”

Yuan then reassured her, “Nothing will happen to her. With Bai Xutao in that condition and Bai Ning leaving the clan, she’s their only hope now.”

“I see. That makes sense. There’s also that little surprise you left behind for her.” Feng Yuxiang chuckled.

Shortly after Yuan left, Bai Sulan quietly returned to her room while the Clan Leader and the elders started another meeting to discuss their future.

When she returned to her room, Bai Sulan was surprised to see a wooden box placed on her bed, something she wasn’t familiar with and hadn’t existed before.

When she opened the box, her eyes widened with shock and disbelief. Inside, there were several glass vials containing blood.

Although no note was left behind and they were sealed, Bai Sulan instantly recognized whose blood it was.

She held it close to her chest and mumbled with a sweet smile on her face, “Once I become a capable adult, I will definitely come find you, handsome big brother.”

Then, she stowed the glass vials away and left her room to train.

After several days of intense discussion, the Clan Leader and the elders decided to adapt to the new rules and continue working towards improving their clan, hoping that, one day, Yuan might return and give them another chance.

chapters.title: Chapter 2095: A Detour
chapters.body:
Chapter 2095: A Detour

Chapter 2095: A Detour

After departing from the Heavenly White Tiger Clan, Yuan began his journey back to the Rising Phoenix Clan, despite still having several months left before the one-year promise.

However, along the way, he was forced to halt when he spotted two groups of cultivators clashing in the middle of nowhere.

Typically, Yuan wouldn’t have paid such a scene any mind, as it had nothing to do with him. But this time, something caught his attention, as the attire of these cultivators looked familiar, compelling him to stay and observe.

“Hm? Those robes… Aren’t they the Demon Sealing Clan’s robes?” Feng Yuxiang commented after seeing them.

“Yes, and the other one belongs to the Demon Sealing Grotto,” Yuan confirmed.

The Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto members noticed Yuan shortly after he arrived, but immediately stopped paying attention to him once they realized that he wasn’t reinforcement from the other side and continued their fight.

These members were mostly Bronze Immortals, with a few still in the Divine Realm.

“I have been getting really tired of you guys lately! Do you think you’re better than the Demon Sealing Clan because you’re slightly better at controlling Demon Sealing Aura?!”

“Hah! What Demon Sealing Clan? You guys are nothing but old news! There’s a reason why most of your original members have left and joined us at the Demon Sealing Grotto instead!”

“Right?! You’re just a bunch of envious bastards! That’s why you schemed against us during the Supreme Demon Sealer qualifications!”

“What scheme?! You’re just slandering us at this point!”

The battle intensified with each passing moment.

“Aren’t you going to stop them?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

Yuan replied calmly, “They may be fighting, but I don’t sense any real killing intent. They’ll be fine—it’s more like watching a bunch of kids squabbling.”

Suddenly, a powerful presence descended upon the place, instantly halting everyone’s movement.

“What do you idiots think you’re doing?! Stop this immediately!” A familiar voice resounded the very next moment, and a beautiful woman appeared before them.

It was none other than Yan Hara.

“Senior Yan, they’re the ones who started this fight!” said one of the Demon Sealing Clan members.

“That’s right! They attacked us on sight! We were only defending ourselves!”

Yan Hara turned to look at the Demon Sealing Grottos with a frown.

“Explain yourselves,” she demanded.

“What is there to explain? Your Demon Sealing Clan started this.”

“…”

Instead of being angry, Yan Hara merely said, “Since nobody was seriously injured, I’ll let you leave. Get lost before I change my mind.”

“Hmph.”

The Demon Sealing Grotto did not stay and quickly left the scene.

“Senior Yan! Why did you let them go that easily?! They’ll think they can bully us and get away with it!”

“We need to fight back, or they’ll just keep doing it!”

Yan Hara looked at them and sighed. “Regardless of how you feel about each other, with the recent movements from the demon worshippers, we’ll need to stand together. Fighting among ourselves won’t just weaken us—it will endanger the rest of the world.”

“So are we just supposed to endure it?!”

Yan Hara narrowed her eyes and said coldly, “Do you really take me for a fool? What are you doing near the Demon Sealing Grotto’s headquarters? If you expect me to believe you came here unaware, I’ve got bad news for you.”

The Demon Sealing Clan’s members instantly panicked, their composure unraveling under her piercing words. Though the Demon Sealing Grotto had attacked first, they had clearly provoked the conflict by deliberately trespassing into their territory.

In other words, they were asking for trouble.

“B-But—”

“Enough with your excuses. Get out of here before I get angry for real,” Yan Hara interrupted, raising her voice.

The Demon Sealing Clan members didn’t dare to linger any longer and swiftly fled the scene.

Once the others had left, Yan Hara finally turned her attention to the presence observing from afar.

At first, she didn’t recognize Yuan, as his aura had changed so drastically. But the moment she saw his familiar, handsome face, her eyes widened in shock and disbelief.

“Y-Yuan?! What are you doing here?!” Yan Hara exclaimed as he approached her.

Yuan greeted her with a calm smile, “I could say the same to you. What are you doing out here in the middle of nowhere? I’m on my way to the Rising Phoenix Clan.”

“The Rising Phoenix Clan…? Well, I am on my way to visit the Demon Sealing Grotto for a meeting. Since Qian Chu is still away, I have to take his place.”

Yuan raised his eyebrows and asked, “Don’t they have the Grand Elders to take his spot?”

“Yes, but my position within the clan has reached the point where even the Grand Elders are wary of me. In fact, they’re the ones who told me to go in Qian Chu’s place. They’re probably hoping that I’d screw up or something.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before responding, “Do you think I can go with you?”

“Eh? I don’t mind, but don’t you have business with the Rising Phoenix Clan?”

He nodded and explained, “Yes, but I still have about half a year to spare.”

“If you say so.”

Thus, Yuan made a detour to visit the Demon Sealing Grotto with Yan Hara.

“When did you arrive at the Eighth Heaven?” Yan Hara asked.

“Very recently.”

“Then what have you been up to?”

“I was just at the Heavenly White Tiger Clan.”

“You have ties to both the Rising Phoenix Clan and the Heavenly White Tiger Clan? Those are incredibly exclusive places—neither of which usually welcome humans.”

Yuan smiled and said, “I’ll tell you about it someday.”

A few days later, they arrived at the Demon Sealing Grotto’s headquarters—a hidden stronghold that existed within its own separate dimension. The portal leading to it was tucked away in a remote city deep within the wilderness.

“I’m Yan Hara of the Demon Sealing Clan, here on behalf of Qian Chu, who’s currently unavailable,” she said, presenting her medallion to the guards as proof of her identity.

“We have been expecting you, Master Demon Sealer Yan Hara, but who is that next to you?”

The guard pointed at Yuan with his gaze.

chapters.title: Chapter 2096: Visiting The Demon Sealing Grotto
chapters.body:
Chapter 2096: Visiting The Demon Sealing Grotto

Chapter 2096: Visiting the Demon Sealing Grotto

When the guard questioned about Yuan, Yan Hara calmly responded, “He’s an Elite Demon Sealer of the Demon Sealing Clan and my disciple. Is there any problem with him being present?”

“Well, the higher-ups will be there, so it might not be a good idea to bring him with you,” said the guard. “The pressure might be too much for him to handle.”

“You don’t have to worry about me. If I get overwhelmed, I won’t blame anyone,” Yuan said with a calm smile on his face.

“You heard him. I also won’t say anything,” said Yan Hara.

“In that case…”

The guard silently stepped aside and activated the array behind him, revealing the portal to the Demon Sealing Grotto’s headquarters.

Yan Hara entered first, with Yuan following closely behind.

On the other side, they were greeted by the sight of a city—more refined and orderly than the one outside, and the entire place was suffused with a dense Demon Sealing Aura.

“So this is the Demon Sealing Grotto, huh?” Yuan muttered as he looked around.

Yan Hara looked at him and asked, “This is your first time visiting this place, right?”

“That’s right.”

“This is where most of their demon sealers train,” Yan Hara explained. “Unlike our Demon Sealing Clan, whose members are scattered across the Nine Heavens, the Demon Sealing Grotto keeps most of its members here. They rarely leave—only venturing out when necessary to investigate or eliminate demon worshippers and demons.”

Yuan smiled and said, “So, in other words, the Demon Sealing Grotto takes demon sealing far more seriously than the Demon Sealing Clan? Sounds like comparing a full-time professional to someone working part-time.”

“T-That’s…” Yan Hara was momentarily speechless at his comparison, but upon reflection, she understood his point.

Compared to the Demon Sealing Grotto, the Demon Sealing Clan was indeed far more relaxed and casual. For many within the clan, being a Demon Sealer was a secondary pursuit—something they practiced alongside their main cultivation path. In contrast, those from the Demon Sealing Grotto treated it as their primary purpose, dedicating their lives wholly to the art of sealing demons.

This was also the reason why the Demon Sealing Clan had declined, no longer able to compare to the Demon Sealing Grotto in recent times.

Yuan wanted to explore the place for a bit, but shortly after their arrival, a member of the Demon Sealing Grotto appeared to escort them to their destination.

Some time later, they arrived before a grand white building that exuded a formal and solemn atmosphere, clearly a place reserved for important meetings and official gatherings.

“The Elders are waiting inside,” said the escort as he opened the door for them.

Yuan and Yan Hara stepped into a vast chamber lined with hundreds of seats, most of which were already occupied by members of the Demon Sealing Grotto.

Every Demon Sealer within the chamber was at least a Golden Immortal, their very presence radiating a dense Demon Sealing Aura that seemed to suppress the air itself.

“Please, proceed to the front,” the escort said, gesturing toward two distinct seats set apart from the rest—clearly positioned as the center of attention. However, one of the two seats was already occupied by an old man radiating a profound aura, leaving Yuan to stand beside Yan Hara as she took the remaining seat.

“Welcome to our humble estate, Demon Sealer Yan Hara.”

The old man seated beside her immediately started the conversation.

“It’s been a while, Senior Zhou,” Yan Hara respectfully greeted him.

“This old man is the Demon Sealing Grotto’s leader, Zhou Yanfei,” she revealed to Yuan using voice transmission.

The old man noticed that she was using voice transmission and turned to look at Yuan.

“I wasn’t expecting a second guest today,” he said.

Though the old man released no pressure, the atmosphere in the room grew oppressively heavy, and Yuan could distinctly feel the weight of hundreds of powerful gazes bearing down on him.

Yet, Yuan remained tranquil, looking completely unaffected by it.

“He is my disciple and an Elite Demon Sealer,” Yan Hara stated calmly. “Since he’s never visited before, I brought him along.”

“Quite the bold decision, Demon Sealer Yan Hara,” Zhou Yanfei responded coolly. “If I were in your position, I wouldn’t risk bringing someone who might become a liability to a place like this.”

Yan Hara smiled faintly and asked, “Why not? Have I walked into a trap? Will you strike us down now that we are in your territory?”

“Unlike a certain someone, we are not so uncivilized,” Zhou Yanfei shook his head, and he continued, “And even if we were, we’re not foolish enough to start infighting while the threat of the demons looms over us all.”

“So, what is this all about? I’m only here to act in Qian Chu’s stead, since he’s unavailable,” Yan Hara asked.

Zhou Yanfei’s expression darkened the moment Qian Chu’s name was mentioned.

“The world teeters on the brink of chaos, and that scheming bastard has vanished without a trace for years now,” he said coldly. “I cannot fathom why the Demon Sealing Clan chose such a bastard as their leader.”

Yan Hara shrugged and calmly responded, “Beats me. I was just an ordinary member who recently joined when he was appointed as the leader.”

Zhou Yanfei sneered coldly. “You incompetent fools are dragging the Demon Sealing Clan into ruin.”

He paused, his gaze sharp. “Our original intention was to force Qian Chu to reveal why he’s targeting our Demon Sealing Grotto, but since he’s absent, our attention will shift elsewhere today.”

“Demon Sealer Yan Hara,” Zhou Yanfei continued, his tone edged with curiosity. “Your recent activities have greatly intrigued me. With how you’ve been quietly rallying members of the Demon Sealing Clan to your side and raising your position within the clan, are you aiming to challenge Qian Chu for his position?”

“Maybe I am. Maybe I’m not. What’s it to you?” Yan Hara replied with a calm smile.

Zhou Yanfei’s eyes narrowed slightly before he declared, “If you truly intend to bring down Qian Chu, the Demon Sealing Grotto is prepared to lend you our support.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2097: Visiting The Demon Sealing Grotto(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2097: Visiting The Demon Sealing Grotto(2)

Chapter 2097: Visiting the Demon Sealing Grotto(2)

“The Demon Sealing Grotto will support me?” Yan Hara raised an eyebrow, genuine surprise flickering in her eyes. “That’s quite the unexpected offer. However, I must ask—what if I am not trying to challenge Qian Chu for his position? What if I am just trying to increase my position for my own benefits?”

Zhou Yanfei paused briefly before speaking, his gaze steady. “That’s difficult to believe. I may not know everything about you, but I know enough to say you’re not the type to act so selfishly. And those you’ve been rallying—they’re all individuals who are not under Qian Chu’s influence.”

“If you’re not aiming to challenge Qian Chu, then whatever you’re planning must be something that will shake the very foundation of the Demon Sealing Clan.”

“You sound very confident in that statement.”

“That’s just the feeling I am getting from you.”

“Well, unfortunately, I have no intention of challenging Qian Chu,” Yan Hara said, lying without the slightest hesitation.

She couldn’t afford to trust Zhou Yanfei so easily—not when there was no telling if his offer was sincere or if spies loyal to Qian Chu were lurking within the chamber.

Zhou Yanfei narrowed his eyes slightly, but he didn’t continue to dwell on the topic and said, “Let’s talk about something else then. During the Supreme Demon Sealer Qualifications… What happened?”

“Why are you asking me that again? I already told you everything back then.” Yan Hara raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

“My memories are getting a little hazy these days, so why don’t you remind me?”

Yan Hara rubbed her eyes and let out a soft sigh. “As I’ve said before, I was in the midst of battling the Demon Emperor when I was suddenly seized by the Demon Sealing Flag. At the time, I didn’t understand what had happened, but I later learned it was Qian Chu who had saved me.”

She paused briefly before adding, “That’s also why I have no intention of challenging him. I could never turn against the one who saved my life.”

Naturally, she’d already realized that it was Yuan who saved her that day, but it was true that she initially thought it was Qian Chu who saved her life.

“Ah, yes. I recall now,” Zhou Yanfei said, his voice laced with curiosity. “Though, I find it rather strange that Qian Chu saved you, yet allowed the others to perish. Why is that? Are you two perhaps… involved in that kind of relationship?”

Yan Hara’s expression turned cold as she said with quiet fury, “Even if you’re the Demon Sealing Grotto’s leader, I won’t allow such disrespect.”

Zhou Yanfei quickly offered a courteous nod. “My apologies if my speculation came off as offensive. But that only deepens the mystery—why did he save you? To my knowledge, Qian Chu has never intervened personally for anyone before.”

“You’ll have to ask Qian Chu since I have no idea what he’s thinking.”

Zhou Yanfei let out a heavy sigh. “That day, three of our most gifted Demon Sealers lost their lives, while the Demon Sealing Clan suffered not a single casualty. We will not rest until the truth is unveiled, and those responsible are held accountable.”

“…”

Yan Hara fell silent. There was nothing she could say to defend that incident, as she understood how guilty the Demon Sealing Clan looked. She had been there, but held no control over what had transpired. And even if Qian Chu had truly tried to orchestrate their deaths as an accident, they had no proof.

“I will ask you one last time. Do you know what happened on that day?” Zhou Yanfei asked again a moment later.

“No.” Yan Hara instantly responded with an adamant face.

“In that case, there’s nothing more to discuss. Should you choose to challenge Qian Chu or want to switch sides, you may contact us again. And before you leave, we’d like you to pass a message to the rest of the Demon Sealing Clan.”

Zhou Yanfei paused briefly, his expression turning cold and his gaze radiating killing intent.

“We, the Demon Sealing Grotto, hereby declare war on the Demon Sealing Clan. From this moment forth, any member of your clan who dares approach us will be killed without mercy.”

Yan Hara’s eyes widened with disbelief. “What happened to not turning on each other because of the looming threat?! You called it foolish just minutes ago!”

Zhou Yanfei responded with a cold sneer.

“I was talking about you, Yan Hara. It would be foolish to eliminate a Demon Sealer of your caliber. But the rest of the Demon Sealing Clan?” He scoffed.

“They’re nothing but dead weight—fodder in the face of war against the demons. Purging them would be a mercy, not a loss.”

“Th-That’s—!”

“Why are you so shocked?” Zhou Yanfei said. “You of all people should’ve seen this coming. Our factions have been at each other’s throats for decades, and it was only a matter of time. This war didn’t begin with us. Your Demon Sealing Clan lit the fire. We only sought to hone our Demon Sealing Aura and hunt demons, but your people forced our hand.”

Although Yan Hara couldn’t refute Zhou Yanfei’s words, she still sought a path to reconciliation. A war between them now, with the looming threat of demons, would be nothing short of catastrophic.

Just as Yan Hara struggled to find a solution, Yuan finally broke his silence and calmly said, “If you don’t mind, there are a few things I would like to say.”

“Huh?” Zhou Yanfei’s gaze snapped to Yuan, his eyes sharp with disdain. “This conversation is far beyond your station, boy. Do yourself and your master a favor: keep quiet and walk away before you make this any worse.”

Despite the threat, a tranquil expression remained on Yuan’s face as he spoke, “I understand your fury over the loss of your Demon Sealers. However, both factions bear some responsibility for what transpired—and if blame must be assigned, Qian Chu bears the greatest burden. To condemn the entire Demon Sealing Clan for the actions of one man would be both unreasonable and shortsighted.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2098: Visiting The Demon Sealing Grotto(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2098: Visiting The Demon Sealing Grotto(3)

Chapter 2098: Visiting the Demon Sealing Grotto(3)

“What…?” Zhou Yanfei’s eyes widened in shock at Yuan’s response.

The other Demon Sealers in the room exchanged uneasy glances, clearly taken aback.

“Do you even know what you’re saying?! Why should we bear any responsibility when it was Qian Chu who clearly plotted against us?!” someone in the room exclaimed out loud, his voice full of anger.

Yuan turned to look at the man who had just spoken and calmly explained, “The fact that both sides agreed to release a Demon Emperor means that both are responsible. You’re naive if you think you have the strength to deal with a Demon Emperor. Who do you think you are? The Divine Paragon?”

The man opened his mouth, but no words came out.

Yuan’s tone remained unwavering as he continued, “I’ve heard that for the last Supreme Demon Sealer Qualifications, you only used a Demon General. So why the sudden shift to a Demon Emperor? I was present during that incident, and I can say with certainty that none of you had the power to kill the Demon Emperor—not even Qian Chu, who claimed to have captured it. If it had truly wished to, it could’ve slaughtered everyone that day. Fortunately, it chose to leave.”

The room fell silent, as nobody knew how to respond.

“What do you know? You’re just a mere Elite Demon Sealer!” Someone there eventually snapped.

“That’s right! What gives you the qualifications to make such claims when you’ve probably never even killed a demon before?!”

Hearing her idol being slandered, Yan Hara could not remain silent and subconsciously defended Yuan, “What the hell do you know?! He’s killed more demons than anyone in this room combined!”

The room fell silent again, but moments later, it erupted with laughter. Even Zhou Yanfei couldn’t help but burst out laughing.

“He’s killed more demons than anyone in this room combined?” Zhou Yanfei repeated in a mocking tone. “Not even Qian Chu would dare make such a claim! Yet, you’re saying that a mere Elite Demon Sealer has killed more demons than him?! If that is true, then why the hell is he still your disciple?! With such an amazing achievement, he should be leading the Demon Sealing Clan instead! Hell, I’d even let him lead the Demon Sealing Grotto! Ahaha!”

Yan Hara looked at Yuan with an apologetic gaze.

“I’m sorry, I couldn’t keep my mouth shut…” she said to him using voice transmission.

Yuan shook his head with a calm smile and said out loud, “It’s true, though.”

“What? You can’t be serious.”

Zhou Yanfei and the others immediately stopped laughing, their expressions hardening with anger. They looked as though they were being mocked.

“Do you really take us for fools?” Zhou Yanfei narrowed his eyes, his voice dripping with disdain.

Yuan didn’t respond right away. Instead, he turned to Yan Hara and asked calmly, “May I?”

Understanding his intent, Yan Hara nodded and stood up, silently giving him her seat. The others watched in surprise, for her actions clearly indicated that Yuan held more status than she did.

Once Yuan sat down, he unleashed a massive wave of Demon Sealing Aura that not only filled the room but also began to petrify those around him, which was only possible if his Demon Sealing Aura vastly surpassed theirs. Yet, everyone in the room was a top-ranking member of the Demon Sealing Grotto and an established Demon Sealer.

“T-This is—!”

Zhou Yanfei’s eyes widened in shock and disbelief. While his body didn’t appear petrified under Yuan’s overwhelming Demon Sealing Aura, it became so stiff that even lifting a finger felt like an impossible task.

“Who the hell are you?!” he muttered with much difficulty.

The sheer volume of Demon Sealing Aura Yuan exuded was unlike anything they had ever felt before. Not even Qian Chu—hailed as the most talented Demon Sealer since the Divine Paragon—could compare to the overwhelming force Yuan was unleashing.

Although Yuan wasn’t hiding his face with a mask, he’d consumed an appearance-altering pill before visiting the Demon Sealing Grotto. After all, if he’d gone in his original appearance, there was a good chance they would recognize him, which could complicate things.
