This method of training bordered on torture. It was harsh, relentless, and unforgiving. Yet despite its brutality, the results were already beginning to show within just a few days.

With each passing day, Yan Hara’s Demon Sealing Aura grew noticeably stronger, denser, more refined, and far more intimidating. Alongside it, a fierce killing intent began to radiate from her body.

Time flowed swiftly, and in the blink of an eye, three months had passed since Yan Hara began her training.

By now, Yan Hara was almost unrecognizable. Though her appearance remained the same, her aura and presence were incomparable to before.

After another month, Yuan suddenly opened his eyes when blood began flowing from Yan Hara’s nose. Seeing this, he muttered, “Looks like it’s time to end her training.”

He commanded Demon Emperor Nightmare to halt its abilities the very next moment, ending Yan Hara’s seemingly endless nightmare.

However, the moment Yan Hara returned to the real world, her eyes instantly locked onto Demon Emperor Nightmare, and they flared with unfathomable hatred and killing intent.

“DEMON!” she roared before shooting herself at it.

Yuan didn’t stop Yan Hara and decided to let her fight Demon Emperor Nightmare.

Yan Hara’s body exploded with immense Demon Sealing Aura. It was so powerful that it leaked out of the training grounds and into the city outside, shocking the nearby Demon Sealers.

“Heavens! Whose Demon Sealing Aura is this?! I have never experienced such a powerful aura! Even Master Zhou pales in comparison!”

“It must be Senior Yan! Only the Divine Paragon’s successor could possibly achieve this level of prowess!”

While Yan Hara had been training, Zhou Yanfei spread the news of her being the Divine Paragon’s successor throughout the Demon Sealing Grotto, news that sent shockwaves through the entire faction.

Though many members were initially skeptical, they couldn’t forget witnessing Yan Hara wielding the Divine Paragon’s techniques during the invasion. Coupled with Zhou Yanfei’s firm endorsement, it didn’t take long for the faction to accept her as the rightful successor to the Divine Paragon’s legacy.

“The Demon Sealing Aura is getting stronger!” The Demon Sealers started gathering around the source, but none of them dared to approach since it was coming from within Zhou Yanfei’s territory.

Eventually, Zhou Yanfei appeared at the training grounds.

Though he was well aware that Yuan and Yan Hara were using the space for their training, curiosity eventually got the better of him, and he decided to step inside for a closer look.

’Heavens! What the hell is a demon doing here?! And that heavy presence… It’s a Demon Emperor!’ Zhou Yanfei was greatly shocked to see a Demon Emperor appearing in the middle of his training grounds, and he wondered if it had snuck inside to try to assassinate Yan Hara.

After a brief moment of contemplation, Zhou Yanfei showed himself and joined the battle with Yan Hara to deal with Demon Emperor Nightmare.

Yuan had already noticed Zhou Yanfei’s presence long before he arrived, but he decided not to stop him, even using it as an opportunity to test his loyalty.

Seeing Zhou Yanfei assisting Yan Hara without hesitation made him give a nod of approval.

After nearly an entire week of fighting, Yan Hara and Zhou Yanfei managed to subdue Demon Emperor Nightmare, sealing it.

“How… How and when the hell did a Demon Emperor sneak inside? We have people guarding the city’s entrance around the clock ever since the Demon Worshippers’ invasion!” Zhou Yanfei exclaimed afterward, wondering if someone had purposefully let it inside.

However, Yuan quickly said, “Relax. This Demon Emperor isn’t real. It’s a puppet I created to help Yan Hara with her training.”

“What…?” Zhou Yanfei stared at him with wide eyes, seeming unable to believe his ears.

chapters.title: Chapter 2112: Leaving The Demon Sealing Grotto
chapters.body:
Chapter 2112: Leaving The Demon Sealing Grotto

Chapter 2112: Leaving the Demon Sealing Grotto

Zhou Yanfei muttered, his face filled with disbelief. “The demon was indistinguishable from the real thing! How did you manage to create such a thing?!”

“That’s a family secret,” Yuan responded with a mysterious smile on his face.

Then, he turned to look at Yan Hara, who was standing before him with a dazed look on her face.

“Are you okay?” he asked her.

Yan Hara lowered her head to stare at her trembling hands and slowly responded, “Give me a few minutes… I need to organize my mind for a bit…”

Yuan silently nodded and returned to Zhou Yanfei.

“So, anything new during these last few months?” he questioned.

“Your trick worked like a charm. We’ve managed to extract the traitors’ memories without any issues after you removed their curses, and we learned from them their background and goal on that day, as well as some information on the thief. Unfortunately, there was very little information about the thief.”

“I see. What did you learn?”

“These traitors were all orphans trained at a young age to be spies in the Demon Sealing Grotto and Demon Sealing Clan. They hail from a hidden family surnamed Mo somewhere within the Disappearing Mountain Valleys, and that’s all we know about them. We also know about their methods of training as well as their purpose.”

“Once they complete their training, they are sent to the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto, where they establish themselves as Demon Sealers and spy on us. Their goal during the invasion was to distract us so the thief, whom they knew as ’Master Mo’, could break into our vault.”

“Master Mo, huh?” Yuan mumbled, pretending to ponder despite already knowing the thief’s true identity.

“So what is the Demon Sealing Grotto going to do now?” he then asked.

“Naturally, we’re going to retrieve the Divine Paragon’s treasures. Once we’re prepared, we’re going to give this Mo Family a visit.”

“I would investigate the Mo Family first before approaching them if I were you,” said Yuan.

“Don’t worry, we won’t act recklessly, especially knowing that they are working together with demons,” Zhou Yanfei reassured.

Zhou Yanfei left Yuan and Yan Hara alone a few moments later.

Nearly half an hour after Zhou Yanfei left, Yan Hara finally opened her eyes and said, “I apologize for the wait.”

“That was quick. I wouldn’t have blamed you even if it had taken a few months,” said Yuan.

Yan Hara sighed and said, “I have lived a total of 176 different lifetimes, with each and every single one of them ending in tragedy. My loved ones and I have died in every possible way to the demons, from being boiled to being eaten alive. These indescribable experiences… if I had known what I was getting myself into, I would’ve probably refused it, but alas…”

After a brief moment of silence, she continued, “At least my mental strength has drastically improved because of it, and my hatred toward demons feels very natural even though I am aware that it all happened in an illusion.”

Yuan nodded with a smile and said, “Not only did your Demon Sealing Aura improve significantly, but even your Soul Strength is nearly incomparable to just three months ago.”

“I only spent three months inside the illusion? It felt like ages,” Yan Hara’s eyes widened at this news.

“If you want to feel better, I have spent several years training in that manner as the Divine Paragon, experiencing thousands of tragic lifetimes. However, since my hatred toward demons was already quite high, it barely improved my hatred for them.”

“If you don’t mind me asking, what created the Divine Paragon?” Yan Hara asked, her eyes flickering with curiosity.

Yuan closed his eyes for a moment before responding, “I had a childhood friend. Her name was Ai Rong, and she was eaten by demons right before my eyes. At that time, I was a powerless, spineless bastard who could do nothing but watch his loved one be eaten. After that incident, I began to desperately search for a method to deal with demons.”

“Back then, the only way to deal with demons was to completely overwhelm them or destroy their crystal. Their regenerative abilities were one of the main reasons for their dominance, so I began researching in that direction.”

Yuan paused for a moment before continuing, “Honestly, I don’t remember how I came up with Demon Sealing Aura. It just appeared in my mind during a period of enlightenment.”

“That’s just how enlightenment works,” Yan Hara said. “It’s how many inventions and cultivation techniques come into existence. It’s called God’s knowledge for a reason.”

Many cultivators believe that the state of enlightenment is akin to receiving divine insight—knowledge bestowed directly by the God of Knowledge, which explains most of the miracles that come with it.

“God’s knowledge, huh?” Yuan muttered.

A moment later, he continued, “Anyway, what are you going to do now? I will leave soon.”

“Well, now that I am aware of the culprit behind the stolen treasures, and I have declared myself as the Divine Paragon’s successor, I can only return to the Demon Sealing Clan and try to take Qian Chu’s position while he’s gone. It won’t be easy, but with the Demon Sealing Grotto now on my side, it’s definitely doable.”

Yuan nodded and said, “If anything happens, you have ’that’ thing.”

Yan Hara smiled and said, “Of course. Nobody would dare to defy me if I reveal that thing—perhaps except traitors who aren’t true Demon Sealers.”

“Then good luck. I’ll come visit you once I am finished with my business. Qian Chu plans on doing something in 20 years, so we’ll likely meet before then.”

“You stay safe as well.”

Shortly later, Yuan left the Demon Sealing Grotto to head to the Rising Phoenix Clan, while Yan Hara remained a little longer to speak with Zhou Yanfei and the rest of the Demon Sealing Grotto to ensure she had their full support in the future.

chapters.title: Chapter 2113: Returning To The Rising Phoenix Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2113: Returning To The Rising Phoenix Clan

Chapter 2113: Returning to the Rising Phoenix Clan

After a month of traveling nonstop, Yuan arrived at his destination, the Rising Phoenix Clan.

Even before he reached their residence, Yuan sensed a change in the atmosphere compared to his last visit. It was much stiffer and heavier, almost as if something grim had occurred recently.

“I wonder what happened.” Feng Yuxiang muttered as she appeared beside Yuan.

“There’s only one way to find out.”

As Yuan and Feng Yuxiang approached their territory, several powerful presences suddenly appeared to intercept them.

“Halt right there and identify yourselves!”

“I’m Feng Yuxiang, and this is my Young Master. We have an appointment with your leader,” Feng Yuxiang said calmly.

“Appointment? We haven’t been informed of any such thing,” the guard replied with a frown. “You’ll have to turn around and leave.”

“You can ask either Feng Zhenyan or the Grand Elder. They’re expecting us.” Feng Yuxiang continued.

“Look here. I don’t care if you have an appointment or not. Unless you’re more important than the Celestial Doctor, you will not see our leader!” The guard roared.

“Celestial Doctor?” Yuan raised an eyebrow upon hearing this, and he asked, “Is the leader sick?”

“Mind your own business. This isn’t something for an outsider to know about.”

However, the very next moment, they sensed another presence approaching. When the guards saw who it was, they quickly lowered their heads with respect.

“I thought I sensed your presence, Young Master.”

This individual was none other than Feng Haoyu.

“Y-Young Master?” The guards stared at him with wide eyes filled with disbelief, as none of them could fathom why their young lord would address someone in such a manner.

“What do you think you’re doing? They’re our esteemed guests.” Feng Haoyu then said.

“T-That’s…”

“But due to the situation, I won’t blame you guys. Leave. I’ll handle things from here.”

“Yes, Young Lord.”

Once the guards left, Feng Haoyu faced Yuan and said, “I apologize for that.”

“No worries. What’s the situation right now? I heard you’re waiting for the Celestial Doctor.” “Please, follow me. This isn’t a subject we should be speaking about out in the open.”

Yuan nodded and followed Feng Haoyu to a private room.

Once they were seated, Feng Haoyu spoke, “The situation is quite dire right now, so everyone is on edge. Our leader—my mother—returned two weeks ago, but she returned greatly injured.”

“What happened to her?”

“We’re still unsure of the situation. She collapsed shortly after returning and hasn’t regained consciousness since, hence why we contacted the Celestial Doctor, who is known as the most experienced doctor in the Nine Heavens.”

Yuan glanced at Feng Yuxiang and asked, “What’s the difference between the Celestial Doctor and the Divine Doctor you introduced before?”

“Well,” Feng Yuxiang explained, “the Divine Doctor mainly treats mortals, while the Celestial Doctor specializes in treating immortals. He’s essentially in a completely different league compared to Li Shizhen.”

“When will the Celestial Doctor arrive?” Yuan then asked.

“He should arrive next week.”

“Do you think I can have a look while we wait?”

“Are you also a doctor?” Feng Haoyu looked at him with wide eyes.

“Well, I wouldn’t call myself a doctor, but I do specialize in curing poison and curses.”

“In that case, please take a look at my mother!”

Yuan nodded, “Alright. Bring me to her.”

Sometime later, Feng Haoyu brought Yuan and Feng Yuxiang to his mother’s private residence, where the Grand Elder and several unfamiliar individuals were gathered.

“Who are they?” The people in the room questioned the moment they saw Yuan.

“Senior Primordial Phoenix!” The Grand Elder greeted him instead.

“What? He’s the Primordial Phoenix that you were speaking of?” The others in the room were utterly shocked to learn this.

Yuan ignored their gaze and approached the woman sleeping on the bed.

However, his path was immediately blocked by someone.

“What do you think you are doing?”

“Step aside, Elder Yikong. The Young Master is an expert with poison and curse, and we already know that she’d been cursed,” Feng Haoyu said out loud.

“Can you really trust him? Although you have sworn your loyalty to him, you barely even know him! It’s not too late to stop your nonsense!” Another elder spoke, criticizing his decision to follow a stranger.

“I may not know much—if anything—about him,” Feng Haoyu said calmly, “but I trust my judgment of people. And my judgment tells me he’s someone who can be trusted.”

“Grand Elder! Please say something! The Young Lord is out of his mind!” The elders turned to look at their eldest and most experienced.

The Grand Elder took a deep breath before speaking.

“Senior Primordial Phoenix, I ask for your understanding regarding their skepticism. As a phoenix, I possess the ability to read one’s character through their flames. After experiencing your True Primordial Fire firsthand, I can say with certainty that you are not someone of ill intent, but I cannot speak for the others.”

He then turned to look at the others and continued, “Senior Primordial Phoenix has no reason to harm the leader. He may be a stranger, but he possesses the Primordial Phoenix’s bloodline, and just from that, we are obligated to give him a chance.”

The elders fell silent.

“Relax. I am just going to check her condition and nothing else.” Yuan reassured them a moment later. “Of course, if you still refuse to trust me, we can all wait for the Celestial Doctor to arrive. Though it might be too late by then.”

“W-What the hell do you mean it might be too late?!”

“Do you really think you can scare us with such nonsense?!”

The elders exclaimed, their faces full of anger.

Yuan shook his head and explained, “I’ve only been in this room for a few moments, and I can already sense her bloodline weakening significantly. If we wait an entire week, she may not die—but she likely won’t recover properly from whatever’s afflicting her either.”

Hearing this, the elders turned to look at their leader and swallowed nervously.

chapters.title: Chapter 2114: A Familiar Curse
chapters.body:
Chapter 2114: A Familiar Curse

Chapter 2114: A Familiar Curse

“There’s no way the leader’s bloodline is weakening! Someone get the Golden Phoenix Needle!” One of the elders shouted, his voice full of nervous skepticism.

Another person there rushed out of the room the next instant, and he returned a few minutes later with a golden needle in his grasp.

Then, using the Golden Phoenix Needle, often used by phoenixes to check the strength of their bloodline, carefully tested the leader’s bloodline through a drop of her blood.

Shortly after testing the leader’s bloodline, the Golden Phoenix Needle emitted a faint crimson glow before a number appeared on the top of the needle’s handle.

“85.2 percent?! Her bloodline quality has dropped by four whole percent!” the elder holding the needle exclaimed in a voice laced with terror.

“FOUR PERCENT?!”

The others cried out in shock, as if they had just seen a ghost. Even the Grand Elder and Feng Haoyu were visibly shaken. Though four percent might not sound like much, in truth, it was a devastating loss—one that could take generations to recover, even by a single percent.

In the world of phoenixes, only those with a bloodline quality of at least eighty percent were deemed royalty. The leader of the Rising Phoenix Clan possessed a peak bloodline quality nearing ninety percent, placing her among the purest and most exalted bloodlines in all the Nine Heavens.

However, with her bloodline diminished by four percent, her status and standing within the phoenix clan would plummet. Should it continue to fall any further, the Rising Phoenix Clan itself could face the unthinkable—having its title as one of the Royal Phoenix Clans revoked.

“Her bloodline dropped by four percent, and it’s only been three weeks since her return! If we wait another week, it could drop by another percent!”

“What should we do?! We cannot wait for the Celestial Doctor’s arrival!”

The entire room suddenly fell silent, and everyone in the room slowly turned their heads to look at Yuan.

After a moment of stillness, they all approached Yuan and fell to their knees.

“Senior Primordial Phoenix! We were wrong for doubting you! Please heal our leader!”

“Please, Senior Primordial Phoenix!”

They abandoned their pride and lowered their heads to beg Yuan.

Feng Haoyu then spoke in a grim voice, “Four percent… this would take at least four million years to recover. Young Master, is there anything you can do to help my mother?”

Yuan spoke firmly, “Calm yourselves. I already said I would examine her condition, but I cannot guarantee I’ll be able to help. As for her weakened bloodline… we can discuss that later.”

Hearing this, the elders quickly stepped aside, clearing a path to the bed where their leader lay in deep slumber.

Yuan stepped up to the bedside, his gaze falling upon the beautiful woman resting there. At first glance, she appeared to be sleeping peacefully, but every so often, her brows would knit together, as if she were trapped in the grip of a troubling nightmare.

Without a word, he placed a hand on her head and closed his eyes.

The room fell into a tense, uneasy silence. Everyone stood motionless, yet their bodies betrayed them with subtle tremors running through them as they waited in anxious anticipation for Yuan’s response.

A few moments later, Yuan lifted his hand from the leader’s forehead and turned to face the others before speaking, “She’s definitely been cursed, and I am quite familiar with this curse.”

His gaze drifted to Feng Yuxiang and continued, “Feng Feng, you were also afflicted with this curse before.”

“Y-You don’t mean…” Feng Yuxiang’s eyes widened at his words.

Yuan nodded, “It’s the same curse that restricted your bloodline.”

“W-What is going on?” Unable to contain their curiosity, one of the elders asked.

Feng Yuxiang then explained in a grim voice, “Shortly after I left the Rising Phoenix Clan to do my own thing, I encountered this bastard who placed a curse on me, which restricted my bloodline and powers, essentially turning me into a human. If it weren’t for the Young Master’s help, I would’ve probably lost all of my powers by now.”

“What?! Such a vicious curse exists?! Who the hell is this bastard, and why did he target our leader?!” The Grand Elder roared in anger.

Feng Yuxiang shook her head and sighed, “I don’t know.”

“We can deal with this bastard later. Right now…” Feng Haoyu looked at Yuan and asked, “Since you have already dealt with it once, you can remove the curse again, right?”

Yuan nodded, “Naturally.”

“Please, save my mother. I beg of you.” Feng Haoyu fell to his knees and begged.

The elders stared with bewilderment, as this marked Feng Haoyu’s first time begging someone on his knees.

“Get up. I have no reason not to help her.” Yuan said.

“Thank you! Truly!”

The next moment, Yuan retrieved the Sacred Chalice of Purification and began removing the leader’s curse.

“This will take some time,” Yuan warned them.

Yet, not a single soul in the room left.

A week passed quickly, but to the Rising Phoenix Clan, that week felt like an eternity. And although they wanted to urge Yuan to cure the leader faster, none of them dared to utter a word.

Suddenly, someone knocked on the door and spoke, “Elders, the Celestial Doctor has arrived.”

“I’ll go greet the Celestial Doctor.” The Grand Elder responded before anyone else could even say a word and left the room quickly after.

Upon arriving at the guest room, the Grand Elder greeted a short old woman in green robes.

“I am the Grand Elder of the Rising Phoenix Clan. Thank you for coming here on such short notice, Celestial Doctor.”

The Celestial Doctor, who was no taller than 4 feet, calmly spoke, “Well, your leader is a good friend of mine. How is her condition right now?”

“She’s still unconscious, but we have someone helping her right now.”

The Celestial Doctor frowned at this information and spoke, “You allowed someone to touch her before I could even arrive?”

chapters.title: Chapter 2115: Celestial Doctor
chapters.body:
Chapter 2115: Celestial Doctor

Chapter 2115: Celestial Doctor

“Are you out of your mind?! How could you let someone touch her before I arrived?!” the Celestial Doctor thundered at the Grand Elder. “What if they made a mistake? It could have cost her life!”

“Please, let me explain, Celestial Doctor…” the Grand Elder said quickly. “This man is no ordinary healer. He’s an expert in curses and poison—and our leader has been cursed, which caused her bloodline purity to drop! In just three weeks, it’s fallen by four percent! If we had waited for you, her bloodline might have weakened far more than necessary!”

“Four percent?!” The Celestial Doctor’s eyes widened at this information.

The Grand Elder nodded with a solemn face.

“As a beast yourself, you should understand why we’re panicking and why we acted so hastily,” he sighed.

“Who the hell are you calling a beast? I am a Celestial Spirit! We’re nothing alike!” the Celestial Doctor shot back.

Celestial Spirits were entities born naturally from Celestial Qi, often formed when vast concentrations of it gathered and condensed over time. They’re akin to treasures but have their own consciousness and soul.

However, because of the immense amount of Celestial Qi required to give them form, Celestial Spirits were exceedingly rare—so rare, in fact, that perhaps only one would be born in an entire era.

As for their appearance, since their bodies were composed entirely of Celestial Qi, they could assume any form they desired. The Celestial Doctor’s short, elderly visage was simply a matter of personal choice, adopted for reasons known only to her.

“Anyway, enough yapping. Let me see your leader and this ’expert’ you speak of.”

The Grand Elder nodded and led her to the leader’s room, where the leader was still being treated.

“That’s the expert?”

The Celestial Doctor raised an eyebrow after seeing the situation inside. Since Yuan only needed the Sacred Chalice of Purification to cleanse the leader’s curse, he didn’t need to do anything after feeding it her blood, so he was just sitting beside her like a spectator.

“Move aside.” The Celestial Doctor said to the elders in the room as she approached the bed.

The elders didn’t say anything and quickly moved aside for her.

Once the Celestial Doctor stood beside Yuan, where she could see his face, her eyes widened with shock and disbelief.

“Y-You! Tian Yingzhe?! What the hell are you doing here? I thought you were dead!” she exclaimed, sounding as if she’d just witnessed a ghost.

Yuan turned to look at the short old lady with a puzzled face and asked, “Tian Yingzhe? Who’s that?”

Of course, Yuan recognized the name Tian Yingzhe, as he was the founder of the Celestial Overlords. However, he had no recollection of the Celestial Doctor himself, so he simply feigned ignorance.

“Who the hell do you think you are trying to fool?” The Celestial Doctor frowned, looking offended.

“In case you have forgotten, I am a Celestial Spirit, and I have the ability to look directly at your soul,” she said.

Yuan immediately fell silent. Though the Celestial Doctor claimed she could see his soul, Yuan wasn’t quick to take such a statement at face value.

“Why don’t we talk somewhere else?” Yuan then said.

“Sure.”

As Yuan stood up and approached the exit, one of the elders asked, “What about the curse?”

He was worried that the treatment might be delayed because of this.

However, Yuan pointed at the chalice on the ground and said, “That doesn’t require any management. It won’t stop working just because I am out of the room, either, so you can relax.”

“If you say so…”

Sometime later, Yuan and the Celestial Doctor left the room. Feng Yuxiang decided to stay behind so she wouldn’t disturb their conversation.

A few moments later, standing in an empty courtyard not far away, the Celestial Doctor faced Yuan and asked, “What the hell is going on, Tian Yingzhe?”

“I wasn’t joking when I said that I am not Tian Yingzhe,” he calmly responded.

“Still trying to fool me? What are you trying to achieve with this?” she narrowed her eyes at him.

“Before I explain myself, what is your relationship with Tian Yingzhe?”

“My relationship? Fine, I’ll entertain your nonsense for a bit.” The Celestial Doctor gave a quick sneer before retrieving an azure-colored medallion from her spatial ring that read ’Celestial Overlords’.

“Tian Yingzhe and I go way back,” the Celestial Doctor began. “I knew him long before I even became a full-fledged Celestial Spirit. In my immature state, I was no different from a living treasure—something that could grant immense benefits to a cultivator. Because of that, I was constantly hunted by humans and beasts alike.”

“I met Tian Yingzhe when he saved me from being captured, and he continued to protect me until I became an adult Celestial Spirit. If it weren’t for him, I wouldn’t be here right now. Anyway, to repay him, I decided to follow him and help him build the Celestial Overlords. I am essentially its co-founder.”

Yuan’s eyes widened upon hearing this, but before he could even respond, the Celestial Doctor’s figure suddenly shifted, transforming herself into a tall and slender beauty.

“Do you remember me now?” she asked.

“Uhh…”

Yuan was speechless for a brief moment.

Then, he explained his situation, “To tell you the truth, Tian Yingzhe died long ago.”

“WHAT?!” The Celestial Doctor exclaimed, not daring to believe her ears.

“Let me finish. Anyway, he died and reincarnated.”

“Are you saying that you are…”

He nodded.

“Yes, I am Tian Yingzhe’s reincarnation. Although I don’t have all of his memories right now, I’ll eventually regain them.”

“Unbelievable… but that explains the state of your soul…” The Celestial Doctor sighed, even rubbing her eyes in a somewhat stressed manner.

After a moment of silence, she asked, “So, about the Celestial Overlords… have you heard about their situation?”

“If you’re talking about their internal struggle, yes, I am well aware of it. At least the surface stuff,” he said.

chapters.title: Chapter 2116: Celestial Doctor(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2116: Celestial Doctor(2)

Chapter 2116: Celestial Doctor(2)

“Internal struggle… that’s putting it lightly,” the Celestial Doctor said, shaking her head. “The Celestial Overlords quickly went to shit after you disappeared. It’d gotten so bad that I decided to leave.”

“Now that you’ve returned, are you going to take control again? Otherwise, I don’t see myself ever returning.”

“We’ll see how things go. The Celestial Overlords is a thing of the past, so I don’t really have much attachment to it, especially since I don’t have most of Tian Yingzhe’s memories. However, that might change when I recover his memories.”

“Is that so? I guess I’ll remain as a wandering doctor for a little longer then.”

The Celestial Doctor retrieved a jade slip before casually tossing it into the air toward Yuan, who quickly caught it.

“You can use that to communicate with me directly. If you ever need my help, don’t hesitate to call,” she said. “I don’t want to brag, but as the Celestial Doctor, I’m one of the most influential individuals in the Nine Heavens. There’s hardly a sickness or injury I cannot heal, and I have treated countless people throughout the years.”

“In that case, I’ll contact you if I ever get hurt.” Yuan chuckled.

The Celestial Doctor nodded before falling into a brief moment of silence.

“By the way, have you met that mischievous little girl yet?” she suddenly asked.

“Mischievous little girl? Who are you talking about?” Yuan asked with his eyebrows raised.

“You know… that little girl who used to follow you everywhere. She’s one of the four Celestial Overlords in your faction.”

“Oh, you mean Xu Jiaqi? Yeah, I have met her. In fact, she is convinced that I am Tian Yingzhe’s reincarnation—not that I have told her the whole truth, yet.”

“Why not? Do you not trust her? You told me about your identity quite easily, even though you don’t even remember me,” she said with a somewhat puzzled face.

“No, that’s not it. I met her long before I even began recalling any memories, so I am just waiting for the right time to tell her. She’s also quite busy right now with the Celestial Overlords, and I do not want to distract her. Regardless, she already knows that I am Tian Yingzhe’s reincarnation.”

“But she doesn’t know you’ve retained Tian Yingzhe’s memories,” the Celestial Doctor said. “Still, that’s not surprising, considering it’s almost unheard of for a reincarnator to keep the memories of their past life. Such a thing would be beyond defying the heavens.”

“Anyway, as much as I’d like to keep talking about the past and catching up, I still have a job to do—and my friend’s life is at risk. Can you tell me about the leader’s condition?”

Yuan nodded and said, “Let’s catch up another time. The leader was cursed with a vicious curse that sealed her bloodline. However, that’s already being taken care of. Since I am not a real doctor, I don’t know if there’s anything else wrong with her.”

“I understand. Then I will have to examine her. Let’s return.”

Some time later, Yuan and the Celestial Doctor returned to the room.

“I’m going to examine the leader now,” declared the Celestial Doctor.

Yuan quickly reassured the elders, “Don’t worry, it won’t affect the curse-cleansing process.”

The Celestial Doctor went to examine the leader shortly later.

“She has some minor internal injuries. However, it’s nothing serious, and they will heal by themselves,” she said a moment later.

“Do you know where she went before returning?” she then asked the elders.

“Well, she had some business with another Royal Phoenix Clan, but I can’t imagine they’d do something like this to her—we’ve always been on good terms. She was probably attacked on her way back or something. We won’t know for sure until she wakes up,” explained one of the elders.

The Celestial Doctor then said, “In any case, since I have examined her and determined there’s nothing I can do for her, I’ll be taking my leave now.”

“W-What about her weakened bloodline? Is there a fix for that?” One of the elders suddenly asked.

The Celestial Doctor responded without hesitation. “Of course, but it’s going to be very costly. I can already think of a few treasures that could restore her bloodline, but each one is incredibly rare and utterly priceless.”

“That’s…”

Naturally, the elders knew of such treasures even without needing the Celestial Doctor to name them, but they were far beyond the reach of even the Rising Phoenix Clan. Even if they somehow managed to acquire these treasures, they wouldn’t dare to use them so carelessly.

“Aside from using such treasures, the only option is for her to slowly restore her bloodline over time,” the Celestial Doctor said.

“That will take millions of years…”

“It’s unfortunate, but there is no shortcut.”

Yuan then spoke up, “I may have a way to improve her bloodline.”

“Really?!” The elders looked at him with skepticism written all over their faces, but none dared to voice their doubts.

Feng Haoyu stepped forward and said, “Have you all forgotten that he carries the Primordial Phoenix’s bloodline?”

“That’s right! Primordial Phoenixes can enhance the bloodline of other phoenixes!” the elders exclaimed, trembling with excitement as they remembered Yuan’s identity.

But before they could celebrate, Yuan’s calm voice cut in.

“Removing your leader’s curse and restoring her bloodline are two entirely different matters. I won’t ask for anything to lift the curse, but if you want me to restore her bloodline, you’ll have to do something for me in return.”

“What do you want us to do?” One of the elders asked.

“We can talk about that once your leader wakes up,” he said.

“Okay…”

“Then I’ll take my leave for real now. Until next time.” The Celestial Doctor said to Yuan before leaving the Rising Phoenix Clan.

Three days after the Celestial Doctor’s departure, the Sacred Chalice of Purification completed its work, fully cleansing the leader’s curse. Just a few hours later, she finally awoke from her long slumber.

chapters.title: Chapter 2117: Fate Maniac
chapters.body:
Chapter 2117: Fate Maniac

Chapter 2117: Fate Maniac

“Leader! You’re awake! Thank the heavens!” The elders immediately rushed to her side after noticing her open eyes.

The leader, a beautiful woman with flowing red hair and sharp facial features, slowly sat up on the bed and turned to look at the group with a puzzled face.

“Where… where am I?”

“You’re back at the Rising Phoenix Clan, mother.” Feng Haoyu said, pushing the elders aside so he could see her.

“I see… I made it back…” she muttered in a low voice.

“What happened out there, mother? Why did you come back injured? Who attacked you?”

The leader gritted her teeth and clenched her fists in frustration as she responded, “It was that damn freak, the Fate Maniac!”

“Fate Maniac?” Yuan raised an eyebrow upon hearing this weird nickname.

The leader continued, “That bastard attacked me out of the blue, claiming he was merely following fate’s command! I don’t know what he did to me, but he left after placing some curse on me, and I was barely able to make it back!”

The room fell silent after everyone learned the identity of the attacker.

“The Fate Maniac… what a troublesome individual.” The Grand Elder muttered in a stressed tone.

“Who is this Fate Maniac?” Yuan asked.

The Grand Elder explained, “We don’t know the Fate Maniac’s true identity, only that he’s a madman obsessed with fate—constantly proclaiming himself a servant of it. He’s incredibly elusive; you can’t seek him out, only be sought out by him. And on top of that, his strength is unfathomable.”

“Servant of fate…?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at the familiar description, his thoughts drifting back to his time in the lower heavens, when he had encountered an unusual man named Jia Chong in the Demons’ Valley.

“Wait a second. Who are you?” The leader suddenly questioned Yuan’s identity as she finally noticed his presence.

“This is—”

“My Young Master.” Feng Yuxiang stepped forward and interrupted Feng Haoyu.

The leader’s eyes widened at her familiar face.

“You are… Feng Yuxiang? Why are you here? I thought you’d left. Have you decided to return to us?”

Feng Yuxiang shook her head. “No, I’m still not affiliated with the Rising Phoenix Clan. I’ve returned because my Young Master has business with you.”

“Sorry for the late introduction. I am Yuan, a Primordial Phoenix.”

“What?! That’s impossible!” the leader exclaimed, abruptly rising from her bed.

Yuan didn’t waste time arguing. Instead, he simply summoned his True Primordial Fire, letting its invisible presence speak for his identity.

“Invisible flames… True Primordial Fire…” The leader fell to her knees in shock.

“It was also the Young Master who removed the curse from you, or you would still be bedridden right now, and your bloodline would’ve continued to decline,” said Feng Haoyu.

The leader’s entire body froze at his words. Then, she turned to look at him with stiff movements.

“W-What did you just say?” she asked in a trembling voice.

“The Young Master removed the curse—”

“No! Not that! What about my bloodline?!” she shouted in a panicked voice.

The room instantly fell silent, and the atmosphere became heavy.

“Someone speak to me, damn it!” the leader snapped, stomping her foot with such force that the entire chamber trembled.

The Grand Elder stepped forward, his voice solemn as he said, “The curse placed upon you has weakened the purity of your bloodline. In just three weeks, it has fallen to 85.2 percent.”

Upon hearing that her bloodline purity had dropped by a full four percent, the leader’s expression froze. Before she could even speak, her body gave way, and she collapsed once more, overcome by shock and despair.

“Leader!”

“Mother!”

Those nearby hurried forward to catch her, gently laying her back onto the bed.

Even as a God Ascension cultivator, with a mind and soul tempered to extraordinary resilience, the blow to her bloodline was too heavy a burden for her to endure.

A few minutes later, the leader’s eyes fluttered open, and she slowly sat up. Turning to the others with a strange smile, she said, “I just had the most unbelievable nightmare… In it, my bloodline purity dropped by four percent. Please, tell me it was just a dream.”

The elders’ faces turned pale once more, and not one of them could find the words to comfort her.

“Sorry, but it wasn’t a dream,” Yuan said calmly. “The curse truly did weaken your bloodline. But look on the bright side. It could have been far worse. For instance, Feng Feng—Feng Yuxiang, endured this same curse for thousands of years before she met me.”

“Also, it’s not impossible to help you recover your weakened bloodline.”

“T-That’s right! You’re a Primordial Phoenix! You can help my bloodline recover, perhaps even strengthen it!” The leader’s eyes began glowing with hope again.

“It’s possible, yes. However, it’s not going to be free.”

“What do you want? Money? Treasures? Just name your price!” The leader said.

“I have no shortage of treasures. That said, there is something that I want from the Rising Phoenix Clan.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, the leader spoke, “Clear the room for us. I would like to speak with him in private.”

The elders in the room immediately left without uttering a sound.

Once only Yuan, Feng Yuxiang, and the leader remained in the room, Yuan spoke plainly.

“I’ll get straight to the point—I want the Ancient Seal in your possession.”

The leader’s eyes narrowed with skepticism.

“I won’t even ask how you learned the Ancient Seal is in our possession, but why are you after it? What would a Primordial Phoenix want with it—if you’re even a real Primordial Phoenix?”

Yuan shook his head.

“Sorry, but I cannot tell you that.”

“You do realize the Ancient Seals were entrusted to us by the Celestial Emperor, right? Handing it over would be akin to betraying him outright. And with the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans having recently allied themselves with him, our clan would have to answer not only to the Celestial Emperor, but to every other Royal Phoenix Clan as well. You’re basically asking us to die with that request,” she said.

chapters.title: Chapter 2118: The Celestial Emperor’S Offer
chapters.body:
Chapter 2118: The Celestial Emperor’S Offer

Chapter 2118: The Celestial Emperor’s Offer

“The Ten Royal Phoenix Clans have allied themselves with the Celestial Emperor?” Feng Yuxiang asked, her voice tinged with disbelief. “That’s hard to believe. Why would they—who have always prided themselves on their independence—choose to work with the Celestial Emperor, of all people?”

The leader looked at Yuan and calmly responded, “Because the Celestial Emperor has a phoenix on his side—a Primordial Phoenix, that is.”

“What?” Feng Yuxiang muttered with wide eyes.

“The Celestial Emperor has a Primordial Phoenix?” she repeated just in case she misheard.

The leader nodded, her expression serious.

“Yes. He promised to elevate all of our bloodlines by at least three percent, regardless of our current bloodline purity, if we agree to work with him for ten million years.”

Though ten million years might sound like an eternity, even the Royal Phoenix Clans considered it an incredible bargain. For those whose bloodline purity was already exceptionally high, gaining even a single percent could take millions of years, making the Celestial Emperor’s offer nearly irresistible.

The Rising Phoenix Clan’s bloodline purity ranged between 80 and 85 percent, with their leader being the highest at 89 percent before it was weakened, and they were ranked sixth amongst the ten Royal Phoenix Clans.

The Royal Phoenix Clans above them ranged between 85 and 88, with the top three ranging between 90 and 92 percent.

The phoenix with the current highest purity has a 94 percent bloodline purity, with no phoenix in history besides the Primordial Phoenix achieving a bloodline purity of over 95 percent, hence why the Celestial Emperor’s offer was extremely enticing and almost impossible to reject.

“This is also why I doubt your identity as a Primordial Phoenix. I appreciate that you helped cure my curse, but I cannot give you the Ancient Seal, as that would spell the end of my life and of this clan.”

A Primordial Phoenix had not appeared in the world for millions of years, so for two to emerge within the same era was something almost impossible to believe.

Naturally, Yuan did not blame her for not believing him.

“How can you be so sure the other Primordial Phoenix is genuine?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“Because we tested their bloodline purity—it was 96.5 percent,” the leader replied. “Only a Primordial Phoenix can naturally surpass 95 percent. And that phoenix also displayed the True Primordial Fire.”

In the end, Yuan decided to deactivate his phoenix transformation and said, “You’re right. I am not a phoenix, much less a Primordial Phoenix.”

“Y-You…” The leader’s eyes widened with shock and disbelief.

“You’re a human?! How in heaven’s name is that even possible?!”

“I’ll tell you if you do me a favor.”

“What is it? And don’t even bother opening your mouth if you’re going to ask for the Ancient Seal.”

“I would like you to help me set up a meeting with the Royal Phoenix Clans,” he said.

“Are you serious? I may be the leader of the Rising Phoenix Clan, but my authority is limited. I can’t summon the others whenever I feel like it. There must be a real reason. Unless you’re a Primordial Phoenix, you can forget about it.”

Yuan smiled and said, “While it is true that I only pretended to be a Primordial Phoenix, it is also true that there is a Primordial Phoenix present right now.”

“You can’t mean…”

The leader slowly shifted her head to look at Feng Yuxiang.

Noticing her gaze, Feng Yuxiang placed her hands on her hips and lifted her chin slightly.

“That’s right—I am a Primordial Phoenix,” she declared, her voice brimming with pride.

The leader’s expression darkened into a frown, looking offended.

“Do you really expect me to believe that? Have you forgotten we tested your bloodline when you first joined us? It was only 83.2 percent back then. How could you be a Primordial Phoenix with that level of purity? You may qualify as a royal phoenix, but that’s it! Are you mocking me right now?”

Feng Yuxiang quickly replied, “That was before I awakened my bloodline and before I met the Young Master.”

“You were also cursed, weren’t you?” the leader pressed. “Wouldn’t that have lowered your bloodline, too? In fact, I’m starting to doubt you were cursed at all. How did you manage to survive thousands of years with it, while I lost four percent in just three weeks?”

“Maybe because my bloodline is much harder to weaken? After all, I am a Primordial Phoenix,” she said.

Yuan then stepped in, his tone calm but decisive. “Rather than wasting time with words, why don’t we settle this with the Golden Phoenix Needle?”

He turned around and walked outside, where the elders were waiting.

“Is the conversation finished?” They asked.

“No. I need to borrow the Golden Phoenix Needle for a moment,” he said.

The elder with the needle quickly handed it to him.

“Thanks.”

Yuan returned to the room and closed the door behind him.

“You can do the honor.” Yuan handed the Golden Phoenix Needle to the leader, who wasted no time testing Feng Yuxiang’s bloodline.

Moments later, the needle emitted a bright golden glow, followed by a number.

“This can’t be… this is impossible!” The leader mumbled to herself after reading the numbers.

Not daring to believe her eyes, she rubbed them and looked again. Yet, the number remained the same.

“97.9 percent!” she exclaimed after confirming the number.

However, Feng Yuxiang frowned and said, “Only 97.9 percent? I was expecting 100 percent!”

“W-What about True Primordial Fire? Can you use it?” The leader asked after snapping out of her daze.

Feng Yuxiang nodded and casually summoned some True Primordial Fire.

“There are currently two Primordial Phoenixes existing in this era? Truly unbelievable…” The leader muttered.

“So, about the gathering… can you do it?” Yuan asked after a brief pause. “If that’s not enough to draw them in, tell them we have an offer even better than what the Celestial Emperor is promising.”

The leader could only stare at Yuan in silence, as if struggling to believe there could be an offer surpassing even the Celestial Emperor’s.

chapters.title: Chapter 2119: The Royal Phoenix Clans’ Response
chapters.body:
Chapter 2119: The Royal Phoenix Clans’ Response

Chapter 2119: The Royal Phoenix Clans’ Response

After a moment of silence, the leader slowly nodded her head and said, “I’ll bring it up with the other leaders. It’ll probably take a few days to receive an answer.”

“We can wait.”

The leader retrieved a communication jade slip from her spatial ring and began sending messages, notifying each and every leader of the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans about the situation.

She had expected it would take several days for them to reply, but to her surprise, many of the leaders responded within mere minutes.

“Six of the other nine clans have responded and agreed to attend the meeting. However, three have refused,” she informed Yuan.

“Who refused?” Yuan asked.

“The Crimson Rebirth Phoenix Clan, Immortal Ember Phoenix Clan, and the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan. They’re ranked 8th, 4th, and 1st in the Royal Clans,” she explained. “So, do you still want to have this gathering?”

He nodded, “Yes, I don’t need all of the Royal Clans on my side—just enough to convince you to give me the Ancient Seal.”

The leader’s mouth dropped.

“You’re seriously doing all of this just for the Ancient Seal? Why do you even want it? Besides being a key that opens the Primordial Realm, I don’t think it has any other uses,” she said.

“I’ll tell you after I acquire the Ancient Seal,” he said with a calm smile.

“Fine. Then, when do you want the gathering to happen? What about the location?”

“As soon as possible. As for the location, I’ll let you decide.”

“I understand. Give me a moment to think.”

A few minutes later, she said, “I can’t really think of anything, so let’s just hold it where we usually do—at the Undying Fiery Summit. Since this topic is too sudden, it’ll take at least a month or two for everyone to be prepared. Let’s hold the gathering three months from now just to be safe.”

“Sounds good.” Yuan agreed.

“Then I’ll let the others know.”

The leader immediately notified those who agreed to the gathering of the location and time. Soon, they responded with acknowledgement.

“Alright. It’s done.”

“Thank you. Now, let’s talk about the Rising Phoenix Clan. Although this could wait until the gathering, we might as well do it now since we’re already here.”

“What do you want from the Rising Phoenix Clan?” she asked, a faint frown of worry creasing her brow.

“Nothing material,” Yuan replied. “I want the Rising Phoenix Clan to serve me. Or, if you prefer a better phrasing, I want you to work with me.”

“That’s—”

Yuan quickly interrupted the leader as she opened her mouth, “Listen to my offer before you give me an answer.”

The leader slowly closed her mouth and silently nodded.

“Not only will we improve the Rising Phoenix Clan’s bloodline purity, but we’ll also help your bloodline evolve into a superior one—if it’s possible.”

“What… did you just say?” The leader’s eyes widened so far that it seemed her eyeballs might pop out.

“Primordial Phoenixes like Feng Yuxiang have the ability to improve other phoenixes’ bloodline purity, but I can do that and evolve it.”

“How can a mere human have the ability to—”

The leader abruptly cut herself off as a thought struck her. The possibility of it being true was almost nonexistent. Yet, it was not entirely zero.

She wanted to voice the question lingering in her mind, but the courage to ask it never came, so she held her tongue and remained silent.

Yuan noticed her weird facial expression and could guess what she was thinking, but he didn’t say anything, only offering a mysterious smile on his face.

“If… If you really have that ability, then the Rising Phoenix Clan will serve you,” she said. “However, how are you going to prove your ability? Surely, you’re not expecting me to just take your word for it.”

“Of course not. As for the evidence… I’ll prove it to you during the gathering.”

“In that case, I’ll wait until then to provide you with my answer.”

Yuan nodded.

“Fair enough.”

“Anything else you want to talk about?” The leader then asked.

“No. We’ll leave for now. See you again in three months at the Undying Fiery Summit.”

“Okay.”

Yuan and Feng Yuxiang left the room shortly after.

“Where are you going, Young Master?” Feng Haoyu asked them.

“I’m going to leave for three months.”

“Leave? Why?”

“Your mother will tell you everything.”

“I understand…”

Though he still had many questions, Feng Haoyu decided to wait until later.

Once Yuan and Feng Yuxiang departed from the Rising Phoenix Clan’s territory, Feng Haoyu and the elders immediately approached the leader for answers.

She did not withhold anything, openly recounting the entire situation to them—including the revelation that Yuan was not the true Primordial Phoenix, but that title belonged to Feng Yuxiang.

“What?! Feng Yuxiang was the Primordial Phoenix?!” The Grand Elder exclaimed.

“That’s impossible! How did that man use True Primordial Fire, then?!”

“Hmph. It was probably some trick. I bet he was only acting like he could use it when, in reality, it was Feng Yuxiang who was using it,” remarked one of the elders.

“Although we were tricked, it doesn’t change the fact that they have a Primordial Phoenix, and Feng Yuxiang’s bloodline purity is even above that of the one with the Celestial Emperor.”

“They already fooled us once. What’s to say they aren’t still deceiving us? What if Feng Yuxiang isn’t truly a Primordial Phoenix either?” another elder asked, his voice thick with skepticism.

“That’s very unlikely,” said the leader, backing Feng Yuxiang. “Not only is it impossible to trick the Golden Phoenix Needle, but it’s impossible to fake her True Primordial Fire, too.”

Suddenly, someone spoke up.

“Young Lord, what are your thoughts on this matter? You swore loyalty to that man, yet he turned out to be a fraud. He’s not even a phoenix, let alone a Primordial Phoenix.”

Everyone turned their attention to Feng Haoyu, who was deep in thought.

“What? You swore loyalty to him?” the leader asked, her frown deepening as she heard this for the first time.

chapters.title: Chapter 2120: Abyssal Underground
chapters.body:
Chapter 2120: Abyssal Underground

Chapter 2120: Abyssal Underground

Under the weight of many gazes, Feng Haoyu let out a heavy sigh before speaking.

“Honestly, I don’t know what to say or even feel. Betrayed? Not necessarily. I swore my loyalty to him not because of his bloodline, but because of his ability and something else. So even if he isn’t truly a Primordial Phoenix, as long as his abilities are real, I have no reason to complain. And… there’s just something about him—something that makes me attracted to him. I can’t explain it.”

He then turned to his mother and continued, “I’m sorry, Mother, for making such a decision without consulting you. I acted on my instincts.”

“Since it’s your decision, I will respect it,” she replied. “However, now that you know he might be a fraud, what will you do? Unless you’ve performed a ritual, you can still withdraw your allegiance, as a verbal declaration isn’t technically binding.”

“Just like you, I’ll wait until the gathering before making my decision. There must be a good reason why Feng Yuxiang—a true Primordial Phoenix—would follow someone like him,” he said.

Hearing this last remark, the elders couldn’t help but agree. It was indeed strange. They could understand why someone might follow the Celestial Emperor, the most influential figure in the Nine Heavens—but Yuan? No one present even knew his true identity.

In their eyes, he was just a human who could somehow transform into a phoenix.

“…”

While everyone in the room pondered deeply yet failed to find a convincing reason, the leader’s mind settled on one possibility. However, she kept it to herself, as voicing it aloud might make her sound utterly insane.

Meanwhile, after leaving the Rising Phoenix Clan for the second time, Feng Yuxiang asked Yuan, “What are we going to do for the next three months?”

“How far away is the Undying Fiery Summit?” he asked.

She pondered for a moment before responding, “If we take the teleporters, it’ll only take twenty days to get there.”

“Are there any good hunting grounds near that place? I want to spend this time hunting magical beasts.”

“Hunting magical beasts?” Feng Yuxiang raised an eyebrow at the unusual activity. “If you’re looking for strong ones for training, I’d recommend—”

“No,” Yuan cut in abruptly. “I don’t care how strong they are. I only care about numbers. I need to kill around a hundred million, after all.”

“A hundred million?!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed after hearing this astronomical number.

“Why in heaven’s name do you need to kill so many magical beasts?” she couldn’t help but ask.

“It’s for a quest.”

“…”

Feng Yuxiang fell silent for a moment to ponder. Then, she spoke, “If you want numbers, you should visit the Abyssal Underworld. All sorts of insect-type magical beasts dwell there, and they typically come in massive numbers, such as the Abyssal Crystal Ants.”

“Sounds like just the place I want to go. How far is it?”

After some calculations in her head, Feng Yuxiang replied, “A month, but it’s quite close to the Undying Fiery Summit.”

“Alright. Lead the way.”

Feng Yuxiang summoned her flying treasure, and the two sped away.

After several teleportations and a month of travel, they finally arrived at their destination.

The entrance to the Abyssal Underworld yawned before them. It was a colossal hole in the earth, vast enough to swallow an entire mountain whole. From the outside, it appeared as nothing more than an abyss of pitch-black darkness, its depths concealing whatever lay within.

“There are a lot of people here,” Yuan said in a somewhat surprised tone.

“The Abyssal Underworld is vast, with countless areas still unexplored. Mapping it can be extremely profitable. And in places untouched by cultivators, treasures are often abundant, making it a hotspot for treasure hunters as well.”

“How strong are the magical beasts inside?” Yuan then asked. “Many of the cultivators here are Bronze and Silver Immortals.”

“The deeper you go, the stronger the magical beasts. The weakest magical beasts inside are the ones by the entrance, and they have a cultivation of Divine King.”

“How deep do I have to go for the Abyssal Crystal Ants?”

“I’m not sure since I’ve never entered this place before. However, the Abyssal Crystal Ants are only around Divine Ancestor, so they shouldn’t be too deep.”

Yuan nodded.

“I should probably buy a map before I enter, right?”

“Or hire a guide who knows where to find the Abyssal Crystal Ants.”

Yuan used his divine sense to look around the place, where many people were camped around the entrance.

Very quickly, he found several people selling maps of the Abyssal Underground, as well as a few people with a sign that read ’guide for hire’.

Since it would be more convenient to just hire a guide to lead him to his destination, Yuan approached one of the guides.

“Good day, Young Master. Are you looking for a guide? Where are you trying to go?”

The middle-aged man greeted Yuan, who was wearing a mask at the moment.

Yuan nodded and said, “I’m looking for the Abyssal Crystal Ants.”

At his words, the middle-aged man’s expression visibly stiffened, a flicker of unease crossing his face.

“What’s wrong?” Yuan asked.

“Well… I don’t have what it takes to take you to the Abyssal Crystal Ants. I’m sorry,” he said.

“But you’re a Silver Immortal,” Yuan remarked with raised eyebrows. “The Abyssal Crystal Ants are only Divine Ancestor, so they shouldn’t be too deep.”

“This must be your first time at the Abyssal Underworld. Although the Abyssal Crystal Ants are quite weak, they actually live deep within the tunnels, where insects at the Golden Immortal level roam.”

“I see. I’ll find someone else, then. Thanks for your time.”

Just as Yuan turned around, the middle-aged man said, “Just a moment, Young Master. I may not be able to assist you, but I know someone who can help you.”

Yuan halted his movements at such words.

“I can take you to him if you’d like.”

“Sure.” Yuan calmly nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 2121: Abyssal Underground(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2121: Abyssal Underground(2)

“Please, follow me.” The middle-aged man led Yuan to a beautiful woman with emerald- colored hair and brown eyes at the other side of the entrance.

Unlike the middle-aged man, who was only a Silver Immortal, this woman had the cultivation of a True Immortal.

“Senior Zeng, I have brought you a potential customer. This is his first time here.”

The woman looked at Yuan with a gentle smile and spoke, “Hello, esteemed guest. I’m Zeng Guoquan, also known as the Jade Guide around here. I have over a thousand years of experience with tens of thousands of satisfied customers. If you’re looking for a reliable and experienced guide for the Abyssal Underground, you have found the right person.”

Yuan nodded and said, “I’m Xiao Yang, and I’m trying to reach the Abyssal Crystal Ants.” “The Abyssal Crystal Ants are located between underground levels 250 and 300, where most Golden Immortal realm creatures roam. I can take you there for 500,000 spirit stones. 750,000 spirit stones if you want me to guide you back. I can even act as your bodyguard, but that will cost an additional 1 million spirit stones.”

“I just need you to bring me there,” Yuan said.

“I understand. When would you like to depart?”

“Immediately, if possible. Otherwise, as soon as possible.”

“We can depart immediately then.” Zeng Guoquan said.

“Stay close to me.”

Yuan nodded and followed Zeng Guoquan into the entrance to the Abyssal Underground.

From the outside, the Abyssal Underworld appeared to be an endless void of pitch black, but once Yuan crossed a certain threshold, a dim light seeped through the darkness. It wasn’t nearly as bright as the surface, more akin to the faint glow of a nightlight in a dark room. Still, for a cultivator like Yuan, the low light made little difference; he could see through the darkness with ease.

“How deep does the Abyssal Underworld go?” Yuan asked as they ventured further into the tunnels.

“No one can say for certain since nobody has ever reached the end. The furthest anyone has descended is to underground level 1,290, where peak God Ascension beings roam,” Zeng Guoquan replied.

“Peak of God Ascension? There are beings that strong within the Abyssal Underworld?” Yuan was surprised to hear this.

“This world is filled with unfathomable beings, after all.” Zeng Guoquan calmly remarked.

The tunnels of the Abyssal Underworld stretched wide and spacious, their ceilings soaring nearly ninety-five meters high, allowing for effortless movement through their vast expanse.

As Yuan and Zeng Guoguan made their way through the tunnels, they came across a myriad of insects and subterranean creatures, each native only in the Abyssal Underworld.

Two days later.

“We’re at underground level 100 right now. We should arrive at our destination in a few more days.”

“Okay.”

“If you don’t mind me asking, why are you looking for the Abyssal Crystal Ants?” Zeng Guoquan suddenly asked.

“For training: Yuan gave a generic answer.

“I see. Where are you from? I used to be a disciple at the Verdant Rivers Pavilion before coming here to work as a guide,” Zeng Guoquan continued to make small talk as they traveled.

“I grew up as an orphan, so I don’t have any background.”

“Wow. You managed to reach that level as an orphan? You must be very talented.”

“I wouldn’t say that. However, I did get lucky with a few fortunate encounters.”

“Well, some say that luck is even more important than talent.”

They passed the time with light conversation, and after two days of unhurried travel, they reached the 200th floor. Thanks to their overwhelming cultivation, none of the creatures dared to approach, allowing their journey to remain calm and untroubled. However, once they reached the 200th floor—where even the weakest beings were at the Silver Immortal realm—the monsters finally began to launch attacks against them. “Just ignore them,” Zeng Guoguan instructed. “If you have a technique to mask your presence, use it.”

With that, she activated a technique that suppressed her aura just enough to slip past unnoticed.

Yuan followed suit, though he deliberately left a trace of his presence unconcealed. Several days passed in silence, Zeng Guoquan uttering not a single word, her attention seemingly fixed solely on guiding Yuan to his destination.

“How much further until we arrive?” Yuan eventually asked.

“We’re only a few hours away,” she calmly responded.

Yuan didn’t say anything else and continued to follow her.

Two hours later, Zeng Guoquan came to a halt and said, “We have arrived at our destination.”

Yuan used his divine sense to sweep the place, but he couldn’t find any Abyssal Crystal Ants.

“Are you sure we’re in the right place?” he asked.

“Yes, I am sure.”

Yuan closed his eyes and sighed.

While he didn’t find any Abyssal Crystal Ants, he did find something else—several powerful presences.

“So much for being a reputable guide,” he sighed out loud.

In the next moment, the presences he had sensed revealed themselves, grinning broadly. Four figures stepped into view—human cultivators, each at the True Immortal realm.

“I didn’t lie to you,” Zeng Guoquan said. “I really am a reputable guide, and almost everyone knows me. But they only know what I choose to show them.”

Unfazed, Yuan asked, “So, how often do you trap your customers?”

“Not often. Only when they’re nobodies who won’t be missed… orphans with no background like you,” she replied with a peal of laughter.

The four cultivators joined in her laughter, their voices reverberating through the tunnel in mocking echoes.

“These idiots have no idea what they’ve gotten themselves into.” Feng Yuxiang shook her head as she watched everything unfold within Yuan’s Dantian.

Yuan then spoke, “So, what are you going to do to me? Kill me and rob my corpse?”

“It won’t be considered robbing if the treasures owner is already dead,” remarked one of the cultivators.

“In that case, hurry up and get it over with,” Yuan said, his voice devoid of even the slightest trace of concern.

chapters.title: Chapter 2122: Abyssal Underground(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2122: Abyssal Underground(3)

“Since you’re so eager to die, allow me to grant your wish!” one of the True Immortals snarled before suddenly lunging at Yuan.

“Supreme Spear Arts, Grand Thrust!”

The cultivator drew his arm back before hurling the spear in his grasp toward Yuan. His aim, however, deliberately avoided any vital organs, as though he wished not to end Yuan’s life too quickly, but to let him suffer a slow, agonizing death.

Yet the instant the spear touched Yuan’s body, it stopped cold against his skin, unable to pierce even a fraction deeper. To the wielder, it was as if he had struck an unyielding wall of steel.

“W-What…2″

The man’s eyes widened in disbelief, refusing to accept what he was seeing. He subconsciously began channeling more Immortal Qi into his strike, pushing with all his strength. Yet, the spear remained frozen in place, unable to advance an inch.

Yuan calmly looked at the spearman and said, “How are you going to kill me if you cannot even harm me?”

“AHHHH!”

The spearman let out a furious shout, unleashing the full force of his cultivation. A moment later, he felt the spear inch forward and allowed himself a brief smile—only for it to vanish when he realized the truth. Yuan had merely moved back ever so slightly, creating the illusion that the weapon was piercing his body.

“Fuck! What the hell are you doing? Stop fooling around!”

Unable to bear watching any longer, another True Immortal roared in frustration and charged forward, swinging a massive mace—twice the size of his own body—straight at Yuan’s head.

BOOM!

A violent shockwave tore through the area as the mace slammed down on the crown of Yuan’s head, hurling him into the ground. The impact shattered the earth, leaving a massive crater in the ground.

“That’s how you do it, Third Brother!” laughed the man wielding the mace.

“Damn it! That was unnecessary, Third Brother! I could’ve killed him myself if you had given me a little more time!”

“Enough yapping and go search his body! He reached the Golden Immortal realm as an orphan, and he’s mentioned having fortuitous encounters. He’s bound to be carrying something valuable,” Zeng Guoquan barked.

“Yes, Elder Sister!” they replied in unison.

But just as they moved toward the crater where Yuan had fallen, one of the other two brothers suddenly shouted in alarm, “Watch out!”

The Third Brother barely reacted just in time, but the Fourth–spear in hand–was a split second too slow. A sword flashed toward his face, the gleam of its edge being the last thing he saw before his consciousness was swallowed by darkness.

Though the Fourth Brother never realized how he died, the others were gripped by a surge of sorrow and fury at the sight of a sword driven straight through his face. “FOURTH BROTHER!”

The other three brothers shouted.

“So loud,” Yuan remarked as he emerged from the crater without even a scratch on his body.

“You bastard! You killed our Fourth Brother!” the remaining three brothers roared, weapons leveled at Yuan as their eyes blazed and Killing intent flooded the area.

“Okay… and?” Yuan replied calmly. “Did you really expect me to stand here and let you kill me without resistance? Did you think I wouldn’t fight back? You should be thankful that I gave him a swift and painless death.”

“Kill him! No! Tear him from limb to limb! Make him regret ever being born!” Zeng Guoquan suddenly shouted.

The next moment, all three remaining brothers rushed at Yuan. While the Third Brother wielded a large mace, the other two wielded a sword and a heavy axe.

Of course, no matter what kind of weapon they wielded or how many there were, none of them could get through Yuan’s physique with their Ancient-grade weapons.

“Elder Sister! Something is wrong! We cannot touch him with our weapons for some reason! Help us!”

“You brainless idiots! If weapons don’t work, use your martial techniques!”

At her urging, the three brothers swiftly stowed their weapons, opting to unleash their martial techniques instead.

The tunnel shook violently as they executed their most powerful techniques. Yet, Yuan remained utterly unfazed, even letting out a casual yawn.

“Impossible! Even our martial techniques can’t touch him!” the brothers exclaimed in disbelief as Yuan emerged without a single scratch.

This time, even Zeng Guoquan stood frozen, utterly dumbfounded.

“I’d let you have some more fun before I end your lives, but seeing how I am short on time, I’ll end things here,” Yuan said a moment later.

“You—*

Just as Zeng Guoquan opened her mouth to speak, Yuan released his Supreme Sword Aura. In an instant, the True Immortals were smothered beneath its razor-sharp pressure, every breath laced with the threat of being cut to pieces should they dare to move.

“W-Who… who the hell are you?” Zeng Guoquan cried out loud, forcing the words out with as little movement as possible, her voice strained and uneven under the crushing aura.

“I’m just a nobody,” he calmly responded.

As his words faded, Yuan’s Supreme Sword Aura struck the three brothers at once. Yet he deliberately avoided their vital organs, ensuring they would bleed out slowly, writhing in agony as death crept upon them.

“How can a mere Golden Immortal possess such power…” Zeng Guoquan muttered, her expression dazed.

Yuan stepped toward her at an unhurried pace and said, “I’m actually glad you revealed your true colors. Otherwise, I wouldn’t have had the chance to rid myself of trash like you today.”

“Please… spare me… Il do anything you want!” Zeng Guoquan begged, her voice trembling as she tugged down her upper robes, shamelessly revealing her alluring figure in a desperate bid for mercy.

“Anything?” Yuan asked.

“Anything!” she confirmed.

“Then die for me.”

Yuan seized the Fourth Brother’s fallen spear with his spiritual energy and, without hesitation, drove it into Zeng Guoquan’s stomach, pinning her to the ground.

Zeng Guoquan let out a piercing scream, yet she refused to yield. In a flash, she produced a treasure from her spatial ring and pointed it at Yuan, shouting, “Even if I die today, I’ll make sure to take you with me!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2123: Rod Of Destruction
chapters.body:
Chapter 2123: Rod Of Destruction

“Die, you bastard!” Zeng Guoquan roared, flooding the treasure with her spiritual energy the instant she drew it, and the treasure began to glow ominously.

“Did you really think I’d let you use it?” Yuan sneered, using the Heaven Sealing Technique on her. In a heartbeat, Zeng Guoquan’s cultivation was sealed, cutting off the flow of spiritual energy and with it, any hope of activating the treasure.

Yuan then plucked the treasure from her grasp and examined it with mild interest. It resembled a wand, yet was forged entirely from some unknown crystal, its core brimming with an immense, destructive energy.

The energy within was so immense that, had Zeng Guoquan managed to activate it, the resulting devastation could have reshaped the very landscape of the Abyssal Underworld.

“What a dangerous item you have here. Where’d you get something like this?” Yuan asked her, as he’d never seen anything like it before.

“I’l tell you if you let me live,” she quickly replied.

“Sure. I won’t touch you if you tell me.” Yuan nodded.

“Swear on your soul!” she demanded.

“If you do the same.”

With no choice but to obey, Zeng Guoquan began swearing on her soul with clenched fists, “I swear on my soul that I will tell you where I acquired the Rod of Destruction—the treasure in your hands if you let me live.”

Yuan then swore, “I swear on my soul that I won’t touch you if you tell me everything you know about this treasure.”

Once they had both completed their oaths, Zeng Guoguan began divulging everything she knew about the Rod of Destruction.

“The Rod of Destruction is an artificial treasure forged by the Nameless Society—a cabal of madmen united by a single goal: to create a weapon capable of unleashing the greatest destruction imaginable. I acquired it through a friend who had close connections with them. That’s all I know.”

“The Nameless Society, huh?” Yuan mumbled.

“Who’s your friend?”

“You already met him. He’s the one who brought you to me.”

I see

“I’ve told you everything I know, so I’ll be taking my leave now.”

Without waiting for Yuan’s reply, Zeng Guoquan wrenched the spear from her body, causing her blood to flow like a waterfall.

“Wait.”

Just as she began flying away, Yuan stopped her.

“Are you going to break your oath? The heavens will destroy your soul if you do!” she shouted with a deep frown on her face.

“No, I won’t do anything to you.”

“But I will.”

An unfamiliar voice suddenly resounded, startling Zeng Guoquan.

In the next instant, Feng Yuxiang emerged from Yuan’s Dantian. Without a word of warning, she leveled a finger at Zeng Guoquan, summoning her True Primordial Fire. invisible flames erupted around Zeng Guoquan, consuming her body in an instant. “Aaaah! You tricked me, you wicked bastard!” Zeng Guoquan screamed, collapsing to the ground as agony tore through her body.

Yuan remained silent, his gaze fixed on her as she was slowly reduced to ash in Feng Yuxiang’s flames.

“I curse you! Curse you! Curse you! I curse youll” Zeng Guoquan screamed relentlessly, but her cries soon faltered.

Mere moments later, her voice fell silent altogether.

With the offenders dealt with, Yuan calmly gathered their spatial rings, transferring all their treasures into his own.

Among the items he discovered within their spatial rings was a map of the Abyssal Underworld, containing detailed information up to the 500th level.

Then, using this map, Yuan made his way to the actual location of the Abyssal Crystal Ants.

A day later, Yuan arrived at his destination.

“So this is the Abyssal Crystal Ants’ colony.”

A faintly excited smile curved Yuan’s lips as hundreds of Abyssal Crystal Ants came into view, scurrying through the depths and tirelessly carving new tunnels into the Abyssal Underworld.

Though they were ants in form, each was the size of a minivan, radiating the aura of the Divine Ancestor realm, while a few among them had reached Immortal Ascension. Without hesitation, Yuan revealed his presence and began slaughtering the Abyssal Crystal Ants. Though famed for their durable exoskeletons that were harder than refined metal used to craft Divine-grade treasures, they were utterly helpless before him, their bodies shattering beneath a single blow of his fist.

After killing several ants with nothing but his fists, Yuan unleashed his Supreme Sword Aura, cutting down many in a single sweep, as it would simply take too long to kill them one by one.

In the blink of an eye, hundreds of ants lay dead. Yet Yuan felt no satisfaction. Because at this rate, completing his quest of a hundred million kills would still take an eternity. Unfortunately, less than two months remained before he was due to depart for the Undying Fiery Summit.

As he continued cutting down hundreds of ants every few minutes, Yuan pondered a faster method to hasten the slaughter.

Suddenly, an idea surfaced in his mind—one that could drastically accelerate his grind, yet was both dangerously risky and downright devious.

“Feng Feng, Yu Ning, Yingying. Hear me out.”

Yuan then laid out his idea to them, seeking their thoughts.

“Young Master… that’s insane—but brilliant at the same time,” Feng Yuxiang said.

“It might not affect you, but it will definitely impact everyone else,” Yu Ning added.

“It sounds good in theory, but it could easily go out of control, and innocent people could die as a result.” Lan Yingying said in a worried tone.

“I’ll make sure to seal off this entire area before I do it, so there won’t be any innocent casualties,” said Yuan.

After discussing the plan in detail and refining it to ensure nothing spiraled out of control, Yuan readied himself to set it into motion.

First, he moved through the surrounding area, sealing every tunnel leading out of the Abyssal Crystal Ants’ territory with formations through Yu Ning’s help, effectively trapping them in one place. However, the territory was so vast that it took Yuan nearly an entire month to complete the task.

Once he was certain that there was no place for them to escape, Yuan retrieved the Baneful Abyss and approached the nearest ant.

chapters.title: Chapter 2124: Mark Of The Baneful
chapters.body:
Chapter 2124: Mark Of The Baneful

With the Baneful Abyss in hand, Yuan calmly approached the nearest Abyssal Crystal Ant.

The moment it sensed his presence—and the ominous aura radiating from the dagger— the creature let out an inaudible cry before turning to flee.

“You can run, but you can’t hide.”

Yuan made no move to chase the ant. In fact, he came to a complete stop.

Then, when it had fled far enough, he drew his arm back and hurled the Baneful Abyss, sending it streaking through the air like a deadly missile.

In the span of a heartbeat, the Baneful Abyss reached its target, striking the Abyssal Crystal Ant’s body—a mass of semi-transparent, greyish crystal.

The blade of the Baneful Abyss pierced the ant’s crystal body as effortlessly as a sharp knife through paper, instantly poisoning it and branding it with the Mark of the Baneful. The venom was so potent that the Abyssal Crystal Ant was brought to the brink of death in an instant.

Then, with the mark’s activation, the dagger’s second ability triggered, and its corpse erupted violently, releasing a dense poison mist that spread in a hundred-meter radius. The nearby Abyssal Crystal Ants were instantly afflicted with the Mark of the Baneful, each one detonating in turn. The chain reaction swept through their ranks without pause, continuing until every nook and cranny of their vast territory was shrouded in the deadly poison mist.

Hundreds… thousands… tens of thousands of ants perished within seconds, and in mere minutes, Yuan’s quest progress had surged by millions of points.

in fact, the Baneful Abyss proved so devastating that it wiped out the entire Abyssal Crystal Ant population in the area.

Pleased by the method’s efficiency—and with ample time still remaining—Yuan decided to expand his hunting grounds.

However, before using the Baneful Abyss, Yuan first ensured that no one else was nearby, as he had no intention of harming innocents.

Then, with the time remaining before his departure for the Undying Fiery Summit, he steadily expanded his hunting grounds, filling vast stretches of the Abyssal Underworld with the spreading poison mist.

Before long, people began to sense that something was amiss. Creatures within the Abyssal Underworld were behaving strangely, appearing in places they were never known to roam. Even powerful beings that should have existed only in the deepest levels were now being spotted in the upper floors, leaving the explorers utterly dumbfounded.
