Of course, this was because they were desperately fleeing from the poison mist unleashed by Yuan. With him relentlessly terrorizing through the lower levels, their only option was to escape toward the upper floors, which led to the entrance.

“Everyone, watch out! Something strange is happening inside the Abyssal Underground!” Someone shouted as soon as they left the underground, warning the people camping near its entrance.

“What’s happening?”

“The creatures that are usually found only within the deeper levels are rushing towards the entrance! I have never seen or heard of anything like this before!”

“Do you know the cause of this phenomenon?”

“No, but there are already people who went down to investigate.”

A few days passed as word of the strange phenomenon spread far and wide. Eventually, reports began to arrive from those who had ventured out to investigate.

“According to the investigators, the lower levels are shrouded in some kind of poison mist. They tried to probe deeper, but formations blocked their path, preventing them from getting any closer,” someone reported.

“Formations? Did someone put them up to prevent the poison from spreading?”

“That’s very likely.”

“Where did this poison even come from? Was it released by some creature, or created by something else?” another person mused aloud.

“There’s no way to know unless we deactivate the formations and search deeper—but doing that could spread the poison even further. And we still have no idea how potent it truly is,” someone remarked.

A debate soon broke out over whether they should deactivate the formations to investigate or keep the poison contained until it dissipated on its own.

Logically, the poison should be left alone to avoid unnecessary trouble and risk. However, the vast territory it now occupied severely hindered exploration, which in turn meant significant losses in revenue.

Moreover, many believed the poison wasn’t particularly lethal and could be bypassed with the aid of poison-resistant pills.

“We should just let the poison spread. That way, itll thin out and disappear much quicker.”

“Since we don’t know where it’s coming from, we can’t say for certain the poison will disappear so easily.”

“I’m actually a master of poisons. I’ll check out the poison first and give you my input.” “I’ll go with you since I also have some experience with poison.”

“Me, too.”

Before long, an expedition was organized, with numerous experts setting out to examine the poison for themselves.

Some time later.

“So, what do you think? How deadly is it?”

“It’s hard to say without experiencing it firsthand, but even so, I can tell it’s unlike any poison I’ve ever encountered. That said, it’s probably not too deadly since its color is only green, which is the most common and weakest color of poison out there.”

“This poison resembles the Jade Poison,” remarked one of the poison experts there. “Though it’s powerful, it’s only strong enough to threaten mortals.”

“If that’s the case, why are even Immortal-level creatures running away from it?”

“Poison doesn’t affect everyone the same way. It’s possible the creatures in the Abyssal Underworld are simply more vulnerable to it by nature.”

“Why don’t we deactivate the formation briefly–just long enough for me to test the poison?”

“Are you sure?”

The poison master nodded. “Truth be told, I’m not doing this out of goodwill. I’m simply very interested in studying this poison.”

“Let’s get an Array Master down here then.”

A few days later, a Level 8 Array Master was brought to the Abyssal Underground to deal with the formations.

When the Array Master arrived and saw the formations, he exclaimed with surprise, “Heavens! This type of formation is incredibly ancient and dates back to the Primordial Era! There are only a few Array Masters in this world who would be knowledgeable enough to create something like this!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2125: A Deadly Mistake
chapters.body:
Chapter 2125: A Deadly Mistake

“I never imagined I’d lay eyes on such an ancient formation…”

The level 8 Array Master was left in awe of the formations crafted by Yu Ning, an Array Master from the Primordial Era. With the passage of countless years, most formations from that era had been lost to time.

“So, can you deactivate it or not?” one of the cultivators there asked the Array Master. The Array Master coldly scoffed, “Of course, I can. However, it’s going to take a little longer than usual since formations from the Primordial Era are much more complex.” Although formations from the Primordial Era were far more intricate in design, they were not necessarily stronger than modern ones. Over time, advancements in array symbols and formations had reduced unnecessary complexity while retaining their power, and in some cases, even enhancing it.

After taking another moment to admire the formation, the Array Master began working on deactivating it.

Somewhere in the Abyssal Underground, Yu Ning instantly sensed something was wrong with her formation and warned Yuan, “Young Master, someone is trying to deactivate one of my formations.”

However, Yuan wasn’t surprised to hear this. In fact, he’d anticipated someone would try to release the poison, as it was in human nature to mess with things they shouldn’t. “Which one?” he asked Yu Ning.

“The formation on the 220th level.”

Hearing this, Yuan immediately began moving in that direction. Though he could get rid of the poison if these people were afflicted with it, he had to be close enough to do so.

A day later, Yuan arrived just in time, as the level 8 Array Master was only minutes away from breaking the formation.

He approached them and spoke out loud, “I wouldn’t do that if I were you.”

“What?”

Everyone there turned to look at him with raised eyebrows.

“And who are you?”

“The one who put up that formation,” said Yuan.

“What? You’re the one who set up these ancient formations?” The Array Master paused his work, staring at Yuan with wide eyes, but he didn’t recognize him at all. After all, every Array Master known to possess the skill to create such formations was a renowned individual.

“I put up the formation for a reason, you know? Don’t you see the poison mist on the other side? Or is everyone here blind?” Yuan mocked them.

The people there quickly frowned, and one of them responded, “While I appreciate your concern for others, do you know how much traffic your formations are blocking in the Abyssal Underground? Even if there is a little poison, it can easily be negated with poison-resistant pills.”

“A little poison? Do you know what kind of poison that is?” Yuan then asked.

“No, but according to the experts, it’s nothing too dangerous.”

Yuan couldn’t help but chuckle at such a response and said, “Nothing too dangerous? Alright. If you truly think that, go ahead and release the poison. I already warned you, so don’t blame me for whatever happens afterward.”

The frowns on their faces deepened after hearing Yuan’s ominous words.

Since everyone present was at least a Bronze Immortal, Yuan wasn’t too worried the poison would kill them instantly, and he could definitely remove the poison in time. However, these cultivators will suffer immense pain for a bit.

“The best way to learn is through pain, after all.”

“What should I do?” The Array Master asked the others for their opinion. “I can continue to remove the formation, or I can stop here.”

The cultivators there began to ponder.

Eventually, one of the poison experts spoke, “If you’re that afraid, you can all stay back and let me prove to you that there’s no need to be afraid of this level of poison.”

The cultivators nodded.

“In that case, let’s do it.”

“Right? That guy probably doesn’t even know anything about poisons and is just paranoid.”

Yuan didn’t try to convince these people and silently watched them remove the formation.

A few minutes later, the Array Master completed his preparations and began dismantling the formation.

The instant it was deactivated, the sealed poison surged outward like an unrelenting wave.

The Array Master immediately retreated, while the poison expert stepped forward, allowing the toxic mist to wash over him.

The others looked on in tense silence.

“See? There’s absolutely nothing to be worried abo—”

The poison master broke off mid-sentence, his once-calm expression twisting suddenly into one of sheer terror and shock.

“A-Are you okay?!” the cultivators cried out.

“H-Help… me…” the poison expert gasped, collapsing to the ground and reaching toward them in desperation as blood poured from his eyes, nose, mouth, and ears.

Panic surged through the cultivators, and they turned to flee—but the poison mist spread far faster than they had anticipated, engulfing them before they could escape. Soon, they were all on the ground, writhing in agony and gasping for breath as the poison’s excruciating pain tore through their bodies.

“What did I tell you? A bunch of idiots.”

Yuan shook his head.

Once a few seconds had passed, and right before the poison claimed their lives, Yuan retrieved the Baneful Abyss and collected the poison with it. Then, with Yu Ning’s help, reactivated the formation.

“This is my last warning to all of you—if you tamper with my formation and release the poison again, I won’t be here to save you.”

Without another word, Yuan turned and vanished into the darkness. Even with the poison purged from their bodies, the cultivators remained unable to move, the damage it had wrought requiring time to heal.

After their near-death experience, the cultivators quickly spread word to the others, warning them not to release the poison.

“That poison was unlike anything I have experienced before as a poison expert! Despite my poison resistance, it was able to completely immobilize me within seconds! If it weren’t for that mysterious expert, I would’ve perished on that day!” The poison expert recalled his experience.

And so, the people at the Abyssal Underground abandoned any further attempts to dismantle the formations, choosing instead to navigate around them—no matter how inconvenient it might be.

chapters.title: Chapter 2126: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans’ Gathering
chapters.body:
Chapter 2126: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans’ Gathering

After the incident with the cultivators deactivating his formation, Yuan continued to spend every moment spreading the poison mist until it was almost time for the gathering at the Undying Fiery Summit.

In just over a month, Yuan had already slaughtered more than seventy million beings within the Abyssal Underworld, leaving him with fewer than thirty million to complete his quest. Of course, he still needed to break through to the True Immortal realm.

Once it was almost time, Yuan retrieved all of the poison mist with the Baneful Abyss before leaving the Abyssal Underground.

Although he could’ve left the poison there to passively grind kills, he didn’t want to risk people deactivating his formation while he was gone, as he wouldn’t be able to save them in time.

Moreover, he could easily return to this place to finish his quest in the near future, and since he still had to reach True Immortal, he was in no rush to complete it.

After emerging from the Abyssal Underworld, Yuan lingered near the entrance for several moments, his divine sense spread wide as though searching for someone. Moments later, he flew off in a particular direction and descended before a familiar middle-aged man.

“Y-You! Why are you here?!” The middle-aged man exclaimed in shock, surprised to see Yuan standing before him, almost as if he was looking at a ghost.

“What’s wrong? You look as if you’d seen a ghost” Yuan remarked, snapping the middle-aged man out of his daze.

“What happened to the Senior Zeng?” The middle-aged man immediately asked about her.

“Oh, it was horrible.” Yuan quickly responded, his voice filled with anguish. “We made it to the 300th floor without any problem, but as we neared the Abyssal Crystal Ants’ territory, the ground beneath us exploded, releasing a bunch of poison mist. Fortunately, I have poison resistance. However, the guide… she was not so lucky, and she perished from the poison.”

“WHAT?! SHE’S DEAD?!” The middle-aged man exclaimed, his face filled with disbelief. “I’m sorry for your loss.” Yuan sighed.

“That can’t be! There’s no way she’s dead!”

Yuan shrugged, “I don’t know what else to tell you. If you don’t believe me, you can go see her corpse for yourself. However, I don’t know if itll still be there by the time you get there.”

The middle-aged man fell to his knees with a blank expression on his face.

Yuan said nothing more and simply turned to leave. Though the man might have been colluding with Zeng Guoquan, Yuan lacked the proof to act. And even if he had it, killing the man in front of so many witnesses would only make him appear the villain, not to mention he needed to convince everyone there about Zeng Guoquan’s true character. After departing from the Abyssal Underworld, Yuan followed Feng Yuxiang toward the Undying Fiery Summit.

Their destination lay within a land of eternal flame—a vast expanse wreathed in undying fire. For thousands of miles, the flames stretched unbroken, growing ever more intense the deeper one ventured, their power marked by a gradual shift in color.

“What’s the story behind this place?” Yuan asked.

Feng Yuxiang replied, “According to legend, a Primordial Phoenix was born here. At its birth, the flames you see now were ignited, and they have burned ever since. It’s said that even a great flood once tried and failed to extinguish them—hence the name, the Undying Flame Valley.”

“This is also a revered place where phoenixes come to cultivate, considered both popular and sacred among them. Humans, however, are strictly forbidden to enter, and any who are discovered will be executed on sight by the phoenixes who train here.”

“So I am in danger?” Yuan chuckled.

“Well, you’re a different story. Also, humans can enter if they received a phoenix’s approval, and with me here, nobody would dare to cause you any trouble,” she said. After two more days of travel, Yuan and Feng Yuxiang finally beheld a colossal solitary mountain in the distance—its entire form wreathed in roaring flames.

When they got close enough, they could see guards surrounding the entire place, treating it like a treasure vault.

“Identify yourselves.”

The guards commanded when Yuan and Feng Yuxiang got close enough.

“We’re the ones who called for this gathering,” said Feng Yuxiang.

“Then you must be the Primordial Phoenix! Please, follow me. The Ten Royal Phoenix Clans are already present and waiting for your arrival.”

And so, Feng Yuxiang and Yuan followed the guard up the mountain’s fiery slopes until they arrived at its summit, where a colossal colosseum-like structure stood. Within its walls lay a grand hall arranged much like a conference chamber.

Within the conference chamber were ten distinct sections, each clearly designated for one of the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans.

“Hm?” Both Yuan and Feng Yuxiang raised their brows at the sight before them.

“What’s going on? I thought only seven of the Royal Phoenix Clans had agreed to participate. So why does it look like all ten are here?” Feng Yuxiang asked as they stepped into the chamber, noting that each section was occupied by groups clad in their own distinct attire.

Noticing their arrival, the leader of the Rising Phoenix Clan spoke up.

“Welcome to the Royal Phoenix Chamber. As for your question, though three clans had initially declined to participate, they changed their minds at the last moment and chose to join the gathering after all.”

Feng Yuxiang glanced at Yuan, who wore a faint smile behind his mask.

Stepping into the spacious center of the chamber, Yuan raised his voice and asked, “Did you come here under the orders of the Celestial Emperor?”

The room fell silent, and the atmosphere instantly became stiff and heavy.

“That’s right,” someone suddenly replied. “Is there a problem with that?”

Yuan turned his gaze toward the phoenix who had spoken and said calmly, “No, not at all. In fact, I’m glad you’re all here. It will make this meeting far more interesting, and it spares me the trouble of hunting you down later.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2127: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans’ Gathering(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2127: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans’ Gathering(2)

Shortly after the Rising Phoenix Clan’s leader informed the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans of the gathering, the others promptly contacted the Celestial Emperor to report the situation, since failing to do so might have been seen as acting behind his back. Naturally, the Rising Phoenix Clan had done the same. That said, the Royal Phoenix Clans had contacted the Celestial Emperor largely as a formality, and few of them truly regarded the matter as treacherous.

When the Celestial Emperor learned of the existence of a second Primordial Phoenix, he was just as surprised and intrigued as the Royal Phoenix Clans. Thus, he suggested that everyone take part in the gathering. Since seven of the ten had already agreed to participate, he was mostly speaking to the three who initially declined.

“Before we even begin, I would like to confirm something.” Another phoenix suddenly spoke out loud. “To not waste anyone’s time, I want to see for myself the evidence that you are a Primordial Phoenix.”

Though nobody else said anything else, many of the phoenixes present could be seen nodding their heads in agreement, as they all shared the same sentiment.

“Sure.” Feng Yuxiang agreed without hesitation.

One of the phoenixes in the chamber produced a crimson needle crafted from a crystal- lie material and spoke loudly, “Instead of the Golden Phoenix Needle, we’ll be using the Celestial Phoenix Needle. It was crafted from a Primordial Phoenix’s feather and blood. Not only is it more accurate than the Golden Phoenix Needle, but it will also produce a distinct reaction upon sensing the Primordial Phoenix bloodline. The other Primordial Phoenix also used this needle to test their bloodline, so it would only be fair to test your bloodline with it as well.”

“Use whatever needle you want. The results won’t change.” Feng Yuxiang responded with full confidence.

The phoenix holding the Celestial Phoenix Needle rose to his feet and approached Feng Yuxiang, needle in hand. Moments later, he drew several drops of her blood and waited. Within seconds, the needle began to tremble before bursting into a brilliant crimson flame, the fire taking the form of a phoenix with wings outstretched.

“It’s true! She’s a genuine Primordial Phoenix!” the man exclaimed, awe written across his face.

The others in the chamber echoed his astonishment, their expressions a mix of shock and delight. No matter their personal feelings, the appearance of two Primordial Phoenixes at the same time was nothing short of a miracle—an event worthy of celebration.

“That’s not all!” The man with the Celestial Phoenix Needle continued. “The purity of her bloodline is at 98.8 percent! It’s much higher than the other Primordial Phoenix’s bloodline!”

“What?!” the leader of the Rising Phoenix Clan exclaimed, leaping to her feet in shock. “I tested her bloodline only three months ago, and it was at 97.9 percent! Are you telling me it’s risen by nearly a whole percent in that short time?!”

“Perhaps the Golden Phoenix Needle failed to measure her bloodline accurately,” one of the phoenixes suggested.

However, Feng Yuxiang dismissed that notion and said, “I don’t know if she mentioned it, but my bloodline was cursed for thousands of years and only began recovering a few years ago. I believed I had already fully recovered, but clearly, that wasn’t the case. I’m willing to bet that my bloodline will reach 99 percent sooner or later.”

The chamber fell into silence, as none of the phoenixes present could truly fathom what it meant to possess a bloodline purity of ninety-nine percent.

“Is there anything else you need to verify before we begin the meeting?” Yuan asked, breaking the silence.

“What about you?” Someone responded a second later. “Who are you, and what qualifications do you have to stand before us, much less speak with us?”

“Do I qualify to be here?” Feng Yuxiang suddenly asked.

“Of course. You’re a Primordial Phoenix with near 99 percent bloodline purity,” the phoenix quickly responded.

“Then he, my master, definitely qualifies to stand before you.”

“Outrageous! How can a Primordial Phoenix like you serve as a mere human’s servant?!” someone exclaimed, their voice brimming with indignation.

“Why not? The other Primordial Phoenix serves the Celestial Emperor, and as far as I know, the Celestial Emperor is human as well,” Feng Yuxiang replied.

“I-That’s a different story! The Celestial Emperor is the most powerful human in the Nine Heavens!”

“Well, I beg to differ. My Young Master is unquestionably superior to the Celestial Emperor,’ Feng Yuxiang declared calmly, not a trace of hesitation in her voice. Her words left the room dumbfounded.

A little over twenty phoenixes were present, with two to three representatives from each of the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans.

“Let’s calm down,” Yuan said after a brief silence.

He went on calmly, “I was the one who summoned this gathering—not you, nor anyone else in this chamber. If you believe I am unqualified to be here, then you are free to leave.”

The phoenixes narrowed their eyes at Yuan’s boldness, but despite their displeasure, they all remained in their seats.

“Now with that settled, let’s begin the meeting, shall we?”

The phoenixes nodded.

“Let’s begin with a quick introduction. My name’s Yuan.”

“I’m Feng Yuxiang.”

Despite giving his real name, none of the phoenixes recognized it. Although his name had spread during the Sword Emperor’s Qualifications, most beasts held no interest in matters irrelevant to them and hadn’t bothered to watch. Even in the wider world, the number of people who had witnessed the event was insignificant when compared to the grand scale of the populace.

The leader of the Rising Phoenix Clan introduced themselves next.

“Feng Lingyang of the Rising Phoenix Clan.”

“Feng Niepan of the Nirvana Phoenix Clan.” The phoenix seated beside the Rising Phoenix Clan went next.

“Feng Qiongyao of the Crimson Rebirth Phoenix Clan.”

“Feng Yushan of the Heavenly Phoenix Clan.”

One by one, the leaders of the Royal Phoenix Clans introduced themselves.

By the end, Yuan had learned the names of every clan.

chapters.title: Chapter 2128: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans’ Gathering(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2128: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans’ Gathering(3)

After the introduction ended, Yuan spoke, “I know your time is very precious, so I will get straight to the point.”

“You’ve allied yourselves with the Celestial Emperor because he promised to help you improve your bloodline by three percent. However, the Celestial Emperor and I are… let’s just say we’re not very friendly with each other, and I want you to serve me instead of the Celestial Emperor.”

The phoenixes in the chamber stared at Yuan with wide eyes, as though unable to believe what they had just heard.

After a moment of silence, they burst out laughing.

“You want us to serve you instead of the Celestial Emperor?! Hahaha! That’s the funniest joke I’ve heard all decade long!”

Once the laughter calmed down a little, the leader of the Crimson Rebirth Phoenix spoke in an offended tone, “First of all, we don’t “serve’ the Celestial Emperor. In fact, we don’t serve anyone. We are working with him. That’s completely different from serving him.” The leader of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan then spoke, “I don’t know how you managed to coax the Primordial Phoenix into becoming your servant, but it won’t work on the rest of us. Regardless of your offer, we won’t serve you.”

“Why don’t you hold onto that thought until after you hear my offer? I did mention that I have an offer even better than the one the Celestial Emperor gave you, hence why some of you even bothered to show up here.”

The room fell silent as the phoenixes began talking with each other through voice transmission.

Yuan couldn’t care less about what they were talking about and patiently waited for them to be done.

Several minutes later, the leader of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan finally spoke. “Very well—we’ll entertain you for now. Let’s hear your offer.”

Yuan’s voice was steady and deliberate as he spoke loudly, “Not only will I raise your bloodline purity by more than three percent, but I will also grant the first three phoenix clans that agree to serve me the chance to evolve their bloodline.”

“What?! Evolve our bloodline?!” Several clan leaders shot to their feet in shock.

However, a few immediately dismissed his claim, sneering with disdain and anger. “You expect Us to believe such nonsense? Is that the same trick you used to deceive the Primordial Phoenix?”

“What good would come from deceiving the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans? I’m not that foolish,” Yuan said with a shake of his head. “While I cannot promise success in evolving your bloodline, I can guarantee that I will raise its purity to its utmost potential. There are also other benefits for serving me, but I will save those for later.”

“Even if you are telling the truth, are you really expecting us to just take your word for it? Just like how we confirmed the Primordial Phoenix’s bloodline, we’ll need evidence for your claims.”

“Of course, I’l prove it.”

Yuan scanned the room with a sharp gaze before stopping at a certain individual.

“Feng Haoyu, come over here.”

“Me?” Feng Haoyu, who had shown up to the gathering with his mother and the Grand Elder, wore a dumbfounded look on his face after being called out.

Yuan silently nodded.

Feng Haoyu stood up and approached the center of the room.

Then, Yuan asked him, “Before we begin, I would like to apologize for fooling you while pretending to be a Primordial Phoenix. I also won’t blame you if you no longer wish to serve me.”

“That’s…” Feng Haoyu began, but finding himself at a loss for words, he quickly closed his mouth.

“Regardless of your decision, I will permanently improve your bloodline as an apology.” Yuan turned to look at the phoenix who tested Feng Yuxiang’s bloodline and said, “You should test his bloodline before I begin so you can see the results.”

The phoenix, who was from the Graceful Phoenix Clan, nodded and approached Feng Haoyu with the Celestial Phoenix Needle in hand to collect his blood.

A few moments after collecting Feng Haoyu’s blood, the Celestial Phoenix Needle revealed his bloodline’s purity.

“86.4 percent.”

“Not bad.” Feng Lingyang, his mother, nodded with a satisfied expression on her face. Due to her weakened bloodline, Feng Haoyu’s bloodline had also suffered, dropping from its original 87 percent.

“86.4 percent, correct? Keep that number in mind.”

As Yuan spoke, he retrieved the Baneful Abyss and, before everyone’s eyes, pricked the tip of his finger with it, drawing several drops of his blood. Though he still had some preprepared blood in his possession, he needed to show the phoenixes evidence that it belonged to him.

The instant Yuan’s fresh blood touched the air, a unique aroma spread throughout the chamber. The moment the phoenixes sensed it, they began to salivate unconsciously. After collecting his own blood, Yuan held it before Feng Haoyu and commanded, “Drink it

“Excuse me?” Feng Haoyu’s eyes widened at his command.

“If you want to improve your bloodline, drink it. If you don’t want to, I’l give it to someone else,” said Yuan.

Feng Haoyu fell into deep thought over Yuan’s offer. For a divine beast, drinking the blood of a human was deemed disgraceful—an act befitting only wild magical beasts who reveled in such savagery.

Yet, despite his distaste for the notion of drinking a human’s blood, Feng Haoyu couldn’t shake the feeling that refusing would lead to immense regret, especially when he noticed Feng Yuxiang staring at the blood with a ravenous gleam in her eyes.

“Okay, I’ll drink it.”

With resolute determination, Feng Haoyu accepted the blood and downed it in a single quip.

“Delicious!” he exclaimed involuntarily, the taste unlike anything he had ever known— refined and divine, as though crafted by the heavens themselves.

Then, a familiar sensation swept through his body, one strikingly similar to the feeling he had experienced when Yuan bestowed his blessing over a year ago.

Just to be safe, Yuan gave Feng Haoyu the Immortal Monarch’s Blessing.

“Alright, you may test his bloodline again,” Yuan said as he turned to the phoenix holding the Celestial Phoenix Needle.

chapters.title: Chapter 2129: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans’ Gathering(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2129: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans’ Gathering(4)

“What? You want me to test his bloodline already? It hasn’t even been a full minute since whatever you did to him! Do you think our bloodline can be improved so easily?” The phoenix with the Celestial Phoenix Needle remarked with a frown on his face.

“You’ll know once you test his bloodline,” Yuan replied with confidence.

“…Whatever. If you say so.”

The phoenix proceeded to test Feng Haoyu’s bloodline again.

A moment later, when the phoenix saw the results, he dropped the Celestial Phoenix Needle onto the ground, his face frozen with shock and disbelief.

“Well? What’s the result?” the other phoenixes pressed, their voices brimming with curiosity and anticipation.

The phoenix swallowed nervously before responding in a dazed voice, “86.9 percent…” “What?! His bloodline purity has improved by half a percent in just a minute?! Impossible! Test his blood again!” The phoenixes urged.

Picking up the Celestial Phoenix Needle, the phoenix looked at Feng Haoyu, who was just as dazed by the result, and said, “Excuse me…”

He tested Feng Haoyu’s bloodline again.

“Impossible!” The phoenix exclaimed a moment later.

“H-His bloodline is now at 87.3 percent!”

The entire chamber fell silent as the phoenixes struggled to comprehend what they were witnessing. In just a minute or so, Feng Haoyu’s bloodline purity has improved by nearly an entire percent, something that normally requires hundreds of thousands of years. “What in heaven’s name is going on?! How can consuming a human’s blood strengthen his bloodline?!” The leader of the Radiant Phoenix Clan exclaimed.

Though they wanted to believe it was some kind of trick, they knew that was impossible, as the Celestial Phoenix Needle could not be deceived. Yet, a few of them soon reached a startling thought.

“Wait… a human’s blood capable of strengthening a divine beast’s bloodline? That sounds exactly like the Immortal Monarch’s power…

None dared to voice such a notion aloud, for it bordered on madness. The Immortal Monarch had perished many eras ago, after all. And yet, the more they turned the thought over in their minds, the more plausible it began to feel.

“Well, are you satisfied? Or do you need more evidence?” Yuan suddenly said, breaking the brief moment of silence in the chamber.

“One… one more time. Let me test your bloodline again!” the phoenix with the Celestial Phoenix Needle demanded, his eyes fixed on Feng Haoyu.

Feng Haoyu quickly nodded in approval, as he, too, was eager to know if his bloodline had strengthened further.

The other phoenixes swallowed nervously.

A moment later, the results came back.

“8…89.3 percent! His bloodline has improved by yet another entire percent!”

“Heavens! Another percent?! Even if the improvements stopped at this point, his bloodline still improved by two percent in just a few minutes! This level of progress is unheard oft”

“How much more will his bloodline improve?” The leader of the Etheral Phoenix Clan asked Yuan.

He shrugged,”Honestly,! have no clue.If you really want to know, you’ll have to wait a few days for the final results-weeks to be safe.”

Very well, I’ll acknowledge your ability to enhance our bloodline.But what about your second claim? If true, it would be an

“Unfortunately, I cannot provide proof at this moment, as I currently lack the ability to do so,” Yuan said. “However, once I regain more of my strength, I will be able to begin attempting it. For now, you’ll have to take me at my word.”

“Regaining your strength…? Just who are you?” The leader of the Jade Phoenix Clan questioned.

“Who am I? I think many of you already have a clue about my identity, so why don’t you try to confirm it?” Yuan responded with a smile on his face.

Feng Lingyang of the Rising Phoenix Clan was the first to stand up and open her mouth, “Then if you don’t mind me asking… Are you related to the Immortal Monarch?”

“What?! The Immortal Monarch?!” Those who did not connect the dots exclaimed in shock after hearing his name.

The atmosphere in the chamber quickly shifted, becoming heavier as the phoenixes anxiously awaited Yuan’s response.

“You can say that.” Yuan acknowledged.

“W-What is your relationship with the Immortal Monarch? Is he still alive?” Feng Lingyang continued to question.

“Yes, the Immortal Monarch is alive. As for my relationship with him…” After a brief pause, Yuan continued, *I am the Immortal Monarch.”

The phoenixes in the chamber leapt to their feet, their eyes widening so much it seemed they might fall from their sockets.

The revelation was so staggering and confusing that silence blanketed the chamber for several long moments.

Then, the stillness was shattered with the sound of laughter.

“Hahaha! I can’t hold it in any longer! You’re the Immortal Monarch? That’s hilarious!” scoffed a handsome young man seated beside the leader of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan.

Yuan turned his gaze to the young man, who had risen to his feet and was now striding toward the center of the chamber, closing the distance between them.

The young man stopped right before Yuan and spoke, “If you’re really the Immortal Monarch, you should be able to make me kneel with just your words, right? Try it.”

“Are you sure you won’t mind the humiliation?” Yuan asked.

“Don’t worry, I won’t be humiliated,” he confidently responded.

“Why? Because you’re a Primordial Phoenix?”

The smile on the young man’s face instantly disappeared.

“Oh? How did you figure it out? I purposefully suppressed my aura to hide it.”

“Just a guess,” Yuan said. “I figured that if the Celestial Emperor wanted the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans present at this gathering, he would also want the Primordial Phoenix to attend. Also, while you can hide your aura, you can’t stop the others from giving you glances.”

“I see… so the others sold me out.”

Realizing his cover had been exposed, the young man released the restraint on his aura, letting it surge forth and flood the entire chamber.

chapters.title: Chapter 2130: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans' Gathering(5)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2130: Ten Royal Phoenix Clans' Gathering(5)

“So, you want me to prove my identity as the Immortal Monarch by forcing you to kneel, is that it?” Yuan asked.

“That’s right. Do you have any issues with my request?” The Primordial Phoenix said. “Honestly, that’s far too boring, as it would take no effort at all. Besides, I’ve no desire to humiliate the Celestial Emperor’s lapdog and give him yet another reason to hate me.” The Primordial Phoenix trembled after hearing Yuan’s words, and he muttered, “What did you just call me? A lapdog? I am nobody’s lapdog, you bastard!”

“Oh? If you’re not the Celestial Emperor’s lapdog, what are you? His slave?”

Flames erupted from the Primordial Phoenix’s body, weaving around him as they formed an armor of pure fire.

“You know what? I don’t care if you’re the Immortal Monarch or not. You’re not going to leave this place until I teach you how to respect a superior being.”

“So quick to resort to violence? Your temper is quite short-even by phoenix standards,” Yuan said with a light chuckle.

“I will not be mocked by a mere human!” the Primordial Phoenix roared, thrusting his right hand-wreathed in True Primordial Fire-toward Yuan’s neck.

Young Master!” Feng Yuxiang cried, instinctively seizing the Primordial Phoenix’s arm. Her eyes burned with killing intent as she snapped, “How dare you lay a hand on my Young Master! Do you have a death wish?!”

The Primordial Phoenix sneered at her. “This doesn’t concern you.”

“Of course, it concerns me! You’re attacking my—”

“It’s okay.” Yuan interrupted. “His flames are too weak to harm me, anyway.”

“Not only did you insult me, but you dare to look down on my fire?” The Primordial Phoenix trembled in anger.

In the very next moment, his entire body erupted with invisible flames, so intense they warped the surrounding space.

The True Primordial Fire instantly engulfed the chamber’s center and swallowed both Yuan and Feng Yuxiang within its blaze.

Just as dragons possess reverse scales that drive them into madness when touched, phoenixes, 100, have their own reverse scales—their flames, which are their identity and pride. For many phoenixes, their flames are more precious than life itself. To have them insulted is an offense most people cannot fathom.

“Senior Feng Danyu, please calm yourself! We’re still at the Undying Fiery Summit, which, regardless of your bloodline, forbids violence! This is a sacred rule passed down by our ancestors!” The other phoenixes quickly warned him.

Though Feng Danyu, the Primordial Phoenix, was seething with rage, he had not lost all reason. Regaining control, he swiftly withdrew his True Primordial Fire. Yet the damage was already done, and the phoenixes could only pray for Yuan—for no human could possibly withstand the True Primordial Fire.

“What?! He’s completely unscathed!”

When the phoenixes looked closer, their shock deepened, as Yuan bore no wounds at all, not even the faintest trace of a burn.

“How is that possible…? Could he have achieved Fire Harmony? No… even that wouldn’t grant complete protection against True Primordial Fire since it bypasses all fire resistance!”

Yuan ignored the shocked phoenixes and focused solely on Feng Daoyu.

“Since you attacked me first, I finally have a reason to mess with you.”

“What are you—”

Feng Daoyu opened his mouth to speak, but before he could utter more than a few words, Yuan cut him off. “Shut up.”

2m

In that instant, Feng Daoyu’s mouth closed. When he tried to open it again, he found he had completely lost control. He tried forcefully opening his mouth with his hands, but such efforts were also futile, almost as if his lips had been glued shut.

The Ten Royal Phoenix Clans swallowed nervously upon seeing this, but they continued to watch in silence.

Yet Feng Daoyu refused to relent. In his struggle, he suddenly unleashed his cultivation base, revealing himself to be at the 7th level of the God Ascension realm.

The moment he unleashed his cultivation, Feng Daoyu broke free from Yuan’s command.

“That doesn’t prove anything!” he shouted.

“Then how about this?”

Yuan narrowed his eyes and commanded in a stern tone, his voice amplified by Immortal Qi.

“Lie on the ground and crawl around like a worm,” he commanded.

“What are you—”

Feng Daoyu was cut off once more as his body suddenly crumpled to the ground, thrashing and writhing like a worm dragged from the soil.

Of course, Feng Daoyu managed to break free within seconds, but by then, the damage had already been done.

“Is that still not enough to prove my identity? If not, then I know only one other way—and that is to forcibly extract your blood essence,’ Yuan said in a somewhat threatening tone.

“You wouldn’t dare!” Feng Daoyu exclaimed as he subconsciously backed away from Yuan.

Yuan shrugged, “You’re right. Even if you are the Celestial Emperor’s pet, I am not cruel enough to use such a vicious technique on you.”

“For the last time, I am not the Celestial Emperor’s lapdog, slave, or pet! Just like the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans, I merely agreed to assist him!”

“Oh? In exchange for what? I doubt you’d be willing to work for free.”

“T-That is none of your business!”

“If you don’t want to tell me, I won’t pry,” Yuan said.

“Then, do you believe that I am the Immortal Monarch?”

Feng Daoyu clenched his fists tightly and growled, “I believe you—but so what? Even if you are the Immortal Monarch, don’t think that alone will make me respect or revere yout”

Yuan smiled behind his mask and said, “I couldn’t care less about how you treat me. I am only trying to prove my identity so the Ten Royal Phoenix Clan won’t have to worry about me being a fraud.”

“Why?” one of the clan leaders suddenly asked. “Why are you trying to make us choose you over the Celestial Emperor? Can we not work with both sides?”

“Because the Celestial Emperor and I are enemies, and we’ll likely clash with each other in the future,” Yuan revealed calmly, dumbfounding everyone in the room.

chapters.title: Chapter 2131: End Of The Meeting
chapters.body:
Chapter 2131: End Of The Meeting

“W-Wait a second! I must’ve misheard you… Did you just say you’re enemies with the Celestial Emperor?” the leader of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan asked aloud, snapping out of her daze.

Yuan calmly nodded, “That’s exactly what I just said.”

“You’re essentially asking us to fight the Celestial Emperor with you! This is much more than just being your follower! Do you have any idea what you’re asking of us?” Another leader exclaimed.

“I am aware, but I want to clear up one thing,” said Yuan. “I am not asking you to fight the Celestial Emperor. I wanted to let you know that he and I will be fighting in the future, so you know what you’re getting yourselves into. I’m not going to ask you to fight for or with me.”

“Are you serious? Why would you even share this with us? What if we refuse to help you and side with the Celestial Emperor instead?” asked the leader of the Crimson Rebirth Phoenix Clan.

Yuan replied, “Because now you also know that siding with the Celestial Emperor may put you in direct conflict with me in the future. I won’t force you to choose. If everyone here decides to support the Celestial Emperor, then so be it.”

“And I’m not asking you to decide right this moment. I’l give you one year to choose whose side you’ll stand on.”

“Hahaha!” the Primordial Phoenix suddenly burst into laughter.

“What’s so funny?” Yuan asked him.

“It’s funny because the answer is obvious!” he said.

“Oh? Enlighten me.”

“Why would we ever choose you over the Celestial Emperor? So what if you’re the immortal Monarch? You’re nowhere near as powerful as you once were.”

“And what makes you say that?” Yuan asked calmly.

The other phoenixes listened with curiosity as the Primordial Phoenix sneered.

“I know more about you than you realize, Immortal Monarch. I know that most of your reputation was built on the Nine Divine Supremes and that most of your strength came from them. But now that they’re gone, you’re just another human cultivator! Sure, you might command beasts and enhance our bloodlines, but on your own, you’re weak!” Hearing the Primordial Phoenix’s reasoning, the other phoenixes found his logic sound and began nodding in agreement.

“He’s right, Immortal Monarch. You were a nobody before you gained the Nine Divine Supremes’ support,” said the leader of the Immortal Ember Phoenix Clan.

“That’s why he wants us to serve him. He’s desperately trying to replace the Nine Divine Supremes!” Another phoenix remarked.

“I don’t need a year to decide. I will continue being by the Celestial Emperor’s side,” said the Primordial Phoenix.

Yuan smiled faintly and said, “You’re right—my reputation spread far and wide largely because of the Nine Divine Supremes. But even if some of them are no longer here, their seats will not remain vacant forever. In fact, I have already begun looking for more comrades.”

“And you’re looking at one right now.” Feng Yuxiang said with a prideful expression on her face.

“Wait… the way you said that… it almost sounds like some of the Nine Divine Supremes are still alive,” remarked Feng Yushan of the Heavenly Phoenix Clan.

“Is that so hard to believe? If I, the Immortal Monarch, am standing right before you, why couldn’t the others still be alive?”

The room fell into silence. If even one of the Nine Divine Supremes still existed in this era, it would be enough to shift the balance of the world.

“Wait! I just remembered—there’s a rumor that the Dragon Goddess Yeyou has returned!” someone suddenly exclaimed. “I dismissed it as nonsense before… but given the circumstances, it might actually be true!”

“I also heard of this rumor!” Another remarked.

“Me too!”

“Is it true? That Dragon Goddess Yeyou is still alive?!” One of them asked Yuan.

Dragon Goddess Yeyou was the strongest of the Nine Divine Supremes. If she were truly alive, she alone would be enough to handle the Celestial Emperor.

However, Yuan shrugged and said, *I don’t know.”

“What…2″ The phoenixes looked at him with wide eyes.

He continued, *I have already said enough. Unless I am certain that you’re going to be my ally, I won’t reveal any more information.”

“With that said, I am going to end the meeting here. And just a reminder. The first three clans to swear their loyalty to me will have their bloodlines improved—possibly upgraded—as well as other benefits. Those who do so afterward wil still have their bloodlines improved, but that’s all. You have one year to decide.”

Without another word, Yuan left the Undying Fiery Summit.

“What are you going to do for a whole year?” Feng Yuxiang asked him.

“I’m going to consume the Dragon Ancestor’s blood essence to prepare for the Primordial Realm,” he calmly replied.

“What if the Rising Phoenix Clan refuses to hand over the Ancient Seal and sides with the Celestial Emperor?”

“If they side with the Celestial Emperor and become our enemy, we can only forcefully take the Ancient Seal from them. That said, I have a good feeling they’ll side with us.” Meanwhile, shortly after Yuan left, the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans remained at the Undying Feiry Summit and began another meeting.

“So, what do we think about the Immortal Monarch’s offer?” The leader of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan asked.

“It’s a tempting offer, and I would’ve definitely accepted it without a heartbeat if he hadn’t mentioned being the Celestial Emperor’s enemy.”

“Well, he did say that he won’t make us fight the Celestial Emperor if we don’t want to.” “Then why is he even trying to gain our loyalty if not to use us against the Celestial Emperor?”

“Perhaps it’s so that he won’t have to deal with us alongside the Celestial Emperor?” “That actually makes sense. He’s already fighting the Celestial Emperor. If I were in his shoes, I wouldn’t want to fight our Ten Royal Phoenix Clans, either.”

“You lot can stay here and continue wasting your time. I’m going to return to the Celestial Emperor to report to him about our situation,” said the Primordial Phoenix as he began walking toward the exit.

chapters.title: Chapter 2132: Consuming The Dragon Ancestor'S Blood Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2132: Consuming The Dragon Ancestor'S Blood Essence

“You’re leaving already? You don’t care about who will choose to side with the Immortal Monarch or not?” The leader of the Etheral Phoenix Clan asked, halting the Primordial Phoenix’s movement.(More chapters in Buyers club for TMT) “Honestly? I couldn’t care less. If you’re foolish enough to side with the Immortal Monarch and go against the Celestial Emperor, then you deserve whatever fate awaits you,” he sneered. Then, without waiting for their response, the Primordial Phoenix continued walking toward the exit, leaving the chamber shortly later. Just as he left the building, the Primordial Phoenix noticed a certain figure approaching the summit. It was Feng Yuxiang. “What are you doing back here? Forgot to say something?” The Primordial Phoenix asked her in a somewhat sarcastic tone. “Well, someone has to stay behind for those who want to swear their loyalty before the one-year deadline,” she said. “Is that so? You’re … Protected Content This chapter requires a free account to read. Sign up or log in to continue reading “Cultivation Online”. =) Sign In / Register < Back to Novel

chapters.title: Chapter 2133: A Month Before The Deadline
chapters.body:
Chapter 2133: A Month Before The Deadline

“It’s already been half a year, and not a single clan has sided with the Young Master…” Feng Yuxiang sighed, her voice echoing softly through the empty chamber. The words of the Primordial Phoenix still lingered in her mind, gnawing at her optimism day after day. She couldn’t help but wonder what would happen if none of the Royal Phoenix Clans chose to side with Yuan and instead continued their allegiance to the Celestial Emperor. A few days later, the chamber doors creaked open, and a lone figure stepped inside. Feng Yuxiang turned, expecting to see one of the clan leaders—only to be disappointed. “Oh… It’s just you,” she muttered, her tone tinged with disappointment. “What do you want?” Feng Haoyu bowed slightly and said, “I wish to swear my allegiance to the Immortal Monarch—or rather, to reaffirm my loyalty to him.” “That’s cool and all, but what about your … Protected Content This chapter requires a free account to read. Sign up or log in to continue reading “Cultivation Online”. =) Sign In / Register < Back to Novel

chapters.title: Chapter 2134: The Ten Royal Phoenix Clans' Decision
chapters.body:
Chapter 2134: The Ten Royal Phoenix Clans' Decision

“My reasoning, huh? It’s actually quite simple. I know you’re worried about the other Royal Phoenix Clans, but once we side with the Immortal Monarch, their stance won’t matter anymore. In fact, even the title of “Royal Clan’ will lose its weight,” said the Grand Elder.

“What…2” Feng Lingyang’s eyes widened. His reasoning wasn’t what she expected, yet the more she considered it, the more sense it made.

The Grand Elder continued, “Even if we’re the only clan to follow the Immortal Monarch, the others won’t dare touch us with him behind us. But if we side with the Celestial Emperor, we’ll inevitably end up clashing with the Immortal Monarch—and if you ask me, I believe his strength surpasses the Celestial Emperor’s.”

“Is it because of Dragon Goddess Yeyou?” Feng Lingyang asked.

“Not just her,” he replied. “Though he didn’t state it outright, I believe—based on his words—that other Divine Supremes have also survived. And if the Immortal Monarch defeats the Celestial Emperor, our Rising Phoenix Clan will undoubtedly soar to the top, where we won’t even need the title of “Royal Clan.”

“That’s just pure speculation, and you can say the same thing for the others. If the Celestial Emperor defeats the Immortal Monarch, that would undoubtedly spell the end for our Rising Phoenix Clan.”

“It all comes down to whether you want to believe in the Celestial Emperor or the immortal Monarch.” The Grand Elder sighed.

Feng Lingyang didn’t give a response. Instead, after a long moment of silence, she said, “I’m going to the Undying Fiery Summit.”

“I’ll come with you.”

Sometime later.

“Mother? Grand Elder?” Feng Haoyu’s face lit up as he saw the two enter the chamber. Feng Lingyang’s gaze swept across the room before settling on Feng Yuxiang.

“Where is the Immortal Monarch?” she asked.

“He hasn’t returned yet. If you have any questions, I’l be more than willing to answer them.”

“I would like to know why the Celestial Emperor and the Immortal Monarch are enemies. What started the feud between them?”

“That’s…” Feng Yuxiang began, only to close her mouth again, realizing she had no answer to give.

“I’m sorry—I don’t know the exact cause. But what I can say with certainty is that it was the Celestial Emperor who showed hostility first. He cursed my Young Master even though they’ve never met each other before.”

Feng Lingyang raised her eyebrows and replied in a doubtful tone, “The Celestial Emperor cursed the Immortal Monarch even though they’ve never met before? That’s hard to believe and completely irrational.”

“I understand your skepticism, and I am just as puzzled, but it’s the truth. Have you heard about the incident at the Dragon Spiral Mountain recently?”

“You mean when the Celestial Emperor attacked the Holy Dragon Clan? I am aware of the incident, but nobody knows the reasoning behind his action.”

“It’s related to my Young Master,” Feng Yuxiang revealed.

“Seriously…2″ Those present stared at her with wide eyes.

“If you want to know more, you’ll have to ask my Young Master himself.”

Feng Lingyang nodded, “Okay.”

While they waited for Yuan to return, Feng Haoyu approached Feng Lingyang and asked, “Mother, has the clan reached a decision yet?”

“As you know, the majority of the clan are in support of siding with the Immortal Monarch, but in the end—”

“It’s all up to you,” Feng Haoyu interrupted.

“I have also made a decision, but I want to know the reasoning behind their enmity first.” Time continued to pass, and in the blink of an eye, an entire year had passed since the end of the meeting.

A few days before the deadline, the other Royal Phoenix Clans returned to the Undying Fiery Summit.

“Where’s the Immortal Monarch?” They asked upon their arrival.

“He’s not here yet, but he should arrive soon,” said Feng Yuxiang.

“Feng Lingyang, did the Rising Phoenix Clan swear their loyalty to the Immortal Monarch already?” The leader of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan questioned her after seeing that she’d arrived before them.

“No.” Feng Lingyang replied with a simple and straightforward answer.

“Are you sure? I heard that you arrived a month early,” pressed the leader of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan.

“So what?”

The atmosphere in the chamber grew heavy, tension pressing down on everyone present.

But it shattered the moment the door opened and a lone figure stepped inside, drawing every gaze in the room.

However, it wasn’t Yuan.

“What are you doing here? Weren’t you clear about siding with the Celestial Emperor? Or have you changed your mind?” Feng Yuxiang asked the Primordial Phoenix, who had just entered the chamber.

The Primordial Phoenix sneered. “In your dreams. I’m only here to watch the show—and to savor the disappointment on the Immortal Monarch’s face when he realizes that he’s no longer relevant in this day and age.”

He looked around the room and continued, “So, where’s the Immortal Monarch?”

“He’ll arrive as soon as he’s done with his business.”

“He’s the one who made the deadline, yet he’s the last to show up? It’s almost like he doesn’t even care.”

“Calm down, little bird. It’s not like I am late.”

A calm voice suddenly resounded.

“What?” The Primordial Phoenix and everyone else in the chamber turned toward the entrance—only to see Yuan standing there, having appeared out of thin air.

“The hell? How long has he been there? I didn’t sense a thing until he spoke!”

Everyone in the chamber was shaken by his complete lack of presence.

“This bastard… what has he done in the past year? He feels like an entirely different person!”

Though none of them could explain it, they all felt it. Something within Yuan had changed drastically, and he was vastly different from when they last saw him a year ago. Yuan ignored their stares and strode unhurriedly to the center of the chamber.

His eyes swept across the gathered phoenixes as he spoke in a calm, clear voice, “So, have you reached a decision? Will you continue to serve the Celestial Emperor, or will you stand beneath my banner?” (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2135: The Ten Royal Phoenix Clans' Decision(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2135: The Ten Royal Phoenix Clans' Decision(2)

“Before we begin, I would like to ask you something.”

Feng Lingyang suddenly stepped forward.

“Go ahead.” Yuan calmly nodded.

“Why are you enemies with the Celestial Emperor?”

“A good question. Unfortunately, I do not know the answer to that question.”

“What? You don’t know why you’re enemies with the Celestial Emperor? How’s that even possible?” said the leader of the Crimson Rebirth Phoenix Clan.

“I bet you he’s making it up,” said the Primordial Phoenix.

“What would I gain from making this up? I’ve never even met the Celestial Emperor. Yet, he placed a curse on me for some reason.”

“Are you trying to claim that the Celestial Emperor showed you hostility for no reason? Sorry, but I don’t believe that,” said the leader of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan. The Primordial Phoenix burst into laughter. “You’re so desperate to paint the Celestial Emperor as a villain, but all you’re doing is making yourself look like a foot”

He turned to face the others and spoke loudly, “Is this really someone you want to serve?! A jealous, arrogant, and foolish human like him?”

The room fell into silence.

But it did not last long, for it was soon broken by the leader of the Crimson Rebirth Phoenix Clan, who declared firmly, “The Crimson Rebirth Phoenix Clan rejects your offer, Immortal Monarch. We will continue to stand with the Celestial Emperor.”

Then, like a chain reaction, the others began voicing their decisions.

“The Immortal Ember Phoenix Clan refuses your offer, Immortal Monarch.”

“The Burning Lotus Phoenix Clan also refuses.”

“The Radiant Phoenix Clan refuses…”

One after another, the rejections echoed through the chamber, pouring in without pause. “My Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan rejects your offer.”

“Looks like no one’s willing to side with you, huh? Just as I predicted,” the Primordial Phoenix mocked.

Yet despite the wave of rejections, Yuan remained calm and composed.

Eventually, only a single clan had yet to respond—the one clan that still hadn’t refused Yuan’s offer.

“Mother…” Feng Haoyu looked at his mother, his face tight with worry.

The others in the chamber turned their glares upon her, and under the crushing weight of their stares, Feng Lingyang swallowed hard, her body trembling.

“What’s wrong, Feng Lingyang? We’re all waiting for you,” said Feng Tianhuang, the imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan’s leader.

Feng Lingyang closed her eyes and took several deep breaths. When she opened her eyes again, they flickered with resolution.

“The Rising Phoenix Clan will accept your offer, Immortal Monarch,” Feng Lingyang declared.

Feng Haoyu and the Grand Elder’s faces lit up with joy at her words, while the other clans openly displayed their disappointment and disdain, making no effort to conceal it. “What is the meaning of this, Feng Lingyang?” Feng Tianhuang stepped forward, her body radiating oppressive pressure.

“Explain yourself!” she demanded.

Feng Lingyang met her gaze steadily. “Why should I need to explain myself? While I am grateful to the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan for allowing us into the ranks of the Royal Phoenix Clans, you do not own us—nor do we owe you an explanation. Still, as a courtesy, I’l tell you. As clan leader, I have an obligation to heed the will of my people, and the vast majority wish to follow the Immortal Monarch. Quite simple, isn’t it?”

“You will regret your decision.”

“I’ll deal with it when that time comes.”

Feng Tianhuang sneered. “As much as I’d like to expel the Rising Phoenix Clan from the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans right this moment, protocol must be followed. That said, it will take little effort to see you removed.”

“Then allow me to save you the trouble,” Feng Lingyang declared. “From this moment onward, the Rising Phoenix Clan renounces its title and withdraws from the Ten Royal Phoenix Clans. From now on, we shall stand as an independent clan once more.”

The room fell into stunned silence, the atmosphere growing oppressively heavy. None of the other phoenix clans had ever anticipated such an outcome.

Though none of them spoke aloud, a few secretly admired Feng Lingyang’s courage. She had dared to stand up for herself when they could not. In truth, some among them also wished to side with the Immortal Monarch—but fear of the Imperial Vermilion Phoenix Clan and the Primordial Phoenix kept them silent.

“You will not regret your decision,” Yuan’s voice suddenly rang out, shattering the silence.

“From this moment on, the Rising Phoenix Clan is under my protection. Should anyone dare to harm them, I— the Immortal Monarch—will unleash my full power to destroy the offender.”

Yuan’s words were not merely a welcome to the Rising Phoenix Clan. They were also a warning—if any dared to retaliate against them for their choice, he would personally see to their destruction.

“Let’s go. We’ve already wasted enough time here,” Feng Tianhuang declared coldly before turning on her heels. Her robes flared as she strode toward the exit, her aura sharp enough to cut the air.

Without another word, the remaining phoenix clans, their expressions ranging from disdain to unease, silently followed her out of the chamber.

“So you’ve managed to deceive one clan to your side, huh?” The Primordial Phoenix, who decided to linger a little longer, remarked.

Yuan looked directly at the Primordial Phoenix and asked, “So, are you going to attack us now?”

The Primordial Phoenix smirked. “Why would I do that?”

“I wouldn’t be surprised if the Celestial Emperor had ordered you to ‘take care’ of us.”

A scoff escaped the Primordial Phoenix’s lips. “Still trying to make the Celestial Emperor look like a villain, huh? Sorry, but it won’t work. Although you’ve stirred up trouble for the Royal Phoenix Clans, you’ve done nothing that warrants punishment. Clans breaking ties or shifting allegiance are things that happen constantly in the cultivation world. It’s nothing new or illegal.”

His tone was dismissive, but the cold gleam in his eyes made it clear that he wasn’t very pleased about the outcome, as he’d expected a complete victory. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2136: Immortal Monarch'S Grand Blessing
chapters.body:
Chapter 2136: Immortal Monarch'S Grand Blessing

After a brief silence, the Primordial Phoenix finally spoke, “Though I cannot punish you for luring the Rising Phoenix Clan to your side, I can certainly punish you for slandering the Celestial Emperor’s name.”

“Just because you refuse to believe it doesn’t make it slander,” Yuan replied calmly. “Still, if you wish to fight, I’ll be more than willing to oblige.”

The Primordial Phoenix silently swept his gaze over Yuan and the others in the chamber. Alongside Yuan and Feng Yuxiang stood Feng Lingyang, Feng Haoyu, and the Grand Elder. Though his cultivation surpassed them all, he ultimately chose not to act.

“You should be grateful the Celestial Emperor forbade me from touching you,” the Primordial Phoenix said. “At least, for now.”

Yuan raised his brows in surprise.”Oh? The Celestial Emperor told you not to fight me? How unusual… or perhaps he intends to deal with me personally?”

“The Celestial Emperor has far more important matters than wasting time on an insect like you.” The Primordial Phoenix sneered, then turned and strode toward the exit.

“Of course, the same goes for me.”

After saying that, the Primordial Phoenix left the room and disappeared from the summit shortly after.

“So… what now?” Feng Yuxiang turned to look at Yuan and asked.

“Let’s return to the Rising Phoenix Clan for now,” he said.

“Alright.”

As they traveled, Yuan said, “I will fulfill my promise to you and strengthen the entire Rising Phoenix Clan. Once we arrive, can you gather everyone for me?”

Feng Lingyang immediately nodded in agreement.

Before long, they returned to the Rising Phoenix Clan’s territory. At Yuan’s request, Feng Lingyang swiftly summoned every member of the clan. Yet even before she could announce the results of their journey, the clan members already understood the outcome, as Yuan’s very presence within their grounds spoke louder than words.

“As you may have already realized, your leader has accepted my offer. From this day forward, the Rising Phoenix Clan stands under my command.”

“If you are unwilling to follow me, I will grant you the chance to leave now,” Yuan declared. “There is no need for words or explanations-simply depart in silence, and no punishment will follow.”

He then stood in patient silence, giving time to those who wished to go. After several minutes, a handful of figures quietly took to the skies, vanishing into the distance without a trace. Yet, despite their departure, the overwhelming majority of the clan remained.

“Anyone else?” he asked.

When the remaining members shook their heads, Yuan nodded. “Very well. As a reward for choosing to stand with me, I will bestow upon you all a blessing.”

With a single gesture, Yuan directed his will toward the gathered clan members. At once, the heavens above bloomed with radiant golden symbols, each one pulsing with profound might.

“Those who choose to serve me, accept the Immortal Monarch’s symbol. From that moment forth, you shall be my followers, and my decree shall be your law.”

One by one, the clan members reached out to the golden symbols, allowing them to sink into their bodies and fuse with their Dantians.

Once everyone had accepted his symbol, Yuan activated another skill.

<Immortal Monarch’s Grand Blessing has been activated>

In the blink of an eye, a wave of radiant power swept across the Rising Phoenix Clan. Unlike an ordinary blessing, the Grand Blessing etched its effects permanently into the bodies of all who had accepted his symbol.

“This feeling! My body… It’s overflowing with power!”

Cries of astonishment echoed as the clan members suddenly felt their strength rising beyond imagination. “This is the Immortal Monarch’s power…? Feng Lingyang mumbled, her voice filled with awe. However, Yuan shook his head and said, “This is just a simple blessing. The real thing begins now.” Yuan pricked his fingertip, letting a stream of blood flow forth. It quickly condensed into a sphere the size of a fist, hovering in his palm. With a firm clench, the sphere burst apart, scattering into countless drops of crimson light. Guided by his will, one drop entered the mouth of every clan member present.

The clan members shuddered in ecstasy, their senses overwhelmed by the peerless taste of his blood, a flavor surpassing every delicacy they had encountered in their lives.

“I just fed each of you a drop of my blood. Soon, your bloodline purity will improve,” he said to them a moment later.

“Just like that?!”

“If you can’t believe it, you can test your bloodline,” Yuan remarked.

“Hand me the Golden Phoenix Needle!” Feng Lingyang commanded loudly.

The next moment, one of the elders approached her with the needle in hand.

Feng Lingyang immediately tested her bloodline while everyone watched in anticipation.

When the results appeared seconds later, Feng Lingyang’s hands went limp. The Golden Phoenix Needle slipped from her grasp and clattered to the floor as she stood frozen, her face blank with shock.

“Clan Leader?! What’s wrong?!” the Grand Elder cried, his voice laced with worry.

“M-My bloodline…” she whispered in a daze. Too shaken to form her thoughts into words, she simply lifted her trembling fingers and pointed toward the fallen needle.

The Grand Elder bent down and picked up the fallen Golden Phoenix Needle, his gaze fixed upon the results.

“By the Heavens!” he exclaimed, his voice trembling as his eyes grew wide with disbelief.

The other elders hastened to gather around him. When their eyes fell upon the results, their expressions froze, as though their very souls had been shaken.

“91.2 percent?!” one of the elders revealed.

“What?!” Gasps rippled through the crowd as the rest of the members heard the result, their surprise mirroring the elders’ reactions. None of them, however, knew the hidden truth-that Feng Lingyang’s bloodline had long been weakened by a curse, a secret known only to the elders and a select few.

Therefore, in their eyes, Feng Lingyang’s bloodline had only increased by two percent, not six percent, as her bloodline had weakened to 85.2 percent. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2137: Acquiring The Final Ancient Seal
chapters.body:
Chapter 2137: Acquiring The Final Ancient Seal

“M-My blood! I must test my bloodline!” the Grand Elder stammered, quickly seizing the Golden Phoenix Needle after snapping out of his daze.

Moments later, his eyes widened, and he exclaimed with unrestrained joy, “90.9 percent! My bloodline purity has surpassed ninety percent! This is a miracle!”

Stirred by his words, the other elders hastily tested themselves as well. Sure enough, nearly all of them discovered that their bloodline purity had risen by four to five percent.

“My lord! With only a single drop of your blood, our bloodlines have undergone unimaginable growth! And to think the Celestial Emperor only offered us no more than three percent!” one of them exclaimed.

Yet even in their excitement, unease gnawed at their hearts, as the results seemed almost too good to be true.

“Immortal Monarch, forgive my boldness, but… what kind of drawbacks should we expect from this?” someone finally asked, instantly silencing the place.

The clamor of celebration died down at once, and every gaze there shifted to Yuan, their faces full of worry and realization. They all knew that although innumerable treasures existed beneath the heavens, many of them had effects that were more detrimental than beneficial.

Now that their excitement had died down and their minds had cleared up, they began wondering what sacrifices must be made for such a powerful boost to their bloodline.

However, what came out of Yuan’s mouth in just a moment left them all stunned again.

“Drawbacks? There are none. You can enjoy your improved bloodline without such needless worry.”

“Are you serious?” Even Feng Lingyang couldn’t believe it.

Yuan smiled and said, “If you think I am lying, I can swear on my soul.”

“T-That won’t be necessary! We believe you. It’s just… I didn’t expect such an overpowered ability to exist… Maybe that’s why the Celestial Emperor is trying to get rid of you.”

Yuan chuckled. “If you think this is overpowered, just wait until I fully recover my strength.”

“Recover? Are you injured?” Feng Haoyu asked in surprise.

“No,” Yuan replied calmly. “I only reincarnated recently, so much of my power has yet to return.”

“I see…” Feng Haoyu murmured.

“Anyway, I have fulfilled the first part of our agreement. As for evolving your bloodline, you’ll need to wait until I recover more of my strength, as I currently lacks the ability to do so.”

Feng Lingyang nodded. “Don’t worry. We never expected you to improve our bloodline so quickly in the first place, much less evolve it.”

“Thank you for your understanding.”

“So… what is the plan now? What do you want the Rising Phoenix Clan to do?” Feng Lingyang asked.

Yuan’s expression grew solemn. “For now, focus on training and familiarizing yourselves with your newfound strength. I cannot say when, but I am certain the Celestial Emperor and the other Royal Phoenix Clans will not stay still and will soon act.”

Feng Lingyang sighed at his words, but she was not surprised. In fact, she anticipated something like this would happen and had already mentally prepared for it.

“I don’t know about the Celestial Emperor, but the Royal Phoenix Clans will definitely come after our clan for humiliating and defying them. However, I doubt they will come after us anytime soon-at least not until they figure out your prowess and whether or not they would be able to get away with it.”

“In that case, I’ll leave the Phoenix Rising Clan for now and return later,” said Yuan.

“I understand.”

“Before I go…” Yuan looked at Feng Lingyang and made a beckoning gesture with his hands.

Feng Lingyang stared at his hand with a puzzled face for a moment before she finally realized what he wanted from her.

“G-Give me a minute! I’ll grab ‘that’ for you now!”

Without another word, Feng Lingyang flew away, disappearing deep into their territory.

About half an hour later, Feng Lingyang returned with a spatial ring in her grasp.

“I’m sorry it took so long, but here is the thing you wanted,” she said as she offered the ring to him.

Yuan accepted it and looked inside. Sure enough, the Ancient Seal was inside.

“Thank you.”

With the fourth and final Ancient Seal in his grasp, Yuan could finally enter the Primordial Realm and meet with Xiao Hua’s family-the Asura Clan.

“Can you tell me why you want the Ancient Seal now?” Feng Lingyang asked.

Yuan smiled and said, “The Ancient Seals only serve one purpose. What else am I going to do with it?”

Feng Lingyang swallowed nervously.

‘I knew he wanted to open the Primordial Realm, but I wanted to believe the Ancient Seal had other purposes…’ she sighed inwardly.

“Can I ask why you’re trying to open it?”

“Some of my friends from the past are imprisoned within, and I intend to free them,” Yuan said.

“But the Primordial Realm is filled with…” Feng Lingyang began, but she stopped herself before uttering

the word ‘criminal’.

“I know what you want to say, but not everyone inside the Primordial Realm is a criminal. The previous Celestial Emperors have imprisoned innocent people inside just for standing up to him.”

“Alright. I won’t question it anymore. I’m sure you have your reasons.”

Sometime later, before Yuan left the Rising Phoenix Clan’s territory, he said to Feng Lingyang, “If anyone dares to mess with your clan, don’t hesitate to contact me.”

Feng Lingyang nodded with a smile and said, “Don’t worry. Our Rising Phoenix Clan isn’t so weak that anyone could bully us. We didn’t earn the title Royal Phoenix Clan by luck, after all. However, if we do encounter a situation we cannot handle, we will definitely request your help.”

“Even if nothing happens, I’ll visit once I regain enough power to evolve your bloodline.”

“What about me, Young Master? Is there anything I can do for you?” Feng Haoyu asked.

“Continue doing what you normally do. I’ll contact you if I ever need your help.”

“As you command.”

With that, Yuan left the Rising Phoenix Clan.

“Are we going straight to the Primordial Realm?” Feng Yuxiang asked Yuan shortly later.

He shook his head and replied, “No, I still need to prepare some more.” (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2138: A Hundred Million
chapters.body:
Chapter 2138: A Hundred Million

After leaving the Rising Phoenix Clan, Yuan traveled straight to his next destination to prepare for the Primordial Realm. “The Abyssal Underground? You wanted to come back here?” Feng Yuxiang quickly realized where he was trying to go. He nodded and explained, “I still need a few more million kills to complete my quest, so I might as well finish it up before I head to the Primordial Realm.” The Primordial Realm was filled with dangerous Immortals and even God Ascension realm cultivators. If he wanted to traverse it safely, he needed to prepare properly for it. Some time later, Yuan returned to the Abyssal Underground. This time, instead of looking for a guide, he flew straight into the entrance, disappearing into the void. However, instead of starting his hunt at the 300th level, Yuan went as far as the 500th level, where the creatures were all between the 1st … Protected Content This chapter requires a free account to read. chapters.title: Chapter 2139: White Hell
chapters.body:
Chapter 2139: White Hell

White Hell-a place so perilous it earned the name Gateway to Hell. There were four such forbidden lands within the Nine Heavens, and Yuan had already explored one of them, the Vanishing Valley.

Being one of the coldest places in the Nine Heavens, White Hell is a vast expanse of endless snow and towering mountains, shrouded in extreme cold. The chill is so severe that even those with strong cold resistance struggle to survive in its outer regions, let alone venture into its depths.

And yet, in spite of its peril and the countless lives it claims, White Hell draws thousands of cultivators every year-some to hone their strength, others to pursue the treasures buried within its icy depths.

All of this was made possible by the Sun God’s Temple, a unique sanctuary where cultivators gathered to cultivate their Yang Qi. They also possessed a powerful treasure that, while unable to eliminate the cold of White Hell, granted enough resistance for cultivators to endure its frost and traverse its perilous lands. This treasure was known as the Sun God’s Lake, a small yet extraordinary lake within the Sun God’s Temple’s territory. Not only was it one of the most sought-after training areas in the world, but it could even evolve one’s physique. Naturally, it could not be taken into White Hell itself. Instead, it was used to imbue Sun Stones, small rock-like objects that shielded their bearers from the cold.

However, the Sun Stones carried limited power, losing their effectiveness after a certain period of time. To obtain one, cultivators had to approach the Sun God’s Temple and make an offering in exchange, whether it be spirit stones or treasures.

Right outside the White Hell’s entrance, several individuals wearing the Sun God’s Temple’s outfit could be seen talking to a group of female cultivators.

“This here is the One-Year Sun Stone, which you will need to traverse White Hell safely.”

In one member’s hand rested a glowing pebble, radiating such immense Yang Qi that it warmed the air around them, even in the freezing cold.

“It has soaked in the Sun God’s Lake for a year, and it will last for ten years while inside White Hell.”

Then, he produced another glowing stone, pulsing with even more intense Yang Qi. “And this,” he said, “is a Hundred-Year Sun Stone. It will last for a hundred years in the outer regions, and its might is sufficient to defy the cold of the second region, reaching even into the third.”

White Hell was divided into nine regions, beginning with the outer region. The deeper one ventured, the colder and more perilous it became. As for the Sun Stone, the most refined ones could allow a cultivator to venture safely into the fifth region. However, each stone needed to be immersed in the Sun God’s Lake for over a million years, making them unimaginably expensive.

One might assume the Sun God’s Temple could simply throw countless Sun Stones into the lake to satisfy demand. However, doing so would drain the lake’s power, and since its primary purpose was as a training ground, the temple was unwilling to create too many.

“I would like to venture into the fourth region,” one of the female cultivators said.

“Then you will need a Ten-Thousand-Year Sun Stone, and it will only last you ten years in the third region,” said one of the Sun God Temple’s disciples.

The female cultivator nodded and calmly handed a spatial pouch to the man.

“Is this donation enough?” she asked.

The disciple peeked into the spatial pouch and nodded, “Yes, this is more than enough. Thank you for your donation. Here is a Ten-Thousand-Year Sun Stone.”

He handed her the Sun Stone.

“The Sun Stone will begin to lose its glow when it’s near its limit, so make sure you leave White Hell the moment you see it.”

“I understand.”

One by one, the female cultivators purchased a Sun Stone before stepping into White Hell.

A few hours later, another group of female cultivators arrived, their matching attire revealing that they belonged to the same sect.

“I’d like to purchase 23 Hundred-Year Sun Stone.”

The woman leading the group handed a spatial ring to one of the disciples, who then quickly checked the ring and handed the Sun Stones to them.

Minutes after they departed, another figure appeared at the entrance. This time it was not a beautiful female cultivator but a strikingly handsome man.

The disciples of the Sun God’s Temple watched as he approached, expecting him to purchase a Sun Stone. To their surprise, however, the handsome man ignored them entirely and strode straight past.
