“W-Wait a second! Where are you going?” One of the disciples subconsciously called out to him in a surprised tone.

The handsome man stopped walking and turned to look at him.

“Where does it look like I am going?” he calmly responded.

Another disciple spoke, “Do you already have a Sun Stone?”

“A what?”

The disciples were baffled by this man’s ignorance. How could one even think about entering White Hell without their Sun Stone?

“You don’t even know what a Sun Stone is, and you’re attempting to challenge White Hell? Are you courting death?”

“Listen here. The White Hell is a place where even cultivators with Cold Immunities are helpless. The only way you can traverse White Hell is with the help of the Sun God Temple’s Sun Stone.”

The handsome man smiled and asked, “So, the Sun Stone keeps me safe from the cold within White Hell, is that correct?”

“That’s right.”

“If you want-“

The man interrupted and said, “In that case, I don’t need it.”

“Excuse me?”

The disciples’ eyes widened with surprise, wondering if they’d misheard him.

“I came to White Hell to train, so the Sun Stone will only hinder me. Now, if you will excuse me.”

Before the disciples could even open their mouths again, the handsome man turned around and continued moving toward White Hell, quickly vanishing into the snowstorm. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

Chapter 2120

chapters.title: Chapter 2140: White Hell(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2140: White Hell(2)

“What a fool. What can he do without the Sun Stone? Even if he has Cold Resistance, he won’t be able to withstand the cold enough to train properly.” The disciples of the Sun God’s Temple shook their heads at the handsome man who refused to heed their warning.

“Forget it. There will always be arrogant people like that.”

“I doubt we’ll ever see him again.”

Unbeknownst to the disciples, the handsome man—Yuan—had no need for their Sun Stone. With the power of the Primordial Phoenix flowing within him, the cold of White Hell posed no threat. No matter how frigid its depths, his True Primordial Fire could easily protect him.

As Yuan crossed into the first region, he officially stepped into White Hell. The outer region stretched out as endless white plains buried beneath layers of snow. The drifts were so deep that walking on the ground was impossible, and only by flying through the skies could one traverse the land.

Despite possessing Supreme Cold Immunity—enough to remain unaffected even by the Extreme Yin Lake near the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect—the cold of White Hell was so intense that it had already begun tempering his resistance further.

“This place sure is great. I’d just stepped into it, and my cold resistance is already improving with every passing second.” Yuan mumbled with a satisfied smile on his face. “White Hel… it’s been a long time since I last heard that name,” Yu Ning remarked. “I had nearly forgotten this place even existed until you mentioned it.”

“What was it like during the Primordial Era?” Yuan asked.

“I doubt much has changed,” Yu Ning replied. “White Hell has been infamous for as long as I can remember. Back then, during the Primordial Era, the deepest anyone managed to reach was the third region.”

“I’ve never been here before, so I don’t know how impressive that is, but according to what those disciples from the Sun God’s Temple said, their Sun Stones could help one survive even the fourth region.”

Yuan had been listening to the disciples marketing the Sun Stones and only pretended to be ignorant.

“Sun God’s Lake, huh? I would like to visit it in the future when I have the chance.”

After reaching the midway point of the first region, Yuan noticed a group of cultivators slightly ahead of him. Though the snowstorm blanketed the world in white, his divine sense cut through it with ease.

Since he wasn’t hiding his presence, the group ahead also noticed him.

“Am I seeing things, or is that man really traveling through White Hell without a Sun Stone?” one of the women asked aloud, clutching the glowing stone in her hand.

“Even if he has Cold Immunity, what a reckless thing to do.”

“He’s probably just lingering in the first region.”

In truth, every woman in the group possessed some degree of Cold Immunity. Had they not intended to enter the deeper regions, they wouldn’t have needed to rely on Sun Stones at all.

However, as they drew closer to the second region, the group was astonished to see Yuan stil right behind them, almost as if he intended to enter the second region as well. Yet, they still believed that Yuan would not venture into the second region.

A week later, they arrived at the threshold of the second region, where the snowstorm transformed into a barrage of hail. Razor-sharp ice shards constantly rained down, and without the protection of spiritual energy, they could pierce flesh as easily as ordinary Sword Aura, riddling one’s body with holes.

Naturally, the cold within the second region was far more severe, being at least two to three times harsher than that of the outer region. Mountains also began to emerge, though only one appeared every few days along their path.

“Heavens! Look over there! That man actually stepped into the second region without a Sun Stone, and he isn’t even protecting his body with spiritual energy!”

The group stared in shock, their curiosity toward him rapidly intensifying.

“Is he just trying to show off to impress us or something?” one of the women muttered. “That’s likely.”

“How typical.”

“Let’s see how long he’ll last.”

While the others chuckled at their own misunderstanding, the one leading them in the front remained silent, quietly observing Yuan through her divine sense to ensure he wasn’t up to anything suspicious.

As they ventured deeper into the second region, magical beasts began to appear, many concealed beneath the snow, striking from ambush. With danger lurking at every step, the women soon stopped paying attention to Yuan, turning their focus entirely to their own safety.

Of course, a few of them couldn’t resist sneaking glances at Yuan, who trailed behind in silence, almost like a stalker. That said, there were still thousands of miles separating them.

Eventually, one of them realized that no magical beasts had attacked Yuan for several days, even though their own group was assaulted constantly.

This left them greatly puzzled. Yuan made no attempt to conceal his presence, nor employed any special means, yet for reasons beyond their understanding, the magical beasts left him untouched.

“Why aren’t the magical beasts attacking him?” one of them finally asked.

“What if… he’s not trying to impress us, but instead using us as bait?” another suggested cautiously.

“Elder, should we question him? He’s only a Golden Immortal, so I doubt he can do anything to us, but I have a bad feeling about him.”

The woman leading them said calmly, “We’ve been moving in a straight line since entering White Hell—it might just be a coincidence. If you’re that concerned, we can stop and let him pass us.”

The others agreed with her suggestion and chose to rest. They carved out a massive crater in the snow before settling down inside it.

A few minutes later, Yuan had caught up to them. They silently hoped he would keep flying past, but their expressions darkened when he suddenly stopped directly above them.

“I knew it! He’s up to no good!” one of the ladies hastily exclaimed. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2141: White Hell(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2141: White Hell(3)

When the group of ladies noticed Yuan stopping right above them, they hastily retrieved their weapons and prepared to fight him.

“What do you want from us, you stalker?” one of them shouted at him.

Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“Stalker? Just because we’re heading in the same direction doesn’t mean I am stalking you. It’s just a coincidence,” he calmly replied.

“Then why did you stop? If you’re not here for us, then hurry up and get lost!”

After a moment of silence, Yuan responded, “In that case, I’ll stop bothering you and be right on my way. Sorry for the disturbance.”

Without another word, Yuan flew away.

“What a creepy fellow.”

“Right?”

Only after Yuan’s presence vanished entirely did the group allow themselves to breathe freely, settling back into their rest. However, their rest was short-lived, as the earth beneath them quaked without warning, sending a jolt of alarm through the group. “W-What’s going on?”

The leader’s voice rang out sharply, “An ambush! Take to the air!”

At that instant, the ground split wide, and from its depths burst a colossal serpent, its body clad in translucent scales that gleamed like sculpted ice.

“It’s a White Hell Glacial Serpent! The strongest magical beast in the second region! Run! We don’t stand a chance against a monster like that!”

“S-Scatter!”

The group quickly scattered in different directions, hoping to confuse the serpent. But the beast was swift and decisive, striking immediately at the weakest among them. Unfortunately, there was nothing anyone could do for her. After several moments of desperate struggle, the female cultivator was devoured by the beast.

However, the beast was not satisfied and immediately chased after its next prey.

In truth, Yuan had noticed the magical beast and wanted to warn them, but they chased him away before he could do so, even calling him a stalker.

A few days later, Yuan stepped into the third region. Here, the hail grew even more violent, its force great enough to pierce through ordinary spiritual energy. To withstand it, one needed Immortal Qi-making this region accessible only to Immortal cultivators.

Though his Cold Immunity was progressing much faster than the second region, it was hardly enough to satisfy Yuan, so he continued going deeper.

As Yuan traversed the third region, magical beasts at the Golden Immortal level would constantly attack him. Unlike the second region, the beasts in the third region did not fear his aura.

Naturally, none of them posed even the slightest threat to him, and all it took was a single attack with Supreme Sword Aura to one-shot the beasts.

Since he had come to train, Yuan didn’t bother using Immortal Qi to shield himself from the hail. Though the ice shards couldn’t pierce his skin, each impact left a layer of frost, freezing his flesh on contact.

In the blink of an eye, a month had passed, and Yuan stepped into the fourth region. Here, the hail had ceased, and the skies were clear, yet the temperature had dropped even further. The cold was so intense that Yuan’s body was continuously being frozen just by existing within it.

“Still not enough,” Yuan muttered as he proceeded to make his way toward the fifth region.

Although his Cold Immunity had been pushed to its limit after consuming the Tear of the Azure Heavens, it still had not reached the realm of Cold Harmony.

A few hours into his journey toward the fifth region, Yuan spotted a lone figure locked in battle with a mammoth-like magical beast. The creature was nearly the size of a small mountain, and a gleaming blue crystal was embedded between its brows.

The cultivator was at the 1st level of God Ascension, while the beast was only at the True immortal realm. Yet the extreme cold rendered her movements stiff, and much of her focus was spent maintaining a shield of Immortal Qi to protect her body.

Not wishing to disturb her, Yuan quietly flew past without stopping.

Some time later, Yuan finally reached the border of the fifth region. Even without stepping inside, he could sense that his Cold Immunity would be useless there.

Unlike the previous four regions, the fifth was a mountain valley, where a raging snowstorm howled between towering peaks in every direction.

After mentally preparing himself, Yuan entered the fifth region.

The instant he entered the fifth region, Yuan’s body began turning into an ice statue.

Still not content with the progress speed, Yuan chose to advance toward the sixth region. Yet without a Sun Stone or other protective treasure, his body froze solid the instant he attempted to proceed. Thus, he summoned forth his True Primordial Fire, engulfing himself in its blaze as though donning a fiery armor, to withstand the frigid domain.

With the True Primordial Fire shielding him, Yuan’s body instantly thawed, granting him freedom of movement once more. In fact, the fire’s power was so overwhelming that he couldn’t even feel the cold in the slightest.

Without hesitation, Yuan flew straight into the sixth region. The deeper one ventured into White Hell, the smaller each region became. While it had taken him nearly three months to travel from the outer region to the fifth, it required only a single week to reach the Sixth.

Upon entering the sixth region, Yuan began searching for a suitable place to cultivate. After a few minutes of wandering, he settled on a random cave and stepped inside. Once he was ready, Yuan extinguished the True Primordial Fire. The warmth vanished in an instant, and his body was immediately overwhelmed by the immense cold, freezing him solid like a statue and turning his very blood to ice.

Despite every inch of his body—even his organs and blood—being frozen solid, Yuan remained alive, forced to endure the endless agony.

Time flowed swiftly, and before long, a year had passed. Throughout it all, Yuan remained encased in ice, an unmoving statue in the heart of the sixth region. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2142: White Hell(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2142: White Hell(4)

One year… two years… three years slipped by since Yuan began his cultivation in White Hell’s sixth region. For all that time, he remained a frozen statue within an unremarkable cave, one among countless others scattered through the land.

While he had spent most of that time alone, sometime during his second year, he received an unexpected visitor.

it happened one seemingly ordinary night, when a lone figure suddenly flew into his cave and crashed to the ground.

The intruder was a woman, her body battered with wounds and stained with frozen blood.

However, having fallen unconscious shortly after entering the cave, she remained unaware of Yuan’s presence. For the next few days, she lingered in a fragile slumber, surviving only thanks to the Sun Stone clutched tightly in her grasp.

Upon awakening, her eyes fell upon Yuan, and for a moment she was startled. However, she quickly calmed once she mistook him for nothing more than a frozen corpse.

“Damn it, you scared me for a moment,” she muttered, sitting up and leaning her back against the wall.

For the next several days, she kept her eyes fixed on the cave’s entrance with unwavering vigilance, as though expecting someone to appear.

“I doubt they would be foolish enough to follow me to the sixth region…” she muttered. The next moment, she retrieved several recovery pills and swallowed them, beginning the process of healing her injuries. Although she could have hastened the process through cultivation, she chose not to risk exposing her presence and relied solely on the pills for recovery.

A few weeks passed, during which the female cultivator stayed inside the cave without once stepping outside.

“At this rate, I should fully recover in about a year…” she muttered as she looked at the Sun Stone in her grasp.

“I’m glad I bought this Million-Year Sun Stone after all… otherwise, I wouldn’t have been able to hide in the sixth region at all,” she sighed.

Though the Million-Year Sun Stone was considered most effective only up to the fifth region, it could still aid entry into the sixth if the cultivator possessed other advantages, such as unique physiques or powerful treasures. In her case, she had a heaven-defying physique that granted her powerful resistance to the yin element, naturally giving her cold immunity.

“Those hateful bastards… Once I recover from my injuries, I’l tear them limb from limb!” Once the female cultivator was certain her pursuers would not suddenly appear, she turned her full focus to recovery.

After ten months had passed and the majority of her wounds had mended, the female cultivator’s eyes snapped open. A sound unlike any she had heard in this cave reached her ears—the sharp crack of ice breaking. Until now, only the endless howling of the snowstorm had filled the silence.

Startled, she stood and turned toward the sound reverberating from the cave’s depths. But bewilderment followed, for the only thing at the far end was the frozen corpse, unmoving since the day she had first stumbled upon it nearly a year prior.

It had never crossed her mind that the frozen figure might still live. After all, it was completely unprotected, and not even a God Ascension cultivator could survive in this region without some form of protection.

When the noise ceased, the female cultivator cautiously approached its source. A moment later, upon reaching the end of the cave, her eyes widened in shock and disbelief. The ice statue she had long assumed to be a corpse was no longer frozen, and standing before her was a man, very much alive.

“Hm? Who are you?” Yuan asked with mild surprise, not expecting company the moment he awoke from cultivation. During his training, he had entered a trance-like state of enlightenment, fully immersing himself and remaining unaware of the female cultivator’s presence until he broke free from the ice.

The woman could not bring herself to answer. She simply stood there, eyes wide and lips parted in shock, unable to comprehend how this man had endured the merciless cold of the sixth region without the slightest protection.

However, the woman quickly snapped out of her daze and stammered, “S-Sorry, I didn’t realize you were alive. I hope I haven’t disturbed your training.”

“No, it’s fine. I didn’t even realize you were here until I woke up,” Yuan said calmly, waving his hand in a dismissive gesture.

He then noticed her blood-stained body and asked, “Are you okay?”

“I… I will take my leave now.”

Without another word, the woman turned around and quickly left.

Yuan silently watched her depart, making no attempt to stop her.

His attention shifted to the notification.

“How long have I been cultivating?” Yuan asked the others.

“Three years.” Yu Ning calmly responded.

“Three years, huh? Not bad, but my training is not finished.”

Although he had attained Cold Harmony, it had yet to reach its full potential. Thus, Yuan left the cave shortly after and began making his way toward the seventh region, determined not to leave White Hell until he achieved the highest attainable realm of Cold immunity.

Over the course of five days, as Yuan made his way toward the seventh region, he encountered only one magical beast. The sixth region’s environment was so merciless, so inhospitable, that only the rarest of creatures could exist within its bounds.

As Yuan stood at the border of the seventh region, his eyes widened at the sight ahead. On the horizon, a titanic beast, mammoth in form, loomed, its massive frame so towering it rose above the tallest mountains themselves.

However, what truly caught his attention was not the mammoth itself, but the faint silhouette of a human, barely visible, seated upon the beast’s head.

Yuan narrowed his eyes at the distant figure. Though he could not make out their features, he could clearly feel the weight of their gaze fixed upon him.

However, the gaze did not linger, and a moment later the mammoth disappeared into the horizon. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2143: The Demon Sealing Faction'S Return
chapters.body:
Chapter 2143: The Demon Sealing Faction'S Return

Once the colossal mammoth and the silhouette disappeared, Yuan entered the seventh region before looking for a random cave.

The seventh region was so frigid that even with Cold Harmony, Yuan needed to cloak himself in True Primordial Fire just to move. Without it, he would be frozen instantly, reduced to an ice statue.

Some time later, Yuan sat cross-legged within a cave and withdrew the True Primordial Fire, allowing the cold to engulf him—thus beginning his training anew.

Normally, it would take thousands, if not millions, of years to advance Cold Harmony to the next level. But Yuan was forcing his body to the extreme. What truly accelerated his progress, however, was his heaven-defying physique.

The Heaven Refining Physique was a one-of-a-kind existence, so rare that most cultivators had never even heard of it. It first appeared in the Primordial Era, when Tian Yang stumbled upon the Heaven Refining Crystal that granted him this physique. Yet, even after countless years and many reincarnations, Yuan still could not uncover the true origin of that crystal.

Stil, despite possessing the Heaven Refining Physique, Yuan would continue his seclusion in the cave for no less than a decade.

Meanwhile, in the Seventh Heaven, the members of the Demon Sealing Faction returned to the Super Perfect Physique Tempering Sect after spending over a decade training in the outside world, gaining experience as cultivators.

Once everyone had returned safely to the sect, they gathered to share their experiences. “It’s been a while,” Wang Ming said with a smile. “How was your journey?”

“It was a pain in the ass,” Chu Liuxiang sighed. “No matter where we go, people are always trying to hit on us. And when we brush them off, it somehow always turns into a fight. Thanks to that, my combat experience is now through the roof.”

“Fortunately, my group was made up entirely of men, so we didn’t have to worry about anyone hitting on us,” Shi Lang said. “Even so, we still ran into plenty of trouble—mostly cultivators trying to take advantage of us.”

“You guys are getting into fights? Pretty much all of my encounters back away after learning of my background,” said Wu Zao.

The others looked at Wu Zao and sighed, “You’re using Kelan’s name as a shield? How are you going to gain experience if you avoid everything?”

“It’s not like I avoided every fight. I just avoid the ones I find a pain in the ass.”

Once they finished talking about their journey and experiences, Wang Ming asked, “Hey, have you heard anything from Yuan? It’s been a while since we last heard from him.” Meixiu shook her head and said, “No, he hasn’t contacted us since he went to this place called “White Hell’ for training.”

Before they parted ways, Yuan gave Meixiu, Chu Liuxiang, and Yu Rou a communication jade slip, allowing them to speak with each other even across great distances. Still, not wanting to interrupt his cultivation, it was usually Yuan who initiated the conversations. “It’s been nearly twenty years since we last saw him… and to think, back then, we worried when he was gone for only a few months,” Wang Bingbing remarked.

“What can we do? As our cultivation increases, so will the time we need to progress. Although it’s been nearly two decades, it oddly doesn’t feel that long.” Wang Ming said. “In the future, we might see each other once every few hundred, if not a few thousand years.” Xi Meili said.

“This is just the nature of the cultivation world.” Kelan, who was also there, remarked. “However, you’ll quickly get used to it.”

“That said… to think he’d go train at White Hell. He’s truly the most reckless person I know.”

“What’s that place like?” Xi Murong asked.

“It’s one of the coldest and most perilous regions in the Nine Heavens,” Kelan replied. “There exist four domains that cultivators universally avoid, known as the Forbidden Zones. White Hell stands as the third most dangerous of them all. Unless you have a powerful physique or a Sun Stone from the Sun God’s Temple, you can forget about visiting that place.”

“Have you ever been there?” Wang Ming asked him.

“I’ve been there several times for training. It’s not a pleasant place, especially since I hate the cold.” Kelan shuddered just from recalling his experience within White Hell. Sometime later, Hong Xiuquan asked, “Senior Kelan, what should we do next in our training?”

“Honestly, there’s only one thing you can do if you wish to continue improving, and that is to train at other sects—ones better suited to your specific needs,” Kelan replied. “As much as I would like to continue guiding you guys, I have already given you all I can in the basics as a cultivator. From here on, you must refine your own specializations.”

He continued, “For example, Wang Xiuying—since your focus is Alchemy, I’ll recommend you to one of the top sects for Alchemists. And Wang Ming, with your swordsmanship, I’l send you to a sword sect. It doesn’t have to be a sect, either. I can find you personal teachers if you wish. Of course, if you wish to stay here, I won’t stop you.”

After a moment of silence, Kelan spoke, “I’ll give you all some time to think over it. Once you have decided, I will speak with—”

“I am willing to go!” Wu Zao immediately gave a response. “I am already noticing a drastic decline in my progress, after all.”

Before Kelan could say anything else, the others also responded.

“I will also go,” said Wang Ming.

“Me too,” said Shi Lang.

In the end, everyone but Li Jinxi gave a response, agreeing to train at other places. “Alright, I’ll speak with Senior Bai later. Not only does he have more connections, but he has much more experience in this field than I do,” Kelan nodded.

“What about you, Li Jinxi?” he turned to ask her a moment later.

Li Jinxi pondered for a moment before responding, *I think I will continue training by myself.”

“Are you sure? With your talents, you could easily get into the top sects with ease, even without our help,” Kelan said, worried that she might be wasting her potential.

“I am,” she confidently confirmed.

“If you say so…” Despite saying that, Kelan wasn’t going to give up easily.

“Let’s see if Senior Bai can convince her…” he thought to himself. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2144: The Demon Sealing Faction'S Return(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2144: The Demon Sealing Faction'S Return(2)

“What about us?” Xi Meili suddenly asked, referring to herself and Liya, as they were both dragons.

Kelan gave her a stiff smile. “To be honest, I’m not sure. I do have a few non-human friends I could reach out to, but I can’t promise anything. Do you know of any place where you could train?”

Xi Meili fell silent to ponder, and after a minute of silence, she said, “I do have a place in mind… but I’m not sure they’ll be okay with it.”

Kelan raised an eyebrow and asked, “Where is this place?”

“The Holy Dragon Clan. I trained with them for some time when I went there with Yuan, and I really enjoyed it. If it weren’t for the Celestial Emperor, I would’ve stayed there to train a little longer.”

In truth, Xi Meili wanted to stay behind with the Holy Dragon Clan at the Dragon Spiral Mountain, but after what happened to them, she felt that it would be inappropriate for her to linger, so she ultimately decided to leave with Yuan.

“The Holy Dragon Clan, huh? In that case, I think it would be for the best if you personally reached out to them.”

“Aren’t they the ones at the Dragon Spiral Mountain?” Liya asked after recalling their name.

Xi Meili nodded, “That’s right. You saw them right after arriving in the Nine Heavens.” Liya recalled the mountain of corpses she saw and asked, “Do you think I can come with you?”

“Of course. It was already my intention to bring you along.”

Kelan spoke a moment later, “I’l contact the others immediately, but it might take some time for a response. You can do whatever in the meantime.”

Once their meeting concluded, the members of the Demon Sealing Faction returned to their living quarters to rest. Many of them decided to log out of Cultivation Online to train their real bodies on Earth.

After logging out, Meixiu left her room to visit Yu Rou and the Eternal Lotuses, who had risen to become the second-strongest faction on Earth.

“It’s been a while, Senior Sister Meixiu.”

As she passed through, the members of the Eternal Lotuses bowed their heads, greeting her with utmost respect.

Although Meixiu wasn’t officially part of their faction, she often visited to train them. The other members of the Demon Sealing Faction would also stop by from time to time, but with everyone following their own schedules, it had become rare for them all to gather together.

“Are you here to train us today, or are you here for Senior Sister Yu Rou?”

The members approached her and asked.

“I’m here for Yu Rou today. I’l train with you all next week,” she said.

“Okay!”

Melxiu arrived at their courtyard a few minutes later, where at the center stood a tall, beautiful woman instructing a group of younger girls.

The woman had long, silky black hair and warm brown eyes. Her very presence radiated a natural grace that drew people to her.

“Your posture is wrong. Move your hip a little to the right and straighten your back some more!”

“Yes, Senior Sister Yu!”

The woman was none other than Yu Rou.

Meixiu didn’t disturb their training and waited for them to be done.

An hour later—”Okay, we will end our training here today. We’ll continue next week at the same time. You are all dismissed.”

As the group of females dispersed, Yu Rou approached Meixiu with a calm smile, “It’s been two years, Meixiu. How have you been?”

“I just finished my journey and have returned to Kelan’s sect for now. What about you?” “The same as usual. Training the newbies. Is it just your group that’s returned?”

“The others have returned as well. Everyone is safe and sound.”

“That’s good to hear.”

Knowing that Yu Rou will ask about Yuan, Meixiu mentioned him first, “Unfortunately, Yuan is still away,” she said.

“Is that s0? Can’t say I’m surprised,” Yu Rou replied with a bittersweet smile.

“You shouldn’t be too hard on him.”

“I would have been a little sad or lonely. I know he has a lot on his plate, but it’s been nearly twenty years since I last saw his face. If you consider the other times he’s been absent and how long we’ve been alive, it’s about half of our lifetime.”

“I know how it feels, but trust me, it will get better.”

“I know. Anyway, what are you going to do now that you’ve returned?”

“I’m going to rest a little before I continue my training at another place.” Meixiu proceeded to tell Yu Rou about Kelan’s plans for the Demon Sealing Faction.

“So you guys will be doing your own things separately, huh? It makes sense, considering you all have your own talents and path.”

Sometime later, another presence approached them.

“Hello, Sister Lianhua.”

“Hey, Meixiu.” Bai Lianhua returned the greeting. “Mind if I join?”

“No, go ahead.”

Bai Lianhua sat down beside them, and the three of them started chatting. After Meixiu recalled the end of her training journey, Bai Lianhua spoke about the Eternal Lotuses and Earth’s overall situation.

Ever since Yuan opened the gateway between Earth and the Nine Heavens, flooding the world with spiritual energy, cultivators on Earth had advanced at a pace unlike anything before. Yet, no matter how far their cultivation rose, they soon discovered they could only wield the strength of a Spirit Grandmaster.

This meant that even Spirit King cultivators on Earth were no stronger than a Spirit Grandmaster. Since Yuan secretly created the formation that limited everyone’s cultivation, nobody but the Demon Sealing Faction knew the cause of this phenomenon. Thus, much like the phenomenon of Cultivation Online, no one questioned it, choosing instead to continue their lives as usual.

However, Meixiu knew that things would not remain this way forever and that they needed another solution sooner or later. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2145: Yu Family'S Return
chapters.body:
Chapter 2145: Yu Family'S Return

After spending half a day with Yu Rou and Bai Lianhua, Meixiu returned home.

“You’re back.”

When Meixiu walked through the entrance, she was greeted by a familiar face sweeping the ground.

“Mother.”

It was Meifeng. Although the members of Demon Sealing Faction spend most of their time inside Cultivation Online these days, Meifeng continued to clean the mansion daily. “How have you been lately?” Meixiu asked.

“The same as usual,” Meifeng calmly replied as she continued sweeping the floor. “You’ve entered Spirit Lord. Congratulations.”

Meixiu said after noticing Meifeng’s cultivation had reached the second level of Spirit Lord.

“It’s nothing compared to your Spirit Sovereign cultivation.”

“Your cultivation would be a lot higher if you didn’t spend hours cleaning the house every day.” Meixiu shook her head. “Honestly, it’s a waste of time.”

Meifeng halted her movements and replied, “I have been doing this long before you were even born. Even if the entire world changes, it doesn’t mean I have to change. I enjoy doing this, so I will continue doing it, even if it’s not necessary.”

“Also, I have nothing else to do nowadays. Although the Demon Sealing Faction used to be at the center of attention and constantly flooded with requests and emails, we’re pretty much forgotten nowadays since we haven’t made any appearances since Yuan left

“Why don’t you explore Cultivation Online more? You can even meet Yuan there,” Meixiu suggested.

“I tried. It was fun at first, but after several years, I got bored.”

“But you don’t find housework boring? I can’t understand you.” Meixiu sighed.

“Why are you so worried about what I do? It’s not like I am going to die.”

“The world is constantly evolving. If you cannot keep up with it, you’ll eventually be consumed by it.”

Meifeng smiled and said, “Fortunately, I have a capable daughter and son-in-law to protect me when that happens.”

Meixiu opened her mouth, but no words came out, as she was left speechless by her mother’s words.

“What? You’re not going to protect me?” Meifeng asked, raising her eyebrows.

“I never said that,” Meixiu replied. “I just didn’t expect to hear those words from you, since you’ve always taught me that we can only rely on ourselves and nobody else.” “That was our life working for the Yu Family, after all. However, things are different now.” Meixiu fell silent for a moment before asking, “Have you learned anything about the Yu Family? You mentioned you’d been trying to find them, and that was over ten years ago.” After a brief look of hesitation on her face, Meifeng responded, “Yes, I have. In fact, I found them.”

“Really?!” Meixiu’s eyes widened at this news.

“Where are they? And what have they been doing since they disappeared?”

“What are you going to do with that information?” Meifeng asked.

“What am I.” Meixiu raised her eyebrows. “You’re making it sound like I am going to do something to them once I learn their whereabouts. I’m just curious.”

“I’ll tell you after I finish cleaning, or are you too busy to wait?”

“No, I can wait. I won’t be doing anything for a while since I just finished my training in Cultivation Online.”

Meifeng nodded and returned to her cleaning.

Sometime later, Meixiu went to look for Meifeng in her room.

“Come inside.”

Meixiu opened the door and stepped inside Meifeng’s room.

Once they were seated, Meifeng began, “When their reputation was shattered, the Yu Family went into hiding, and I wasn’t able to get any information on them. However, a few years ago, they returned.”

“Returned?” Meixiu asked with raised eyebrows.

“As in they stopped hiding. It’s most likely because Yuan had completely disappeared from both Earth and Cultivation Online for the last few decades, and they probably believe he’s dead or something.”

Since Yuan was no longer a Player, his achievements were no longer announced by the system, and because this wasn’t the first time he had disappeared for long stretches, no one thought much of it at first. But as years passed without a single announcement bearing his name, and with nobody having seen him on Earth, people gradually began to speculate that he had died.

People have tried searching for Yuan in Cultivation Online, but since the Players were only able to ascend up to the Fifth Heaven these days, they were nowhere near close to reaching him.

“So, where are they right now? And what are they doing now that they have returned?” Meixiu asked.

“They’re currently running a business for the Xiong Family.”

“The Xiong Family? Aren’t they the number one family in the Legacy Ranking? Do they know the Yu Family’s background?”

Meifeng nodded, “Yes. In fact, they agreed to work with the Yu Family because they are aware of their connection with Yuan. As for the Yu Family’s business… they run a talent- recruitment business.”

“Is that 50?” Meixiu muttered.

“Are you going to tell Yu Rou?” Meifeng suddenly asked.

“What’s the point? I doubt she even cares about them at this point.”

“If you say so.”

After learning about the Yu Family’s current situation, Meixiu and Meifeng continued chatting about other things, such as their future plans.

“You may not look like it, but you’re already twice the age I was when I gave birth to you. When can I expect a grandchild?” Meifeng asked.

“You’re asking me that when Yuan isn’t even on Earth anymore? Are you asking me to find a new partner?”

“Of course not. Yuan may not be here right now, but it’s not like he’ll never return.” Meixiu fell silent for a moment before speaking, “Yu Rou plans on leaving Earth to follow Yuan in the Nine Heavens in the future. I also plan to do the same. What about you?” “It’s not like I have any special attachments to this place. If both my daughter and son- in-law are at another place, I will naturally go there with you,” she said. “The question is whether I’ll still be alive by the time that happens.”

“You can live for hundreds of years as a Spirit Lord. But if you’re worried and you don’t want to die before seeing your grandchild, then you should put more effort into your cultivation,” said Meixiu.

“If you put it like that, I guess I have no choice but to work harder on my cultivation,” Meifeng said with a bittersweet smile. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2146: Arrangements
chapters.body:
Chapter 2146: Arrangements

A few days after the Demon Sealing Faction’s return, Wang Ming and everyone who came from the Jaded Garden decided to leave the mountain to visit their family.

“We’ll return in a few months.”

“Have fun,” Meifeng said to them.

“I’m also going to visit my grandfather for a few weeks.”

Wang Xiuying also left the Dragon Spiral Mountain two days later, leaving only Chu Liuxiang, Meixiu, and Meifeng at the house.

Although everyone communicated with their families often through phone calls and video chats, this was their first time going back home in person in many years.

With nothing else to do, Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang spent most of their time with Yu Rou and the members of Eternal Lotuses. Besides Eternal Lotuses, they also visited the Lord, who spends most of his time trying to regain his cultivation.

At the Jaded Garden, the Six Spiritual Families held a grand feast to celebrate their geniuses’ return, a celebration that lasted an entire week.

“Do any of you regret leaving home to follow Yuan?” one of the elders asked during the banquet.

“Of course not” Wang Ming replied firmly. “To have met someone like him is the greatest fortune of my life—and to follow him is an even greater blessing.”

“Right? Without him, we could have never reached where we are now, not even in a ifetime,” Shi Lang added, and everyone nodded in agreement.

“How’s Yuan doing these days? I haven’t heard a peep from him for many years now. Some even think he’s dead. Nothing’s happened to him, right?” another asked.

Wu Zao chuckled and said, “You don’t know Yuan like we do. That man is a freak. There’s nothing in this world that can threaten him, so you don’t have to worry. He’s definitely fine.”

“Hey, is Yuan looking for any partners right now? I’d like to introduce my daughter to him.”

“You should give up. He’s way out of our league,” Xi Murong said.

“But didn’t Jinxi manage to capture his heart?” Wang Ming remarked.

“What?!”

Everyone not part of the Demon Sealing Faction snapped their heads to look at Li Jinxi after hearing Wang Ming’s comment, as this was their first time hearing about her relationship with Yuan.

“W-When?! When did this happen?!” Li Jinxi’s father shouted as he stood up with a face full of shock.

However, Li Jinxi didn’t say anything and remained silent.

“It happened right before Yuan left, I think.” Shi Lang said.

“That long ago?! And none of you even bothered to tell us?!”

“Well, we were busy.”

“What about you, Bingbing? How far have you progressed with him?” The Wang Family asked.

“W-We’re just friends!” Wang Bingbing hastily said with a flushed face.

“You really need to step up your game.” Her mother sighed.

“S-Shut up!”

Sometime later, Li Jinxi spoke up, “There’s something I need to tell you all.”

The chattering stopped, and everyone turned their focus to her.

“As you know, Yuan left Earth and went to the Nine Heavens. I wasn’t able to follow him at that time, but in the future, when there is another opportunity, I will also leave Earth,” she said. “There’s still plenty of time before that happens, but I wanted to let you know ahead of time so you wouldn’t be puzzled if I were to vanish one day suddenly.”

Hearing Li Jinxi’s words, Wang Ming then said, “I also plan on leaving Earth in the future. After experiencing the Nine Heavens, I am no longer content with this small world.”

The others began voicing the same thing afterward—that they planned on leaving Earth in the future.

However, despite the sudden announcement, no one there seemed surprised by it.

After a moment of silence, one of them spoke out loud, “We already anticipated this would happen when Yuan left Earth back then. In fact, we were surprised that none of you actually followed him to the Nine Heavens.”

“Well… It’s not that we didn’t want to. We just couldn’t,” said Hong Xiuguan.

“Will you be unable to return to Earth once you leave?” someone asked.

“We should have no issues coming back-granted nobody messes with the gate like last time.”

“In that case, I see no problem with you leaving. It is a fact that this world has become too small for you all.” Tʜe sourcᴇ of thɪs content ɪs novᴇl(ꜰ)ire.ɴet

“So you have no clue when you’ll leave?”

“No.”

Their families continued asking questions until they were satisfied. In the end, none of their family had any issue with them leaving Earth. In fact, they even encouraged it.

Time passed quickly, and in the blink of an eye, an entire year had passed.

The Demon Sealing Faction returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain and logged back into Cultivation Online for the first time.

Shortly after their return, Kelan summoned them.

“The arrangements have been made,” he said. “But nothing is final yet. In a few days, your potential masters will arrive to interview you. If they find you suitable and agree to accept you, the decision will then be yours. After all, we won’t force you to join a place you do not approve of.”

“Li Jinxi. Go speak with Senior Bai at the Myriad of Techniques tomorrow. He wants to have a conversation with you.”

Li Jinxi silently nodded.

“Any questions?”

“Can you tell us more, so we know what to expect?” Shi Lang asked.

Kelan nodded and proceeded to explain the places they might be going in detail to them. Once satisfied, they were dismissed.

The following day, Li Jinxi visited the Myriad of Techniques.

“Senior Bai.” Li Jini respectfully greeted him after entering his world.

“Li Jinxi, correct? This will be our first one-on-one conversation. Please, sit down.” Senior Bai gestured to the table, where two tea cups were prepared in advance.

Senior Bai continued as he poured her a cup of tea, “You’re probably wondering why I called you, so I will get straight to the point. According to Kelan, not only are you the most talented in your group, but you’re also the hardest working. Yet, you intend to train on your own while your friends all agree to train at other sects. May I ask for your reasoning?” (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2147: Heaven'S Sect
chapters.body:
Chapter 2147: Heaven'S Sect

Hearing Senior Bai’s question, Li Jinxi responded calmly, without a trace of hesitation. “It’s quite simple. I just don’t believe there’s anything these sects can teach me.”

After all, she was a reincarnator who carried the memories of her past life. Unlike the others, she was already far ahead in terms of experience. The most update n0vels are published on N(o)vᴇl(F)ire.nᴇt

Cultivation sects were typically stepping stones for the inexperienced or havens for those wishing to settle in one place. But Li Jinxi was neither, and she would gain far more by wandering the world on her own. In fact, most cultivators who graduate from their sects more often than not set out on their own adventures, hoping to encounter good fortune along the way.

However, Li Jinxi couldn’t reveal such information to Senior Bai even though she trusted him.

“So, you deem the sects unworthy of your time?” Senior Bai murmured, closing his eyes in contemplation.

Ordinarily, such words would come only from cultivators blinded by arrogance. Yet in Li Jinxi’s voice, he detected no such conceit. Even though this was his first true conversation with her, he could already discern that she was different from the rest. There was something oddly familiar about Li Jinxi—a feeling he would sometimes get when speaking with Yuan, but he couldn’t pinpoint it.

Li Jinxi spoke a moment later, “It’s not that I am looking down on these sects. I have more experience than it appears, and I have already graduated from several sects.”

“No, I understand where you’re coming from. However, you cannot compare the sects in the lower heavens to the sects here,” said Senior Bai, misunderstanding her.

He knew Li Jinxi was a Player who started her journey in the lower heavens, so in his eyes, all of her experience came from the lower heavens, which is incomparable to the upper heavens.

Realizing Senior Bai’s misunderstanding, she said, “I think it would be easier to explain with my abilities.”

“I see… that is a good idea. It’d definitely be quicker if you showed me your current abilities. That way, I can better gauge whether you would benefit from being in a sect or not.” Senior Bai nodded.

He turned around and flew to an open area before beckoning her, “Come at me. No need to hold back.”

Li Jinxi nodded and retrieved her treasure, the Golden Empress.

“A fine sword.” Senior Bai remarked with a smile on his face.

“Here I come.”

In the next moment, Li Jinxi unleashed her full cultivation without hesitation, striking at Senior Bai with the clear intent to defeat him.

In their very first exchange, Senior Bai was forced back.

His eyes widened as he said, “My apologies. I underestimated you.”

Li Jinxi gave a silent nod, allowing Senior Bai a moment to ready himself before launching her second strike—one even stronger than the first. She had known he would underestimate her, which was why she hadn’t gone all out at the start.

However, Senior Bai was prepared this time and managed to block her strike. Yet, he was still astonished by her overwhelming prowess.

“She’s only a Bronze Immortal, but she exudes the power of a Golden Immortal! Kelan was right—her talent is overwhelming! It’s like I am fighting Yuan!” he cried inwardly.

Li Jinxi’s overwhelming prowess and talent reminded Senior Bai of his first exchange with Yuan.

Senior Bai quickly understood why Li Jinxi had no confidence in sects and would rather train herself.

After a while, Senior Bai said, “That’s enough. Let’s stop here.”

Li Jinx halted right away. Even after giving it her all, she hadn’t managed to land a clean strike, which frustrated her.

“You’re undeniably talented—far more than Kelan gave you credit for,” Senior Bai remarked. “Your movement and technique are good enough to become an instructor at an elite sect, and you clearly have a lot of combat experience. However, you also still have a lot of room for improvement, and I firmly believe that you would benefit a lot from joining a sect. While there are only a handful of sects that would qualify to train someone of your caliber, and all of them only accept the best of the best, you should have no issue being accepted by them.”

Li Jinxi remained silent for a good moment before speaking, “If you were to recommend me a sect, which one would it be?”

Senior Bai closed his eyes to ponder.

“Either the Divine Essence Monastery or Heaven’s Sect. The Divine Essence Monastery is a top sect in the Ninth Heaven that only accepts female disciples, and Heaven’s Sect is operated by the Tian Family, which is the second strongest family in the Nine Heavens. Do you remember Tian Yuexing from the Sword Emperor’s Qualification?” “The so-called “number one genius’ who challenged Yuan and got utterly defeated? I remember.”

“He’s from that sect. Not just him. Many experts who currently stand at the peak of the cultivation world came from Heaven’s Sect. In fact, I also used to be a disciple there,” said Senior Bai.

He continued, “With your talents, you won’t have any problems getting in. If you want, you can decide after taking a look at them first.”

“I will need some time to consider,” Li Jinxi responded a moment later.

“Take all the time you need. Once you decide, let Kelan know so he can contact me.” “Alright.”

Li Jinxi left shortly after.

Once she left, Senior Bai sat down and sighed, “What a terrifying young lad. The newer generation is truly full of monsters. I’m afraid that it won’t be long before the older generation becomes irrelevant.”

After leaving Myriad of Techniques, Li Jinxi returned to her room, where she lay on the bed and quietly stared at the ceiling.

“Heaven’s Sect… that’s where I died in my past life…” she muttered.

Though she had yet to recover all her memories—or the details of her death—she remembered her final moments taking place within Heaven’s Sect.

And for the next several days, Li Jinxi remained inside her room to ponder her choices. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2148: Keling
chapters.body:
Chapter 2148: Keling

On the fourth day of the Demon Sealing Faction’s return to Cultivation Online, Kelan summoned Wang Ming when his recruiter arrived at the sect.

“This junior greets Senior.” Wang Ming bowed to the handsome middle-aged man standing beside Kelan.

The man nodded and introduced himself, “My name is Liu An, and I am the Sect Master of the Origin Sword Sect. We’re a top-five sword sect within the Nine Heavens and number two in the Eighth Heaven.”

The man proceeded to give a brief history lesson on the Origin Sword Sect to Wang Ming, as well as some of their biggest achievements.

At the end, Kelan spoke, “After watching over you for the last few decades, I believe your sword style is compatible with the Origin Sword Sect. However, don’t just take my word for it.”

Liu An then spoke, “I am going to give you a quick test to determine whether you are qualified for the Origin Sword Sect, as well as show you what my sword style is capable of.”

Wang Ming nodded and followed them to Kelan’s private training ground.

Inside, Wang Ming and Liu An faced each other with a sword.

“Come at me with the intention to kill,” said Liu An before the fight began.

During the fight, Wang Ming used every card in his hand to try to defeat Liu An. However, the results were quite obvious, and Wang Ming was unable to touch Liu An even after six hours of continuous fighting.

“Let’s stop here.” Liu An said after placing his sword against Wang Ming’s neck.

After the fight, Wang Ming felt a little dispirited. He had anticipated a difficult battle, but he never imagined the gap would be so vast-that he wouldn’t even be able to land a single hit on Liu An.

Noticing Wang Ming’s face, Liu An spoke, “I heard from Sect Leader Kelan that you’re not even a hundred years old. Is that true?”

Wang Ming silently nodded.

“Then you have immense potential and plenty of room to grow. In fact, I can see you quickly becoming a top disciple in my sect if you work hard enough.” Find the newest release on novęlfire.net

“Is that so…”

Liu An then asked, “So, what do you think of my sword style? Do you feel any desire to learn it?”

“I-I do!” Wang Ming quickly responded.

Liu An smiled and said, “Why don’t you come to my sect and take a look around before you make a final decision?”

“I will do that, then.”

Liu An turned to look at Kelan and smiled, “Thank you for giving our sect the opportunity to recruit such a young talent. We owe you a great debt.”

Kelan shook his head.

“You don’t owe me anything. I am just doing what’s best for the kid.”

“When can you leave?” Liu An asked Wang Ming.

“Immediately!” he quickly responded.

“You don’t need to say goodbye to your friends?” Kelan asked.

Wang Ming smiled and said, “I already said my goodbyes to them.”

“Very well. Let’s go then.”

The next moment, Liu An retrieved a flying treasure and boarded it with Wang Ming before disappearing from the sect.

A few hours after Wang Ming left the sect, another expert arrived at the sect, and Shi Lang was summoned.

“I am Zhong Man, also known as the Demon Spear. I am the Sect Leader of the Heaven-Piercing Spear Sect, and we are the number one spear sect in the entire Nine Heavens. Though you were recommended by Sect Leader Kelan and Sect Leader Bai, I still need to evaluate you to see if you qualify to join.”

Shi Lang nodded, “Exactly how I want it.”

“The evaluation will take place at our sect, so you can take a look around while you’re at it. When can you leave?”

“I can leave right now.”

“Very well.”

Wasting no time, Zhong Man left with Shi Lang a minute later.

Over the next few days, more and more experts arrived, each coming to take members of the Demon Sealing Faction away.

“I am an elder at the Celestial Cauldron Sect. We’re the number one sect for Alchemists in the Nine Heavens, and most of the top Pill Masters in this world have studied under us. If that’s not enough, our Sect Leader is the current God of Alchemy. If your talent permits it, you could even become her disciple.”

“God of Alchemy…” Wang Xiuying swallowed nervously at this title.

“If you wish to join us, you will need to pass several exams. However, I have to be honest. Considering your lack of experience, I don’t think you have what it takes to pass the exams,” said the elder.

Unlike cultivation sects, which place talent above all else, alchemy sects require both innate talent and accumulated experience. As Wang Xiuying had yet to live a full century, her experience was still lacking.

Even with the Celestial Overlords’ influence, Kelan was only able to give Wang Xiuying the opportunity to take the exam. If she wanted to enter the sect, she needed to do so with her own abilities.

“Even if there’s a 99.99 chance that I’d fail, I would still like to take the exam,” Wang Xiuying said with a determined expression.

Sometime later, Wang Xiuying left with the elder, leaving just Meixiu, Chu Liuxiang, and Li Jinxi at the sect.

Two days later, Kelan called for Meixiu.

When she arrived, Meixiu could see a tall and muscular woman who rivaled Kelan’s physique standing next to him.

“Hello…” Meixiu had to tilt her head to look at the woman’s face.

Despite her rough appearance, the woman smiled and said, “Hello, little one. I’m Keling.”

“Keling…?” Meixiu turned to look at Kelan, who smiled back.

“This is Keling, my elder sister.”

“I didn’t know you had siblings,” Meixiu remarked.

Kelan sighed aloud. “I usually avoid anything related to my family-we don’t exactly have the best relationship.”

“That’s an understatement,” Keling cut in. “You despise us. So imagine my surprise when you suddenly reached out to me.” (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2149: Keling (2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2149: Keling (2)

“That’s not right.” Kelan shook his head and elaborated, “I despise our parents. I harbor no hard feelings toward any of my siblings. After all, it was our parents’ decision to feed me giant’s blood.”

“Even though you gained immense strength from it? All of our siblings envy you, you know?” Keling said.

“That strength didn’t come for free,” Kelan replied, his fists clenched tightly. “I endured decades of indescribable pain just to absorb it. Even now, my body still aches at times, as if remembering what I went through back then.”

“…” Keling fell silent.

Although she had also consumed a giant’s blood, it wasn’t anywhere close to the amount Kelan had to consume, not to mention that he’d consumed high-tier giant blood, so she couldn’t even pretend to understand the torment Kelan had to endure.

“Anyway, let’s get back on topic,” Kelan said a moment later.

Keling nodded and continued, “Let me start over. I’m Keling, Kelan’s elder sister. I specialize in archery, and I’m known as the Star-Piercing Huntress. I came because Kelan begged me to guide you. But to be honest, I’ve never taken on a disciple before, and my experience as a teacher is minimal. I may not be the most suitable person to teach you. Hell, I don’t even know why Kelan came to me for help.

Kelan then said, “You’re the best archer I know, and I only want the best for her.”

Keling raised her brows and asked suspiciously, “Is she your girl or something? You don’t even treat your own disciples this well.”

Flustered, Kelan quickly replied, “She’s not! She already has someone-and her partner is a person I owe a great deal to!” The source of this content ɪs ⓝovelFire.net

“Is that so? Well, that won’t change the fact that I might not be a suitable teacher, even if I am the best archer in the Nine Heavens,” she said.

“I don’t mind.” Meixiu suddenly said. “If it doesn’t work out, I can always find a different teacher.”

“Are you sure? My training methods might not be suitable for a fragile-looking lady like you. Honestly, it’s quite barbaric.”

“You shouldn’t judge her by her appearance,” Kelan said. “Despite how she looks, she’s very hardworking and surprisingly tough. After all, she endured all of my training without uttering a single complaint. The only other person in their group who didn’t complain the slightest was Li Jinxi, and she’s in a league of her own.”

Because of Meifeng’s upbringing, Meixiu’s mental fortitude far surpassed that of the others—and that was without even considering the experience from her past life.

Keling stared at Meixiu in silence for a good moment before sighing, “Alright. I’ll train you. I’ve been thinking about starting my own sect recently, so this will be a good experience.”

“Then it’s settled,” said Kelan.

“I’ll come back for you in a month, so use this time to prepare yourself,” Keling said, as she also needed some time to prepare.

Meixiu nodded.

The following day, Chu Liuxiang was summoned by Kelan.

“Hm? Where’s my teacher?” Chu Liuxiang asked when she saw Kelan by himself.

“He’s running a little late, but he’ll be here soon.”

“Okay.”

A few minutes later, a familiar figure appeared before them.

“Senior Bai? Don’t tell me you’re going to be my teacher?” Chu Liuxiang asked with a surprised face.

Senior Bai smiled and said, “Not quite. You specialize in spiritual techniques-specifically the Yin Element -and you’ve also been studying Array Symbols, correct?”

Chu Liuxiang nodded and elaborated, “I am studying Array Symbols, but it’s more of a side project- something I do when I want a breath of fresh air. My focus is still cultivation.”

“I understand,” said Senior Bai. “Originally, Kelan intended to send you to a sect specializing in the Yin Element, such as the Frozen Cloud Ethereal Sect. But I have a different proposal-why not join my Gates of Heaven instead? We specialize in traditional cultivation methods and spiritual techniques, and we also have many experts in the Yin Element. And should you wish to pursue Array Symbols, I can personally guide you, as I am a Level 7 Array Master.”

“Really? That’s great because I’d rather train in a place with familiar people,” Chu Liuxiang said.

Senior Bai nodded and said, “I have another proposal. As you know, there is a Gates of Heaven in the Fifth Heaven, Seventh Heaven, and another in the Ninth Heaven. If you want the best resources and most experienced lecturers, you should join the Gates of Heaven in the Ninth Heaven.”

Chu Liuxiang’s eyes widened at his proposal, and she said, “I am already humbled that you would let me join the Gates of Heaven, but to bring me to the Ninth Heaven… are you sure? I’m afraid that I am not talented enough.”

Senior Bai spoke in a serious tone. “I’ll be honest with you. If not for our connection, you wouldn’t have qualified to enter the Gates of Heaven in the Seventh Heaven, let alone the Ninth. But talent alone doesn’t determine everything in the cultivation world-luck and connections matter just as much. That said, if you choose to join the sect in the Ninth Heaven, you will likely face ridicule from those around you. I can shield you to a certain degree, but I cannot afford to show excessive favoritism. Take some time to think it over.”

It wasn’t only Chu Liuxiang. The other Demon Sealing Faction members would also face ridicule for their young age, especially if people discovered they had joined through connections.

Yet, despite such a possibility, Chu Liuxiang did not hesitate to reply, “I don’t need to think about it. It’s true that I don’t qualify to step foot in such a place, let alone train with others who joined through their own efforts. I won’t give up such an opportunity just because someone might ridicule me.”

Senior Bai smiled and said, “Don’t make it sound like you didn’t put any effort into it. I only made this proposal because of how hard you trained with Kelan, and I believe you will thrive despite the difference.” (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2150: Returning To The Holy Dragon Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2150: Returning To The Holy Dragon Clan

“Since it’s decided, I will take you to the Ninth Heaven so you can train in the Gates of Heaven. However, it’ll take some time to get approval, so you can continue staying here until then,” Senior Bai said.

Chu Liuxiang nodded, “Take your time.”

“It shouldn’t take too long–three months at most.”

“I understand.”

Senior Bai left shortly after.

“Don’t worry too much about it,” Kelan said afterward. “There may be a few bad apples, but most disciples of the Gates of Heaven have good character–a reflection of their leader, Senior Bai. However, if things do get out of hand, don’t hesitate to ask Senior Bai for help.”

“I’m not worried at all,” Chu Liuxiang said with a confident smile.

Sometime later, Chu Liuxiang went to speak with Meixiu and Li Jinxi about her arrangements.

“So, I’ll be studying in the Ninth Heaven at the Gates of Heaven,” she announced.

“Senior Bai’s sect, huh? I’ll be training with Kelan’s elder sister,” Meixiu said.

“Huh? Kelan has a sister?” Chu Liuxiang’s eyes widened with surprise at this news.

Meixiu nodded. “He has several other siblings, too.”

“What about you, Sister Jinxi? Where will you be going?”

The two looked at her.

“I haven’t decided yet. I was going to train alone in the wilderness, but Senior Bai gave me other options. I can train at the Divine Essence Monastery or Heaven’s Sect. Both of them exist within the Ninth Heaven.”

“I think you would still benefit from training at a sect even with your experience,” said Meixiu.

“I think so, too.” Chu Liuxiang agreed.

Meanwhile, Xi Meili and Liya just arrived at the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“Halt! Identify yourself!” A guard stopped them once they got close enough.

“Hello, my name is Xi Meili-“ For more chapters visit ɴovelfire.net

“I remember you! You’re Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s Envoy’s friend!” The guard interrupted.

“Yes… I came here with Yuan some time ago.”

“Why did you return? Did something happen?”

“No. This may sound weird, but I was wondering if I could train here with my friend.”

“Training, huh? Let me speak with my superior. Follow me.”

Xi Meili and Liya followed the guard to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

Upon returning to the Dragon Spiral Mountain, Xi Meili and Liya were brought to a guest house before the guard left to contact the elders.

“Xi Meili? That young dragon from last time has returned, and she wants to train here?” The elders raised an eyebrow at this news.

“Should I send them away?” The guard asked after seeing the elder’s reaction.

“What? Are you insane? They’re the Immortal-Dragon Goddess’ Envoy’s companion! They can train here for as long as they’d like!” The elder said.

Besides the elders and highest-ranking members, the rest of the Holy Dragon Clan were unaware of Yuan’s true identity as the Immortal Monarch.

“I-I understand!”

Thus, Xi Meili and Liya began living at Dragon Spiral Mountain with the Holy Dragon Clan. The children were overjoyed to see Xi Meili again, while the adults grew curious about Liya’s identity.

Although she was also a dragon, Liya had grown up on Earth, and her scent and aura carried a difference that stirred their curiosity.

A month later, Keling returned to take Meixiu away.

“Good luck, Meixiu,” Chu Liuxiang and Li Jinxi said in farewell.

“See you later.”

Another month passed, and Senior Bai personally escorted Chu Liuxiang to the Ninth Heaven, where she would begin her training at the Gates of Heaven.

Now, it was just Li Jinxi who remained at Kelan’s sect.

Kelan didn’t rush her and patiently waited for her to decide.

A few days later, Li Jinxi made up her mind.

“I will go train at Heaven’s Sect,” she said to Kelan.

“Really? That’s wonderful!” Kelan’s face lit up with joy. This meant she would finally receive proper training and be able to unleash her true potential.

“However, I want to ascend to the Ninth Heaven with my own powers,” she quickly added.

“If that’s what you want.” Kelan didn’t object to her request.

A few days later, Li Jinxi departed from the sect to begin her journey to the Ninth Heaven. Without delay, she headed straight for the Stairway to Heaven to test whether she could ascend to the Eighth Heaven at her current level.

After spending nearly twenty years in the seventh region of White Hell, Yuan finally emerged from his cultivation. However, he had yet to achieve the next realm of Cold Harmony.

“How long have I been in cultivation?” Yuan asked.

“Almost twenty years,” Yu Ning responded.

“That’s quite long. Although I am close to reaching the next level of Cold Harmony, I don’t think I will be able to achieve it in a reasonable amount of time in this environment. I must head to the eighth region, if not the ninth region.”

Before leaving the cave, Yuan sent a message through his communication jade slip to Meixiu, Chu Liuxiang, and Li Jinxi about his situation. Normally, they would have quickly responded, but they were currently busy with training and hadn’t noticed that Yuan had contacted them.

Without waiting for their response, Yuan left the cave and began making his way toward the eighth region.

However, shortly after leaving the cave, he sensed a faint presence nearby-so weak that it was clear its owner was on the brink of death.

Unable to ignore it, Yuan made a detour and followed the presence.

As he got closer, he sensed several more presences, and they all appeared to be chasing after the weakened presence.

Realizing the situation, Yuan quickened his pace and soared through the harsh environment with his body cloaked in True Primordial Fire.

Sometime later, he caught up to the source of the weakened presence and saw a familiar-looking woman struggling through the seventh region. The Sun Stone she clutched was strained to its limits, unable to protect her fully.

“Are you unharmed?” Yuan asked, calling out gently without drawing too near.

Yet, his words startled her deeply, and without so much as a glance at him, the woman unleashed a fierce martial technique against him. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2151: White Hell Frost Lotus
chapters.body:
Chapter 2151: White Hell Frost Lotus

“Whoa!” Yuan wasn’t expecting to be attacked so suddenly, but he still managed to dodge her attack.

“Calm down! I am not your enemy!”

Hearing his familiar voice, the woman halted her movements and turned to look at his face. “You’re… the

one from the cave…” the woman muttered in a weak voice.

“Hm? No wonder I find your face familiar. So you were that woman who came into my cave during my

cultivation in the sixth region,” Yuan said after seeing her face clearly. “You look like you’re on the verge

of death. Need some help?”

“It doesn’t concern you… If you don’t want to die, leave me alone,” she said before turning around and

flying away.

Yuan didn’t chase after her. However, he didn’t leave either, almost as if he was waiting for something or

someone.

Sometime later, Yuan could sense several silhouettes appearing in the distance and prepared to confront

them. Within moments, these silhouettes arrived before Yuan. When they stopped, they immediately

surrounded and questioned him, “Who are you? And where’s the woman?”

“Woman? What are you talking about?” Yuan asked, pretending to be ignorant of the situation.

“Don’t play dumb. We know you met and spoke with that woman,” said one of them.

Just as Yuan had sensed their presence from afar, those cultivators also detected his-and naturally, they

could tell he was with the woman. Though they had only been together briefly, it still marked them as

connected in some way.

“If you’re talking about the injured woman I encountered just now, I don’t know her or where she went.

Why are you guys chasing after her, anyway?” Yuan said a moment later.

There were four of them in a group, three men and one woman, and they were all between the second

and third level of God Ascension. In other words, they were all powerhouses. As for the woman, she was

only at the first level of God Ascension.

“If you don’t know her, then why do you care about our business with her?”

“Not only was she greatly injured, but she was clearly running away from your group. If you have a good

reason for going after her, I won’t interfere. I am just a concerned bystander, after all.”

“Interface? What can you, a mere Golden Immortal, even do to stop us? We can kill you with a sneeze.”

One of the male cultivators sneered in disdain. Chapters first released on novel·fiɾe·net

“What can I do? You don’t want to find out,” Yuan responded with a calm smile. “If you’re not doing

anything wrong, you won’t have to, either.”

“Enough!” The female cultivator suddenly barked. “Why the hell are we wasting our time on this meddler?

That bitch is getting away with our White Hell Frost Lotus!”

“White Hell Frost Lotus?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at the unfamiliar name. Still, he quickly pieced the

situation together-it was clear this group was chasing the woman for that treasure.

“You’re right. Let’s just kill him and get this over with! Gu Ruyan, you can take care of this bastard. We’ll

go on ahead.”

“Leave him to me. It will only take a few moments to erase his existence from this world,” said the man

named Gu Ruyan.

Without another word, the other three left the scene to chase after the woman. Yuan didn’t stop them and

focused on the man surnamed Gu before him.

“Your surname is Gu? Do you happen to be from Seven Legacy Families?” Yuan asked, realizing that it’d

been a long time since he last spoke of their name.

Gu Ruyan’s eyes widened slightly with surprise. “You know me? And you still decided to stand in my

way?”

Yuan chuckled and said, “No, I have no clue who you are. In fact, this is my first time hearing your name.

That said, I have had several encounters with the Gu Family before, so I am very familiar with your family.”

Gu Ruyan narrowed his eyes at Yuan, uncertain whether he was a friend or foe of the Gu Family. If Yuan

turned out to be an ally, the situation would become far more complicated-and killing him would not be

so easy. “What’s your relationship with my Gu Family?” Gu Ruyan asked.

“We’re good friends,” Yuan lied, a bright smile on his face.

“R-Really?” Gu Ruyan was speechless.

“No, I am joking.”

“You!” Gu Ruyan’s expression immediately darkened, his gaze full of anger and killing intent. “How dare

you mock me!”

“Relax, that was a joke as well. I really am friends with the Gu Family-but the one I know is from the

lower heavens,” Yuan said calmly.

“The lower heavens? Why would you be familiar with-” Gu Ruyan suddenly halted his words, and his

expression grew grim. “There was an incident in the lower heavens where many members of the Gu

Family, including their patriarch, were slaughtered. No, it wasn’t just the Gu Family. The other Legacy

Families, as well as the Ji Family, were also involved. You wouldn’t happen to know anything about that,

would you?”

After a moment of silence, Yuan responded, “You just brought back some unpleasant memories, but to

answer your question… Yes, I happen to know a thing or two about it. After all, I was also there when it

happened.”

Gu Ruyan’s expression darkened further as he asked in a cold voice, “I never asked for your name… but if

I had to guess, it wouldn’t happen to be Yuan, would it?”

“Wow, that’s amazing. How did you guess? Are you a fortune teller, by any chance?”

“I’LL KILL YOU, YOU BASTARD!”

Without another word, Gu Ruyan drew his sword and lunged at Yuan, his entire cultivation base surging

forth.

Yuan drew Number One Under Heaven to block the incoming strike. The moment their swords collided, a

powerful ripple spread through the area-yet it barely disturbed the raging snowstorm.

“According to the information we received, you’re one of those outsiders who entered the Nine Heavens

with the Celestial Emperor’s help! It hasn’t even been a century since then! How in the world did you

reach the Eighth Heaven so quickly?!” Gu Ruyan demanded, unable to believe a Player could advance to

their realm in such a short time. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2152: Gu Ruyan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2152: Gu Ruyan

“So you know that I am a Player, huh? Is it really that surprising for me to be here?” Yuan responded with a calm smile as he blocked Gu Ruyan’s next strike with ease.

“We have been keeping track of their progress, and most of them are still in the Fifth Heaven! So how in Heaven’s name are you in the Eighth Heaven? In the seventh region of White Hell, no less!” Although those in the upper heavens were forbidden from approaching Players until they reached the Seventh Heaven, many influential clans and powerful sects still kept close watch on their progress. This way, they could not only recruit them once they qualified, but also prepare themselves to deal with them if necessary.

“And how the hell are you still alive? I thought the others had already taken care of you, hence why we stopped looking for you!” Gu Ruyan couldn’t believe that Yuan had been alive this entire time. The events in the lower heavens sent shockwaves throughout not only that realm but even the upper heavens, for never before had anyone dared to provoke the Ji Family, much less slaughter Lord Ji.

“Why wouldn’t I be alive? You guys didn’t even attack me. In fact, that ambush you laid at the Stairway to Heaven had only managed to kill innocent Players.” When Yuan first heard about the incident, he couldn’t help but laugh and feel sorry for the players at the same time.

“Hmph! Regardless, your luck ends here. I’ll finish today what the others failed to accomplish!” Gu Ruyan declared, retrieving a familiar-looking treasure.

Yuan narrowed his eyes at the rod in his hand. “The Rod of Destruction, huh? You must be desperate to kill me if you’re bringing that out so soon.”

“What?!” Gu Ruyan’s movements suddenly halted, and he stared at Yuan with wide eyes. “Y-You… How do you know about the Rod of Destruction?! This item is extremely scarce and only available to a few people!”

Without any explanation, Yuan retrieved the Rod of Destruction he obtained from Zeng Guoquan at the Absyssal Underground and showed it to Gu Ruyan.

“You! Why the fuck do you have that?! How did you obtain it?!”

“I picked it up.”

“Bullshit!”

“So, what are you going to do now? Want to use it at the same time and see who comes out alive?” Yuan said with a calm smile on his face.

Hearing Yuan’s words, Gu Ruyan sneered, “You probably don’t know this, but there are three tiers of rarity for the Rod of Destruction, and the higher the tier, the more powerful it is! While your Rod of Destruction is only tier 3, mine is tier 2! I’ll completely obliterate you!”

“In that case, why don’t you show me just how powerful your tier 2 Rod of Destruction really is? If not, I’ll use mine,” Yuan said, spreading his arms in a provoking gesture.

Without hesitation, Gu Ruyan began pouring his spiritual energy into the Rod of Destruction. Although using such a powerful treasure may seem excessive at face value, Gu Ruyan considered Yuan to be threatening enough to use it. Although Yuan was only a Golden Immortal while he himself was a God Ascension cultivator, Gu Ruyan realized from their brief exchanges that Yuan actually surpassed him in sheer prowess. Moreover, with Yuan’s status as a Player blessed by the Celestial Emperor, Gu Ruyan knew he could not afford to treat him lightly. “This bastard was strong enough to kill Lord Ji and the others in the lower heavens. He cannot be underestimated!” Gu Ruyan thought to himself as he pointed the Rod of Destruction at Yuan. “Pulverize this bastard!”

The Rod of Destruction released a surge of black aura, and from its tip burst a crimson beam that struck toward Yuan in an instant. At the same time, Gu Ruyan used a treasure that instantly teleported him tens of thousands of miles away from his original location.

The instant the crimson beam touched Yuan, it erupted, creating a massive mushroom cloud that swept away the raging snowstorm of the seventh region and obliterated every mountain within a five-thousand- mile radius. The destruction was so powerful that even a God Ascension cultivator would instantly perish beneath its prowess.

“What the fuck?!” The other three from Gu Ruyan’s group were shocked when they saw the commotion behind them. “Did that bastard really use the Rod of Destruction to take care of a mere Golden Immortal?! What is that idiot doing?!”

“The two of you go ahead! I’ll go back and see the situation!” one of the men said. The other two nodded their head and continued their pursuit. Meanwhile, the one who stayed behind began traveling back to Gu Ruyan.

“Holy shit…” Gu Ruyan muttered, his eyes wide and his mouth agape as he stared at the devastation. It was his first time witnessing the Rod of Destruction in action-and its power far exceeded anything he had imagined. “No wonder the others were going crazy for this thing. The range might not be that wide, but the raw power is insane… anyone caught in the blast would be wiped out without a trace.” Updates are released by novel-fire.ɴet

Sure enough, the power was so overwhelming that not even Yuan’s physique could withstand it. His body was completely obliterated, but his soul endured, suffering only minor injuries that quickly healed on their own.

“Alright, that was a little scary,” Yuan muttered as his body rapidly regenerated from nothing but his soul. When his form reconstituted, he found himself far from the blast site, having been hurled away by its sheer force.
