“By the heavens… never have I witnessed a treasure so fearsome,” Yu Ning murmured, her voice dazed. Feng Yuxiang nodded her head in agreement. “Though the world holds many powerful treasures, few-if any can rival its sheer destructive might.”

“And this isn’t even the strongest Rod of Destruction. If this is only the second strongest, how terrifying must the first-tier one be?” Yuan sighed. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2153: Gu Ruyan (2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2153: Gu Ruyan (2)

“What are you going to do next, Young Master?” Yu Ning asked Yuan shortly later. Yuan initially planned to return to Gu Ruyan, but he changed his mind when he sensed that one of Gu Ruyan’s friends had returned. “He definitely thinks I died to the Rod of Destruction, so I’ll deal with Gu Ruyan later. Right now, I should save that woman before his friends catch up to her and kill her.” Yuan had activated Shadow Veil the moment he restored his body, so Gu Ruyan was unaware that he had survived the attack. Thus, Yuan chased after the other two while Gu Ruyan reunited with his friend who had just returned.

“Gu Ruyan! What the fuck do you think you are doing, using the Rod of Destruction in this place?! Don’t tell me you used it just to take care of that Golden Immortal!” The friend immediately began lecturing Gu Ruyan the moment they reunited.

“I-I had no choice! He also had a Rod of Destruction with him! If I hadn’t used it, he would’ve used it on me!” Gu Ruyan exclaimed his reasoning.

“What? That Golden Immortal had a Rod of Destruction, too? Unless he’s part of the faction, that’s impossible. And if he really had one, why did you kill him? What if he was an ally?!”

“No, he wasn’t an ally. He got the Rod of Destruction from someone else. I heard that Zeng Guoquan had recently perished at the Abyssal Underground. It’s likely that he took it from her.”

“Didn’t Zeng Guoquan die from a natural phenomenon there?”

“That’s what we were told, but nobody truly knows since no one actually witnessed her death. If not for that, I can’t imagine how that bastard got his hands on a Rod of Destruction. Another reason I know he’s no ally is because that man was Yuan-the one who slaughtered Lord Ji and many of the Seven Legacy Families’ leaders back in Spirit Heaven,” Gu Ruyan revealed.

“W-Wait a second. He was that Yuan?! I thought he’d died in the ambush at the Stairway to Heaven!” His friend exclaimed with disbelief.

“He said that the ambush had only killed innocent people. I don’t know how he managed to slip away back then, but he’s definitely dead now. There’s no chance he could’ve survived the Rod of Destruction.” He was certain the Rod of Destruction had struck Yuan because once its laser locked onto a target, it would cling to them. Even if Yuan had tried to escape with a movement technique or a treasure, it would still have followed him.

“Regardless, let’s get out of here before someone comes and questions us. We cannot let the world know of the Rod of Destruction’s existence right now. It’s still too early.” Gu Ruyan nodded, and the two quickly departed to resume their pursuit. However, they chose not to rejoin their companions right away, wary that someone might have witnessed everything.

Meanwhile, the male and female cultivators who had never abandoned the chase finally caught up to the woman they had been pursuing all along. “We’ve finally caught you, you little rat who stole our White Hell Frost Lotus!”

“That’s funny, because I don’t recall ever stealing it from you. In fact, I was the one who harvested the White Hell Frost Lotus-you bastards were the ones trying to steal it from me!” the injured woman snapped.

“That’s nonsense! We had been camping the White Hell Frost Lotus long before you ever showed up! You just got lucky and harvested it while we were distracted!” one of them retorted.

“Hah! Empty words! Anyone can claim that without proof! I watched over the White Hell Frost Lotus for an entire year, and not once did I see any of you there!” she shot back.

The female pursuer shook her head and said, “Why bother arguing with someone who will soon be a corpse? If you obediently hand over the White Hell Frost Lotus, we’ll let you die a peaceful death. Otherwise, we’ll make it as painful and shameful as possible.” The injured woman gritted her teeth in frustration, her eyes clouded with despair as the realization struck-that there was no one to help her, and she was truly going to die here. Tears welled in the woman’s eyes, but the extreme cold of White Hell froze them the instant they appeared. Tʜe source of this ᴄontent ɪs novel·fire.net

“This is why you should’ve accepted my help when I offered it.” A familiar voice suddenly echoed through the air.

“Who’s there?!” The two pursuers tensed in alarm, but no matter how they searched, they couldn’t sense a single presence nearby, as if they were hearing the voice of a ghost. The next moment, a figure appeared before the injured woman like a ghost.

“Y-You! You’re that Golden Immortal from before! Why the hell are you here?! What happened to Gu Ruyan and Lin Canye?!” The two instantly recognized Yuan’s handsome face and exclaimed in a startled voice. “Don’t tell me you killed them!” said the male pursuer.

“That’s impossible! There’s no way a Golden Immortal like him could kill two God Ascension cultivators!” the woman expressed her doubt.

offer still stands. Do you need my help?” Yuan asked her.

“Why would you risk your life to help me…?” she asked skeptically. “We don’t even know each other.”

Yuan smiled warmly. “So what if we don’t? Since when do we need to know someone in order to help them? I saw someone in need, so I offered my hand. That said, you look like someone I know.” The injured woman was left speechless. Benevolent cultivators like Yuan were rare in the cultivation world, especially at their level. After all, the best way to survive was to mind one’s own business, and meddling in matters that didn’t concern them was the quickest way to court death.

“W-What is your name?” she suddenly asked.

“My name is Yuan.”

“Yuan?” The other two frowned, their brows lifting at the familiar name, though they didn’t immediately recognize it.

“Please… help me, Yuan,” the injured woman pleaded. Yuan gave a silent nod in acknowledgment, his gaze steady. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2154: Familiar-Looking Sword
chapters.body:
Chapter 2154: Familiar-Looking Sword

“Hah!” The other woman suddenly sneered out loud. “I don’t know how you managed to escape Gu Ruyan and Lin Canye, but you’re in no position to help others.”

Yuan turned to face the woman and asked, “And who are you? Are you also surnamed Gu? Or do you belong to one of the other Legacy Families?”

“You know who we are?” The woman expressed surprise at Yuan’s words.

“Not really, but now I know you’re with the Seven Legacy Families.”

“You know our background, yet you still want to defy us? You’re dumber than I thought,” said the man beside her.

Yuan shifted his gaze toward him and sneered. “Let’s not pretend the Seven Legacy Families are some grand power in the upper heavens. You may have been all-powerful in the lower realms, but up here, your influence is nothing special.”

“How dare you! Our families may not appear as imposing in the upper heavens, but that’s only because we choose not to stand out! In truth, our influence is anything but small!” the man barked.

“Shut up! Why the hell are you telling him everything?!” the woman suddenly snapped at him.

“S-Sorry… but does it really matter? He’s going to die soon, anyway.”

“If your friend couldn’t kill me even with the Rod of Destruction, what can the two of you little sheep do to me?” Yuan said.

“?!?!” Their eyes went wide, astonishment all over their faces, as Yuan’s words were nothing short of unbelievable. Gu Ruyan had a tier 2 Rod of Destruction, which could instantly kill anyone below the third level of God Ascension. Yet, the man standing before them was only a Golden Immortal. Unless Gu Ruyan missed the attack, there was simply no chance that Yuan could’ve survived it.

“What should we do, Sister Chen? If what he says is true-“

“Zhuang Maojiang, are you an idiot?! Of course he’s bluffing! Now hurry up and kill him! I will take care of that thieving bitch!” she shouted.

Zhuang Maojiang gave a hurried nod before drawing forth his battleaxe, then charged toward Yuan in the next instant. Expecting Zhuang Maojiang to finish Yuan quickly, the woman surnamed Chen circled around the fight and rushed toward the injured woman.

However, in their very first exchange, Yuan completely overpowered Zhuang Maojiang, sending him flying back before turning to confront the woman.

“What the hell are you doing, Zhuang Maojiang?! Stop playing around and kill him already!”

“What the ?!” Zhuang Maojiang’s face twisted in shock as he realized he had just lost to Yuan’s sword. His cultivation was higher, and his battleaxe should have held the advantage in direct clashes-yet he still lost, and so decisively at that.

However, the woman’s voice jolted him back to his senses, and he forcefully shook off his shock before flying at Yuan once more. This time, he didn’t just use raw strength.

“Heavy Dragon’s Descent!” Zhuang Maojiang raised his battleaxe high, activating a martial technique that caused the weapon to swell in size. Once it had grown several times larger, he brought it down in a vertical slash aimed at Yuan.

“Hmph.” Yuan sneered and casually swung his sword upward, meeting Zhuang Maojiang’s axe head-on once again. When their weapons collided, the mountains behind Yuan and the ground beneath them split apart. Yet Yuan stood perfectly fine-his grip steady, his stance unshaken, barely moving from the clash. After refining the Dragon Ancestor’s Blood Essence, Yuan’s raw power had skyrocketed, surpassing not only the limits of humans but even those of dragons. At this moment, there was no one who could rival him in sheer strength, and this doesn’t even take into account his True Dragon Awakening, which would only further boost his strength.

“Scram!” Yuan swung his sword the next moment, sending Zhuang Maojiang and his axe flying into the distance.

“Impossible!” The woman surnamed Chen exclaimed after witnessing their exchange. “Who the hell are you?!” Content originally comes from noᴠelfire.net

“I already gave you my name,” he calmly responded.

“That’s not what I meant!” she cried out loud.

However, her brows suddenly twitched, and her eyes slowly widened as though she had just realized something. “Wait a second… Yuan? That’s the name of the madman who slaughtered the Lord of Spirit Heaven, along with the leaders of the Seven Legacy Families that ruled there! How are you still alive?! I was told you had been taken care of!”

“Sorry to disappoint you, but as you can see, I am very much alive.”

The woman gritted her teeth and drew her weapon-a purple sword that radiated an ominous aura that distorted the very air around it.

Yuan raised his eyebrows when he saw her familiar-looking sword, but before he could even say anything, the sword in his grasp trembled, and Zi Xuan’s voice boomed inside his head, “That’s my sword!” The sword in the woman’s grasp was none other than Violet Dream, one of the Exalted Blacksmith’s earliest creations. It was also something he had given to Zi Xuan in the past. And Zi Xuan, who had been asleep this entire time, was jolted awake by its familiar aura.

“You should’ve stayed hidden after barely escaping with your life last time. Now that you’ve shown yourself, I’ll make sure to finish the job,” the woman said as she leveled Violet Dream at Yuan’s face.

“This bitch…” Zi Xuan growled, her trembling voice on the verge of erupting. “Not only is she tainting my treasure with her filthy hands, but she dares to point it at you?! I’ll tear her apart, limb by limb!”

“Calm down. There’s no need to waste your energy on a weakling like her,” Yuan said quickly, trying to soothe her anger.

“But my Violet Dream!”

“Don’t worry, I’ll get it back for you.”

“Alright… but you better hurry, or I might accidentally do it myself.”

Meanwhile, the woman surnamed Chen suddenly felt a chill run down her spine, and an unexplainable urge to flee seized her. Even though Zi Xuan had not emerged from the sword, her killing intent was so overwhelming that it alerted Chen’s survival instincts. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2155: Creator'S Mark
chapters.body:
Chapter 2155: Creator'S Mark

“So, what are you going to do with that Violet Dream? Put me into an eternal slumber?” Yuan asked the woman, snapping her out of her daze.

“You recognize this sword?” she muttered in a surprised tone, before quickly adding, “No, I shouldn’t be surprised. This is a very famous sword, created by the legendary blacksmith, the Exalted Blacksmith, after all!”

“Where’d you get it?” Yuan then asked.

“That’s none of your business. Instead of wasting your last few words on such questions, why don’t you beg for your life instead?”

“Beg for my life? I don’t think I have done so in all my lives,” Yuan responded with a smile.

“Lives?” The woman raised an eyebrow at his strange choice of words but dismissed it as a slip of the tongue.

“Sister Chen, let’s fight him together! He’s far more powerful than he appears!” Zhuang Maojiang shouted as he finally returned after being sent flying into the distance. He could still feel his arms trembling from his clash with Yuan.

Lady Chen then spoke, “Get him!” At her command, Zhuang Maojiang lunged at Yuan from behind as Lady Chen pressed in from the front. However, it didn’t take long for them to realize that defeating Yuan wouldn’t be easy, even when attacking together. They could not fathom how they were being held at bay by one bound to the Golden Immortal realm.

Sometime later, as they were beginning to lose hope, Gu Ruyan and Lin Canye finally returned to their side. “What the fuck?! That bastard is still alive! Gu Ruyan, you imbecile! You recklessly used the Rod of Destruction and didn’t even kill your target?!” Lin Canye shouted after seeing Yuan.

“That’s impossible”! Gu Ruyan exclaimed in a startled voice, sounding like he’d just witnessed a ghost. “I am certain that the Rod of Destruction had attached to him! His presence even completely vanished after the explosion!”

“Then are you trying to say that we’re looking at a ghost right now?! One’s that’s fighting Sister Chen and Zhuang Maojiang?!” Lin Canye pointed at Yuan’s figure in the distance.

Gu Ruyan gritted his teeth and said, “I don’t know how he managed to survive, but we can figure that out later. We need to help them first! That bastard is much stronger than his cultivation suggests!” Without any hesitation, the two of them joined the fight the very next moment.

“Welcome back,” Yuan said to them.

“What took you two idiots so long to come back?!” Lady Chen reprimanded.

“We had to make sure nobody saw the Rod of Destruction going off, so we took a slight detour,” Lin Canye explained.

“Whatever. Let’s hurry up and kill this bastard. The White Hell Frost Lotus is waiting for us!” And so, Yuan was forced to contend with all four God Ascension cultivators simultaneously. The injured woman, witnessing this, could only stare in stunned silence, her lips parted and her face awash with disbelief.

“What’s taking you so long to take care of a few pipsqueaks? Hurry up and kill them!” Zi Xuan urged, her patience running thin.

“Then why don’t you remove some of the restrictions you have placed on the sword? I should be strong enough to handle more now,” said Yuan, referring to the Number One Under Heaven having its power limited by her due to his lack of cultivation.

“I guess it’s fine to loosen a few restrictions right now, but you are still far away from handling me at my full potential.” The next moment-<Sword Spirit ‘Zi Xuan’ has removed a restriction placed upon Number One Under Heaven><Number One Under Heaven has been updated>[Unique Ability (5): When wielding Number One Under Heaven, the wielder’s Sword Aura efficiency and power are increased by 100,000%] Sometime later, when they still hadn’t managed to defeat Yuan, Lady Chen shouted in frustration, “Restrict his movements for me!” To unleash Violet Dream’s ability, she needed to land a direct hit, but so far, she hadn’t even touched Yuan. At her command, the other three immediately charged at Yuan, intent on pinning him down so Lady Chen could strike.

“Even if you manage to land a hit, Violet Dream won’t affect me.”

“You’re bluffing!” Lady Chen snapped.

“You think so? Then go ahead.” Yuan suddenly ceased moving, spreading his arms wide in a defenseless stance as he beckoned her forward.

Without hesitation, Lady Chen lunged forward and stabbed the Violet Dream directly in his chest, purposefully avoiding his heart so he wouldn’t die immediately. After landing her strike, Lady Chen immediately tried to activate Violet Dream’s ability. But her expression darkened when she realized it wasn’t responding. Since Number One Under Heaven was out, it’d sealed Violet Dream’s ability, turning it into an ordinary sword. However, even without Number One Under Heaven, Violet Dream still wouldn’t have any effect on Yuan.

“W-Why?! Why isn’t it working?!” Lady Chen cried, violently shaking the sword as though she could force it awake.

“It’s useless.” Yuan suddenly said as he moved his arm and pointed at the Violet Dream before uttering, “Reveal yourself.” In the next instant, a glowing symbol surfaced on the sword’s blade, radiating a divine

aura. ʀᴇᴀᴅ ʟᴀᴛᴇsᴛ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀᴛ novel[f]ire.net

“This… this is…” Lady Chen’s eyes widened in a daze as she stared at the golden symbol, unable to believe what she was seeing.

“Isn’t that the Creator’s Mark?!” Gu Ruyan remarked, shock flashing across his face.

“What? But isn’t that something only its creator can reveal?” Lin Canye cried. A Creator’s Mark is something blacksmiths embed within their creations as proof of authorship, serving both as a signature and a means of verifying the weapon’s legitimacy. Although anyone could see the Creator’s Mark on a treasure, only its creator could activate it.

“Wait… if the mark has manifested at this moment… does that mean he’s the creator?” Zhuang Maojiang questioned, his voice laced with confusion. Nobody answered his question, as they didn’t want to believe the implications. After a long silence, Lady Chen slowly lifted her gaze from the sword to Yuan, who hovered before them with a cold smile.

“Impossible…” she muttered. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2156: Barbarians
chapters.body:
Chapter 2156: Barbarians

“That’s impossible! You can’t possibly be the Exalted Blacksmith!” Lady Chen shouted, her voice sharp

enough to cut through the air. “I know! This sword must be a fake—it can’t be the real Violet Dream!” Desperately, she created an excuse, however absurd it may be. To her, it was still more reasonable than accepting the notion that Yuan could be the Exalted Blacksmith.

“You can believe whatever you want, but it won’t change the fact that you’re holding onto something that belongs to me,” Yuan said as he beckoned for the weapon. “Hand me the sword, and I will grant you a painless death. Refuse, and I will take it myself—along with your fingers, perhaps even your limbs,” Yuan said as his aura surged violently.

Seeing this, the four of them tensed instantly, and Lady Chen quickly drew her Rod of Destruction. Yuan glanced at the rod in her grasp and smiled. “Have you already forgotten? Your companion tried that on me once before, but here I am, still alive.”

“If one isn’t enough, how about two?!” Lin Canye barked, drawing his own Rod of Destruction. “I have one as well!” Zhuang Maojiang shouted, quickly brandishing his rod after the others. Seeing this, Gu Ruyan could only watch with clenched fists.

“I’m flattered, but it doesn’t matter how many of those you bring out-you won’t kill me,” Yuan said with a calm smile.

“Then prove it with your life!” Lady Chen snapped, spiritual energy surging as she poured it into the Rod of Destruction. When the other two noticed Lady Chen truly preparing to unleash the Rod of Destruction, they quickly funneled their spiritual energy into their own treasures.

The smile on Yuan’s face suddenly stiffened, his expression darkening.

“Hah! So it was all a bluff! You’re actually trembling in fear, aren’t you?!” Lady Chen laughed mockingly. In truth, Yuan had no concern for their Rods of Destruction, as such weapons were beneath his notice. His frown was born from something far more troubling.

In the very next instant, several powerful presences emerged, surrounding them. Before the others could even react, many silhouettes materialized, encircling them completely.

“What the ? Who the hell are you?!” Gu Ruyan shouted.

Lady Chen opened her mouth to speak, but the words died in her throat as she realized that their encirclement was made entirely of God Ascension powerhouses. While the other four panicked, Yuan remained calm, his gaze sweeping over their encirclement. There were exactly twenty-two figures, each clad in crude garments of thick animal fur, their rugged features giving them the appearance of barbarians. As for these barbarians, their attention was focused on the Rods of Destruction in their hands. “W-What do you want from us?” Zhuang Maojiang stammered.

One of the barbarians finally spoke, though his words weren’t directed at him. “Is that the weapon you saw?”

A calm, gentle voice echoed the next moment. “Yes. That is the weapon responsible for the explosion- the one that devastated much of our land.”

The barbarian who had first spoken stepped forward, his voice carrying authority. “The six of you will come with us. Refuse, and you will die where you stand.”

“Who the hell are you to tell us what to do? Do you even realize who we are? We are ” Lin Canye scoffed, his voice dripping with challenge. Fresh chapters posted on novel{f}ire.net

However, he never even got to finish his sentence, as the barbarian leader’s arm blurred through the air, and in the next instant, Lin Canye’s head was sent flying, severed cleanly from his body. Of course, as a God Ascension cultivator, losing his body did not mean losing his life. But the one who struck did not pause, and after severing Lin Canye’s head, he extended his hand in a grasping motion toward the lingering soul. An immense pressure coiled around Lin Canye’s soul, then crushed it without mercy. This was all done in an instant, faster than anyone could even blink, leaving the other three wide-eyed, trembling in shock and fear. Lin Canye’s corpse began falling from the sky, but one of the barbarians went to retrieve it.

“Anyone else have any objections?” The barbarian leader asked.

“…” Lady Chen and the others hastily shook their head in silence, too afraid to even speak.

And so, Yuan and the others had no choice but to follow the barbarians deeper into White Hell. Of course, this included the injured woman.

“W-What’s happening…?” she stuck close to Yuan and asked him.

“We’ll know in a bit,” he responded in a calm voice.

He then glanced at her and asked, “How’s your condition? Can you endure the cold?”

The woman looked at the Sun Stone in her grasp for a moment before responding, “I’m fine for now, but I don’t think my Sun Stone will last much longer.”

Yuan then said, “Don’t worry, nothing will happen to you. I promised to help you, so I will get you out of here safely.”

“Alright… By the way, I don’t think I have introduced myself yet. My name is Yaoqin.”

“Nice to meet you, Yaoqin.” Yuan smiled.

Gu Ruyan and the other two were in utter disbelief. While they trembled at the thought of being kidnapped by these unknown barbarians, Yuan appeared completely at ease, almost as if their current predicament didn’t exist at all, and he was just with a group of old pals. The barbarians, too, noticed his calm demeanor and grew curious. Within their ranks, one individual fixed their eyes on Yuan, watching him with far greater intensity than the others.

Yuan noticed the intent gaze and boldly turned to confront it. Positioned at the very back of the formation was a figure wrapped in a great fur robe that concealed most of their features. Yet what could not be hidden were their eyes-clear, piercing blue, and as immaculate as untouched ice beneath a winter sky. Though he didn’t recognize the individual, a strange sense of familiarity stirred within him. Suddenly, the image of a silhouette riding a massive mammoth, which he had glimpsed upon first entering the seventh region, surfaced in his mind. ‘So you were that silhouette, huh?’ Yuan thought inwardly. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2157: Barbarians (2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2157: Barbarians (2)

After several days of travel, the barbarians halted at the border of the Eighth Region, where a small settlement unexpectedly stood. Lady Chen and the others were stunned-none of them had believed it was possible for anyone to survive White Hell’s extreme environment long enough to build a home there. Shortly after arriving, the barbarians gathered them together and stood around them in silence. After several long minutes of silence, Gu Ruyan finally gathered the courage to ask, “What are you going to do to us?” The barbarians turned their gazes on him, and the sheer weight of their eyes made him tremble with fear.

“What do you think we’re going to do?” a calm voice suddenly replied. Gu Ruyan and the others turned toward the sound, where a tall figure stepped out from one of the nearby buildings. She was a tall, muscular woman, her frame radiating dominance. From her surged an overwhelming aura-the unmistakable might of an expert at the Seventh Level of God Ascension. Following closely behind her was the barbarian who killed Lin Canye, as well as the individual riding the giant mammoth.

“H-How should I know? You’re the ones who kidnapped us without a word of explanation,” Gu Ruyan finally replied.

“Without explanation, you say?” The muscular woman lifted her arm, revealing the Rod of Destruction taken from Lin Canye’s corpse. “Because of this little toy, you not only destroyed a large part of our territory, but also killed one of our people,” she said coldly.

“No way…” Gu Ruyan muttered in a daze. “I-I didn’t know there were people living inside White Hell, much less in the seventh region, hence why I decided to use it!” he said.

“I don’t care for your reasoning. It doesn’t change the fact that one of our people is dead because of you.”

“Wait just a second!” Lady Chen suddenly cut in. “He’s the one who triggered that weapon and killed your people! Why drag all of us into this? You’ve already killed one of ours, so why not call it even?”

“Call it even?” the barbarian snarled, her glare burning into Lady Chen. “Do you truly believe your lives are worth the same as ours? Even if we slaughtered every last one of you, it still wouldn’t repay the loss of a single one of our people!”

“That’s unreasonable!” Zhuang Maojiang cried.

“Unreasonable?” the barbarian retorted coldly. “I heard you were ready to unleash this weapon again before my people intervened. If not for them, even more of our land would have been reduced to ruin!”

Yuan then spoke, “I understand why you’d want their lives, but what about me and her?” He gestured to Yaoqin, who was standing so close to him that she was essentially clinging to his arm. “We’re also their victims,” he continued.

The barbarian sneered, “Victims or not, it doesn’t change the fact that you are involved. If you hadn’t started a fight with them, they wouldn’t have needed to use the weapon in the first place.”

Yuan sighed at her response. “Since you’ve clearly made up your mind, there’s no point in arguing. So tell me, what do you intend to do with us for our crimes?”

The barbarian’s eyes narrowed. “Simply executing you would be far too easy. And besides, it would be a waste, as few ever venture this deep into White Hell.” A vicious smile curved across her lips as she continued, “Here’s how it will be your men will serve as training dummies for our warriors and your women will entertain them in other ways.”

Lady Chen and Yaoqin’s expressions turned grim at the barbarian’s words. Nᴇw novel chapters are publɪshed on NoveI(F)ire.net

“I’d sooner die than become a toy for you savages!” Lady Chen shouted, drawing her Rod of Destruction. “You were fools not to strip us of our treasures!”

The barbarian laughed. “We didn’t take your treasures because we’re not the least bit worried. I can kill you long before you even think of activating that weapon.”

Lady Chen gritted her teeth, feeling powerless before these brutes. “I-If you kill us, our families will avenge us-they’ll wipe out your insignificant clan!” Lady Chen shouted desperately.

The barbarian sneered. “Do you know how long we’ve lived here? We know White Hell like the back of our hands. Your families would die long before they even reach us, and even if a few survived, they’d never be able to pursue us through this place.”

“T-That’s-!” Lady Chen faltered, unable to find her words, and fell silent. The others, too, sank into despair as the weight of their situation settled over them. Yuan alone remained calm, unshaken amidst the tension.

At that moment, the cloaked figure who had been watching Yuan all along could no longer restrain their curiosity and slowly approached him. Standing before Yuan, the figure finally spoke. “Why aren’t you afraid?” The voice was that of a young woman, clear and unassuming.

“Should I be afraid?” Yuan replied with a calm smile.

The figure glanced at the others, trembling in fear, before turning back to him. “Is it because of your body?” she asked.

“My body? What are you talking about?” Yuan asked with his eyebrows slightly raised.

“I noticed it the first time I saw you twenty years ago. Your body is different from the others,” she said. At her words, the surrounding barbarians narrowed their eyes at Yuan. They, too, could sense something unusual about him, though none could discern exactly what it was.

“Now that you mention it…” The other female barbarian stepped forward, studying Yuan intently. “His body isn’t being affected by White Hell’s environment at all. If anything, it looks as though it grows stronger with each passing moment. It’s a bit similar to our own physique.” After a brief silence, she continued, “You know what? Rather than letting the others kill you, we’ll study your body instead.”

Yuan let out a faint sigh. “I couldn’t care less what you do with those three idiots, but if you harm me or my friend, I’ll be left with no choice but to slaughter everyone here in self-defense.” His sharp words rang in the barbarians’ ears, instantly igniting their auras and filling the air with killing intent. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2158: Absolute Dragon Awakening
chapters.body:
Chapter 2158: Absolute Dragon Awakening

After a long, tense silence, the barbarian suddenly burst into laughter, her voice so powerful it rattled the air itself. “You’ll slaughter us all? With what-your pitiful cultivation base?” she mocked.

“I wouldn’t recommend it, but you’re more than welcome to find out,” Yuan responded with a calm smile on his handsome face.

The barbarian’s grin vanished, replaced by a chilling frown. “I dislike you. Arrogant, weak, and oblivious- you don’t even know when to hold your tongue.” Without giving Yuan a chance to respond, she lifted her arm high and struck. In the blink of an eye, his head was reduced to nothing.

“Yuan!” Yaoqin cried, her eyes going wide with horror. How could it be? He had fallen so easily, when only moments ago his calm and confidence had seemed unshakable.

Seeing this, Lady Chen muttered, “I knew he was bluffing this entire time… I should’ve killed him when I had the chance!” Strangely, even after his head had been annihilated, Yuan’s body showed no sign of collapse. It stood tall and unwavering, exuding the eerie impression that he was still very much alive.

“How unpleasant.” The barbarian clicked her tongue and reached for Yuan’s body. Though she had destroyed his head, she had deliberately left the rest intact since she still intended to study his physique.

What followed left the barbarian-and everyone else-frozen in shock. The headless corpse moved. Against all logic, it raised an arm and caught the barbarian’s hand in an unyielding grip. Then, everyone watched as Yuan’s destroyed head regenerated completely.

“Heavens! It’s a monster!” The barbarians cried out loud.

Once Yuan’s regeneration was finished, he calmly stared at the barbarian before him and said, “You’ll need to do more than just destroy my head to kill me.”

“What the hell are you…?” The barbarian muttered in a low voice.

“Is that how he survived the Rod of Destruction?” Gu Ruyan stared at Yuan in a daze.

The barbarian yanked and twisted, trying to break away from Yuan’s grip. But to her utter disbelief, she could not budge an inch. His grasp was immovable, heavy as if a mountain itself were shackled to her arm. ‘What is happening?! His raw strength… It’s beyond comprehension!’ the barbarian cried inwardly, panic flashing in her eyes.

While she was still caught in disbelief, Yuan pulled his other arm back and unleashed a devastating punch toward her face. Despite being lost in thought, the barbarian still managed to intercept Yuan’s punch. But his strength was monstrous and beyond her comprehension. The instant their clash resounded, her body was wrenched from the ground, and before she even understood what had happened, she was sent crashing backward.

“Chief Mu?!” The other barbarians exclaimed in shock after seeing her being pushed back by a mere Golden Immortal.

The female barbarian, Chief Mu, quickly steadied herself in midair, though the shock on her face lingered and refused to fade. After staring at Yuan in silence, excitement suddenly lit up Chief Mu’s face. Her cultivation burst forth in the next instant, flooding the area with tyrannical pressure. “I don’t care who or what you are. Survive ten of my attacks, and I’ll let you and your friend live,” she declared.

Yuan’s lips curved into a smile. “You’d better not go back on your word… or I’ll kill you.” As Yuan stepped forward, he activated Absolute Dragon Awakening the evolved form of True Dragon Awakening after consuming the Dragon Ancestor’s Blood Essence. His aura soared skyward, surging to unimaginable heights. Compared to his former True Dragon Awakening, this new power was at least a hundred times stronger. More astonishing still, his cultivation, which had stagnated at Golden Immortal, suddenly shattered its bottleneck, propelling him into True Immortal. Yet this advancement was not a true breakthrough but merely a temporary boost brought on by Absolute Dragon Awakening. For that reason, it could not be counted toward his quest.

“?!?!” Chief Mu could scarcely believe what she was seeing-her features frozen in bewildered awe. And yet, instead of fear, a spark of exhilaration flared ever brighter in her eyes.

Since his opponent was a seventh-level God Ascension powerhouse, Yuan couldn’t afford to go easy. Not only did he use Absolute Dragon Awakening, but he also retrieved Number One Under Heaven to fight Chief Mu. Though she stood at the Seventh Level of God Ascension-an expert nearing the peak of the cultivation world-Chief Mu didn’t dare underestimate Yuan, despite him being merely an Immortal. She summoned her weapon: a pair of axes gleaming with a chilling light, as though carved from pure ice. Gripping them firmly, she advanced to confront Yuan.

With Number One Under Heaven in hand, Yuan cloaked it in his Supreme Sword Aura. Enhanced by its fifth unique ability, the aura sharpened to an unimaginable degree-so keen that even the slightest movement seemed to rend the fabric of space, leaving a visible scar in the air. In response, Chief Mu coated her axes with Supreme Axe Aura. Yet compared to Yuan’s enhanced Supreme Sword Aura, hers was clearly inferior. To bridge the gap, she infused her weapons with Celestial Qi.

When they clashed a split second later, their weapon auras erupted outward, tearing countless slits through the surrounding space and exposing the ominous void beyond. Yet, in the next breath, the wounds in reality swiftly mended themselves.

“That’s one,” Yuan said.

“I can see why you’re so damn arrogant, but you still have to survive nine more strikes!” Chief Mu exclaimed as she prepared her second strike.

Meanwhile, Gu Ruyan and the others could only watch in stunned disbelief. “We’ve been fighting a monster like that this whole time…?” Lady Chen murmured in a daze. Thɪs chapter is updated by novelFirｅ.net

“Impossible… How can an outsider wield such power…?” Gu Ruyan collapsed to his knees in despair.

Yaoqin remained silent, yet the astonishment in her eyes spoke louder than words. The barbarian wrapped in fur robes narrowed her eyes on Yuan, seemingly in deep thought. Moments later, Chief Mu unleashed her second strike, and Yuan confronted it with a smile. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2159: Ten Strikes From Chief Mu
chapters.body:
Chapter 2159: Ten Strikes From Chief Mu

Chief Mu’s second strike carried even greater force than her first. When it collided with Yuan’s, the clash of their weapon auras unleashed a ripple that cleaved every mountain within a thousand miles cleanly in half. After using her second strike and seeing Yuan emerge completely unscathed, Chief Mu understood that ordinary attacks would not be able to defeat a monster like him.

On her third strike, Chief Mu gathered her spiritual energy and prepared to launch a martial technique. “Glacial Roar!” Chief Mu smashed her axes together, sending millions of tiny ice shards in Yuan’s direction. Despite their small size, each of these shards was powerful enough to kill Immortals. Yet, Yuan didn’t even bother blocking them, merely using his physique to endure them. <Your Cold Resistance has improved greatly> Follow current novels on N()velFire.net

“Heavens! He used nothing but his body to block Chief Mu’s Glacial Roar! I’ve never seen anything like that before!” The barbarians stared in astonishment. Realizing that spiritual energy had no effect on Yuan, Chief Mu immediately shifted to using Immortal Qi. Though she was capable of wielding Celestial Qi with ease, she held back, as she was enjoying their battle too much to end it quickly.

For her fourth strike, Chief Mu raised her axes high. In the next instant, they swelled to the size of mountains before crashing down toward Yuan’s figure, tiny and ant-like in comparison. In response, Yuan activated Number One Under Heaven’s third ability, increasing the size of his sword to match Chief Mu’s axes. Whoosh! Their clash unleashed a ripple of force so immense that every trace of snow in the surrounding land was erased in an instant. At the point of collision, space itself fractured once more, dark cracks spreading wide-and unlike before, the damage lingered, struggling to mend. Once again, Yuan endured her attack without any injuries.

‘Just how many tricks does this monster have up his sleeves?!’ Chief Mu cried inwardly as she prepared her fifth strike. For her fifth, sixth, and seventh strikes, Chief Mu continued to wield Immortal Qi, each time pouring in drastically more than before, and every single clash resulted in the space around them breaking.

After their seventh clash, the skies above White Hell darkened over Yuan’s battlefield, as though the heavens were warning them to cease or suffer its wrath. “Look at the sky! It suddenly turned dark!” one of the barbarians exclaimed. “You’re right! But why? It’s not even close to nightfall-and it feels unnatural.” “Heaven’s Wrath…” Gu Ruyan muttered in a daze. “If they continue, they will have to face Heaven’s Judgement!” Their clash had reached such terrifying heights that even the heavens stirred, fearing the disastrous destruction that would follow if it were allowed to continue.

Chief Mu glanced at the dark clouds for a brief moment before disregarding them. As for Yuan, he didn’t even spare it a glance, his gaze and focus remaining on Chief Mu like a predator locked onto its prey. Chief Mu sneered after seeing Yuan’s calm demeanor and said, “Alright, enough playing around. I’ll get serious now.” After saying that, Celestial Qi surged within her body.

“Thank god. I was beginning to think that was all you had as a Seventh-Level God Ascension powerhouse,” Yuan said sarcastically. “I suppose it’s only fair I get serious, too.” As his words fell, Yuan’s aura erupted violently, so fierce it momentarily split apart the dark clouds above.

“He wasn’t going all out this entire time?!” Zhuang Maojiang exclaimed in a terrified voice. “What an ominous aura he has! Is he even human?!” The barbarians subconsciously stepped back after sensing Yuan’s Chaotic Essence. However, Yuan wasn’t done yet. The very next moment, Celestial Qi began gathering around his body, boosting his aura even more.

“You can use Celestial Qi?!” Chief Mu couldn’t contain her surprise and cried out loud.

“It’s only Celestial Qi. What are you so surprised about?” Yuan calmly responded.

“There’s no way he’s using Celestial Qi as a Golden Immortal! He must be hiding his true cultivation! It’s no wonder he could match Chief Mu! Everything makes sense now!” The barbarians said, convinced that Yuan was just suppressing his cultivation.

“No, that’s his real cultivation.” The barbarian wrapped in fur robes remarked.

“What? How is that possible?” The barbarians looked at her with puzzled faces. “If he’s really just an Immortal… How is his body able to endure it?”

“I…” she opened her mouth only to instantly close it back up before silently shaking her head, as she was just as puzzled as the rest of them.

Suddenly, another voice rang out, “Heaven Refining Physique. That’s how he’s doing it.” At those words, everyone turned in shock. An old woman had appeared among them without a sound, as if she were a ghost.

“G-Gramny Mu!” The barbarians stared at her with wide eyes.

“Grandmother, what are you doing here? Weren’t you in seclusion?” the fur-robed barbarian asked. Gramny Mu glanced at Yuan and Chief Mu before replying, “With this kind of commotion, who could possibly concentrate? If I forced myself to continue, I’d only end up injured.”

Lady Chen and the others swallowed nervously at the old woman’s presence. Though no aura radiated from her, they could tell she was anything but ordinary-perhaps even stronger than Chief Mu herself.

“By the way, what was that you just mentioned? Heaven Refining Physique?” The fur-robed barbarian continued to ask.

Granny Mu stared at Yuan’s figure for a moment before responding, “It’s a one-of-a-kind physique, so only one of them can exist at any given time. The last person to possess it stood uncontested at the peak of the cultivation world. To be honest, even I do not know its full capabilities, but I can say this much- back then, its wielder was unrivaled.”

“In that case, shouldn’t we stop my mother before she kills him? It’d be such a waste to kill someone with such a rare and powerful physique.”

Granny Mu burst out laughing at her words and quickly said, “If he’s who I think he is, your mother won’t be able to kill him.” The barbarians stared at her with their eyes filled with disbelief, wondering if she knew Yuan. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2160: Granny Mu
chapters.body:
Chapter 2160: Granny Mu

Hearing such words, the fur-robed barbarian asked what was on everyone’s mind, “Grandmother… you know him?”

“Hard to say,” she calmly responded. “He looks exactly like someone once knew, but his cultivation is much lower, and there’s something different about him. That said, the one I knew disappeared millions of years ago, but with his cultivation, I wouldn’t be surprised if he is still alive.”

“Is this person the same one you would constantly reminisce about to Mother and me?” the fur-robed barbarian asked.

Granny Mu responded with a silent nod of her head.

In the next instant, Chief Mu delivered her eighth blow, merging her martial technique with Celestial Qi to unleash a strike of devastating force. Yuan, however, met it head-on, unflinching. An exhilarated smile spread across his face. It had been ages since he’d been able to fight with his full strength, and the thrill of it set his blood ablaze.

Right before the clash, Granny Mu released her Celestial Qi, enveloping the barbarian settlement to shield it from the impending blast. Just as Granny Mu raised the barrier, a violent ripple tore through the land. Everything within ten thousand miles was erased, save for what her protection covered. Their clash not only reshaped White Hell’s landscape but also left behind a massive crater.

However, moments after the cataclysm, the broken terrain began to mend on its own. This was no miracle, but a natural occurrence in places where spiritual energy condensed so heavily that even the land restored itself, some faster than others, depending on the area.

After the clash, Yuan slowly wiped his face with his hand, only to see a faint smear of blood. It was the first time he had been injured-but the wound was nothing more than a shallow cut, so insignificant it barely counted as an injury at all.

He turned to Chief Mu, who looked unscathed. Though her body bore no wounds, the shock of Yuan’s unfathomable existence had damaged her mind.

Meanwhile, the dark clouds above swelled outward, their shade deepening into an even heavier darkness.

“Maybe we should’ve taken this fight somewhere else—a place where we cannot be disturbed,” Chief Mu spoke as she looked at the dark clouds with a slight frown on her face.

Yuan smiled and said, “What are you scared of? It’s just some dark clouds. It’ll go away sooner or later.”

Chief Mu narrowed her eyes. “Just some dark clouds? Don’t tell me you’ve never faced a Heavenly Tribulation before.”

“On the contrary,” Yuan replied calmly. “I’ve experienced them several times.”

Chief Mu wasn’t surprised to hear that Yuan had already experienced several Heavenly Tribulations because of how heaven-defying his existence was.

“Anyway, enough yapping and give me your ninth strike,” Yuan said a moment later. “I don’t have too much time to waste here.”

Chief Mu’s eyebrows twitched, and she spoke in an annoyed tone, “You’re so certain that you will endure all ten of my strikes, huh? Unfortunately for you, that won’t happen!” Suddenly, Chief Mu released a chilling aura that made Yuan’s skin crawl. This cold was unlike the frigid air of White Hell-it was something else, something otherworldly. <Your Cold Resistance has drastically increased!>

Yuan blinked in surprise at the notification. Chief Mu’s chilling aura was so intense that merely standing near her gave him the same result as enduring the seventh region of White Hell. ‘Is this the secret to how they can live within White Hell?” he wondered inwardly, his curiosity piqued.

Chief Mu then spoke with a confident smile on her face, “Forget about the tenth strike. If you survive this ninth attack, I’ll consider it your win.”

Yuan nodded, “If you say so.”

This time, Chief Mu took her time preparing for the next strike. Her cold aura steadily intensified, soon surpassing even the seventh region’s frost. It grew so dense and concentrated that it condensed into a semi-transparent armor around her body.

Yuan briefly considered taking the attack head-on to temper his body, but quickly dismissed the thought. The cold radiating from her felt potent enough to freeze even his soul.

Realizing what Chief Mu was trying to do, Granny Mu hastily said to the others, “Get as far away as you can from here.” Without hesitation, all of the barbarians turned to flee in the opposite direction from the battlefield.

“W-Wait! What about us?!” Lady Chen exclaimed.

“And who the hell are you?” Granny Mu looked at them with raised eyebrows.

The fur-robed barbarian proceeded to explain their situation to her.

“I see. Then you will remain where you stand. If you can survive the aftermath, I may consider letting you go,” Granny Mu said, a cold smile curling her lips.

“No way…” Lady Chen and the others collapsed to their knees, their eyes fixed on Yuan and Chief Mu. As Chief Mu’s aura swelled in preparation for her ninth strike, Yuan braced himself. His Chaotic Essence surged to its fullest, and he summoned forth the True Primordial Fire. Flames of primordial origin erupted around him, engulfing his figure until he stood encased in an infernal armor, radiating heat that defied the very cold closing in on him.

On top of that, Yuan brought out all of his treasures, such as the Robes of Destruction and the Soul Emperor’s Crown-treasures he had decided to wear only when he was fighting in earnest. After all, Yuan was already overwhelmingly strong without relying on treasures. If he wore them all the time, he would feel no excitement in battle, and worse, it would only hinder his training. ɴᴇᴡ ɴᴏᴠᴇʟ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀʀᴇ ᴘᴜʙʟɪsʜᴇᴅ ᴏɴ novel{f}ire.net

Gu Ruyan and the others stared in disbelief, mouths agape, as Yuan covered himself in treasures of immense power. Until this moment, they had been certain he was already fighting with the aid of treasures to overcome their differences in cultivation, but seeing him summon them now proved how wrong they had been.

As though the treasures weren’t enough, Yuan also activated God of War’s Astral Arts, wrapping himself in yet another layer of protection.

Chief Mu also completed her preparations at the same time. She looked at Yuan and muttered, “Though it was brief, I had fun. Unfortunately, it’s time for you to die now.” (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2161: White Hell Barbarian Tribe
chapters.body:
Chapter 2161: White Hell Barbarian Tribe

“It’s time for me to die?” Yuan spoke with a smile on his face. “Who are you to decide when I die?”

“Chief of the White Hell Barbarian Tribe, a seventh-level God Ascension cultivator, that’s who I am,” Chief Mu calmly responded, her aura continuously increasing with every passing moment.

“Then allow me to apologize in advance, since I will be disappointing you.”

“Arrogant even in the last few moments of your life, huh?” Chief Mu sneered.

“And you continue to underestimate me even after all this time? That will be your downfall.”

“Enough talking. You can die now.”

Chief Mu’s aura abruptly stilled for a heartbeat before erupting outward like a nuke. In the same instant, she shot forward like a meteor, her voice ringing out, “Shiva’s Glacial Sunder!”

Yuan’s smile vanished, replaced by a grave expression as his aura shifted, becoming something entirely otherworldly.

“Ahhh… It’s truly him…” Granny Mu fell to her knees after sensing this otherworldly aura coming from Yuan.

A portion of Yuan’s aura suddenly turned golden before wrapping itself around Number One Under Heaven. The golden aura radiated such unfathomable might that Chief Mu’s heart trembled, a primal sense of danger gnawing at her soul to escape. But the moment had already passed, and there was no turning back now.

Wreathed in a golden aura, Yuan swung Number One Under Heaven, releasing a single golden sword arc forged from Supreme Sword Aura and something otherworldly. Not only was his Supreme Sword Aura enhanced by Number One Under Heaven’s ability, but the golden aura strengthened it even further, and it flew toward Chief Mu with unstoppable force.

Chief Mu gritted her teeth and swung her axe, unleashing all the energy she had gathered. It erupted like a massive fireball-yet it was white, and so cold that it froze the very space around it, leaving a trail of ice in its wake.

Chief Mu braced for their energies to explode on impact, but to her shock, Yuan’s Supreme Sword Aura cleaved straight through her attack and passed on, severing both of her arms in a single stroke.

Once cleaved apart, the condensed energy of Chief Mu’s attack lost all control and erupted, unleashing a catastrophic explosion that instantly consumed and froze half of White Hell, the frost spreading as far as the fourth region.

Right before the explosion occurred, Granny Mu shielded the fur-robed barbarian behind her, as well as Yaoqin. As for Gu Ruyan and the other two, they hastily retrieved all of the life-saving treasures from their spatial ring and activated them without hesitation.

For several minutes, White Hell-the coldest place in the Nine Heavens-froze in its entirety for the first time. Not only the mountains and land, but even the air and space themselves turned to ice. From the outside, White Hell appeared as nothing more than a colossal block of frozen crystal. Of course, this frozen state didn’t last for long, and within minutes, it began to melt.

<Your Cold Resistance has improved drastically>

<Your skill ‘Ice Harmony’ has developed into ‘True Ice Harmony’> Yuan, who was the closest to the explosion, had managed to upgrade his Ice Harmony to the next level within those few minutes, a testament to how powerful Chief Mu’s attack was.

When the ice finally melted and White Hell returned to normal, Chief Mu stared at Yuan in utter disbelief, unable to comprehend what had just transpired. “He cut through… my most powerful attack? And he is still alive after eating that much energy? There’s no way…” she muttered in a daze.

Though Yuan had survived Chief Mu’s martial technique, the effort drained him so greatly that he lost control of his body and plummeted from the sky. Though consciousness never left him, and his exhausted body regenerated almost instantly, Yuan could not move a muscle. His paralysis stemmed not from the exhaustion but from the crushing burden of the golden aura he had dared to wield. Indeed, Yuan had managed to invoke Heaven’s Supremacy without fully completing his quest, though he could only summon enough of its power to enhance his Supreme Sword Aura once.

‘Looks like I managed to use Heavenly Supremacy, even if only a little,’ Yuan thought with an inward smile, satisfied with the result. More importantly, he realized something-that he didn’t need to complete the quest to wield it. After all, would he truly be worthy of Heaven’s Supremacy if he relied on the system to wield it?

Just as Yuan was about to crash into the ground, a gentle energy wrapped around him, slowing his descent and placing his paralyzed body softly upon the earth. Of course, even without the aid, a simple fall would not have harmed him, as his physique was strong enough to withstand even powerful treasures.

Granny Mu walked up to the motionless Yuan, looked down at him, and said, “You’re still as crazy as ever, aren’t you?”

Though he was unable to move his body, Yuan was able to make facial expressions and move his lips. He raised an eyebrow and asked as he stared at Granny Mu’s unfamiliar face, “Who are you?”

“Hm? You don’t remember me?” Granny Mu said. She continued, “I guess my appearance has changed a lot since we last met. Mu Hanyan, does that name ring any bells for you?”

“Sorry, it doesn’t…”

Granny Mu narrowed her eyes slightly, and she began doubting whether Yuan was truly the one she knew. “That feeling his golden aura emitted… there’s no mistaking it, but what if someone else besides him could use it?”

After a moment of silence, Granny Mu asked, “What is your name?”

“Yuan.”

“Are you sure it’s not Tian Yingzhe?” she asked with a skeptical gaze.

Yuan’s eyes widened slightly upon hearing this familiar name. Granny Mu could tell that Yuan recognized the name but didn’t say anything and patiently waited for his response.

However, before he could do so, Chief Mu descended from the sky and landed right before him. “What the hell was that golden aura?!” She shouted as she wrapped her spiritual energy around him and pulled him off the ground. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)
