chapters.title: Chapter 2162: Let Them Go
chapters.body:
Chapter 2162: Let Them Go

“Does it really matter what it was? I survived your ninth strike, so I win our little game,” Yuan responded with a calm smile on his face.

“I’m gonna kill you, you damn freak!” Chief Mu angrily shouted, but she was not emitting any killing intent.

“That’s enough.” Granny Mu suddenly said. “Put him down.”

“H-Huh? Mother? How long have you been there?” Chief Mu was startled to see Granny Mu. She had been so focused on Yuan that she didn’t even realize Granny Mu’s presence.

“Long enough to watch the entire thing. You’ve lost spectacularly, my foolish daughter.”

“T-That’s-!” Chief Mu cut herself off, grinding her teeth in frustration. Never had she imagined being unable to kill a mere Immortal even after exerting her full might. Though the Nine Heavens greatly restricted her power, the gap in their cultivation should’ve allowed her to win with ease, and this humiliation would surely linger and haunt her forever.

After a moment of silence, Chief Mu released Yuan. She intended to drop him onto the ground like trash, but Granny Mu prevented his fall a second time with her spiritual energy.

“Why are you being so gentle with him? He won’t die even if you crush him with a mountain,” Chief Mu couldn’t help but ask, a slight frown on her face.

Granny Mu ignored her and asked Yuan again, “Are you sure your name isn’t Tian Yingzhe?”

“It’s a bit complicated,” Yuan responded.

“So you are Tian Yingzhe,” she said in a confident tone. “Since you don’t remember me, you must have amnesia…”

“Something like that.”

Granny Mu sighed, “To think that after millions of years, our reunion would be in such a fashion.”

“Wait… you know him?” Chief Mu asked, her eyes widening.

“I do. But you’ve no time to worry about my affairs,” Granny Mu replied, tilting her gaze toward the heavens. The dark clouds their clash had scattered had already gathered once more this time, heavier and angrier than before.

“Oh… I completely forgot about that…” Chief Mu sighed.

As a seventh-level God Ascension cultivator, Chief Mu normally wouldn’t worry about this level of Heaven’s Judgment. However, she had exhausted most of her energy trying to kill Yuan, and she’d even lost both of her arms.

“I’ll deal with it,” Yuan suddenly said.

“What? How the hell are you going to do that when you can’t even move?” Chief Mu said while glaring at him.

Yuan looked at Granny Mu and said, “You can put me down now. I have recovered enough to move my body.”

After Granny Mu set him down, Yuan soared into the sky, heading straight for the rumbling black clouds. “We both know you can’t harm me,” he said, his mocking smile fixed on the heavens. “So stop delaying and get this over with.”

The heavens roared in fury. In the very next instant, judgment descended-not a single bolt, but two. One bore down on Yuan as his rightful punishment. The other, meant for Chief Mu, veered unnaturally, drawn into Yuan’s body as though he had seized her punishment for himself.

Naturally, Heaven’s Judgment had no effect on Yuan. If anything, it only tempered his body further. What’s more, since one of the strikes had been meant for Chief Mu-a Seventh-Level God Ascension cultivator-it carried far greater power than his own judgment. Updates are released by NoveI(F)ire.net

“Hahaha! That’s it! Keep up the good work!” Yuan laughed out loud.

“That’s insane… He not only possesses Ice Harmony, but also a Heavenly Physique that can withstand Heaven’s Judgment?” Chief Mu muttered, swallowing nervously. Even at her peak, uninjured and with her full energy, she would not be able to face Heaven’s Judgment as effortlessly as Yuan.

A few minutes later, the judgment ceased, and the dark clouds scattered almost instantly as the lightning subsided.

Once Heaven’s Judgment ended and Yuan returned to the ground, he looked at Chief Mu and spoke, “So, as promised, you’ll let me and my friend live, right?”

Chief Mu sneered, “Whatever.” Although she didn’t want to admit it, she realized that she wouldn’t be able to kill Yuan even if she wanted to.

“What about them?” Yuan suddenly turned to look at Gu Ruyan and the others. Yaoqin was completely unhindered thanks to Granny Mu, but the other three had also somehow managed to survive barely with the help of their life-saving treasures.

Before Chief Mu could respond, Granny Mu said, “I said that I would consider letting them go if they could survive the aftermath.”

“What?! But they killed one of our people! You can’t let them go!” Chief Mu exclaimed.

Granny Mu turned to look at Yuan and asked him, “What do you think? Should I let them go or…?”

“P-Please! Let us go!” Lady Chen cried. “I swear I’ll never trouble you again!”

“Me too!” The other two also shouted.

Yuan’s lips curved into a gentle smile as he said, “Let them go.”

“What? You can’t be serious!” Chief Mu stared at him in disbelief.

“If you say so,” Granny Mu replied with a calm nod.

But before Gu Ruyan and the others could breathe a sigh of relief, Yuan continued, his smile turning colder, “After all, I want to kill them with my own hands.”

“What?!” Without another word, Yuan charged at them.

Gu Ruyan and the other two hastily readied themselves to face Yuan. But Yuan no longer toyed with them, and he fought as though he was still battling Chief Mu. The three resisted with all they had, yet before Yuan’s overwhelming power, they were utterly helpless.

In just a few moments, Yuan had destroyed all three of their physical bodies and seized their souls.

“Please! Spare us!” Gu Ruyan cried.

“Have mercy!” Zhuang Maojiang shouted.

“I-I will do anything you want! I will be your slave or whatever! Just let me live!” Lady Chen pleaded. However, Yuan ignored them and struck their souls with his Supreme Sword Aura, instantly cutting their souls in half and killing them for good.

“As ruthless as ever:” Granny Mu said with a smile. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2163: Mu Hanyan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2163: Mu Hanyan

While Yuan dealt with Gu Ruyan and the others, Chief Mu consumed a recovery pill that restored her severed arms and exhausted energy. Unfortunately, there was no pill that could erase the humiliation she felt from losing to Yuan, nor was there anything she could do about it since she had already given him her word that she wouldn’t touch him if he won, not to mention her mother seemed to know him. Chief Mu may be the leader of the White Hell Barbarian Tribe, but her mother, Granny Mu, the leader before her, held more power and authority.

“Mother, who is that man?” Chief Mu asked.

A bittersweet smile appeared on her face as she responded, “Your father…”

“WHAT?!” Chief Mu exclaimed.

“At least that was my dream at one point in my life,” Granny Mu continued.

“D-Don’t ever do that again, mother… It’s really bad for the heart… and soul.”

Once Yuan was finished with the members of the Legacy Families, he returned to Yaoqin’s side and asked her, “Are you okay?”

She nodded in a daze, “Yes… thank you…”

After pondering for a moment, she retrieved a glowing blue flower from her spatial ring and extended her arms, offering it to Yuan. “This is the White Hell Frost Lotus. I’ll let you have it for saving my life,” she said. Yuan glanced at the lotus and reached for it. However, instead of taking the treasure, he pushed it back toward her and said, “You risked your life for it, so keep it. While it’s a decent treasure, it won’t benefit me right now.” Had she given it to him before he acquired True Cold Harmony, it would’ve definitely helped a lot.

Yaoqin stared at him with a flushed face, and she silently nodded her head before stowing the treasure back inside her spatial ring.

“Why don’t we have a private chat now that everything’s calmed down?” Granny Mu said as she approached them.

Yuan nodded, “Sure.”

Before turning around and walking away, Granny Mu glanced at Yaoqin before throwing a necklace in her direction and said, “Wear it. It’ll keep you warm in White Hell up to the eighth region.”

“T-Thank you, Senior!” Yaoqin hastily bowed to her. The moment she wore the necklace, all of the coldness magically disappeared, something even the Sun Stone could not achieve.

Sometime later, Yuan followed Granny Mu into one of the buildings at the settlement.

Once seated, Granny Mu asked, “So, what brought you to White Hell?”

“Before that, can you tell me what your relationship with Tian Yingzhe was?” Yuan asked.

“You’re speaking as if you’re not Tian Yingzhe.” ʀᴇᴀᴅ ʟᴀᴛᴇsᴛ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀᴛ novel_fіre.net

“I will explain it once you answer my question.”

Granny Mu sighed. Suddenly, she retrieved an appearance-altering pill and consumed it, transforming from her elderly appearance to that of a beautiful teenager with white hair and ice-blue eyes. Seeing Yuan’s lack of response, she sighed again.

“So, you truly don’t remember anything. Then let me start from the beginning,” said Mu Hanyan. “Since ancient times, women have led the White Hell Barbarian Tribe. To preserve this tradition, each leader leaves White Hell to seek out a partner, receiving his talents before returning home and raising the child alone. Since I was the last Chief’s daughter, I was destined to be the next leader, so I left White Hell to find a suitable partner when I was of age, and that’s where I met you-Tian Yingzhe. He was outstanding and the most talented individual I have ever seen, so I decided to bring his talents to White Hell. Unfortunately, Tian Yingzhe did not seem to like the idea of me going after him merely for his genes, so he refused. However, even though he already refused my request, I still decided to follow him, and I was even prepared to never return to White Hell for him. As you can see, that did not happen… mostly because Tian Yingzhe vanished like a ghost.”

Yuan had many questions after hearing her story, but he couldn’t help but ask this one, “What if the Chief’s child turns out to be male?”

“That’s not possible. We can only give birth to females.”

“Huh? But what about the other barbarians? There were many men in the tribe.”

“We may be from the same tribe, but the leaders have a different bloodline, and our bloodline only gives birth to females,” she calmly explained.

After a moment of silence, Mu Hanyan spoke, “Now that I have explained my relationship with Tian Yingzhe, shouldn’t it be your turn to explain? If you are not Tian Yingzhe, who the hell are you?” From her tone and the look in her eyes, Yuan could tell Mu Hanyan wasn’t lying-that she truly cared about Tian Yingzhe.

“I am Tian Yingzhe and not at the same time,” Yuan said. “I died and reincarnated.” He gave a simple yet clear explanation.

The room fell silent again.

“How’d you die?” Mu Hanyan asked.

“I don’t remember. I have yet to recall all of my memories, hence why I don’t have memories of you, either.”

“Makes sense.” Mu Hanyan sighed.

“Well, now that you’re back, why don’t you fulfill my old request and give me your seeds?”

Yuan raised his eyebrows, but before he could respond, she continued, “I’m just joking. We are only allowed to have one child.”

“Anyway, what are you doing in White Hell?” she then asked.

“I’m here to train my cold immunity. I have achieved True Cold Harmony thanks to your daughter, but it seems like it can still be improved on.”

Mu Hanyan stared at him with wide eyes, and she asked him after a moment of stunned silence, “How long has it been since your reincarnation?”

“I stopped paying attention long ago, but it hasn’t been a hundred years yet,” he calmly answered. “Not even a hundred years ago?! And you’ve already achieved True Cold Harmony?! You’re as ridiculous -if not even more ridiculous than before!” she exclaimed. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2164: Shiva'S Hell
chapters.body:
Chapter 2164: Shiva'S Hell

“You know, I’ve only just achieved True Cold Harmony after cultivating for tens of millions of years…” Mu Hanyan sighed. “Unfortunately, I might not be able to achieve the next realm so quickly.”

Hearing his words, Mu Hanyan fell silent for a moment before speaking, “If you want fast progress, I may know a place where you can further improve your Cold Harmony.”

“Really?” Yuan’s eyes lit up with excitement. “Where is it?”

“It’s where I was training before I was disturbed by your appearance,” she said. “That said, it’s not a place accessible by strangers. In fact, it’s only accessible by the Chiefs of our tribe. Despite our relationship, I cannot let you enter it so easily.”

Yuan smiled and replied, “Since you brought it up, then there’s a way for me to train there, right? Otherwise, why even bother telling me about it?”

“You’re right. There is a way.” Mu Hanyan leaned forward and continued with a mysterious smile, “If you agree to fulfill one of my requests, I’ll let you train in there. Considering that you’ve achieved True Cold Harmony in such a short time, I’m willing to wager that you’ll achieve the next realm in another fifty years.”

“What’s the request?” Yuan asked.

“I’ll tell you later, but you must make a decision now. Don’t worry, it won’t be anything unreasonable,” she said, reassuring him.

“You’re not going to tell me to impregnate your granddaughter, are you?” Yuan looked at her with a skeptical look. “If so, I’ll have to decline.”

Mu Hanyan stared at him with wide eyes and a dumbfounded expression. “Hahaha!” she suddenly burst out laughing. “So you caught on, huh? Fine, I won’t ask you to impregnate anyone.”

“…” Yuan was speechless.

“But you still won’t tell me your request now?”

“Nope.”

Yuan sighed, falling silent as he pondered her offer. He had no idea what kind of place Mu Hanyan referred to, but one thing was clear-if he wished to evolve his physique, he first needed to advance his True Cold Harmony. And if he pursued it through normal means, it would take him at least a hundred years. Moreover, even though Mu Hanyan predicted that he could achieve it within 50 years, he had a feeling that he would be able to do so even faster than her prediction.

After a long moment of silence, Yuan nodded his head in a defeated manner, “Alright, you win. I’ll accept your request as long as it doesn’t involve me impregnating anyone.”

“Then it’s a deal.”

Sometime later, Yuan and Mu Hanyan left the building.

When she saw Yuan leaving, Yaoqin, who had been waiting outside, approached him and asked, “If you don’t mind me asking, how much longer do you intend to stay in White Hell? If possible, I’d like to stay with you until you leave.”

“I can’t give you an exact time, but I plan to train here for several more years,” Yuan said. “I don’t mind if you wait, but you won’t be able to follow me to where I’ll be going.”

“I see…” Yaoqin fell quiet for a moment before nodding. “Then I’ll keep training here-if they don’t mind, and if I finish before you return, I’ll leave on my own. I plan to consume the White Hell Frost Lotus, so it’ll take several years.”

Yuan looked at Mu Hanyan and asked, “You wouldn’t mind letting her train here for a bit, do you?”

“Who is she to you, anyway?” she asked, staring at Yaoqin with slightly narrowed eyes.

“She was in trouble, chased by three I killed, and I helped her.” Thɪs chapter is updatᴇd by novel·fıre·net

“So you’re her savior, huh? Why am I not surprised? You’ve always gone out of your way to help those in need. Whatever. As long as she doesn’t cause any trouble, I don’t mind,” Mu Hanyan said.

“Thank you.”

Yaoqin bowed to her, “Thank you, Senior. I promise I won’t cause any trouble. I will also return the necklace when I am done.”

Mu Hanyan pointed at a certain building and said, “You can use that place to train.”

Yaoqin didn’t linger and immediately went inside the building to begin her cultivation. “When do you want to start your training?” Mu Hanyan asked Yuan shortly later. “Right now.”

She nodded and said, “Follow me.”

Chief Mu noticed where Mu Hanyan was bringing Yuan and blocked them, “W-Wait a second! Mother, don’t tell me you’re going to let a stranger enter that place, which is only meant for us?! That’s against our tradition!”

“Nothing is eternal. It’s for a good cause,” she calmly replied.

“That doesn’t make any sense!”

Mu Hanyan narrowed her eyes at Chief Mu and asked in a cold voice, “Are you questioning my decision? You must think you’re above me now after taking my position a mere thousand years ago, huh?”

“That’s…” Chief Mu clenched her fists in frustration, but she held her tongue. Even as the tribe’s chief, her fear of her mother was still deeply rooted in her heart.

“Also, he’s not the only one I am going to break tradition for,” Mu Hanyan suddenly continued.

“What do you mean?” Chief Mu questioned with a puzzled face.

Mu Hanyan turned to look at the fur-robed figure in the distance and beckoned her. “Come over here, Xuelian.”

“You don’t mean…” Chief Mu swallowed nervously.

“What is it, grandmother?”

“You’re on the verge of reaching Cold Harmony, correct? Even though you’re not the Chief yet, I’ll make an exception and allow you special permission to enter Shiva’s Hell.”

“Really?!” the fur-robed woman cried out, her tone overflowing with joy and disbelief.

Mu Hanyan nodded and turned to face Yuan. “If I am going to make exceptions, I might as well do it for two people. Also, it’s a pain in the ass to open the training area, so you’ll be training with my granddaughter, Mu Xuelian.”

“I don’t mind.” Yuan calmly nodded.

“Follow me.” Mu Hanyan began flying toward the eighth region with Yuan and Mu Xuelian following behind. Although Chief Mu no longer stopped them, she still decided to follow them. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2165: Shiva (1)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2165: Shiva (1)

After leaving the White Hell Barbarian Tribe’s settlement, Mu Hanyan brought Yuan and Mu Xuelian into the eighth region. Of course, Chief Mu followed behind.

“How are you feeling? Can you handle the cold here?” Mu Hanyan asked, her head turned toward Yuan.

“It’s a bit chilly, but it feels good,” he calmly nodded. After achieving True Cold Harmony, the seventh region’s chill no longer fazed him. To Yuan, the once-lethal cold now felt like nothing more than a refreshing breeze against his skin. As for the eighth region, it felt no more than a morning chill at the end of summer.

“Good. Because we’ll be heading to the core of White Hell-Shiva’s Heart,” said Mu Hanyan.

Hearing the name Shiva twice now, Yuan couldn’t help but ask, “What’s with this Shiva? This is the second time you’ve mentioned this name now.”

“It’s the name of our guardian spirit, or something along that line. They’ve been protecting our tribe ever since we moved into White Hell. No, you can even say that it’s because of Shiva that we can live within White Hell,” she explained.

“A guardian spirit? So it’s a beast?” Yuan asked, raising an eyebrow.

“I don’t know. That’s just what our ancestors called them—though no one has ever actually seen them,” Mu Hanyan admitted with a shake of her head.

“What? You’ve never even seen them? Then how do you communicate with them-or even know they exist?” Yuan pressed.

“Oh, we communicate,” she said firmly. “Though we’ve never seen them, they speak with us through voice transmissions.”

A doubtful look appeared on Yuan’s face as he said, “Sorry if this offends you, but… are you sure it’s not a scam?” Follow current ɴᴏᴠᴇʟs on NoveI(F)ire.net

“H-How dare you insult our guardian spirit!” Chief Mu angrily shouted. “I’ll rip your tongue out!”

Mu Hanyan burst into laughter. “I knew you’d say that. But they’ve been with us since ancient times, and in all that time, they’ve never asked for anything. On the contrary, they’ve granted us powerful cultivation techniques and unique physiques. Why would a deceiver give so much, and for so long?”

“I guess it doesn’t make sense…” Yuan mumbled.

Some time later, they reached the end of the eighth region and stood before the entrance to the ninth. “What… is this place?” Yuan muttered, swallowing nervously. This was nothing like the snowy wasteland he expected of White Hell. Before him stretched not ice or snow, but a field of white and blue flowers, and the ninth region was so small that Yuan could see its far edge clearly with his divine sense.

“Are we still in White Hell?” Yuan had to ask.

Mu Hanyan smiled and said, “Don’t let the peaceful and warm appearance fool you. If you don’t have a special physique-my physique, not even a Cultivation God could linger in this place without being frozen.”

“Don’t tell me this is the training area?” Yuan remarked.

“No, this isn’t the training ground. Do you see that glacial tree in the center?” Mu Hanyan asked, her gaze fixed on the massive, crystal-like tree that stood there, resembling a towering structure of pure ice.

Yuan nodded. “I do. It’s impossible to miss.”

“Your destination, Shiva’s Hell, is there.”

Yuan used his divine sense to look around the crystal tree, and sure enough, there was a sealed gate on the ground right before it that seemed to lead underground.

Yuan’s eyes narrowed slightly upon seeing this gate, almost as if he recognized it.

“So, how am I supposed to get there when even a Cultivation God isn’t safe in there?” Yuan asked a moment later.

“That’s why I brought my granddaughter here with you,” said Mu Hanyan as she turned to look at Mu Xuelian, who was calmly standing beside her. “There’s a reason why only the leader of the White Hell Barbarian Tribe is allowed entry-because only we have the physique capable of entering.”

“That doesn’t explain anything about my predicament,” Yuan said.

“It’s quite simple. You just need to enter while holding hands with my granddaughter.”

Yuan became speechless. After a moment of silence, he spoke, “You serious?”

Mu Hanyan stared at his face for another moment before responding in a serious tone, “No, I am just joking.” Before Yuan could even respond, she retrieved a necklace that was similar to the one she gave Yaoqin and said, “You can enter as long as you’re wearing this.”

“…” Yuan accepted the necklace in silence, but he couldn’t help but wonder if she always had such a character.

“Alright, let’s go.” The next moment, Mu Hanyan led the way and entered the ninth region. Mu Xuelian and Chief Mu followed her without hesitation.

The moment Yuan stepped into the ninth region, a chilling sensation coursed through him, making his entire body tremble. ‘What is this feeling…’ He couldn’t describe it. It was cold and hot at once, and the chill itself felt different-unnatural, otherworldly. On top of that, his instincts screamed danger, warning him not to take another step forward. However, Yuan gritted his teeth and endured the sensation as he forced himself to proceed.

When they reached the sealed gate, Mu Hanyan suddenly produced a dagger whose blade looked as though it had been forged from the same crystal as the tree. Without hesitation, she drove it into her palm, the blade piercing clean through her hand. She then scattered her blood across the sealed entrance. Moments later, the barrier shifted and parted, revealing a staircase descending into the abyss below.

“Go ahead. We won’t follow you inside,” Mu Hanyan said.

Mu Xuelian immediately began descending the staircase, and Yuan followed behind. Once their figures disappeared into the darkness, the entrance sealed itself again.

“Mother… can you tell me the real reason you allowed him to enter Shiva’s Hell?” Chief Mu suddenly asked as she stared at Mu Hanyan. “Don’t even bother lying to me. I know you better than anyone else in this world.”

After a moment of silence, Mu Hanyan responded in a calm voice, “You’re right. It wasn’t our relationship that made me break our tradition. It was Shiva’s instruction to bring him here.”

“W-What did you say?!” Chief Mu exclaimed, her eyes wide with shock. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2166: The Heart Of Shiva
chapters.body:
Chapter 2166: The Heart Of Shiva

“Say that again!” Chief Mu demanded, not daring to believe her ears just now.

Mu Hanyan turned to look at her and calmly repeated, “I brought him here because Shiva instructed me to.”

“N-No way…” Chief Mu’s jaw dropped. “When did that happen? When did Shiva contact you?”

“It happened right after your clash with him ended,” she replied. “Bring me the man with the golden aura -that was Shiva’s exact instruction, word for word.”

“Seriously…” Chief Mu sighed out loud. “Why didn’t you tell me something this important beforehand?” “What’s the point? If Shiva wanted you to know, they would’ve contacted you.”

“Wait… then what about my daughter? Did Shiva also ask for her?”

Mu Hanyan nodded.

“Did Shiva tell you why they wanted to see them?”

“No.”

Chief Mu clenched her fists and muttered, “I don’t care what happens to that man, but if anything happens to my little girl…”

“Relax a bit. I doubt Shiva would harm them, especially Xuelian.”

“I know Shiva’s never harmed us before, but I cannot help but have a bad feeling about this event. It’s too sudden.”

“Shiva is an existence beyond our comprehension. There’s no point trying to understand them or predict their goals.”

Mu Hanyan turned to stare at the crystal tree with a profound gaze. “The last time Shiva contacted us, it was to instruct me to leave White Hell and find a partner… And now, after millions of years, just as I reunite with Tian Yingzhe, they reach out again. This… this cannot be a coincidence…” she thought inwardly. ‘Oh, Tian Yingzhe… what in heaven’s name are you, truly?’

Meanwhile, after many minutes of descending, Yuan and Mu Xuelian still haven’t reached the end.

“What’s your relationship with my grandmother?” Mu Xuelian suddenly started a conversation.

“We used to travel together, I guess.”

“You guess? What’s that supposed to mean?”

“Well, I don’t have all of my memories, and it was your grandmother who said that we used to travel together.”

“Amnesia, huh? If you’ve traveled with my grandmother, you must be quite old.”

“Yes and no. It’s complicated. Anyway, why don’t you tell me a little about yourself, too?”

“There’s nothing interesting about me. I have never left White Hell, and if I am not spending my time cultivating my physique, I am training with my mother and grandmother.”

“What kind of physique do you have that allows you to live within a place like White Hell?” Yuan then asked.

“Sorry, but I cannot tell you,” she swiftly and firmly rejected.

“Is that so?”

The conversation ended there, and the two of them would not utter another word to each other for a few more hours until they finally reached the bottom of the staircase, where a small, narrow tunnel awaited them.

“This is your first time here, correct?” Yuan asked as they began walking through the tunnel.

“That’s right. Only those who have inherited the position of Chief may enter, and I am nowhere near inheriting my mother’s position.” In order to take over her mother’s position, Mu Xuelian would need to leave White Hell and find herself a talented partner to receive their seeds.

Minutes later, they emerged from the tunnel into a dazzling light. A cavern of surreal beauty unfolded before them, its towering ceiling veined with massive roots of crystal, each faintly aglow as though pulsing with hidden life. At the center, the roots coiled tightly around a colossal block of ice, suspended in midair like a hanging lantern. Beneath the roots, at the cavern’s center, lay a small glowing pond-barely large enough for two people. Its surface shimmered and flickered, as though countless stars dwelled within its depths.

“So, how does training here work?” Yuan asked as Mu Xuelian slowly approached the pond.

She gazed at it for a long moment, as though lost in its beauty, before answering. “According to Mother, we simply need to submerge our bodies in this liquid.”

“Is that all? Are there any special preparations or rules that I need to know before doing so?”

“Not as far as I know,” she shook her head.

Mu Xuelian then circled the pond and continued forward until she came upon a long, thick block of ice carved in the shape of a bed. Standing before it, she showed no hesitation as her hands moved to her fur robes, calmly removing them. She first pulled down her hood, unveiling her long, beautiful white hair. Then she slipped off her robes, letting them fall onto the ice bed.

Yuan’s eyes widened as her flawless, jade-like skin and naked buttocks came into view. To his surprise, Mu Xuelian had worn nothing beneath her fur robes.

“Um…” Yuan parted his lips to speak, but before a word escaped, Mu Xuelian slowly turned around. The cavern’s glow fell across her bare figure, every delicate curve revealed without concealment, her beauty laid bare before his eyes.

Yuan expected Feng Yuxiang’s voice to resound at any moment, yet to his surprise, there was only silence. He instinctively reached out to their connection, only for his brows to twitch as he realized it had been severed. ‘Feng Feng? Yu Ning? Yingying? Can you hear me?’ When nobody responded, a ripple of panic ran through Yuan as he turned his focus inward, peering into his Dantian. Sensing that their presence was still there, he sighed in relief. But for some reason, he could no longer communicate with them.

“Are you done staring?” Mu Xuelian suddenly spoke, snapping him out of his daze.

Yuan quickly realized that he had been unconsciously staring at her and apologized, “Sorry, I didn’t mean to stare.”

“You can continue staring if you want, but I will go ahead and begin my cultivation.” Follow current ɴᴏᴠᴇʟs on nοvelfire.net

Mu Xuelian moved to the pond and paused at its edge. With cautious grace, she lowered her foot, letting her toe touch the shimmering surface. When she confirmed the pond would not harm her, Mu Xuelian slid her foot deeper before stepping fully into the water. She made her way to the edge, saving some space for Yuan, and sat down, her body gradually sinking until she was immersed in the luminous liquid, as though surrendering herself to its embrace. chapters.title: Chapter 2167: Shiva (3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2167: Shiva (3)

Watching Mu Xuelian enter the pond so calmly, Yuan thought it looked comfortable-an odd sight for him. “This place is supposed to temper my Cold Harmony… How could training possibly feel comfortable?” he wondered. After all, the path that had brought Yuan to where he stood today had been paved with hardship. Unless he was consuming a treasure, every method of training he had known was grueling and agonizing, offering not a single breath of peace or comfort. ‘I should start my training, too.’

Yuan snapped out of his thoughts and also began to undress, placing his neatly folded robes on the ice bed. He approached the pond and silently stared at it. Though Mu Xuelian had saved him some space, there was barely enough room for him, and it seemed impossible to train in it together without touching her. “There’s not enough space for both of us, so my back will be touching yours, if you don’t mind,” he said. Of course, they could take turns cultivating inside the pond, but it would take too much time, something Yuan didn’t have.

“I don’t mind,” Mu Xuelian calmly responded.

“Then if you’ll excuse me…” Yuan entered the pond, the glowing liquid rising to embrace his body as he submerged himself. As expected, the space was just enough for the two of them, their backs pressing lightly against each other. The touch of Mu Xuelian’s skin was astonishingly soft and smooth as the finest tofu. Yet Yuan immediately banished the thought, clearing his mind to focus on cultivation. Almost the moment he entered the pond, a bone-chilling cold surged through him, seeping into his core until it felt as though his very organs were encased in ice. ‘I knew it wasn’t as comfortable as it looked!’ Yuan cried inwardly. Yet, it wasn’t anything unbearable. In fact, the pain didn’t compare to his other experiences.

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun refining Shiva’s Essence>

<Your Cold Resistance has improved tremendously>

‘Shiva’s Essence… is that the identity of this liquid?’

A year passed in the blink of an eye. Throughout that time, Yuan and Mu Xuelian remained motionless within the pond, the cavern unchanged save for one thing-a single drop of Shiva’s Essence that fell from the ice above, rippling into the water below. The second drop appeared almost two years later. A single drop of Shiva’s Essence would enter the pond every year, slowly refilling it, but with Yuan and Mu Xuelian absorbing it, the liquid was being exhausted much faster than it could be replenished.

After another year of cultivation, Yuan could already feel his True Cold Harmony reaching the next level, which was incredibly shocking, considering it took him nearly 20 years to achieve True Cold Harmony. However, just as Yuan was about to have a breakthrough, a chilling pressure suddenly filled the room, and he could feel Mu Xuelian’s body trembling nonstop. In the next moment, before Yuan could even ask if she was okay, Mu Xuelian suddenly leaped from the pond, landing directly on the ice bed.

Yuan opened his eyes to see Mu Xuelian seated cross-legged on the bed, completely exposing her little sister. Without warning, luminous blue symbols began to surface on her skin, etching themselves across her body like living tattoos. Not daring to disturb her, Yuan watched in silence as the symbols shifted across her body, eventually converging into a single, massive mark upon her chest. As the symbol glowed brighter, the presence in the cavern grew heavier, clearer-almost as if something otherworldly was descending upon them. “This pressure… It’s the same as back in Han Zexian’s Cavern!” Yuan clenched his fists and prepared to face whatever was about to happen.

In an instant, the glowing symbol on Mu Xuelian’s chest faded from sight. A breath later, her eyes opened, shimmering with a profound blue light that seemed deeper than before. And if one looked closely, they would see that the vanished symbol was now etched within her pupils.

“Who are you?” Yuan spoke first, breaking the silence.

Mu Xuelian didn’t respond and merely stared at him with curiosity. A moment of silence later, she opened her mouth and spoke, but it was in a foreign language. Yet, for some reason, Yuan could understand it. “I am Shiva,” she said, her voice echoing with power. “The one who reigns over all that is cold.”

“Shiva…? So you’re the guardian spirit Mu Hanyan mentioned. What have you done with Mu Xuelian?”

“I am merely possessing her body.”

“Why?” Read complete version only at novel⚑fire.net

“So that I can speak with you, human who wields the power of gods.”

“Power of gods? What are you talking about?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

In response, she extended her arm, raising a finger toward him. At its tip shimmered a faint golden aura. A chill moved down Yuan’s spine, and his eyes widened at the familiar golden aura. “Heaven’s Supremacy?!” he exclaimed, so shocked that he couldn’t remain seated and stood up. Although Shiva’s golden aura couldn’t be compared to Yuan’s, it was definitely Heaven’s Supremacy.

“How… how are you using that power?” Yuan asked, as he always thought it was a power unique to him. Shiva gently blew on her finger, snuffing out the golden aura as though it were a candle. “What a foolish question. You are asking why a god can wield godly power,” she calmly replied.

“I don’t understand. I thought it was some kind of technique,” he admitted.

“A technique?” Shiva’s laughter rang out, sharp and sudden, only to cease just as quickly. Her expression hardened, her tone dropping into icy disdain. “Don’t you dare compare our power to your mortal tricks.”

Yuan clenched his fists tightly before asking another question, “When you say god… are you referring to the Outer Gods? Are you an Outer God?”

“An Outer God, huh? That’s merely how the beings of this world address us, not our true title. That said, it is true that we are gods-true rulers of this universe.” (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2168: Outer Gods' True Identity
chapters.body:
Chapter 2168: Outer Gods' True Identity

“If Outer God isn’t your title, then what is your true one? How should I address your kind from now on?” Yuan asked.

“Your kind…?” Shiva’s eyes narrowed dangerously. “Bold words for a mere human. Were I not powerless in this moment, I would kill you for such disrespect.”

Yuan’s lips curled into a smile. “Oh, I’m terrified,” he mocked.

Shiva said nothing. Her silence was heavy, her displeasure evident in the narrowing of her eyes. Yet for some reason, she held her fury in check, as though restrained by something unseen. “We call ourselves Eternals, as our existence is everlasting.”

“Eternals, huh? So, what does an Eternal like you want from me? You possessed Mu Xuelian’s body just to speak to me, so surely, you want something from me.”

“Indeed, I want something from you.” Shiva spoke as she slowly lifted her head to look at the crystal above him and continued, “Do you see that block of ice above you?”

“What about it?”

“My very essence is sealed within that ice, and I would like you to break that seal and set me free,” she said with a solemn expression.

“You’re sealed?” Yuan raised an eyebrow, and he immediately recalled the cavern he found at the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s territory, where another Eternal was being sealed. “Aren’t you an almighty god? How and who has the power to seal you in this place?”

Shiva fell silent again. “I’ll tell you once you release me,” she eventually said.

“No way.” Yuan immediately declined. “Since you don’t want to tell me anything, allow me to tell you what I think about Eternals and my relationship with them. In my past life, I have fought and defeated Eternals—”

“Hahaha!” Shiva suddenly laughed, interrupting him. “A human defeating Eternals? That’s not possible. Not even your strongest cultivators—a Cultivation God-would be able to touch us, let alone defeat us!”

Yuan shrugged and said, “Whether you believe me or not is up to you. Anyway, while I don’t remember everything, I know that I have been fighting Eternals since the beginning, so if you think I will release you, who could be a potential threat to me and this world, you can keep dreaming.”

Shiva’s eyes narrowed, her voice low and edged with disdain. “Did you imagine all Eternals to be on the same side? Hardly. We are no strangers to discord. Just as the beings in this world have factions that collide, so do we. Moreover, if you’re worried that I might destroy your world or something after being released, that is needless worry. We, Eternals, cannot directly intervene here. In other words, we cannot touch this realm.”

“What?” Yuan’s eyes widened with shock after learning this new information. “Eternals cannot touch the Nine Heavens? Why?”

“Because this world is protected by an absolute power that even we Eternals cannot touch. It is also why I was sealed in this world.”

“Absolute power? Can you elaborate?” Yuan asked.

“No, because you wouldn’t understand it.”

“Then tell me who sealed you in this world and why they did it.”

Shiva sighed, “Very well. If it’s the only way to convince you to unseal me.” After a deep breath, she explained, “I was sealed in this world because of the absolute power that protects this place. Since I am sealed here, it means the other Eternals cannot help me, as the seal can only be removed with god’s power. As for who sealed me here… It was another Eternal. I lost our fight, and as punishment, I was sealed here.”

“How long have you been sealed here?” Yuan asked.

“Too long.”

Yuan fell silent to digest this information. ‘So she was sealed in this world because Eternals cannot interfere with this place, and since she can only be unsealed with Heavens’ Supremacy, she’s essentially trapped in here forever…or until someone who can wield Heavens’ Supremacy-someone like me comes to her rescue.’

Sometime later, Yuan asked, “What are you going to do once I unseal you? Return to the void beyond our world?”

“If only it were that simple,” she shook her head. “As Eternals, our bodies cannot be destroyed even by another Eternal. In order to seal me in this world, they had to separate my ‘Essence’ from my body. In my current state, I do not have the power to return to the void, much less regain my body.”

“Your essence… so something like your soul? Can’t you just make another body like cultivators who reached God Ascension can do?”

“Make another body? Don’t compare ours to your fragile shells,” she sneered. “They cannot simply be recreated. Even if it were possible, it would take trillions of years.”

“Then what are your plans?”

“Leave this world and regain my body,” she said.

Yuan raised an eyebrow. “Isn’t that basically what I said?”

“Yes. I didn’t say you were wrong.”

“How are you going to achieve that without a body? Don’t tell me you’re going to use Mu Xuelian…” Yuan frowned.

“That is exactly what I am going to do. I protected and cultivated their tribe for countless years for a reason,” Shiva said in a firm tone. “Nothing is free in this world.”

“What if I placed your Essence in another place and helped you regain your body? Like a Soul Weapon, which can hold souls?” Yuan asked.

“That’s impossible,” she said flatly. “I won’t repeat myself—do not apply the logic of this world to my existence. No Soul Weapon can contain my Essence. The only reason I can inhabit this girl is because of her unique physique, which I have cultivated for countless years. Even if you unseal me, I will not be able to go anywhere without this girl.”

Unsure of how to respond, Yuan remained silent. ɪꜰ ʏᴏᴜ ᴡᴀɴᴛ ᴛᴏ ʀᴇᴀᴅ ᴍᴏʀᴇ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs, ᴘʟᴇᴀsᴇ ᴠɪsɪᴛ novel✶fire.net

“Now that I’ve told you everything, what is your answer?” Shiva spoke a moment later. “Of course, I am not asking your help for free. If you free me, I will not only help you evolve your physique, but I will even show you the full potential of your physique.”

“What do you even know about my physique?”

“You mean the Heaven Refining Physique?” Shiva said with a profound smile. “Or should I call it the Eternal Constitution?”

“The what…?” Yuan stared at her, his eyes wide with disbelief. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2169: Eternal Constitution
chapters.body:
Chapter 2169: Eternal Constitution

“Eternal Constitution, you say…?” Yuan swallowed nervously.

A cold smile tugged at Shiva’s lips as she spoke, “You don’t even know about the Eternal Constitution, and you dare claim that I don’t know anything about your physique? I don’t know how you came to possess the Heaven Refining Physique, but it is basically an inferior version of the Eternal Constitution—a physique that only belongs to Eternals. It is also because of your physique that you’re able to use Heaven’s Supremacy. Otherwise, your body wouldn’t have been able to handle it.”

“…” Yuan fell silent. He’d acquired the Heaven Refining Physique due to a fateful encounter with a treasure in the Primordial Era. However, he didn’t know anything about its origin until now. ‘So it turned out to be related to the Outer Gods, huh?’ he sighed inwardly.

“Free me from my seal and I shall teach you how to use the Heaven Refining Physique to its true potential,” Shiva repeated her offer.

After pondering for a moment, Yuan spoke, “Before I give you an answer, I’d like to ask you another question.”

“If you want any more information from me, you’ll have to accept my offer. Otherwise, I won’t say another word,” she responded in a firm tone.

However, Yuan ignored her words and continued asking his question, “Do you know an Eternal by the name of Shura?”

Shiva’s eyes widened abruptly, surprise flashing across her face. “Shura, you said…?” she muttered.

Seeing her reaction, Yuan continued, “I’ll assume you know him, judging by your reaction. Now my real question is… are you friends or foes with Shura?”

After a moment of silence, Shiva opened her mouth and spoke, “Why does my relationship with another Eternal matter to you, a human?”

“It’s very important, but I cannot tell you until you give me an answer.”

Just in case Shiva planned on lying, Yuan retrieved the Grail of Truth.

Shiva narrowed her eyes at the treasure and sneered inwardly, ‘Does he really think such a toy would work on me? I don’t like how he’s constantly underestimating me, but I need his help to free me from this place…’

“Shura, huh? It has been a long time since I last heard that name.” A flicker of nostalgia crossed Shiva’s face as she spoke. “He’s my twin.”

“What?” Yuan’s eyes widened upon hearing this shocking revelation. “Twin as in siblings? Eternals can be siblings? Does that mean you also have a parent?”

Shiva shook her head and explained, “We are twins because we were born at the same time and in the same area. Eternals are being born from the universe itself, and if we are all born from the same source, we are technically all siblings. Of course, not every Eternal sees it this way. Anyway, since we were born in the same place, we naturally grew up together. That said, we’re neither allies nor foes with each other. He’s something akin to a neighbor.”

“Sounds complicated…” Yuan muttered.

“Does my answer satisfy your question?” Shiva then asked.

“Yes, it does.”

“Then what is your answer? My patience is limited. If you continue to test me, I will kill you even if you are the key to my freedom. I simply need to wait longer-until the other Eternals destroy this world.”

Yuan fell silent after hearing her last words.

“You heard that right. There are Eternals who want to destroy this world. However, that’s all I am going to say.”

Yuan didn’t become silent because he was shocked to learn that Eternals were trying to destroy the Nine Heavens, as he’d known this since the Primordial Era.

Sometime later, Yuan sighed, “Alright, I’ll free you.”

Shiva’s expression immediately brightened. “You won’t regret your decision.” Thıs text ıs hosted at noveⅼfire.net

“So, what am I supposed to do?” he asked.

Shiva pointed at the ice above him again and said, “It’s quite simple. All you need to do is destroy that block of ice with Eternal Essence-or what you call Heaven’s Supremacy.”

Yuan’s eyes lingered on the block of ice, his expression calm yet resolute. In the next breath, he drew Number One Under Heaven, its blade shrouded in the golden radiance of Heaven’s Supremacy, and unleashed a strike against the frozen monolith. Yet the ice not only repelled his sword, it even dispersed the golden aura surrounding it.

Shiva sighed at the results and remarked, “As I figured, your Eternal Essence is too weak to break the seal. Is that all you got?”

Yuan gave no reply to her remarks. Instead, he steadied himself for another attempt, carefully gathering the power of Heaven’s Supremacy. When the time came, he invoked Absolute Dragon Awakening, his aura erupting as he delivered his most powerful strike. But even so, the block of ice remained untouched, and not even the faintest scratch marred its surface.

“It doesn’t matter how much you increase your mortal strength with techniques or what martial techniques you use. The only thing that can damage the seal is Eternal Essence, so even if you increase your cultivation by a million times, nothing will change until you strengthen your Eternal Essence.”

“In other words, I have to evolve my physique, reach True Immortal, and fully regain my ability to use Heaven’s Supremacy before I can break your seal. Then you’ll have to wait a little longer. I was just on the verge of a breakthrough before your interference.”

“And how long will that take?” Shiva asked.

“Who knows? But it shouldn’t be too long.”

Without another word, Yuan resubmerged his body in the pond and closed his eyes, continuing his cultivation.

A moment later, he opened his eyes and asked, “Are you going to possess Mu Xuelian’s body until I free you?”

Shiva didn’t immediately respond. She looked at her own palm and mumbled after a moment of silence, “This girl’s body is reaching its limits, so I will have to leave for now. I’ll see you again soon.” Mu Xuelian’s eyes suddenly returned to normal, and her body collapsed the next instant. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2170: True Immortal
chapters.body:
Chapter 2170: True Immortal

“Hey, are you okay?” Yuan hurried out of the pond and rushed to Mu Xuelian’s side, finding her drenched in sweat and gasping for breath.

After catching her breath, Mu Xuelian lifted her head to glare at him. “Who… who are you…?” she muttered.

“I’m Yuan,” he instantly replied.

“You know that’s not what I meant… You know what? Forget it…” Though Shiva had possessed her, Mu Xuelian had remained conscious the entire time, seeing and hearing everything that transpired throughout their conversation.

Mu Xuelian quietly climbed back onto the ice bed and began cultivating.

Seeing that she was fine, Yuan returned to the pond and continued his cultivation.

In the blink of an eye, another year had passed.

<Your ‘True Cold Harmony’ has developed into ‘Flawless Cold Harmony’>

<Progress: 100% >

The instant Yuan’s physique evolved, he could feel his bottleneck instantly being broken, and his cultivation surged all the way to the peak of True Immortal.

Yuan opened his eyes after reaching True Immortal and learning Eternal Essence. Within his gaze flickered a profound radiance, vast and unfathomable, like the reflection of a secret that belonged solely to the universe.

Mu Xuelian, who had been cultivating on the ice bed for the entire time, also opened her eyes upon noticing Yuan’s movements. Yuan stood from the pond and tilted his head to gaze at the ice. He retrieved Number One Under Heaven and prepared for his third attempt. This time, instead of using Absolute Dragon Awakening or other martial techniques, Yuan focused solely on his Eternal Essence.

Silence lingered for only a moment before a golden brilliance surged from Yuan’s form, flooding the cavern with light. With a swift motion, he raised his sword and swung it at the ice in a strike so powerful it shook the cavern to its core. ᴛhis chapter is ᴜpdated by novel·fıre·net

Mu Hanyan and Chief Mu, who had been waiting for them outside, sensed the tremble and were immediately startled by it. “Heavens! What was that?!” Chief Mu exclaimed.

Mu Hanyan looked at the ground and muttered, “It came from below…”

“What the hell are they doing down there?” Chief Mu swallowed nervously.

As curious and worried as she was, there was nothing either of them could do but wait.

Meanwhile, underground, Yuan stared at the ice with a frustrated expression. Despite reaching True Immortal and regaining his ability to use Eternal Essence, he was only able to put a faint scratch on the ice.

“Don’t tell me I waited an entire year just for this pitiful progress,” Mu Xuelian’s voice suddenly echoed in the

cavern.

Yuan turned to look at Mu Xuelian, who was staring at him. However, when he saw that her eyes appeared normal, he asked, “Did Shiva ask you to say that?”

Mu Xuelian quietly nodded.

Yuan sighed. “At least I can damage the ice now. If I keep at it, it should eventually break.”

“Hah! At this rate, it’ll take you thousands of years to break the seal-she said,” Mu Xuelian relayed.

“Then do you have any suggestions? Maybe you can teach me how to improve my Eternal Essence.”

“Eternal Essence isn’t something that can be taught. However, I do have a way to improve its strength, and that is to ” Mu Xuelian suddenly halted her sentence, her eyes wide with disbelief.

“What did Shiva say?” Yuan asked.

“…” After a moment of awkward silence, Mu Xuelian finally spoke, “If you want to strengthen your Eternal Essence, cultivate with this girl and refine her essence… is what Lord Shiva said.”

“What kind of nonsense are you spewing?” Yuan asked with a dumbfounded look on his face.

“I-It’s not nonsense. Your Heaven Refining Physique isn’t limited to treasures and energies. You can also refine other things. For example, this girl’s essence.”

Yuan narrowed his eyes at Shiva’s words. “Isn’t that just dual cultivation?” Yuan asked with a raised eyebrow.

“It’s not the same thing. You’ll be doing more than just absorbing her energy and improving your cultivation. You’ll be refining her essence into Eternal Essence.”

“What even is this ‘essence’ that you’re talking about? It’s not her soul, right?”

“Everything in this universe is made of essence-from humans to treasures-and for us Eternals, it’s Eternal Essence. So when you wield that power, you’re essentially turning your own existence into your power. Don’t mistake it for spiritual energy because it’s not the same thing. The stronger one’s influence in the universe is, the denser their essence.”

“Turning my own existence into my power? That’s confusing as hell,” Yuan remarked. “Does that mean if I use too much Eternal Essence, I could erase my existence?”

“Yes, it’s possible.”

“Fuck! You should’ve told me something that important earlier!” Yuan exclaimed.

“What are you worried about? You have the Heaven Refining Physique, so you can always replenish your essence.”

“That’s not the issue here…” Yuan sighed.

He continued after realizing something, “Hold on. If you’re asking me to refine Mu Xuelian’s essence, aren’t you basically asking me to refine her existence? I’m not going to do that.”

“While you can definitely erase her existence if you refine too much of her essence, she’ll be fine as long as you don’t do that. You are also underestimating how much essence a human being carries.”

“What about injuring her? Would it affect her talent or health?”

“It will affect her talents, and depending on how much you refine, it could also affect her health.”

“And why does it require us to dual cultivate? Isn’t this something meant for Eternals?”

“As Eternals, we can refine essence with nothing but will alone,” Shiva explained. “But you are human, and for humans, it is easier—and far safer—to refine another human’s essence through dual cultivation, since your bodies are directly connected.”

“You can’t be serious…” Yuan sighed as he rubbed his eyes in a stressful manner. “What’s with this weird development?” (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.body:
Chapter 2171: Refining Essence

“What are you hesitating for? I thought humans loved the act of dual cultivation, especially men,” Mu Xuelian said, relaying Shiva’s words. More chapters in t.me/+9n1w1vacSaZhYzAy

“This isn’t ordinary dual cultivation-and I already have partners,” Yuan said with a slight frown. “I won’t cultivate with someone I just met, let alone against her will.”

“I don’t understand.”

“In case you forgot, that body doesn’t belong to you but to Mu Xuelian.”

“It… It is not against my will,” Mu Xuelian suddenly said.

“What?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“Lord Shiva’s will is my will. If Lord Shiva commands me to cultivate with you, then I shall,” Mu Xuelian said.

Yuan frowned. “Is that what Shiva told you to say?”

She shook her head. “No, these are my own words. The White Hell Barbarian Tribe exists only because of Lord Shiva. Her will is our will.”

Yuan’s gaze hardened. “Then you don’t understand what it means to have a will of your own. If it isn’t something you truly desire without external influence, you can’t call it your will. What you’re describing is simply obedience. You’re no different from a servant following orders. Anyway, I am not going to cultivate with you just to strengthen my Eternal Essence.”

Mu Xuelian’s eyes suddenly shifted, a familiar symbol emerging within them and shining with profound radiance. “Then how else are you going to strengthen your Eternal Essence? Are you going to tell me to wait for you to chip away the seal slowly?” Shiva asked.

Yuan smiled and said, “You said that ‘essence’ exists in everything, correct?”

“That is correct. From the very air you breathe to the dirt beneath your feet-everything contains essence.”

“Then I can simply refine those essences. Why does it have to be dual cultivation?”

Shiva fell silent for a moment before she suddenly burst out laughing. “If you are thinking about refining nature’s essence, you can forget about it. Your Heaven Refining Physique is merely a fake-a replica of our Eternal Constitution. You may be able to refine essence, but that is limited to only the essence of other living beings. Only those with a true Eternal Constitution can refine nature’s essence! In other words, only an Eternal such as myself can do it!” Thᴇ link to the origɪn of this information rᴇsts ɪn 𝓷𝓸𝓿𝓮𝓵※𝖿𝗂𝗋𝖾※𝙣𝙚𝙩

“My physique is a fake? And what makes you so certain of that?” Yuan questioned.

Shiva’s lips curled into a sneer. “Because it’s impossible. How could a mere human carry such a physique? Even if you had somehow obtained a genuine Eternal Constitution, your soul remains human. And a human soul will never have the capacity to refine the essence of nature itself.”

“We won’t know until we try.” Yuan sat down and closed his eyes.

“How are you going to try when you don’t even know how to sense essence?” Shiva remarked.

Yuan ignored her. A few moments later, he smiled and said, “I can sense ‘essence’.”

“What?” Shiva’s eyebrows twitched upon hearing his words; her gaze filled with disbelief. “He can sense essence in such a short time when he’d only just learned of its existence? I guess he didn’t obtain the Heaven Refining Physique for no reason…”

<Eternal Constitution (Incomplete) activated>

<Eternal Constitution (Incomplete) has begun refining ‘Qi’>

As a cultivator, he was most familiar with spiritual energy, so he decided to absorb it first. Yet unlike before, where he would refine the spiritual energy and store it inside his Dantian, Yuan did not stop there. He continued breaking it down, refining it past all limits, until the spiritual energy itself was erased from existence.

<You have successfully refined ‘essence’ from ‘Qi’>

At the moment of his success, the cavern’s spiritual energy dropped by nearly half. Yet the essence he managed to refine was so minuscule it was practically nonexistent.

‘He managed to refine nature’s essence?!’ Shiva’s eyes widened at the unbelievable sight before her. ‘Does this mean he actually possesses a true Eternal Constitution?! That’s impossible!’

After his initial success, Yuan turned his attention to the pebble in front of him. Although refining spiritual energy into essence was simple since he knew how to absorb spiritual energy, he wasn’t sure how to ‘absorb’ the pebble before him. After pondering for a moment, Yuan had this thought-‘If I cannot absorb it because of its solid form, I just need to break it down to the point where I can absorb it!’

Yuan reached for the pebble before him, channeling Eternal Essence through his hand. With a light squeeze, the stone crumbled-shattering further and further until it dissolved into particles so fine they vanished, as though it had evaporated from existence. He then attempted to absorb the ‘essence’ that was left behind.

<Eternal Constitution (Incomplete) activated>

“It worked!” Yuan exclaimed, a bright smile spreading across his face.

He looked at Shiva with a boastful smile and said, “So, what were you saying just now?”

“…” Shiva remained silent as she stared at him, a deep frown etched across her face. Her gaze on him carried a dangerous edge-like killing intent that was there, yet not.

“What? Too speechless to even speak?” Yuan chuckled.

She finally responded, “What is your relationship with Shura?”

“Why are you asking me this now?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“You’ve just proven that you possess a true Eternal Constitution. But that should be impossible… unless an Eternal willingly bestowed their own upon you!” Shiva explained. She continued, “Since you mentioned Shura, I can only assume he gave you his Eternal Constitution. But that would also mean he gave up his body for you.”

“…” After a moment of silence, Yuan responded, “I don’t know.”

“You don’t know? What the hell is that supposed to mean?” Shiva frowned.

“Exactly what it sounds. I don’t know because I don’t remember. I recently reincarnated, so my memories are still in shambles. I remember meeting Shura and him bestowing me a ‘gift’ in a past life, but I do not recall anything else,” he admitted.

‘He doesn’t seem to be lying…’ Shiva thought inwardly.

Yuan then asked, “If Shura gave me his physique, does that mean he’s somewhere out there with just his ‘essence’ like you currently are?”

She silently nodded. (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2172: Refining Shiva'S Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2172: Refining Shiva'S Essence

“For Shura to have given you his Eternal Constitution… It’s unimaginable for an Eternal to do such a thing,” Shiva murmured, disbelief still lacing her voice. “Your connection with him must be unfathomable…” She continued, “But merely possessing a true Eternal Constitution wouldn’t let you refine nature’s essence—not with a human soul. Since you mentioned reincarnation, your soul must have already surpassed human limits. In other words, you are no longer truly human, but something between that of a human and an Eternal.” More chapters in t.me/+9n1w1vacSaZhYzAy

“So I’m a half-god?” Yuan remarked with a smile.

“Don’t flatter yourself. You may have surpassed human limits, but you are nowhere near a god-not even one percent,” Shiva sneered.

“Maybe not right now, but eventually.”

Shiva didn’t respond. She couldn’t. As much as she wanted to dismiss his seemingly foolish words, she knew that if Yuan continued to grow and refine his Eternal Constitution, he would one day reach true godhood.

“I will concede this-you are remarkable for a human,” Shiva said, her tone grudging. She shook her head lightly before continuing, “But even with your ability to refine nature’s essence, this place holds far too little of it to truly advance your Eternal Essence. If you want to see any drastic improvements to your Eternal Essence, you need to refine this girl’s essence through dual cultivation.”

Yuan’s gaze shifted to the liquid and asked, “What about this liquid? I can sense an immense amount of essence coming from it.”

Shiva sneered, “Are you out of your mind? That liquid was created from my essence. It’s not something your body or mind can handle. Stop being stubborn and cultivate with this girl already. Or is there another reason you keep refusing? Do you like men?”

Yuan frowned and said, “Keep your delusions to yourself. I already told you that I have partners. I won’t betray them by cultivating with a stranger.”

“Then you can just make this girl your partner,” Shiva said.

“That’s not how it works.”

“I fail to understand,” Shiva said with a weary sigh. “You say you already have multiple partners, so clearly the notion of having more than one does not trouble you. Why, then, is this girl any different? Why will you not accept her? Are you worried about her talents? I can guarantee she’s exceptionally talented-and this is coming from a god. Even her body and appearance are far beyond that of ordinary humans.”

Yuan sighed. “I don’t expect a god born of the void to understand human emotions or morals. You’ll just have to accept it.”

“Is that so? Then I will waste no more words on you,” Shiva said, her voice edged with disdain. “If you wish to refine the essence, go ahead. But try not to die, or all I’ve done for you will be for nothing.” She closed her eyes, and when they opened once more, the profound light within them had vanished.

Mu Xuelian’s gaze returned, clear and human, as she regained control of her body. ᴛhis chapter is ᴜpdated by 𝗇𝗈𝗏𝖾𝗅•𝖿𝗂𝗋𝖾•𝗇𝖾𝗍

Shortly after regaining control, Mu Xuelian glared at Yuan and asked, “Do you dislike me? Have I done anything that has offended you?”

“Of course not,” he calmly replied. “I understand why Shiva doesn’t understand, but aren’t you human? Surely, you understand why I refuse to cultivate with you.”

Mu Xuelian narrowed her eyes slightly and responded, “While it may have been Lord Shiva’s will, I still have my own feelings, and I did not reject the idea of cultivating with you. You’re clearly an extraordinary individual. If I had encountered you during my travel to the outside world to seek a partner, you would’ve definitely been my first choice. In fact, now that I am aware of your existence, I probably won’t settle for anyone else.”

Mu Xuelian rose from the ice bed, her figure gliding with grace as she suddenly advanced toward him.

“What are you doing?” Yuan asked, his voice steady as she stepped close, leaving only inches between them.

Mu Xuelian lowered her head for a moment, her eyes flicking downward to his naked sword before locking with his. “I want your child. These are my words, spoken from my own will,” she said the next moment, her voice firm and full of determination.

A fragment of Tian Yingzhe’s memories suddenly surfaced in Yuan’s mind. In it, a woman resembling Mu Xuelian stood before him, naked as she was now, speaking nearly the same words.

“Sorry,” Yuan said, his tone calm but firm. “I won’t impregnate someone simply because they ask, especially if that person is someone I barely know.” The words left his lips unconsciously, the same words Tian Yingzhe had once spoken to Mu Hanyan.

“In that case, I’ll ask you again once we get to know each other better,” Mu Xuelian said softly, unaware that she was repeating the exact same words her grandmother, Mu Hanyan, said in response to Tian Yingzhe’s rejection.

Before Yuan could even respond, Mu Xuelian turned around and returned to the ice bed to cultivate in silence.

“…” Yuan rubbed his eyes and sighed before clearing his mind to focus on the liquid in the pond. ‘Since it’s liquid, I can’t reduce it to dust like the pebble… should I try to vaporize it instead?’

Acting on the thought, Yuan called forth his True Primordial Fire, its searing flames engulfing the pond’s surface. But to his astonishment, the liquid remained utterly unchanged, and not even a single bubble rose. The liquid didn’t boil, much less vaporize. ‘If even True Primordial Fire cannot vaporize it, then I’ll have to find a different method…’

After a moment’s contemplation, Yuan sank into the pond, letting the liquid envelop him fully. Then, with the same method he used in body tempering, he began trying to absorb the energy in the liquid before refining it.

<Eternal Constitution (Incomplete) activated>

<Eternal Constitution (Incomplete) has begun refining Shiva’s Essence>

‘It’s working!’ Yuan felt a surge of elation at the results. Yet the joy was fleeting, for in the next instant, his body was overwhelmed, assaulted by waves of pain so intense they defied imagination. But despite the pain, he endured it.

Sometime later…

<You have glimpsed a realm that lies beyond Flawless Cold Harmony> (More chapters in Buyers club for TMT)

chapters.title: Chapter 2173: Releasing Shiva From Her Seal
chapters.body:
Chapter 2173: Releasing Shiva From Her Seal

Chapter 2173: Releasing Shiva From Her Seal

’A realm beyond Cold Harmony, huh?’ Yuan wasn’t surprised by this new discovery. In fact, he anticipated something like this existed because of the existence of Eternals.

If Cold Harmony was the limit of humans, then there must be a realm only achievable by gods.

’This human… he’s actually refining my essence!’ Shiva could only stare in shock and disbelief as Yuan refined the liquid from the sealed ice above. Despite its name, Shiva’s Essence was not truly her existence, but something born from it.

’Even though it’s not truly my essence… It’s not something that can be refined by someone who’s not even a god!’

Shiva’s intrigue toward Yuan deepened.

’He may have surpassed my expectations, but even then, he’ll need many years to refine enough of my essence to break the seal.’

Yet, not even a week later, Shiva noticed that the liquid in the pond had drastically decreased by half.

’He absorbed half of the essence already?!’

Shiva began to wonder if she had fallen into an illusion, for everything unfolding before her eyes defied logic. Yet she knew that only another Eternal could possibly affect her, and since Eternals could not touch the Nine Heavens, she was forced to accept that the impossible before her was, in truth, reality.

Yuan’s progress did not slow down after refining half of Shiva’s Essence. If anything, it increased, and after another three days, Yuan completely absorbed every last drop of liquid in the pond.

<Your ’Flawless Cold Harmony’ has transcended past its limits and evolved into ’Absolute Cold Harmony’>

<Through Shiva’s Essence refinement, you have learned the Skill ’Shiva’s Eternal Frost’>

[Shiva’s Eternal Frost]

[Rank: Eternal]

[Mastery Level: ???] Thɪs chapter is updated by NoveI(F)ire.net

[Description: The pinnacle of coldness—powerful enough to freeze even gods]

<You have discovered ’Eternal’ rank techniques>

[Warning: Eternal-rank techniques cannot be used within the Nine Heavens due to ???]

’I cannot use Eternal-rank techniques in the Nine Heavens, huh? This must be due to the ’absolute power’ that Shiva mentioned…’ Yuan thought to himself.

<Your understanding of Eternal Essence has improved drastically>

After absorbing all of Shiva’s Essence, Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and began gathering his Eternal Essence.

’It’s gotten so much more powerful than before!’ Mu Xuelian swallowed nervously.

Even though she could not comprehend it, she understood that his golden aura had strengthened tremendously compared to just days ago.

’With that much Eternal Essence, it might be possible!’ Shiva trembled in anticipation as she watched Yuan prepare to strike the ice.

The next moment, Yuan swung his sword at the ice.

Sha!

Like a hot knife going through tofu, Yuan completely sliced the block of ice in half, revealing a glowing blue orb within.

’I’M FREE!’

After being freed from the seal, Shiva instantly shot toward Mu Xuelian, who was sitting on the ice bed.

“What are you doing?!” Yuan cried out.

But his body, drained and paralyzed from pushing Eternal Essence to its very limit, refused to move. Helpless, he could only watch as Shiva slipped into Mu Xuelian’s body.

Mu Xuelian’s body stiffened for a brief moment before her eyes began to glow.

“Relax, I am not going to hurt this girl,” Shiva said.

“Then what are you doing?”

“It’s not like I can wander around without a body,” she explained. “Until I regain my body, I will have to use this girl’s body as my own. That being said, it’s not like I will take complete control. I will only take control when necessary, as my essence puts too much of a burden on her body.”

“What are you going to do now that you’re free from the seal? Leave the Nine Heavens to find your body? Something else entirely?” Yuan asked her a moment later.

“As much as I want to start looking for my body immediately, I don’t know where to begin. The Eternals who sealed me have definitely hidden my body somewhere, and I doubt I will ever find it by wandering.”

“My original plan was to find another Eternal who could help me, but I have changed my mind.”

Shiva jumped from the ice bed and landed right in front of Yuan.

“I’m very intrigued with your existence, and I’m confident that if I follow you, I’ll eventually encounter Shura or another Eternal, so that’s what I am going to do.”

“You’re going to follow me…?” Yuan felt a headache forming the instant he heard those words. He wanted to refuse, yet deep down he knew that Shiva wasn’t asking.

“Why do you seem dissatisfied? You’re going to have a god by your side. You should feel honored,” Shiva sneered.

“…”

Although he didn’t want to admit it, it would definitely benefit him to have an Eternal by his side. After all, he still had a lot of questions about their existence. Moreover, he could keep an eye on her.

’If she’s by my side, I won’t have to constantly worry about an Eternal roaming the Nine Heavens…’ he sighed inwardly.

Furthermore, Mu Xuelian was a cultivator at the 3rd level of God Ascension. Her power could come in handy.

“So, what do you say?” Shiva asked.

“It’s not like I have a choice,” Yuan replied.

Shiva narrowed her eyes and spoke, “You may be an inferior being—one that’s not even worthy of being in my gaze—but I have made you a promise, and I intend to keep it.”

“What are you talking about?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“Have you forgotten already? I said that I’d help you realize your physique’s true potential and even evolve it.”

“Oh?” Yuan was pleasantly surprised; he had expected her to say she’d already helped him enough with the information she provided him.

Shiva suddenly placed a hand on his chest and closed her eyes.

“Now that I am free, I can inspect your body properly,” she said a moment later. “You indeed have the Eternal Constitution, but it’s not complete. I don’t want to owe a human any debt, so I will help you complete it.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2174: The Method To Completing The Eternal Constitution
chapters.body:
Chapter 2174: The Method To Completing The Eternal Constitution

Chapter 2174: The Method to Completing the Eternal Constitution

“Are you going to directly evolve my physique with your god-like power or something?” Yuan asked.

Shiva looked at him with an odd gaze.

“I may be a god, but I am not omnipotent. Furthermore, I do not have my body, and my powers are sealed within the Nine Heavens.”

“Then how will you help me?”

“By telling you the steps to complete it. I am going to explain it now, so listen carefully.”

Shiva proceeded to explain the process, “In order to complete your Eternal Constitution, you will need to attain the three supreme physiques. You already possess one of them, so you need the other two.”

“Supreme physiques…?” Yuan recalled Xiao Hua mentioning it in the past, calling them the pinnacle of physiques.

“I currently possess the Golden Immortal Physique, and I am aware of the Harmonic Spirit Constitution, but what is the third one?” he asked.

“The Myriad Impervious Physique, which is obtainable by attaining the highest level of immunity for fire, cold, poison, and lightning—something you have already achieved. All that remains is for you to merge them and forge the physique.”

“Hm? But I have only just attained the highest level of Cold Immunity—Absolute Cold Harmony. I still need the other three,” said Yuan.

Shiva shook her head and sighed, “Let me rephrase it—the Myriad Impervious Physique, which is obtainable by attaining the highest level of immunity attainable by humans. Your Cold Immunity has surpassed human limits. Hell, you’re probably the only human to have ever reached such a realm.”

“Is that so… then how do I combine them?”

“Since you already have the immunities, you just need to consume the Iridescent Harmony Fruit.”

“The Iridescent Harmony Fruit, huh? What about the Harmonic Spirit Constitution?”

“You will need three treasures and an Untainted Body. A Seven-Colored Herb, Boundless Spirit Dew, and a Primordial Crystal. Ah, you also need something that contains an immense amount of spiritual energy—something like a large and high-quality Spirit Jade—so you technically need four treasures.”

“…”

Yuan stared at Shiva with a dumbfounded look on his face.

“What is it? I know they’re extremely rare—perhaps even extinct by now, but without them, you won’t be able to complete the Eternal Constitution.”

Without a word, Yuan drew three items from his spatial ring.

“What in the…” Shiva’s eyes widened at the sight.

A rainbow-colored flower, a black pearl, and a palm-sized Ancient-grade Spirit Jade lay before her.

To her surprise, Yuan had already collected three of the four treasures required for the Harmonic Spirit Constitution.

The Seven-Colored Herb, the Ancient Spirit Jade, and the Primordial Crystals—Yuan had carried these treasures for as long as he could remember. The Ancient Spirit Jade and the Seven-Colored Herb, in particular, had followed him since his days in the lower heavens.

“I also have an Untainted Body, so I am just missing the Boundless Spirit Dew,” Yuan said.

Shiva couldn’t help but laugh, “You sure came prepared. It’s almost like you knew this would happen.”

Yuan shook his head with a smile and said, “No way. It’s just a coincidence… or fate.”

“In any case, once you attain all three supreme physiques, I’ll tell you the next step.”

“There are more steps?”

“Of course. Did you think it would be that easy to obtain a complete Eternal Constitution?” Shiva sneered.

“Easy…? I don’t think anyone has ever attained all three supreme physiques before…” he remarked.

Though he doesn’t have all of his memories, Yuan knew that none of his past lives had ever attained all three supreme physiques in one lifetime, so this would truly be an unprecedented achievement if he managed to do it.

Sometime later, Yuan and Mu Xuelian left the cavern and returned to the surface, where Mu Hanyan and Chief Mu were waiting.

“They’re done with training already?” Mu Hanyan remarked when the door suddenly opened.

Yuan emerged from the hole the very next moment.

“Hey! What happened down there?!” Chief Mu immediately questioned him. “And what happened to my daughter?! I swear, if anything—”

Chief Mu cut off mid-sentence as Mu Xuelian stepped out of the hole a moment later. However, there was something different about Mu Xuelian. There was an aura around her that made both Chief Mu and Mu Hanyan freeze with apprehension.

“I’m done here,” Yuan said, snapping them out of their daze.

“Already…? It’s barely been three years! Are you telling me that your True Cold Harmony reached the next realm in this short time?!” Mu Hanyan exclaimed, not daring to believe her ears.

“Something like that,” Yuan responded with a mysterious smile, keeping the truth to himself.

No one could predict how she might react if Yuan revealed the truth—that he had not only attained Flawless Cold Harmony, but had ascended beyond it, stepping into a realm that transcended the very limits of humanity.

“D-Did anything else happen down there?” Chief Mu asked.

“Are you referring to Shiva? Yes, I spoke with her.” Yuan calmly admitted.

“What did she say?! Why did she summon you?!”

Yuan looked at Mu Xuelian with a raised eyebrow.

“I see. So it wasn’t because Mu Hanyan was trying to help out an old friend but because of your command that she led me here,” he said, sounding a little disappointed.

“How else would we have met—is what Lord Shiva said,” Mu Xuelian relayed.

“What are you talking about…?” Chief Mu muttered with a dumbfounded look on her face.

Mu Xuelian closed her eyes for a brief moment, and when they opened again, a profound symbol shimmered within their depths.

“T-That symbol and presence!”

Mu Hanyan instantly fell to her knees and kissed the ground with her forehead.

“This lowly servant greets Lord Shiva!”

“What?!” Chief Mu’s eyes nearly fell out of their socket after hearing Mu Hanyan’s words. However, she didn’t have the time to ponder and quickly followed her mother’s action, kowtowing on the ground. ʀᴇᴀᴅ ʟᴀᴛᴇsᴛ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀᴛ NoveIꜰire.net

“This lowly servant greets Lord Shiva!”

Shiva then spoke, “I have been released from my prison and taken residence within this girl’s body.”

“?!?!?!”

Her words left Mu Hanyan and Chief Mu speechless.

chapters.title: Chapter 2175: Leaving White Hell
chapters.body:
Chapter 2175: Leaving White Hell

Chapter 2175: Leaving White Hell

“C-Congratulations on regaining your freedom, Lord Shiva!”

After a brief moment of silence, Mu Hanyan snapped out of her daze and spoke loudly.

Shiva nodded and said, “The White Hell Barbarian Tribe has fulfilled their duties well. I shall bestow upon your tribe a fitting reward before my departure.”
