Meanwhile, inside Yuan’s head—

“Young Master! You’re okay!” Feng Yuxiang’s worried voice resounded.

“Feng Feng? What happened to you?” Yuan asked.

“That’s what I wanted to ask! Our connection with you severed the moment you entered that cavern! However, we were obviously still within your Dantian.”

After Yuan entered Shiva’s Hell, their connection severed, so they’d spent the last three years in the dark.

“Did you try leaving?” Yuan then asked.

“I did, but there was a mysterious force that kept us from leaving your Dantian, almost like we were sealed inside a prison.”

“Is that so… Anyway, I’m fine. I’ll explain everything to you later,” said Yuan. ᴛʜɪs ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀ ɪs ᴜᴘᴅᴀᴛᴇ ʙʏ novęlfire.net

Sometime later, Yuan and the others returned to the White Hell Barbarian Tribe’s settlement.

Mu Hanyan gathered every member of the tribe to greet Shiva.

“We greet Lord Shiva!”

Shiva, who was in control of Mu Xuelian’s body, proceeded to bless every member of the tribe with her powers, instantly improving their physiques and even increasing their cultivation by an entire level directly.

Once everything was done, Yuan approached one of the members and asked, “Do you know what happened to Yaoqin?”

“Ah, you mean the woman who was cultivating here? She left a month ago, but she also left behind something for you.”

The tribe member went inside the building where Yaoqin had cultivated and grabbed the items left behind by Yaoqin before handing them to Yuan.

“Thank you.”

Yaoqin had left behind a note and a medallion with her name on it.

[Unfortunately, I was unable to see you again before I left. However, if you are ever near the Divine Essence Monastery, please visit me so that I can properly repay the debt I owe you for saving my life. Signed Yaoqin.]

“The Divine Essence Monastery, huh? That’s in the Ninth Heaven. I was going to visit that place one day, anyway.” Yuan stored the note and Yaoqin’s medallion inside his spatial ring.

“Yingzhe, Lord Shiva has called for you,” Mu Hanyan suddenly approached him and said.

“Call me Yuan. I am no longer Tian Yingzhe,” he said.

“Alright…”

Yuan followed Mu Hanyan to another building, where Chief Mu and Mu Xuelian were present.

“You’re leaving soon, right? Lord Shiva asked what your plans are afterward,” Mu Xuelian said.

“The Primordial Realm,” he calmly responded.

“What?” Mu Hanyan and Chief Mu stared at him with wide eyes. Although they have spent most of their lives within White Hell, even they were aware of the notoriety behind the Primordial Realm.

“That place where countless dangerous criminals are imprisoned? Why are you going there?” Mu Hanyan couldn’t help but ask.

“I have some business there, that’s all.”

Yuan looked at Mu Xuelian and said, “I know you want to follow me, but if you don’t want to go there, you can wait here until I return from the Primordial Realm.”

However, Mu Xuelian shook her head and said, “No, I will follow you.”

Yuan looked at Chief Mu and asked, “Did your daughter tell you about her plans to follow me?”

Chief Mu silently nodded.

“Nothing to say?”

“What is there to say? She may be my daughter, but she’s also Lord Shiva’s vessel right now. I do not have the authority to question her decision.”

Mu Hanyan then said, “Honestly, I was already planning on letting Xuelian leave White Hell. Do you remember our agreement? I was going to ask you to let her follow you.”

“I had a feeling it would be something like that, but it’s pretty shameless of you to demand anything when you tricked me,” Yuan said, his eyes slightly narrowed.

Mu Hanyan faced him and apologetically bowed her head, “I’m sorry. If you wish to punish me, I will accept any punishment you may have for me.”

“Forget it. Considering our past, I’ll forgive you.”

“Thank you…”

“When are you going to leave?” Mu Xuelian asked.

“Right now, but I can wait for you if you need some time to prepare,” he said.

“Then give me a few hours to say goodbye to my tribe.”

Yuan nodded.

Sometime later, Mu Xuelian left to say her goodbye to the rest of the tribe.

“Have you reunited with that mischievous little girl yet?” Mu Hanyan suddenly asked Yuan.

“Who?” Yuan asked with a puzzled face.

“You know… that little girl you brought everywhere as Yingzhe. The one who was constantly getting into trouble and required our saving. I think her surname was Xu…”

“Xu Jiaqi? Yes, I have already met her. Why do you ask?”

“How is she doing these days? I haven’t left White Hell since your disappearance.”

“She’s currently a leading figure of Celestial Overlords,” said Yuan.

“I see… Give her my regards the next time you see her again.”

“I will.”

A nostalgic smile tugged at Mu Hanyan’s lips as she reminisced about their past.

“That little girl was constantly getting in my way. I find it cute now, but it was pretty annoying back then. Treat my granddaughter well.”

“Are you not worried about Shiva possessing her body?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“No, Lord Shiva is our guardian spirit. She wouldn’t do anything to harm her.”

Yuan then asked, “How much do you know about Outer Gods—or Eternals?”

“Besides the very basics? Not much… if anything.”

“Alright.”

A few hours later, Mu Xuelian returned and was prepared to leave White Hell.

Yuan didn’t linger and left with her shortly after.

As they traveled, Yuan asked, “Hey, Shiva, do you know anything about an Eternal sealed within the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s territory?”

“Heavenly White Tiger Clan? I know of a few other Eternals sealed within the Nine Heavens before me, but none from that area, so they were most likely sealed after me.”

“There are more sealed Eternals in the Nine Heavens…?”

Her words left Yuan speechless.

chapters.title: Chapter 2176: The Final Moments Of The Immortal Monarch’S Life
chapters.body:
Chapter 2176: The Final Moments Of The Immortal Monarch’S Life

Chapter 2176: The Final Moments of the Immortal Monarch’s Life

“There are more sealed Eternals in the Nine Heavens…?” Yuan muttered in a daze.

“Of course. Since we cannot kill each other, the Nine Heavens is the perfect place to seal each other,” said Mu Xuelian, relaying Shiva’s words.

“Eternals cannot kill each other? That’s the first I’ve heard of it,” Yuan remarked.

“It isn’t a law that binds us,” Shiva explained. “It’s simply that none of us possesses the capability to kill another Eternal.” Follow current novels on noveⅼfire.net

“The most we can manage is to cast one another into a deep slumber… or seal each other away,” she continued.

A sudden flicker of realization crossed Yuan’s face, and he murmured softly, “I remember…”

“What?”

“I remembered the end of the Immortal Monarch’s life…” Yuan sighed, his gaze distant as he recalled the final moments of the Immortal Monarch’s life.

The Immortal Monarch, with the Nine Divine Supremes by his side, fought against several Eternals. Despite their seemingly overwhelming prowess, they were only able to put the Eternals to sleep, and it came at a heavy cost.

The devastation of their battle had shattered the Divine Heavens, dividing them into the Nine Heavens and giving birth to Earth, which drifted unfathomably far away. Moreover, the Immortal Monarch and several of his companions perished as a result.

’It’s been a long time since their slumber… those Eternals should be waking up soon, and when that happens, the Nine Heavens will become chaotic again…’

“Shiva, I must ask… Why are the Eternals so interested in the Nine Heavens? Aren’t you guys the most powerful beings in the universe? Why pay attention to a small world like ours? I doubt we have anything worth your attention.”

Shiva smiled faintly and said, “You’re right. The Nine Heavens has nothing that could capture our interest. That is why Eternals pay no heed to the world itself… but rather to what lies hidden within it.”

“What is hidden within the Nine Heavens…? A treasure? Do we have a treasure that’s powerful enough to capture even Eternals’ attention?”

Yuan turned to Shiva for an answer. However, Mu Xuelian merely shook her head and said, “Lord Shiva said she’s not going to tell you.”

“What? Why not? You already revealed so much.”

“She said she doesn’t want more competition.”

“Competition…? So you’re after this treasure as well.”

Yuan’s eyes narrowed.

His voice hardened as he continued, “Then let me warn you clearly—if you do anything that might harm the Nine Heavens, I will stop you myself, just as I did with the Eternals I cast into slumber.”

Mu Xuelian’s eyes flickered as Shiva took control.

“You’ve put Eternals to sleep before? I’m very intrigued. Tell me more,” she said with a chilling smile on her face.

“I’ll tell you if you tell me what you’re looking for,” he said.

Shiva sighed and returned control to Mu Xuelian without uttering a word, a clear refusal to his demands.

“Don’t tell me then. I’ll figure it out eventually, anyway.”

’A human putting Eternals to sleep? How ridiculous…’ Shiva sneered inwardly.

Sometime later, they finally left White Hell’s territory.

“So this is the world beyond White Hell…” Mu Xuelian gazed at the endless greenery stretching toward the horizon, her face brimming with wonder and astonishment—resembling Xi Meili when she had first stepped out of the Ancient Dragon City.

“Is this your first time outside?” Yuan asked.

She nodded, “Besides the tribe chief, we can only leave White Hell when we look for partners.”

“Is there a particular reason such a rule exists?” Yuan asked.

Mu Xuelian answered calmly, “My mother said it’s tied to our physiques. While we possess a body that can endure—and even flourish—in extreme cold, the moment we leave such conditions, our strength begins to wane. If we remain outside for too long, our bodies will deteriorate… and eventually, we would cripple ourselves and die a painful death.”

Yuan looked at her with raised eyebrows and said, “Doesn’t that mean you won’t be able to follow me for too long?”

After a moment of silence, Mu Xuelian continued in a dazed voice, “According to Lord Shiva, that is no longer the case, as she’d just fixed the physique’s weakness as a reward for our loyalty, so we can leave without worry now. Also, as the chief’s daughter, my physique is different, so that weakness was never a problem for me.”

“Is that so…”

Sometime later, Yuan said, “Let’s stop by a city first so I can think about our next steps.”

“Didn’t you already decide to visit the Primordial Realm?” Mu Xuelian asked.

“Yes, but the Primordial Realm isn’t a place you can just visit as you please. It’s a heavily protected place. Also, I don’t know where it’s located, so I will need to acquire the directions first.”

“Okay.”

After reaching a city, Yuan found a restaurant and settled in a private room to eat his fill while quietly pondering his next steps.

Naturally, he ordered the entire menu.

“I ordered every dish on the menu, so you can pick whatever you want to eat,” Yuan said to Mu Xuelian, who silently nodded with a dazed look on her face.

When the dishes were brought out, Mu Xuelian’s eyes widened and her mouth fell open in astonishment at the colorful array before her. In White Hell, they could only eat the meat of magical beasts they hunted themselves, prepared in the simplest of ways—a far cry from the vibrant feast laid out before her now.

Without hesitation, she began trying every single dish on the table.

Seeing how much Mu Xuelian was enjoying the food, Yuan ordered the entire menu for a second time.

Of course, it wasn’t just the two of them. Yingzi, Feng Yuxiang, and Lan Yingying also came out to eat shortly later.

Mu Xuelian stared at the three who appeared like ghosts with a dumbfounded look on her face. “Who are they…? And where did they come from?”

“Oh, right. I hadn’t introduced you to my companions yet.”

Thus, Yuan began their introduction the very next moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 2177: Dusk Intelligence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2177: Dusk Intelligence

Chapter 2177: Dusk Intelligence

“My name is Feng Yuxiang. As you can probably guess from my aura, I am a phoenix, and I am the Young Master’s most loyal and number one servant,” Feng Yuxiang introduced herself first.

Lan Yingying went next.

“I’m Lan Yingying. I am also a beast—a Divine Serpent.”

“Yingzi.”

“They’re all your servants?” Mu Xuelian asked Yuan.

He shook his head and said, “Although Feng Yuxiang and Lan Yingying are bound to me through a contract, I don’t see them as servants. They’re my precious friends and companions. As for Yingzi, she’s just following me around as a traveling companion.”

“Is that so… I’m Mu Xuelian. Nice to meet you all.”

While they dined, Feng Yuxiang asked, “Young Master, if you don’t know where the Primordial Realm is, why don’t you just ask those guys from the Celestial Overlords?”

Yuan shook his head and sighed, “No, I cannot get them involved. They will also try to persuade me not to go, anyway. I’ll probably have to find an information broker—”

“Oh, right. There’s the Dusk Intelligence. Although we’re in the Eighth Heaven, they should also have a branch here.”

Feng Yuxiang sighed softly. “I don’t want to sound grim, but Xiao Hua’s condition is worsening. Lately, she’s been moving about more and more in her sleep, as though trapped in pain… or lost in a nightmare she cannot wake from. I’m worried.”

Yuan nodded with a solemn face, “That’s why we cannot delay our trip to the Primordial Realm any longer.”

Once they finished eating and paid the bill, Yuan began asking around about the Dusk Intelligence. Sure enough, they also existed in the Eighth Heaven.

Several teleportation formations later, Yuan found himself on the other side of the Eighth Heaven.

Rather than being nestled within a bustling city, the Dusk Intelligence Yuan sought was currently hidden away in a remote city, isolated in the middle of nowhere. The atmosphere in this place was much more gloomy and suspicious, almost like Yuan had found himself in a dark alley.

“What is your purpose in this city?” The guard of this city questioned Yuan when he got close to the gate.

“I’m here for the Dusk Intelligence,” he said.

“The Dusk Intelligence, huh?” The guard sighed.

“Is something wrong?” Yuan asked.

“You see, the Dusk Intelligence in our city has temporarily closed its doors, so nobody can enter. It has been like that for the last four years.”

“Did they say their reason for closing?”

“No, they just suddenly closed their doors.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before nodding, “Thanks for letting me know, but I will take a look for myself anyway.”

“Well, I can’t stop you. However, the fee for entering this city is—”

“Is a spirit stone enough?” Yuan showed the guard a single spirit stone.

“Y-Yes…” The guard nodded in a daze as he accepted the spirit stone.

Yuan and Mu Xuelian entered the city shortly after and made their way to Dusk Intelligence’s operating building.

Sure enough, when they arrived, the doors to the building were closed. Yet, someone was sweeping the ground outside.

Yuan calmly approached the young man cleaning and asked, “I have some business with the Dusk Intelligence.”

The young man gave him a brief glance before he returned to focusing on the ground.

“Can’t you see that the doors are closed? The Dusk Intelligence hasn’t opened for four years.”

“If it’s closed, why are you cleaning it?”

“Because I am getting paid to do it.”

“…”

Yuan suddenly retrieved a black medallion from his spatial ring and showed it to the young man.

“Is the Dusk Intelligence still closed?”

“Look here—”

The young man’s throat stiffened at the sight of the black medallion in Yuan’s grasp.

“T-Th-That’s the—!!!”

“Since you recognize this medallion, you must also be part of Dusk Intelligence.”

The young man immediately dropped his broom and bowed to Yuan, “Please forgive me for my rudeness. I wasn’t aware of your identity.”

“Forget it. Is the Dusk Intelligence really closed?” Yuan asked.

“Please, follow me.”

Without offering an explanation, the young man began walking away. Follow current novels on novel⟡fire.net

Yuan silently followed him to the back of the building, where another door was located.

The young man knocked on the door.

“What is it?” A cold voice resounded.

“The God of War has arrived.”

“What?!”

The door instantly swung open, and a middle-aged man walked out to confirm Yuan’s identity.

“Welcome, God of War! Please, we have been anticipating your arrival!” The middle-aged man said.

“You’ve been anticipating my arrival?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at his wording.

The middle-aged man nodded and said, “You’ll understand in a few minutes. Please, come inside.”

Yuan quietly nodded and entered the building.

Once they were inside, the middle-aged man brought Yuan to the top floor, where a teleportation formation existed. Not only that, it was already activated.

“What’s this?” Yuan questioned.

“The gateway to our—the Shadow Army’s hideout,” answered the middle-aged man without an ounce of hesitation in his voice.

“The Commander is waiting for you.”

“The Commander… Dong Ye?”

“That is correct.”

“Alright.”

Without hesitation, Yuan stepped onto the teleport formation and disappeared from the building.

When Mu Xuelian tried to follow, the middle-aged man hastily stopped her, “W-Wait a second. Who are you, and what is your relationship with the God of War? We weren’t expecting extra company…”

“I’m someone who will be carrying his child in the future,” Mu Xuelian calmly responded.

“Y-You must be his wife! I apologize!” The middle-aged man hastily stepped aside, allowing Mu Xuelian access to the teleport formation.

Without another word, Mu Xuelian stepped into the teleportation formation and vanished from the building. When she emerged moments later, she found herself standing in an entirely different city.

Yuan was standing right in front of her, and he appeared to be in a daze as he looked at their surroundings.

“This place is… the City of Xian…” he mumbled, a sense of nostalgia washing over him.

A minute later, before they could go anywhere, a figure appeared before them like a ghost.

“Welcome to the Shadow Army’s hideout, my liege.”

Dong Ye greeted him with a bow.

chapters.title: Chapter 2178: Primordial Realm’S Location
chapters.body:
Chapter 2178: Primordial Realm’S Location

Chapter 2178: Primordial Realm’s Location

“Dong Ye. It’s been a while,” Yuan returned the greeting with a warm smile.

“Indeed, it has.” Dong Ye nodded.

“I heard that you’ve anticipated my arrival.”

Dong Ye smiled and said, “I knew you’d come here sooner or later. Let’s talk somewhere more private, shall we?”

Yuan nodded.

As they walked through the empty streets of the city, Yuan asked, “Did you create this place?”

“No, I did not. As much as I loved the city I grew up in, I wouldn’t do something as disrespectful as recreating it without your approval.” Newest update provıded by n0velfire.net

“Then…”

“It was created by my liege,” Dong Ye revealed, confirming what was in Yuan’s mind.

“I see… Unfortunately, I still don’t have all of my memories as the God of War.”

“It’s fine. You’ll eventually recall everything. By the way, my liege, who is your new companion?”

“I’m someone who will be carrying his child in the future,” Mu Xuelian calmly responded, repeating what she said earlier.

“What?” Dong Ye turned to look at Yuan with raised eyebrows.

Yuan rubbed his eyes and sighed out loud, “It’s nothing like that. She’s from the White Hell Barbarian Tribe.”

“The White Hell Barbarian Tribe? No wonder she looks so familiar,” Dong Ye remarked.

“You know them?”

“How could I not? Someone from her tribe used to follow you around. I believe her name was Mu Hanyan…”

“That’s my grandmother,” Mu Xuelian said.

“Unbelievable… To think her granddaughter would be following you after so many years… Is she still alive?” Dong Ye asked.

“She’s still alive,” Mu Xuelian said.

Sometime later, as they drew closer to the towering pagoda in the distance, Yuan remarked, “This place is quiet. It’s like a ghost town.”

“Ah, that’s because most of them are in training. One doesn’t become part of our Shadow Army without extensive training, after all.”

“How do you find these people?” Yuan asked.

“From the streets. Most of them are either homeless or orphans.”

“I see… and how do you train them?”

“Believe it or not, this place is where those who have reached the very end of their training curriculum come to train. Naturally, we cannot allow anyone we do not trust to enter,” said Dong Ye.

“That’s why we begin by cultivating their loyalty. Since most of them come from the streets, it isn’t difficult to earn their trust. Still, we test them thoroughly for many years. Only when we are certain of their loyalty do we gradually reveal the truth—introducing them to the existence of the God of War and dispelling the false history woven by the previous Celestial Emperors.”

“Once we are certain they will be able to handle it, we invest our full resources into them—raising their cultivation and instructing them in the Shadow Army’s techniques.”

Yuan then asked, “What happens to those who fail?”

“What do you think we do with them?” Dong Ye asked with a mysterious smile on his face.

“I doubt you would do something as stupid as disposing of the failures… right?” Yuan narrowed his gaze at Dong Ye.

He immediately chuckled and said, “Of course not. I wouldn’t have lived this long if I had done such audacious acts behind your back. Anyway, to answer your question, we simply set them free.”

“I have the ability to eat memories, so I take away the memories of those who do not qualify to stay with us. However, it’s not like we abandon them afterward, either. We continue to support those who deserve it from the shadows, and those who do not… we simply let them go.”

“How does it work?” Yuan asked, his curiosity piqued.

“For example, we have a young boy who failed his training quite early on. After taking away the necessary memories, we sent him to a sect we manage, where he can continue his life as a cultivator. Of course, it’s not like we forced him on this path, either. We confirm what they want to do in their life before we take their memories.”

“As for the troublemakers who don’t deserve our support… we just toss them back into the street, where they belong.”

“I see… It’s not a perfect system, but nothing in this world is perfect. Most importantly, it helps people in need,” Yuan mumbled with a satisfied smile on his face.

Sometime later, they arrived at the pagoda.

“Invincible Heaven,” Yuan read the grand name of the pagoda hanging by the entrance.

Once inside, they were moved to the highest floor, where a throne stood—untouched for millions of years, yet without a single speck of dust upon it.

“Please, my liege.” Dong Ye gestured toward the throne.

Yuan stepped forward and seated himself upon it, his gaze sweeping through the open windows that offered an unobstructed view of every corner of the city.

“…”

Mu Xuelian gazed silently at Yuan. The instant he sat upon the throne, a profound and innate aura of dominance surged from him, stirring within her an almost irresistible urge to bow in reverence.

Tears streamed down Dong Ye’s face, and in that moment, he looked like a child who had finally been reunited with his parents after being lost for countless years.

“Dong Ye, do you know why I visited the Dusk Intelligence?” Yuan suddenly asked.

Dong Ye’s expression immediately turned serious as he nodded, “Does it have something to do with the Primordial Realm?”

Yuan nodded, “I want to know where it’s located.”

Dong Ye then said, “Do you know of a place called the Primordial Chaos?”

Yuan’s eyes narrowed slightly at this familiar name.

“Of course. Just like White Hell and the Vanishing Valley, it’s one of the Forbidden Zones. Don’t tell me the Primordial Realm is in such a dangerous place?”

“The Primordial Chaos is not only a Forbidden Zone, it’s the most dangerous Forbidden Zone, so it’s essentially the most dangerous place in the Nine Heavens. Unfortunately, the Primordial Realm is inside that place. Fortunately, it’s only at the outskirts,” Dong Ye revealed.

“How troublesome,” Yuan sighed at this information.

chapters.title: Chapter 2179: Primordial Chaos(End Of Immortal Monarch’S Return Arc)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2179: Primordial Chaos(End Of Immortal Monarch’S Return Arc)

Chapter 2179: Primordial Chaos(End of Immortal Monarch’s Return Arc)

The Primordial Chaos was among the most perilous places in the Nine Heavens, its unique environment cutting cultivators off from spiritual energy. Once their reserves were spent within its depths, they could not recover their strength until they escaped its boundaries.

Though the Primordial Chaos was technically part of the Nine Heavens, only its outermost edges lay within those boundaries. To venture any deeper was to step beyond the Nine Heavens’ protection and into the unknown. Moreover, the creatures that roamed within its depths were all at least of the God Ascension realm.

“Although the Primordial Chaos is a dangerous place, it’s not the location that’s the most worrying, but the Celestial Emperor’s dogs that are protecting that place,” Dong Ye sighed.

“It’s constantly guarded around the clock by at least ten God Ascension realm cultivators. Five of them are at the 5th level God Ascension, and two are at the 7th level.”

“Ten God Ascension experts, huh? That is indeed troublesome…” Yuan sighed, wondering how he would get past ten experts.

Seeing his reaction, Dong Ye confidently patted his chest and declared, “Don’t worry, my liege. I have spent the last few decades preparing for this exact purpose. The Shadow Army will distract the guards while you enter the Primordial Realm.”

“Won’t that alarm the Celestial Emperor, though? Even if I manage to get inside, getting out is my biggest issue, especially if you cause a scene right outside.”

Dong Ye smiled and said, “My liege, you’re underestimating how much we’ve prepared for this moment. I know I said decades, but in reality, we’ve been preparing for this moment since the Primordial Era.”

“What do you think the members of the Shadow Army do when they complete their training? They infiltrate the Celestial Emperor’s side as spies. Currently, three of the ten experts guarding the Primordial Realm are our people, so even if we get rid of the other seven, we won’t immediately alert the Celestial Emperor.”

“I see… and how long do you think I have inside the Primordial Realm?”

Dong Ye pondered for a moment before speaking, “The guards at the Primordial Realm are replaced once every one thousand years, and it has already been eight hundred years since the last replacement, so you have about two hundred years before the Celestial Emperor is alerted.”

“Two hundred years? That’s more than enough time,” Yuan nodded.

“When would you like to depart for the Primordial Realm?” Dong Ye then asked.

“As soon as possible.”

Dong Ye nodded and said, “In that case, please give me a few days to prepare for your escort.”

“Alright.”

“Then I’ll go prepare now. If you’ll excuse me—”

“Dong Ye.” Yuan suddenly stopped him.

“My liege?” Dong Ye turned around with a puzzled face.

A warm smile spread across Yuan’s face as he said, “Thank you for your efforts and sacrifices through all these years. When our battle is over, I swear upon my soul—I will repay you.”

Dong Ye’s eyes widened before he immediately dropped to his knees.

“My liege, this worthless life of mine has been yours ever since you saved me from the streets and bestowed upon me a purpose. From that day onward, all I have ever sought is to repay you, so there’s no need for you to say such words.”

“Even if you say that…” Yuan didn’t know how to respond as tears flowed from Dong Ye’s eyes.

Dong Ye’s past mirrored Tian Xian’s—growing up without a family, he spent much of his childhood living as a bandit. If it weren’t for Tian Xian, Dong Ye would’ve been executed long ago.

After Dong Ye departed, Yuan rose from the throne and walked to the window. As he drifted into thought, he suddenly became aware of a powerful gaze fixed upon him.

“Who are you?” Yuan asked out loud.

“…”

There was no response, and a moment later, the mysterious individual withdrew their gaze.

Yuan narrowed his eyes toward the distant mountain, where a lone silhouette stood upon the peak—gazing unmistakably in his direction.

However, this silhouette vanished like smoke before Yuan could do anything, disappearing like a ghost.

Yuan felt no concern that the figure posed any danger, as he doubted Dong Ye would allow such a threat into this place. Yet curiosity stirred within him, for the individual’s presence seemed even more formidable than Dong Ye’s.

When Dong Ye returned to his side a few days later, Yuan questioned him about the silhouette.

“A-A silhouette, you say…?” Dong Ye swallowed nervously.

Yuan looked at him with raised eyebrows.

“What’s with that reaction? Don’t tell me that was an intruder?”

“No, that’s not possible.” Dong Ye immediately dismissed that notion.

He continued, “I have an idea who you’re talking about, but I cannot disclose any information on that individual.”

Yuan became even more intrigued after hearing this. ᴛhis chapter is ᴜpdated by N0v3l.Fiɾe.net

“You can’t tell me? So that person is even more important than me, huh?” Yuan remarked with a smile.

Dong Ye’s eyes widened at his statement, and he hastily responded, “Not a chance! There’s nobody more important in my eyes than my liege! However, that person’s circumstances are unique…”

After a moment of silence, Dong Ye sighed and said, “I can tell you this, though. My liege, you are very familiar with that person.”

“I am? Then why are they keeping their distance?”

Dong Ye shook his head.

“I am not sure myself, but I’m sure they’ll approach you once they’re ready.”

“If you say so. In any case, have you finished with the preparations?”

“Yes, we can leave for the Primordial Realm at your command.”

“Let’s leave now, then.”

“Please, follow me.”

Yuan nodded and followed Dong Ye away.

Meanwhile, somewhere in the Shadow Army’s hideout, the silhouette mumbled in a longing tone, “Tian Yang…”

Some time later, Dong Ye led Yuan to their destination, where a carriage awaited them. Yuan’s brows rose—not at the carriage itself, but at the flag it bore.

“Isn’t that the Celestial Family’s flag?” he remarked.

chapters.title: Chapter 2180: Entering The Primordial Realm
chapters.body:
Chapter 2180: Entering The Primordial Realm

Chapter 2180: Entering the Primordial Realm

“It is the Celestial Family’s flag,” Dong Ye confirmed with a calm smile on his face.

“This way, we’ll be able to approach the Primordial Realm without being stopped.”

“Impersonating the Celestial Family, huh? Most people wouldn’t even dream of doing something like this,” Yuan chuckled.

Not even madmen would dare impersonate the Celestial Family—the most powerful clan in the Nine Heavens. For those bold enough to try, death would be the least of their concerns.

However, offending the Celestial Emperor was the Shadow Army’s specialty.

“Greetings to the God of War.”

Aside from the carriage, there was a group of God Ascension experts there.

“They will not only escort you to the Primordial Realm but will also act as a distraction,” Dong Ye said.

“It is an honor to finally be of assistance to the God of War,” they all said, bowing to him.

Yuan acknowledged with a nod, “I’ll be relying on you guys.”

After Yuan and Mu Xuelian stepped into the carriage, Dong Ye swallowed an appearance-altering pill before taking his place at the front as the rider.

Moments later, they left the hideout and appeared in a location that was entirely different from the building Yuan had entered from.

Then, without hesitation, they soared toward the sky, entering the Starry Sky hours later.

“My liege, it’ll take two years just to get there, even at our speed, so make yourself comfortable,” Dong Ye said.

“Alright.”

Yuan retrieved the Mystic Sword Tablet to study it.

As for Mu Xuelian, she spent most of her time staring out the window with a constant look of wonder, as this was her first time entering the Starry Sky.

Time flew by, and in the blink of an eye, almost two years have passed.

“My liege, I should’ve probably asked you this sooner, but you have the Four Ancient Seals, correct?”

“I do.”

“We’re almost at our destination, so if you could, assemble the Ancient Seals now so you don’t have to do it later.”

“I have to assemble the seals?”

The next moment, Yuan retrieved the Four Ancient Seals and examined them closely. At first glance, each appeared whole on its own, yet when placed side by side, it became clear—they were fragments meant to fit together into a greater whole.

<You have assembled the Four Ancient Seals, creating an Ancient Key>

Despite its name, the Ancient Key did not resemble a key at all. If anything, it looked like a large seal stamp.

A month later, Dong Ye’s voice resounded, “My liege, we’re almost at the Primordial Chaos. Please prepare yourself.”

A few minutes later, Yuan realized that the spiritual energy in the air had suddenly vanis hed.

<You have discovered Primordial Chaos>

<You have entered Primordial Chaos>

For cultivators, a world devoid of spiritual energy was no different from fish stranded without water. Without spiritual energy, once a cultivator’s reserves were depleted, their dantian would gradually wither—and given enough time, the cultivator themselves would perish.

“I cannot sense any spiritual energy…” Mu Xuelian was not used to such an environment and instinctively began to panic.

As for Yuan…

<Chaotic Dragon Core has activated>

<You have begun refining Chaotic Essence>

Though the Primordial Chaos was devoid of spiritual energy, it was anything but empty, and it brimmed with Chaotic Essence in overwhelming abundance.

Yingzi emerged from his shadow after sensing the familiar energy.

“This is Chaotic Essence,” she remarked after quickly recognizing it.

She looked at Yuan and questioned, “Have we returned to the Primal Expanse?”

He shook his head and explained, “No, we’re still in the Nine Heavens.”

Yingzi walked to the window and stared outside.

“But this feeling… It’s the same as the Primal Expanse,” she muttered.

Meanwhile, somewhere in the outer reaches of the Primordial Chaos, ten God Ascension realm experts sat in silent cultivation, encircling a massive black metal door that loomed alone in the desolate expanse.

However, since there was no spiritual energy for them to absorb, these God Ascension cultivators were merely doing mental cultivation and not actually cultivating.

Suddenly, the expert at the seventh level of God Ascension opened his eyes and spoke out loud, “We have company.”

The other nine opened their eyes at his words.

“That flag… the Celestial Family. Was there an inspection scheduled for today?” Official source ıs NoveI(F)ire.net

The experts showed no alarm at the sight, for the Celestial Family frequently came to inspect the Primordial Realm.

“It might be a surprise inspection.”

“I’ll go speak with them,” said one of the fifth-level God Ascension experts as he rose to his feet and approached the carriage.

Sometime later, the cultivator returned and said, “It’s a surprise inspection. Prepare to greet the Celestial Family.”

“What a pain in the ass.” One of them complained in a low voice.

Soon, all ten of them left their post and approached the carriage.

“We greet the Celestial Family.”

However, in response, the experts guarding the carriage suddenly encircled them.

“What is the meaning of this?” one of them questioned with a deep frown.

Still concealed by his disguise, Dong Ye suddenly drew out a treasure and activated it without warning. In the next instant, a massive barrier materialized, enclosing everyone within its confines.

“T-This is—! It’s an ambush!”

After confirming the guards were trapped, Dong Ye turned to the carriage and said, “My liege, you’re good to go. We’ll take care of things from here.”

Yuan and Mu Xuelian emerged from the carriage the next moment and immediately flew toward the metal black door in the distance.

“They’re here for the Primordial Realm!”

Even upon realizing this, the guards did not panic. Instead, mocking laughter erupted from them.

“Haha! What a futile effort! Without the Ancient Key, not even a Cultivation God can open the Primordial Realm!”

But the moment they saw Yuan draw forth the Ancient Key, their laughter died instantly, their faces twisting into shock and horror.

“Why does he have the Ancient Key?!”

“Impossible!”

“W-We must stop him from opening the Primordial Realm!”

The guards erupted with power at once, unleashing the full extent of their cultivation without hesitation.

“Kill them,” Dong Ye’s commanding voice resounded the next moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 2181 Inside The Primordial Realm
chapters.body:
Chapter 2181 Inside The Primordial Realm

Upon hearing Dong Ye’s command, the Shadow Army began its fight with the guards of the Primordial Realm.

“We cannot let that crazy bastard open the Primordial Realm and let the criminals inside loose!” The guards fought with desperate ferocity, as though not only their own lives, but the lives of their families, hung in the balance.

The Primordial Realm housed countless dangerous and heinous criminals, and none of them dared even to imagine what would happen if these criminals were set free in the Nine Heavens.

Meanwhile, upon reaching the entrance of the Primordial Realm, Yuan poured his spiritual energy into the Ancient Key, activating it. Then, without the slightest hesitation, he drove it into the waiting keyhole.

The sealed doors opened, causing the entire ground to tremble.

“Let’s go.”

Yuan entered the Primordial Realm with Mu Xuelian following behind.

Once Yuan and Mu Xuelian’s figures disappeared into the Primordial Realm, the doors closed again.

“What? They didn’t fully open the Primordial Realm and instead went inside?” The guards were left dumbfounded by the unexpected development, as they thought Yuan was there to release the criminals.

“Who the hell are you bastards and what is your goal here?! Do you have any idea what you’ve done?!” The seventh-level God Ascension expert roared at Dong Ye, his opponent.

“We are the Shadow Army, and we are here to piss off the Celestial Emperor,” Dong Ye laughed out loud.

“The Evil God’s Shadow Army?!” The guards instantly recognized their infamous name.

“Hah! Kill us then! Let’s see what will happen afterward!”

Dong Ye shook his head and said, “Although I told them to kill you guys, we’re not really going to kill you. Do you think we aren’t aware that the Celestial Emperor is tracking your lives with a Life Tablet and that if we kill you, it would immediately alert him?”

“We won’t kill you for now, but we will destroy your physical body in case you try anything.”

In the middle of their fight, three of the guards, who were Shadow Army spies, suddenly betrayed the other seven.

“What the fuck are you three doing?!”

“They must be spies!”

“Do you think you’ll get away with this?!”

With the betrayal and the Shadow Army’s overwhelming might, the fight ended in the blink of an eye.

The Shadow Army destroyed the guards’ physical bodies but left their souls alone so that the Life Tablet tracking their lives wouldn’t activate.

“Great job, everyone. However, we’re far from done,” said Dong Ye.

He proceeded to use his memory-stealing abilities on the guards, stealing their identities. “The Celestial Family does random inspections, and we must be ready for them when that happens,” he said afterward.

“Yes, commander!”

Meanwhile, Yuan and Mu Xuelian appeared inside the Primordial Realm.

“What is this place…?” Mu Xuelian murmured, her eyes wide with curiosity and disbelief as she studied their surroundings. Above them stretched a pitch-black sky, where streaks of crimson lightning slithered like serpents through the void.

The land was even more dreadful—a desolate plain of scorched earth, fractured with countless cracks, as though it bore the aftermath of an ancient apocalypse.

Yet, despite the dark skies, the red lightning appeared frequently enough to constantly illuminate the world.

*Cough* *Cough*

Mu Xuelian suddenly started coughing.

“The air outside was already awful, but in here it’s even worse! The atmosphere feels suffocating!” she exclaimed, raising her hands to cover her mouth.

Yuan faced her and shrugged, “I suggested you not follow me in here earlier, but you wanted to be stubborn.”

He looked at the sky and sighed, “But this place is definitely very… tragic. Not only does this place lack spiritual energy, but the Chaotic Essence is at least ten times denser than outside.”

“There’s no way any cultivator can survive in this environment. Why did you want to come here?”

Yuan shook his head and said, “No, there are definitely people living in here. I can sense someone not too far away from us.”

“You’re using divine sense? Aren’t you worried that you’ll exhaust your spiritual energy? You can’t replenish it in this place, after all,” Mu Xuelian asked.

“I don’t have any issues replenishing my spiritual energy since I can refine the Chaotic Essence in this place. Ah, Chaotic Essence is what you’re sensing right now, and it cannot be refined by humans.”

“As Lord Shiva said… you’re not human…” Mu Xuelian stared at him with a dumbfounded face.

“So, what’s next?” she asked a moment later.

“Ask for directions,” he answered, his gaze shifting toward the presence he had already sensed.

Just as Yuan began to take flight, Mu Xuelian suddenly shouted, “Wait!”

“Hm?” Yuan turned around to see Mu Xuelian glaring at him from the ground.

“Oh, right. You cannot replenish your spiritual energy, so it’d be wise to save it for when you truly need it.”

Yuan made a gesture toward her figure, and the next moment, Mu Xuelian’s body lifted off the ground and flew beside him.

“Thank you,” she said.

Yuan silently nodded before flying away with Mu Xuelian.

They flew for hours without encountering so much as a single tree—or any sign of life at all. Newest update provıded by novel•ﬁre.net

Some time later, Yuan halted before a half-destroyed mountain, its upper half completely gone, while the remaining portion was riddled with cracks, as though it might collapse into ruin at any moment.

“Wait here,” Yuan instructed Mu Xuelian before striding toward the lone cave in the distance. When he was halfway there, a silhouette emerged from its depths.

When the figure finally stepped out of the cave, it was revealed to be a gaunt, middle-aged man. His limbs were so frail and thin they seemed as though they might snap under the mere weight of his own stance.

Yet, his eyes were filled with life and killing intent.

Seeing Yuan’s healthy appearance, the man frowned and said, “There’s nothing for you to take here.”

Yuan responded with a calm smile, “I’m just here for directions.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2182 Inside The Primordial Realm(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2182 Inside The Primordial Realm(2)

“You’re here to ask for directions…?” The skinny man wore a dumbfounded look on his face after hearing Yuan’s words.

He focused on Yuan’s appearance again. In the Primordial Realm, due to its chaotic environment, having a healthy appearance was a privilege accessible only to the most powerful individuals and families.

But what truly set Yuan apart from the ordinary dwellers of the Primordial Realm was the vast reservoir of spiritual energy within him. Though he tried to conceal it, those who had long been starved of such energy could instinctively sense its presence.

Yet a privileged background did not mean one was above resorting to robbery. In the Primordial Realm, where resources were scarce and the law of the jungle reigned supreme, even the lofty could be driven to desperate means. In fact, it was usually the most powerful individuals who took advantage of their privilege. “Who… Who are you looking for?”

“The Asura Clan,” Yuan calmly answered.

“The—?!”

The man’s eyes widened in shock and disbelief at the mention of the Asura Clan, as though he had just laid eyes on a ghost.

“You’re seeking the Asura Clan?! Get lost! How dare you toy with me!” the man roared, slashing the air with his arm.

Yuan tilted his head, his expression puzzled. “What makes you think I’m joking? I’m being serious.”

“Nonsense! Nobody in this world would willingly seek out the Asura Clan—not even the insane!”

“I’m not insane, but I am seriously looking for the Asura Clan,” Yuan shrugged.

“N-No… I don’t know why you’re looking for the Asura Clan, but I want no involvement.”

Without another word, the skinny man turned around and walked back inside the cave, his figure disappearing into the darkness.

“…”

“It seems finding the Asura Clan will take more effort than I expected…” Yuan sighed.

He turned to leave, heading back toward Mu Xuelian. But after only a few steps, he halted, a strange sensation prickling across his back, forcing him to turn around.

When Yuan turned, he found the skinny man—who had vanished into the cave—now standing behind him, a dagger pressed firmly against his back.

“What is the meaning of this?” Yuan asked, his voice calm and steady.

“I–Impossible…!” the man muttered, disbelief etched across his face. Ignoring Yuan’s words, he lashed out again, this time with even greater force.

However, Yuan had no intention of simply standing still, even if the strike posed no threat to him. In a swift motion, he twisted his body, extended his arm, and clamped his hand firmly around the man’s neck, lifting him into the air.

“How?! You’re just a mere True Immortal!” The skinny man shouted.

Despite his frail appearance, he was a cultivator at the first level of God Ascension. In his mind, it was impossible for an Immortal to withstand his strike.

“I don’t think I have done anything deserving of this backstab,” Yuan said.

After snapping out of his daze, the skinny man laughed out loud, “You speak like someone from the outside. This is the Primordial Realm! Do I need any reason to attack someone so defenseless?!”

Yuan narrowed his eyes and said, “I’ll give you a chance to live. Tell me where I can find the Asura Clan, and I’ll spare your life for your offense.”

The man opened his mouth—not to speak, but to spit.

“Hak-too!” he snarled, hurling the glob straight at Yuan’s face.

Yuan calmly blocked the spit with spiritual energy.

“Hahaha! What a waste of spiritual energy!”

Without a word, Yuan clenched his fist, crushing the man’s neck. However, the man wasn’t dead yet. As a God Ascension cultivator, unless Yuan destroyed his soul, he would remain alive.

Even so, in the Primordial Realm—where cultivation resources were almost nonexistent—reconstructing a body was impossible for the weak.

Yuan nonchalantly tossed the body on the ground and returned to Mu Xuelian’s side.

“Quite ruthless,” she remarked.

“He struck first,” Yuan replied calmly. “Besides, most who dwell in this realm are criminals deserving of death.”

“So, what now?” The rıghtful source is NoveI-Fire.ɴet

“Look for someone else who can help us.”

Yuan looked around and continued a moment later, “Unfortunately, I don’t sense anyone else nearby, so we can only wander around until we encounter someone.”

After picking up Mu Xuelian, Yuan flew away and continued their search.

‘Although that man was weak for a God Ascension cultivator, he still had some power in him. The Primordial Realm hasn’t opened since the Primordial Era, so how did that man cultivate, much less reach God Ascension?’ Yuan pondered as they traveled.

He couldn’t fathom how cultivators survive in this environment. Also, there was the existence of Exiles, people who are allowed outside for a chance of freedom through the Supreme Heaven’s Legacy.

Sometime later, Yuan stopped moving when he noticed a drastic change in the atmosphere.

“Do you sense that too?” Mu Xuelian asked with her brows slightly raised.

He nodded.

“Something is about to happen,” Yuan murmured, tilting his head toward the blackened sky, where the lightning serpents were converging upon a single point.

Moments later, he could sense several presences appearing from a distance, all of them rushing in their direction.

“People are coming.”

A few minutes later, four cultivators appeared before Yuan and Mu Xuelian. Unlike the skinny man they previously encountered, these four cultivators appeared a little healthier. Two of them were True Immortals, while the other two were at the first and second levels of God Ascension.

“Scram if you don’t want to die,” said the second-level God Ascension cultivator.

“…”

“Why don’t you scram?” said the other God Ascension cultivator.

The two True Immortals didn’t say anything, but it was clear they also had no intention of leaving.

“I will not warn you again,” said the second-level God Ascension cultivator.

“Let’s not be too greedy. There are only a few of us here, so we can share it. Even if you fight and defeat us all, you might lose more than you gain,” said one of the True Immortals.

The second-level God Ascension cultivator frowned and fell silent to ponder.

chapters.title: Chapter 2183 Inside The Primordial Realm(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2183 Inside The Primordial Realm(3)

As the second-level God Ascension cultivator pondered, his gaze fell upon Yuan and Mu Xuelian. Although Yuan was only a True Immortal, his appearance and abundant spiritual energy implied that he came from a powerful family. However, what truly surprised him was Mu Xuelian’s existence.

There were many God Ascension realm cultivators within the Primordial Realm, but those who had as much spiritual energy as Mu Xuelian were incredibly rare, including those with a much higher cultivation than hers.

‘Besides that white-haired woman, I can defeat the others, but it’ll exhaust too much energy. Since there are only a few of us, there’s no need to risk it.’

The second-level God Ascension cultivator didn’t acknowledge it openly, but he decided not to chase the others away.

Minutes later, the massed lightning within the clouds erupted, unleashing a single devastating bolt that descended like a heavenly judgment.

The bolt was so intense it ripped through the very fabric of space, leaving behind a rift that seethed with turbulent spiritual energy.

Seeing this, the cultivators immediately began absorbing the spiritual energy, replenishing what little reserve they had left.

Yuan and Mu Xuelian watched with intrigue, as they’d never seen anything like this before.

‘So this is why that cultivator wanted everyone else to leave…’ Yuan thought to himself, finally understanding the situation.

However, he still had questions, such as where the spiritual energy was coming from.

‘Why aren’t those two cultivating and just watching? Are they mocking us?’ The cultivators wondered when they realized Yuan and Mu Xuelian were idling not far away.

‘They might attack us while we’re cultivating…’

Worried that Yuan and Mu Xuelian might attack out of nowhere, the cultivators remained vigilant.

A few minutes later, Yuan suddenly spoke, startling the others.

“Hey, I have a question. Do you know where I can find the Asura Clan?”

“?!?!”

The cultivators snapped their heads toward Yuan, their faces frozen in disbelief. Yet after a brief silence, they quickly averted their gazes, acting as though they had never heard his words at all.

Seeing this, Yuan started to absorb the spiritual energy from the rift at a terrifying rate, shocking the cultivators.

‘What the hell is that insane absorption rate?! At this rate, he’ll absorb all of the spiritual energy in minutes!’ they cried inwardly.

“If nobody answers, I will continue to absorb the spiritual energy,” Yuan said.

“I will answer—!” one of the True Immortals hastily said.

However, before he could answer Yuan’s question, a powerful presence emerged nearby, swiftly closing in on their location. Even before the figure came into view, an aggressive voice thundered through the air, “If anyone still dares to absorb that spiritual energy after this warning, I’ll skin you alive when I get there in a minute.”

“This presence… It’s a fifth-level God Ascension cultivator!”

Everyone there subconsciously halted absorbing the spiritual energy, and the True Immortals even prepared to flee, as this fifth-level God Ascension cultivator seemed to have plenty of spiritual energy just by how he was willing to use voice transmission.

“Before you leave, can you answer my question?” Yuan said, snapping them out of their daze. Follow current ɴᴏᴠᴇʟs on novelFirｅ.net

“S-Screw you! If I cannot acquire any more spiritual energy, what’s the point of answering you and offending the Asura Clan?!”

Then, the True Immortal turned around and flew away. The other True Immortal also left silently.

As for the God Ascension cultivators, they exchanged glances before turning to look at Mu Xuelian and said, “If the three of us worked together, we might be able to deal with this bastard.”

Mu Xuelian raised an eyebrow and asked, “Why should I work with you? This is your problem, not mine.”

The second-level God Ascension cultivator frowned and responded, “Why the hell are you here if not for the spiritual energy?”

“We were just passing by when this phenomenon happened. I don’t need the spiritual energy, anyway,” she calmly responded.

“You don’t need the spiritual energy…?” Not just the second-level God Ascension cultivator, but even the other guy trembled in anger after hearing her words.

In the Primordial Realm, nearly every cultivator was starved for spiritual energy. For someone to claim they had no need of it was like boasting of never needing food because they are always full and have never starved before, while standing before the homeless, gaunt with hunger.

The God Ascension cultivators wanted to tear Mu Xuelian apart after hearing her words, but they controlled themselves, as they would gain nothing from it, not to mention the incoming threat.

“Hmph! If you want the spiritual energy, you can fight for it!”

Without hesitation, the second-level God Ascension cultivator began absorbing the spiritual energy again.

Seeing this, the other cultivator also followed.

“Courting death!”

The fifth-level God Ascension cultivator arrived at the scene a few minutes later. Upon his arrival, he immediately unleashed a powerful martial technique at the two who ignored his warning.

“This crazy bastard!”

The two cultivators hastily stopped their cultivation to block the incoming attack.

“Fuck! My spiritual energy!”

While they were able to block the attack properly, it had consumed more spiritual energy than what they had absorbed from the rift.

“Hahaha! This is what you fools get for defying my warning! Now, die for your sins!” The fifth-level God Ascension cultivator laughed madly as he continued his assault on the other two.

“Damn it! I’m not going to waste my spiritual energy on a madman! It’s not worth it!”

The second-level God Ascension cultivator did not hesitate to run away.

Seeing this, the first-level God Ascension cultivator also fled.

“…”

The fifth-level God Ascension cultivator didn’t chase after them. He turned to look at Yuan and Mu Xuelian for a brief moment before ignoring them to absorb the spiritual energy from the rift.

However, Yuan also started absorbing the spiritual energy the very next moment.

“What the fuck do you think you’re doing, you little brat?!”

Yuan responded with a calm smile, “If you tell me where I can find the Asura Clan, I’ll let you have all of the spiritual energy here. If not, I don’t mind fighting to the death.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2184 Asura Clan'S Location
chapters.body:
Chapter 2184 Asura Clan'S Location

The man’s expression darkened after hearing the name Asura Clan.

“What did you just say?! The Asura Clan?! Are you insane? How dare you utter their name before me! You must be trying to get me killed!”

Yuan narrowed his eyes slightly and responded, “What are you so afraid of? You’re not the first person I’ve asked about the Asura Clan, but you all have the same terrified reaction, almost as if you’ll die just for talking about them.”

“What nonsense are you spouting? Anyone living in this forsaken world would tremble at their name! Unless… you’re not from the Primordial Realm? No… that can’t be.”

For a brief moment, the man considered the possibility that Yuan had come from the outside, but he quickly dismissed the thought as impossible. After all, the Primordial Realm has been closed since the Primordial Realm.

“So what if I am from the outside world?” Yuan suddenly said.

The man’s eyes widened with shock and disbelief.

“You… are you serious?” The man swallowed nervously, as he couldn’t imagine what sort of heinous crime Yuan had committed to force the Celestial Emperor to throw him in an abandoned prison like the Primordial Realm.

Yuan silently nodded in acknowledgement.

‘No wonder his appearance is so clean… but he’s only a True Immortal. What could he have done to—No, I shouldn’t involve myself in his business. He’s too dangerous.’

“Why are you looking for the Asura Clan?” the man then asked.

“I just have some business with them. Why does it matter to you?”

The man frowned and said, “I want no involvement in your search for the Asura Clan if you’re trying to fight them. If they kill you and discover I was the one who pointed you their way, I’ll be as good as dead myself.”

‘Is that why everyone was afraid to tell me the directions? Because they’re afraid that I might fight them?’ Yuan wondered to himself.

He then smiled and said, “Don’t worry, I’m not going to fight them.”

The man pondered for a moment before pointing toward the south and saying, “You can find the Asura Clan at the border of the Spirit Tree.”

“Spirit Tree, right? Thanks for the information.”

Yuan stopped absorbing the spiritual energy.

“Before I leave, I have one more question. Where is the nearest city?”

The man then pointed west and said, “There’s one a week away in that direction.”

Yuan didn’t linger and left with Mu Xuelian shortly after.

Once Yuan left, the man sighed in relief.

“For the first time since the Primordial Era, someone new has been tossed inside the Primordial Realm… I can only hope this is just a one-time event.”

The man worried over the implications. If the current Celestial Emperor were to make use of the Primordial Realm again, it could plunge the realm into chaos—disrupting the fragile society and norms its residents had managed to build through many eras.

“Let’s not overthink it and hurry up and consume the spiritual energy before someone else comes…”

Meanwhile, Yuan and Mu Xuelian sped toward the nearest city, arriving at their destination in under a day of travel.

Although the man said it would take a week, he was talking about walking distance since cultivators in the Primordial Realm only fly when it’s an emergency, as it would be a waste.

Once they arrived in the city, Yuan used his divine sense to scan the city. He wanted to look for a teleport formation, but he didn’t find one even after searching the entire city.

“Looks like we will have to go to another city,” Yuan said to Mu Xuelian.

Yuan shifted his attention to a nearby pedestrian, who was a young man and a pure mortal.

“Hey, can I ask you a question?”

The young man turned to face Yuan, and the instant he saw his appearance, shock overtook his face.

“How may this lowly one serve you?!” he cried, dropping to his knees and kowtowing before Yuan.

Yuan raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner at the young man’s unexpected reaction and questioned, “Do you recognize me?”

The young man, not daring to raise his head, responded, “No, I do not!”

“Then why are you reacting like that?”

“Because you are a cultivator!” “…”

“He’s probably like that because cultivators are rare in the Primordial Realm. In a world where spiritual energy rarely exists, I highly doubt many would even attempt to become one,” Yu Ning remarked.

“That makes sense…” Yuan mumbled, finding Yu Ning’s words very logical.

“Anyway, I would like to ask you… Do you know which city has a teleport formation?” he then asked the young man.

“A… what?” The young man responded in a confused tone, almost as if he’d never heard of this term.

“I don’t think teleport formations exist in this world,” Yu Ning suddenly said.

“If you think about it, teleport formations can only be activated with spiritual energy. Even if they somehow manage to activate it, they’ll still need spiritual energy to teleport.”

“…”

Yuan became speechless when he realized that he’d overlooked this fact.

“Senior Immortal?”

The young man spoke when Yuan didn’t say anything for some time.

Yuan snapped out of his daze and asked, “Then do you know how far away the Spirit Tree is?”

“The legendary Spirit Tree, where only the most powerful cultivators go?! I’m sorry. While I have heard of its name, I don’t know where it’s located. I reckon that only powerful cultivators will know since it’s not something us ordinary mortals even dream of.”

“Is that so…”

Yuan pondered for a moment before speaking again, “Hey, I have another favor. Can you tell me more about this world? I’ll treat you to a meal for your assistance.”

“Really?!”

Yuan nodded, “I’ll even let you choose the restaurant. Let’s talk while we eat.”

“Okay!”

“…” ʀᴇᴀᴅ ʟᴀᴛᴇsᴛ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀᴛ N0veI.Fiɾe.net

After a moment of awkward silence, the young man asked, “Is it okay for me to stand up now?”

“Of course. You don’t need to be so formal, either. Just treat me like a friendly neighbor or something,” Yuan said.

chapters.title: Chapter 2185 Primordial Realm'S Three Region
chapters.body:
Chapter 2185 Primordial Realm'S Three Region

“I have never met a cultivator as kind as you, Senior Brother,” said the young man as he led Yuan to a nearby restaurant.

“Are you sure you want to eat at this place?” Yuan asked with a raised eyebrow after seeing the rundown building the young man had brought him to.

“Don’t let its crummy appearance fool you, Senior Brother. This shop has some of the best noodles in this city—and the cities nearby.”

“Alright. I’ll trust you.”

Yuan scanned the restaurant with his divine sense just in case the young man was luring him into a trap. Of course, he wasn’t worried about being ambushed in the slightest. Fortunately, the restaurant was clear, and he found nothing suspicious.

Once they were seated inside, the young man ordered for them.

“Boss, let me get three large bowls of noodles with meat dumplings.”

“Three large bowls of noodles with meat dumplings on the way,” said the old man working behind the counter.

“Now, what did you want to know about this world? I may look young, but I am quite knowledgeable, if I may say so myself. I even know some things that only cultivators would know,” said the young man in a boastful tone.

“You see, I live in a secluded part of this world and only recently began to expand my views, so I don’t know much about the situation of the rest of the world.”

“In that case, should I tell you what I know about our world?”

Yuan nodded.

“Very well,” the young man began. “As you likely already know, we now live in the Primordial Realm—a place once used to imprison criminals during the Primordial Era. At first, the criminals fought endlessly among themselves, but after tens of thousands of years of bloodshed, they came to realize they might never leave. So, they ceased their battles and instead turned their efforts toward building a society of their own.”

“Now, the Primordial Realm is controlled by four powerful backgrounds. The Zhao Family, Chaotic Sect, the Inferno Alchemists, and the Asura Clan. Not only do they each rule over the majority of the Primordial Realm, but they are absolute powers that even cultivators cannot defy—especially the Asura Clan. Despite having the smallest population amongst the powerhouses, the Asura Clan is undoubtedly the strongest in the Primordial Realm.”

“With that said, the Primordial Realm is split into three regions. The Realm of Chaos, ruled by the Chaotic Sect. Zhao Province, ruled by the Zhao Family. The final region is the Land of Inferno, ruled by the Inferno Alchemists. Although the Asura Clan is a powerhouse, they only rule a small area of the Primordial Realm. However, the place they rule is arguably the most important area in this entire world.”

“The Spirit Tree?” Yuan said.

The young man nodded and elaborated, “The Spirit Tree is the only place in this world that lets cultivators… well, cultivate. Of course, there are the Spiritual Rifts, but those are rare and random, so cultivators cannot rely on them.”

“Spiritual Rifts? Is that when lightning strikes and tears through space, releasing spiritual energy?” Yuan asked.

“Yes, that’s what I heard. Unfortunately, I have never personally witnessed it, even with my 20 years of experience.”

Yuan then asked, “How spacious is the Primordial Realm? Let’s say I want to get to the other side of the world. How long would that take?”

The young man pondered for a moment before responding, “I don’t think the Primordial Realm is that big. From what I’ve heard through traveling merchants, it could take up to 10 years to travel around the world on foot, so it’s probably not that big for cultivators who can fly.”

“Only ten years on foot? The Primordial Realm is quite small, then. By the way, which region are we currently in?”

“The Realm of Chaos.”

“I see.”

The owner of the restaurant approached the table with their noodles shortly after. The latest_epɪ_sodes are on_the novel-fire.net

“What do you think?” the young man asked.

“It’s actually delicious. What is this meat? I have never tasted anything like this before,” Yuan remarked as he gazed at the meat dumplings.

Before the young man could respond, Yingzi suddenly emerged from Yuan’s shadow.

“Whoa!” The young man was startled by Yingzi’s sudden appearance and stared at her beautiful face with a dazed look.

“What’s up?” Yuan looked at her.

Yingzi pointed at the meat dumpling and said, “I want to taste it.”

“Go ahead.”

Yuan handed her a dumpling.

After tasting the meat, she responded, “As I thought, I have tasted this meat before. It’s from a common beast in the Primal Expanse.”

“What? Really?” Yuan’s eyes widened at this information.

Yingzi nodded, “I am certain. I have feasted on them a few times before.”

“…”

Yuan fell silent to ponder the implications.

‘Beings from the Primal Expanse can be found in the Primordial Realm? Just what is this place, anyway?’ he wondered inwardly.

“Anyway, let’s order some more. I won’t be satisfied with just one.”

Yuan gestured at the owner and said, “I want 20 more of this.”

“T-Twenty?!” The old man’s eyes widened.

“Um… I’ll need you to pay upfront if you’re going to order that much food…”

Yuan nodded, “How much?”

“Including the three bowls I’ve already served, 23 small chaos coins.”

“Uhh…”

The old man frowned at Yuan’s reaction and said, “You don’t have the money?”

“Wait just a minute…”

Yuan turned to the young man and asked, “Do you have any money on you?”

“What?! Don’t tell me you actually don’t have any money?! Then why did you say you’ll treat me?!” The young man exclaimed.

“I do have money. I just don’t think they’ll work here since I have never heard of ‘chaos coins’.”

“Each region has their own currency, but it can be used in other regions,” said the young man.

Yuan retrieved some gold coins and showed them to the young man, “What about this? It’s gold coins.”

“Gold coins?! That’s ancient currency!” the young man exclaimed, his eyes wide with disbelief.

chapters.title: Chapter 2186 A Single Gold Coin
chapters.body:
Chapter 2186 A Single Gold Coin

“I-Is that real?” the young man stammered after hearing it was a gold coin. Most of the earliest people cast into the Primordial Realm had arrived empty-handed, but in the chaos, a few managed to smuggle in their treasures and valuables. As a result, items like gold coins, spirit stones, and other treasures did exist in the Primordial Realm—but they were exceedingly rare and priceless.

This was especially true given that many eras had passed since the Primordial Era, and most of such valuables had long since been claimed by the powerful or lost to the ravages of time.

“Of course, it’s real.” Yuan nodded. “Anyway, can I pay with this?”

“Pay with the gold coin?! I apologize for my language, but are you insane?! A single one of those coins can turn a poor family into a rich one! If you’re going to use that gold coin to buy noodles, I’d rather you give me the gold coin and I pay for the noodles for you! Hell, I’ll even go into debt to pay for your noodles!” said the young man, making his voice as low as possible even though they were the only customers inside the restaurant. Yuan smiled and said, “Let’s do that then.”

He placed the gold coin on the table and calmly slid it in front of the young man. Google seaʀᴄh noveⅼfire.net

“…”

The young man didn’t immediately accept the coin and could only stare at it with a dazed face. He couldn’t comprehend how Yuan could casually give away such a precious item.

‘Am I being scammed right now?’ he wondered inwardly.

However, the possibility of a cultivator scamming a mortal for some noodles was just as inconceivable. ‘Whatever. Even if I might go broke, I’m willing to take the risk!’

After a moment of hesitation, the young man grabbed the gold coin and immediately put it inside his pocket.

“Sorry for the delay, owner. I’ll pay for the noodles right now.”

The young man got up from the table and went to pay the old man.

“Is that your friend?” the old man asked as he accepted the money, a slight frown on his face.

“Something like that.”

The old man shook his head and sighed, “He’s sketchy. Don’t get involved with people like him. I’m warning you because you’re a regular at my restaurant.”

“Thank you for the advice…” The young man didn’t argue and returned to the table to wait for the food.

“Do you have any more coins? I’d like to take a look at one.” Yuan suddenly asked.

“Here… It’s my last coin…”

The young man handed Yuan a small black coin with a hint of purple mixed within, its surface gleaming with a crystal-like sheen. Yuan studied it closely, though the material and design were unfamiliar to him.

Yingzi moved her head closer to the coin and said, “This is made of shadow crystals…”

“What? You also recognize this material?” Yuan looked at her with surprise.

She nodded and said, “Shadow Crystals are rare resources found only in places untouched by light. My homeland had them in abundance. They are similar to spirit stones but contain Chaotic Essence instead.”

“And they’re using such rare materials to make coins to use as currency? Well, I guess it doesn’t really hold any value to cultivators since they cannot cultivate Chaotic Essence.”

“Thanks.” Yuan handed the coin back to the young man before asking, “Is there a place where I can sell some gold coins? It’s not convenient to travel without money.”

The young man pondered for a moment before responding, “Our city is quite small and poor, so you won’t find anyone able to buy them here. How many are you trying to sell?”

“A handful?”

“A hand—?! You have that many?! But you don’t have a single chaos coin?! That doesn’t make any sense!”

If the young man knew Yuan actually had tens of millions of gold coins in his possession, not even the heavens would be able to predict his reaction.

“I came from a very, very seclusive place…” Yuan gave a random but somewhat believable excuse.

“In fact, I am currently traveling the world and gaining experience for a trial, and my family didn’t give me any money when I left.”

“A trial…? Cultivators are truly something else… If only I could become one, too. Unfortunately, I was born into an ordinary family, and I am not desperate enough to risk my life for glory.”

Yuan smiled faintly and said, “To become a cultivator in an environment like this… I can hardly imagine how one could even begin the path without the fortune of a privileged birth.”

“Truly.” The young man sighed. “Forget spiritual energy—even if someone were granted an endless supply, most couldn’t so much as obtain a cultivation technique.”

Sometime later, Yuan asked, “Since you don’t know where the Spirit Tree is located, do you know someone who does?”

“You can try the First City. Their city is the most prosperous in the Realm of Chaos, and it’s where most cultivators gather. Oh, you can probably sell your gold coins there, as well.”

“First City? What is the name of this city?” Yuan then asked.

“The Seventh City.”

“What an odd naming choice. How many cities are in this region?” he asked further.

“Seven,” the young man swiftly replied. “Each city is ranked according to its value, and that rank becomes its name. As such, the names can change.”

“So this is the worst city, huh?”

“Only by a small margin. Besides the top three cities, the disparity between each city isn’t as much as you imagine.”

The noodles began arriving shortly later, quickly filling the table.

“You’re not going to eat?” Yuan asked when the young man didn’t touch the noodles.

“A single bowl is enough to keep me going for an entire day.”

“One bowl for an entire day? No wonder you’re so skinny.”

“What can I do when that’s all I can afford? I’m used to it by now, anyway,” he said with a bittersweet smile.

“Well, today is a special day, so eat until you’re full,” said Yuan. “If you say so.” The young man nodded and reached for a bowl.

chapters.title: Chapter 2187 A Single Gold Coin(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2187 A Single Gold Coin(2)

Although there were 20 bowls of noodles, the group finished them in less than half an hour.

“Wow, I have never been this full before,” said the young man as he patted his stomach, which was noticeably rounder.

“Will you be heading to the First City immediately?” he then asked.

Yuan nodded, “Yes, that’s my intention.”

The young man fell silent, almost as if pondering something.

Seeing his expression, Yuan asked, “What’s on your mind?” “Well… I was just wondering if I could follow you to the First City. After all, I won’t be able to sell the gold coin you gave me in this city, and it’s too dangerous to travel alone. Of course, I understand if you refuse.”

Yuan smiled and said, “I don’t mind. In fact, we can sell the gold coins together. After all, I am not familiar with the First City.”

“I have never been to the First City, either, so I don’t think I’ll be of much help there. However, I’ll still try my best.”

“Sure.”

“Before we depart, I need to return home and let my family know I’ll be gone for a long time,” the young man said.

“You won’t be gone for too long. It’ll take a few days at most.”

The young man wore a puzzled look on his face and said, “What? But the First City isn’t close to our city. It’ll take at least a few months just to get there.”

Yuan chuckled and replied, “That may be the case if we walked there. I intend to fly there. Of course, I’ll take you with me.”

“Fly?! You can actually fly?!” The young man stared at him with awe.

“I sure can.”

“That’s amazing! I’ll go home right now to let my family know!”

Since he had nothing else to do, Yuan followed the young man to his home.

“Mother, father, I’m going to leave this city for a few days—a few weeks at most—to seize an opportunity.”

“Where are you going?” asked the mother.

“The First City.”

“What? Are you sure? That’s at least two months away—not including the time it’ll take you to come back!” the father exclaimed.

“Oh, I won’t be walking there. I made a new friend, and he’s a cultivator who can fly.”

His parents looked at him with worried expressions on their tired faces.

“Wu Qi… are you sure you’re not being tricked by this person? Why would a cultivator want anything to do with you, much less take you to the first city?”

“Don’t say that! He may be standing outside, but he can definitely hear you!”

“Where and how did you meet this cultivator?”

The young man, named Wu Qi, summarized the situation to them.

“A gold coin for twenty-three bowls of noodles…? Aiya… I knew I raised a gullible son, but to think you were this stupid…” The mother sighed while shaking her head.

“Son… listen to your mother and wake up. I doubt this guy is a cultivator, much less generous enough to give you a gold coin for some noodles.” Tʜe source of this ᴄontent ɪs novel-fire.net

Wu Qi gritted his teeth in frustration and shouted, “Fine! Don’t believe me if you don’t want to! You can even call me stupid, but I am going to trust my guts and head to the First City!”

Without another word, he turned around and stomped away.

“Wu Qi!”

“Stop right there!”

Seeing this, his parents immediately went to block his path.

“Let me go!” Wu Qi shouted.

“No! You’ll have to step over our dead bodies if you want to throw away your life!”

“Do you have any idea how much effort we’ve put into raising you?!”

“…”

Wu Qi fell silent after hearing such words.

At that moment, Yuan entered their home and said, “I apologize for the intrusion. I wasn’t going to intervene, but seeing how things have progressed, I think it would be for the best if I stepped in.”

“You! Are you that so-called cultivator?! We know you’re trying to scam our son, so get lost!” The mother approached Yuan in an aggressive manner.

“Mother! Don’t!”

Yuan silently shook his head before hovering his body off the ground.

“As you can see, I am a real cultivator.”

Everyone in the room froze, their eyes staring at Yuan in disbelief.

To further convince them, Yuan used his spiritual energy to grab a nearby chair, placing it behind him and sitting on it.

After a moment of silence, the mother who was standing right before Yuan snapped out of her daze and immediately fell to her knees.

“Please excuse this lowly mortal for her foolishness. I am willing to accept any punishment—”

“Forget it,” Yuan interrupted.

“I know you’re worried for your son, but rest assured that I will bring him back safely.”

“If Senior Immortal says so, we will believe it.”

Of course, despite saying that, the mother was still worried. Unfortunately, as mortals, they were powerless before a cultivator, so all they could do was nod their heads and agree.

“Don’t worry, mother. I trust Senior Brother,” said Wu Qi as he patted her back.

Once things were settled with Wu Qi’s family, they left his home.

“Do you know the direction to the First City?” Yuan asked.

Wu Qi scratched his head for a moment before responding, “Not exactly, but I know it’s somewhere in that direction.”

He pointed toward the north.

“Alright.”

Without another word, Yuan wrapped his spiritual energy around Wu Qi and Mu Xuelian before flying into the sky and soaring toward the north.

“Whoa! I am flying! I am really flying!” Wu Qi exclaimed, his face full of excitement.

“Oh, by the way, my name is Wu Qi. May I ask for your name, Senior Brother?” Wu Qi said after realizing they hadn’t had any introductions.

“My name’s Yuan. She’s Mu Xuelian.”

“Can I call you Senior Yuan?”

“Go ahead.”

A few hours later, they arrived at another city.

“This is the Fourth City, so we’re definitely heading in the right direction,” said Wu Qi.

“Alright. Let’s keep going this way, then.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2188 Chaos' Auction House
chapters.body:
Chapter 2188 Chaos' Auction House

After arriving at the Fourth City, Yuan continued flying in the same direction without stopping for another full day.

That night, Wu Qi slept while still in the air, as he was a mortal who needed sleep. Though they were flying at great speed, the world felt strangely still when he closed his eyes, granting him a surprisingly peaceful rest.

When Wu Qi opened his eyes again, it was already morning, and he could see another city in the distance.
