“That tall black building in the city… It’s the First City. We’re almost there,” he said after noticing the pagoda in the distance.

“Alright.”

Hearing this, Yuan sped up some more.

A few minutes later, they arrived at the city entrance.

When the guards at the entrance saw Yuan, who was fast approaching in the air, they instantly lowered their heads—both in respect and in an effort to avoid drawing his attention.

“Is this the First City?” Yuan asked as he descended before the guards.

“Correct, Senior Immortal. This humble city is indeed the First City,” responded one of the guards in a respectful manner.

Although they have seen many cultivators come and go, they rarely see any of them fly to travel, as that was a luxury only the most powerful and influential cultivators could enjoy.

“I got a few questions, if you don’t mind.”

“We’ll do our best to answer Senior Immortal’s questions.”

“I’m trying to sell some valuables. Do you know of a place that deals with such things?”

Without raising their heads, the guards responded, “For valuable treasures, there are two options.”

“If Senior Immortal is in a hurry to sell his treasures, there is the Inferno Marketplace.”

“If Senior Immortal isn’t short on time, he can try the Chaos’ Auction House for a higher chance of obtaining more money. The auction house opens once every two weeks, and they plan to open in two days.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before speaking, “Can one of you show me the way to the auction house?”

“It’d be my pleasure.” One of the guards stepped forward and bowed respectfully.

Their reverence left Yuan feeling uneasy. In the Nine Heavens, where nearly everyone was a cultivator, such displays of deference were rare.

Sometime later, the guard led Yuan to the auction house, which was only fifteen minutes away from the entrance.

“This is the Chaos’ Auction House. If Senior Immortal has any more questions, this humble servant will be more than willing to answer them.”

“No, that’s all. Thank you.”

“Then, if you’ll excuse me.”

The guard gave him one last bow before turning around and walking back the way they came.

“Let’s go,” Yuan said as he entered the auction house.

“Welcome to Chaos’ Auction House, the most reliable and best auction house in the entire region! Are you here to buy or sell?” A young woman approached Yuan almost immediately after he entered the building.

“I’m here to sell.”

“In that case, please follow me to the seller’s room.”

Once they were seated inside a medium-sized room, the young woman gestured toward the table and said, “I will get the treasure examiner right now. In the meantime, you can place everything you plan on selling at our auction house on the table.”

After saying that, she gave them a slight bow before leaving the room.

Yuan reached inside his spatial ring and grabbed a handful of gold coins without even looking and casually placed them on the table.

Wu Qi, who was sitting next to him, stared at the pile of gold coins with wide eyes and his mouth agape.

Yuan noticed his reaction and asked, “Is this too much?” Wu Qi silently nodded his head.

“I see.”

Without another word, Yuan reduced the pile of gold coins by half.

“What about you?” Yuan then asked.

He shook his head and said, “Well… you’re already selling gold coins—many of them, at that. I doubt my single gold coin will catch anyone’s attention. Instead, I’ll sell it at the Inferno Market.”

Yuan smiled and said, “We can just ask the auction house to sell your gold coin before mine. That way, you’ll definitely be able to sell it for much more. Or, you can sell it together with mine.”

“I don’t think the auction house will let us manipulate them like this…” Wu Qi swallowed nervously.

However, before Yuan could respond, the door opened, and a well-dressed middle-aged man walked inside the room.

“Sorry for the wait. I am your examiner, Chen Cheng. Let’s see what you have—”

Chen Cheng’s movements froze when he saw the small pile of gold coins on the table.

“Are my eyes deceiving me, or are those gold coins?!” he exclaimed after a moment of silence.

Yuan nodded and confirmed, “They are indeed gold coins.”

Hearing this, Chen Cheng swiftly approached the table, nearly tripping on himself in the process. Follow current novels on noveⅼfire.net

When he reached the table, he dropped to his knees, his eyes fixed on the gleaming gold coins laid out before him.

“There are so many! And in such pristine condition?!” Chen Cheng’s jaw fell open at the sight. In his fifty years as an examiner at the auction house, he had only ever encountered two gold coins—and both had been badly worn and damaged beyond recognition.

Since every gold coin in the Primordial Realm dated back to the Primordial Era, it was only natural that most had long since become worn or damaged.

“I’ve only ever seen gold coins in such condition within books! To think I would behold so many at once today! I could die this very moment without regret!” Chen Cheng exclaimed.

However, he suddenly snapped out of it and cleared his throat.

“Ahem… my apologies for failing to contain my excitement. If you don’t mind, I’ll need to examine these coins and verify their authenticity before we proceed to the next steps,” he said.

Yuan nodded.

“Take your time.”

“Oh, you missed one.” Yuan gestured at Wu Qi, who was holding a single gold coin in his grasp.

chapters.title: Chapter 2189 Chaos' Auction House(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2189 Chaos' Auction House(2)

“You also have one?!” Chen Cheng exclaimed as he stared at the gold coin in Wu Qi’s grasp.

“Uh… yes?” Wu Qi quickly placed the gold coin onto the table.

“Alright, give me a few minutes to verify them.”

Chen Cheng retrieved a pair of white gloves and a monocle-like item and carefully inspected each coin once he was prepared, treating each of them as though they were priceless treasures.

Some time later, after setting down the final gold coin, he drew in a deep breath.

“I have examined them all… and I can confirm each one is genuine,” he said, his voice still tinged with daze and disbelief.

“Since you’ve confirmed their authenticity, will the auction house accept them?” Yuan asked.

Chen Cheng nodded.

“Yes, we can sell them for you. When would you like to put them up for auction?”

“As soon as possible. You open up in two days, right? Can you sell them in two days?” Google seaʀᴄh n͟o͟v͟e͟l͟f͟i͟r͟e͟.net

“That’s…” Chen Cheng’s expression immediately shifted to one of concern upon hearing Yuan’s response.

“Is there a problem?” Yuan asked with a raised eyebrow.

Chen Cheng hesitated for a moment before responding, “To be honest with you, there are too many gold coins. I highly doubt we’ll be able to sell them at a satisfactory price on such short notice.”

“If you want the most profit, I suggest spreading their auctions and selling a single gold coin every few years. If you wish to sell all of them at once… Well, there are very few people in this world who would be able to afford them, so you’ll be losing out on the majority of their value.”

Yuan shook his head and said in a firm voice, “I cannot wait a few years. Just sell them all in two days to the highest bidder. You can spread them out, such as selling them in small batches, but I want it all to be sold as soon as possible.”

He couldn’t care less about losing the majority of the profit; to him, it was only a handful of gold coins—worthless in his eyes.

Of course, to Wu Qi and Chen Cheng, Yuan’s actions seemed outrageously wasteful. “Please, Esteemed Guest… It isn’t that I object to selling it your way, but this auction house is under the management of the Chaotic Sect. If they discover how much money I squandered, losing my position would be the least of my worries…” Chen Cheng said with a weary sigh.

“In that case…”

Yuan fell silent to ponder.

After a moment of silence, he said, “Alright, I won’t sell the gold coins.”

“What?! But that’s—!”

“Let me finish.” Yuan interrupted him and retrieved something from his spatial ring.

“I’ll give you one week to sell this,” he said as he placed a pebble-sized stone on the table.

“What is that?” Chen Cheng asked with a puzzled face.

Wu Qi was also curious about the item Yuan had just taken out.

Before they could ask him how this pebble could replace the gold coins, Yuan revealed the item’s name to them.

“It’s a Spirit Stone.”

“A what?!” Chen Cheng exclaimed, stumbling back in shock.

Wu Qi only looked more puzzled, as he’d never heard of a Spirit Stone before.

Once Chen Cheng recovered from his shock, he approached the spirit stone on the table and inspected it from afar, not daring to get too close.

“If this is really a Spirit Stone… It’ll definitely sell more than the gold coins, as it’ll attract not just collectors but cultivators as well!” he exclaimed.

“What is this Spirit Stone?” Wu Qi asked out of curiosity.

“It contains spiritual energy and is what cultivators require to cultivate,” Chen Cheng gave a brief explanation.

“So it’s something like the Spirit Tree? I heard that place also has spiritual energy,” Wu Qi remarked.

“No, the Spirit Tree is different.” Chen Cheng shook his head. “Anyway, although one week is not a lot of time… I think it’s enough to spread the word far enough.”

Yuan nodded and said, “By the way, do you know where the Spirit Tree is located?”

“The Spirit Tree…? No, I do not. It’s a heavily guarded secret by the rulers of this world, so only the most powerful and influential cultivators are privy to that information.”

“I see. Then, do you think the Spirit Stone will attract someone from the ruling backgrounds?”

“Oh, surely. I have no doubt that someone from the Chaotic Sect will show up for it since I will be contacting them later about it. The others are also likely to appear, but I cannot guarantee it.”

“Alright. As for the gold coin… can you still sell one of them?”

“Of course. Do you still want to sell the gold coin in two days, or would you like to sell it alongside the Spirit Stone?” Chen Cheng asked.

“Two days,” he said.

“I understand. Then would you like the auction house to hold the Spirit Stone for you?”

“Go ahead.” Yuan nodded.

“Thank you for your trust. We will not disappoint you. I’ll take the Spirit Stone to our vault now. Be right back.”

Chen Cheng left the room shortly after with the Spirit Stone and a single gold coin.

“Why didn’t you wait a little longer to sell the gold coin?” Wu Qi suddenly asked.

“I want to bring you back to your home before the auction,” Yuan said. “Eh? I don’t mind staying here a little longer,” he said.

However, Yuan shook his head and said, “It’s for your safety. I have a feeling that things might get chaotic at the auction for the Spirit Stone.”

“If you say so…” Wu Qi nodded.

Chen Cheng returned to the room a few minutes later and said, “I have secured your treasures. You can come back on the day of the auction if you have nothing else for me now.”

“Then we’ll be back in two days,” Yuan said.

“Here is a token.”

Chen Cheng continued after handing them three metal tokens, “Show this to our workers when you show up. It means you are our esteemed guests.”

Yuan accepted the token before handing one to Wu Qi and Mu Xuelian.

“See you in two days.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2190 Auction For A Single Gold Coin
chapters.body:
Chapter 2190 Auction For A Single Gold Coin

“See you in two days.”

However, just as Yuan and his group approached the exit, Yuan came to a sudden halt.

“Did you forget something?” Chen Cheng asked.

“Actually, I have an odd request,” Yuan faced him and continued, “Can I borrow some money?”

“Excuse me?” Chen Cheng’s eyes widened with disbelief, not daring to believe his ears.

How could someone with so many gold coins and even a spirit stone ask for money? It made zero sense.

Seeing Chen Cheng’s confused expression, Yuan explained, “You see, we don’t have any currencies. That’s why we’re trying to sell the gold coin and spirit stone as soon as possible. Don’t worry, I’ll return twice the amount you lend me.”

“H-How much do you want?” Chen Cheng asked in a dazed voice.

“Just enough to rent a few rooms and eat some food for the next two days.”

Chen Cheng reached into his pocket and retrieved a large leather bag and said, “Here’s ten large chaos coins and a hundred small chaos coins. You can live luxuriously for an entire month, much less two days.”

“Thanks.” Yuan accepted the money and left the auction house shortly after.

Once they were outside, Yuan asked Wu Qi, “How much did he give us? I’m not familiar with its value.”

Wu Qi then explained, “Ordinary people like my parents earn about two hundred small coins a month. As for your large coin… a single one is worth ten thousand small coins, so you essentially have 100,000 small coins.”

“I see.”

“In that case, let’s go eat.”

Wu Qi nodded, “I’m glad you said that because I am starving.”

They proceeded to wander the city until they found a restaurant that seemed appealing enough to enter.

Sometime later, after they finished their meal, Yuan’s group went to rent three rooms in a hotel.

“Senior, what are you going to do for the next two days?” Wu Qi asked him. Orıginal content can be found at NoveI-Fire.ɴet

“I’m going to cultivate,” he said.

“In that case, I’ll take a look around the city. I doubt I will ever come back here, after all.”

“Here, take this.”

Yuan handed him a single large coin.

“This is…?” Wu Qu stared at it with a dumbfounded face.

“You don’t have any money, right?”

“I can’t take this…”

“Just take it. Or I’ll just throw it away.”

Yuan flicked the coin into the air, forcing Wu Qi to catch it.

“Thank you…” Wu Qi muttered as he stared at the coin in his grasp.

“Then I’ll leave now. Have fun.” Yuan said before entering his room to cultivate.

Since the Primordial Realm was full of Chaotic Essence, he could improve his Chaotic Dragon Core again.

Meanwhile, Wu Qi wandered through the city. Though the Primordial Realm was a land steeped in danger and lawlessness, its cities remained remarkably safe due to an iron rule.

Compared to the Nine Heavens—no, even compared to Earth—the population of the Primordial Realm was laughably small. Since most of its dwellers were mortals, strict laws forbade cultivators from fighting in or near the cities, lest humanity itself edge toward extinction.

Those who violate this law would be branded as criminals and hunted by all four ruling backgrounds.

However, the moment one stepped beyond the safe zone, everything became fair game—whether mortal or Immortal. Though some might call this contradictory, such was simply the way their society functioned.

Two days passed in a flash, and in that time, word of a gold coin being sold at the Chaos Auction House spread to every corner of the city, stirring the interest of many collectors. Unfortunately, the news went no further; with spiritual energy scarce in the Primordial Realm, communication jade slips were exceedingly rare and reserved only for true emergencies.

However, while the gold coin had stirred the interest of mortal collectors, the announcement of a spirit stone to be auctioned in a week spread like wildfire, reaching even the ears of cultivators in distant regions.

“What? A spirit stone in the Primordial Realm? How is that possible?” A cultivator questioned the legitimacy of this news.

“The Chaos’ Auction House announced it, so it must be true. I doubt they’d do something as foolish as lying about this.”

“Even if it’s a spirit stone, I don’t get the hype. It doesn’t last forever, and I doubt it’ll have much effect on cultivators beyond a certain level.”

“You really don’t get it, huh? How do you think mortals become cultivators in a world that lacks spiritual energy?”

“You don’t mean…”

“That’s right. The only way for a mortal to become a cultivator is either to go to the Spirit Tree or a Spiritual Rift, as these are the only ways for us to acquire spiritual energy. However, neither of these options is viable for most people. Although a single spirit stone may not benefit cultivators like us, it’s a godly treasure for ordinary mortals. I bet a lot of powerhouses will fight for it to help their children become cultivators.”

To embark on the path of cultivation, one must first absorb spiritual energy. Yet in the Primordial Realm, there were only two sources: the Spirit Tree, guarded within the Asura Clan’s territory, and the rare Spiritual Rifts—unpredictable anomalies that could never be relied upon.

Because of this, spirit stones became treasures of immense value. In fact, every spirit stone brought to the Primordial Realm in the past had been used in precisely this way. Unfortunately, spirit stones have long since become extinct, so the number of people becoming cultivators has drastically decreased. On the day of the auction for the gold coin, Yuan made his way to the auction house with Wu Qi and Mu Xuelian.

“Wow, there are so many people here,” Wu Qi remarked as they arrived at the auction house. The street was so crowded it became a sea of bodies, making it impossible to move any closer.

“Are they all here for the gold coin? Or are they waiting for the spirit stone? Either way… I like this!” he chuckled.

chapters.title: Chapter 2191 Auction For A Single Gold Coin(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2191 Auction For A Single Gold Coin(2)

It took a couple of hours, but Yuan’s group eventually made it to the entrance of the auction house.

“I’m sorry, but the seats are already fully occupied. If you’re here to bid for the gold coin, you’ll have to do so out here,” said the worker at the entrance.

Yuan didn’t say anything and retrieved the token he got from Chen Cheng, showing it to him.

The worker’s eyes widened at the sight of the token, and he quickly said, “Please excuse me for not recognizing you, esteemed guests! We have reserved seats for you all in room three!”

He then stepped aside to let Yuan’s group into the building.

“Hey! How come they get to go inside but we’re not?!”

Those who weren’t allowed inside began complaining outside. However, Yuan’s group simply ignored them and quickly disappeared inside.

A few minutes later, they arrived at their room and went inside. Their room was located above the ground floor, allowing them not only a perfect view of the stage but also of other guests.

Yuan and the others took a seat by the window and began waiting for the auction to begin.

“Most of these guests are mortals with a few cultivators sprinkled within,” Yuan remarked as he gazed at the crowd.

“I’m not surprised. Only collectors would be interested in something like a gold coin since it doesn’t have any practical use,” said Wu Qi.

Minutes later, they heard a knock on the door.

“Esteemed guests, it’s me, Chen Cheng. May I bother you for a moment?”

“You can enter.”

“Thank you.”

Chen Cheng opened the door and bowed to them, “I came here immediately after hearing of your arrival. I hope this room is to your liking. If not, we can change it.”

“Our room is just fine. Do you need something from us?” Yuan asked.

“It’s not me. The Chaotic Sect wants to have an audience with you,” said Chen Cheng. “After I spread news of the spirit stone and notified the Chaotic Sect, I was contacted by them and was told they wanted to speak with you.”

“Did they say what they wanted to speak to me about?”

Chen Cheng shook his head.

“No, I was not told the details.” ʀᴇᴀᴅ ʟᴀᴛᴇsᴛ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀᴛ novel·fire·net

Yuan pondered for a moment before responding, “Tell them I’ll speak with them after the auction—for the spirit stone.”

“Is it not possible to speak to them before? They sounded a bit impatient…”

“So what? It’s just five days. They can wait,” Yuan calmly replied, leaving Chen Cheng speechless.

“I understand. I’ll let them know. If you need any service or have any questions, there will be a servant available right outside your room. Now, if you’ll excuse me.” Chen Cheng bowed to him before leaving the room.

“Why are you making the Chaotic Sect wait? They’re one of the most powerful and influential backgrounds in this world…” Wu Qi stared at him with a dazed and confused face.

“They probably want to see if they can buy the spirit stone directly from me. Even if that’s not the case, I’m just trying to send a message.”

“A message?” Wu Qi became even more puzzled.

Yuan smiled but did not elaborate.

Seeing how Yuan didn’t want to explain, Wu Qi didn’t press further and returned to looking at the platform below.

About an hour later, Chen Cheng walked onto the platform and stood before the crowd with a satisfied smile on his face. The Chaos’ Auction House had a thousand seats, but there were usually no more than a hundred or so guests. However, not a single seat was empty today. “Welcome to the Chaos’ Auction House, fellow guests and esteemed guests. I am Chen Cheng, an appraiser for the auction house. Normally, the auction is done by someone else, but due to circumstances, I will be hosting it today. Therefore, allow me to apologize in advance for my lack of experience and any mistakes I may make during the auction.”

“Before we begin, allow me to explain some of our rules…”

Chen Cheng proceeded to reveal the typical auction rules.

“You are not allowed to threaten other guests with your background. Once you call out a bid, you are not able to take it back—”

A few minutes later.

“Now, let us begin the auction! For our first treasure, we have an excellent sword crafted by the renowned blacksmith Xi Yang from the…”

Yuan watched the auction with interest at first, but his interest disappeared after several items when he realized that everything being auctioned was meant for mortals. ‘What was I expecting from the Primordial Realm, a world where there is no natural spiritual energy?’ Yuan shook his head inwardly.

A world without natural spiritual energy didn’t just make cultivation difficult. It also made crafting treasures almost impossible, so most of the weapons in this world were ordinary weapons without any special capabilities.

After a dozen items were sold, it was finally time for the gold coin to be auctioned.

“Before we begin selling the last item for the day, I would like to thank everyone here for their participation and appearance,” said Chen Cheng as another worker pushed a cart onto the stage.

Atop the cart rested a single gold coin, displayed upon a luxurious cloth and treated as though it were a priceless treasure.

“And now, the main item of today’s auction—a gold coin untouched by time! Not only is it perfectly pristine, but it bears not a single mark of damage!” Chen Cheng proclaimed as he gestured at the cart.

“Oooh! It really is a pristine gold coin!” many guests exclaimed, leaning forward in their seats to catch a clearer glimpse.

Naturally, the auction house had cleaned up the gold coin before the auction, but even if they hadn’t done such a thing, the coin was like new.

After a brief pause, Chen Cheng’s voice rang out once more, “We will now begin the auction for this gold coin! The starting bid—five hundred thousand small Chaos Coins!

chapters.title: Chapter 2192 Auction For A Single Gold Coin(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2192 Auction For A Single Gold Coin(3)

“Five hundred thousand? For a single coin?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at the absurd starting price. Now he understood why Chen Cheng gave him so much money without hesitation.

“550,000!”

Someone immediately increased the bid.

“560,000!” “590,000!”

“690,000!”

The guests continued bidding one after another, and in the blink of an eye, raised the bid to over a million small Chaos Coins.

“A million…?” Wu Qi’s eyes widened, his jaw falling open as he stared at the chaotic scene. For someone from an ordinary family like him, such a sum was nothing short of astronomical. It was something he could only dream of.

However, the auction was just getting started.

“1,200,000!”

“1,500,000!”

“3,000,000!”

In the blink of an eye, someone doubled the highest bid, causing heads to turn.

“Isn’t that the Golden King, who is a hardcore collector for anything gold?”

“I knew he’d show up for the gold coin.”

“I heard that almost everything in his house is gold—including the house itself.”

The other guests started chatting with each other. Although a thousand guests were present, most of them had no plans to bid for the gold coin, as it was obviously beyond their capabilities.

However, the bidders inside the auction house were not the only ones competing. Many guests who had been unable to enter due to limited space were placing their bids from outside.

The bidding for the gold coin continued fiercely for several more minutes before it slowed down, and the number of bidders drastically decreased.

Sometime later, Chen Cheng announced the winner.

“S-Sold to the Golden King for 70 million small Chaos Coins!” Sure enough, the Golden King won the bid.

“Holy shit… 70 million?! I knew gold coins were expensive, but I didn’t think it would be worth this much!” In the past, most gold coins had sold for only a few million at best, with the highest ever recorded at seven million. But those had all been damaged coins. A flawless coin like the one Yuan offered had never been seen before.

“This marks the end of today’s auction. Once again, I would like to thank everyone who has come today for your participation.” Chen Cheng bowed to the crowd.

“If you have purchased an item, please head to the collection room to complete the transaction.”

“One last announcement before I take my leave… If you haven’t heard already, the Chaos Auction House will be offering a Spirit Stone for sale in five days. However, due to its immense value and high profile, strict requirements will be enforced for participation. I ask for your understanding,” he said before turning around and disappearing from the stage. Thıs content belongs to novel⟡fire.net

“Let’s collect our money, shall we?” Yuan turned to face Wu Qi with a smile.

“Okay…” Wu Qi nodded in a dazed manner.

After leaving the room, they were led to the collection room by the servant who had been waiting outside. There were two collection rooms in the auction house. One was for the buyers and the other for sellers.

“You can collect your money here once the buyer completes the transaction,” said the servant.

Sometime later, Chen Cheng showed up in their room with a large chest.

“After deducting our auction house’s fifteen percent fee, your profit from the sale of the gold coin amounts to 59,500,000 small Chaos Coins. We’ve already converted them into large Chaos Coins for your convenience—there are 5,950 in total,” Chen Cheng explained as he opened the box and revealed a mountain of coins in it.

“Do you have any questions for me?”

Yuan retrieved thirty large Chaos Coins from the box and handed them to him.

“This is more than I lend you…” Chen Cheng said with a surprised face.

“Consider it a bonus,” Yuan said with a smile.

“Thank you!” Chen Cheng bowed deeply.

Yuan turned to Wu Qi and asked, “You ready to return home?”

“Yes!” he nodded.

“I’ll carry the money for you,” Yuan said as he stored the box inside his spatial ring.

“?!?!”

Both Wu Qi and Chen Cheng stared at him with a bewildered look on their faces, as they had never seen a spatial ring before.

“W-Where did it go?!” Wu Qi asked.

Yuan showed him the ring and said, “In here.”

“I have never seen anything like that before!” he remarked.

“That function… don’t tell me it’s a Spatial Ring…?” Chen Cheng asked after swallowing nervously.

Yuan calmly nodded.

“Heavens…” Chen Cheng was speechless.

Spatial Rings were unimaginably rare—so rare that fewer than ten existed in the entire Primordial Realm, each belonging only to the realm’s most powerful figures.

‘Not only does he have many pristine gold coins and a spirit stone, but he also has a Spatial Ring?! Just who in the world is this guy?!’ Chen Cheng cried inwardly.

“If there’s nothing else, we’ll be taking our leave now,” said Yuan.

“O-Oh, okay. I’ll see you in five days for the spirit stone’s auction.” Chen Cheng nodded.

Yuan and his group started walking toward the door, but before they could reach it, it swung open, and two figures stepped into the room.

When Chen Cheng saw these two figures, his face immediately twisted with surprise.

“This humble servant greets the Chaos Elders!” “Chaos Elders?” Yuan raised an eyebrow as he looked at the man and woman, both dressed in black, standing by the entrance.

They also stared back, and after a moment of silence, the man opened his mouth to speak, “Are you the one with the Spirit Stone?”

“Who’s asking?” Yuan responded.

“We’re from the Chaotic Sect,” replied the woman.

“I see. And what do you want?”

“We’re here to talk.”

“Talk? I’m pretty sure I already said to wait until after the auction for the spirit stone,” Yuan said.

The woman frowned and said, “How impudent. The Chaotic Sect went out of its way to approach you, and you’re telling us to wait? Do you really think you’re above us because you have a spirit stone?”

chapters.title: Chapter 2193 Wu Qi'S Gift
chapters.body:
Chapter 2193 Wu Qi'S Gift

The atmosphere in the room instantly became heavy with the woman’s words.

However, Yuan remained unaffected and calmly responded, “Is the Chaotic Sect so great that waiting five days is considered offensive?”

The woman’s face flushed red as she spoke, “Are you mocking our Chaotic Sect?!”

Yuan casually shrugged, “If you find everything that comes out of my mouth offensive, then let’s just stop here. I have other things to do.”

Without waiting for a response, Yuan walked around them and toward the exit.

“Where the hell do you think you’re going?! We’re still talking!” “We can talk in five days, or we can not talk at all. The choice is up to you,” Yuan replied without stopping his legs.

“If you dare take a step through that door, you will regret it for the rest of your life,” said the woman in a grim tone.

Yuan halted his steps and turned his head to look at her with slightly narrowed eyes.

“Are you threatening me? In that case, you can forget it. I won’t talk to the Chaotic Sect anymore.”

“You impudent bastard! Have you forgotten where you are?! Not only are you in the Chaotic Sect’s territory, but you are also selling your Spirit Stone at our auction house!”

“So what you’re saying is, I should sell the Spirit Stone elsewhere? Am I hearing that correctly?”

“I’m going to kill you!” The woman shouted as her body surged with killing intent.

“Stop.” The man finally stepped in and spoke, “Let’s all calm down a bit.”

However, Yuan said, “Sorry, but you’ve stepped in a little too late. I have already made up my mind that I won’t speak with the Chaotic Sect. If you continue to pester me, I’ll even take back the spirit stone that will be auctioned in five days.”

“That… would not be possible,” said the man. “While I can look past the issue of not talking, I cannot let you take back the spirit stone, especially after all the advertisements we’ve done for it. If you take back the spirit stone and halt the auction, it’d cause tremendous damage to the Chaotic Sect’s reputation, and we’d have to stop you at all costs.”

“If you don’t want such a thing to happen, then let’s end our conversation here.”

The very next moment, Yuan left the room without any hesitation.

“Are we really going to let him go after all that?” The woman asked the man.

After a moment of silence, he spoke, “There are too many people here now. Let’s lay low until the auction is finished. We’ll take care of him afterward.

“Alright.”

The man turned to look at Chen Cheng and said, “Unless you have a death wish, you will act like you heard and saw nothing today.”

Chen Cheng was so terrified that he didn’t even dare to speak and silently nodded his head.

Meanwhile, after leaving the auction house, Yuan immediately left the city to bring Wu Qi back to his family.

“Senior… I don’t mean to be nosy, but why did you offend the Chaotic Sect? Nothing good will come out of it.”

“Why? I didn’t like their attitude.”

“That’s it?” Wu Qi was dumbfounded.

“Yes, that’s it.”

“Aren’t you afraid that they might hunt you later?”

“It’ll be fine. Although this means that I won’t be able to get information on the Spirit Tree from the Chaotic Sect, there are other places I can go, such as the Zhao Family and the Inferno Alchemists.”

“I just hope that they won’t hunt me down for being with you…” Wu Qi sighed.

Yuan smiled and said, “Don’t worry, they didn’t even look at you, as all of their attention was on me.”

Sometime later, they returned to the Seventh City.

Yuan brought Wu Qi straight to his home, where his parents were anxiously waiting.

“Mother! Father! I am back!” Wu Qi said as he entered the building.

“Oh! You’re really back!” His mother cried after seeing him.

“Is there really a need to cry? I told you that I would be back.”

Once they calmed down, Yuan retrieved the box of money from his spatial ring and placed it on the floor.

“Here’s your money,” he said.

Wu Qi then asked, “We’re not going to split it?” “No, it’s all yours. After all, it came from selling your gold coin.”

“But you gave it to me…” Wu Qi said in a dumbfounded voice.

Even if Yuan said he wanted 90 percent of the money, he wouldn’t have refused. Hell, even one percent of the wealth from selling the gold coin would allow his family to live comfortably for the rest of their lives.

Yuan smiled and said, “If I really need money, I can just pawn off a few gold coins. Moreover, I will have plenty from selling the spirit stone in a few days.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Wu Qi sighed, “I don’t even know how to begin to thank you for changing my—our lives.” Follow current novels on novelfire(.)net

“You don’t have to thank me.”

“Even if you say that… Oh! I know! Please wait here for a minute!”

Wu Qi suddenly turned around and ran away, quickly disappearing from their view.

He returned moments later with something in his grasp.

“I bought this a few years ago from a wandering merchant. Unfortunately, I don’t know if it has any uses or if it’s even valuable at all since I got it because it looked neat.”

Resting on Wu Qi’s palm was a crystal the size of a finger. At first glance, it looked pitch-black, but upon closer inspection, faint black liquid seemed to swirl within.

Yuan accepted it casually, but the moment he examined it more closely, his eyes widened in surprise.

‘There’s an immense amount of Chaotic Essence within this crystal!’

He wasn’t sure what it was, but he was certain that if he absorbed it, his second cultivation would skyrocket several realms without any issues.

Yingzi emerged from his shadow to take a look since it also piqued her curiosity.

“You know what this is?” he asked.

She shook her head and said, “No… but merely looking at it sends chills down my spine.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2194 Auction For The Spirit Stone
chapters.body:
Chapter 2194 Auction For The Spirit Stone

“Thanks, Wu Qi, for this gift,” Yuan said to him after stowing away the crystal.

“Do you think we can meet again in the future?” Wu Qi asked.

“Honestly, I don’t know, since I won’t be staying in the Primordial Realm for long.”

“What?” Wu Qi raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

Yuan smiled and said, “I am actually not from this world, but from the Nine Heavens, which exists beyond this world.”

Wu Qi’s jaw dropped, too speechless to even utter a sound. However, everything about Yuan made sense after learning of this revelation.

‘No wonder he has so many gold coins and treasures that are insanely rare in this world! To think he had come from the outside world!’

After a moment of silence, Wu Qi asked, “Can I ask why you decided to come to our world?”

“I have some business with the Asura Clan, that’s all.”

Wu Qi swallowed nervously. Though he still had many questions, he decided not to ask them.

“In that case, good luck…”

Yuan nodded, “Goodbye.”

Yuan left the city shortly after and immediately made his way back to the First City.

As he traveled, he pondered whether he should consume the black crystal he got from Wu Qi.

‘The amount of Chaotic Essence it contains is unlike anything I have ever seen. If I consumed it, I could probably create at least two more Chaotic Hearts…’

However, he also understood that it was unwise to consume something so mysteriously powerful. Though he may have a god-like body, it was not omnipotent.

Moreover, the vast amount of Chaotic Essence within the crystal could take him several years just to absorb, so he also needed a place and time to do it properly.

A day later, Yuan returned to the First City.

He’d only been gone for a little over two days, but the number of visitors had grown several times since then, and most of them were cultivators.

Among the cultivators, nearly half wore matching attire, divided into three distinct groups. One faction was clad in black robes, another in red and gold, and the last in green. Obviously, these three groups belonged to the Chaotic Sect, Inferno Alchemists, and the Zhao Family—the three ruling factions in the Primordial Realm.

However, the Asura Clan was nowhere to be seen.

As Yuan and Mu Xuelian made their way through the crowded streets, nearly every gaze was drawn to them. Mu Xuelian, a peerless beauty with cultivation at the peak of the God Ascension realm, commanded admiration, while Yuan radiated an unfathomable aura—even as he suppressed it.

“Who’s that beauty? With her cultivation and appearance, there’s no way I haven’t heard of her.”

“Judging by her appearance, she doesn’t seem to be with any of the top three factions. Maybe she’s from a hidden family?”

“Look at that handsome man next to her. Although his cultivation isn’t too impressive, I can feel something unfathomably profound coming from his very presence.”

“He’s suppressing it, but he clearly has a lot of spiritual energy in his body!”

Despite their interest, nobody tried to approach them. In the Primordial Realm, cultivators preferred to mind their own business, as those who stand out tend to become targeted.

Of course, that didn’t apply to everyone, and eventually, someone approached Yuan and Mu Xuelian.

“Excuse me, may I have a moment of your time?” A well-dressed middle-aged man approached them and said.

“How can I help you?” Yuan responded as he glanced at the man’s green robes.

“I am a recruiter from the Zhao Family. I was wondering if I could treat you two to some tea.”

“Maybe another time. We’re busy now,” Yuan calmly replied.

“I understand. If you ever have the time, please visit the Zhao Family in the Zhao Province.” The man nodded and then offered him a token.

Yuan accepted the token and continued walking.

‘At least they’re not as arrogant as those two from the Chaotic Sect,’ Yuan smiled inwardly as he made his way to the auction house.

They arrived at their destination shortly after, and unsurprisingly, it was filled to the brim with people, far more than before. Of course, most of the people there belonged to one of the three top factions.

When Yuan eventually reached the entrance, he was immediately greeted by the same worker he had met the previous week.

“Welcome back, esteemed guests. Allow me to escort you to your room.”

Yuan followed the worker to the same room from last time.

“Please, make yourself comfortable while I let Appraiser Cheng know of your arrival.”

The worker left, and several minutes later, Chen Cheng knocked on the door.

“Esteemed guest, it’s me, Chen Cheng.”

“Enter.”

Chen Cheng entered the room at his command.

“Welcome back, esteemed guest. I hope—”

“Did those two idiots make things difficult for you after I left?” Yuan suddenly asked, catching him off guard.

“I-Idiots…?” Chen Cheng swallowed nervously as he quickly closed the door behind him.

“Sorry about how things went, but I didn’t appreciate their attitude.”

“No… there’s no need for you to apologize…” Chen Cheng recalled the Chaos Elders’ plans for Yuan after the auction and couldn’t help but feel bad for him.

“I guess something did happen after I left,” Yuan suddenly said, startling Chen Cheng.

“W-What do you mean? Nothing happened after you left! Although the Elders were upset, they left without saying anything!” Yuan narrowed his eyes at Chen Cheng and sighed, “You’re not very good at lying.”

“T-That’s—!” Chen Cheng was speechless.

“So, what did they say? Are they going to kill me after the auction or something along those lines?” “!!!”

Seeing Chen Cheng tremble, Yuan calmly chuckled, “I see. So that’s their plan.” Get full chapters from novel~fire~net

“How…” Chen Cheng suddenly spoke. “How can you be so calm? You’re going to be hunted by the Chaotic Sect, you know! Unless you have connections with the Asura Clan, you will never be safe in this world ever again! If you had agreed to talk with them, none of this would’ve happened!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2195 Auction For The Spirit Stone(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2195 Auction For The Spirit Stone(2)

Yuan smiled at Chen Cheng’s words and said, “I appreciate your concern, but the Chaotic Sect is nothing in my eyes.”

“Nothing… in your eyes?” Chen Cheng swallowed nervously. In his mind, only someone from the Asura Clan would dare speak with such arrogance. ‘Is he actually from the Asura Clan? But then why would he need information on the Spirit Tree? No, let’s stop thinking about this. It’s beyond me.’

After a moment of hesitation, Chen Cheng said, “The Chaos Elders intend to take care of you after the auction. Please be careful.”

He gave a swift bow to Yuan before walking toward the exit.

“Thanks for the information,” Yuan said as Chen Cheng left the room.

Sometime later, Chen Cheng appeared on the stage to begin the auction.

“Welcome or welcome back, esteemed guests, to the Chaos’ Auction House. I am Chen Cheng, an appraiser, and just like last week’s auction, I was tasked with today’s auction.”

He proceeded to give the same speech as last week, going through the rules and whatnot.

“Now, without further ado, let us begin this week’s auction.”

“For the first item, we have a rare Divine-grade sword. Its name is Bloody Enlightenment, and it was once wielded by the Mad Bloodhound.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow when he saw the item being auctioned. For more chapters visıt novel·fire.net

“They’re selling a Divine-grade sword for the first time? I guess they decided to bring out all of the good stuff for the guests today,” he remarked.

Since most of the guests at the auction were cultivators, there was no sense in offering items meant for mortals. Besides, with the Spirit Stone so highly anticipated, everyone present was guaranteed to have deep pockets.

“A hundred million!”

“A hundred fifty million!”

In the blink of an eye, the item was sold for 210 million small Chaos Coins. Though spiritual treasures required spiritual energy to unleash their full potential, they remained incomparably more durable than ordinary weapons, granting their wielder a tremendous advantage over those without one. For this reason, they were still highly sought after and expensive.

Of course, they also served as symbols of status and power.

The next several items were also spiritual treasures, and each was better than the last.

Sometime later, it was finally time for the spirit stone to be auctioned.

“And now, the moment you’ve all been waiting for—”

With a flourish, Chen Cheng pulled away the cloth covering the display case, revealing the spirit stone within. Though it appeared far less impressive than the treasures auctioned before it, the instant it was unveiled, every guest’s eyes lit up with awe and excitement.

“It’s really a spirit stone! We haven’t seen one of those since they were all used up long ago!”

“If I had that spirit stone, my son would be able to cultivate! I must get my hands on it even if I have to go into debt!”

Chen Cheng went on, “Before we begin the bidding, allow me to introduce today’s item. As you all surely know, a spirit stone is a cultivation resource brimming with spiritual energy. While such stones are plentiful in the outside world, within the Primordial Realm, one has not been seen for millions of years since they’ve all been used.”

“A spirit stone never decays nor loses its potency, so there is no need to rush in using it.”

“Now then, the auction will begin at two hundred million—”

“One billion!”

Someone swiftly bid for the spirit stone before Chen Cheng could even finish, which started a chain reaction.

“One billion and a hundred million!”

“One billion and two hundred million!”

In the blink of an eye, the bidding for the spirit stone soared to an astounding one billion, five hundred million.

“Heavens… I knew it was going to be a fierce competition, but to think it’d reach a billion at the very start…”

“The Chaotic Sect is especially fierce today.”

The next several items were also spiritual treasures, and each was better than the last.

Sometime later, it was finally time for the spirit stone to be auctioned.

“Who do you think will win in the end?”

“Surely, it must be the Inferno Alchemists, right? They’re the wealthiest faction in the world.”

“But they have the most access to the Spirit Tree, so they’re not really starved for spiritual energy.”

“So it’s a fight between the Chaotic Sect and the Zhao Family, huh?”

“The Zhao Family will likely win the auction since they have more money than the Chaotic Sect.”

Most had expected the Zhao Family to claim victory, yet by the end, all were left speechless when the Chaotic Sect secured the spirit stone with a bid of eleven billion.

“Eleven billion? That’s insane! Do they even have that much money?”

Unbeknownst to the crowd, the Chaotic Sect would not have hesitated to pay even more—for they knew they would soon reclaim their wealth regardless.

“And with that, today’s auction comes to an end. We look forward to seeing you all at the next—” Chen Cheng began.

“Wait! Who is the owner of the spirit stone? I would like to speak with them!” Someone from the crowd suddenly interrupted.

“Me too! I would also like to speak to the seller!”

The place quickly filled with people wanting to meet Yuan.

“I-I’m sorry, but the seller wishes their identity to remain a secret—”

But before he could finish, the doors to the auction hall burst open, and a figure strode inside, declaring, “I am the owner of that spirit stone.”

“What?”

Everyone in the room turned their heads to look at Yuan, who’d just entered the room.

“Who is that?”

“I don’t recognize him, either.”

Chen Cheng stared at Yuan with wide eyes.

“Who are you? And can you prove that you are the seller?” someone suddenly asked.

Yuan calmly nodded.

“Of course.”

He proceeded to reach inside his pocket and retrieved another spirit stone.

“Heavens! He has another spirit stone!”

While the crowd erupted with excitement at the appearance of a second spirit stone, the auction’s winners wore twisted expressions—ugly, as though they had just swallowed a live fly.

They turned to glare at Chen Cheng, who noticed it and quickly shook his head, as if trying to say he didn’t know anything about a second spirit stone.

chapters.title: Chapter 2196 Auction For The Spirit Stone(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2196 Auction For The Spirit Stone(3)

“A second spirit stone! He actually has a second spirit stone!”

“Do you intend on selling that spirit stone as well?!”

Yuan responded to the inquiry, “Yes, I do intend on selling the spirit stone, but I don’t want money for it.”

“What do you want?” someone asked.

“I’ll give this spirit stone to whoever can provide me with the location of the Spirit Tree.”

“What…?”

The room fell into instant silence, the guests glancing at one another with uncertain, wary looks.

Although the spirit stone was an attractive offer, it wasn’t something worth offending the Asura Clan for.

Seeing the deafening silence, Yuan reached into his pocket and retrieved a third spirit stone, causing everyone to widen their eyes again.

“He’s pulled out another spirit stone!”

“Heavens! How many of those does he actually have?!”

While some started considering telling Yuan about the Spirit Tree, others wondered if he would pull out more spirit stones if they waited a little longer.

“If you don’t mind me asking, why are you searching for the Spirit Tree?” Someone suddenly asked.

Yuan turned to look at the individual, a middle-aged man wearing green robes, who was the same person bidding against the Chaotic Sect for the spirit stone at the very end.

The man continued, “Even if you know its location, you cannot enter it without the Asura Clan’s permission.”

“I want to go there exactly because I have some business with the Asura Clan.”

“…”

The room fell silent again.

When no one responded, Yuan spoke up. “Perhaps some of you prefer not to speak in public, so I’ll end it here. My offer will remain in effect until I am informed of the information I need to know. You can find me in room 302 at the Black Inn, four blocks from here.”

Having said that, Yuan turned around and disappeared, leaving everyone speechless.

The other guests began leaving shortly after.

Meanwhile, Yuan went to collect his earnings for the spirit stone.

“Your earnings for the spirit stone are nine billion, three hundred and fifty million small Chaos Coins after deduction from our auction house’s 15 percent fee. However, due to the massive amount, we regret to inform you that it’ll take some time for the money to be transferred,” said Chen Cheng.

“And how long will that take?” Yuan asked.

“We should have the money ready by next week,” he said.

Yuan smiled and asked, “You sure the Chaotic Sect isn’t trying to screw me over?”

“I… have no comments…” Chen Cheng shook his head.

“Very well. Since I have some time to spare, I’ll wait. However, if I don’t have my money by then, I’ll personally visit the Chaotic Sect, and there won’t be any conversations, if you know what I mean.”

“T-That’s…” Chen Cheng opened his mouth but didn’t know how to respond.

Yuan continued, “Don’t worry, I wasn’t talking to you, but the sneaky rat in this room.”

“?!?!”

Chen Cheng’s eyes widened at his words.

In the next moment, the hidden presence unveiled itself, revealing none other than the male Chaos Elder from the other day.

“That’s quite the bold threat, even though it’s nothing but an empty one.” ɴᴇᴡ ɴᴏᴠᴇʟ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀʀᴇ ᴘᴜʙʟɪsʜᴇᴅ ᴏɴ noveⅼfire.net

“If you think it’s an empty threat, you can go ahead and test me. Then, I’ll be back in a week.”

Yuan turned and left. After departing the auction house, he returned to his hotel room to wait for someone to accept his offer—and it didn’t take long.

At the sound of a knock, Yuan opened the door to find a figure cloaked in grey, their appearance concealed from head to toe.

“Is the offer still available?” asked the man in a rough tone.

“Sure is. Want to talk inside?”

However, the man shook his head and said, “There are too many people here. Let’s talk somewhere more private.”

“Sure. Lead the way.”

“Want me to come with you?” Mu Xuelian asked him from the other room through voice transmission.

“No, you can stay here.”

And so, Yuan followed the man out of the hotel. Before long, they had left the city behind entirely, traveling until even the distant outline of its walls was lost from sight.

“Surely, this is far enough,” Yuan said.

The man halted his movements and turned toward Yuan, who wore a calm expression despite the sketchy situation.

“You’re a little too calm. Are you not worried that I might rob you?” asked the man.

“Would I have followed you all the way out here if I were worried?” Yuan responded.

“Fair enough.”

The cloaked figure pulled back his hood, revealing himself as the very man who had questioned Yuan at the auction house about his interest in the Spirit Tree’s location.

“I don’t mind telling you the location of the Spirit Tree, but I must ask, what business do you have with the Asura Clan?”

“Just some personal business.”

The man narrowed his eyes and fell silent to ponder.

“Are you… planning to fight the Asura Clan?” he finally asked.

Yuan raised an eyebrow. “And what makes you think that?”

“You’re wealthy enough to possess three spirit stones, so you’re clearly from a powerful background. If I had to guess, you belong to one of the factions long denied access to the Spirit Tree… and now you seek revenge against the Asura Clan.”

Yuan was amazed at the misunderstanding, but he didn’t deny it and played along to see where they would go.

“Are you now going to say that you won’t tell me the Spirit Tree’s location because you don’t want any involvement?” However, to Yuan’s surprise, the man shook his head and said, “No, that’s not it. In fact, I—we would like to help you.”

“We?”

“The Asura Clan has monopolized the Spirit Tree since ancient times, and there are many people who are sick of it. That’s why many of us are going to put an end to it. Why don’t you join us? We’ll get you to the Spirit Tree and back you in your revenge,” the man urged.
